The average decoding time was reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5,982.83$</tex> milliseconds to 79.97 milliseconds, while the average memory usage decreased from 15.6 megabytes to 1.73 megabytes for 3,000 test samples. These results prove the efficiency and practicality of the proposed method. With this decoding method, the applicability of the 4SQR-Code increased because low latency and constrained memory are critical in real-world scenarios.

title: Exploring Factors Affecting Entrepreneurs' Intention towards Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI): Using an Extended TOE Framework
authors: Arif Hasan, Navita Nathani, Vikas Kumar Khare, Sandeep Raghuwanshi, Umesh Gautam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The present study explores the application of AI by Indian entrepreneurs as per Technology-Organisation-Environment (TOE) based framework. Being one of the approaches to combining qualitative and quantitative methods, it considers how AI is improving the business processes, decision-making and efficiency in the service, trade and manufacturing sectors. The empirical data were examined through Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA), followed by Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA) and Structural Equation Modelling (SEM), employing SPSS 18 and AMOS 20 for statistical analysis. Previous results indicate, Artificial intelligence is enhancing the accuracy of the business and livelihood of the people as it can predict the information and the market. Policy recommendations and practical advice should be circulated to popularise AI among entrepreneurs. It suggests in drafting the importance of technological, organisational and environmental stress launching the AI as a Platform to draft the Programmes and policies can be made to support and facilitate economic growth.

title: Research on Deep Learning-Based Methods for Detecting Broken Strand Defects in Ground Wires
authors: Shiqiang Yang, Xiaolai Li, Xiangkui He, Xinwei Feng, Yuxin Wu, Xiaying Xiong
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the challenges of high dynamic range, complex backgrounds, and variable illumination in images, this paper first proposes an improved YOLO-based enhancement algorithm to improve robustness under these conditions. Next, an RDN-based super-resolution reconstruction module is introduced to enhance the detail features in low-resolution images. ly, experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can reliably detect transmission line conductors and their defects (strand looseness and breakage) in various environments, achieving an overall performance metric of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 8 \%}$</tex>.

title: FedFall: A Federated Transfer Learning Framework for Privacy-Aware Fall Detection in Elderly Care using IoT-Edge Devices
authors: Abdulrahman K. Alnaim, Khalied M. Albarrak, Abdullah A. Alnuaim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Falls are a leading cause of injury, hospitalization, and mortality among elderly individuals, posing significant challenges to healthcare systems worldwide. Traditional fall detection systems often rely on centralized cloud processing, which introduces latency, privacy concerns, and limited adaptability to individual users. To address these limitations, we propose FedFall, a novel federated and transfer learning-based framework for real-time, privacy-pre-serving fall detection on IoT-edge devices. FedFall enables on-device inference and personalization by fine-tuning a pretrained base model using user-specific data while pre-serving raw data locality. The framework supports secure model aggregation through federated averaging and integrates optional differential privacy to enhance confidentiality. Designed for low-power wearable devices, FedFall achieves efficient deployment without compromising responsiveness or accuracy. We validate the framework using standard activity recognition datasets and simulated edge-client scenarios, demonstrating its feasibility in delivering accurate, adaptive, and privacy-conscious fall detection across heterogeneous edge environments.

title: Coordinated Interaction Strategy of Source-Grid-Load-Storage Based on Transformer Stability
authors: Junhong Chen, Qiran Zhang, Zhaobin Zhong, Wenzhen Zhang, Bochuan Gu, Weiting Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In the process of constructing a new power system, the multi-subject and multi-dimensional interactive relationship between Source-Grid-Load-Storage has become increasingly complex. As the core hub equipment for power transmission and distribution, the operational stability of transformers is facing the superimposed impacts of sudden power changes caused by large-scale integration of new energy, instantaneous current surges during energy storage charging and discharging, and random fluctuations in user-side loads. Traditional Source-Grid-Load-Storage coordination strategies mostly focus on optimizing overall system benefits, making it difficult to achieve efficient allocation of system resources while ensuring the safe and stable operation of transformers. To address this issue, This paper proposes a set of collaborative interaction strategies: first, by constructing a high-precision Source-Grid-Load-Storage resource characteristic model, the operating laws of various elements under transformer stability constraints are accurately depicted; second, with the safe and stable operation of transformers as a hard constraint, the operating costs and benefit demands of power source, grid, load, and energy storage subjects are comprehensively balanced, and an effective incentive mechanism is established to mobilize the enthusiasm of all subjects for collaborative participation; meanwhile, a distributed coordinated control platform is developed to realize the observability, measurability, controllability, and optimal dispatching of Source-Grid-Load-Storage resources.

title: Integrating Deep Reinforcement Learning with AI Algorithms for Optimal Path Planning of Indoor Handling Robots
authors: Jinran Song
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Indoor handling robots play a critical role in automated warehousing, manufacturing workshop material transfer, and other industrial scenarios, where path planning directly affects operational efficiency, safety, and energy consumption. Traditional path planning algorithms face inherent limitations: global algorithms (e.g., <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm) excel in finding shortest paths based on static maps but lack adaptability to dynamic obstacles (e.g., temporary goods, pedestrians); local algorithms (e.g., fuzzy logic) enable real-time adjustments but tend to fall into local optima; and single deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods, while adaptive to dynamic environments, struggle with global path optimality and require extensive training. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid path planning method integrating the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm (as the AI-based global planner) and DRL (as the local dynamic adjuster). For global path planning, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm uses a Manhattan-distance heuristic function to generate the shortest path based on preknown indoor maps (shelf layouts, passage distributions), providing a foundational navigation framework. For local dynamic adaptation, a DRL module (based on Deep Q-Network, DQN) is designed: the robot acts as an agent, with state inputs including its position, heading angle, and dynamic obstacle information; the reward function balances obstacle avoidance, path tracking, and operational efficiency; and the agent learns real-time obstacle avoidance strategies through training. Experiments were conducted in both simulated (ROS/Gazebo) and real-world warehouse environments, comparing the proposed method with single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{*}$</tex>, single DRL, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{*}+$</tex> fuzzy logic algorithms. Results show that the proposed method increases dynamic obstacle avoidance success rate by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \% - 2 0 \%}$</tex>, shortens path execution time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 \%}-\mathbf{1 2 \%}$</tex>, and controls path length deviation within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 \%}$</tex> compared to baseline methods. This work effectively balances global optimality and dynamic adaptability, meeting the requirements of automated warehousing and workshop material transfer scenarios.

title: An Explainable Machine Learning Approach for Agricultural Crop Recommendation
authors: Ankit Jaiswal, Pushpendra Kumar, Aryan Patel, Gaurav Singh, Anil Kumar Yadav
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Crop selection is a pivotal decision-making process for farmers, impacting cultivation outcomes and the efficient allocation of resources. Traditionally, crop selection has been based on farmers' experience and local knowledge. However, under dynamic environmental conditions such as soil quality, climate change, and weather variability, this traditional approach makes it difficult to make well-informed decisions. This paper introduces “Harvestify,” a crop recommendation system that combines predictive accuracy and interpretability. Harvestify in-corporates various machine learning (ML) techniques, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, K-Nearest Neighbors, LightGBM, and XGBoost, and rigorously evaluates their crop prediction capabilities. Comparative analysis revealed that the XGBoost classifier demonstrated superior performance, leading to its adoption as the implemented model. To ensure transparency, the SHAP (Shapley Additive explanations) approach is introduced to generate interpretable explanations for crop recommendations based on various soil and climate parameters. The SHAP approach is an interpretability framework based on game theory, assigning a unique value to each feature in the dataset, indicating the contribution of each feature to the model's prediction. This research demonstrates the synergistic integration of machine learning techniques and explainable artificial intelligence (AI) for precision agriculture, providing data-driven insights to farmers while maintaining transparency in decision-making.

title: Design and Implementation of a Line Array CMOS High-Speed Acquisition System Using FPGA
authors: Bing Li, Linxian Liu, Jia Gao, Yang Xu, Xuehai Qiao, Wenjie Liang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a high-speed acquisition system for linear CMOS sensors based on an FPGA. The system utilizes the Hamamatsu S11108 linear CMOS as the image sensor. An FPGA chip and Verilog HDL are employed to implement overall system control. The output signal from the CMOS is sampled and converted by an AD9826 A/D converter, with the resultant data transmitted at high speed via a USB 3.0 interface. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves high-speed acquisition for 2048 pixels at a frame rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4,670 \text{fps}$</tex>. The system exhibits high frame rates, high sensitivity, stable performance, and portability, and portability. It also offers a degree of versatility, providing reliable low-level support for applications such as spectral analysis, industrial inspection, and precision measurement.

title: Early Detection of Varicose Veins Using Vision Transformers in Deep Learning
authors: J. M. Jenilyn, V.Mulli Mary, M. Jasmine Mary, Y. Mary Jansi Rani, C. Jasphin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Varicose veins, a prevalent vascular disorder, are commonly observed among individuals engaged in prolonged standing occupations such as teachers, conductors, and retail workers. Early detection is crucial to prevent severe complications like chronic pain and venous ulcers. However, conventional diagnostic methods are manual and time- consuming. This paper proposes an automated approach for early varicose vein detection using Vision Transformers. Our method leverages self-attention mechanisms to efficiently classify medical images of lower limbs. The experimental results show that the ViT model significantly outperforms traditional classifiers like Support Vector Machines and Convolutional Neural Networks in terms of accuracy and scalability, achieving 95.8% accuracy. The model was deployed in a web-based application, making early screening accessible to a broader population.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Multi-Dimensional Trust Evolution and Adversarial Resilience Mechanisms in Flying Ad Hoc Networks
authors: Sumit Kumar, Jay Prakash
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Flying Ad Hoc Networks (FANETs) are increasingly used for critical missions that need secure and reliable multi-UAV collaboration. However, their open wireless links and changing topologies make them vulnerable to various insider and external attacks. Traditional cryptographic methods alone are not enough to identify compromised or misbehaving UAVs. In this work, we introduce a new blockchain-based, multi-dimensional trust evolution framework to improve security and resilience in FANETs. Each UAV maintains a trust vector consisting of five behavioral aspects: Packet Delivery Ratio, Link Stability, Cryptographic Compliance, Mobility Consistency, and Energy Behavior. Trust dynamics are modeled with Kalman filter smoothing and Bayesian inference for detecting sophisticated adversaries like on-off, collusion, and Sybil attackers. Trust updates are stored on a lightweight blockchain for tamper-proof, auditable trust dissemination. The framework is implemented using NS-3 for UAV network simulation and Python for trust evaluation and blockchain integration. Results show that our approach achieves high attack detection rates (over 95%), quick trust convergence, and low overhead, while greatly enhancing network reliability during attacks. The proposed framework offers an effective, scalable, and auditable trust management solution for nextgeneration secure FANET deployments. This study supports SDG 9, SDG 11 of the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

title: Adaptive Window-Length Adjustment and Feature Fusion for Enhanced CFAR Detection
authors: Zhongli Zhang, Xuezhen Cheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper proposes an APSO-CFAR algorithm incorporating multi-feature fusion discrimination and adaptive window length adjustment. The traditional Cell Averaging CFAR (CA-CFAR) tends to have an increased detection threshold due to interfering targets in multi-target environments, while also suffering from a sharp increase in false alarms at clutter edges. Although the Smallest Of CFAR (SO-CFAR) alleviates multi-target interference to a certain extent, it still has the issue of relatively high false alarms at clutter edges. The proposed method in this paper constructs a more discriminative fused detection metric by integrating multi-dimensional features such as clutter power, target distance, and velocity, thereby enhancing the robustness of target representation. Meanwhile, it dynamically optimizes the reference window length based on local variance: a larger window length is adopted in homogeneous backgrounds to improve estimation accuracy, while the window length is shrunk at interference or clutter edges to suppress interference from non-homogeneous samples. Experimental results show that the proposed algorithm significantly improves multi-target detection capability and adaptability to clutter edges while maintaining a stable false alarm rate.

title: AI Regulation in the U.S.: Lessons from Commodity Futures Legislative Journey and a Perspective CFTC-NFA Model for AI
authors: Di Kevin Gao, Jingdao Chen, Shahram Rahimi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The emergence of ChatGPT in November 2022 and the subsequent rush to “AI-ize” everything from scheduling to speech synthesis to surgery have decisively shifted the focus of AI regulation from “if” to “what” and “how”. While other major economies, especially the European Union, have taken the lead in AI regulation, substantial disagreements on what prospective AI regulation should look like have inhibited necessary progress in the United States. It is critically important for the AI community to coalesce around a shared vision to advance AI regulation effectively. To that end, the authors examined the US legislative history for insights. After extensive research, we found an equally disruptive and controversial parallel: commodity futures. Futures contract, a 19th-century invention, was initially hailed by growers, buyers, and bankers as a breakthrough. Its legislative journey, however, was tumultuous. It was almost banned nationwide, as anti-option laws passed both chambers of Congress, but was ultimately spared when Congress failed to reconcile the House and Senate bills. In the next 100 years, Congress experimented and patch-worked various versions of futures laws and finally settled on a principles-based, central-agency-managed, and self-regulatory organization (SRO)-supported regulation, facilitating its exponential growth. Demonstrating their adaptability, commodity futures laws have become a primary vehicle for regulating emerging digital assets such as cryptocurrencies. In this article, the authors analyze the similarities between the legislative experience of AI and futures: both face an emotional and opinionated general public, a slow-moving Congress, and active state legislatures. Futures' treacherous legislative journey, near-death experiences, and subsequent astronomical growth provide AI with a cautious yet promising roadmap. At the conclusion of this article, the authors recommend a principles-based law, central-agency-led architect, and SRO-supported model to regulate AI, supported by AI regulatory enablers including AI registration and disclosure, AI monitoring, and AI enforcement.

title: Radiative Cooling for Enhanced Efficiency in Outdoor LED Lighting Systems
authors: Guillermo del Campo, Igor Gomez, Edgar Saavedra, Asuncion Santamaria
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Radiative cooling (RC) materials are emerging as a promising passive strategy to lower the operating temperature by combining high solar reflectance with strong mid–infrared emissivity. This paper presents an experimental assessment of a commercial RC film, with and without compound parabolic concentrator (CPC) optics, applied to a commercial LED street luminaire under realistic outdoor conditions. Different configurations were evaluated: full <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R C}$</tex> coverage of the luminaire head, partial RC strips over critical zones (LED and driver), and RC combined with 2D CPC elements mounted above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R C}$</tex>–coated regions. Surface temperatures were measured at multiple points on the luminaire, together with ambient temperature and solar irradiance, during both daytime and nighttime operation. RC film consistently reduced luminaire surface temperatures during daytime compared with the reference configuration, with typical reductions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7-8^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and peak differences of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> approaching under strong irradiance. Partial RC coverage still yielded local cooling of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> degrees in critical regions, while requiring less material and having lower impact on the product form factor. CPCs preserved or slightly enhanced the RC effect, acting as incremental modifiers rather than primary drivers. The study also discusses material characteristics (durability, mechanical properties), environmental robustness of CPC geometries, and preliminary cost–benefit considerations. The results are intentionally exploratory, but they provide system–level evidence that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R C}$</tex> and RC+CPC integration is feasible and beneficial for LED street luminaires, and they establish a quantitative baseline for future optimization and long–term studies in smart–city lighting applications.

title: Global Path Planning for Unmanned Surface Vehicles Based on Line-of-Sight Guidance
authors: Xing Liu, Jianyi Li, Qinghua Huo, Bing Yang, Kali Feng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the development of marine resources and the increasing demand for maritime missions, as an important marine operation tool, the global path planning technology of unmanned surface vehicles has become a research hotspot. This study aims to design an efficient and safe global path planning method for unmanned surface vehicles to improve their mission execution capabilities in complex marine environments. Based on the line-of-sight guidance planning method, by simulating the line-of-sight guidance method of human drivers, an optimal path from the starting point to the end point is planned for the unmanned surface vehicle. This method combines the line segment and arc segment guidance methods, which is not only simple in calculation and strong in real-time performance, but also can effectively cope with the path planning needs in different marine environments. The research finds that the global path planning method of unmanned surface vehicles based on line-of-sight guidance has significantly improved the efficiency and safety of unmanned surface vehicle mission execution, and provided strong technical support for the development of unmanned surface vehicle technology.

title: BhashaHMPV: Multilingual HMPV News Articles Dataset for Indian Regional Languages
authors: Jigyasa Verma, Shakshi Sharma, Rahul Goel
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In this work, we present a multi-lingual dataset of news stories that were gathered by online automation from Google News. The majority of infodemic analysis research focuses on datasets in the English language, however, our study bridges a significant gap by taking into account a variety of linguistic situations. Several Indian languages, including Hindi, Punjabi, Gujarati, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, Malayalam, and Urdu, are represented in the dataset's news items. Understanding how false information and stories about the Human Metapneumovirus (HMPV) disseminate across languages is crucial given India's rich linguistic variety. Language identifiers, full-length content, article sources, and links to original news stories are all included in the dataset's comprehensive metadata. In order to gain a better understanding of how HMPV is addressed in various geographical contexts, we employ topic modeling tools to extract prevalent themes across languages. Additionally, we perform descriptive analysis by looking at commonly used phrases, social media references, and myths related to HMPV. This dataset is a useful tool for multi-lingual NLP applications, public health communication research, and misinformation detection. The dataset has been released for public use<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1https://zenodo.org/records/15194116.

title: Front Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)

title: Frequency Response Modeling and Analysis of Variable Speed Pumped Storage
authors: Yian Yan, Shanying Li, Han Xie, Tao Wu, Qianlin Zhao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Since Variable speed pumped storage (VSPS) units have adjustable rotational speed, they offer greater frequency regulation (FR) potential and have been widely promoted in recent years. To fully exploit VSPS frequency regulation capability, the FR mechanisms of the hydraulic and electrical subsystems of the VSPS unit are first analyzed. Based on this analysis, a model of the VSPS unit participating in the system frequency response is established. Subsequently, the root locus method is employed to investigate the influence of key FR parameters on system stability, and analytical expressions for typical frequency response performance indicators during VSPS participation are derived. Finally, the accuracy of the proposed model and the derived indicator expressions is verified through Simulink simulations.

title: An Implementation Framework for Application Aware CDN
authors: Gao Chen, Cao Chang, Tao He, Ying Liu, Junbin Pan, Ran Pang, Xiongyan Tang, Yuerui Ma
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the continuous innovation and large-scale application of IPv6/IPv6+ technology, IP networks had the foundation for the collaboration between the layer of service network and the layer of bearer network. The paper introduced an innovative concept of Application Aware CDN (Content Delivery Network) and proposed a implementation method based APN (Application-aware Networking). This kind of new CDN could maintain the application mode of CDN to IPv4 networks, solved the infeasiblity problem that every internet application is required to implement APN independently in the existing APN applicaion mode, broke the traditional independence between CDN services data and network, achieved deep integration of service distribution, network scheduling, and data transmission, and helped build a unique “intelligent media pipeline” for telecom operators.

title: Fault Diagnosis Model for Photovoltaic Arrays Using an Improved Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Shiyan Li, Kailiang Teng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Efficient fault detection in photovoltaic (PV) arrays is critical for ensuring the operational safety and reliability of PV power plants. Conventional detection techniques are often hampered by low efficiency, high false positive and false negative rates, and inadequate adaptability to complex operational conditions. Furthermore, manual hyperparameter tuning of deep learning models is not only inefficient but also susceptible to convergence on local optima, thereby constraining the models' diagnostic performance. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a fault diagnosis method for PV arrays utilizing an improved Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm to optimize a hybrid CNN-Transformer model. Initially, a simulation model of the PV array is developed to analyze the output characteristics of its components under various fault scenarios, facilitating the selection of appropriate fault features. Subsequently, to address the challenge of optimal parameter matching in the CNN-Transformer hybrid model for fault classification, a PSO algorithm is employed to automate the search for optimal hyperparameters. Moreover, to mitigate issues inherent in conventional PSO, such as inertia weight stagnation, poor adaptability to diverse parameter types, and limited local search capability, an enhanced strategy incorporating adaptive control theory and a normal distribution is introduced to formulate a dual-factor adaptive weight mechanism. This approach is further augmented by integrating crossover and mutation operations from genetic algorithms, enhancing its robustness for optimizing both discrete and structured parameters while mitigating the risk of local optima entrapment. The proposed fault diagnosis model was validated using both the simulated photovoltaic array fault dataset and the public dataset. Experimental results show that the model achieved high diagnostic accuracy rates of 97% and 95.1% respectively on the corresponding test sets, thus demonstrating its effectiveness and generalization ability.

title: Improving Hardware and Software Systems for Monitoring Geomechanical Characteristics of Rocks in Boulder-Sand-Gravel Quarries
authors: Victor A. Khakulov, Vitalii A. Shapovalov, Dana V. Khatukhova, Ibragim A. Nogerov, Amina E. Shinakhova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The paper presents a solution on improving hardware and software systems for monitoring the geomechanical characteristics of rocks in boulder-sand-gravel quarries.

title: An Assessment of Rural Consumer Shopping Orientation Towards FMCG
authors: Manmohan Bansal, Somesh Sharma, Darshneel Grover, Prakash Garia, Mohnish Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: As the economy continues to grow and evolve, understanding the factors influencing consumer behavior is essential for businesses, particularly in rural areas. This study focuses on understanding rural consumer shopping orientation towards fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG). It segments rural consumers based on different shopping orientation styles. A structured survey was used to collect data from 501 respondents. Principal component factor analysis was employed for data analysis. The study reveals that rural consumers can be segmented into six shopping orientation segments: experiential, quality, convenience, price, recreational, and brand-oriented shoppers. The findings assist marketers in developing strategies to segment, target, and position their products effectively in rural markets.

title: Dynamic Modeling and Performance Analysis of PEM Electrolyzer–Fuel Cell Systems for Sustainable Power Generation
authors: Shravan Jaiswal, Anmol Ratna Saxena
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This study aims to develop and validate a simulation framework for the design and optimization of smallscale integrated PEM electrolyzer-fuel cell systems. The core of the framework is a detailed electrochemical model of a PEM electrolyzer, incorporating the Nernst equation and overpotential losses to generate accurate polarization curves. The model extends to hydrogen production calculation, dynamic storage tank pressure monitoring, and fuel cell operation. To ensure practical utility, the system employs a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \Omega$</tex> load resistance to achieve Maximum Power Point (MPP) operation, enabling the fuel cell to deliver power at its optimal efficiency. This configuration, coupled with a power conversion stage, ensures stable DC outputs of 24 V and 30 V. The results, simulated in MATLAB/Simulink. The study concludes by highlighting the system's feasibility as an efficient platform for renewable energy storage, laboratory testing, and education.

title: FedSiaNet: A Privacy Preserving Federated Siamese Network for Real-Time ECG Based Stress Anamoly Detection
authors: Amrita Choudhury, Kandarpa Kumar Sarma, Lachit Dutta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Stress related cardiac abnormalities have become an emerging healthcare concern. Elderly individuals and patients with pre-existing conditions are particularly vulnerable. Therefore, early detection mechanisms are essential for timely medical intervention. Traditional centralized healthcare systems often issue regarding privacy, slow communication and not always adaptable to individual needs. The decentralized, privacy preserving learning methods can solve these limitations. Among them Federated Learning allows many sites to train a model collaboratively without sharing sensitive patient data. It improves privacy and enables personalized heart monitoring even in remote areas. In this study we have present a Federated Siamese Network (FedSiaNet). This is a federated learning system that uses siamese network to detect stress related heart issues from ECG signals. To extract discriminative temporal features from ECG signal pairs each branch of the network uses a temporal convolutional module (TCM), BiLSTM layers and L2 normalization. The model uses contrastive loss to separate normal and stress conditions. FedSiaNet trains the model on data stored across multiple devices, without sharing raw ECG signal. This preserves the patient privacy. The results from the experiments shows the accuracy 97% and ROC-AUC of 0.98. The t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) and principal component analysis (PCA) visualization show the distinct class separation in the embedding space.

title: Cyberbully Detection using Improved RoBERTa and Deep Learning based ResNet50
authors: Archana Gupta, Rajneesh Kumar Singh, Annu Mishra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Cyberbully is one of the most prevalent and dangerous issues nowadays. It is significantly higher in young generations than the older ones. Social media and other common platforms are some key realms of this issue. Background: According to current statistics by UNICEF, every third individual is being bullied on such platforms. More than 50 % students have reported to be cyberbullied and harassed according to the report by Cyberbullying Research Center. The common way of cyberbully are memes. These are the unexplored category of cyberbully detection techniques. Purpose: We need to surveil cyberbully and find a firm method to detect and report such cases at earliest. Methodology: Thus, this article presents one such method to detect the cyberbully using sentiment analysis and emotion recognition. The pre- trained RoBERTa was employed along with BiLSTM followed with ResNet50. Results: The accuracy gained was 96.2 % and F1-Score was observed to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 8 0}$</tex> on MultiBully dataset. Conclusion: The study was carried to use multi-modal data to classify cyberbullying content into more than one type. The findings were compared with prior work that concentrated on analogous tasks, namely in the classification of not-bullying, degrading, insensitive, and violent classes derived on MultiBully dataset

title: A Multi-Modal AI and Blockchain-Based Framework for Automated Academic Certificate Authentication
authors: Deepak Singla, Prakash Tukaram Raut, Shipra Goel, Raju Shanmugam, Deepali Gupta, Reshma R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The rise in educational certificate fraud in India has created significant verification challenges for institutions and employers, as existing manual validation methods are slow, inconsistent, and prone to human error. This paper presents a prototype of a web-based system designed for automated certificate authentication using a multi-modal artificial intelligence pipeline supported by lightweight blockchain hashing. The system integrates Tesseract OCR to extract textual information from uploaded documents and employs a CNN-based model (ResNet) to detect signs of forgery in certificate images. Additionally, each certificate's hash is stored in a shared ledger to ensure tamper-evident verification. Experiments conducted on a dataset of 200 authentic and forged certificates achieved an accuracy of 78% with an average processing time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5$</tex> seconds. The results highlight the potential of AI-driven verification; however, broader datasets and institutional adoption are essential for real-world deployment and improved reliability.

title: A Novel UAV Formation Control Strategy: Combining Virtual Target Points and Auxiliary Forces
authors: Yufeng Chen, Yafei Qu, Xiue Gao, Yulan Sun, Bo Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: To address the limitations of existing approaches that combine the artificial potential field method with consensus theory—such as adaptation conflicts and insufficient robustness in dynamic environments—this paper proposes a UAV formation control strategy that integrates virtual target points and auxiliary forces. The virtual target points are generated by synthesizing information from the leader, neighboring agents, and the overall potential field. Meanwhile, a nonlinear auxiliary force is introduced to break force equilibrium and escape local minima. Simulation experiments were conducted, and the results demonstrate that the proposed strategy effectively resolves the local minimum problem, achieving significant improvements in convergence speed, stability, and energy efficiency.

title: Optimized Adaptive Multiobjective Evolutionary Algorithm for Cognitive Radio Resource Scheduling Using Deep Neural Network
authors: Rushikesh Pawar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This paper shows how to use Genetic Algorithms (GA) and Deep Neural Networks (DNN) together to solve multi-objective optimization problems in wireless communication systems. It tries to find the best balance between three goals: Transmission power, modulation order, and symbol rate. GA uses evolutionary operations to explore the solution space, and the DNN is trained on an interval of previous generations regularly. It then predicts the best candidates, which, alongside the candidates from GA, increases the mating pool, to help the search go more quickly. This design speeds up convergence while keeping computational redundancy low. The DNN can change to fit different situations, which lets you change its parameters on the fly. The test results show that it works better, with metrics like Hyper-volume (HV), Inverted Generational Distance (IGD), Mean Squared Error (MSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and R-squared. This method provides a practical, scalable solution that potentially improves the performance and flexibility of modern wireless communication systems, especially in changing environments where reliable optimization is important.

title: A Space-Efficient Multi Rotor Wind Turbine System for Decentralized Power
authors: Jawaharlal Bhukya, Somasri Yaswanth Reddy Alavala, Vivek Chandra Kodamasimham, Yaswanth Koyaneni, Yaswanth Koyaneni, Siddhesh Bhushan
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Reliable electricity supply in rural and remote areas remains challenging due to the intermittency of solar photovoltaic systems during low-irradiance conditions. This paper presents a compact, low-cut-in-speed multi-propeller wind energy system intended for effective operation in weak winds. The proposed system utilizes four eight-blade propellers arranged at 90° intervals and mechanically coupled via a planetary gearbox to a common generator shaft, allowing for efficient torque aggregation while maintaining a compact, rooftop-capable footprint. Prototype blades were 3D-printed in PLA with a radial span of 145.5 mm, a curvature length of 175 mm, and a 27° twist. Laboratory testing of the prototype shows a cut-in speed of approximately 2.8 m/s and a monotonic increase in generator speed, voltage, and output power with wind speed, achieving 12.73 W at 6.7 m/s under a 25Ω load. These results demonstrate the feasibility of the multi-propeller planetary configuration as a modular solution for low-wind rural electrification and hybrid renewable systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for ECG Based Human Identification
authors: Sanatan Pande, Anand Mohan, Dilnawaz, Manoj Tripathy, R S Anand
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: ECG signals are personal. They exhibit subtle physiological patterns that vary from one individual to another, making them well-suited for biometric identification tasks. We exploited this property by designing a hybrid deep learning model that combines three complementary components - 1D CNNs to capture local waveform morphology, BiLSTM layers to model temporal signal evolution, and multi-head self-attention to capture broader contextual dependencies across the sequence. Our experiments use the ECG-ID database, where raw signals are cut into overlapping <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 0}$</tex>-sample windows before being fed into the network. Rather than standard normalization, we applied median- and MAD-based scaling to handle common outliers in ECG recordings better. To prevent overfitting data augmentation was performed through the addition of noise, amplitude scaling, a temporal shift, and MixUp. Training of the model is done by using a AdamW optimizer with label smoothing and a WarmUp-Cosine learning-rate schedule for stable convergence. The proposed model achieves 97.80 % validation accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.975, surpassing prior ECG-based identification methods that rely solely on CNNs or recurrent models.

title: XAI for Agriculture: Low-Complexity AI Solutions for Real-Time Plant Health Monitoring
authors: S. Ashok Kumar, A. Ramesh Babu, A. Hima Sree, C. Amrutha, E. Bhavani, S. Althaf
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Plant diseases are a major challenge in producing agriculture products, especially in areas where the diagnosis is often made through simple visual inspection and traditionally poor access to experts. While deep learning methods have proven to be very accurate in a controlled setting, real-world implementation is limited because of high computational demands and difficulty in clear decisions and usability for farmers. To circumvent these problems, in this paper, we propose a lightweight and interpretable plant disease detection model named XAI-Plant, that is designed to work in real-time on resource constrained device. The proposed system employs a small convolutional neural network optimized for execution on a CPU combined with a version of Grad-CAM explainability module that highlights the disease affected areas in the leaf images. In addition, a multifaceted (multilingual) output interface is incorporated in order to enhance accessibility of users with different linguistic backgrounds. The model is then trained with a combination of benchmark data sets and images collected in the field in order to assure robustness during actual farming conditions. Experimental evaluation demonstrate that the proposed system has 95.1% accuracy of controlled data set and 92.3% accuracy of field data while also keeping low calculation. The results present that useful plant disease diagnosis can be achieved with interpretable and efficient artificial intelligence models, giving rise to feasible support for agricultural applications.

title: ScaffSmart: Deep Learning Based Real-Time Scaffolding Inventory Management
authors: Salar Ahmed Mirza, Sabeen Masood, Ali Zain, Danish Ashfaq
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Construction sites are intricately complex environments that demand constant coordination among stakeholders and the management of materials. Traditional Management Information Systems (MIS) are inadequate because they are only capable of recording information and enabling simple communication, and none are specifically tailored to the intricacies of scaffolding management. This paper presents ScaffSmart, a novel system designed to automate the operations of organizations that rent scaffolding. It uses the YOLOv8 deep learning algorithm to automatically identify and count scaffolding items from site images, achieving a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 87.5 % on a dedicated dataset, greatly improving inventory accuracy. ScaffSmart combines this computer vision feature with a full range of features such as rental management, owner-client communication, notification alerts, and data management through Firebase for real-time synchronization. Through automation of normally manual and error-prone processes, the system streamlines the entire MIS workflow, thereby demonstrating a practical pathway to significantly enhance efficiency and reduce operational costs in the scaffolding industry.

title: Demonstration of the Diagnostic Potentiality of the Anti-Tetracycline Aptamer on Screen-Printed Electrodes for Detection of Tetracycline
authors: Shuhadah Ismail, Marimuthu Citartan, Amirul Firdaus, Asrulnizam Abd Manaf, Mohd Syamsul, Shaili Falina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Tetracycline (TET) has been used in the poultry sector as a growth promoter. Excessive use of antibiotics in poultry can lead to resistance, reducing the effectiveness of treatment for human illness. This highlights the need for a tool to monitor antibiotic use in poultry. This study focuses on the aptamer-based electrochemical sensor (aptasensor) for detecting TET. The aptasensor was developed by functionalizing the TET-specific aptamer onto gold (Au) working electrode of a printed circuit board electrode (PCBE) and was assessed for TET detection using the differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) technique. The preliminary study shows that the aptasensor is responsive to the presence of the TET, where the peak current of the aptasensor was observed to be decreased to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$117.27 \mu ~\mathrm{A}$</tex> from an average peak of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$142.99 \mu ~\mathrm{A}$</tex> recorded for the bare aptasensor upon exposure to the TET in buffer solution. Furthermore, this study also examines the limit of detection (LOD) of the aptasensor using different TET concentrations. The LOD achieved was 1 mM. Overall, this preliminary study demonstrates the potential of the PCBE aptasensor to be used as a practical antibiotic monitoring tool in the poultry sector.

title: Reducing Middleware Latency in Telecom CRMs Using Deep Reinforcement Learning Focus: Optimizing API Call Paths and System Queues Dynamically
authors: Manoj Kota
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The telecom middleware in Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems faces challenges such as growing Application Programming Interface (API) overhead, dynamically varying service interactions and responding to rapid service calls. Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is an interesting training as it is exposed to learn to make optimal choices through the assistance of performance records. Nonetheless, DRL cannot succeed because the approvals are rarely performed, the training does not work, and they are not aware of the dynamics of middleware architectures. To address these constraints, Neural Architecture search to DRL Deployment (NASDRL) is suggested as the remedy to the issue of efficient DRL architecture, prioritizing workload behaviour by default. The Contrastive Learning improvements the quality of inputs in DRL by training it to be able to differentiate between various instances based on unlabeled record data. Moreover, it is possible to detect the emerging dependencies between services and to mine network facts and sequences of the traffic system using Dynamic Graph Convolutional Networks (DGCNs). Also, Graph Policy Networks (GPNs) are developed to model graphbased middleware environments, the purpose of which is the ability to learn adaptive policies and choose optimal paths. The proposed technology will bring improvements to the performance of middleware by bettering learning capacity, structured information push and pull, intelligent decision-making and minimizing latency in CRM applications applied in telecom industry. This is accomplished by adding the routing API call paths and routing the routing API call paths in the system.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Machine Learning Framework for Precision Farming: Optimizing Crop Yield and Profitability
authors: B. Dilip Chakravarthy, M. Ananda Gopal Reddy, D. Fareedha, Yama Charitha, L. Ganesh Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper introduces a hybrid machine learning and quantum, inspired framework for the prediction of crop yield and profit in precision agriculture. The presented system combines classical machine learning models with quantum, inspired learning such that the model's predictability and generalization can be increased. Random Forest regressors are employed to estimate crop yield and economic profit, thus providing a stable and reliable baseline for the performance. Quantum Kernel Ridge Regression and a Variational Quantum Circuit have been added to this framework as corrective components to enhance the model's generalization. The proposed technique involves methodical data preprocessing: categorical attributes are encoded (soil type, season, crop name, and disease status), and numerical features are scaled (rainfall, nutrient level, fertilizer usage, and cultivated area). Instead of using image, based disease detection methods, disease condition is considered as a structured input feature and is merged with the yield and profit estimation process. The quantum-inspired module, which is implemented with Qiskit, employs Principal Component Analysis for reducing dimensions and utilizes a ZZFeatureMap to encode quantum features to construct quantum kernels. By providing an effective framework for data-driven agricultural decision support, the current system is deployed via an interactive Streamlit application for dataset uploading, model training and prediction, and visual analysis through correlation heatmaps and yield-profit trend plots. Experimental results have shown that a hybrid integration of classical machine learning and quantum- inspired methodology could enhance predictability, interpretability, and practical usability, thus offering this framework suitable for data-driven agricultural decision support.

title: A Novel Combined Control Structure for an Isolated Wind System with Reduced Turbine and Battery Sizing
authors: Mitali Mahapatra, Swagat Pati, Manoj Kumar Debnath
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents an isolated Wind Energy Conversion System (WECS) integrating a pitch angle-controlled turbine and a battery energy storage system (ESS) to minimize the required capacities of both the generation and storage units. In isolated WECS configurations, the pitch and ESS controllers regulate the same parameter which is the system frequency, often causing instability when operated independently. To address this issue, a priority-based coordinated control algorithm is proposed to determine the respective contributions of pitch and ESS controls in maintaining frequency stability. The system comprises a pitchcontrolled wind turbine coupled with a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG) supplying a linear RL load, with the ESS interfaced through a bidirectional converter. Dynamic modeling and simulation are performed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment, and the results confirm that the proposed control strategy effectively maintains system frequency stability under varying wind and load conditions.

title: CFD: A Browser-Based Tool for Real-Time Carbon Emission Awareness
authors: Shivam Agarwal, Shubh Arora, Tanvi Sharma, Imran Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Climate change is now a normal part of life, so it's important to keep track of and limit your carbon footprint. Every moment we spend in our environment generates carbon in different forms. We generate carbon emissions through activities such as breathing, moving, using electronic devices, and accessing the internet. This paper primarily focuses on the carbon emissions resulting from internet usage. This paper introduces the Carbon Footprint Detector (CFD), a Chrome extension for browsers that uses real-time data analytics to measure the carbon emissions caused by using the internet. The system uses the Chrome API to collect data on network transmissions, uses real-world conversion factors, and gives users session-based metrics like energy use (kWh), carbon emissions (grams <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>), and total environmental impact. The result of the experiment shows that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{20}-\mathbf{300} \text{gms}$</tex> of CO2 is produced by the use of the internet, depending upon the use. The CFD tool has a capability to capture data with 98.5% accuracy and track all the emissions of CO2 by the different activities done by the user, and your current session summary while online. The system was designed to increase people's awareness of environmental issues, which may encourage them to support sustainability policies.

title: Performance Enhancement of Average Current Control of Boost PFC Converter for Electric Vehicle Charging Applications
authors: Majhrul Israr, Sachin Chauhan, Biranchi Narayan Kar, Amiya Kumar Naik, Nitish Kumar Rajak, Paulson Samuel
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Rectifiers with power factor correction (PFC) are essential in AC/DC conversion systems to achieve a high power factor and low input current distortion. Among various topologies, the boost converter operating with average current control is widely adopted due to its simple structure and effective control characteristics. However, maintaining high power quality and fast output voltage regulation simultaneously remains challenging because the low-frequency ripple (LFR) in the sensed output voltage can distort the reference current, leading to increased harmonics in the input current. Conventional PI controller tuning using the Ziegler-Nichols (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}-\mathbf{N}$</tex>) method often results in suboptimal dynamic reference tracking performance. This paper presents a Bayesian Optimization (BO) based tuning method for the PI current controller to enhance the performance of the inner current loop in a boost PFC converter. The optimized controller parameters significantly improve reference current tracking, yielding better transient and steady-state performance. Simulation results obtained in the MATLAB/Simulink environment demonstrate that the proposed control strategy achieves a low Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), a near-unity power factor, and a well-regulated DC-link voltage, making it highly suitable for electric vehicle (EV) charging applications.

title: Detection of Fraudulant Transaction Over Credit Card Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Ekant Ratanlal Tekam, Afrin Sheikh, Satish D. Kale, Anant Kaulage, Yuvraj S. Suryavanshi, A. S. Sasipriya
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The tremendous use of credit card payment that affect to increase in credit card frauds and posing serious risks to financial institutions and customers. Traditional fraud detection methods often struggle with the highly imbalanced nature of transaction data, where fraudulent cases are rare. This paper proposed ensemble learning-based approach to enhance the fraud detection performance over the credit card transactions. The model combines multiple machine learning classifiers such as Random Forest, AdaBoost, K-Nearest Neighbors, and a Deep Neural Network. A publicly available credit card transaction dataset was used, and class imbalance was addressed using SMOTE. The proposed model obtained the accuracy score of 99.94% without SMOTE and improved recall of 87.75 % with SMOTE shows the effectiveness in identifying fraudulent transactions while minimizing false positives. The final outcome of proposed ensemble model shows that when balancing the fraudulent transactions dataset significantly improve the fraud detection performance in real-world financial systems.

title: Real-Time Cyber Threat Intelligence Detection and Response Using Deep Learning for Advanced Threat Classification
authors: Sriram Muganthan G, Srivignesh L, S. Rajashree, A. Jemshia Miriam, M. Nafees Muneera, Santhanakrishnan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper introduces a Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) that is a real-time system, which combines packet-level detection, flow detection using features, machine learning classification, and explainable AI. The system uses the scapy-based packet sniffing to extract the number of connections, rate of services, TCP flag interactions, and protocol metadata and is run against both flow-based analytics and a trained random forest classifier. An adversarial deep autoencoder enhances the process of identifying anomalies due to reconstruction errors, and LIME gives model explainability to security analysts. Streamlit and Flask-SocketIO are used to deliver real-time dashboards and facilitate them to be constantly monitored, dynamically visualized, and blocked on IP. Extra modules compute structured data sets to be used in offline training and model federation, simulating model updates which prevent deterioration of data privacy. It has the capability to detect high-frequency DOS, Probe, R2L, and U2R attacks, and offer transparency, flexibility, and scalability to the current cybersecurity landscape, with the combined architecture.

title: Intelligent Merging Unit Enabled Substation Automation: A Critical Survey and Laboratory Setup
authors: Gayatri Mohapatra, Prakash Chandra Sahu, Sarat Chandra Swain, Sunil Kumar Bhatta, Buddhadeva Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The research efforts are being directed toward the future deployment of the Centralized Protection and Monitoring (CPM) System in substations due to the growing need for adaptable protection and control algorithms in the field of substation automation. Intelligent Electronic Devices (IED) are currently widely used in digital substations to implement protective algorithms. This paper explores commercial substation automation projects and provides a thorough analysis of how industry leaders have integrated International Electro-technical Commission-61850 (IEC 61850) based CPM in substations. This investigates the IEC 61850 communication protocol's potential for the CPM in constructing contemporary substation automation. A CPM system with software-defined power analytics has great potential for the development of analytics-enabled substation automation, according to the inquiry. A CPM system with software-defined power analytics has great potential for the development of analytics-enabled substation automation, according to the inquiry. The key enablers for reaching the CPM include standardization initiatives in the development and implementation of virtual IED (vIED) software, configuration tools, performance benchmarking, and dynamic, agile compliance testing to ensure interoperability. This study explores these efforts, evaluates their advantages for substation operations, and explores possible future research directions while taking cybersecurity into account.

title: Comparative Analysis of Vanilla Reinforcement Learning vs Q-Learning in Smooth Path Planning Strategies for Mobile Robots
authors: Debaditya Majumdar, Soumya Ranjan Mahapatro, Sudhansu Kumar Samal, Basant Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In the real-world scenario, there is an intense need for path-planning robots for various needs. The most used technique to perform path finding is by Vanilla Reinforcement Learning. It is the most basic form of reinforcement learning where the reward system works in a way that the robot finds different paths to find the end point while it keeps on adding the rewards and, in the end, it chooses the path with most rewards. There is also a type of Reinforcement Learning known as Q- Learning. This works on the reward mechanism. As it moves it calculates each episode as a reward or not. This way, it finds the most optimal path to reach its destination. In our paper we are comparing Vanilla Reinforcement Learning and Q-Learning for path planning of a same-dimensional robot. This paper also keeps in mind that the path won't always be straight and there might be various obstructions to find the correct and shortest path. So, we have done a detailed comparative analysis of two algorithms that are Reinforcement Learning and Q-Learning. We have simulated and implemented a Q-Learning algorithm for a mobile robot in a dynamic environment with obstacles and compared it with a MATLAB simulation for Vanilla Reinforcement Learning to understand the differences in their performance in smooth path planning.

title: Hybrid Microgrid Stabilization Through Adaptive Virtual Inertia and Nonlinear Compensation-Based Control Architecture
authors: Sarthak Mohanty, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Pravat Kumar Rout, Manoj Kumar Debnath
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This manuscript proposes the design of an adaptive virtual inertia-based control scheme (AVICS) employing a nonlinear compensation and synchronization (NCS) method to enhances the voltage and frequency stability in distributed microgrid aspects. The planned control framework successfully compensates abnormal voltage fluctuation, dynamic oscillations, and nonlinear proliferation effects arising from the interface between converters and load variations. The incremental conductance-optimum power algorithm (IC_OPA) offers dynamic tuning of the boost converter modulation index to confirm optimum energy production at an optimal level under varying irradiance and temperature conditions. Allowing virtual inertia path control, additional voltage control has been considered to counteract the oscillatory deviations in the power–voltage response of converter units, which, in turn, offers the steady-state voltage and transient voltage performance. The NCS techniques tunes the voltage signal to compensate the nonlinearity propagation and remarkably reduce the THD at the same time. The developed model has been analyzed broadly in Simulink studies, beginning that the system has better transient performance, quicker synchronization, and harmonics mitigation than conventional droop-based control techniques. Overall, the suggested control paradigm confirmations great promise for enhancing power quality (PQ) and operational reliability as well as energy stability in future microgrids.

title: Leveraging Transfer Learning for Efficient Plant Pathology on Mobile Devices
authors: Samruddhi Sathawane, Shreya Mate, Nikhil Nandanwar, Pranav Gambhire, Sugat Lokhande, Kiran Gavhale
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: World-wide, agriculture plays a significant role in the economy and is the major source of food security. Yet one of the main problems the agriculture sector has to deal with is plant diseases. Consequently, there will be a drop in crop yields and the question of the sustainability of farming will arise. Farmers depending on digital agriculture to minimize losses will have to rely on early disease recognition to take the right measures, make smart decisions, and thus minimize losses. Therefore, the researchers develop a rapid deep learning-based system that is able to pinpoint the plant diseases only by looking at the pictures of leaves. Initially, the researchers employed a binary classification system to differentiate between healthy and unhealthy leaves. In the course of their study, the researchers compared two deep learning models when they used the models of ResNet50 and MobileNetV2. ResNet50 triumphed over MobileNetV2 with an accuracy of 97.08% as opposed to 82.04% for the latter. ResNet50 also showed results of 92.62% and 74%, respectively, in the multiclass disease classification test, indicating that more layers indeed lead to better outcomes. Moreover, a custom CNN model was developed that was able to spot multiple diseases in different plants and crops with a rather impressive accuracy rate of 83.39%, considering the difficulty of the dataset. The researchers decided to create a mobile app that was going to deliver immediate binary predictions on the basis of leaf photos so as to make it ware easier for daily use. All in all, the system proposed is a rapid, precise, and widely accessible remedy that can support the use of intelligent, efficient, and environmentally friendly farming techniques perfectly.

title: Design of Self-Charging Fibrous Power System by Integrating Tribovoltaic Nanogenerator and Supercapacitor
authors: Sikai Zhang, Yunfang Jia
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: To address the pressing demand for flexible selfcharging power sources in wearable electronics, this work proposes a fiber-shaped self-charging power system achieved by monolithically integrating a tribovoltaic nanogenerator (TVNG) with a fiber-shaped supercapacitor (FSSC). First, a rigorous theoretical framework is established for this system, applying a quantum mechanical model of the “Bindington” excitation mechanism in the tribovoltaic effect, and analyzing the charge transport mechanism and thermodynamic energy conversion pathways. Subsequently, the theoretical model is validated through coupled multi-physics finite element simulations. The simulation results provide quantitative performance benchmarks, demonstrating that the TVNG can generate stable direct current (DC) output and achieve rectifier-free self-charging of the system. A key advantage of this system is that the DC power converted by the TVNG can be directly stored in the FSSC, eliminating the need for rigid rectifier circuits typically required in conventional self-charging systems. This work provides a validated theoretical and numerical foundation for developing wearable, washable, and biocompatible self-powered textiles, with potential applications in health monitoring, smart fabrics, and the Internet of Things.

title: Decentralized Cloud Storage Systems Using Blockchain Technology
authors: Shafique U Rehman Sahito, Sijjad Ali, Shuaib A Wadho, Uzair Aslam Bhatti, Muhammad Waqas Nadeem, Mehran Sayed
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The growing reliance on cloud storage has brought into focus critical concerns related to centralization, data privacy, security, and reliability. Traditional cloud storage systems are often vulnerable to single point failure, data breaches, and high operational costs. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a decentralized cloud storage system that takes advantage of blockchain technology. Blockchain, with its inherent properties of immutability, transparency, and distributed consensus, ensures secure and reliable storage without the need for a central authority. The proposed system incorporates a blockchain-based architecture in which data is encrypted, fragmented, and distributed across multiple nodes in a peer-to-peer network. Smart contracts are used to automate processes such as verification of data integrity, access control, and user authentication, thereby improving operational efficiency and reducing manual intervention. Key features include cryptographic techniques for data privacy, fault tolerance redundancy mechanisms, and a tokenized economy to encourage participation within the network. Experimental results demonstrate the robustness of the system in terms of scalability, data retrieval latency, and cost effectiveness compared to traditional centralized models. The results validate the efficacy of blockchain in addressing trust and transparency issues while ensuring the confidentiality and availability of stored data. This research highlights the potential of decentralized cloud storage systems to revolutionize data management, offering a secure, scalable, and economically viable alternative to traditional models. Future work will explore advanced consensus mechanisms and strategies to further improve system scalability and performance.

title: Vastu Generative Framework (VGF): A Hybrid AI Framework for Vastu-Compliant Architectural Layout Generation
authors: Amit Kumar, Rakshit Malik, Madhumita Mahapatra, Seema Verma, Pridhi Arora
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Architectural design requires knowledge and expertise in space planning, technical and cultural standards. Existing tools are either used to analyze designs that already exists, or to produce layouts without cultural context. This creates a disconnected workflow requiring iterative manual corrections. This paper introduces methodology Vastu Generative Framework (VGF) which is a hybrid Artificial Intelligence (AI) architecture that generates the Vastu compliant architectural plan directly from Natural Language input. The framework includes large language models (LLMs) on requirements extraction, a new Vastu Potential Map on how to convert the expression into continuous suitability landscapes, and a rule-based optimiser of Simulated Annealing to optimise both architectural and Vastu Shastra requirements constraints. Experiments with rectangular, L-shaped and triangular plots have proven that the maximum VGF complies between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85-94 \%$</tex> with zero geometric overlaps where methods based on the baseline yield only between 15 45% compliance. The system produces production ready vector layouts at practical solve times ranging between 30 seconds to 90 seconds with a residential configuration. This means combining traditional's architectural wisdom with this modern, generative artificial intelligence technology; this transformative new workforce means that culturally aware design has become a reality for the user without needing technical expertise from this process or repeated iteration

title: Ensemble Regression Approach to Predict Students' Performance Using Pearson Correlation Feature Selection
authors: Ayesha Ashraf, Mazhar Ali, Shumaim Liaquat, Faiz Nadeem, Qasim Arain
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The academic performance of students is influenced by multiple demographic, personal, and socioeconomic factors. Predicting student performance is a significant challenge for educators seeking to enhance learning outcomes. This study proposes an ensemble regression approach integrated with Pearson correlationbased feature selection to predict students' exam performance more accurately. The dataset was obtained from Kaggle (“Students' Performance in Exams”), and after preprocessing and one-hot encoding, key features such as gender, parental education, and test preparation courses were selected based on Pearson correlation. Multiple regression models, including Linear Regression, Support Vector Regression (SVR), Decision Tree Regression (DTR), K-Nearest Neighbor Regression (KNNR), and Multilayer Perceptron Regression (MLPR), were trained and compared using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> score. The best-performing model was MLP Regression (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.08, \text{MSE}=0.013, \mathrm{R}^{2}=0.999$</tex>). The proposed ensemble voting regression model (MLP + LR) further improved accuracy (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.027$</tex>, MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.001, \mathrm{R}^{2}=0.99$</tex>). This demonstrates that combining linear and nonlinear regressors enhances both prediction accuracy and interpretability for predicting student performance.

title: Design and Fabrication of an Optical Pancreatic Phantom for Fibrosis Assessment
authors: Nithiyaa Dharshini M, Subitcha Jayasankar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Identifying pancreatic fibrosis is challenging as radiographic imaging methods often miss early-stage anatomical and biological changes. Optical spectroscopy techniques, such as Diffuse Reflectance Spectroscopy (DRS), are used to detect cellular-level alterations in early fibrosis; however, they require calibration and standardization to reliably identify fibrosis at an early stage. To calibrate, various phantoms that simulate tissue optical properties exist, but the multilayered structure and inhomogeneity of the pancreas are not accurately represented. To address this, a three-dimensional hydrogel-based phantom that replicates the structural and optical properties of the pancreas is created using a 3D-printed mold that resembles the pancreas. The optical properties are controlled by adding specific amounts of scatterers and absorbers. The optical properties of this multilayer phantom were assessed using an inverse Monte Carlo light transport model with DRS measurements. The absorption and reduced scattering coefficient of the fabricated phantom were within the desired optical property limits of the pancreas, making it an adequate calibration standard for fibrosis detection using DRS.

title: A Review on AI-Based Cyber Threat Detection in Internet of Medical Things (Io-MT) Networks
authors: Khushboo Kumari, Ranjan Kumar, Md. Atif, Himanshu Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This paper classifies Al-driven detection methods. compares databases. and determines important areas of research towards protecting lo MT networks. Artificial Intelligence (AI). and more specifically Machine Learning (ML). has come to the fore as a strong solution to improve lo MT security. Al models. by observing traffic patterns, are able to identify anomalies and malicious traffic more accurately and dynamically. This review paper discusses an in-depth overview of the existing threat environment in lo MT networks and investigates diverse Al-based methods for threat detection. It also discusses popular datasets, tools, and frameworks used in the literature. The literature review spans from 2019 to 2025. including both base studies and recent developments. The paper also points out current challenges such as data privacy, memory scarcity, and the demand for real-time detection. Future research needs to concentrate on building explainable, privacy-preserving Al models and standardizing realworld lo MT datasets for real-world deployment.

title: Research on Control of an Electro-Hydraulic Active Suspension System
authors: Hongyu Ye, Lijun Zhang, Dejian Meng
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: This paper conducts modeling and control studies on an active suspension system. The modeling approach involves formulating individual components of the active suspension system, including vehicle model, actuator model, two types of road profile models, lower-level controller design, and three distinct upperlevel controller designs. The results indicate that the performance metrics of the active suspension system equipped with the three upper-layer control algorithms are optimized under both road conditions compared with the passive suspension system.

title: An Intelligent Type-2 Fuzzy Logic Framework for High-Accuracy Power Quality Disturbance Classification
authors: Rahul, Fatima Ansari, Manoj Kumar Mukul, Yogesh Pratap, M. Gangadharappa, Pooja Devi
