publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Application of power quality disturbance identification and classification is a basic requirement for reliable power system functioning. This work proposes an intelligent method that combines the Curvelet Transform with Type-2 Fuzzy Logic for improved classification results. The Curvelet Transform has superior time-frequency analysis capabilities due to its ability to efficiently extract characteristic disturbance patterns. Type-2 Fuzzy Logic handles multicriteria decision-making in uncertain situations. The proposed method is tested on real-world data sets that include harmonics, flicker, transients, voltage sags, and voltage swells. The experimental results show that the proposed framework has a classification accuracy of 99.7%, which is higher than many existing methods, including Eigen-based Type-2 Fuzzy Logic (99.08%), FPGAbased online detection <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(98.57 \%)$</tex>, Linear Discriminant Analysis (98%), and deep learning with 2D wavelet scalograms (97.67%). The accuracy is also comparable to the IAST methodology with Random Forest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.56 \%)$</tex>. The accuracy and low computational complexity of the proposed framework emphasize the efficiency and applicability of the Curvelet Transform-Type-2 Fuzzy Logic framework for automated power quality disturbance classification.

title: Federated Intrusion Detection for VANETs under Byzantine Model Poisoning Attacks
authors: Shivanshi Mishra, Shruti Singh, Kanchan Sharma, Brijesh Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has drawn interest because of its decentralised and privacy-preserving features for intrusion detection in Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs). However, these systems are prone to Byzantine model poisoning attacks, in which hacked vehicles alter local updates to hinder global learning. The impact of gradient-scaling model poisoning on federated VANET intrusion detection under realistic non-IID data distribution and partial client participation is examined in this work. Several aggregation strategies, including Federated Averaging, Median, Trimmed Mean, and Krum, are evaluated for robustness against such attacks. The impact of false-negative cars on downstream routing is also measured by analyzing nodelevel network reliability indicators in addition to packet-level detection performance. The findings show that robust aggregation, especially Krum, significantly improves network stability and improves malicious detection.

title: A Drone Shape Substrate Integrated Waveguide Backed Self-Quardruplexing Antenna for C-Band Applications
authors: Ravi Bhushan Roy, Prashant Kumar Jain, Prabhat Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: In this paper a Drown shape simple SIW (Substrate Integrated Wave-guide) antenna with backed antenna with self-quadruplexing structure for four-band operation application is presented. Copper-filled cylindrical vias are placed through the substrate between the top and bottom cavity layers, forming a single printed circuit board. The SIW cavity is excited through four distinct patches having different lengths, and each one radiates at a unique resonant frequency. These patches are formed on the upper surface of the cavity. The antenna design follows a simple three-step process aimed at improving the self-quadruplexing feature. In the initial stage, a rectangular slot along with two ports is created Secondly, the same slot is redesigned into a drone-shaped structure incorporating four patches. In the third step, all four ports are created and radiates at four different resonant frequencies at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.23 \text{GHz}, 6.42 \text{GHz}, 7.41 \text{GHz}$</tex> & 8.48 GHz, with gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.2,5.2,6.4$</tex>, and 6.5 dBi respectively. The proposed structure has been simulated using ANSYS HFSS on RT-Duroid (5870) Substrate.

title: Intelligent and Optimized Fraud Detection Framework for UPI Transactions
authors: Akash Raghuvanshi, Satyam Singh, Pawan Kumar Singh, Manish Kumar, Rahul Singh, Gunjan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The Digital Payment sector witnessed a transformation in the last couple of years, in part due to the increase in the use of UPI Transactions in India. However, this also led to an increase in vulnerable, sophisticated, and complicated fraud schemes. In this paper, we propose an integration of Seagull Optimization Algorithm (SOA) and LightGBM, which can achieve desirable results in terms of accurately forecasting fraudulent UPI Transactions. In our method, we capture the fraud indicators using detailed feature engineering, which includes temporal patterns, transaction behavioral analytics, and transaction anomalies. The SOA metaheuristic algorithm optimizes the hyperparameters of LightGBM and outperforms traditional optimization methods in accuracy and performance. We demonstrate the model results using an experimental dataset of 100,000 transactions, achieving 98.08 % cross-validated AUC, 98.02 % test AUC, and 94.95% accuracy at the optimal decision threshold of 0.30. The model achieves high recall of 99.98 % while maintaining precision of 80 %, effectively balancing false positives and false negatives. We also showcase the importance of critical fraud detection indicators: Device_ID, Customer_ID, IP_Address, and Merchant_ID, demonstrating this research's contribution in creating an effective, real-time, and reliable fraud detection model for UPI Transactions in Digital Payment Systems.

title: Energy-Aware Hybrid Localization in IoT and WSNs Using Multilateration and Grey Wolf Optimization
authors: Jyoti Sharma, Ashish Payal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: For the seamless functioning of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs), precise and energy-efficient localization is of utmost importance. Existing range-based localization approaches are able to ensure high accuracy but suffer from high energy incurred due to computational part, while existing range-free localization approaches provide low computational cost but suffer from low precision issues. This paper addresses these limitations and presents a novel hybrid approach to localization that incorporates rangefree approach, i.e. weighted centroid estimation, rangebased approach, i.e. Multilateration, and a bio-inspired meta-heuristic optimization approach, i.e. Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) to improve precision while keeping the computations as well as energy expenditure at a minimum. Multilateration is used as an initial estimates and tunable fusion parameter is used to balance weightedcentroid and Multilateration, final position estimates are then refined using GWO method. This work uses a simplified energy model for both computation and communication costs. Proposed hybrid model is evaluated against a baseline Weighted Grey Wolf Optimization (WGWO) algorithm, focusing on Root Mean Square localization error and energy efficiency that encompasses both computation and communication costs. The simulation results show that the proposed hybrid model achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \%}$</tex> lower localization RMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 - 1 0 \%}$</tex> lower total energy consumption in comparison of the WGWO baseline. Moreover, CDF analysis also confirms the hybrid model's enhanced dependability and accuracy. This research provides a substantial promise toward optimizing algorithms for localization within large-scale IoT and WSN systems, strategically manoeuvring the constraints of accuracy and energy consumption. These findings demonstrate the potential of the proposed approach for real-time localization in resource-constrained IoT and WSN systems.

title: HRNG and Hard Haptic Response Based Personalized PIN System Immune to Shoulder Surfing
authors: N. Nanthini, Sanjitha V, Shreenithhi N., Sreeni S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: PIN-based authentication is widely used in ATMs, banking, IoT, and secure devices,but is vulnerable to shoulder surfing and observation attacks. This study proposes a novel authentication system integrating a Hardware Random Number Generator (HRNG) and Hard Haptic Response to create a dynamic, personalized, and secure PIN entry method. The HRNG generates unpredictable keypad layouts for every login, while haptic feedback provides tactile confirmation, allowing users to enter PINs without visual dependence. The system ensures immunity against shoulder surfing and screenrecording attacks, while maintaining user convenience and scalability. Experimental results demonstrate improved resistance to observation-based threats and positive user experience, making the solution cost- effective and adaptable for real deployment.

title: AI-Driven Personal Finance Systems: Methods, Limitations, and Open Challenges
authors: Ujjwal Jain, Vasu Pandit, Reyansh Kaushik, Shivam Kumar, Kavya Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This review analyzes information learning Across a set of 20 studies that reported on personal finance systems in 2023 and 2024. We investigate the role of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Deep Learning (DL), Machine Learning (ML), Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) in facilitating primary financial operations such as predicting budgets and categorizing expenditures. We developed a consolidated ML pipeline from the studies to specify at least a minimum set of activities in the integration of data, feature selection, modeling, evaluation, and iterative modeling through feedback. The study also examined the important obstacles to implementing the results in practice, such as data privacy, lack of generalization to a diverse population, limited interpretability of complex models, and the lack of stability of adaptable systems in real time. Finally, this paper consolidates these trends and limitations to establish a research agenda prioritizing transparent, adaptive, privacy preserving, and user aligned financial AI systems.

title: Optimization of EKF Noise Covariance Matrix and PMSM Parameter Identification Based on Firefly Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yu Guan, Xiaodong Kang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: When the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is used for parameter identification of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM), the process noise covariance matrix <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex> and measurement noise covariance matrix <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> directly affect identification accuracy. The Firefly Algorithm (FA), an efficient swarm intelligence optimization method, has strong optimization robustness and insensitivity to initial values, which can solve the optimal selection problem of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex>. This paper focuses on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F A^{\prime}$</tex> s core design logic, process model, and the fitness function adapted to EKF parameter identification, providing concise and effective theoretical support for algorithm implementation.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Blockchain-Fog Vs Cloud-Based Models for Real-Time Health Monitoring
authors: Ashish Kumar, Aanchal Vij, Ashish Jain, Jitendra Singh, Mekhala, Kamini
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this study, we conducted a real-time evaluation of two healthcare monitoring architectures, one cloud-based and the other integrating fog and blockchain technologies. The cloud system is typically based on centralized processing servers, whereas the proposed architecture combines the fog devices and a permissioned blockchain to achieve secure real-time data handling at the network edge. Both models were implemented using NodeMCU microcontrollers and MAX30100 sensors for real-time physiological data recording, including heart rate and SpO<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. In fog-blockchain deployment, smart contracts are used for controlling data access, and 'off-chain storage' and it is supported by the Inter-Planetary File System (IPFS), to reduce blockchain overhead. To evaluate both setups, we measured three performance metrics such as latency, transaction throughput, and average CPU load

title: Beyond Convolutions: a Performance-Efficiency Analysis of Vision Transformers for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Ayush Shukla, Anubhav Kesarwani, Amol Dubey, Aniket Tiwari, Shyam Bahadur Verma, Vijay Kumar Dwivedi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Deep learning has opened promising avenues for automated skin lesion recognition and early cancer screening. Nevertheless, many top-performing architectures impose heavy computational requirements that limit their use in everyday clinical practice, especially on portable and edge-computing platforms. We tackle this deployment bottleneck through a structured comparative evaluation of modern, resource-aware deep learning designs. Our benchmark spans well-known lightweight CNNs such as MobileNetV2 alongside heavier baselines like ResNet50, and pits them against a recent compact Vision Transformer variant. Experiments on the publicly available HAM10000 collection show that TinyViT-5M, a small-footprint Vision Transformer containing just 5.0 million learnable parameters, reaches a Macro F1 of 0.83, surpassing every other model tested. Importantly, it achieves this while consuming a fraction of the resources demanded by larger networks, evidencing a highly favorable accuracy-to-cost ratio. These findings strongly support compact Transformer designs as a viable path toward building the next wave of precise, lightweight, and broadly deployable computeraided dermatological screening tools.

title: A Metadata Driven Approach to Quantify and Overcome Data Fragmentation in Crop Models
authors: Ashutosh Singh, Ayush Verma, Bhavesh Gautam, Chhavi Pachauri, Akhilesh Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Data such as climate, soil, irrigation, and remotesensing are scattered across multiple portals and diversified formats, thus leading to poor generalisation in robust agricultural machine learning models in India. Without a unified data discovery mechanism or platform, it becomes difficult to reproduce models and reduces their reliability. As a solution to this issue, we proposed a system, namely AgriSangam, a collaborative metadata-driven platform designed to organise agricultural datasets. In contrast to other repositories, our platform does not automatically ingest data, which enables the researcher to investigate the suitability of the data before it is integrated. To explain the platform need, a validation of such integration, we performed a controlled evaluation comparing identical XGBoost models trained on isolated single-domain sources (e.g., climateonly) with crop as a baseline against those trained on integrated datasets. We have implemented the exact protocols to prevent the spatiotemporal leakage, employing Out-of-Time (OOT) forecasting on 2016-2017 and a 5-fold Spatial Cross-Validation (CV) on held-out districts. Single-domain trained models had moderate predictive power (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex>-squared values between 0.66 and 0.69) that was primarily due to crop-type differences instead of generalisation. Conversely, the integrated models showed clear improvement in predictive accuracy with (OOT R-squared value is 0.744 and MAE is 0.442). Furthermore, spatial cross-validation demonstrated robust generalisation to unseen regions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex>-squared of 0.789 with standard deviation of 0.026). Lastly, by applying SHAP-based analysis, we found that a smaller set of 49 features could retain the predictive power of (OOT R-squared of 0.747). The major problem identified from these findings states that data fragmentation is the primary bottleneck in agricultural AI. Thus validating the necessity of metadata-driven platforms like AgriSangam to facilitate systematic, multi-modal integration for real-world research.

title: Analyzing Bitcoin's Intraday Variance Using LSTM, GARCH and Hybrid Model
authors: K. Vanitha, Adarsh S, Ayyanar M, Abinash B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies have become a hip financial instrument that is extremely volatile with disconnected trading. The current paper investigates the intraday volatility of Bitcoin based on econometric and datadriven models to capture short-term changes in volatility. The paper introduces a graphical method of data analytics in the form of the heatmap that is used to identify clusters of volatility and time dynamic in the trading day. Using GARCHtype volatility models and advanced machine learning techniques with LSTM-GARCH hybrids, the system estimates and display changing market behavior based on 1-minute and 5-minute tick data from Digital Currencies 2024. The results confirm the existence of a great deal of intraday heterogeneity, which is provided by the convergence of the global market and the investing spirit, and which provides guidance to traders, analysts, and policymakers in forecasting risks and microstructure market behavior. Performance measures found that the hybrid framework had the lowest MAE (0.025), RMSE (0.053), and MSE (0.002809), and the highest R2 of 0.91.

title: Optimized Fault Location in STATCOM-Integrated Transmission Networks Using Binary Quantum Walrus Optimizer (BQWO)
authors: Tejal Sanjay Barhate, S. A. Naveed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Accurate fault location in STATCOM-integrated transmission networks remains a critical challenge due to nonlinear interactions between converter-based compensation and transient system dynamics. The present paper proposes a new Binary Quantum Walrus Optimizer (BQWO), developed to estimate optimal fault locations in such complex environments. The approach formulates fault distance and resistance estimation as a nonlinear optimization problem, which minimizes the differences between voltage measurements of two terminals. The quantum binary encoding improves global exploration, whereas walrus-inspired cooperative foraging achieves faster and more stable convergence. The optimizer is combined with Kernel Regression Residual and Improved Intrinsic Time-Scale Decomposition (K2RS2ITSD) preprocessing to remove distortions that STATCOM introduces in the voltage and current signals. The proposed approach is validated on an IEEE 9-bus system in MATLAB/Simulink under multiple fault types, locations, and resistances. The findings indicate that BQWO achieves fault location errors below 1 %, convergence time under 150 ms, and higher robustness compared with PSO and GA algorithms. The method provides an effective and scalable framework for highaccuracy fault localization in modern FACTS-compensated transmission systems.

title: CrimeSketch AI: Text-To-Image Synthesis for Criminal Identification Using Deep Learning
authors: Amrutha Sivakumar, Shreya Bhanot, Srinidhi M, Jyotsna C, Santhanalakshmi S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Facial identification based on verbal eyewitness descriptions remains a significant challenge in contemporary forensics, particularly in the absence of visual evidence. Traditional methods such as CNN-based models and Haar cascade classifiers struggle with occlusion, poor lighting, and low-resolution inputs, while artist-drawn sketches are impractical for scalable automation. To overcome these limitations, we introduce CrimeSketch AI, a deep learning pipeline for generating realistic facial images from natural language descriptions. Our system combines Stable Diffusion fine-tuned by LoRA for text-to-sketch generation, Canny edge detection with ControlNet or CycleGAN for sketch-to-image translation, and StyleGAN2-ADA with CLIP for text-to-face synthesis. Identity matching is done using InsightFace with ArcFace embeddings to assure the accurate recognition. The model was tested on publicly released datasets, such as CelebA and a cleaned criminal mugshot dataset. CrimeSketch AI presents a scalable, privacy-preserving method for AI-powered suspect identification, augmenting the capability of law enforcers based on text-based descriptions alone.

title: Risks Associated with Cloud Computing and Data Security: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Sarasij Majumdar, Shivanath Ghosh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Cloud computing along with its foundational technology, modern information systems, facilitates dynamic access to shared computing infrastructure, on-demand computing, storage, and flexible delivery of services. Even though it is a widespread technology, cloud computing also carries many securities and substantively privacy challenges arising from its virtualization technologies, distributed architecture and multi-tenant environments. Among the foregoing challenges, data security reigns paramount over all, particularly with sensitive data of privacy at masses at disposal to the public and in hybrid cloud. The paper surveys and analyses security risks in cloud computing, particularly focusing on vulnerabilities to data security. As important risk factors, it takes note of virtualization exposure, environment exposure in public clouds, lack of robust authorization and access control, vendor lock-in issues, and risks associated with multi-tenancy and shared resource allocation. Security threats can be classified based on the various states of data, but this study gives the highest stress on vulnerabilities pertaining to data at rest and in transit-where threats such as unauthorized access to composite data, data leakage, interception, and compromise are manifested. Cryptographic defence mechanisms working in risk mitigation, e.g., symmetric key encryption: block ciphers and stream ciphers, and their existing plethora of drawbacks, adequacies, and effectuality towards either performance, scalability, and security requirements in cloud environments only further the contention. With such a background review and a juxtaposition of ongoing research and security practices, it is organized to present current challenges and actions to resolve issues concerning cloud data security, for bettering the design of secure and trustworthy cloud computing-systems, hopefully valuable to researchers, cloud service providers, and implementers.

title: Energy-efficient Silicon Nitride Optical Switching
authors: Yusen Huang, Jiaojiao Shi, Linye He, Xiaoyan Zhang, Perry Ping Shum, Feng Gao
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: The rapid scaling of artificial intelligence data centers driving an urgent demand for energy-efficient and reconfigurable optical switching technologies to overcome the bandwidth, latency, and power limitations of electronic interconnects. Silicon nitride (SiN) photonics offers ultra-low loss and CMOS compatibility but suffers from an intrinsically weak thermo-optic coefficient, leading to prohibitively high-power consumption in large-scale optical switch fabrics. To solve this, we propose a heterogeneous embedded-silicon (HES) design, where a thin silicon waveguide is integrated inside a low-loss SiN waveguide. This approach leverages the strong thermo-optic response of Si while preserving the excellent optical performance of SiN. Our optical switch based on a Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI) demonstrates a high peak extinction ratio of 44.7 dB at an ultralow electrical power of only 1.31 mW. This represents an 85% reduction in power consumption compared to conventional SiN optical switches with suspended design, achieving a near tenfold enhancement in modulation efficiency over state-of-the-art suspended SiN phase shifters. What's more, the entire structure is fabricated using a standard CMOS compatible process without exotic materials or post-processing, ensuring scalability and manufacturability. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first demonstration of a SiN optical switch with a sub-2-mW power consumption, establishing a new benchmark for energy-efficient photonic switching. Our approach directly addresses the power-efficiency bottleneck in large-scale optical interconnects and provides a practical pathway toward terabit-scale, low-energy photonic networks for next-generation AI accelerators.

                    MFM Capacitors With In Situ CF

                    Plasma at TiN Electrode
authors: Dong-Ru Hsieh, Yi-Hsiu Chen, Li-Quan Yang, Chia-Chen Wan, Li-Ting Chou, Yi-Chen Chiang, Kuan-Lin Chen, Shang-Lin Hsieh, Yi-Sin Rao, Chun-Jung Su, Tien-Sheng Chao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this study, HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO) metal–ferroelectric–metal (MFM) capacitors with in situ CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasma treatment at the bottom HZO/TiN interface were demonstrated and exhibited a sufficiently large two remnant polarization (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$26~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and significantly reduced <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> loss (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Delta 2{P}_{\text {r}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}, \text {pristine}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> from 52% down to 4% after the endurance test up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{{9}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cycles at 2 MV/cm since the metal oxide (TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> and TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>N<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {y}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at the TiN bottom electrode (BE) surface could be reduced by in situ CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasma treatment, and then, the formation of thin TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub> layer at the bottom HZO/TiN interface might be effectively suppressed by using a F-rich TiN BE during the thermal processes, therefore reducing the amount of oxygen vacancies (V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {o}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and repressing the formation of V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sub>-rich HZO. This is beneficial to restrain the generation of new V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sub> and then reduce the dipole pinning effect during field cycling, thereby enhancing the fatigue effect immunity. Using a CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasma-treated TiN BE with a rougher TiN surface to upgrade the charge density, the capacitance value is around doubled from 32 up to 61fF/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. From the ferroelectricity, charge storage capability, and reliability viewpoints, this approach is a promising candidate for embedded dynamic random access memories (eDRAMs).

title: Feature Engineering with Long Short-Term Memory Approach for Malicious Risk Identification in Mobile P2P
authors: Sri Sai Krishna Mukkamala
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Malicious actions against mobile peer-to-peer payment systems are increasingly becoming a threat and need to be countered by powerful detection systems. This study presents a feature engineering based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) malicious risk identification with the Risk Activities Dataset 2024 (RBD24). Min-Max Normalization is used to do data preprocessing to address value scaling. Statistical-Behavioural Feature Extraction is used to extract network and user-centric activity patterns and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is used to extract features to eliminate redundancy and enhance the efficiency of the model. An LSTM based sequential learning model is used to perform the final classification and is effective to capture the temporal dependence in traffic of the P2P network. Experimental results show an accuracy of 98.7 %, precision of 97.6 %, recall of 97.8 %, and F1-score of 97.8%, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed feature-engineered LSTM framework. This paper shows the usefulness of integrating engineered features with deep temporal models in the security of mobile Peer-to-Peer (P2P) systems.

title: Swarm Robotics for Precision Agriculture: Overcoming Coordination Scalability Limits
authors: Loso Judijanto, Sri Nurhayati
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: The rapid evolution of precision agriculture demands innovative approaches to enhance productivity, efficiency, and environmental sustainability. Among these, swarm robotics emerges as a promising paradigm, offering decentralized coordination, fault tolerance, and adaptability for large-scale farming operations. However, significant coordination and scalability limitations continue to hinder its real-world deployment, particularly in heterogeneous and dynamic agricultural environments. This study aims to systematically explore existing strategies to overcome coordination and scalability challenges in swarm robotics for precision agriculture. A Systematic Literature Review (SLR) was conducted using the PRISMA protocol, focusing on peerreviewed articles published between 2021 and 2025. The research employed a qualitative meta-synthesis approach to extract themes, models, and performance outcomes across multiple studies. Data were sourced exclusively from the ScienceDirect database, covering peer-reviewed articles published between 2021 and 2025. The search process involved keyword combinations such as “swarm robotics,” “coordination,” “scalability,” and “precision agriculture.” After screening and eligibility checks, 36 articles were selected based on open-access availability and methodological clarity. Thematic analysis was applied to extract coordination strategies, simulation validations, energy management approaches, and integration patterns. Findings indicate that hierarchical coordination models, biologically inspired algorithms (e.g., ACO, PSO), and hybrid communication protocols are the most effective in addressing scalability.

title: Tracking the Dieback of Palm and Olive Groves in The Skoura Oase (Morocco): A Spatio-Temporal Approach Using Remote Sensing and Machine Learning
authors: Youssef Lassiane, Farid El Wahidi, Hoda Benazun, Hassan Ait Naceur
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Desertification remains one of the most pressing environmental challenges in arid and semi-arid regions, undermining ecosystem resilience and agricultural productivity. In southern Morocco, oasis landscapes constitute highly fragile socio-ecological systems where vegetation dieback has intensified under the combined influence of climatic stress and human pressure. This study explores the spatio-temporal dynamics of palm and olive tree dieback in the Skoura Oasis (Morocco) from 2015 to 2025, using multi-temporal Sentinel-2 imagery processed on the Google Earth Engine (GEE) platform. A Random Forest algorithm was applied to tasseled cap transformation (TCT) components-Brightness, Greenness, and Wetness-to extract ecological indicators related to vegetation vigor and surface moisture. The model achieved an overall accuracy exceeding 89%, confirming its robustness in discriminating vegetation health conditions. Results indicate a marked acceleration in palm and olive dieback during the last decade, with the southern sections of the palmeraie exhibiting the most severe losses. In the central oasis, a persistent and continuous dieback pattern was also detected. These findings reveal the alarming pace of ecosystem deterioration across the Draa oases, reflecting the second agro-ecological crisis driven by progressive aridification and groundwater depletion. This research demonstrates the high potential of integrating Sentinel-based remote sensing with machine learning for long-term ecological monitoring, providing critical insights for sustainable oasis management and the preservation of traditional agroforestry systems in dryland environments.

title:
Ultraviolet Aging Effects on the Dielectric Properties of PVC/Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Nanocomposites Cables Insulation
authors: Sabrina Amraoui, Abdallah Hedir, Ferhat Slimani, Mustapha Moudoud, Omar Lamrous, Ali Durmus
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is extensively employed as an electrical insulating material in high-voltage cable applications, offering reliable dielectric performance and mechanical durability. due to its excellent mechanical and electrical properties. However, under operational conditions, it is exposed to aging factors, particularly UV-radiation, which is one of the most aggressive stressors affecting its stability. The degradation induced by UV exposure leads to structural modifications, loss of mechanical properties, and alterations in dielectric performance. To enhance PVC durability, nanoparticles are commonly incorporated into the polymer matrix to mitigate aging effects. This study investigates the effects of UV aging on the dielectric properties of PVC/Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> nanocomposites. Samples were prepared with varying aluminum oxide (Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) concentrations (0, 2.5 and 5.0 wt.%) and subjected to accelerated UV aging tests. The study involved evaluating dielectric permittivity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex>'), dielectric loss factor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tan \delta)$</tex>, and dielectric loss index (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex> “) as a functions of frequency and exposure time. Obtained results shows that adding Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> contributes to the improvement of the dielectric performance of PVC while also acting as a protective agent against UV radiation.

title: National Data Centres and Sovereign AI Infrastructure: Designing Scalable Platforms for Jordan and Beyond
authors: Ahmad Al-Qerem
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The adoption of large-scale artificial intelligence (AI) models has tightened the link between digital sovereignty and access to high-performance computing infrastructure. For emerging economies like the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan this poses both opportunity and risk. On the one hand, AI promises to transform sectors ranging from fintech and e-commerce to health and logistics, and Jordanian startups are already deploying chatbots, fraud detection and personalised recommendation systems to improve efficiency and customer experience. On the other hand, reliance on foreign hyperscale clouds can undermine data sovereignty, export capital and constrain national innovation. This paper delivers a data-grounded blueprint for a national data centre (NDC) and sovereign AI platform tailored to Jordan's economic and geopolitical context. Our design integrates detailed local metrics-such as the country's eight existing data centres (seven in Amman and one in Aqaba), electricity tariffs for large industry (0.17 USD <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\cdot \text{kWh}^{1}$</tex>), renewable energy contributing roughly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27-30 \%$</tex> of generation, the presence of 20 universities offering AI programmes to about 3000 students, and Jordan's commitment to train 15000 people and launch 25 government AI projects by 2027. We present a modular, pod-based architecture with secure sovereign enclaves, high-density AI pods and support for regional service delivery. A total cost of ownership (TCO) model grounded in Jordan-specific data shows that a 50 -rack phase-one facility would require roughly $ 25.7 M in capital expenditure and $ 3.5 M in annual operating expenditure, yielding a five-year TCO around 43.4 M; energy costs dominate long-term OPEX. We examine sensitivity to power-usage-effectiveness (PUE), electricity tariffs and GPU pricing. Finally, we map governance recommendations to existing national bodies defined in the Jordanian AI Strategy and illustrate the platform's impact through local startup case studies, including Repzo's AI-enabled CRM with more than 2,500 representatives and Abjjad's e-reading service with 1.5 million registered users.

title: Numerical Design and Optimization of a Medium Temperature Difference Gamma Stirling Engine for Power Generation Based on Renewable Energy
authors: Kaoutar Laazaar, Noureddine Boutammachte
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: The current environmental situation in the World is very critical and requires an urgent intervention. The best solution to solve this problem is the development of renewable energy. Stirling engine is one of the performant technologies that uses renewable sources. Gamma Stirling engine is one of Stirling engine types that can be built easily and has several advantages. Most works in the field have only focused on studying Gamma Stirling engine with Low Temperature Difference which limits the use of these machines in specific applications. The aim of this study is modeling and optimizing a Medium Temperature Difference Gamma Stirling engine for power production based on biomass as heat source to enlarge the use of this type in other applications. The mathematical model used in this research is the non-ideal adiabatic model. The results of our model were compared and validated against experimental data and previous numerical models. The findings of this paper have revealed that the engine can produce high performance with Medium Temperature Difference.

title: Integrated Federated Multimodal AI for Early Cancer Detection Using Radiology, Pathology, and Genomic Signals
authors: Maruf Ahmad, Salman Qavi, Md Mohiuddin, Md Ariful Islam Rana, Md Akhtaruzzaman, Farhana Ferdous
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: In the current study, a combined federated multimodal AI architecture is proposed that is expected to improve the idea of early cancer detection by a combination of radiological, pathological and genomic cues. The architecture uses a federated learning model which enables joint training of models on distributed data sources without compromising the privacy and security of data. Combining multimodal data (radiologic images, e.g., X-rays, CT scans, histopathologic highresolution slides and genomic datasets) in the framework is aimed at enhancing diagnostic accuracy and faster early breast cancer detection. The integrative methodology will allow conducting the analysis of multi-omic data of complex nature in a seamless way without centralizing sensitive patient data, thus complying with privacy standards. Empirical findings indicate that the federated multimodal AI model significantly enhances detection accuracy, sensitivity and specificity of cancer diagnosis. The results indicate that the presented framework could lead to the change of current early cancer detection practices, making the process more accurate, timely, and privacy friendly.

title: Enhancing Precision Agriculture With Lightweight Object Detection of Date-Palm Flowers
authors: Thowayba Elkaffash, Toqa Alremawi, Alya Al-Adbah, Mohamed Sultan Mohamed Ali, Asan G.A. Muthalif
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: Agricultural production remains one of the most labor-intensive sectors, where repetitive and physically demanding operations are often carried out under harsh conditions. In date-palm cultivation, many critical tasksparticularly pollination-are still performed manually at considerable heights, posing safety risks and limiting scalability. While computer vision has advanced automation in harvesting and disease monitoring, flower identification for pollination remains largely underexplored. This paper presents a comparative study of lightweight deep learning models for datepalm flower detection, aimed at enabling real-time autonomous pollination under constrained hardware. Three models: YOLOv8n, YOLOv8s, and RT-DETR-I were evaluated under identical conditions, comparing their detection accuracy, inference speed, and computational efficiency. Results showed that YOLOv8n scored the fastest detection, while YOLOv8s achieved the best balance between speed and size as well as the highest recall. RT-DETR-1 provided the highest accuracy in mAP95 scores, but at a significantly higher computational cost. The findings highlight the trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency and offer practical insights into selecting perception models for vision-based robotic pollination systems.

title: Mathematical Modelling and Drying Kinetic Assessment of Coconut Meat Under Infrared Drying
authors: Jack O. Bertulfo, Eddie A. Carmelotes, June Kierlynne M. Beltran
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: This study evaluated the drying behavior and kinetics of coconut meat using a cabinet-type infrared dryer as a modern alternative to traditional sun and kiln drying. Recognizing the economic importance of coconut products and the quality issues in conventional drying, an Arduino-controlled infrared dryer was designed and tested for thin-layer drying at Agusan del Sur State College of Agriculture and Technology. Experiments used three drying temperatures <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(80^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 90^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$</tex> at a fixed 100 mm irradiation distance. Moisture content and drying time were measured, revealing that higher temperatures significantly reduced drying duration: 180 minutes at 80° C, 150 minutes at 90° C, and 135 minutes at 100° C. Among five mathematical models, the Midilli model best described the drying kinetics, with the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{R}^{2}(0.9986)$</tex> and lowest RMSE values for drying temperature of 90° C and 100° C. Findings conclude that infrared drying, particularly at 90° C, is an efficient method for producing high-quality copra and suitable for industrial optimization.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on Cybersecurity Education: Trends, Challenges, and the Role of AI
authors: Yassine Mouhssine, Hicham Boutracheh, Amine Sadik, Aniss Moumen
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Cybersecurity education has become a crucial area of focus as cyber threats continue to evolve rapidly. This study presents a systematic literature review exploring the current trends, challenges, and advancements in cybersecurity education, with a particular emphasis on the role of artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity competitions. To ensure a comprehensive analysis, we retrieved more than 500 articles from Scopus, of which over 298 were identified as directly relevant to our study. Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions, cyber ranges, and other hands on learning environments have emerged as essential tools for bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical cybersecurity skills. However, challenges such as scalability, accessibility, and real-time skill assessment persist. AI is increasingly being integrated into these competitions to enhance personalized learning, automate challenge generation, and provide intelligent feedback to participants. This review examines how AI-driven methodologies contribute to improving cybersecurity education by offering adaptive learning experiences, optimizing competition structures, and facilitating real-time performance evaluation. By synthesizing insights from recent research, this study identifies existing gaps and proposes future directions for leveraging AI in cybersecurity competitions to better equip students for real-world cyber defense scenarios.

title: Cryptocurrency Volatility Forecasting Using Garch, LSTM, Hybrid GARCH-LSTM and Hybrid CNN-LSTM with Intraday Frequency
authors: Houssemeddine Jlassi, Haykel Hamdi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Risk is a key concept in the world of finance. Measuring and predicting this critical aspect is therefore essential. Our paper compares three volatility prediction techniques: a traditional GARCH <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1,1)$</tex> model, a machine learning model and two hybrid models: GARCH (1,1)-LSTM and CNN-LSTM. In contrast to the existing literature, our results show the invalidity of the hybrid models, which is outperformed by the traditional GARCH model and the LSTM model. This is explained by the slow reactivity and poor tail risk capture of these models. Thus, we note that the LSTM model is the most adequate and reliable for all subsamples.

title: Rest Allowance Models for Optimizing Task Scheduling and Worker Well-Being in Industry
authors: Dorsaf Aribi, Olfa Belkahla Driss, Hind Bril El Haouzi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Rest Allowance (RA) models have long served as essential tools for managing worker fatigue, optimizing task scheduling, and ensuring sustainable productivity in industrial environments. This paper provides a comprehensive review of existing RA models, encompassing traditional approaches based on standardized time allowances, ergonomic assessments, and more recent developments that account for dynamic fatiguerecovery patterns. We also identify key limitations that hinder their effectiveness in modern, human-centric manufacturing systems. These include the lack of personalization, limited integration of mental fatigue, and insufficient consideration of task sequencing and inter-worker dependencies. The paper concludes by highlighting the need for adaptive, personalized RA models that integrate physical and cognitive demands to enhance wellbeing and efficiency in reconfigurable production systems.

title: An Intelligent Dimension-Reduction Method for Emergency-Supplier Evaluation Indicators Based on Filter-Wrapper and PSO-BP
authors: Jiayi Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: To address high-dimensional, redundant evaluation indicators and strong subjectivity in expert scoring within emergency-supply-chain contexts, an intelligent dimension-reduction model is proposed that integrates Filter-Wrapper feature selection with a PSO-BP neural network. An information-gain ratio is first employed for rapid preliminary screening, followed by hybrid recursive feature elimination that ensembles SVM, Random Forest and Gradient Boosting Machines for refined selection. Particle swarm optimization (PSO) is then applied to globally optimize the weights and thresholds of a BP neural network, yielding an adaptive evaluation model. Experiments on data from 93 suppliers indicate that the indicator dimensionality is compressed from 27 to 17 core features. With the PSO-BP approach, the model achieves a prediction accuracy of 94.6% and a recall of 93.3%, significantly outperforming SVM, Random Forest and the standard BP model. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of machine-learning techniques in supply-chain risk assessment and provide a reusable technical framework for indicator optimization in high-dimensional, small-sample scenarios.

title: Scalable and Explainable Heart Disease Prediction on Imbalanced Data
authors: Mayssa Ben Fekih, Farah Jemili, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Heart disease remains a leading cause of mortality worldwide. With the increasing availability of digital health records, machine learning has emerged as a promising approach to facilitate early diagnosis. However, traditional deep learning methods often suffer from lengthy processing times, limited interpretability, and dependence on complex data formats, such as ECG signals. In this study, we present a practical and interpretable machine-learning framework for heart disease prediction. Our approach integrates Spark Streaming for scalable real-time data processing, LightGBM for efficient classification, SHAP for feature-level interpretability, and SMOTE to address class imbalance. We benchmark our method against recent deep learning and ensemble-based models. Experiments conducted on the large-scale Heart_2020_cleaned dataset from Kaggle demonstrate that our framework achieves high accuracy and AUC scores while maintaining fast execution and clear interpretability, making it a strong candidate for deployment in real- world clinical environments.

title: Selective Federated IDS Framework with Adaptation for Large-scale Segmented Industrial Networks
authors: Muhammad Adil, Danish Javeed, Prabhat Kumar
publish: ACM Transactions on Internet Technology
abstract: None

title: Emerging AI Methodologies in the Renewable Energy Ecosystem: Successes, Challenges, and Future Research Directions
authors: Temitope Bobola, Temitayo Fagbola
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming the renewable energy sector by enabling intelligent, adaptive, and data-driven solutions across key operational domains. This paper presents a comprehensive review of emerging AI methodologies applied to renewable energy forecasting, real-time grid coordination, energy management, predictive maintenance, and market optimization. Techniques such as machine learning (ML), deep learning (DL), and reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved notable gains in forecast accuracy, system reliability, distributed energy resource (DER) coordination, and economic efficiency. Despite these advancements, widespread adoption is hindered by data quality issues, legacy infrastructure, limited explainability, and cybersecurity risks. This review synthesizes current successes, identifies persistent challenges, and outlines future research priorities-including edge computing, transfer learning, human-in-the-loop systems, and regulatory frameworks-to support the scalable and secure deployment of AI in renewable energy systems. By addressing these barriers, AI can play a pivotal role in accelerating the transition to a resilient and net-zero energy future.

title: Supply Chain Attacks in Cloud Native Web Applications
authors: Thoufeeq Ahamed, Akash Saminathan, Balasubramanian Prabhu Kavin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: One of the biggest cybersecurity risks in recent years has been supply chain attacks using cloud-native web applications. North Korea launched an attack in mid-2025 that used fictitious job interviews to release 67 malicious NPM packages, resulting in over 17,000 downloads and infecting developer computers with malware families like BeaverTail, XORIndex Loader, HexEval, and InvisibleFerret. Because the attack reveals untrusted third-party dependencies that have the potential to infect entire ecosystems, it highlights the vulnerability of contemporary CI/CD pipelines. Here, we present an AI-Augmented Dependency Trust Verification Framework that integrates three defense in-depth layers: sandboxed verification at runtime prior to being added in cloud-native build pipelines; static and dynamic AI-powered malware detection as the initial indication of suspicious code patterns; and cryptographic package signing and provenance veerification at the Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts (SLSA) and Sigstore levels. To combat social engineering attack vectors, the framework also includes zero-trust developer onboarding guidelines. With the suppression of unsigned dependencies, obfuscated payload detection, and enforcement against malicious action exe- cution, the study demonstrates that the multi-layered protection would have easily destroyed the North Korean NPM attack. The system offers a useful template for cloud-native applications' software supply chain security.

title: Thermal Analysis of TIG Welding: Experimental Study and Comparison with FE and FD Numerical Methods
authors: Abdel Halim Zitouni, Mouloud Aissani, Lotfi Beghdadi, Younes Benkheda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This work presents a thermal analysis of the TIG (Tungsten Inert Gas) welding process. In the experimental phase, 304L stainless steel sheets were welded and thermal cycles at various locations were recorded using thermocouples. The second phase involved developing a mathematical model to describe the heat flux generated by the TIG arc welding process without filler metal. Two numerical methods were used to implement this model: the first employed a Finite Difference (FD) approach using a custom FORTRAN program, while the second utilized the Finite Element (FE) method via COMSOL Multiphysics. The main objective was to compare the experimental data with the results from both numerical approaches to evaluate the accuracy, strengths, and limitations of each. The resulting heat distribution calculations enabled the identification of critical regions affected by the welding process, including the Heat-Affected Zone (HAZ) and the Melted Zone (MZ).

title: Forecasting Electrical Outages in Adrar, Algeria: Towards a Smart Energy Management System
authors: Samia Bentaieb, Driss Nehari, Ammar Neçaibia, Messaoud Hamouda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this study, we present a predictive modeling framework for anticipating power outages using temporal, meteorological, and historical outage data. The proposed methodology gathers aggregate temporal and contextual data from multiple sources, and then engineers features based on time, binary indicators, and derived features. A Random Forest classifier is developed using a train-test partition and the model robustness is established through cross-validation. The model is designed to predict outages based on patterns in the energy consumption and environmental conditions. The performance of the model is evaluated using several metrics, it reached an accuracy of approximately 99 %, with precision, recall, and F1-scores all exceeding 98 %. The findings of this study provide evidence that supports the proposed approach for anticipating power outages to create smarter grid management and better reliability in power systems.

title: Designing a Coding Schema and Generating Sequence for Recompression Compressed Data Files
authors: Bekir Tevfik Akgün
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Our compression suite includes 5 different compression programs for recompressing compressed files. Here is a hierarchical partitioning schema designed for converting an input data file into fixed-sized segments. The pages of a segment are compressed by executing our skip second word (SSW) compression algorithm in SSW programs, which are recursive, incremental, and iterative compression programs. There is an off-the-shelf compression program for non-SSW programs, which are heuristic and sequence compression programs. The SSW compression algorithm starts to find two identical words placed on the same page. The first word is kept as the original value and position in the output file, while the second word is skipped. There is an addressing issue of restoring these skipped words in their original locations during decompressing. An encoded addressing information (EAI), which contains the locations of both original and skipped words, must be included in the output file. The bit size of encoded values of locations for each selected page will be crucial for the compression performance. In this paper, a variable-length and mixed-type coding schema, which has flags, Huffman coding, binary coding, and combinadics, is designed. The non-SSW programs require deterministic selections of pages that will be recompressed. The heuristic program contains a heuristic algorithm for testing and selecting pages with encoded page locations that is a subset of the EAI coding schema. Whereas, the sequence program contains a constant number sequence for selecting pages. Therefore, there is no need to save page locations. In the previous work, this constant sequence was generated using random functions of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}$</tex> standard library. For enabling the compilation of the source code using different compilers for different operating systems or rewriting it in different languages, this constant sequence must be saved. So that the same file can be obtained by decompression. Two portable generator functions are introduced in this paper. Here, we do not need to fulfill all requirements of the random number generation. We tested three number generators for the sequence program. As a result, the compression performance of the proposed sequence generator is better for selected examples, mostly.

title: Atomic Orbital Search with Convolutional Neural Network Based Cloud Assisted Centralized Prediction Model for Enterprise Financial Risks
authors: Pavan Nutalapati, Geol Gladson Battu, Rajeev Agarwal, Venkata Sai Nageen Kanikanti, Ashanand, Rahul Chauhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: With the rapid growth of financial technology and the enlargement of enterprise financial environment, financial risk prediction's effectiveness, timeliness and accuracy have become important factors to guarantee enterprise stability and sustainable growth. However, traditional financial risk assessment models tend to have the disadvantages of poor adaptability and inadequate accuracy in predicting financial risks using large-scale and dynamic financial data. In order to overcome these problems, a cloud-aided centralized financial risk prediction model based on convolutional neural network (CNN), optimized by atomic orbital search (AOS) algorithm, is proposed in this study. In this type of model, the enterprise's financial data is encrypted securely with an encryption algorithm and stored the data in the cloud environment to provide data confidentiality and integrity. The cloud hosted financial data is then run through the CNN to predict potential financial risks wherein the AOS algorithm is used to optimize the hyperparameters to enhance the learning proficiency and prediction performance of the CNN model. The annual reports of enterprises are used as the primary data source to validate the effectiveness of the proposed model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed cloud-assisted AOS-CNN model achieves superior accuracy, recall, and F1 score compared to conventional financial risk prediction methods.

title: Behavioral Profiling–Driven Zero-Trust Security Framework for Smart City IoT Ecosystems
authors: Uman Ahmed Mohammed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Over the past few years, the intensive growth of city IoT systems has triggered the prevalence of heterogeneous devices, thus creating complex and highly dynamic ecosystems that significantly increase the size of the potential attack surface. Traditional perimeter-based security concepts and passive, identity-based controls are insufficient to ensure such environments are protected against advanced adversarial approaches, hacked devices and abnormal post-authentication activities. Based on which, we offer a Behavioral Profiling-Driven Zero-Trust Architecture (BP-ZTA) that integrates machine-learning-assisted behavioral analytics with zero-trust concepts to provide a continuously adaptive and context-aware security to smart-city IoT networks. The framework uses multi-layer behavioral modelling at the edge, fog, and cloud layer, using anomaly detection, graph-based learning, and reinforcement learning to generate dynamic scores of device-trust. A policy engine then consumes these trust scores and coordinates real-time micro-segmentation of access-control decisions. It is designed to support both hybrid-cloud and multi-hyperscaler deployments, with the containerised microservices, infrastructure components prepared with AI, and federated learning pipelines. This architecture guarantees scale ability, privacy and availability. Empirical assessments show that the BP-ZTA framework significantly increases the accuracy of anomaly-detection and the false-positiveness rate as well as makes smart-city infrastructures more resilient without compromising latency and resource-effective use. The study provides an end-to-end security model consistent with the current enterprise architecture and customizable to a broad spectrum of applications of smart cities.

title: Cost-Effective Reliability Analysis of RE Rich Grid with BESS by Horizon-Optimal Technique
authors: Prahlad Mundotiya, Arun Kumar Nayak, Santosh Kumari, Nirmala Yadav, Chandra Prakash Naga, N.K. Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources, particularly wind and photovoltaic systems, has transformed modern power system operations, introducing both environmental benefits and operational challenges due to their stochastic and weather-dependent nature. Accurate forecasting of renewable generation has thus become crucial for reliable and economical grid operation. This paper proposes a horizonoptimal linear programming framework that integrates probabilistic renewable forecasts directly into the battery energy storage system dispatch problem. Unlike deterministic or computationally intensive stochastic optimization methods, the proposed model incorporates multiple forecast scenarios within a rolling horizon, enabling adaptive, uncertainty-aware decision-making in real time. The approach jointly minimizes operating costs while maintaining system reliability and tractability. Simulation studies conducted on the IEEE 39-bus test system demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively balances economic performance and reliability under renewable uncertainty, outperforming traditional rule-based dispatch strategies. The results reveal that optimal BESS scheduling achieves up to a 13 % reduction in operational costs and a significant reduction in renewable curtailment compared to the no-storage scenario.

title: Smart Home Energy Management System Considering Battery Degradation Cost
authors: Vinay Kumar Yadav, Basant Kumar Sethi, Chandani P. Gor
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Since, smart home energy management systems (SHEMS) are becoming more popular, the homeowner has the opportunity to maximize energy utilization and make use of renewable resources effectively. However, the increased adoption of SHEMS leads to frequent battery charging and discharging, that effect life of the battery significantly. To deal with this issue, effective scheduling strategies must be considered for battery degradation costs along with homeowner requirement. The major objective of this method is to coordinate the operation of smart home appliances, energy resources, and the battery itself to achieve an overall reduction in household energy expenditure. Apart from it, the approach simultaneously integrates a model for battery degradation. The study rigorously investigates the effects of strategic, efficient demand scheduling on battery longevity and overall energy expenditures.

title: Technoeconomic Analysis and Smart Scheduling of Neighbourhood EMS Considering Shading Effect of PV and Ageing Effect of ESS
authors: Anchal, Sandeep Kakran, Ratna Dahiya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: With the global rise in the integration of solar photovoltaics (PV) with the grid, consideration of energy storage systems (ESS) becomes important for efficient utilization of energy for the prolonged sustainability. ESS may include battery for storing and providing the energy generated from distributed energy resources (DERs). Consideration of degradation cost and life cycle of battery becomes an important aspect while including ESS in the system. This article includes solar PV, battery energy storage systems (BESS) and electric vehicles (EV) as DERs in the presence of peer-to-peer (P2P) energy exchange across the prosumers within the residential community. The research work further explores the impact of shading on the energy scheduling of a neighbourhood energy management system (NEMS) and also evaluates the performance of the BESS considering various parameters during the life cycle of battery. Battery capacity fade due to calendar-ageing and cyclicageing based degradation is calculated to estimate the end-of-life of the battery. This paper also analyses the payback period of PV coupled with battery system. The formulated problem is solved using SBB solver of General Algebraic Modelling Systems (GAMS) to optimize the cost function under Time of Use (TOU) demand response scheme. After optimization, 0.59 % decrement is obtained in the amount of cost of energy consumed by the community along with 9.1 % reduction in the amount of energy generated by solar PV due to consideration of shading effect in the system.

title: Modulating Emotional Perception through Colour in Neurodivergent Individuals
authors: Ruoxi Yin, Peter Raynham, Jemima Unwin Teji
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: An increasing number of studies suggests that neurodivergent individuals may experience differences in emotion perception, with atypical visual processing proposed as a contributing factor. Despite colour is a salient component of visual input, its role in shaping emotion perception in neurodivergent populations remains underexplored. This pilot study employed a two-stage design in which 29 neurodivergent participants first selected a preferred colour and then rated the perceived intensity of facial emotional expressions presented under personalised chromatic and achromatic conditions. Results showed an emotion-specific effect: angry expressions were perceived as significantly less intense under personalised colour conditions than under achromatic presentations. These findings provide preliminary evidence that personalised colour context can selectively modulate perceived emotional intensity in neurodivergent observers, with potential implications for inclusive lighting and built-environment design.

title: HPC Controlled Algorithm for Solar PV Systems Driving Non Linear Load
authors: Nawaz Rahman, Sonali R Nandanwar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative approach toward enhancement in grid-interfaced three-phase solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, with advanced control strategies. The aspect of maintaining voltage stability, together with minimizing harmonic distortions and ensuring the efficient extraction of power in variable environmental circumstances, is becoming more critical with the increasing penetration of solar PV systems worldwide. Two systems are analyzed here: System I, which employs the conventional Perturb and Observe (P&O) algorithm for MPPT; and System II, which proposes the use of a hybrid P&O-Cuckoo Search Algorithm (HPC) for enhancing efficiency and robustness. The performance of the systems is evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink-based simulation under dynamic irradiance and non-linear loading conditions. System I suffer from frequent voltage oscillations and higher Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), which severely compromise power quality. By comparison, System II shows good voltage regulation and THD reduction, thus validating the appropriateness of the HPC approach. Some recent MPPT approaches are also reviewed and benchmarked, such as using Fuzzy Logic, Dynamic Group-Based Cooperation Optimization (DGBCO), Regression Models, and metaheuristic optimizations such as Firefly and Antlion algorithms. The findings support that the proposed hybrid method could efficiently address the partial shading and harmonic disturbance implementations. The results suggest accepting hybrid optimization techniques in the areas of future smart grids, particularly for areas characterized by variable solar irradiance and non-linear load characteristics.

title: Core Temperature Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries Using EKF and Offline-Based Thermal Parameters
authors: Keyur K. Patel, Mahmadasraf A. Mulla, Sanjay Tolani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In Battery Management Systems (BMS), accurate estimation of the core temperature of Lithium-Ion Batteries (LIBs) is crucial for ensuring operational safety, enhancing performance, and preventing thermal runaway. Direct measurement of core temperature is impractical due to sensor placement limitations and reliability concerns. Therefore, model-based estimation techniques are employed. This study proposes a method that integrates an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) with a twostate lumped thermal model to estimate core temperature. The Bernardi model is used to characterize heat generation, accounting for electrochemical over potential and entropy changes. This method uses offline parameter calculations based on cell material properties or datasheet values for parameter identification rather than Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). This greatly lessens the need for lengthy experiments and computation time. The corresponding root mean square errors (RMSE) between estimated and measured core and surface temperatures are approximately 0.052° C and 0.155° C, respectively.

title: Deep Learning Based Forecasting Model for Solar PV Power Generation Prediction in Highly Uncertain Environments
authors: Anandhi Govindarajan, Arjun Deo, Sonali R. Nandanwar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The Solar energy serves is an efficient alternate to conventional energy sources. Solar energy exhibits a high level of uncertainty due to weather conditions. This paper proposes a deep learning-based model aimed at forecasting solar energy output. The proposed model optimizes the training time and reduces the operational costs in a high-dimensional uncertain environment. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves consistent training and validation accuracies of 0.94 and 0.92, respectively, from epoch 7 onward. These findings indicate that 7 epochs can be considered an optimal point for balancing the accuracy and computational efficiency and to reduce the operational cost.

title: Combining Discrete Math with Theory of Computation
authors: Ashwin Lall
publish: ACM Inroads
abstract: None

title: Innovative Iot Applications for Monitoring Undergrown Cows in Large-Scale Smart Farms
authors: Devendra Kumar Misra, Jagriti Pal, K. M. Nilu Gupta, Sapana Sidarth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The growth in demand for effective livestock management in large-scale farming environments has led to increased adoption of IoT technologies, as this technology can be used to develop and implement new IoT applications that can monitor underdeveloped or “under-grown” cows in smart farms. The IoT application developed includes integrating different types of sensors such as RFID tags, accelerometers, weight sensors, and temperature sensors to continually collect data from cow health, cow activity, and cow growth. The collected data is then transmitted through low-power wide-area networks (LPWAN) to a cloud computing platform where it can be analyzed in real time using machine learning-based growth tracking, cow health issue detection, and cow undergrowth condition prediction. The application also offers the farm manager a user-friendly dashboard to view cow growth and cow health status and to receive notifications if there are any abnormalities detected in the cow data so that the farm manager can make timely intervention. Field testing demonstrated the effectiveness and scalability of the system in large-scale farming environments.

title: A Comparative Study of GA, PSO, and SA Algorithms for Improving UAV Endurance through Metaheuristic Optimization
authors: B. Ravi Chandra, Y Sravanthi, K Mounika, P Suneetha, Ajay Roy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The endurance of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) continues to be a significant constraint in extended missions that encompass surveillance, transportation, and environmental monitoring. This study introduces an intelligent UAV endurance optimization approach that combines a windsensitive, airspeed-dependent energy model with adaptive charging control over several charging pads. The suggested model realistically links aerodynamic impacts to power use by linking propulsion energy to airspeed instead of ground speed. Three metaheuristic algorithms-Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Simulated Annealing (SA)-were executed under uniform conditions, utilizing a simulated wind vector of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$[8.0,-3.0] \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The simulation results demonstrated that the model was consistent because all three methods attained the same optimum endurance of 20.62 minutes. The most effective in terms of computation was PSO, which took 0.56 seconds to identify the global optimum, followed by SA at 1.79 seconds and GA at 2.98 seconds. According to these findings, PSO is superior to GA and SA in terms of realtime mission adaptation.
