title: Orientation Optimization of Agrivoltaic Bifacial PV Farms in Morocco
authors: Mattia Parenti, Marco Fossa, Samuele Memme, Caterina Gibelli
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Morocco aims to achieve 52% renewable energy by 2030, prioritizing photovoltaic over solar thermal technologies. Africa's solar potential is immense, with rapidly declining PV costs. Key challenges include grid integration, storage, and financing. Bifacial modules currently represent the most efficient option for utility-scale PV systems, providing increased solar energy output with only marginal additional costs. Agrivoltaics technology integrates solar panels with agriculture, optimizing land use and water efficiency. It enhances crop resilience to heat stress, supports rural electrification, and promotes sustainable development in arid regions. This paper helps in optimizing the productivity of agrivoltaic bifacial PV power plants in Morocco: Typical Meteorological Year data are retrieved from the PVGIS platform for 7 agricultural locations at different latitudes. A cumulative sky radiance is built for each location and an iterative search for the best tilt angle is performed to maximize the annual energy yield (on both front and back surfaces considering direct, diffuse and reflected energy) of south-facing modules, with both ground albedo and PV row spacing taken as parameters. The analysis is then repeated for vertical bifacial plants, finding the best azimuth, and it has been evidenced that the energy yield is on average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-22 {\%}$</tex> for vertical PV, respect to optimally oriented tilted PVs. Latitude is the primary influencing factor for optimal tilt angles, but the results indicate that the Ground Coverage Ratio is also a significant parameter, while albedo has only a minor effect. Finally, the irradiance distribution on the ground surrounding a PV installation has been determined for multiple configurations, quantifying the decreased shading under vertical PVs respect to tilted systems.

title: Surpassing 16% Efficiency in Cd-Free CZTS Solar Cells via Si Back-Contact Engineering and IR Harvesting
authors: Lynda Metref, Essaid Mansouri, Mahfoud Abderrezek, Samira Sali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: CZTS solar cell -We propose a fully Cd 1D, consisting of a ZnO:Al-SCAPS architecture simulated in Si -transparent contact, a ZnS buffer, a CZTS absorber, and a p Si layer -substrate that also acts as a secondary absorber. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> minimizes recombination losses at the back contact while infrared, thus improving -extending absorption into the near overall light harvesting. Simulations under AM1.5G illumination at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 0 ~ K}$</tex> predict an efficiency above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6 \%}$</tex>, with Voc These findings. %68.7 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx$</tex> and FF, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2} 24.6 \approx ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>, Jsc <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.98 \approx$</tex> Si in enhancing device-highlight the synergistic role of p performance and stability, while also demonstrating the efficiency -free, high-1D for designing Cd-reliability of SCAPS. based photovoltaics-kesterite

title: GrapeSense: Grape Leaf Disease Detection
authors: Angel Li, Rishit Agrawal, Sanskriti Farswal, Navid Shaghaghi
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Grape crops are important for both food security amid climate devastation and the global wine-making industry. However, the prevalence of numerous plant diseases can devastate vineyards, making early detection of disease onset crucial. The GrapeSense project offers a sustainable and modern approach to viticulture, successfully developing models to classify ripeness stages and detect optimal harvesting time for large vineyards. This paper presents the project's expansion into detecting grape leaf diseases based on visual leaf condition observable in drone-captured imagery in real vineyard settings. While substantial research has been conducted on classifying single grape leaves under controlled laboratory conditions, the GrapeSense project presents a novel approach using a two-step pipeline to classify images captured directly at a vineyard with numerous leaves and complex environmental features. Vineyard drone-captured imagery were augmented with diseased leaves in various overlaying patterns and a two-step pipeline was developed by extracting leaves with the YOLOv8 object detection model, followed by classifying each leaf using ResNet-18's transfer learning model on the singled out leaf. The leaf detection model achieved a mAP50 of 85.0% and the disease classification model obtained an accuracy of 99.61 %, offering a successful deep learning approach to automate laborious agricultural practices and improve crop yield.

title: Energy Management in Agricultural Microgrids: A Comparative Study of Deterministic and Stochastic Model Predictive Control
authors: Yasmine Achour, Rachid El Bouayadi, Abderrahim Bajit, Hicham Labrim, Hamza Benzzine, Driss Zejli
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: This paper compares deterministic and stochastic Model Predictive Control (MPC) strategies for optimal energy management in an agricultural microgrid integrating renewable sources, battery storage, and controllable loads. The deterministic MPC assumes perfect knowledge of future disturbances, whereas the stochastic MPC accounts for uncertainties in weather and renewable generation through probabilistic forecasting. Both approaches are evaluated in terms of tracking performance, storage utilization, and grid interaction. The study highlights the value of uncertainty-aware control in enhancing the sustainability and resilience of agricultural microgrids under variable operating conditions.

title: NMPC for Vehicle Trajectory Tracking: A Study on Data Loss and Energy Efficiency
authors: Alessandra Elisa Sindi Morando, Roberto Sacile, Pedro Castillo
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: In this paper a Nonlinear Model Predictive Controller (NMPC) is proposed for a steering car's trajectory tracking. The aim is to analyze the robustness of NMPC algorithm to the data loss (loss of measurement of states in the control loop) and its energy efficiency in terms of total traveled distance. To have a full analysis, NMPC have been tested both in simulation and in a real environment by tracking different references with and without data loss. Moreover, to verify the performance of the NMPC algorithm a comparison with a classical controller is proposed. In all scenarios, better tracking performances are obtained with the NMPC strategy. Note that on the average the time required by one NMPC's iteration is almost equal to the sampling time. Regarding the energetic efficiency, although in simulation NMPC outperforms the classical controller notably in presence of losses, in the experiments the total traveled distance is almost the same for both algorithms.

title: Harnessing Machine Learning and Deep Learning to Predict Cumulative Abnormal Returns from CEO Behavioral Profiles
authors: Said Benlakhdar, Mohammed Rziza, Rachid Oulad Haj Thami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are central to corporate growth, yet post-acquisition performance frequently shows declining cumulative abnormal returns (CAR). This study examines how CEO behavioral traits, particularly hubris and learning ability, shape CAR outcomes across 7,786 international M&A transactions conducted between 2012 and 2022. We assess the extent to which successive deal experience, deal completion, and time between deals interact with CEO characteristics to influence acquisition performance. Using econometric analysis enriched with machine learning methods and deep learning techniques, we capture both linear and non-linear patterns linking CEO traits to post-acquisition value creation. Although CEO characteristics account for a modest share of CAR variance, they exert significant effects on deal timing, success likelihood, and value outcomes. Hubristic CEOs display more aggressive acquisition behavior, while learning-oriented CEOs show improved performance over successive deals. These findings advance the understanding of the behavioral drivers of M&A performance and highlight the value of integrating executive behavioral assessment with data-driven predictive tools in strategic decision making.

title: Combining I-BSIF Fingerprints and FlexenTech for Reversible Medical Image Watermarking
authors: Brahim Ferik, Lakhdar Laimeche, Abdallah Meraoumia, Abdelkader Laouid, Khaled Chait
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The rise of digital medical imaging in telemedicine raises concerns about image authenticity and patient privacy. Traditional watermarking methods struggle to achieve robust security and reliable patient authentication simultaneously. This study presents a novel blind reversible watermarking framework that combines orientation-guided Improved Binary Statistical Image Features (I-BSIF) with FlexenTech encryption to improve medical image security. The method utilizes advanced fingerprint feature extraction tailored to local ridge orientations, resulting in compact biometric signatures. These features undergo lightweight FlexenTech encryption before being embedded into medical images through spread-spectrum modulation in mid-frequency subbands of the Lifting Wavelet Transform. Experimental results demonstrate that rotation-invariant feature extraction preserves more than 99% similarity after a 270° rotation, with an average Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) exceeding 51 dB and a Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) of nearly 0.999, confirming the method's effectiveness in maintaining patient authentication and image integrity.

title: Radiomics-Driven Classification of Lung Cancer Subtypes Using Ensemble Machine Learning
authors: Amay Annasamudram, Pranav Kulkarni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Lung cancer is one of the most common types of cancer in the world, killing thousands each year. This study aims to develop a novel multiclass machine learning model to differentiate between the different subtypes of lung cancer (adenocarcinoma, large cell carcinoma, small cell carcinoma, and squamous cell carcinoma) based on radiomic features derived from CT scans using Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrices (GLCM). Unlike other conventional approaches, the proposed model integrates ExtraTrees, LightGBM, and XGBoost algorithms to address class imbalance and variability in texture representation across subtypes. The dataset consisted of over 196,000 lung CT scan images from 355 patients with different kinds of cancer. Basic statistical features were extracted from the images, including mean, standard deviation, minimum, maximum, and median intensity, all to capture the overall variation of the image. The resulting features were then used to train the ensemble model. The model achieved an accuracy of 92.33 %, demonstrating improved performance when compared to other individual and integrated models commonly used in the field. The AUC score, a metric used to evaluate the performance of a classification model, was high for each of the four lung cancer subtypes, with values of 1.00 for large cell carcinoma, 0.99 for small cell carcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma, and 0.98 for adenocarcinoma. The analysis of the features indicated a potential for texture-derived metrics to contribute as potential noninvasive biomarkers, prompting further research in future studies.

title: Investigative Evaluation of the Multi-Carrier Pulse Width Modulation Techniques for Single-Phase 15-Level MLI Topology
authors: P. Anjaneya Vara Prasad, C Dhanamjayulu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Multilevel Inverter (MLI) topologies are the most commonly utilized converter topology in industries for medium voltage applications at present. These MLI topologies play a crucial role in the power management of the industries by being the position of main integrating aspect of the stored backup energy. The importance of the MLI in the industries is provides more challenging issues like making controlled strategic techniques, providing the smooth operation, and reducing harmonics in the system. The best possible establishment of a solution to these issues is to provide an appropriate Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) technique for that MLI topology. The different multicarrier PWM techniques are considered for evaluating the performance of the MLI topology. And these methods are analyzed and compared with different aspects, such as voltage and its Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), with different modulation index values. The simulation outcomes of the MLI topology with different PWM techniques are obtained by using MATLAB/Simulink software.

title: Solar Powered Wireless Charging System for Electric Vehicles
authors: Chintan R. Mehta, Vivek J. Patal, Amar Kumar Barik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This work proposes a static wireless charging system using resonant inductive coupling for electric vehicle (EV) powered by solar energy, in order to achieve the global sustainable goal with a carbon neutral process. It also overcomes the compatibility issues of the charging guns in different EVs with the ports of wired charging stations. As solar power generation is intermittent in nature, the proposed system is simulated in MATLAB for four different climatic scenarios and the performances were analyzed to confirm the charging requirement of EV. Artificial hummingbird algorithm is employed with incremental conductance based maximum power point tracking to extract optimum solar energy from photovoltaic arrays. The available solar power is used to energize the primary coil of charging station through a high frequency DC to AC converter. The receiving coil on EV induce the voltage wirelessly and further converted to DC for fueling EV battery. An auxiliary battery storage unit is included in primary side with solar arrays, to support the charging process uninterruptedly. The performances were compared and reported in this work for four different cases based on availability of solar generations to imitate all time EV charging support.

title: Reducing Energy Consumption in LoRa Networks for Smart Agriculture
authors: Gaurav Jangir, Neeraj Kumar Gupta, Tanishk Patodi, Ghanapriya Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: For modern agriculture, it is critical to monitor environmental conditions efficiently since it requires major manpower. This work presents a smart agriculture system that monitors temperature, soil moisture, and humidity in real-time to support farming, resource and power optimization. For minimizing energy consumption, there has been employment of a hierarchical network architecture with a three-tier layout for large farmlands and a two-layer layout for smaller farmlands. Long Range (LoRa) technology is used to ensure long-range, lowpower communication. The results of the Simulation demonstrate that the system provides high scalability and energy efficiency. The short-range configuration covers of 97.9 meters with 0.517 mJ per transmission, but in case of long-range, it covers 12.3 km with 5.16 J per transmission. The data compression reduces energy usage by 26.98 %, and the layered network design additionally reduces 86.57 % When combined together, these achieve up to 97.79 % energy savings for 5000 acres, stabilizing around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 2 \%}$</tex> for larger areas. The architecture offers a low-power solution that is suitable for both small and large-scale smart farming applications.

title: Advanced Multilevel Inverter with Integrated Fault Tolerance: A Novel Approach
authors: Gulshan Yadav, J. Venkataramanaiah, Suresh Lakhimsetty
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Reliability is a paramount concern for multilevel inverters, particularly in sensitive industrial applications. Conventional multilevel inverters are susceptible to abnormal operating conditions and potential severe equipment damage when a fault occurs in one of their semiconductor switches. This paper presents an efficient single DC source based fault-tolerant(FT) inverter topology to address these limitations. in this topology one half bridge leg are connected to another legs via bidirectional switch. additionally, The proposed FT control scheme achieves rapid fault recovery, operating within a single fundamental cycle of the inverter. Moreover, it require only one current and one voltage sensor for fault diagnosis, and its operation is independent of the underlying switching technique. The proposed fault-tolerant converter topology and its associated control method will be rigorously validated through comprehensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations.

title: ML Forecasting of Tunisia's Regulatory Freedom Index
authors: Amani Driss, Yamina Mathlouthi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This article explains how machine learning techniques can be used to forecast the ‘Regulation’ component (Area 5) of the economic freedom index published by the Fraser Institute for the next five years (2023-2027) for Tunisia. This component examines the effect of government regulations on voluntary trading in the credit, laborbor and business markets. The study demonstrates Tunisia's institutional inertia through the Ridge regression, Random forest regression, and XGBoost regression methods. The results were compared to those of simple univariate forecasting methods such as the naive, average, and drift models used in previous studies. Institutional theories underpin the results, which highlight persistent challenges and suggest improvements to the regulatory framework.

title: Spray Cooling Optimization for IVR-ERVC Applications
authors: Vitor Fernandes de Almeida, Jingqi Zhao, Lining Liu, Feng Xie
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Severe nuclear accidents impose extreme thermal loads on the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), where localized focusing effects can produce dangerous heat flux intensification and threaten vessel integrity. Ensuring effective In-Vessel Retention through External Reactor Vessel Cooling (IVR-ERVC) requires enhanced strategies capable of mitigating these hotspots. This study investigates multi-nozzle spray cooling as a promising method to reinforce IVR-ERVC. Numerical simulations were performed at nozzle outlet pressures ranging from 1.0 to 3.0 bar to analyze droplet impingement, liquid film formation, and wall temperature response under high heat flux conditions. Results show that increasing the nozzle pressure improves the liquid film thickness and uniformity, thereby reducing temperature gradients and accelerating surface cooling. Optimal performance occurs near 2.5 bar, where film coverage becomes highly stable and wall temperatures are minimized, while further pressure increases yield only marginal benefits. These findings confirm that spray cooling can effectively suppress focusing effects, increase critical heat flux margins, and strengthen the safety of advanced nuclear reactor designs.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)

title: Modelling Coating Disbondment Rates Using Acoustic Emission Technique: A Pseudo Kinetic Perspective
authors: Noor A'in A. Rahman, Zazilah May, Lukman Ab Rahim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Coating disbondment is a serious concern for the long-term durability of metallic structures, especially when these structures are exposed to high voltages in harsh environments. In this study, a pseudo kinetic modelling approach was used to quantify how fast disbondment occurs under different voltage levels. To do this, the acoustic emission (AE) parameters were used to indicate the interfacial damage. AE data were acquired during cathodic potential on the coated test specimen at a variety of voltage ranging from 3 to 6 Volt. After applying a cathodic potential to the coating substrate, the disbonded area was measured according to the test standard ASTM G8. The pseudo kinetic constant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>), representing normalized AE activity over time, was derived for each condition and modelled using seven different fitting approaches: linear, exponential, power-law, logarithmic, polynomial, sigmoid, and saturation growth models. The results suggest that the stages of coating disbondment is dissolved resulting in the delamination of coating around the initial defect, which related to the increasing of the cathodic potential strength. The results of the pseudo kinetic modelling indicate that Average Signal Level (ASL) provides the most accurate and consistent fit among all tested AE parameters when correlating with the disbondment process under varied voltage stimuli. This is evidenced by its highest coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 7 3}$</tex>) and RMSE value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.07$</tex> given by the Quadratic model, suggesting that ASL captures the voltage-dependent evolution of disbondment more effectively than other eight (8) AE metrics. These results suggest that ASL could serve as a reliable, realtime diagnostic tool for detecting and monitoring coating failures. Overall, this study offers a fresh, data-driven perspective on using AE technology to model coating degradation and supports future efforts in predictive maintenance using AE signal analysis in future.

title: Review on Adversarial Attack Defense Mechanism in Machine Learning Models
authors: Vibhakti Bagade, Sargam Kamble, Samiksha Sadanshiv, Kundan Patle, Deep Gaoner, Durgeshnandini Meshram
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Based on the opposition with small input perturbation, with such well crafted attacks, predictions may often be steered strongly in the wrong direction and even erratic; therefore, machine learning models are highly susceptible to adversarial attacks. Some of these techniques have trade-offs, such as less model accuracy on clean data, increased computational cost or vulnerability to novel attack techniques. It might not however be possible to implement a strong and effective countermeasure to such attacks in the real world application. To solve the above-mentioned issues, the following research aims at creating a strong adversarial defense mechanism by combining various techniques such as adversarial training, feature smoothing, and ensemble learning. A proposed technique was a combination of these techniques to boost the resilience of the models to all the varieties of adversarial attacks with the quality maintained in terms of accuracy and computational efficiency. Develop defense mechanisms to make machine learning models more resilient to adversarial attacks without making a substantial impact on them.

title: Variance-Entropy Guided SWT Fusion with P-Sharpened Softmax for Enhanced Medical Imaging
authors: Mohammed Rafiq, Ankur Maurya, Prabhishek Singh, Manoj Diwakar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The primary goal of medical image fusion (MIF) is to combine important medical information from different medical modalities, including computed tomography (CT) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), into a single image. This research paper proposes a novel MIF method that integrates Stationary Wavelet Transform (SWT) with content-aware saliency and soft decision rules. SWT breaks down the image into low-frequency (LF), and high-frequency (HF) domain. The LF components are fused using Variance-Entropy (VE) saliency measure. A temperature-controlled softmax function generates smooth fusion weights, ensuring robust approximation component integration. The HF components are fused using psharpened softmax strategy. The fused image is reconstructed using inverse SWT that has high visual quality with less time complexity. The comprehensive experimental evaluation is done using metrics like (Qab/f, Qy, Qcb, and Qs). This shows the superiority of the proposed method compared to other MIF models. The proposed method achieves an effective balance between interpretability, novelty, and clinical applicability.

title: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: The Effect of Gameplay on Student Stress: A Statistical and Psychophysiological Analysis of Influencing Factors
authors: Pratishtha Agarwal, Mayukh Chakraborty, Sakshi, Jyoti Yadav, Nikita
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this paper, the immediate impact of stress levels of students has been analyzed with the help of Electrocardiography (ECG). Additionally, factors responsible for stress and changes in immediate stress levels before and after playing the game are identified using paired t-tests and chisquare tests. The setup for experiment was created with 54 participants, being asked to play a horror game on a standard mobile device. Physiological stress levels have been calculated by recording ECG before and after playing the game under consistent laboratory conditions and psychological stress have been assessed using self-reported answers on the Depression Anxiety and Stress Scale (DASS-42). Additionally, demographic information of participants has been recorded. The stress levels have reduced after playing the game since p>0.05. Psychological stress is significantly associated with family income category (p=0.0096) and place of residence (hostel or day scholar) where p=0.029. The immediate change in physiological stress is significantly related to gender (p = 0.0026), sleep duration (p=0.027), and water intake (p=0.03).

title: Pose Estimation for Real-Time Worker Activity Recognition in the Oil and Gas Industry Using Mediapipe
authors: Ibnu Zakaria Arrazi, Ahmad Bukhari Aujih
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This study evaluates two AI approaches for realtime worker activity detection in the oil and gas industry. The first approach is based on object detection using YOLOv8. The second approach is a new proposed method that uses pose estimation with a classification model, built using MediaPipe library. Both approaches showed similar accuracy in identifying key activities like climbing and lifting. However, pose estimation methods were proven to be more efficient, using less computational power and processing frames faster, making it better suited for real-time monitoring on resourcelimited devices. By analyzing human body posture instead of just detecting objects, the pose estimation approach offers more precise recognition of unsafe worker behaviors. These findings highlight that the proposed approach has a strong potential to improve safety monitoring, offering faster and more re-source-friendly solutions to reduce accidents in highrisk industrial settings.

title: LoRa-based Environmental Monitoring System
authors: Muhammad Syahmi Abdul Kadir, Saeed S. Ba Hashwan, Illani Mohd Nawi, Mohd Haris Md Khir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Internet of Things (IoT) technology has significantly enhanced environmental monitoring, particularly in weather observation systems. This project focuses on the design and implementation of a LoRabased autonomous environment monitoring system that enables real-time environmental data collection and transmission over long distances with minimal power consumption. The system is designed to overcome key limitations of existing monitoring solutions, such as reliance on Wi-Fi connectivity, limited mobility, and high energy consumption. The proposed system consists of an autonomous solar powered sensor node equipped with multiple environmental sensors, including temperature, humidity, pressure, and gas level detectors. These sensors collect environmental data, which is then transmitted via the LoRa (Long Range) protocol to a master node for storage and analysis. The autonomous mobility feature allows the system to cover a wider geographic area, ensuring comprehensive data collection.

title: AI Driven Early Warning System for Brain Stroke Risk
authors: Sarabjeet Kaur, Shiraz Khurana, Vidushi Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Stroke is considered a significant medical condition since it is closely linked to a high level of disability and mortality. Stroke is usually categorized into two main classes: ischemic and stroke caused by hemorrhage. Ischemic happen when there is a blockage in the brain's blood vessels, while hemorrhagic is characterized by bleeding in the brain. Stroke's early detection is considered a vital aspect in assisting medical practitioners in administering various treatment measures, such as thrombolytic and anticoagulant drugs. Stroke's early symptoms and warning signs are important in mitigating the severity of a stroke's consequences. This study's primary goal is to apply different artificial intelligence models in creating a predictive model that can assist in assessing a patient's early risks of having a stroke. Various classification models were utilized in this research, Specifically, K-nearest-neighbours (KNN), a standard Random Forest (RF), Adaptive Boosting (AdaBoost), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), the conventional Decision Tree Classifier, and naive Bayes. The models' performance was assessed using various metrics, such as Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. Among all methods under evaluation, the performance of the XGBoost classifier was found to be superior with an accuracy of 88.9 % and precision of 94.9% compared to all other machine learning methods under evaluation for this study.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A

title: Vertically Integrated Moisture Flux Convergence Associated with the Rainfall Event Over the West Coast of India
authors: Anshu Choudhary, Shashi Kant, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In the Western Coast of India, the coastal fisheries, port infrastructure and several plantations are the major contributors to the regional economy. The Ghats on the west contain extensive forests and are recognized as a biodiversity hotspot. These western Ghats are a steep and continuous mountain chain running parallel to the coastline and hence have a major impact on the climate of the area. The proximity to the Arabian Sea and presence of the Western Ghats create one of the most efficient orographic rainfall zones in the monsoon. June 2025 delivered a notably intense rainfall along the western coast. During 12<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> – 16<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> June 2025, the western coast received a prolonged heavy spell of rainfall that broke the historical records. The moisture transport plays a crucial role in shaping the rainfall over a region. This paper aims to study and examine this prolonged heavy spell of rainfall from 12<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> – 16<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> June 2025 using Vertically Integrated Moisture Flux, along with some other dynamical and thermodynamic diagnoses. This paper utilises various sources to gather data and apply computing to visualize and understand this episode. The visualization through computing showed that relative humidity remained (850-500 hPa) high during the study period. The negative omega revealed the rising motion of air, and the divergence at 200hPa supports the convection and rainfall occurrence. The findings of this study provide valuable insights into disaster management and forecasting, as well as improved management in various sectors, including infrastructure and agriculture, in the western region of India.

title: Intelligent Zonal Control and Energy Optimization of TRNSYS-Based Radiant Floor Heating Systems
authors: Haiying Song, Zhenyu Pan, Yingkai Sun, Wei Zheng, Tianqi Liu, Zekai Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: To address the high energy consumption and limited intelligence of conventional continuous radiant floor heating systems, this study develops a TRNSYS-based model that integrates smart room-by-room control with a solar-assisted airsource heat-pump floor-heating system. A two-bedroom, two-hall apartment is selected as the reference building and fully represented in TRNSYS, incorporating detailed modules for room geometry, envelope properties, solar collectors, air-source heat pump, radiant floor circuits and PID controllers. A sparrowsearch algorithm is employed to auto-tune the PID parameters of the heating loops. Annual simulations reveal that, relative to conventional uninterrupted heating, the proposed adaptive zoning strategy cuts total energy use by 29.5 %, raises the seasonal system COP to 3.26 and still maintains every room within the comfort band prescribed, thereby demonstrating that real-time adjustment of heating delivery to occupancy patterns can secure thermal comfort while markedly improving energy efficiency.

title: Securing the Metaverse: Challenges and Solutions in Virtual Reality Cybersecurity
authors: Shaharyar Alam Ansari, Nikhil Sharma, Shashank Chaudhary
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The metaverse will introduce unparalleled opportunities and cybersecurity challenges with its integration of VR, AR, and Internet technologies. Immersive environments introduce new attack surfaces through VR hardware and the collection of vast amounts of personal data, introducing concerns around privacy, identity theft, and digital asset protection. This academic review maps the current landscape of metaverse cybersecurity, identifies core gaps in user privacy awareness, mismatched AI/IoT security models, weak infrastructure security, inadequate authentication and digital identity, interoperability issues, inconsistent regulatory policies, visualization vulnerabilities-including inference attacks and deepfakes-and privacy trade-offs in blockchain configurations. We further propose cross-platform privacy protocols, Zero Trust Architecture models native to the metaverse, adaptive AI security, secure cross-world identity verification, privacy-preserving AR/VR rendering, and decentralized identity management. Addressing these gaps can help guide researchers, platform developers, and policymakers toward a safe and privacy-by-design metaverse.

title: Compact Meander-Line Rectenna Operating at 3.5 GHz for RF Energy Harvesting in IoT and Wearable Devices
authors: Atul Sharma, Vandana Vikas Thakare, P.K. Singhal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The proposed research work focuses on development of high-performance meander-line rectenna resonating at 3.5 GHz. The antenna is fabricated using FR-4 substrate. Initially the proposed antenna is simulated in CST software and the achieved return loss is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 8 ~ d B}$</tex>. In the later stage the antenna is fabricated and tested on Vector Analyser. The obtained results are more better with the values of return loss equal to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{2 0. 5 0 ~ d B}$</tex> and a gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 5 2 ~ d B i}$</tex>. The rectifier full-wave circuit is developed using HSMS-2850 Schottky diode and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi$</tex> matching network on ADS Software with 80% RF-to-DC efficiency and 1.42 V DC across a 350Ω load.

title: Thompson-Lampard Theorem Based Cross-Capacitive Sensor for the Detection of Conductivity and Adulteration in Liquid Foods
authors: Mohammad Haris Bin Anwar, Mirza Mohammad Shadab, Anwar Ulla Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The quality and safety of liquid foods, such as milk, is a worldwide problem due to the common presence of adulterants. Moreover, it is difficult to solve this problem using common laboratory techniques, such as chromatography and spectroscopy, since this technique possesses a low rate of rapidity and is not applicable in the field. In this paper, the design, simulation, fabrication, and evaluation of a crosscapacitive alternating current (A.C.) sensor are discussed. The design of this sensor is based on the Thomson-Lampard theorem, in which it is possible to determine the conductivity of a solution with limited dependence on geometric calibration. At the same time, this sensor is compact and economical, and it is possible to read it with common multi-meter equipment. Moreover, it is possible to determine that the results of measuring the conductivity of ground-water, tap-water, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R O}$</tex>-water are consistent with theoretical expectations. In addition, it is possible to determine that this sensor is useful to detect changes in dielectric constant with the addition of water or urea to milk using a finite element simulation.

title: Research on the Use of Generative Adversarial Networks to SDN-Enabled Home Networks to Classify Traffic on the Internet with Encryption
authors: Rajesh Kumar, Deepak Sharma, Kavita
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The exponential rise in encrypted network traffic presents significant challenges for traditional classification techniques, particularly within Software-Defined Networking (SDN)-based home networks where packet inspection is limited by privacy constraints. To address this, we derive a novel Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-based framework that classifies encrypted traffic without requiring decryption, thus preserving user privacy while enhancing detection accuracy. The GAN leverages adversarial learning to model complex traffic distributions and differentiate between benign and malicious flows based on flow metadata and statistical features. Experimental evaluation using the ISCX VPN dataset demonstrates that the proposed model outperforms conventional classifiers-such as CNN, RNN, SVM, and Random Forestachieving an accuracy, precision, and recall of 98.8 %,an AUC of 0.995, and a low inference time of 2 ms. Additionally, the model maintains extremely low positive and negative rates of 0.006, confirming its robustness and reliability in dynamic, real- time environments. This research presents a scalable, efficient, and privacy-preserving solution for encrypted traffic classification in SDN-enabled home networks, paving the way for secure and intelligent network management.

title: Edge-Based Clique Similar Formation Control for Second-Order Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Yupeng Mao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a distributed similar formation control method for second-order multi-agent systems, relying solely on relative position and relative velocity information. By constructing a maxclique-based cost function incorporating rotation matrices and scaling factors, the designed control strategy achieves both geometric similarity maintenance and velocity consensus under dynamic constraints. Utilizing the edgebased clique addition topology, the method guarantees formation convergence to a globally consistent configuration without the need for a global coordinate frame. Stability analysis based on the Lyapunov method and LaSalle's Invariance Principle demonstrates that the closed-loop system globally asymptotically converges to the desired equilibrium set, wherein all agents attain unified rotation, scaling, and steady-state velocity. Simulation experiments in both two-dimensional and three-dimensional scenarios further verify the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: Optimization of Fuzzy Control Strategy for Electric Vehicle Based on Cross Entropy Algorithm
authors: Jie Wang, Minyu Liu, Liu Yan, Chong Song, Zhenjun Liu, Te Mu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: In order to study the influence of energy management strategy on the performance of composite vehicles in hybrid electric vehicles, nickel-hydrogen batteries and super capacitors were selected as composite power sources, and the fuzzy control strategy was used to allocate the energy demand of vehicles. Based on the constraints of UDDS operating conditions, the vehicle power consumption and the braking energy recovery capability of supercapacitors in the composite power supply are optimized. Firstly, the basic framework of energy management strategy is obtained through the analysis of vehicle structure and working conditions. Secondly, the fuzzy controller based on the framework is built in MATLAB, and the cross entropy algorithm(CEA) is used to iteratively optimize the position of the membership function in the fuzzy controller. The parameters are compared with the NSGA-II, and the optimization ability of the two algorithms under the same conditions is studied. The research results show that the fuzzy controller can achieve the desired goal by the cross entropy algorithm. The supercapacitor energy recovery has gained 16.3%, and the vehicle power consumption has been reduced by 63.67%. At the same time, the vehicle performance has also been improved. The increase was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$82 \%, 25 {\%}$</tex> and 12.77% in the nickel-hydrogen battery DOD, maximum grade and maximum speed. In summary, it is valid to use the cross entropy algorithm to optimize the fuzzy energy management strategy of the hybrid power supply.

title: Integration of Convolutional Neural Networks and Morphological Feature Analysis for Tomato Leaf Disease Diagnosis
authors: Siddharth R Shigehalli, Manish Bharat, Ritesh Dash, Bhabasis Mohapatra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Tomato leaf disease classification is critical challenge in modern precision agriculture where nearly and accurate prediction can significantly enhance the crop health and yield. This steady presents a CNN based classification model designed to detect and categorize tomato leaf disease using an automated approach. The methodology involved systematic collection of modular data set, processing trough augmented procession and thereby validating the model with the testing data which is 20% of the classical data set considered for the analysis. 6layer architecture CNN model with leaky-ReLu activation function has been used. Different statistical parameters such as ROC, AUC, PR, Hyper parameter tuning has been adopted in order to validate the model under extremely adverse conditions. Python 6 with augmented base has been utilized for analysis.

title: Research on Electrochemical Mechanism Modeling and Steady-State Characteristic Simulation of Alkaline Water Electrolysis for Hydrogen Production
authors: Haiying Song, Changhao Guo, Zhifeng Li, Min Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Alkaline water electrolyzers are pivotal to water-electrolysis hydrogen production. In this work, an electrochemical model built in Matlab/Simulink was used to quantify how steady-state performance depends on operating temperature, electrolyte concentration and system pressure. Raising the temperature consistently lowers the cell voltage required at a given current density. As the KOH concentration is progressively increased, the cell voltage first declines to a minimum that coincides with the electrolyte's optimal conductivity and then increases again;consequently, faradaic efficiency peaks around 30 wt %, whereas overly dilute or concentrated solutions both incur penalties. Elevating pressure slightly increases the operating voltage yet markedly improves overall electrolysis efficiency. The simulations elucidate the mechanistic links between these parameters and both the polarization curve and energy efficiency, these findings were subsequently tested with an orthogonal experimental design that targeted the optimal ranges of the three variables; measured performance agreed closely with the model predictions, providing a theoretical basis for the optimal design and high-performance operation of alkaline water electrolyzers.

title: Optimizing Climate Condition Prediction Using Q-Learning, Deep Q-Networks, and Policy Gradient Reinforcement Learning Methods
authors: Sivanesan N, Thankaraj A, Satheesh S, John Peter V, Satheesh Kumar J, Uma Devi M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Future climate condition predictions contribute essential functions to environmental supervision and agricultural resource planning and disaster response preparation. The study uses reinforcement learning methods with artificial intelligence progress to develop next-day climate event forecasting as a sequential decision problem. A predictive model receives rewards through accuracy metrics that rate its predictions of following-day rainfall based on historical temperature and humidity and rainfall data. The Indonesia Climate Dataset receives evaluations through implementation of Q-learning and Deep Q-Networks (DQN) and Policy Gradient method (REINFORCE). Q-learning implements a tabular method yet DQN makes action-value approximations using deep neural structures and REINFORCE optimizes the policy directly. The evaluation includes both classification metrics like accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score and reinforcement learning metrics that measure cumulative reward and average reward per episode. The experimental outcome reveals that DQN delivers optimal prediction results by accumulating more rewards than its competitors Q-learning and reinforce.

title: Multimachine Hydro Turbine Governor Controller for Small Signal Stability Enhancement of Renewable Penetrated Power System
authors: Samarjeet Satapathy, Rajeswari Behera, Satya Narayan Barik, Sanghamitra Behera, Jyoti Ranjan Sahoo, Sangram Keshari Sahoo, Narayan Nahak, Akshaya Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: For multimachine power system, maintaining stability is an important challenge brought by intermittency in renewable sources integration. This work presents an efficient damping torque implementation by hydro governors to improve stability of power system. The perturbation taken here is step and discrete integration of solar & wind sources. Solar increased by 0.3 pu and wind increased by 0.5 pu step raised and the settling time decreased by 75 %. The case study consists up of two areas four machine system with four hydro governors and 39-bus system with multiple hydro governors imparting simultaneously damping torque to oscillations brought by solar and wind source penetrations. To optimize the governor gains, a multi objective sand-cat swarm optimization (SCO) has been implemented. The SCO is less complex and more efficient because it employs only pre optimizing parameter. The SSO has been contrasted with PSO and DE algorithms. The case studies reveal that, the hydro governor can be employed efficiently to handle oscillation in power system for stability improvement, if their gains are tuned efficiently like SCO algorithm.

title: Triple-Band Ultrawideband Antenna for Multi-Standard Wireless Applications
authors: Vidushi Sharma, Neeraj Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: This work describes the design and assessment of a novel triple-band ultrawideband (UWB) antenna that can cover communications in S-band, C-band, and X-band, including 4G/5G, Wi-Fi, and radar systems. Aside from the 2-3.7 GHz band, the antenna can operate between 5.2 and 6.4 GHz and 7.75 and 10.8 GHz. Across all bands, the proposed antenna achieves maximum gains of 4 dBi at 3.6 GHz, 3.5 dBi at 5.7 GHz, and 5.7 dBi at 9.6 GHz, indicative of strong performance. At 2.45 GHz, the antenna reaches its maximum radiation efficiency of 98%, and it maintains efficiencies above 75% across all bands it operates in. Utilizing multi-resonant structures, the design attains ultrawideband qualities across all bands. These bands have fractional bandwidths of 59.65%, 20.69 %, and 32.88 %, respectively. Incorporating modern wireless communication systems, Internet of Things devices, and satellites-this antenna's compact size, strong gain, and exceptional radiation efficiency make it ideal.

title: Comparative Analysis of Various Deep Learning Models for Image Deblurring
authors: Rabbuni Rajakumari P, Ashish Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Image deblurring is a crucial part of computer vision. It restores the latent, sharp original images from the blurred or distorted images. Recently deep learning algorithms have gained popularity among researchers compared to conventional deblurring techniques. However, for real-world outdoor images, the quality of the deblurred image is one of the major concerns in these deep learning algorithms. This work investigates the performance of all state-of-the-art deep learning models on real-world outdoor scenes. we consider four deep learning–based models, such as U-Net, Res-UNet, MPRNet, and DeblurGAN in this work and evaluate their performance using two widely utilized measures: Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM). Among all these models, Res-UNet achieved the best results with a PSNR of 29.40 and an SSIM of 0.92. all these models were tested on the Make3D dataset, which contains real-world outdoor scenes, to demonstrate the model's effectiveness in deblurring tasks.

title: Transformer-Based Multimodal Fall Detection Using VARISHTA-MM15 Dataset
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: With the world population aging so rapidly, fall detection and activity monitoring have become increasingly important areas for research. Multimodal Human Activity Recognition (HAR) approaches may provide a potential solution by integrating wearable inertial sensor monitoring with visual signals to achieve the integration of motion and spatial information processing. We propose a fully implemented and benchmarked implementation of multimodal anomaly detection using the newly developed VARISHTA-MM15 dataset. The data set consists of synchronized inertial and video recordings from 15 older people (aged 60 and older), conducting a diverse selection of Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) and simulated fall events under controlled conditions. This data set includes synchronized data preprocessing, modality alignment, windowed feature extraction, and early fusion to create a single flow. We evaluated three transformerbased baselines: (i) an Unsupervised Multimodal Autoencoder (UMMAE), (ii) a Perceiver IO model for heterogeneous sen-sor-video fusion, and (iii) a Cross-modal Autoencoder (Cross-AE) for robust representation under missing or occluded modality environments. A subject-wise evaluation protocol is used, enabling generalization to unseen participants. The data results show that Cross-AE significantly exceeds unimodal baselines, leading to the best ROC-AUC and F1 scores of all the datasets tested. In addition, convergence to multimodal fusion-based methods for training and validation loss curves is significantly faster than unimodal methods. The VARISHTA-MM15 dataset, along with opensource code and alignment protocols, provides a reproducible basis for advanced deep anomaly detection research in the elderly health monitoring space.

title: Comparative Analysis of Single, Dual, and Triple Phase-Shift Control Strategies for Dual Active Bridge DC-DC Converters
authors: Jajjalya Bikash Kashyap, E S N Raju P
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The dual active bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter has emerged as a key topology for bidirectional power conversion in solid-state transformers, renewable energy interfaces, and electric vehicle charging systems. Owing to its symmetrical structure, galvanic isolation, and high-frequency operation, it is well suited for medium- and high-power applications. The converter's performance is strongly influenced by the modulation technique, which governs power transfer capability, current stress, and soft-switching behavior. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of three phase-shift control strategies such as single phase shift (SPS), dual phase shift (DPS), and triple phase shift (TPS). Analytical models have been developed for each control method to characterize their switching dynamics and power transfer mechanisms. Simulation studies have been conducted under identical operating conditions to evaluate variations in zero voltage switching (ZVS) range, current stress, and conversion efficiency. The results demonstrate that while SPS offers simplicity, DPS enhances soft-switching and current optimization, and TPS achieves superior efficiency and control flexibility. The presented comparison provides valuable insight into selecting an appropriate phase-shift control strategy for high-performance DAB converters in advanced power conversion systems.

title: Formal Modeling of Staking and Validator Creation in PoS System
authors: Raeesa Mukhtar, Nazir Ahmad Zafar, Hamra Afzaal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Blockchain Technology has presented a tamperproof and secure structure that supports the modern digital environment. Among the many consensus blockchain systems, the Proof of Stake (PoS) has emerged as an extensively used protocol due to its lower energy consumption and high security. However, the staking mechanism and creation of the validator process are the main parts of the PoS network, and any flaw in these processes can cause financial loss and breach the system's security. Therefore, we use formal verification methods for the verification and validation of the staking mechanism. In this study, we presented a formal model of staking processes in a PoS-based system, emphasizing the verification of accurate signer, stake allocation, creation of a validator with an NFT minted, and update timeline. The CSP\# language is used to specify these processes, and Linear Temporal Logics (LTL) are used to define the properties against these specifications. To verify that the system satisfies the critical properties, like verification of an authentic signer, secure stake registration, and deadlock-free validator creation using the Process Analysis Toolkit (PAT) model checker, and ensure the system's liveness and safety. Our results describe the importance of formal verification in enhancing the Polygon Blockchain's integrity by ensuring the staking mechanism in the stake manager contract is secure and reliable.

title: Simulation and Optimization of an Intelligent Sorting System for Industrial Robots Based on Digital Twin and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yunshuo Gai
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: To meet urgent demands for flexible sorting in intelligent manufacturing, this study explores an innovative solution combining digital twin technology with deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Using MATLAB/Simulink, we developed a high-fidelity virtual simulation accurately replicating dynamic behaviors of six-degree-of-freedom industrial robots, conveyor belts, and vision sensors. Unlike traditional path planning relying on pre-programmed trajectories or precise physical models, our approach employs the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm [1], enabling the control agent to autonomously learn optimal grasping strategies through environmental interaction. Simulation results demonstrate that the fully trained controller efficiently accomplishes dynamic target sorting tasks, achieving an average success rate exceeding 94%, while exhibiting satisfactory stability when handling uncertainties like conveyor belt speed fluctuations and sensor measurement noise. This method's key advantage is avoiding explicit solutions to complex robotic kinematic models, offering a promising technical pathway for constructing adaptive control systems integrating perception, decision-making, and execution.

title: Predicting Clinical Trial Site Performance using Machine Learning
authors: Sumanth Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Success of clinical trials depends extensively on the operational performance of the participating site, yet early detection of underperforming sites remains an ongoing challenge. In this work, the authors are presenting a machine learning-based framework for predicting the performance of clinical trial sites from historical operational and enrollmentrelated indicators. For early risk identification and data-driven support for decision-making in a trial management team, several supervised learning models have been evaluated to classify site performance outcomes. Experimental results showed that ensemble-based models significantly outperform traditional baselines regarding prediction accuracy and robustness. The proposed approach emphasizes the potential of artificial intelligence to improve trial efficiency by mitigating operational risk and enabling proactive planning of clinical trials.

title: Control-Based Simulation of COPD Physiology using Adaptive Solvers for Digital Health Applications
authors: Anju Saini, Shazmani, Kalpana Chauhan, Sarita
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this study, a control-theoretic framework for simulating COPD respiratory physiology was proposed. The proposed framework utilizes adaptive numerical solvers for simulating the disease's effects on the body. It also has the potential for developing new digital health applications. A nonlinear pulmonary model was used for simulating the effects of airflow limitation in the lungs, which is a major characteristic of COPD. The dynamic control of alveolar ventilation was achieved by adjusting the inspiratory pressure based on the difference between the simulated oxygen saturation signal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\Delta \text{SO}_{2}\right)$</tex> by a proportional controller. The default numerical solver for MATLAB's integration routine, ODE45, was used for ensuring the stability of the simulated results. For simulated desaturation episodes, the proposed method has shown a qualitatively consistent response for ventilatory adjustments. This is due to the numerical stability of the proposed method. It is a control-oriented method that has the potential for being a fundamental component for the development of digital twins for COPD management. It utilizes computer modeling and remote sensing for personalizing disease management.

title: Design and Development of a Low-Cost Portable Potentiostat for Screen Printed Electrodes
authors: RajithKumar B K, Navya Singh, Sphatika S Jain, V Megha, Shreya Hanimanal, Priyal Khandelwal
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This work presents the design and development of a low-cost, portable potentiostat to be used for flexible electrochemical sensing applications. The system is built using operational amplifiers for precise potential control and current measurement. It uses a Blue Pill (STM32F103C8T6) microcontroller, which provides 12-bit digital waveform generation while maintaining low power consumption and small size. The entire circuit was simulated and validated prior to integration. The potentiostat was evaluated using cyclic voltammetry (CV) in a standard redox electrolyte <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(5 \text{mM} \mathrm{K}_{4}\left[\text{Fe}(\text{CN})_{6}\right] in 0.1 M KCl\right)$</tex>, scanned from −0.6 V to +0.8 V at scan rates of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 - 100 \text{mV} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The device produced clear and well-defined redox peaks, demonstrating proper control of the working electrode potential and accurate current detection. The measured response showed an anodic peak at approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+ 0.20 \mathrm{V}$</tex> and a cathodic peak at around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-0. 1 9 \text{Vvs}$</tex>. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ag}/\text{AgCl}$</tex> with a peak separation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \text{Ep}$</tex>) of approximately 390 mV. Overall, the results confirm that the potentiostat achieves reasonable sensitivity, reproducibility and stable electrochemical performance, making it suitable for applications such as point-of-care diagnostics, environmental monitoring and biomolecule detection. This contributes to affordable and accessible analytical tools aligned with SDG 11 for sustainable communities.

title: Implementation of MPPT Algorithms Control for Grid-Connected PV Inverter
authors: Arpita Panda, Kumkum Mallick, Souvik Mondal, Riju Nandi, Arnab Ghosh
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increased need for sustainable energy sources around the world has led to a faster adoption of photovoltaic (PV) systems into the main power grid. This paper presents the development and simulation of a grid-tied photovoltaic (PV) system with maximum power point tracking (MPPT) capabilities, utilizing the MATLAB/Simulink platform. The model gives a full picture of important parts of the system, like the PV array, MPPT controller, Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) generator, and Phase-Locked Loop (PLL) mechanism. In this single-stage setup, the PV array goes straight to a DC-AC inverter. To accomplish real-time tracking of the maximum power under dynamic irradiance and temperature conditions, MPPT is applied by dynamically controlling the inverter's output current reference. By controlling the inverter, the DC power from the PV array may be regulated into high-quality electricity, with the output current phase-and frequency-synchronized with the grid voltage. PLL is essential in ensuring precise phase tracking, hence, improving grid compliance and power quality. The system represents the performance of an actual PV plant and proves the viability of smart control methods for enhancing efficiency and reliability in renewable energy systems.

title: An Embedded Vision-Based Haptic System to Assist the Visually Impaired Using Kinect Sensor and NVIDIA Jetson
authors: Karthikeyan G, Samuel Venkatesan, Mothesh K, Nakshathra N, Anania Maman M, Kaushikram M, Venkata Ramana Rao S, Mahiban Lindsay N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Millions of individuals suffer from restricted mobility and freedom due to visual impairment, making it a major global public health concern. Conventional assistive devices, such as the long cane, have limitations; they can only detect impediments at ground level within a short distance and do not provide protection from head-level threats or object classification. In order to provide complete ambient awareness through haptic feedback, this research suggests a revolutionary wearable assistive device that makes advantage of The Microsoft Kinect sensor. The recommended method uses a MS Kinect sensor model paired with a Nvidia Jetson Nano processing unit to capture and process the real time 3D data of the user's surroundings. It uses a prebuilt AI model which detects objects like vehicles, trees and humans. This integrated data, which determines both what an object is and how far away it is, is translated into distinct, pre-defined vibratory patterns. These patterns are transmitted to the user via a wearable bracelet fitted with an Adafruit DRV2605L haptic motor controller, delivering intuitive, non-visual cues about the environment.

title: Integrating Explainability, Fairness, and Privacy in AI Powered Clinical Decision Systems
authors: Anshul, Aleem Ali
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is now part of the current healthcare systems, especially clinical decision support systems as well as diagnostic imaging. Despite the high diagnostic accuracy of deep learning models, their practical implementation is still restricted because of issues that are connected to interpretability, algorithmic bias, data privacy, and accountability. The paper explores how the principles of ethically oriented AI, i.e., explainability, fairness, and privacy can be applied to the AI-based clinical decision-making systems. Explainable AI methods are also used to increase the transparency of the model and allow clinicians to understand the diagnostic predictions with visual and featurebased explanations. Learning strategies that are fairnessconscious are integrated to prevent demographic bias and achieve equal diagnostic performance between different groups of patients. Besides that, a privacy preserving federated learning is also examined to allow collaborative model training without a central storage of information, enabling the preservation of sensitive patient data. The results of experimental assessment based on publicly available medical imaging datasets indicate that ethically enhanced AI models are competitive in the diagnostic accuracy, and they are also expected to significantly enhance their interpretability, fairness, and data security. The results indicate that to foster trustful, open, and clinically credible decision support systems within health facilities, the ethical concerns of AI systems need to be directly incorporated into the design of such systems.

title: Virangana: Advanced Women's Safety Application
authors: Maithili Kohale, Drishti Sharma, Ritesh Bobade, Shrushti Pohankar, Yashaswi Khobragade, Rutuja Chirwatkar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: We introduce Virangana, an advanced, featurerich mobile application designed to enhance women's safety. It provides features like the panic/SOS button and location sharing, along with advanced features such as fake calling, voice-assisted SOS alerts, safe route suggestions and zone highlighting, and a proactive safety monitor on the dashboard. Our app uses rulebased machine learning on user context parameters to calculate safety scores for each zone on the map. These safety scores are used to indicate the safety level on the proactive monitor and to efficiently suggest safe routes using the A* search algorithm. The app is intuitive to use and has been developed using agile methodologies. It is implemented using Dart and Flutter to support cross-platform deployment. Through comparison with existing methods, we demonstrate that our app outperforms all existing methods and contributes to enhancing women's safety.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Approach to Real-Time Traffic Prediction using Large Language Models: Model Performance and Scalability Challenges
authors: Chandra Nikitha Shatdarsanam, Srinivasa Rao Kurakula, Satyam Sheshansh, Balaji Salem Balasundram, Riaz Ahmed Mohammed Sait, Sivakoteswararao Katta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Semantic and accurate real-time contextdependent traffic prediction is essential to develop smart city intelligent transportation systems, which can be used to improve route plan, decrease traffic jams and rapid response of emergency. Despite the partial success of classical machine learning and conventional deep learning paradigms, they failed to accurately model the complex traffic data with spatial and temporal patterns. In this paper, we introduce an innovative machine learning-based framework that exploits sequence modeling of large language models (LLMs) including fine-tuned GPT and LLaMA models on real-time traffic prediction. The framework represents multi-source traffic features to tokenized sequences, which enables LLMs to exploit long-range temporal dependencies and cross-location traffic flow interactions. We perform extensive experiments on the benchmark datasets METR-LA, PEMS-BAY, and NGSIM, and compare the LLMbased method with classical models, such as SVM and LSTM, as well as the recently developed transformer-based architectures. We demonstrate that LLMs provide substantial improvement in predictive accuracy and out-of-sample consistency compared to baselines in terms of RMSE and MAE metrics. But we also emphasize considerable drawbacks, especially about latency, computational overhead, and scalability for the edge placement. The paper concludes with potential and future research directions on optimizing the models, tokenization methods, as well as largescale LLM architectures that can be tailored for intelligent traffic systems.

title: A Spatiotemporal CNN-LSTM Framework for Forecasting Crop Disease Progression
authors: Shashank Sahu, Sunny Teotia, Yash Jain, Ritwik Goswami, Santosh Kumar Upadhyay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The majority of deep learning methods used in plant pathology are based on the classification of the static images and do not consider the way diseases change with time. This limitation impairs premature diagnosis and accurate severity determination, in particular in the field, dynamic farm conditions. To overcome this, we suggest a spatiotemporal deep learning model that brings together a convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for image extraction with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network on temporal sequence modeling. This hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture studies the consecutive images in order to capture the progression of disease symptoms. Real time crop data are available only in small datasets, and time-varying datasets do not involve real-time. With scarcity, we present a synthetic sequence generation approach that simulates the disease development out of still pictures with alpha blending. The individual frames are run in a pre-trained inceptionV3 model to generate strong feature vectors, which the LSTM then use to learn the temporal patterns. The proposed approach is demonstrated to work with the publicly available rice leaf disease dataset constantly achieves higher performance than a state-of-the-art static CNN baseline in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and f1-score. By incorporating temporal information, this framework offers a more credible and informative instrument of predictive disease surveillance in precision agriculture.

title: An Investigation into the Influence of Head Movements on Fundamental Eye-Gaze Directions Using Mediapipe-Based Mesh of Intersection Point and Head Pose Estimation
authors: Abdul Rehman, Muhammad Syaiful Amri bin Suhaimi, Ooi Joo On, Sayed Ahmad Zikri Bin Sayed Aluwee, Pringgo Widyo Laksono
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Eye-gaze tracking is one of the most vital parameters for understanding human behavior, cognitive processes, and visual perception in computer vision. It has wide applications in real-world scenarios for behavior analysis, particularly in tracking the real-time attentiveness of students in online learning. However, most existing research overlooks the impact of head movements on gaze accuracy, despite it being a frequent and unavoidable behavior during real-time interactions such as online learning. This gap highlights a critical limitation in current attentiveness monitoring systems, which often assume a static head position and fail to account for how head pose affects gaze direction. To explore this in this research we used Mesh of Intersection Point method for eye-gaze tracking, a featurebased method suitable for real-time analysis, offering reliable results in detecting and tracking eye using laptop's webcam to analyze the head movement behavior. The study is carried out in two phases. In the first phase, we analyze the accuracy drop in eye-gaze tracking caused by head movements across five reference direction points on a laptop screen. In the second phase, separate machine learning classifiers are trained for each gaze direction, for allowing horizontal and vertical head movement for each direction. This research investigates how head movements influence eye-gaze accuracy and uses machine learning to enable gaze direction classification even when head movements occur.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Intelligent Control Strategies for AVR Performance
authors: Muhammad Shehzore Hashmi, Muhammad Shahjahan Hashmi, Zahid Muhammad
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This paper conducts a comprehensive comparative evaluation of Automatic Voltage Regulator (AVR) models for thermal and hydro power systems, focusing on the performance evaluation of conventional Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) and intelligent control strategies, namely Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Fuzzy Logic Controller (FLC), and Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS). The AVR models are meticulously designed in MATLAB/SIMULINK, employing transfer functions to model critical power system components such as the amplifier, exciter, generator, and sensor. Each controller is integrated to regulate the terminal voltage under dynamic conditions. Key performance measures are the settling time, rise time, and overshoot, which are thoroughly examined to identify the effectiveness of the controller. The outputs show that FLC performs better with the least overshoot (0.0000038) and settling time (1.9421s), closely surpassed by ANFIS, where it has an overshoot of 0.00033 and a settling time of 2.2635s. Comparatively, the tuned PID controller and ANN have more overshoot and longer settling time. This paper highlights why intelligent controllers can be transformative in modern voltage regulation of complex power systems, which gives a solid and scalable theoretical framework to enable both industrial and academic research on the state-of-the-art advances in the area of advanced power system control.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Consent-Based Security for Multi-Face Digital Images Captured in Public Areas
authors: Vivek Kumar Sharma, Renuka Arora, Chandra Shekhar Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Today, digital images are captured everywhere through mobile phones, CCTV cameras, and public surveillance systems. When someone takes a picture in a public place, many other people often appear in the background without knowing it. These bystanders may not want their faces to be captured or shared, but they have no control over where these images go. This creates serious privacy and safety concerns in real-life situations.
