For example, a stranger taking a photo in a mall or park may unknowingly capture several other people, and these images can be uploaded online without their permission. CCTV systems also record thousands of people daily, and these images may be accessed or misused without their consent. To solve this real life problem, this paper proposes a blockchain enabled, consent-based security system for digital images containing multiple faces. In our method, every face in an image is detected automatically and encrypted separately using a fast and secure ChaCha20 encryption algorithm. This means no one's face is visible unless they give permission. When someone wants to view another person's face in the image, a consent request is sent to that person. The person can verify their identity using a simple Aadhaar-style biometric and OTP check, and then approve or reject the request. To protect the faces of individuals in digital images, user-submitted approval and rejection requests are recorded on the blockchain, eliminating any possibility of tampering. Implementation results demonstrate that the proposed framework displays only the faces of individuals who have provided their permission, while the faces of all other individuals remain securely encrypted. During the experiments, it was found that face detection yielded results with 96.4% accuracy. The blockchain-enabled consent framework prevented unauthorized access with 100% effectiveness. The proposed solution effectively preserves the privacy of individuals in digital images captured in public spaces and protects individuals from misuse of technology in society.

title: A Method for Product Rating Improvement Using NLP and Sentiment Analysis
authors: Prateek Gupta, Rajendra Kumar Gupta, Akhilesh Tiwari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The advent of web 2.0 and 3.0 has popularized online shopping applications. E-commerce application provides user generated contents to customers and business organizations. Star rating and review sentiment analysis are important integral component of every e-commerce application. They are used for product decision making process. Reliability of rating system reduces due to mismatch between user provided ratings & sentiment analysis of reviews. Platform specific ratings, rating biases & fake reviews are important issues that need to be solved. To address some of these issues, the presented work has introduced a method for product rating improvement using sentiment analysis of reviews based on natural language processing (NLP). Sentiments from text reviews are calculated by VADER and TextBlob and SentiWordNet. Then sentiment score of them are averaged to get improved sentiment score. The improved sentiment score are utilized to optimize rating. The proposed method produces a more reliable, consistent and trustworthy rating. After that there exists no need to look on individual sentiments. Data under research is collected from Flipkart and Amazon. The result of experimentation shows that combining sentiment adjustments in rating leads to more trusted and reliable ratings, thereby providing help to consumers making better purchase decisions.

title: Fuzzy Slack Enhanced Graph Random Vector Functional Link Based on Multi-View Learning
authors: Reetu Shrivastava, Bhagat Singh Raghuwanshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Machine learning models in real-world datasets tend to give biased predictions. Therefore, it is important to use evaluation metrics other than accuracy, such as precision, recall, F1-score, G-means, and AUC. Conventional backpropagationbased neural networks face problems such as slow convergence, stuckness at local minima, and learning rate sensitivity, while randomization based architectures such as Random Vector Functional Link (RVFL) provide fast training and universal approximation capability. Multi-View Learning (MVL) improves performance by using complementary data representations. Building on all of these, Graph Random Vector Functional Link based on Multi-View Learning (GRVFL-MV) gives strong results but degrades under noise, outliers, and imbalance. To address these limitations, this work proposes Fuzzy Slack-Enhanced GRVFLMV (FSE-GRVFL-MV), which integrates fuzzy membership weighting, leaky ReLU activation, Xavier initialization, adaptive regularization, and weighted ensemble prediction. Experimental validation on multiple imbalanced datasets shows that FSE-GRVFL-MV significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in various metrics, especially in scenarios with highclass imbalance, noise, and outliers, and is also computationally efficient, making it suitable for practical applications where robustly handling imbalanced data is essential.

title: Smart System for Project Allocation and Mapping with Faculty Recommendation and Transformer-Based Report Summarization: A Proposed Approach
authors: Sankalp Goel, Anshika Singh, Kanika Singla, Sushant Jhingran
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Academic project management in the universities is usually an ineffective and opaque exercise leading to wrong mapping of the faculty and students, asymmetrical evaluation, and delays in project execution. Systems that exist today either consider project allocation or faculty recommendation or report summarization, but they certainly do not offer the entire integrated package. This paper proposes and designs a system that automates the entire academic project life cycle, including AI-driven modules for domain-based faculty recommendation, student auto-grouping, evaluation panel allocation, and transformer-based project report summarization. In opposition to earlier attempts, ours is the only approach that combined more than one AI technique-semantic similarity matching, clustering, and abstractive summarization. By leveraging institutional datasets of academic projects combined with the faculty's research data from Google Scholar and ORCID, the system guarantees fair allocation, reduces faculty workload, and shortens evaluation time. This integration creates transparency, optimizes mentor matching, and shortens project assessment time drastically, providing a scalable framework for automating academic processes.

title: Research and System Implementation of LoRa Technology for Intelligent Lighting in LED Street Lamp Power Control
authors: Yunfang Zhong, Shengyu Gan, Jinhui Wang, Huixiong Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Addressing the limitations of traditional LED streetlight control systems employing AC power line carrier communication—where signal transmission relies on AC cables and is susceptible to interference from external electrical equipment, resulting in poor anti-interference capabilities and low signal transmission accuracy—this paper proposes an optimised LED streetlight control solution based on LoRa communication technology. Firstly, an overall block diagram of the LoRa control system is established, comprising a LoRa gateway, terminal control module, and LED driver unit, clearly defining the functionalities and signal interaction logic of each module. Secondly, incorporating spread-spectrum communication theory, a LoRa signal transmission model is established to analyse its antiinterference mechanisms and long-range transmission characteristics. Based on this, an LED streetlight control circuit integrating EMI filtering, AC/DC conversion, constant-current control, and a LoRa communication chip is constructed. Finally, field testing validates the system's performance, comparing signal reception success rates and transmission stability under various operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the designed LoRa-based LED streetlight power supply achieves 100% signal transmission accuracy at standard 33-metre streetlight intervals, with an effective transmission range spanning 2 kilometres. Compared to traditional power line carrier control, interference resistance improves by over 40%, significantly optimising the reliability and stability of LED streetlight control systems.

title: Adaptive Machine Learning for Energy-Efficient Resource Allocation in 6G Networks
authors: Rashmi P Sonar, Priyanka Abhijit Deshmukh, Kaveri A. Katake, Yogesh P. Khadse, Krunal S. Panpaliya, Vikramsingh R. Parihar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: The advent of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks requires the use of smart and energy-aware systems that can help in the efficient management of communication and computational resources. In this paper, an Adaptive Machine Learning (AML) framework is suggested to allocate resources in dynamic 6G environments economically and with reduced energy consumption. The framework is a combination of multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) and federated adaptive coordination, which is used to coordinate distributed base stations to jointly optimize power, bandwidth, and computation resources in real time. An energy-efficiency maximization problem is formulated, where transmit power constraints, bandwidth, and quality-of-service (QoS) requirements are taken into account. The AML algorithm is trained to learn near-optimal allocation policies in response to environmental interactions, retraining, and selftuning of parameter changes, so that the algorithm can be resilient under varying traffic loads and mobility patterns. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed AML framework outperforms static and heuristic benchmarks, achieving up to 35% improvement in energy efficiency, faster convergence, and higher QoS satisfaction. The findings validate AML as a viable, scalable, and sustainable approach for intelligent 6 G resource management, enabling autonomous, energy-aware, and selfoptimizing communication systems.

title: Research on Full-Condition Model Construction and Operation Strategy for Multi-Energy Complementary Integrated Energy Systems Based on Source-Loadstorage Coordination
authors: Hongyi Li, Yucheng Jiang, Weijun Zhong, Lipeng Wang, Wei Xue
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the coordination issue of the fluctuation of renewable energy and the spatiotemporal coupling of load demand in multi-energy complementary integrated energy systems, a full-condition operation optimization strategy based on “source-load-storage synergy” is established. By dynamically matching photovoltaic (PV) systems, heat pumps, energy storage devices, and other equipment, and leveraging time-of-use (TOU) electricity price incentives, the balance between energy supply and multi-energy load is coordinated. Refined mathematical models of PV power generation, air-source/geothermal heat pumps, and heat/electricity storage devices are constructed. By integrating the time-of-use electricity pricing policy in northwest China, a phased operation strategy of “peak discharging and off-peak charging” is proposed to achieve load shifting and efficient utilization of renewable energy. Based on hourly simulation throughout the year, the system's performance is verified from three dimensions: thermal performance, environmental benefits, and economic viability. The case study indicates that the system's primary energy utilization rate reaches 78.51 %, which is a 29.04 % increase compared to traditional separate supply systems. It also achieves an annual <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emission reduction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,905.19$</tex> tons. The energy storage arbitrage profit amounts to 254,400 yuan, and the investment payback period is shortened to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 0 9}$</tex> years, with a PV energy consumption rate exceeding 85%.

title: Optimization of Machine Equipment Fault Prediction Model Based on Ensemble Learning
authors: Jiaquan Dai, Jianzhi Chen, Gongxing Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper is based on the design and development of models using ensemble learning for fault prediction in mechanical equipment. It addresses the issues of imbalanced equipment datasets and the scarcity of data for faulty machines by oversampling the fault data to increase the amount of data for malfunctioning machines. By incorporating a cost function, the model introduces probability estimation and misclassification costs in the classification process, reclassifying the outcomes. Finally, the model is evaluated using ROC curves and confusion matrix metrics to reduce classification errors. The established model provides reliable support for the timeliness of machine maintenance.

title: Application of Ultra-Wideband to a Cut-to-Length Forest Harvester for Decimeter-Level Positioning Under Forest Canopies
authors: Zuoya Liu, Heikki Hyyti, Harri Kaartinen, Tamás Faitli, Matias Mäki-Leppilampi, Antero Kukko, Juha Hyyppä
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: In this paper, we present an ultra-wideband (UWB)based method for locating a cut-to-length forest harvester (CTL-FH) under forest canopies with decimeter-level accuracy. The proposed method involves placing and fixing several UWB beacons around the area of operation and one UWB receiver on the top of the harvester. This work is the first in the literature to integrate UWB positioning into a CTL-FH, which achieves decimeter-level accuracy, addresses the unavailability of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) in positioning the forest harvester under forest canopies, and provides a real-time positioning solution for forest machines. The experimental results show that the proposed method can accurately and continuously locate the forest harvester under forest canopies with a root-mean-square error (RMSE) of 0.28 cm, providing a strong foundation and reference for the future applications of forest harvesters, such as individual tree-based harvest plan.

title: A Neural Algorithm Approach to Turbine Floor Noise Prediction Based on TabPFN
authors: Haitian Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of noise signals is of great significance for evaluating the operation of hydroelectric generating units. This study conducted a comparative analysis using the Random Forest (RF) and Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN) algorithms, and established a turbine floor noise regression model based on rotational speed, guide vane opening, and active power. The results show that both models can describe the overall changing trend of noise. However, RF has problems such as response lag, overreaction and low stability of multiple operation results when facing rapidly changing working conditions. In contrast, TabPFN has demonstrated its advantages in terms of fitting accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.994$</tex>), tracking ability, and stability in multiple modeling, showing its potential in the noise prediction task of hydroelectric generating units. In addition, sensitivity tests show that TabPFN has significantly stronger robustness against input disturbances. When the operating condition variables are subject to small random disturbances, its prediction results can still remain stable. However, as a neural model, TabPFN has insufficient interpretability. Future research may consider integrating physical principles or explainable learning methods into models to enhance their interpretability.

title: Advancing Repurchase Prediction in E-Commerce: RFM-Driven Explainable Hybrid Machine Learning Paradigm
authors: Lovleen Kaur, Paurav Goel, Sonam Khattar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The intense market competition brought about by the fast growth of e-commerce has resulted in the importance of customer retention and proper prediction of repurchase to business success. Well-functioning forecasting allows improving marketing, provides personal approach, and helps to build relationships with customers. Although recent hybrid and ensemble machine learning models have high predictive power, they cannot provide transparency and trust in business decisions due to being black-box models. Explainable Artificial Intelligence can overcome this issue, as it reveals such crucial elements of behavior that determine repurchase, like Recency, Frequency, and Monetary (RFM) variables. This is a systematic exploration of explainable and hybrid machine learning methods to predict repurchases, which focuses on methods, data, performance metrics, and explainability methods. The study will contribute to the improvement of explainable hybrid learning models by combining the RFM knowledge with the XAI techniques to achieve an effective balance between predictive accuracy and interpretability.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)

title: Closed Loop PI and Sliding Mode Control of PV-Wind Driven BBCCI-Quadratic Boost Converter for Motor Applications with Auxiliary Battery Charging
authors: D Vanitha, V. Malathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid PV-wind energy system for dynamic motor loads and battery charging, utilizing a dual-stage converter: a Buck-Boost Converter with Coupled Inductor (BBCCI) cascaded with a Quadratic Boost Converter (QBC). This topology achieves high voltage gain and stable operation despite fluctuating renewable inputs. The closed-loop performance is comparatively evaluated using ProportionalIntegral (PI) and Sliding Mode (SM) controllers. The SM controller demonstrates superior robustness against disturbances and parameter variations. Simulation results confirm that the SM-controlled system achieves faster transient response, lower overshoot, and improved stability under dynamic loading than the PI-controlled system. Due to high component costs, closed-loop hardware validation was not conducted in this work, but is intended for future investigation; the closed-loop control comparison was confirmed solely through detailed simulation.

title: Navigating Artificial Intelligence Adoption Decisions in Entrepreneurial Ventures: Insights from the Extended Technology-Organization-Environment Framework
authors: K.K. Shihab, Arif Hasan, Shubhangi Vitthal Gaikwad, Mini Agrawal, Sonal Sharma, Sandeep Raghuwanshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This study investigates factors that influence how entrepreneurs in India are adopting artificial intelligence. 462 entrepreneurs belonging to different industries such as service, trade and manufacturing sectors were approached. Exploratory factor analysis-(EFA), confirmatory factor analysis-(CFA) along with structural equation modeling-(SEM) techniques were used for data analysis through SPSS (v22) and AMOS (v24). The results show that the organizational preparedness, technological commitment and external pressure play a major role in driving AI adoption whereas, sub-factors perceived ease of use and usefulness have minimal influence. These findings suggest that entrepreneurs need to pay more attention to organization and external market dynamics, rather than simple technological interfaces. This study also contributes to the challenges faced in AI adoption, especially by extending and validating the TOE-TAM framework within an entrepreneurial set-up. These insights can really help policymakers concerning challenges entrepreneurs faced with AI.

title: Research on Control of a Two-Stage LED Driver Based on LLC Resonant Converter and Power Factor Correction Circuit
authors: Min Yao, Muqin Tian, Junzhao Zhang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a two-stage LED driver circuit, which consists of a front-stage power factor correction (PFC) circuit and a rear-stage LLC resonant converter. The front stage employs a Boost PFC structure based on voltage and current dual-loop control, with feedforward control introduced to improve dynamic performance, achieving a power factor (PF) greater than 0.99. The rear stage utilizes a pulse frequency modulation (PFM) controlled LLC resonant converter to provide constant current output to the LEDs.

title: Optimal Charging Schedule for an Electric Metrobus Fleet Under PV-Grid Integration
authors: Tuna Aykut, Sıtkı Güner, Ümmühan Başaran Filik
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: The electrification of public transit fleets plays a vital role in achieving low-carbon urban mobility. This study presents a Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model to optimize the daily charging schedule of a 100-vehicle electric metrobus (EM) fleet operating on Istanbul's Avcılar-Zincirlikuyu line. The proposed framework integrates photovoltaic (PV) generation and grid interaction to minimize total energy cost while improving grid load factor and reducing carbon emissions. Two scenarios are analyzed: a base case with unmanaged overnight charging and an optimized case with PV-supported scheduling and grid power constraints. The findings highlight the effectiveness of integrated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex>-grid energy management in enhancing both the economic and environmental sustainability of urban transportation systems.

title: Based on XGBoost and SHAP: A Predictive Maintenance Framework for Manufacturing Equipment in CAD/CAM Systems
authors: Muyu Teng, Meifa Huang, Zhemin Tang, Yunkun Jiao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a machine learning-based predictive maintenance framework for manufacturing equipment integrated with computer-aided design and manufacturing (CAD/CAM) systems. Utilizing XGBoost for multi-class failure prediction on sensor data, the model achieves high accuracy in forecasting equipment faults, thereby optimizing design parameters such as tool wear and reducing downtime. The framework incorporates SHAP for interpretability, ensuring transparent decision-making in critical sectors. Experimental results on a synthetic dataset demonstrate an accuracy of 98.65%, weighted F1-score of 98.34%, and AUC of 0.8804, outperforming baselines like Random Forest. Ablation studies confirm the importance of features like tool wear, with performance drops when removed. Visualizations, including confusion matrices, feature importance plots, SHAP summaries, ROC and PR curves, violin plots, and dependence plots, highlight model robustness and key insights into fault mechanisms. This approach aligns with Industry 4.0 principles, promoting sustainable manufacturing through proactive maintenance. Future extensions could incorporate real-time data streams and deep learning for enhanced temporal analysis. The framework's efficiency, with training under 1 second, makes it suitable for real-world CAD/CAM integration.

title: Design of a Model Predictive Control Strategy for Adaptive Cruise Control in Vehicles
authors: Safoin Rahal, Dingli Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: This report presents the design and simulation of a Model Predictive Control (MPC) strategy for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) in vehicles, along with a comparative analysis of this approach against a conventional Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) control method. The study seeks to assess the effectiveness of MPC in maintaining safe following distances while ensuring ride comfort across various dynamic traffic scenarios. A linear state-space model that captures the longitudinal dynamics of an ego vehicle has been developed using MATLAB/Simulink, with both controllers implemented to manage acceleration in response to the behaviour of the lead vehicle. Four distinct simulation scenarios are explored: highway cruising, a lead vehicle exceeding the speed limit, highway traffic conditions, and instances of sudden braking. Performance is assessed using various metrics, including speed tracking, inter-vehicle distance, tracking error, acceleration, jerk, control effort, energy consumption, and ride comfort. The simulation results demonstrate that MPC yields smoother control actions and exhibits better adherence to safety constraints, particularly in rapidly changing conditions. The findings support the potential of MPC as a more robust alternative to PID for real-world ACC systems.

title: Improving the Quality of Monitoring Facilities
authors: Oleg N. Bodin, Oksana E. Bezborodova, Vasily M. Zhigachev, Kristina D. Mishina, Dmitry V. Martinov, Alina O. Kholudeneva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The article substantiates the need to develop and apply automated monitoring systems for objects of various origins. Existing systems reviewed. It is noted that they do not allow real-time adjustment of the object parameters. Adjustment is possible, at best, in the next monitoring cycle, and more often after one or more cycles. Disclosed is a method of monitoring the state of an object based on a set of parameters, which improves the quality of monitoring objects. Distinctive features of proposed method are separation of monitoring cycle into several time intervals, measurement of monitored parameter value at the end of each monitoring interval and its comparison with model value calculated using mathematical model of object. Analysis of mismatch of actual parameter value with expected parameter value makes it possible to generate and implement control action depending on value of mismatch of actual parameter value with expected parameter value. Examples of the method for monitoring various objects are given.

title: Mathematical Model for Information Security Risk Assessment in Electronic Document Management Systems
authors: Irina R. Shekhovtsova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The transition to electronic document management systems (EDMS) has evolved into a missioncritical digital transformation initiative. Ineffective implementation of EDMS and its associated business processes can result in operational disruptions, financial losses, missed deadlines, and reputational damage. This situation necessitates the integration of manageable information flows within business processes into the sphere of information security management procedures. A structured approach is proposed to analyze existing business processes with attention to conducting quantitative risk assessment through modeling. Analysis is performed within the developed decision support system to optimize business processes in the EDMS while considering both system quality and information security requirements.

title: BPEC-EKNN Based Consistency Detection for Energy Storage Systems
authors: Nan Wang, Zhen Li, Xichao Zhou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources such as photovoltaic and wind power in the power grid, the problems of grid security and stability caused by their randomness and volatility are becoming more and more prominent. As a cushion for the volatility of renewable energy, the proportion and importance of energy storage in the power grid is gradually increasing. Recently, problems such as explosions and fires caused by overcharging and over-discharging of energy storage systems have occurred from time to time, seriously affecting the safe and stable operation of energy storage systems and energy systems. The main reason for overcharging and discharging of energy storage systems is the inconsistency of the state of the electric core in the charging and discharging process, which not only affects the safety of the electric core, but also influences the overall charging and discharging capacity of the energy storage system. To address the inconsistency of energy storage cores, this paper proposes an energy storage consistency monitoring method under the framework of clustering-classification, which adopts the BPEC and EKNN classification algorithm to realize the detection and localization of energy storage core consistency anomalies under the consideration of time-series data.

title: Deepfake Detection: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach Using ResNext and LSTM Models
authors: Md Shohel Arman, Md Asaduzzaman, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Deepfake technology, leveraging advancements in deep learning, has become a significant threat to digital media authenticity, enabling the creation of hyper-realistic yet deceptive videos that challenge existing detection methods. This paper presents a hybrid approach combining ResNext Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for frame-level feature extraction and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for analyzing temporal dependencies to improve deepfake detection accuracy. The study utilized a balanced dataset comprising videos from FaceForensic++, Celeb-DF, and custom-crafted deepfakes, with preprocessing steps that included facial region cropping, frame standardization, and noise reduction. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 94.87 %, outperforming existing methods by effectively capturing both static and dynamic video features. Key innovations include leveraging the complementary strengths of CNNs and LSTMs to address frame-level and sequential inconsistencies in fake media. This approach is validated through extensive experimentation, demonstrating robustness against evolving generative adversarial techniques. The results establish a strong foundation for scalable and real-time detection applications, with future work aiming to enhance detection for multi-modal data and improve computational efficiency for deployment in resource-constrained environments.

title: YOLO-RDFN: Steel Surface Defect Detection Model
authors: Baolong You, Li Min, Pengyang Song, Yadong Hao, Yuhan Xu, Xinna You, Shizhe Zheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issues of complex structure, large parameter count, difficulty in detecting multi-scale targets, and poor performance in dense scenarios found in current steel surface defect detection models, this paper proposes a lightweight detection model based on YOLOV11. Firstly, the backbone network of YOLOV11 is replaced with RepGhostNet to achieve lightweight design and improve detection speed. Secondly, an RDFPN model is designed and embedded with the multi-scale dense object enhancement attention mechanism, MdAM. Finally, a multi-scale perception loss function based on distance, DWLIOSS, is designed to enhance detection performance in dense scenes. To further balance model lightweighting and detection accuracy, the modified network model is pruned to trim redundant parameters and compress the model further. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the original YOLOV11 model, the improved model achieves a 9.89% increase in MAP, a 25.88% reduction in parameters, and a 17.68% improvement in detection speed.

title: Operational Maintenance Priorities and Critical Technologies for Converter Transformers in Controllable Line-Commutated Converter Systems
authors: Mingjia Zhou, Hongyu Duan, Hongbo Ji, Long Chen, Wenbin Zhao, Chenxi Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Controllable Line-Commutated Converter (CLCC), as a novel converter technology, effectively resolves commutation failure issues. It was officially commissioned at Shanghai Nanqiao Converter Station in 2021. Due to the integration of IGBT auxiliary branches for forced commutation, CLCC's commutation process involves rapid injection of high-frequency voltage pulses. These steep voltage wavefronts propagate through converter transformer windings, where the coupling effect between distributed capacitance and inductance parameters excites multiconductor transmission line effects. This results in complex superimposed phenomena of standing waves and traveling waves within the winding interlayer, turn-to-turn, and ground insulation structures, thereby exacerbating core vibration and noise and posing long-term operational threats to converter transformers. At Shanghai Nanqiao Converter Station, synchronous measurements of physical quantities such as transformer neutralpoint current and vibration were conducted. The operational status of the converter transformer under CLCC conditions was monitored and compared with conventional LCC operation. This study identifies general patterns of neutral-point current and vibration in CLCC-operated converter transformers and highlights key maintenance priorities for subsequent converter transformer operation.

title: Enhancing FANET Security with Elliptic Curve Cryptography: Protocol Evaluation and Analysis
authors: Sandeep Rai, Anoj Kumar, J.Sathish Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) operating in Flying Ad-hoc Networks (FANETs) are becoming essential for applications like surveillance, agricultural monitoring, disaster relief, and military missions due to their fast deployment and autonomous functionality. However, FANETs' dynamic nature, constant movement, and open wireless communications introduce critical security vulnerabilities, making them targets for threats such as data interception, identity spoofing, message alteration, and routing attacks. This study proposes a secure communication approach using Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), a resource-efficient public-key encryption technique ideal for UAVs with limited processing capabilities. The ECCbased security protocol is incorporated into three common routing algorithms—4Ad-hoc On-demand Distance Vector (AODV), Optimized Link State Routing (OLSR), and Destination-Sequenced Distance Vector (DSDV)—which represent different routing paradigms: reactive, proactive, and table-driven. Simulation tests under realistic FANET scenarios, including varying node densities and mobility patterns as well as active security attacks like black hole, Sybil, and man-in-themiddle, assessed metrics such as packet delivery rate, latency, throughput, and control overhead. ECC-enhanced AODV offers the optimum balance between security and performance, according to research showing that incorporating ECC greatly improves security while maintaining network efficiency. This study shows that integrating cryptographic techniques at the routing layer offers a workable and scalable way to address FANET security issues. In order to improve intrusion detection and trust frameworks, future research will examine practical implementation, scalability for bigger UAV groups, and integrating ECC with cutting-edge technologies like blockchain and machine intelligence.

title: The Design and Research of Dynamic Calibration Device for Torque-Speed Sensor
authors: Xu Zhou, Fangjian Yuan, Qiliang Dai, Hang Xiu, Lei Wang, Yu Li, YuanPeng Lv
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Torque-speed sensors are widely used in the output torque and speed detection of rotating power equipment such as motors and generators, and their accuracy and reliability are critical to the healthy operation of power equipment. In this paper, a vertical single-axis torsional pendulum dynamic calibration device and its type selection are proposed. The device adopts servo motor to provide dynamic excitation, connects the sensor to be calibrated through flexible coupling, and eliminates friction interference with air float bearing; The rear end is connected with the self-designed replaceable weight standard inertia module, which combines the circular grating to monitor the angular displacement, angular velocity and angular acceleration in real time. A new dynamic calibration device for torque-speed sensor is provided, and the design of standard inertia module is optimized to improve the calibration accuracy.

title: Table of Content
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)

title: Quantum Encryption Scheme Based on Taylor Series and Fourier Transform
authors: Hari Sudharsan G, Nithin S, Amritavarshini B, Devadarshini M, S. Manimaran
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel quantum encryption scheme that integrates the Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT), Taylor Series expansion, and quantum gates to establish a secure and efficient encryption framework. By blending classical mathematical principles with quantum computational techniques along with No-cloning theorem. QFT is used to transform the data domain, while encoding with Taylor-derived functions forms a robust private-key mechanism, setting foundation for security. Quantum gates act as encryption layers, and a signal derived from secret Taylor terms is added to obfuscate the data during public key generation. This approach upon further development will be particularly suited for privacy-preserving applications such as federated learning and secure machine learning. The results demonstrate reliable state retrieval after encryption and decryption, validating the scheme's practicality. This work sets a foundation for scalable, quantum-cloud-based secure computation and opens avenues for further advancements in quantum cryptography and multi-party secure protocols.

title: Edge-Cloud Intelligence: Adaptive Circuit Topology Perception System*
authors: Yongzhuo Yu, Changju Wu, Chuanyi Wang, Weiwei Yue
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent circuit topology perception system based on a collaborative edgecloud architecture, focusing on algorithmic and model optimization to address challenges in wire connection detection under non-uniform pin layouts and overlapping scenarios. An enhanced YOLOv8n-seg model is deployed at the edge, incorporating a novel dual-stage perspective mapping mechanism that achieves 0.53 mm positioning accuracy. A wire feature enhancement module maintains a 90.4% recognition rate under varying illumination and wire overlaps. Coupled with a dynamic load balancing strategy, the system attains 93.1% wire recognition accuracy and 202 ms end-to-end latency, significantly improving accuracy and efficiency in electronic experiment teaching.

title: Sustainable Public Lighting: A Stakeholder Survey on Addressing Ecological, Economic, and Social Complexities
authors: Johannes Weninger, Maximilian Dick, Martina Ascher
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Urban public lighting faces growing ecological, economic, and social challenges, as higher efficiency technologies have not yielded proportional environmental benefits and have instead contributed to higher overall urban illuminance levels. This study investigates how current and emerging lighting technologies can address these challenges while reflecting diverse stakeholder perspectives. An online survey of 239 participants, including municipal authorities, lighting professionals, manufacturers, and standardization bodies, assessed perceived responsibilities, trust, technology potential, and implementation barriers. Results highlight human health and biodiversity as primary objectives, with federal institutions assigned key responsibility. Advanced technologies were recognized as environmentally and economically beneficial, yet adoption is constrained by regulatory gaps, integration challenges, maintenance demands, and privacy concerns. The findings indicate that sustainable public lighting requires integrated governance context-sensitive technology deployment to achieve sustainable outcomes.

title: Effect of Electrosprayed Aluminum Nitride (AlN) Coatings on Thermal Performance of Multi-Chip Gan Devices
authors: Ibrahim Saadouni, Oumaima Douass, Bousselham Samoudi, Adel Asselman, Laurent Canale
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Electrosprayed Aluminum Nitride (AlN) coatings are promising for thermal management applications due to their high thermal conductivity and electrical insulation properties. This paper numerically investigates their effect on the thermal performance of a multi-chip GaN LED mounted on a microchannel heat sink with seven pin fins. A 40 W device is analyzed under natural and forced convection using a validated CFD model. AlN layers with thicknesses ranging from 0.01 to 2 mm are applied to the heat sink base and fins. The numerical model is validated against literature data with a maximum deviation of about 2%. The results show that AlN coatings improve heat spreading and reduce thermal resistance by approximately 6% under natural convection and up to 25% under forced convection, demonstrating their effectiveness as a passive cooling solution for high-power GaN LED devices.

title: Design Optimisation and Analysis of Nanosheet EET Devices
authors: Km Nitu Gupta, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan, Baljit Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Progress in semiconductor technology has been driven the electronics sector to the center stage. The nanosheet (NS) device is an example of gate-all-around (GAA) structure, suggest better gate electrostatics, mitigating the adverse effect of short-channel effects (SCEs) and allowing scaling lower than sub-7nm technology node. This article imparts a thorough design examination of a two-sheet NSFET, exploring its geometric configurations. Major digital and analog or RF figures are analysed and compared considering the length of gate variations, variations of sheet thickness, and variations of the sheet width. The work presents simulation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 n m}$</tex> nanosheet FET (NS-FET) structures using gate-all-around and fully depleted silicon-on-insulator technologies. The paper presents crucial insights into NSFET design optimizations for sub- 7 nm and advanced technology node applications. The nanosheet (NS) device, which is gate-all-around based, gives better gate control, reducing short channel effects (SCEs) and allowing more scaling lower than the sub-7nm technology node. This analysis explaining a detailed design analysis of a two-sheet NSFET and examines the effect of a range of geometric parameters on the device.

title: Forecasting Air Quality Index in a Himalayan City: A Comparative Analysis of Statistical and Machine Learning Methods
authors: Ishaan Dawar, Poorvi Singh, Vasudev Pant, Anil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Himalayan cities have specific problems with air quality forecasting because of the complicated terrain, specific atmospheric patterns, and seasonal changes. This paper discusses the necessity of precise Air Quality Index (AQI) prediction through comparison of conventional statistical techniques with the latest machine learning (ML) techniques. The four methodologies included Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average with Exogenous variables (SARIMAX), Vector Autoregression (VAR), CatBoost Regression, and XGBoost Regression (XGBR). The training and testing of models were performed with the help of historical data on air quality, going through rigorous cross-validation processes. As can be seen, the ML algorithms performed much better than the statistical ones, with XGBR having a much higher predictive accuracy, as the following values demonstrate: MAE: 0.0176, MSE: 0.0006, RMSE: 0.0254, and R 2 score: 0.9903. This exceptional performance highlights the importance of capturing complex non-linear relationships between temporal variables and pollutant concentrations in high-altitude environments. The research contributes valuable insights for environmental monitoring in mountainous regions. It offers a methodological framework for developing localized, highprecision AQI forecasting tools applicable to other Himalayan urban centers, potentially enhancing public health interventions and environmental policy decisions.

title: A Novel Flexible Job Shop Scheduling Method via Modified Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Zhixuan Zhang, Pengpei Gao, Chunhe Song
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The flexible job shop scheduling problem is a typical complex optimization challenge in intelligent manufacturing and production systems. To overcome the shortcomings of standard particle swarm optimization (PSO), which often falls into local optima and struggles with large-scale instances, this paper proposes an improved discrete PSO integrating simulated annealing and variable neighborhood search. The method employs a nonlinearly decreasing inertia weight to balance exploration and exploitation, while simulated annealing and multi-neighborhood strategies enhance local search. In addition, a dynamic rescheduling framework is developed to handle disturbances such as machine breakdowns and urgent orders. Experimental results on the Kacem and MK benchmarks show that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing methods and demonstrates superior robustness and adaptability in dynamic environments.

title: Robust UAV Deployment and Task Offloading Based on Road Traffic Flow in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Weifeng Zhong, Jiahai Xiao, Xumin Huang, Jiawen Kang, Shengli Xie, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper studies long-term decision-making for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) deployment and task offloading in a UAV-enabled low-altitude wireless network (LAWN), which offers computing service to moving vehicles on a road segment. A key challenge in such high-mobility scenarios is that real-time decision-making demands frequent updates, leading to considerable implementation costs. Hence, our objective is to derive steadfast decisions that remain valid throughout system operation and robust against vehicle mobility under certain traffic flow, thereby eliminating the need for repeated decision updates. We formulate a data-driven joint UAV deployment and task offloading optimization model, where incorporating richer historical vehicle data yields more robust decisions. The optimization aims to minimize deployed UAVs' energy consumption, resulting in a nonconvex mixed-integer problem. To solve the problem efficiently, we develop a tailored heuristic that integrates the alternating direction method (ADM) and analytical target cascading (ATC). Results demonstrate that the proposed heuristic can adapt UAV placement and offloading strategies to specific traffic conditions and produce decisions that are robust to on-road vehicle dynamics.

title: Adaptive MPC-based Path Following Control for Autonomous Vehicles with Random Time Delays
authors: Chao Yang, Ruixin Zhang, Weida Wang, Yuhang Zhang, Qi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Autonomous vehicles are further improved with the development of in-vehicle networks and X-by-wire technologies. Due to the increasing integration of vehicle systems and the growing complexity of control links, time delays in information transmission and execution have become increasingly prominent. Because of late responses to control inputs, autonomous vehicles have deterioration in terms of path following accuracy and control stability, which poses a threat to driving safety. To solve this problem, an adaptive model predictive path following control strategy is presented in this paper. Firstly, a real-time delay estimator is designed based on historical feedback information processing and the gradient descent algorithm. Secondly, based on the estimated delay, a delay-augmented model with variable dimension is proposed for adaptive control to mitigate the impact of time-varying delay. Meanwhile, a tube-based model predictive control method is modified for the proposed model to deal with uncertainties generated by time-varying delays. Finally, the proposed control strategy is verified by simulations and experiments in multiple cases. The results show that the proposed strategy achieved a smaller lateral offset than that in the ordinary model predictive control method, leading to a 52.86% improvement in accuracy under the existence of time-varying delays.

title: A Data-Driven Optimal Control Architecture for Grid-Connected Power Converters
authors: Ruohan Leng, Linbin Huang, Huanhai Xin, Ping Ju, Xiongfei Wang, Eduardo Prieto-Araujo, Florian Dörfler
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: Grid-connected power converters are ubiquitous in modern power systems, acting as grid interfaces of renewable energy sources, energy storage systems, electric vehicles, high-voltage DC systems, etc. Conventionally, power converters use multiple PID regulators to achieve different control objectives such as grid synchronization and voltage/power regulation, where the PID parameters are usually tuned based on a presumed (and often overly-simplified) power grid model. However, this may lead to inferior performance or even instabilities in practice, as the real power grid is highly complex, variable, and generally unknown. To tackle this problem, we employ a data-enabled predictive control (DeePC) to perform data-driven, optimal, robust, and adaptive control for power converters. We call the converters that are operated in this way DeePConverters. A DeePConverter can implicitly perceive the characteristics of the power grid from measured data and adjust its control strategy to achieve optimal, robust, and adaptive performance. We present the modular configurations, generalized structure, control behavior specification, inherent robustness, detailed implementation, computational aspects, and online adaptation of DeePConverters. High-fidelity simulations and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) tests are provided to validate the effectiveness of DeePConverters.

title: Sustainability of AI Use in Higher Education: The Role of Perceived Usefulness and Self-Efficacy
authors: Samah AL Hassan, Noura Yassine
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The pandemic, along with the technological revolution, has shifted the world toward fundamental digital transformation in different fields, particularly in education. To address the digital needs of higher education, several institutions have integrated digital learning into their academic approaches. The rapid integration of technology in education highlights the importance of exploring the drivers behind AI adoption in the academic setting within a sustainable framework. This research examines the interplay between AI features such as system updates, memorability, and non-language barriers and their sustainable usage in educational contexts. The roles of perceived usefulness and self-efficacy in this relationship are also assessed. Data were collected from 203 university students. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) using SmartPLS 4.1 was employed to assess the associations within the research model. The findings indicate a positive correlation between the intelligent capabilities of AI and students' sustainable use of AI in education. Moreover, the results reveal that this relationship is mediated by perceived usefulness and self-efficacy. In addition, contextual variables such as the type of university were found to exert a moderating effect on this relationship.

title: Smart Power Management in PV Systems with Battery–Supercapacitor Hybrid Storage for Electric Vehicle
authors: Dounia Touahria, Amel Bouchemha, Sami Kahla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This study suggests a system of hybrid energy storage (HESS) for use alone photovoltaic (PV) applications. The system integrates a lithium-ion battery and a supercapacitor (SC) to ensure both long-term energy supply and fast dynamic response to power fluctuations. While the battery provides sustained energy delivery, its lifetime is negatively affected by frequent high-current demands. To mitigate this issue, the SC is introduced to absorb rapid transient loads, thereby reducing stress on the battery and extending its service life. The PV system charges both storage elements when solar irradiance is sufficient and ensures energy availability during low irradiance conditions. To highlight the benefit of the SC, a baseline configuration using PV with battery-only storage is also simulated. Power distribution across the storage devices is coordinated using a Genetic Algorithm (GA)-optimized energy management method based on proportional-integral (PI) controllers. MATLAB/Simulink results under varying irradiance and load profiles demonstrate that, compared to the baseline, the proposed HESS reduces battery SOC fluctuation, improves DC-bus voltage stability, and enhances overall energy utilization efficiency.

title: Sensor Placement for Agricultural Field Coverage
authors: Ilir Shinko, Erison Ballasheni, Lutis Picorri, Vladi Kolici, Dritan Nace, Alban Zyle
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: This paper addresses the sensor placement problem in agricultural LoRaWAN networks under sensor failure scenarios. We extend proportional fairness (PF) optimization from network controller placement to agricultural sensor deployment, where coverage is defined as the ability of deployed sensors to successfully monitor target field locations within their sensing range. In addition, we integrate machine learning approaches for coverage prediction with spatial clustering techniques to enforce diversity. Experimental validation demonstrates that the new approach outperforms baseline methods across varying sensor failure intensities.

title: Calibration Machine Implementation for Fine Phase Control Adjustment in Phase Interpolator Using a Discrete Current Modulation Profile
authors: Erick J. Arenas Mendoza, Iurhitsi Elias Valdez, Ernesto Conde Almada, Rodrigo Jaramillo Ramirez
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Electron Devices Conference (LAEDC)
abstract: This paper presents a digital Calibration Machine (CM) architecture designed to improve the linearity of a 127step phase interpolator with coarse and fine control topology by the means of the adjustment of biasing current per code in the Discrete Modulation Profile (DMP). The digital design flow covers from coarse/fine control technique until CM layout implementation. The CM process has two different operations, which are calibration and characterization. In calibration mode, the CM saves the data for fine-control adjustment through the DMP. The second operation, characterization, is used to determine the order in which the codes obtained in calibration are turned on. The digital control was designed in Verilog and implemented in 130 nm SkyWater technology using the OpenROAD workflow from RTL-to-GDSII. Input stimuli are provided by a Time-toDigital Converter (TDC), which measures phase offsets between ideal timing references and the interpolated output, forming a closed-loop feedback mechanism for calibration.

title: Unbalance Fault Detection in an IoT-Connected Motor Network Using Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Massine GANA, Zakia CHELLI, Rafik SADDAOUI, Hakim ACHOUR, Mourad LAGHROUCHE
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a low-cost integrated system for the preventive detection of imbalance faults in a network of interconnected induction motors using Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The system monitors two key parameters in real time (vibration and temperature) via non-invasive sensors. These signals are collected and transmitted to an ESP32 microcontroller, which forwards the data to the ThingSpeak cloud platform for centralized monitoring and communication between machines. A graphical user interface (GUI) enables remote visualization of motor status, and alerts are generated automatically when predefined thresholds are exceeded. A lightweight artificial neural network (ANN), implemented on the ESP32 board, performs real-time diagnosis of motor health every 15 seconds. To enhance the quality of vibration data and reduce the impact of sensor noise, a Weighted Moving Average (WMA) filter is applied. This filter offers a computationally efficient and adaptive smoothing method, well suited for embedded systems, by emphasizing recent measurements while attenuating fluctuations. The proposed system ensures high motor availability by enabling timely, accurate fault detection and early intervention. Experimental validation demonstrates the system's effectiveness in distinguishing faulty motors from healthy ones, making it applicable to a broad range of industrial contexts, including hydraulic pumps and wind turbines.

title: A Heuristic Optimization Approach for Wind Turbine Dimensions to Enhance Energy Capture and Reduce Costs
authors: Mourad Naidji, Mohamed Ilyas Rahal, Alla Eddine Toubal Maamar, Aicha Aissa-Bokhtache, Maamar Latroch, Radu-Florin Porumb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Emerging as a vital component in the worldwide shift towards environmentally friendly power generation is wind energy. Maximizing energy output and reducing cost depend on well-designed wind farms, particularly considering the growing demand for renewable energy. This work expands on several ideal wind turbine configurations suggested in previous work and chooses them as basis for more thorough investigation and enhancement. While most previous studies have concentrated mostly on lowering the cost per kilowatt of generated power, this work takes a more all-encompassing view aiming to improve general wind farm efficiency through strategic optimization of turbine tower heights and rotor diameters, so simultaneously lowering total costs. This kind of approach might produce more flexible and efficient wind farms. A Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) method is used to find the best arrangement of rotor diameters and tower heights over the wind farm in order to reach these targets.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Based Optimisation Techniques for Energy Management of Hybrid PV/Wind/Fuel Cell Microgrids
authors: Imane Benkhadir, Badre Bossoufi, Mohammed Amine Hoummadi, Chakib EL Bekkali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Over the past few decades, the growing cost of fossil fuels combined with the urgent need to decarbonize the energy sector has intensified the global shift toward renewable energy sources (RES). Achieving a sustainable future largely depends on the widespread generation of clean energy, in which renewable technologies play a decisive role. The integration of renewable sources such as photovoltaic (PV) panels, wind turbines, and fuel cells (FCs) into microgrids provides a sustainable and efficient pathway for electricity production. The reliable operation of such systems hinges on sophisticated Energy Management Systems (EMS) supported by effective optimization strategies. While conventional optimization methods offer fundamental solutions for scheduling, dispatch, and control, they often struggle to maintain performance under the intermittency and uncertainty inherent to RES. To address these limitations, Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based optimization techniques have recently emerged as powerful tools to enhance EMS adaptability and efficiency. Accordingly, this review delivers a comprehensive overview of hybrid microgrid configurations, EMS types, conventional optimization frameworks, and AI-driven approaches, emphasizing their objectives, methodologies, and major contributions.

title: Solar Inverter Performance Prediction Using Double Exponential Curve Fitting
authors: Lyazid Kaci, Amar Hadj Arab, Rachid Zirmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this paper, a performance model is developed and mainly evaluated for the SMA/ Sunny Boy 3000TL grid-connected photovoltaic inverters based on field data from the power plant of the Renewable Energy Development Center (CDER) located in Algiers. The model is based on double exponential curve fitting method. The inverter performance is expressed by the model as a function of the inverter AC output power. The accuracy of the developed double exponential model is compared to the SANDIA National laboratories for the SMA single phase inverter. Throughout system operation the performance model of the grid-connected photovoltaic inverter can be used to continuously monitor the inverter performance

title: Investigating the Impact of Medical Knowledge on Self-Medication Practices Among Non-Medical Students
authors: Saumendra Ku. Mohanty, Raghu N, Ansh Kataria, V.J.K. Kishore Sonti, Kunal Meher, Varalakshmi Dandu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Self-medication among non-medical students has become a prevalent global issue, often influenced by varying levels of medical knowledge. Understanding the relationship between knowledge and self-medication behaviours is essential for minimizing associated health risks. Existing studies primarily rely on descriptive surveys, which fail to capture nuanced risk patterns and knowledge-behaviour associations. To address these limitations, this study introduces the Knowledge-Behavior Impact Model (KBIM), integrating KMeans Clustering for grouping students by knowledge-practice profiles and Chi-Square Analysis for testing statistical associations in risk classification. The proposed model enables the accurate identification of high-risk self-medication behaviors based on objective knowledge levels and behavioral indicators. Findings reveal that students with lower medical knowledge scores exhibit significantly riskier self-medication practices <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> for antibiotics, while those with higher knowledge demonstrate safer and more informed behaviours. The KBIM framework thus provides a robust analytical basis for designing targeted educational interventions in nonmedical academic settings.

title: Attacker-Aware Contrastive Learning for Robust Malicious URL Classification
authors: Sara Afzal, Abraham Gebrehiwot, Farrukh Aslam Khan, Filippo Maria Lauria
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Uniform Resource Locators (URLs) are frequently exploited as entry points for cyberattacks such as phishing, malware distribution, and website defacement. Detecting malicious URLs directly from their lexical structure, without page retrieval, is essential for low-latency defense but remains challenging due to adversarial obfuscations (e.g., homoglyphs, zero-width insertions, subdomain stuffing) and severe class imbalance in real-world corpora. To address these issues, we propose an attacker-aware lexical classification pipeline that combines structure-preserving tokenization, a library of attacker-style augmentations, supervised contrastive (SupCon) pretraining, and class-weighted focal loss fine-tuning. The Transformer encoder serves as the feature extraction model, while a lightweight MLP head performs classification. Empirical evaluations performed on the large Kaggle malicious phishing dataset show that the proposed methodology achieves the accuracy of 99.57% and the macro-F1 score of 99.38% on a hold-out test set, and thus consistently outperforms previously established state-of-the-art baselines. Notably, minority classes, which includes malware, achieves an accuracy and macro-G1 score greater than 99% in all aspects of measurement, highlighting the resentment of the method to adversarial string perturbations. The framework developed is computationally efficient, content agnostic and capable of deployment in a real-time operational scenario that provides a pragmatic defence mechanism against modern cyber threats.

title: Making Zero-Days Hard: A Deployment-Oriented Review of Preventive Defences
authors: Praneeth Kasiraju, Harshak Golusu, Balasubramanian Prabhu Kavin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Exploitation of previously unjammed vulnerabilities in functioning environments is known as zero-day exploitation and is a constant challenge in the cybersecurity arena. This review focuses on the various preventative approaches implemented from March 2022 up to July 2025, divided in eight different categories at the eight different levels that range from design-time, runtime and operational level. Case studies are taken from across the board of Android, Chrome, Windows, Linux, the Apple platform, the financial sector, and how the changes in the use of memory safe languages, compiler based and hardware based protections, architectural isolation, proactive bug discovery, supply chain controls and operational mitigations are changing the nature of the defensive posture. The analysis also presents persistent challenges, such as the use of mitigations in runtime, issues with reproducibility and lack of automation for enforcement of SBOM/VEX. Overall, the review sets a general trend: prevention is no longer an add-on, but rather it is a basic principle of secure system design. While issues of memory safety are decreasing, logical loopholes, supply chain gaps and phishing attacks are becoming more common impacting ways to implement adaptable, proactive defenses. This review provides a deployment facing synthesis of preventive measures using a lifecycle taxonomy to systematically provide a organization of recent platform rolls, adoption barriers, and measurable impacts. It further highlights open challenges which require further advancement in order to effectively address the zero day exploitation.

title: What Remains of the Human in the Age of AI and Other Disruptive Technologies?
authors: Jason Idriss Sparkes
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Many experts and members of the public fear that AI and other disruptive technologies pose an existential threat to humanity. This paper argues that this threat is rooted not primarily in technical concerns but in a deeper crisis of human intention and purpose, which can only be addressed by returning to first principles. Writing from a transdisciplinary humanities perspective and engaging substantially with religious studies and Islamic studies, this intervention examines the foundations of that crisis. As we outsource core human functions to machines, we risk forgetting who we are. Rather than begin with technical fixes, we must revisit fundamental questions: What is a human? What is our purpose? What is our place in the cosmos? Recentring the human is urgent and requires cross-civilizational dialogue. Drawing on classical philosophy, Abrahamic thought, and the transdisciplinary humanities, this analysis demonstrates that intelligence and knowledge cannot be severed from intention, presence, virtue, and ethical purpose. When machines are built without clear human ends in mind, they risk reshaping us into beings we no longer recognize. Education becomes procedural and technology a mirror of our confusion. In response, we must recentre the human as a moral and existential agent, not merely a productive or computational one. Tools must serve our higher aspirations, and education must rekindle awe, contemplation, and responsibility. This is not a call for nostalgic retreat but for principled renewal. Only by clarifying our intention can we design technologies that support, rather than undermine, our flourishing in body, heart, mind, and spirit.

title: Adversarially Robust and Explainable AI Systems for High-Risk Clinical Decision Support with Iot-Integrated Health Monitoring
authors: Md Ariful Islam Rana, Zubaida Iftekhar, Md Akhtaruzzaman, Salman Qavi, Maruf Ahmad, Md Mohiuddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: High-risk medical settings are mainly about making decisions, and the implementation of artificial-intelligence (AI) solutions can significantly enhance clinical outcomes. However, the effectiveness of AI is often posed by adversarial attacks that alter the outputs of the models and might have disastrous effects. In order to alleviate this problem, we present an adversarially resistant AI system that can be used to assist high-risk clinical decision support in Internet of Things integrated health-monitoring infrastructures. Our system applies stringent AI techniques enabling the system to be resistant to adversarial perturbations to guarantee reliability of the predictions even in dynamically hostile and risky surroundings. Furthermore, we incorporate explainable-AI (XAI) techniques to maximize transparency and establish trust in professionals working in the healthcare sector making the system more palatable in clinical practice. We test the system with the help of a full healthcare dataset and show its performance in adversarial scenarios or in the real-time decision making problem. The findings indicate that our solution yields a robustness to the AI models against adversarial attacks, in addition to enhancing interpretability, thus leading to an excellent clinical decision-making and patient safety.

title: Unveiling Volatility Drivers: Machine Learning and SHAP Across Weak and Strong Institutions
authors: Mohamed Malek Belhoula, Ichrak Dridi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Leveraging eleven machine learning models in a cross-country analysis, this study investigates the drivers of stock market volatility, emphasizing institutional quality's (IQ) pivotal role across high- and low-quality regimes in emerging markets. Employing regularization techniques (Lasso, Ridge, Elastic Net), tree-based models (Decision Tree, Extra Tree, Random Forest, LightGBM, XGBoost), and deep learning approaches (LSTM, ANN, GRU), alongside volatility models (EGARCH, HAR-GARCH, GARCH-MIDAS), we analyze 16 emerging countries with stable policy frameworks, over 1995Q1–2024Q1. Both mean SHAP feature importance and SHAP interaction techniques reveals distinct volatility dynamics: in high institutional quality environments, volatility is shaped by long-term market fundamentals and stable global financial linkages. In contrast, in low institutional quality settings, volatility is driven by immediate economic fragilities, speculative trading, and external uncertainties. Robust across tranquil and crisis periods, these findings affirm institutional quality's moderating role as a critical determinant of financial market stability. These insights offer significant implications for risk management and policy formulation in emerging economies.

title: HOPF-Bogdanov-Takens Bifurcation in a Delayed Hopfield Network Model with Bidirectional Connection
authors: Houssem Achouri, Chaouki Aouiti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: In this study, we examine the dynamical behavior of a three-neuron Hopfield network that includes bidirectional connections as well as time-delay effects. Our primary goal is to identify and analyze a higher-order singularity of codimension three, specifically a Hopf-Bogdanov-Takens (H-B-T) point, arising in this framework. Such a degeneracy appears when a double zero eigenvalue occurs simultaneously with a pair of purely imaginary eigenvalues, giving rise to a variety of local phenomena that blend the dynamics typical of Hopf and Bogdanov-Takens bifurcations. By employing center manifold reduction together with normal form computations, inspired by the methodology presented in [1], [2], we rigorously establish the normal form corresponding to the H-B-T degeneracy. These analytical developments are then applied to the delayed Hopfield system, where the synaptic weights and the delay parameter act as unfolding parameters. A thorough bifurcation analysis enables us to construct diagrams capturing the intricate behavior of the model near the singularity, revealing multiple possible local scenarios. To complement and validate the theoretical predictions, we also carry out numerical experiments that illustrate the rich dynamical patterns exhibited by the network.

title: A Synergistic Approach to Secure and Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning Pipelines for Real-Time Anomaly Detection Systems in U.S. Critical Infrastructure
authors: Asif Iqbal, Md Risalat Hossain Ontor, Shahadat Hossain, Tanvirahmedshuvo, Md Golam Sarwar, Md Mohiuddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The growing reliance on critical infrastructure systems throughout the United States is bound to necessitate the creation of sophisticated anomaly detection systems that could not only be able to detect advanced cyber threats but also protect the privacy and security. This paper presents a new hybrid system of real-time anomaly detection, combining the use of machine-learning models with differential privacy and reinforcement learning. The proposed model contributes to the detection capacities of industrial control systems (ICS) by Deep Q-Learning (DQL) used in adaptive detection and privacy-preserving techniques such as the Laplace mechanism. The framework has several anomaly-detection methods, including Convolutional Autoencoders (CAE) and Random Cut Forests (RCF), and tests them on benchmark data, thus proving that this system is effective and efficient in the real-world. Accuracy, F1 score and false positive rate (FPR) are some of the metrics that are used to evaluate the performance of the system, highlighting its strength in very critical-infrastructure scenarios. We also describe the efficiency of our model in terms of the computation which supports its use in real-time deployments. Differential privacy ensures high standards of privacy protection incurs low performance costs. The suggested framework, therefore, provides a safe, scalable and adaptable solution to the detection of anomalies in the cybersecurity situation of critical infrastructure.

title: BotonyBuddy: AI-Enabled Precision Plant Nursery Management for Multicrop Disease Classification and Phenology-Aware Age Estimation
authors: Pandula Pallewatta, Samantha Mathara Arachchi, Kasun Karunanayaka, Trapp Kayuni, Tinmei Aleksandr, Aditi Bhattarai, Thilina Halloluwa, Kelvin A Nicodemas, Williams Kyei
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: Plant nurseries face a critical challenge, as manual inspection of plant health and growth is labour-intensive, inconsistent, and unable to scale with rising demand. Early disease detection and accurate age estimation are crucial for nursery quality assurance; however, traditional methods remain subjective and prone to error. To address this gap, this paper presents BotonyBuddy. Smart plant nurseries can automatically benefit from AI to monitor plant health and growth. This AI-driven smart nursery management system unifies disease diagnosis and age prediction for four tropical crops (Mango, Bell Pepper, Durian, and Anthurium) using real nursery and greenhouse imagery. The system leverages deep learning (DL) models (VGG19, ResNet50, EfficientNet-B7, and DeiT Vision Transformer) for leaf disease classification on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 7,500$</tex> images and integrates a vision-based age estimation pipeline trained on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 1,200$</tex> labelled samples. Two modelling strategies are explored: (i) Direct age regression/classification with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and transfer learning, and (ii) an object-detection approach (YOLOv8) to count leaves and branches as inputs to an age prediction model. EfficientNet-B7 achieved the highest disease classification accuracies, 95.08% (Bell Pepper), 95.94% (Durian), 94.36% (Mango), and 92.02% (Anthurium), with DeiT close behind <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{8 9. 8} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{9 3. 7 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, while ResNet50/VGG19 performed between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 85-92 {\%}$</tex>.
