For age estimation, the hybrid CNN and leaf-count approach achieved a mean absolute error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 1 5}$</tex> days for young plants and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 5}$</tex> days overall, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 8 \%}$</tex> classification accuracy across age groups, with per-crop MAE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{4}$</tex> days (Bell Pepper), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 18$</tex> days (Mango), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 21-22$</tex> days (Durian), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15-16$</tex> days (Anthurium). A prototype web system enables QR-code-based plant identification, customer photo uploads, and personalized care recommendations. Results confirm that BotonyBuddy delivers reliable, real-time insights into plant health and growth, offering a scalable, AI-driven plant nursery tool for precision and smart agriculture. Future directions include expanding disease coverage, adopting multi-view imaging, and incorporating IoT data for higher-fidelity predictions.

title: Renewable Energy Deployment Efficiency: Analyzing Algeria's Achievement Gap and Optimization Strategies
authors: Elmer Arellanos-Tafur, Marcelo Damas-Niño
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Algeria's renewable energy initiative reached only 15.68% of its projected capacity by 2020 (686 MW of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4,375 \text{MW}$</tex> target), placing it among the lowest implementation rates worldwide. This paper examines the technical and institutional constraints contributing to this shortfall using data from Algeria's National Renewable Energy Program (PENREE), and outlines optimization measures to align progress with the updated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22,000 \text{MW}$</tex> objective for 2030. A comparative review with successful renewable programs in peer economies identifies key bottlenecks and introduces a phase-gate implementation framework capable of raising achievement levels to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 - 7 5 \%}$</tex>. Results show that grid connection delays account for nearly 40% of project disruptions, followed by financing issues (35%) and permitting processes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(25 \%)$</tex>, underscoring structural weaknesses in inter-institutional coordination. Based on official IRENA data, this research delivers the first quantitative evaluation of deployment efficiency for renewable projects in the MENA region and establishes a replicable methodology for assessing large-scale transition programs. While the proposed strategies could substantially accelerate implementation, their effectiveness remains contingent on institutional capacity development and stronger project management practices. Technology-specific outcomes reveal considerable variation: solar PV achieved 13.33% of its target, whereas wind projects reached only 5%. Regional comparisons show unexpected patterns, with remote zones such as Adrar attaining up to 70% success rates, compared to 15% in grid-connected northern areas. These results challenge the prevailing assumption that resource endowment alone determines deployment performance.

title: Energy Management of a Smart District Using the Neural Predictions and Predictive Control Model (MPC)
authors: Zoulikha Ouchefoun, Mourad Hasni, Lakhdar Guenfaf
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this article, we will conduct an optimized energy management study for a smart neighborhood that integrates renewable energy sources (solar and wind) with storage systems (batteries and vehicle-to-grid technology), alongside residential and commercial loads, all connected to the grid. The goal is to develop a robust model for optimal energy management by employing artificial neural networks (ANN) to forecast residential and commercial energy demand based on time. These predictions are essential for optimizing energy production, real-time import and export, and for implementing model-based predictive control (MPC) to minimize costs and enhance energy efficiency. The results demonstrate that this model significantly improves renewable energy management, reduces dependence on the central grid, and promotes sustainable energy use while lowering costs associated with energy imports.

title: Data-Driven based Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics for Stability Analysis of Mixed Grid-Following and Grid-Forming Inverters
authors: Houssam Deboucha, Bessam Deboucha, Elyazid Amirouche, Said Aissou, Ali Berboucha, Kaci Ghedamsi, Saad Mekhilef
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: The growing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) in modern power systems has introduced significant dynamic complexity and challenges in microgrids, frequently giving rise to instability and pronounced transient phenomena, particularly under islanded operating conditions. In this paper, a pure data-driven modelling framework based on Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamical Systems (SINDy) has been proposed, enhanced through delay embedding via Hankel matrices, to analyse the transient dynamics of hybrid Grid Following (GFL) and Grid Forming (GFM) inverters. Proper Orthogonal Decomposition (POD) is applied to extract the dominant spatial-temporal modes. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated and validated through numerical simulations against Hankel Dynamic Mode Decomposition (DMD), involving both time-domain and eigenvalue analysis on a microgrid with diverse grid-forming (GFM) control architectures, including Droop control, Dispatchable Virtual oscillator, and Grid Following (GFL) inverter with PLL. The model-free design SINDy-based framework successfully captures system dynamics and enables rigorous stability assessment of both GFM and GFL inverters based on the available measurements.

title: Deep Learning Fusion Model with CNN and LSTM for Network Traffic Anomaly Detection
authors: Mohamed Ali FAKRI, Rachid Ben Said, Abdellah Najid, Nezha El Idrissi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Software-Defined Networking (SDN) is an important technology for building flexible and programmable networks, but it also creates new security challenges. In this paper, we present a fusion Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to better capture both spatial and temporal features of network traffic. We use regularization techniques like L2 regularization and dropout to reduce overfitting and improve generalization. Our model is tested on both NSL-KDD and ToN-IoT datasets, a well-known benchmark for network intrusion detection. The results show that fusing CNN and LSTM improves detection accuracy, achieving 99.22 % on NSL-KDD and 99.40 % on ToN-IoT which is better than using each model alone. This work helps advance more reliable IDS for future network security.

title: Smart Agriculture for Olive Farming: AI-Driven Disease Detection and Embedded System Automation
authors: Abdellah Nabou, El Hassan Abdelwahed, Hasna Elalaoui El Abdellaoui, Zaikarani Khalid, Bellani Ayoub, Ennhili Yassine
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: live farming faces significant challenges in disease detection, irrigation efficiency, and resource management, often exacerbated by manual monitoring's subjectivity and delays. This paper presents a smart agricultural system addressing these issues through AI-driven disease detection and embedded system automation. The solution integrates a ResNet-50 model, fine-tuned on the “Zeytin Yaprak 224-Aug” and “Olive Leaf Image Dataset” (resized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224$</tex>), to classify olive plant health into three classes: Healthy, Cycloconium_oleaginum, and rust_mite. A mobile rover, powered by an ESP8266 for motor control and a servomotor for ESP32-CAM positioning, captures live images, while sensors (FC-28, DHT22, LDR, MAX471, water level) monitor environmental conditions. Automation of irrigation, dome control, and solar tracking is achieved via HTTP communication with a React Native interface for real-time data visualization and control. Experimental results demonstrate 97 % accuracy in disease classification, 30 % water savings, and enhanced field coverage. This cost-effective, scalable system reduces manual effort and resource waste, offering olive farmers timely interventions and sustainable practices. These findings advance edge AI in agriculture, bridging model efficiency with practical deployment on embedded platforms.

title: A Prototype Design of Smart DC Power Device for Solar System Application
authors: Saad Khadar, Larbi Brahimi, Mohamed Elbar, Meriem Ghezal, Aya Mebarki, Sif Eddine Souadia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Energy monitoring and management is an essential element of modern electrical systems, especially in applications that require precision and efficiency to ensure optimal energy consumption. This project aims to develop a prototype design of smart DC power device that provides accurate measurements of voltage, current, and energy consumption, with the ability to track data in real time. Designed to be reliable and accurate, with seamless integration with Internet of Things (IoT) platforms such as Blynk application, the measurement device can be used in a wide range of applications, including solar system application, making it an innovative and efficient solution for monitoring power/energy consumption with high accuracy.

title: Advancing Infiltration Modeling with Deep Learning: A Superior Alternative to Empirical Approaches in the Mitidja Plain
authors: Amina Mazighi, Mohamed Meddi, Hind Meddi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Irrigation planning and flood risk assessment are critical challenges, particularly in semi-arid regions. This study evaluates the performance of deep learning models-Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)-in predicting infiltration rates across 70 experimental sites in the Mitidja Plain, Algeria, and compares them with traditional empirical models, namely Philips and SCS. Given the limitations of empirical models in capturing the complexity of infiltration dynamics, machine learning offers a powerful alternative for improving prediction accuracy. The LSTM model, designed with two layers (100 and 50 units) and dropout regularization, effectively captures temporal dependencies, while the MLP model, tested with different dimensional configurations, serves as a strong benchmark for static regression tasks. Results indicate that MLP outperforms all other models, achieving a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}\right)$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 5}$</tex>, compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 1}$</tex> for LSTM. In contrast, empirical models demonstrated lower accuracy, with SCS performing better than Philip. These results confirm that deep learning, particularly MLP, provides a more accurate and robust solution for infiltration modeling compared to classical empirical approaches. These findings highlight the transformative potential of artificial intelligence in hydrological studies, offering enhanced precision for soil and water conservation strategies in semi-arid environments. This study contributes to optimizing infiltration estimation and supports the integration of advanced computational methods into hydrology for improved water resource management in the Mitidja Plain, Algeria. Future research should explore hybrid approaches that combine empirical knowledge with deep learning techniques for further refinement.

title: Beer-Lambert Law-Based Optical Biosensor Simulation for PSA Detection Using 45mm CMOS Technology
authors: Ameur El Jeljal, Mounir Ouremchi, Karim El Khadiri, Moulhime El Bekkali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: The creation and simulation of a photodetector sensor for the identification of PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen), a key biomarker in the early diagnosis of prostate cancer, are described in this study. The goal is to create a full optoelectronic sensor based on the Beer-Lambert law that relates optical absorbance to PSA concentration. The sensor would integrate a CMOS photodiode modeled in Verilog-A with a high-gain transimpedance amplifier (TIA) built in 45 nm CMOS technology The model recreates the optical and enzymatic response produced by the HRP-TMB reacting thereby demonstrating the link between the concentration of PSA and the generated photocurrent. Simulations conducted using Cadence Virtuoso show that, as the PSA concentration increases from 0 to 50 ng/mL, the photodiode current decreases from 410 nA to 80 nA and the output voltage Vout changes from 928 mV to 813 mV. The results are consistent with the experimental studies [4] and [5]. With a gain of around 127 dB <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Omega$</tex>, the integrated TIA, which was modeled after the [1] piece provides reliable and low-noise operation. It should be emphasized that this research is completely simulation-based and does not include any actual hardware measurements or experimental verification. This approach forms a strong foundation for the implementation of CMOS multi-sensor biochips designed for optical bio identification.

title: Recent Advances in the Application of TinyML and Edge Devices in Photovoltaic Systems: Bridging the Gap Between Laboratory Research and Industry
authors: Adel Mellit, Marco Zennaro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: With advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques and embedded systems, various platforms have been developed to design and deploy Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) on edge devices for real-time applications. TinyML enables ML models to run efficiently on resource-constrained devices, making it particularly useful in photovoltaic (PV) systems. This paper briefly overviews potential TinyML applications in PV systems, including solar energy forecasting, fault detection and diagnosis, predictive maintenance, and more. Recent applications are discussed regarding the edge devices used, platforms, accuracy and real-time deployment. Most research in this topic is still conducted at the laboratory scale; therefore, bridging the gap between laboratory research and industry is crucial for transitioning from theory to real-world applications. The paper also discusses the challenges and outlines future directions for implementing embedded ML on edge devices in real-world PV system applications.

title: Post-Vaccination COVID-19 Side Effects in Annaba, Algeria: Construction, Analysis, and Clustering of a Comprehensive Database
authors: Soumia Layachi, Amel Slim, Ahlem Melouah, Abdeldjalil Halisse
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: In the effort to control the pandemic, it is important to understand the side effects of COVID19 vaccines for the sake of public health. This study presents a new and thorough database specifically focused on the side effects of COVID-19 vaccines. This is an area of research that has been lacking, especially in the Arab region. The data was carefully gathered from EPSP Annaba of Annaba city in Algeria and processed to ensure accuracy. Through the use of clustering method, relevant information was extracted from the database, revealing specific patterns and connections among side effects. This innovative work provides valuable insights for healthcare professionals and policymakers, contributing to a more detailed understanding of the safety of COVID-19 vaccines.

title: Implementation of Reinforcement Learning Environment for Hybrid Renewable Energy Systems
authors: Udit Mamodiya, Indra Kishor, Nivine Guler, Jamaluddin Hindi, Nithesh Naik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The hybrid renewable energy systems (HRES) must be capable of responding to intermittent supply by photovoltaics/wind, the health of storage, and time-dependent tariffs. Instantaneous, rule-based dispatch cannot operate under such stochastic couplings. Some of the reinforcement learning (RL) experiments rely on ad-hoc simulators, ineffective constraint management, and training regimes, which are not operational in latency. The study has offset this gap by creating a modular and physics-consistent RL environment of HRES that is capable of combining forecast-aware state-construction, battery/grid-safety screening, and reproducible benchmarking of Q-Learning, DQN, PPO, and a novel Hybrid Policy Gradient Algorithm (HPGA). Environment includes projection of constraints (SoC, ramp, feeder limits), multi-objective reward shaping (cost, curtailment, aging), as well as a co-simulation loop that reveals non-stationarity of real market and weather. The platform has shown the final outputs of a representative microgrid (200 kW PV, 150 kW wind, 500 kWh BESS). The PPO has 91.4 percent renewable utilization and 15.8 percent operating cost reduction over a rule base as compared to HPGA, which boosts convergence stability and battery stress. Decision latency reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$32 \text{ms} / \text{step}(\text{PPO})$</tex> versus 47 (DQN) and 62 (Q-Learning), and policies stabilize within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 480$</tex> episodes compared with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{7 0 0}$</tex> for tabular methods. By coupling rigorous physical constraints with sample-efficient RL updates, the proposed environment enables deployable, low-latency control and provides a transparent testbed for future algorithms advancing HRES operation toward higher renewable penetration, lower lifecycle cost, and robust grid support.

title: Detecting Cybersecurity Threats Using the Graph Neural Network in Smart Network Grid
authors: Ashmeet Kaur, Debanjan Ghosh, RamKumar Krishnamoorthy, Susmita Panda, Ramya G Franklin, Amanveer Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Energy distribution and infrastructure stability demand improved cybersecurity as smart network grids become more linked. The complex linkages of data and dynamic assault patterns make cyber threat detection in such networks difficult. Tradition intrusion detection or rule-based cybersecurity solutions struggle with high-dimensional, diverse data and fail to adapt to new threats in real time. To address these limitations, this study proposes the Graph Threat Detection Framework (GTDF), which integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with an Anomaly Detection Algorithm for effective cyberthreat identification. By modeling the network as a graph, GTDF captures relationships between devices, communication patterns, and potential vulnerabilities, enabling an accurate representation of complex interactions. The anomaly detection component further enhances the identification of unusual behavior indicative of cyber-attacks. The proposed framework is applied to smart grid network data to monitor real-time activities and detect malicious intrusions with improved precision. Experimental results demonstrate that GTDF achieves higher detection accuracy, lower false-positive rates, and faster threat identification. The proposed method gradually improves the detection accuracy by 97.2%, reduces FPR by 2%, detection latency by 178 ms, and scalability by 96.9%.

title: Supply Chain Resilient Security for Industrial 4.0 Using AI-Based and Digital Twin Anomaly Detection
authors: Sakshi Pandey, Harsimrat Kandhari, Syed Rashid Anwar, Manju Bargavi S K, Dibya Ranjan Das Adhikary, L. Jegan Antony Marcilin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The rapid innovation of Industry anul\^{} <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\{($</tex> Draft Situation 21 Small ball of paper\} Industry 4.0 has reconfigured supply chains into sophisticated, highly interconnected digital ecosystems, thus recommending robust mechanisms for the guarantees of operational resiliency and security The combination of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Digital Twin technologies presents new opportunities to protect these systems from the emerging threats of cyber and operational attacks. However, existing anomaly detection methods often struggle with scalability, lack contextual awareness of supply chain dynamics, and provide limited adaptability to evolving threats. These limitations reduce their effectiveness in realtime monitoring and early threat mitigation. To address these challenges, we propose the Supply Chain Twin Security Framework (SCTSF), which integrates Digital Twin Modeling to create virtual replicas of supply chain processes with the Isolation Forest Algorithm for advanced anomaly detection. This framework enhances system transparency, contextual monitoring, and predictive risk management. The proposed SCTSF framework is applied for detecting disruptions, fraud, and cyber anomalies across logistics, manufacturing, and distribution nodes, enabling proactive decision-making and rapid response. Findings demonstrate that SCTSF significantly improves detection accuracy, reduces false positives, and enhances resilience by providing real-time insights into vulnerabilities and threats. Thus, it establishes a reliable, adaptive, and intelligent approach for securing Industry 4.0 supply chains. The proposed method reduces response time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 1 \%}$</tex>, enhances resilience by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>, and improves scalability by 89.2%.

title: Intelligent Photovoltaic Array Fault Detection Mechanism in Dynamic Environmental Conditions
authors: J. Jayaprabakar, Umesh Daivagna, Ravi V Angadi, Byomakesh Dash, Manivasagam G, Prabhjot Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) arrays are critical in renewable energy systems, but their efficiency is often hindered by faults arising from dynamic environmental conditions such as shading, dust, and temperature variations. Intelligent fault detection mechanisms are crucial for ensuring reliability, efficiency, and uninterrupted power generation. Traditional fault detection methods rely on static thresholds or manual inspection, which suffer from low adaptability, poor accuracy, and high computational complexity under varying environmental conditions. Such constraints hinder in-time or accurate fault identification; and hence, aggregate energy yield. In view of these challenges, the current research work wishes to propose a Hybrid CNN - Swarm Fault Detection Framework (HCSF) which integrates the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The CNN part is used to extract robust features on various fault patterns (strong feature extraction), while PSO is used to iterative optimize the CNN hyperparameters (iteratively optimize) to achieve adaptive and accurate real time fault detection. The application of this framework takes place for continuous monitoring of photovoltaic (PV) arrays meeting the needs of prompt fault diagnosis and energy losses. It also enhances fault classification precision, making it suitable for large-scale PV systems operating in fluctuating environmental conditions. Experimental findings demonstrate that HCSF significantly improves detection accuracy, reduces false alarms, and outperforms conventional approaches in both adaptability and computational efficiency. The proposed HCSF framework achieves superior performance, with fault detection accuracy ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4}-\mathbf{9 7 \%}$</tex> and response time between 5962 ms across dynamic PV scenarios.

title: Exploring the Interaction of Fluid and Solid Properties in Electrical Tree Behaviour Within Silicon Gel
authors: Praveen kumar Balguri, Ashu Katyal, G. Arun Francis, Danish Kundra, V.J. Vijayalakshmi, Preetham K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The paper looks at how the fluid and solid properties interact in silicon gel to affect the formation and growth of electrical trees. Understanding this interaction is vital to understanding dielectric degradation in power insulation systems. A good deal of traditional research work, in addressing the behavior of materials subjected to electrical trees, does not account for fluid-solid interactions and allowing coupling of Multiphysics, and these oversimplifications yield a comparatively heroic effort to reliably predict the monotonic initiation and propagation of electrical trees. The capacitive Electric Field Induced Dielectric Failure (EFIF) modeling approach integrates, CFD - Computational Fluid Dynamics and FEA - Finite Element Analysis, modeling both the electromechanical and thermal processes of the electrical tree formation. This modeling approach also allows us to alternate boundaries and simulate the time dependant dynamic stress distributions, any heat dissipation, and charge transport processes in silicon gel-providing a more realistic representation of the tree propagation process. Amongst some of the results from the EFIF modeling approach, the accuracy of the tree growth is improved, explanations of the fluid and solid interactions together creating dielectrics before electrical breakdown, continuing work on optimizing materials.

title: Ethical AI Frameworks for Sustainable Clean Energy Integration: Challenges and Pathways
authors: Meshari Almutlaq, Abdulaziz Al-AIAbbad, Feroz Shaik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into the infrastructure of the systems of sustainable and clean energy can prove to be a revolutionary endeavor in the sense that it could help achieve a net-zero emission by 2050. But, when everything is conscionably determined, justice is jeopardized. The review is introduced to explore the idea of ethical AI systems clean energy, conceptualize and take into bankruptcy the challenges, like the existence of algorithmic bias, the guilt and problems associated with data privacy and environmental impact of AI, to boost sustainable, transparent and ethical growth and sustainable operationalization. It investigates the AI input in the areas of designing policies and optimization, and in predicting renewable energy. This is in a bid to foster fairness, sustainability, accountability, privacy and transparency. The various challenges and opportunities that are facing reality are given the context of the current oil economy of Saudi Arabian business in a case study of the Vision 2030. By the introduction of hybrid shapes of governance, where ethical audit is given, the stakeholders will be able to guarantee that AI is used to promote fair changes to clean energies. The review presents a description of how AI innovation can be aligned with existing human rights and environmental objectives.

title: An Analysis of Different Training Algorithms on Facial Expression Recognition System using Deep Learning
authors: Bulbul Agrawal, Kuldeep Narayan Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The human face plays a crucial role in distinguishing individuals based on their unique features. Facial expressions, in particular, serve as an essential medium for conveying non-verbal information. However, analysing facial expressions becomes a challenging and computationally demanding task for machines, especially when dealing with large-scale datasets. To address this, the present study proposes a deep learning-based architecture for facial expression recognition. Deep learning techniques are applied to image data to accurately identify and classify various emotional states. The performance of the proposed system is evaluated using three different learning algorithms on two benchmark datasets: the IMPA-FACE3D dataset and the FEI Face Dataset. Experimental analysis demonstrates that, for the binary classification task (FEI Face Dataset), the BR Backpropagation algorithm achieves the highest accuracy. In contrast, for the multiclass classification task (IMPA-FACE3D dataset), the SCG algorithm exhibits superior performance. Overall, the study highlights the potential of deep learning methods to significantly improve the automated recognition of human emotions from visual data.

title: Mosquito Growth Monitoring Device and System
authors: Shraddha Patle, Samiksha Moon, Shravani Bhrushundi, Tushar Chakole, Kapil Kumbhare, Swapnil Sur, Prachi Palsodkar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: In India Mosquito borne diseases such as dengue, malaria, chikungunya, and Zika virus remain a major global health concern. In India especially coolers are showing responsible for Dengue Larva growth during beginning of the rainy season. To address this problem, the mosquito growth monitoring device and system (MGMDS) is developed as a smart and automated solution to monitor mosquito larvae growth. The system continuously measures key environmental factors such as temperature, turbidity, pH, and humidity to determine whether the surrounding conditions support mosquito breeding. By using multiple sensors connected to an Arduino Nano and displaying data in real time, the device helps detect potential breeding hotspots and provides early warnings.

title: Network Security Auditing and Penetration Testing Framework for Robust Cyber Threat and Threat Mitigation
authors: Prachi Pathak, Rushita Kolekar, Karthik Devadiga, Rutwik Guraki, Advait Zende, Shreyas Koulage
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In the technologically evolving digital world, protecting Web applications, organizational networks and devices from cyber threats has became more difficult. The Eagles Eye framework also known as Network Security Auditing and Penetration Testing Framework, was developed with the aim of solving problems related to this by providing strong and easy to use solution. This framework includes various features such as advanced vulnerability scanning, penetration testing and real time threat detection which evaluate and reduce security risks. Eagles Eye uses effective tools and techniques such as Nmap, Metasploit, and OpenVAS to provide legitimate analysis of network infrastructure and potential cyber risks. A user friendly and professional graphical user interface has been created in the framework to allow professionals to check potential vulnerabilities more easily and effectively. This framework is designed with the goal of being modular so that it can scale, compatible with different environments and work perfectly with pre-existing security systems, which helps to perform network auditing and web application penetration testing easily with professionally generated report and visualizations. The Eagles Eye framework aim is to innovate, automate and provide reliability, thereby enhance cyber defence against unethical practices such as cyber threats and contributing important role in modern day digital ecosystem.

title: Enhancement of Frequency Regulation in LFC with a Modified Fractional Controller in Multi-Area Restructured Power Systems
authors: Abhishek, Ashiwani Kumar, Prabhat Kumar Vidyarthi
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The principle of load frequency control (LFC) is to retain power system frequency near its nominal value. The issue of frequency fluctuations in power system is addressed in this study. The power system is combination of two different energy sources thermal power and gas power is taken as investigated power system. The Quasi Opposite Crayfish Optimization Algorithm (QOCOA) is used as a soft optimization tool to fine-tuned of the suggested Controller parameters for LFC, by which we got the optimal control and dynamic system performance. so, with the use of proposed controller and optimized by QOCOA got improved frequency response of the power system. The suggested controller (FOPI-(1+PIDN)) is compared to basic PID and (PI-FOPID) controller and proposed controller's parameter fine-tuned with QOCOA were also compared with PSO and PSO with quasi opposite. System compares with renewable energy sources (RESs)

title: Fuel Cell Powered EV Charging with Modified SEPIC
authors: Venkatesh Reddy Birudala, Manasa Kalluru, Lohitha Kollam, Mohan Kumar G P, Poojitha K S, Niharika Kasetty, Shaik Rafi Kiran
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The blooming popularity of Electric Vehicles (EV) necessitates construction of grid-independent, sustainable and viable charging infrastructure. Grid stability, energy prices, and dependence on carbon-intensive electrical sources are issues facing current grid-tied systems. A Fuel Cell (FC) powered EV charging station using a high-efficiency, ripplemitigating Modified Single-Ended Primary Inductor Converter (SEPIC) architecture as the primary DC-DC boosting stage is presented in this work as a novel solution. While the improved SEPIC tackles the common problems of high input current ripple present in conventional boost converters, which can drastically reduce the fuel cell's lifespan and compromise power quality, the fuel cell stack offers a decentralized, emission-free power source. In order to reduce input current harmonics and obtain high voltage gain appropriate for EV battery management systems, the suggested improvement uses either an active filtering method or sophisticated interleaving/control. According to simulation and experimental results, the modified SEPIC converter ensures stable operation, maximizes fuel cell longevity, and provides a clean, dependable power interface for quick EV charging. It also offers superior efficiency (peak efficiencies above 97%) and significantly lower input current ripple (reduced by over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 {\%}}$</tex> compared to conventional SEPIC/Boost). This method offers a scalable and eco-friendly alternative for the electrification of transportation in the future.

title: SIMless Intranet Calling System
authors: Prabhakar Dorge, Prajyot Yelne, Shashank Sarve, Soham Phalke, Yash Borsare, Yash Channe
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Reliable voice communication is vital throughout the institutions and organizations, but it is prone to the failures of internet connectivity and cellular network in constrained or isolated settings. Our physical system is a SIM-less intranet calling platform that is going to be implemented on a Raspberry Pi based on the open-source Asterisk PBX platform to provide the Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) communication between SIP-based clients. Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) takes care of the registration of the user, call setup, and signalling, whereas Real-Time Transport Protocol (RTP) takes care of a data transfer, providing stable peer-to-peer communication. Softphone applications like Zoiper are used to make the connection to the users and authentication is done with the help of Asterisk configuration files like sip.conf and extensions. conf. The viability of using embedded platforms to power safe and decentralized intranet telephony was confirmed by the result of experimental tests that confirmed good connectivity, low developing latency, and audible performance. A solution suggested can be applied to campuses, laboratories, small businesses, and emergency operation where the constant use of the internal communication network is needed and does not rely on the public network. This work will help to develop autonomous VoIP communication structures which make them more robust, private, and affordable by combining low-cost hardware and open-source software.

title: Generative-Transformative Models for Sketch-To-Face Synthesis and Suspect Identification in Forensic Applications
authors: Shruthi A, K S Naghul Pranav, Sharmila B, Varun G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Diversity and multimodality of descriptions given by witnesses pose significant obstacles to early suspect identification and match in forensic investigations. Although the existing deep learning approaches are encouraging, they still have limitations, particularly in terms of the effective use of textual data and the use of sketch generation and sketch identification of the suspects. A novel generative-transformative framework is suggested to address these concerns. This system takes witness transcripts to generate detailed suspect sketches, uses the Stable diffusion to generate images, a Bayesian model to address uncertainties in human memory, and an implementation named Suspect Identification Module (SIM) to examine sketches against a face database with the assistance of deep learning-based face recognition. A hierarchical process enhances inter-modal consistency and selective fusion. Wide benchmarking on test data demonstrates that this framework can exceed state-of-the-art baselines, with sketch accuracies of 92 %, identically high AUC of 96 % and high identification precision. Furthermore, in-built interpretability techniques provide an easy understanding of how the model reaches decisions and makes it practical, understandable and useful tool of forensic work.

title: High-Sensitivity Detection of Paraquat via Water-Gated High-Electron Mobility Transistor
authors: Amirul Firdaus, Najihah Fauzi, Shuhadah Ismail, Mohammad Nuzaihan Md Nor, Hiroshi Karawada, Shaili Falina, Muhammad Ajmal Khan, Hideki Hirayama, Mohd Syamsul
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: A sensor based of high-electron mobility transistor (HEMT) offer significant prospective for real-time, label-free and direct detection. By utilizing the distinctive properties of two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG), these sensors enhance current variations in response to potential changes caused by molecular interactions, making it highly sensitive to surface charge fluctuations. The WGHEMT sensor provides a notable advancement in paraquat monitoring, offering accurate detection over 1–15 mg/L. With a dual-pool design separated by an ion-selective membrane, the device achieves a voltage sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$47.63 \mathrm{V} / \text{gL}^{-1}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.94912$</tex>, respectively. Additionally, its strong hysteresis performance underscores its suitability for real-world applications.

title: Dual Renewable Energy System Based on PV and Wind for Grid Support
authors: Kinan Saleem, Narendra Kumar, Vinod Kumar Yadav
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper discusses the extensive research in hybrid renewable energy system (HRES) that demands the increasing need for eco-friendly and sustainable energy worldwide. The extensively used renewable energy resources mainly includes solar photovoltaic (PV) and wind. Though, due to their multi-distributed character do not favor stable and reliable grid-connected power generation. In this study, the integrated windphotovoltaic power system is model for grid support purposes. The proposed concept utilizes a voltage source converter (VSC) interfacing to the grid and encloses a wind turbine and solar panel with an associated photovoltaic array combined through a shared DC link. DC link voltage is regulated and both active power (P) and reactive power (Q) are controlled by synchronizing technology and pulse width modulation (PWM) in the VSC. The results of simulation shows that the power combination controller can perform the stable running, reasonable distribution power for sources and specified capacity in constant wind speed and solar irradiance. This hybrid scheme emerges as a reliable and efficient unfolding for smart grid systems and sustainable grid integration.

title: IoT-Based Smart Agriculture System for Capsicum Frutescens Growth Optimization Using Led Technology and Multi-Parameter Monitoring
authors: Liu HanXu, Beh Khi Khim, Mohamad Adzhar Md Zawawi, Farhan Aizuddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent agriculture monitoring system for optimizing the growth of Capsicum frutescens (Cili padi) using Internet of Things (IoT) technology, full-spectrum Light-Emitting Diode (LED) lighting, and multiparameter environmental control. The system integrates microcontrollers with temperature/humidity sensors, capacitive soil moisture sensors, and Machine Vision System for Plant Growth Monitoring. The study examined seed germination and plant development over multiple growth phases, from initial 50° C heat treatment through <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70+$</tex> day cultivation periods using T8 LED grow lights with red-blue spectrum optimization. Results show 91.8 % germination rates were achieved with optimized seed treatment protocols. Growth monitoring from 7day seedlings to mature plants (up to 29.5 cm height) demonstrated significant variation based on fertilizer application and LED supplementation. The research contributes to precision agriculture by developing a digital monitoring framework combining LED technology with systematic growth measurement protocols, advancing sustainable food production through digital innovation in agriculture.

title: Performance Analysis of a Solar Powered Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor with Speed Control Using the MPC Method
authors: Siva Asapu, D. Susitra, Raju Lakkabattula, Sowjanya Kadali, Pichika Dolikananda, Kota Sri Rajeswari
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper presents the implementation of a Model Predictive Control (MPC) method on a solar powered - Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Drive (SP-PMSMD) system to achieve rapid and high-precision motor speed control under varying solar irradiance conditions. PMSMs are widely used across various industries due to their precise control capabilities, compact size, and high efficiency. The proposed work has two main objectives: the primary objective is to control motor speed using the MPC method under varying solar irradiance conditions, while the secondary objective is to ensure the extraction of peak power from the solar PV system and supply that peak power to the PMSM. Through a negative feedback loop, the MPC continuously generates a reference current signal for the Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) technique to control the frequency of the stator current. This paper analyzes the motor speed performance when the motor is controlled by the MPC method under different solar irradiance levels and evaluates both transient and steady-state behavior. The performance of the proposed system is tested using the MATLAB 2024a Simulink platform.

title: Electrical and Thermal Performance of BIPV Cladding System in Winter and Summer
authors: Priyanka Rai, Amartya Chowdhury
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This study presents a parametric analysis of a naturally ventilated building-integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) cladding system under representative winter and summer conditions. A two-dimensional numerical model is developed and validated using experimentally measured PV back-surface temperature and air-channel velocity data. Model accuracy is assessed using the mean absolute error and the coefficient of variation of the root mean square error, with values remaining below 3 % and 5 %, respectively, confirming the reliability of the numerical framework. The validated model is employed to investigate the effect of air-channel thickness on both the thermal and electrical performance of the BIPV cladding system for different façade orientations. Thermal indicators include PV operating temperature and heat flux through the building envelope, while electrical performance is evaluated in terms of power output and temperature-dependent efficiency. A multi-criteria decision-making approach based on the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution (TOPSIS) is applied to identify the optimal air-channel thickness. The results indicate that an air-channel thickness of 150 mm is optimal for south-facing façades, while 170 mm provides improved performance for east- and west-facing facades. These optimized configurations ensure balanced thermal regulation and enhanced electrical performance, offering practical design guidance for energy-efficient BIPV-integrated building envelopes.

title: Advancements in Converter Technologies for Renewable Energy Integration in Smart Grids
authors: Abhimanyu Kumar, Chirag Gupta, Varsha Mehar, Priyanka Maheshwari, Ambrish Pati Tripathi, Vinod Meena
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: One effective strategy to address global energy issues is the incorporation of sources of renewable energy to smart grids. The goal of this paper is to review recent advancements in converter technologies for the smooth integration of renewable energy sources (RES), such as wind and solar power, into smart grids in order to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and set sustainability in energy supply. Renewable energy is erratic and variable, but the following fundamental converter technologies enable grid stability and efficiency, including: multi-level converters, bidirectional converters, DCAC inverters, and AC-DC rectifiers. Wide bandgap semiconductors, machine learning and artificial intelligence for converter optimization, and distributed power converter systems are a few of these innovations that are briefly discussed in the paper. These technologies include superior efficiency in energy conversion and the minimization of losses in the course of power conversion with an overall improvement in the dependability of the systems, especially in integration with renewable sources of energy. The study continues with control strategies that include advanced and adaptive and predictive control methods with improved performance of the converter to dynamic grid conditions. Energy storage system integration improves flexibility in grids and makes real-time energy management possible. This will help the smart grids tackle the issue created by RESs' intermittence and enable a more reliable, resilient, and sustainable energy supply.

title: Intent-Aware Occupation Retrieval: From Natural-Language Queries to Standardized NCO Codes
authors: Manya Johri, Surya Dev Singh, Suryansh Singh, Uma Tomer, Nitin Tyagi, Biswajeet Pandey
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The data shown in National Classification of Occupation (NCO-2015) consists of several job opportunities and occupations for the citizens but it is very difficult to search just because of the use of traditional keyword search method which is a static method. Our system that is AIspire uses modern AI-based semantic searching mechanism with Sentence Transformer and multilingual translation to understand the intent of the user just like a normal human conversation. This system can retrieve the relevant occupations and perform better than BM25 keyword search. In this system Flask -based backend and structured JSON dataset derived from NCO-2015 was also implemented. It also has a voice -based search feature that improves the interaction and accessibility. The evaluation also shows that proposed AIspire approach improves the relevance as well as retrieved results compared to traditional methods. The main aim of the system was to provide more accessibility for the NCO-2015 and make it user friendly for students, job seekers and citizens of our country.

title: IoT-Driven Food Management System for Freshness Tracking and Predictive Analytics
authors: Charvi Suri, Shiwanshu Suresh Gilorkar, Shreyas Santosh Challawar, Saurabh Naresh Kuchar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Preserving the freshness of perishable produce, like climacteric fruits, is a global challenge. Traditional storage methods and current IoT solutions mainly offer passive monitoring. They lack the ability to control ripening conditions, which results in premature spoilage. This paper introduces a cost-effective food management system driven by IoT, integrating real-time sensing, closed-loop control, and predictive analytics. The prototype uses an ESP32 microcontroller, a DHT11 sensor for temperature and humidity, and an MQ-135 sensor for detecting CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and ethylene with Rs/Ro calibration. A Peltier module and a ventilation fan automatically manage temperature and gas levels. Meanwhile, real-time data is sent to the Firebase cloud for analysis. In a case study with bananas, the system increased shelf life from five to eight days by maintaining a temperature close to 1 5° C and humidity around 9 0%. A cloud-based machine learning model further predicts freshness in real time. This integrated IoT-ML framework shows a practical, low-cost way to reduce food waste and improve small-scale storage management.

title: EEG-Based Cognitive State Classification for Real-Time Car Simulation Control Using Deep Learning
authors: R. Sravanth Kumar, Katta Gnana Teja Reddy, Vaddi Brahma Siddhartha, Kothuri Koushik
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This research explores the use of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals for identifying cognitive states and interacting with a real-time driving simulator. A deep learning braincomputer interface (BCI) model is developed to classify mental states as Focused, Unfocused, and Drowsy using raw EEG data. A one-dimensional convolutional neural network (Conv1D) is trained on an openly available dataset collected using the Emotiv EPOC headset. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 84.93 % and a macro F1-score of 0.81 on unseen data. The predicted mental states are used to control a simulated vehicle, resulting in adaptive real-time behavior based on cognitive information.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Model Framework for Depression Prediction Using Socio-Demographic, Lifestyle, and Academic Variables
authors: Ruchika Pradeep, Deepa Gupta
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Depression is a chronic psychological illness that affects individuals' physical and mental well-being, leading to loss of interest, unexplained aches, which results in impairments in mood, health, and daily activities. This paper addresses individuals from diverse backgrounds in India, and a comparative study is carried out among ten machine learning models to predict the likelihood of depression in order to come up with the best-performing classifier. Ensemble models, which include boosting and bagging algorithms, and tree-based models, which includes decision trees and random forests and other classifiers such as support vector machine (SVM), logistic regression, KNearest Neighbor (KNN), and multilayer perceptron (MLP), are implemented and evaluated. Seven feature selection techniques are applied, and grid search has been used to improve the performance of the models. XGBoost performed the best with 93% accuracy, followed by Random Forest, SVM, AdaBoost and CatBoost, which achieved 92 % accuracy each. These results point to the potential of machine learning classifiers to identify underlying risk factors and make it suitable for predicting depression.

title: Impedimetric Detection of Alpha-Synuclein using Gold Interdigitated Tetraelectrodes: Morphological and Electrical Assessments
authors: Hussaini Adam, Subash C. B. Gopinath, Tijjani Adam, Mohd Khairuddin Md Arshad, Evan T. Salim, Makram A. Fakhri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Alpha-synuclein is a protein implicated with Parkinson's disease and as a potential biomarker for diagnosis. This study focuses on developing an electrochemical immunosensor using gold interdigitated tetraelectrodes (AuIDTEs) to detect alpha-synuclein. The research involves morphological and electrical assessments to analyze the sensing surface performance in detecting alpha-synuclein interaction with the immobilized antibody. High-power optical microscopy and 3D nano-profiler imaging techniques were used to evaluate electrode fabrication quality and surface modifications. Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) using different modes was employed to measure the electrical response across concentrations ranging from 1 attomolar to 1 picomolar of alpha synuclein. The results show distinct impedance profile and the resulting Nyquist and Bode plots confirmed a decrement in charge transfer resistance (Rct) with increasing concentrations. Sensor demonstrates high sensitivity, detecting below 100 aM, with consistent surface morphology and high signal-to-noise ratio. These findings support the viability of AuIDTEs as a highperformance sensing platform for early Parkinson's disease diagnostics.

title: Litter Vision: Evaluating Roadside Cleanliness
authors: Devaang Kumar, Krissh Batra, Aman Kumar, Sudha, Neha Sehgal, Hina Hashmi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The issue of urban littering is posing a growing challenge to environmental sustainability and public health. Traditional manual monitoring and cleanup strategies are inefficient, costly, and fail to scale with the rapid urbanization of smart cities. This paper presents Litter Vision, an automated deep learning-based system to get real-time litter detection, classification using the YOLOv8 object detection framework. The model utilizes enables immediate intervention responses, hence improving transfer learning on the TACO dataset optimized via public accountability and alleviating reliance on manual advanced pre-processing, normalization, and model tuning to intervention. Dependence upon irregular or post-event cleanup ensures robustness in varied environmental conditions. efforts do not do much to tackle the underlying causes of littering Experimental results show a mean Average Precision behavior, which tend to be driven by social, (mAP@50) of 0.985, precision of 0.976, and recall of 0.96, psychological, and cultural issues. The integration of outperforms past YOLO architectures. The computer vision introduced in this approach to urban surveillance infrastructure has some benefits in this sense. Municipal waste management systems to promote cleaner, safer, and smarter cities.

title: Automated Parking Lot Management System A Low-Cost Prototype for Automatic Garbage and Cleaning
authors: Pranav Kumar R, Darshan K, Hemanth T, Logappriya K, M.Aarthi Elaveini
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Parking in urban areas has become a major challenge because of the increasing number of vehicles. This results in traffic and wasted time. In most places, humans still control parking; this is inefficient and can be unreliable. For that, the project proposes an automated system for managing parking lots that reduces human effort while ensuring efficient utilization of space. The system consists of a collection of Infra- red (IR) sensors detect any vehicle in each slot, a microcontroller with inputs from the sensors that processes the input, and servo motors are used to manage the entry and exit gates. A I2C LCD display will be used to display real time slot availability, as well as assign a defined slot as each vehicle enters the parking lot and indicate when the parking lot is full. By removing the search to find a vacant space by allocating slots automatically this will improve parking processes, avoid overfilling and make the overall process much simpler. The aim is to create a cost- effective automated solution that compliments user convenience while provided overall improvements in the parking service. As this is a prototype all of its current form is highly adaptable and through the use of IoT, mobile apps and number plate checks it can be put into large scale use.

title: Attention-Driven Bidirectional LSTM with Metaheuristic Optimization for Smart Grid Stability Analysis
authors: Manohar Mishra, Soumya Ranjan Bhoi, Bighnaraj Naik, Geetanjali Bhoi, Rajat Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources, distributed generation, power electronic interfaces, and intelligent loads has significantly increased the dynamic complexity of modern smart grids, making accurate and realtime stability assessment a critical challenge. Conventional analytical and rule-based stability evaluation methods often fail to cope with fast-varying operating conditions and system uncertainties. To address this issue, this paper proposes an Attention-based Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Attention-BiLSTM) model optimized using the Adaptive Aquila Optimizer (AAO) for smart grid stability prediction. The Attention-BiLSTM architecture captures bidirectional temporal dependencies in grid operational data while dynamically emphasizing critical time steps and system features influencing voltage, frequency, and synchronism. AAO is employed to automatically optimize key hyperparameters of the model, including the number of LSTM units, dropout rate, and learning rate, thereby enhancing robustness and generalization. The stability prediction task is formulated as an F1-score maximization problem to effectively handle class imbalance and improve sensitivity to unstable conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed Attention-BiLSTM–AAO model outperforms GA-, PSO-, and AO-based counterparts, achieving an F1-score of 0.9961 and an F-beta score of 0.9972 with faster convergence. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework for reliable real-time smart grid stability prediction.

title: Wavelet-Alienation Based Diagnosis of Broken Rotor Bar Faults in Induction Motor
authors: Ravi Soni, Nandkishor Gupta, Bhuvnesh Rathore
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent and robust protection scheme for the fault detection and diagnosis of broken rotor bar faults in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-\phi$</tex> squirrel cage induction motors (SCIM). The proposed method employs Wavelet Transform for decomposing the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-\phi$</tex> current signals in the stator and extract the approximate coefficients that capture essential fault-related features. Subsequently, alienation coefficients are computed from correlation analysis among the phase components, serving as reliable fault indices. Fault conditions are identified by comparing these indices against a predefined threshold. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed algorithm offers high accuracy, computational efficiency and robustness in identifying rotor bar faults under diverse operating conditions.

title: Equivalent Circuits as Limitless Solution for OLED Modeling and Simulation
authors: Rimeh Sassi, Ghada Mbarki, Slimane Oussalah, Houssein El Housseiny, Cedric Renaud, David Buso, Skandar Douss, Mohamed Khalfaoui
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Compact electrical models are essential for OLED driver design, as physical fabrication is costly and TCAD simulations (e.g., SILVACO ATLAS) are computationally intensive. Buso's equivalent circuit provides a simple framework but assumes fixed capacitance and uses an exponential I-V law inadequate for multilayer OLEDs. This paper develops an improved LTSpice model for a specific multilayer structure (ITO/NPD/Alq3/BCP/Ca), calibrated against ATLAS reference data. Key enhancements include: a behavioral current source <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(I(V)=V / a+b \exp (c * V))$</tex> fitted to I-V characteristics, and voltage-dependent charge <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{V})=\mathrm{C}_{0} \sum \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{n}} \text{Vn}\right)(0-4 \mathrm{V}$</tex> range) yielding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{V})=\text{dQ} / \text{dV})$</tex>. Results demonstrate superior performance: I-V error reduced to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<5 \%$</tex> vs. 15% for Buso model, with accurate reproduction of capacitance peak <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sim 5.5 \mathrm{V})$</tex> and drop. This enhanced model facilitates rapid system-level simulation of OLED drivers, with potential extensions to thermal/aging effects.

title: Integration of Smart Agriculture and Energy for Empowering Rural India
authors: Pranjal Jog, Suwarna Shete, R.K. Kumawat, Ritu Tak, Pravin Sonwane, Gaurav Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper explores the transformative potential of integrating smart agriculture and renewable energy to empower rural India. The agricultural sector faces critical challenges, including low productivity and environmental degradation, while rural areas suffer from energy poverty. This paper argues that a symbiotic relationship, where smart farming technologies are powered by decentralized renewable energy systems, can create a sustainable and economically viable ecosystem. We detail key technologies, analyze socio-economic impacts, and propose a roadmap for large-scale adoption, contributing to rural revitalization and national food security.

title: Federated Meta-Learning for Lifelong Adaptation in Smart Building Portfolios: Learning to Learn without Forgetting
authors: Adam Bouras, Ali Benbrahim, Mohamed Wassim Fekih
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Smart building control systems face a critical limitation: their static intelligence degrades as building usage evolves, requiring costly manual recommissioning. This paper presents a novel framework combining federated learning with metalearning to enable building portfolios to collaboratively develop adaptive intelligence while preserving occupant privacy. Our architecture allows buildings to share learned adaptation strategies, not raw sensor data, enabling rapid response to concept drift through learning-to-learn mechanisms. Each building maintains a local multi-agent system with dual-memory continual learning, while participating in privacy-preserving federated meta-learning across a building portfolio. Through simulation studies on a 20-building portfolio expe-riencing diverse drift scenarios, we demonstrate that federated meta-learning achieves 87% faster adaptation to new patterns (2.3 days vs. 18 days to reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 \%}$</tex> optimal performance), maintains 89% knowledge retention across task transitions (vs. 34% for standard retraining), and reduces commissioning time for new buildings from 8-12 weeks to 3-5 days. The system achieves 31% energy savings compared to static control while maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\epsilon=2.4; \delta=10^{-5})$</tex> differential privacy guarantees

title: Smart Lighting and 5G Connectivity: Toward a Unified Infrastructure for Smart Campus Applications
authors: Rahim Kacimi, Laurent Canale, François Thiébolt, Yves Le Henaff, Georges Zissis
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Lighting infrastructures are evolving from static systems to dynamic, connected ecosystems that host sensing, communication, and computing functions. This paper presents the design and deployment of a 5G Standalone (SA) private network integrated with a smart lighting infrastructure on the Toulouse University campus. The objective is to build a multi-purpose, energy-efficient, and secure platform enabling experimentation in smart mobility, environmental monitoring, and digital twin applications. Smart lamp posts act as nodes combining LED lighting, IoT sensors, 5G and C-V2X radio units, and edge computing. Preliminary results demonstrate the feasibility of using lighting networks as communication and sensing backbones for advanced campus services, providing lowlatency and high-bandwidth connectivity to support AI-based control and real-time data collection.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)

title: Data-Driven Evaluation of Ev Charging Stations Using Multi-Criteria Scoring
authors: Jyoti, Purnima Garg, Priyanshu, Meeta Chaudhry, Sakshi, Vinay Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The necessity of efficient EV charging systems has grown to a large extent because the rate of EV adoption is rapidly growing. The selection of an appropriate EV charging station is extremely important to provide better user satisfaction and boost the adoption of EV in the market. The research produces a multi-dimensional decision tool that determines the appropriate charging stations based on three major variables, which are the location of users, level of traffic, and the accessibility of the facility ies. The data that is obtained on Kaggle consists of the data about charging stations, their location, power supply, the data about the vendor, and the timeframe of functioning. Combining data pre-processing and feature engineering with visualization applications enabled the development of a rating system based on composite scoring methods to be applied to the station. The analysis produced by the system is used to create useful suggestions because the top-rated stations provide excellent access and convenience to the user. The proposed approach will enable EV consumers to make informed choices and become the basis of real-time recommendation models.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning & Time-Series Approaches for Hourly GHI Forecasting—Dehradun (NSRDB 2000-2014)
authors: Aniket Kumar Rawat, Rakhi Bhammarkar, Mamta Dahiya, Manish Kumar Lakhiwal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Accurate short-term Estimation of solar radiation levels (especially Global Horizontal Irradiance, GHI) is critical for grid integration of solar power. This study uses a 15-year NSRDB dataset (2000-2014) for Dehradun (India) to develop and compare several forecasting models. After creating a continuous datetime index and extracting temporal features (hour, day, month, weekday) and a one-hour lag of GHI, we trained various ML models like Random Forest (RF), feedforward neural network (MLP) and deep learning models like LSTM, CNN+LSTM hybrid alongside a classical ARIMA time-series model. Assessment of performance was done using MAE, RMSE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^2$</tex> on a hold-out test set. Results show that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R F$</tex> model achieved the lowest error (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M A E \approx 9.05, R^2 \approx 0.994$</tex>), far outperforming ARIMA (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 231.6$</tex>, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 262.6, R^2 \approx 0.480$</tex>). The LSTM and CNN-LSTM hybrids also gave low RMSE (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 54.5$</tex> and 56.3, respectively) with strong accuracy. These findings demonstrate the advantage of nonlinear ML approaches (especially ensemble and deep learning) for GHI forecasting in this region. A performance comparison table is provided. This study's unique contribution is the integration of temporal feature engineering with hybrid models, specifically optimized for the Dehradun site, leading to improved performance over previous studies. Future research will investigate a broader range of weather inputs and enhanced hybrid ensemble techniques.

title: A Conceptual Framework for IoT-Integrated Sketch-Based Deep Learning Image Retrieval System
authors: Shubham Sharma, Akhilesh Tiwari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: This paper outlines a conceptual framework for IoT-enabled image retrieval using sketch-based deep learning. The framework integrates IoT sensing systems with deep learning models to retrieve visually related images from distributed IoT data sources in real time based on user supplied rough sketches. Instead of relying on a centralized convolutional neural networks (CNNs) pipeline, the architecture embeds sketch and image features at the edge using lightweight convolutional models, allowing the retrieval stage to remain responsive even in dense IoT deployments such as smart surveillance grids, medical monitoring systems and urban sensing platforms. Early evaluations show a noticeable jump in performance. Retrieval accuracy improves by roughly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-10 \%$</tex>, and the overall response time drops by more than half once the computation is shifted closer to the data sources. The paper also discusses suitable datasets, constraints in distributed deployment, communication overhead, and the practical hurdles of scaling this approach. The framework is positioned as a step toward making sketch-based querying feasible in real IoT scenarios, rather than just in controlled lab settings.

title: Unified Voxel-Centric LiDAR-Visual-Inertial SLAM for Large-Scale GNSS-Denied Environments
authors: Yinong Cao, Chenyang Zhang, Xin He, Pingfeng He, Tingwei Wang, Yuwei Chen
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Large-scale SLAM in GNSS-denied environments requires unified architectures integrating odometry estimation and loop closure detection while managing computational complexity. We present an adaptive voxel-centric SLAM framework employing a Hash-Octree structure for multi-modal information organization. The system extends single-camera voxel mapping to multi-camera configurations through multi-camera patch propagation. Each voxel maintains geometric plane parameters from LiDAR and multi-camera visual observations with temporal metadata. The Error-State Iterated Kalman Filter (ESIKF) tightly couples point-to-plane LiDAR constraints with multi-camera photometric residuals. Dual point cloud accumulators construct rotation-invariant descriptors in polar coordinates for efficient loop closure detection. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates superior performance in kilometer-scale environments.

title: GeoGuide: Immigrant Accommodation Navigator Using K-Means Clustering
authors: Aradhna Saini, Karishma Chauhan, Bikender Kushwaha, Ayush Singh, Himanshu Pathak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The GeoGuide: Immigrant Accommodation Navigator is an online platform made to make it easier for immigrants and people moving to new cities to find a place to live. It gives home suggestions based on what the person needs, like nearby places, location, and how much money they can spend. The platform uses smart tech like K-means clustering to find patterns in the housing data and what users like. There is also a GenAI Multilingual Chatbot: GeoGuide GPT that helps people right away if they have questions. GeoGuide also shows houses on a map, so users can see how close they are to jobs, schools, or bus and train stations. This makes moving easier and less stressful. With smart suggestions, helpful assistant, and easy-to-use maps, the platform makes the house search better and faster.

title: Simulation and Hardware Realization of Open-Loop SPS Controlled SiC-Based DAB Converter for Battery Charging
authors: Saswat Satpathy, Medipally Saivivek, Vikash Varma Katru, Monalisa Pattnaik
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The use of SiC MOSFETs enables faster switching, reduced conduction losses and higher breakdown voltage, making them ideal for high-frequency isolated power conversion. This paper presents the design and simulation of a silicon carbide (SiC) based dual active bridge (DAB) converter for electric vehicle (EV) battery charging applications. The converter operates at a switching frequency of 50 kHz, interfacing a 400 V DC source with a 60 V battery load. The power transfer is obtained by using single phase-shift (SPS) control strategy. A detailed analysis is developed to investigate the influence of leakage inductance and phase-shift angle on power transfer capability. Simulation results for a 5 kW, 400 V/60 V EV battery are presented to justify its compatibility in battery chargers. A hardware prototype of a DAB using SiC-based H-bridges interconnected via a highfrequency transformer and a leakage inductor is implemented for a resistive load. The SPS control is implemented using TMS320F28379D microcontroller to control the power transfer in the DAB converter. This work can be further extended in both simulation/hardware to charge EV battery in constant currentconstant Voltage (CC-CV) charging mode using an advanced closed-loop control technique, which is essential for optimizing battery charging performance.

title: A Systematic Analysis of Ontology-Based Techniques for Cloud Service Discovery and Composition
authors: Tanishka Rastogi, Vishal Kumar Singh, Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Cloud computing provides virtualized resources on- demand in the form of IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS, but the variety of cloud environments makes service discovery and composition difficult. The ontology-based methods solve this problem by providing semantic descriptions, reasoning, and interoperability to augment automated service matching and integration. This study focuses on a systematic review of ontology-based approaches to cloud service discovery and composition, where the main frameworks, reasoning methods, and semantic models suggested in the recent literature are analyzed. The paper divides existing methods into ontology-based discovery, semantic matchmaking, and composition with AI support and provides a comparative analysis in terms of scalability, adaptability, and quality-of- service (QoS) awareness. Key research gaps include alignment of the ontology, dynamic adaptation, and standardization. Lastly, there are emerging trends that include hybrid ontology-AI models, ontology-driven federated cloud discovery, and semantic trust management on blockchain.
