authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)

title: Digital Image Denoising: Evolution From Statistical Analysis to Neural Network Approaches
authors: Sumit R. Vaidya, Nidhi Tiwari, Rajesh Kumar Nagar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Picture speaks a thousand words; it is important to improve pictures. A relatively active area of image processing research involves removing noise from colour pictures. In this study, mean, median, gaussian, conservative, and Crimmins speckle removal are implemented and compared. Performance indicators including the Maximum noise to signal ratio, square root error (MSE), and number of Architectural Comparisons are employed to measure how well the repair filters work (SSIM). The findings and analyses show that gaussian, mean, and median are significantly more effective at repairing the deteriorated picture than conservative and Crimmins. The analysis also makes clear that SRCNN filters consistently perform better.

title: Parameter Matching and Modeling of Power Battery for Medium-Sized Electric SUV
authors: Yan Huang, Jianfeng Zhang, Chenglong Dai, Lin Ji, Hao Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In recent years, China has witnessed rapid growth in vehicle ownership, making the development of pure electric vehicles a crucial direction for industrial transformation. However, challenges such as limited battery energy density and motor control technology still require breakthroughs in addressing range and energy consumption issues. Solving these key technical challenges is vital for advancing pure electric vehicle development. This paper focuses on parameter matching and simulation verification for the power battery of a mid-sized pure electric vehicle. By establishing a system-matched Simulink model considering factors like battery discharge characteristics, this approach enables predictive system performance evaluation before physical prototype manufacturing, significantly reducing trial-and-error costs. Through virtual validation of different configuration schemes, we efficiently identify optimal designs, shorten R&D cycles, and accelerate product market entry.

title: Study on Methods for Resonant State Maintenance and Load Constant-Voltage Output in MCR-WPT System
authors: Chaoqun Nie, Guozhen Hu, Han Fu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address this issue and enable the Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) system to operate more stably in the resonant state while achieving constant voltage output, this study integrates a precise rectifier impedance modeling method with an enhanced BP-PID (Back Propagation-Proportional-Integral-Derivative) adaptive control approach. First, an equivalent impedance model of the rectifier is established to optimize the parameter design of the LCL-S compensation network, which substantially improves the accuracy of the system's resonant parameter configuration. Second, a PID controller based on an enhanced BP neural network is developed; this control algorithm eliminates the need for an accurate system equivalent model and can adaptively regulate the system's operating frequency and the inverter's output voltage through online learning. Finally, simulation experiments are performed using MATLAB/Simulink: initially, the resonant states of the traditional and optimized compensation parameter design methods are compared. Additionally, after optimizing the compensation parameter design, variations in inductance and capacitance during system operation, as well as changes in battery load resistance during charging, are incorporated into the simulation. On this basis, comparisons are conducted among the proposed control method, the fuzzy PID (FPID) control method, and the system without any control algorithm, focusing on their resonant states and output voltages. The simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed combined strategy, offering a practical technical solution for efficient wireless power transfer in complex environments.

title: Emotion-Aware Smart Lighting Via Large Language Models: A Conceptual Framework for Voice-Driven Sentiment-Based Control
authors: KeRen Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: This paper proposes a conceptual design for an intelligent lighting system that interprets human voice commands using large language models (LLMs) to identify user intent (e.g., switch on/off, dim/brighten) and emotional tone (positive, neutral, negative). The extracted semantics are mapped to RGBbased lighting adjustments, enabling mood–aware lighting control. This framework utilizes in–context learning to adapt pretrained LLMs for the sentiment–intent extraction task without fine–tuning. The paper integrates findings from emotion–aware lighting literature and emphasizes the role of AI in creating personalized lighting environments. Mathematical formulations support the mapping logic, and the framework sets a foundation for real–world adaptive lighting systems aligned with occupant emotion.

title: Eyesight Integrative AI for Noninvasive Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Using Pupillometry and Ensemble Deep Learning
authors: K Murugeswari, B Sridevi, D.Jeyamani Latha, Swedha V, Jayashree E, Trisha N
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: A novel non-invasive technology to screen and classify the severity of Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) based on Pupillary Response data. Rather than using conventional imaging techniques, this is based on the pupil light reflexage that are learnt by deep learning models such as ResNet, DenseNet, and EfficientNet. These models collaborate to categorize DR in five stages, which contributes to early diagnosis and treatment decision- making. Streamlit internals the application has been held together with Streamlit, a tool that makes it dead simple, fast and safe to code web front- ends. It has features to authenticate user and backend in Json to store the corresponding patient details, medical reports and scan results. This allows for a very user- friendly and secure system for controlling sensitive medical data. The results of our study indicate that Eyesight can objectively grade DR severity by evaluating pupil reflex alterations. It's an affordable and accessible tool, particularly in areas where traditional methods for screening eyes may not be available. Because of its combination of medical accuracy with convenience, the system makes more people inclined to check their own eye health routinely. This approach is interesting by its usage of pupil response data that was rarely investigated in DR detection. Eyesight brings a better solution for the world of eyes.

title: AI Impact on Sustainable Economy Growth
authors: Mhamed Serdaoui, Mounia Cherkaoui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly recognized as a transformative driver of sustainable economic growth, yet its effects remain ambivalent. On the one hand, AI enhances efficiency, transparency, and competitiveness across environmental, social, governance, and economic domains; on the other, rebound effects, inequality, and governance deficits can undermine its contributions [1]; [2]; [3] A review of the literature reveals three critical gaps: fragmentation across sustainability pillars, inconsistent measurement of AI's sustainability impact, and limited integration of AI into green finance mechanisms, particularly with respect to Scope 3 financed emissions [4]. Building on the five-pillar framework of [5], this paper addresses these shortcomings by proposing an integrated Green Finance–AI Framework and platform. The proposed solution links financial allocation with AI-enabled monitoring, verification, and reporting, allowing banks to transition from symbolic commitments to measurable decarbonization strategies. Using the MENA region as a case study of both vulnerability and opportunity, the findings show that the convergence of AI and green finance advances sustainability across all five pillars: environmental protection, social inclusion, economic resilience, governance accountability, and digital transformation. In doing so, the study contributes to scholarly debate and policy practice by positioning financial institutions as active agents of decarbonization and inclusive growth.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Droop Control and Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) Control for Grid Forming Inverter in Weak Grid Condition
authors: Jacintha Dias, Asheesh Dhaneria
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Grid-forming inverters are crucial for the future power grid with high penetration of renewable energy sources. They use a power-based synchronization design concept to generate voltage. This allows them to operate reliably in lowstrength grid areas and deliver crucial system support, which includes voltage regulation, frequency stability and inertia. This paper carries out the comparative analysis of two control strategies for grid-forming inverters in weak grid condition droop control and virtual synchronous machine control. While droop control utilizes P-f and Q-V relationships to emulate synchronous generator behavior enabling active voltage and frequency regulation, the virtual synchronous machine emulates the complete dynamic behavior of a traditional synchronous generator including its mechanical inertia and damping characteristics. The paper outlines the merits of virtual synchronous machine in terms of inertia and rate of change of frequency when compared with droop control.

title: Solar-Assisted Electric Vehicle Charging System with Bidirectional G2V and V2G Operation in MATLAB/Simulink
authors: Aman Anand, Nikhil Arunrao Bodkhe, Debayan Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Global transition toward renewable energy has intensified interest in integrating solar photovoltaics (PV) with electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure. This study presents the design and simulation of a solar-assisted EV charging circuit capable of operating in both Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) modes. The system is modeled and analyzed using MATLAB/Simulink, incorporating advanced power electronic interfaces such as a bidirectional AC-DC converter, DC-DC boost and buck-boost converters, along with intelligent control strategies. A Proportional Resonant (PR) controller is employed to enhance power quality, while a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithm ensures optimal solar energy utilization. Unlike conventional unidirectional charging systems, the proposed framework enables efficient bidirectional power flow that not only preserves battery health but also actively contributes to grid stability under varying operating conditions. Simulation results validate the system's effectiveness, underscoring its potential as a sustainable and intelligent charging solution for next-generation smart grid applications.

title: Implementation of a Smart Lighting System Based on IoT Networks at the PTV Waste Management Facility in Madrid
authors: Guillermo del Campo, Igor Gomez, Edgar Saavedra, Asuncion Santamaria
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Smart Lighting is one of the most mature and high-impact applications of the Internet of Things (IoT) in Smart Cities, enabling dynamic control of luminaires according to real demand, environmental conditions and safety requirements. Yet many existing deployments remain as vertical silos, vendor-specific and poorly integrated with other urban services. This paper describes the design, deployment and initial evaluation of an IoT-based smart street-lighting system at the Valdemingomez Technology Park (PTV) in Madrid, a large waste-management facility characterized by highly variable activity patterns and demanding operational conditions. The system is built on the IoTMADLab reference architecture, an open, neutral and interoperable IoT network model. The deployment at PTV includes 60 IoT-controlled LED luminaires, motion and parking sensors, environmental probes and power meters, supporting cross-service interoperability, network management and advanced monitoring. Initial results show that the system can characterize daily and nightly activity patterns, identify high-traffic routes and loading bays, adapt illumination to real use through presence and parking detection, quantify the energy impact of smart control and support predictive maintenance and anomaly detection. The interoperable design allows the lighting system to be managed as one vertical while sharing infrastructure with other services, easing future cross-domain applications.

title: Comparative Study on DGA and UV Characteristics of Liquid-Phase Products in Transformer Oil Discharges
authors: Bo Lu, WenBin Zhao, XuKun Qiang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Power transformers are critical infrastructure components. Frequent transformer failures underscore the need for accurate fault diagnosis to ensure operational safety and reduce maintenance costs. This study employs gas chromatography (GC) to measure dissolved gases (H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, CH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) in transformer oil under varying fault conditions. By applying the three-ratio method and Duval Triangle technique, the correlation between gas types and fault types is analyzed, and fault inference based on gas content variations is explored. Dissolved gas content changes under different discharge durations are also measured. Additionally, UV spectrophotometry examines oil composition changes before and after discharge, identifying altered components and analyzing their causes. The findings offer insights for transformer fault diagnosis and early warning.

title: Modeling of a DC Corona Discharge: The Plasma Physics Foundation for EHD-Driven Ionic Wind in Advanced Illumination Applications
authors: Ahlem Ben Halima, Zouhour Araoud, Laurent Canale, Georges Zissis, Kamel Charrada
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: The present study details a numerical model of a positive DC corona discharge operating in dry air under atmospheric pressure. A coaxial geometry is considered, which is representative of configurations found in devices like electrostatic precipitators and electrohydrodynamic (EHD)driven air movers. The model consists of a central wire electrode (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> radius) to which a potential of 10 kV is applied, and a grounded concentric electrode separated by a 10 cm gap. The primary objective is twofold: first, to analyze the spatial distribution of charged particles and the electric field, and second, to provide insight into how these plasma properties constitute the driving force for the EHD phenomenon known as ionic wind. This work lays the essential groundwork for modeling the resulting gas flow by precisely calculating the EHD body force <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{f} \_\mathbf{E H D}=\mathbf{\rho E}\right). { }^{\prime \prime}$</tex>

title: Power System Transient Stability Assessment Based on Data Processing
authors: Wenbin Sun, Xiao Tan, Zhenyu Xu, Yao Xiao, Chenglin Zhang, Hongyan Zeng, Jianxiang Fu, Lijia Xu, Jiajia Xiang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Real-time measurement data typically demonstrate characteristics such as high dimension, feature redundancy, insufficient boundary information, and class imbalance, which reduce the training effect of transient stability assessment model. Particularly, feature redundancy and fuzzy boundary information make certain samples in a stable fuzzy region, which further hinders the model from fitting a stable boundary. Meanwhile, the class imbalance makes the model biased towards samples from the majority class, which goes against the original intention of the power system to attach importance to instability samples. To address the problem of feature redundancy and insufficient boundary weight, this paper proposes a data preprocessing method, which integrates time-series self-encoder (TCN-AE/LSTM-AE), pre-training (DeBERTa), self-supervised time-series feature learning (SimSiam-TCN). More specifically, TCN-AE/LSTM-AE is employed to process the time-varying noise and dynamic outliers of transient data, while DeBERTa is adopted to improve the reliability of feature importance evaluation, with SimSiam-TCN determining the association of low-redundancy time-series features. To address the class imbalance of samples, this paper combines time-series diffusion model (TimeDiffusion) and comparative learning oversampling (SimCLR-OverSampling) method to enhance instability-class samples to balance the proportion of samples and strengthen the feature discrimination of boundary samples. Experimental results indicate that samples after data processing present the characteristics of nonredundancy, class balance, and enhanced boundary weight. The proposed data processing mode is capable of effectively improving the training accuracy of various machine learning models. Furthermore, given the adaptability of data processing method and tree structure model, deep forest is selected as the evaluation model. In this connection, the simulation results of examples demonstrate that compared with other models, the proposed transient stability assessment method exhibit higher recognition rate for instability-class samples, stronger noise resistance, and excellent extensibility for large-scale samples.

title: Nighttime Traffic Accidents and the Role of Headlamp Glare: A Statistical Analysis of Swedish Crash Data
authors: Maria Nilsson Tengelin, Stefan Källberg
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Traffic safety during nighttime remains a critical concern, particularly for vulnerable road users such as pedestrians and cyclists. This study investigates the role of glare from headlamps and other artificial lighting as a contributing factor in traffic accidents using Swedish crash data from the STRADA database (2010–2024). A total of 549 injury-related accidents were identified as associated with nighttime glare. The analysis reveals that while glare-related accidents constitute a small fraction of overall traffic incidents (approximately 37 per year or 0.2% of all nighttime accidents), certain patterns emerge: collisions involving pedestrians carry the highest risk of severe outcomes, single-vehicle incidents are the most frequent, and wildlife collisions show the highest proportional link to glare. These findings indicate that, although uncommon, glare can contribute to serious accidents, particularly for vulnerable road users, and underscore the importance of appropriate headlamp usage and glare mitigation strategies during nighttime driving.

title: Uses of Drone for Evaluating Gram Yield Under Customized Fertilizer Regimes
authors: Jahar Singh Rawat, Pramod Kumar Patel, Jinendra Birla, Raj Kumar Dhakad, Rakam Prashanth, Leena Shrivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The use of drones in precise farming is explored in this investigation. The agriculture sector can now handle issues like pest infestations and abrupt climate changes that can have a major influence on crop quality thanks to recent technological breakthroughs. In India, gram is an essential pulse crop for sustainable agriculture and food security, particularly in areas with black soils (Vertisols). Using drones, the results showed that personalized fertilizer applications greatly increased important growth indicators such grain yield, seed index, and root nodule weight at 30 and 90 days after sowing (DAS). The sub-optimized and control (T6) treatments, on the other hand, showed reduced profitability and productivity. The research finds that within black soil conditions and drone surveillance, a customized fertilizer application based on crop requirements and soil nutrient status greatly increases Gram productivity and profitability. In order to evaluate the effectiveness of two insect identification algorithms, Random Search and Distributed Search, this study compares them.

title: Alkaline Electrolyzer and Fuel-Cell Integrated System for Green Hydrogen Production and Power Generation
authors: Shravan Jaiswal, Anmol Ratna Saxena
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This paper presents a mathematical modeling and simulation framework for an Industrial alkaline electrolyzer integrated with hydrogen storage and fuel cells for renewable energy applications. The electrochemical performance of the electrolyzer is described using a semi-empirical logarithmic equation that accounts for activation and ohmic losses. A 221-cell electrolyzer stack was modeled with an input voltage of 400 V and an operating temperature of 60° C, while polarization curves show for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and 80° C to evaluate temperature effect. The produced hydrogen was stored in a pressurized tank and subsequently supplied to a fuel cell to regulate output voltages of achieve maximum power point operation, resulting in both voltage and current reaching their optimal values simultaneously. varying source and load conditions. The MATLAB/Simulink-based model enabled analysis of hydrogen flow rate, Faraday's efficiency, and system voltage stability. The results demonstrate that higher operating temperatures reduce voltage requirements at a given current density, thereby improving efficiency. Overall, the proposed framework offers a reliable tool for designing and optimizing green hydrogen energy systems with applications in renewable energy storage, fuel cell vehicles and smart grid integration.

title: Cognitive Biases and Consumer Buying Decisions: A Bibliometric Perspective on Marketing Strategies
authors: Sudheer Nandi, Preetha Subrahmanyan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In recent years, a lot of advertising and marketing experts have been very interested in consumer behavior, online purchasing, and how users make decisions. The process of consumer decision is complex and influenced by many variables, including cognitive bias. These biases, which are natural mental shortcuts or tendencies, can significantly impact the decisions made by consumers. Marketers and strategists who want to successfully target and influence their target audience must comprehend how cognitive biases impact consumer decision-making. As a result, customers may display biases such as anchoring bias, confirmation bias, and availability prejudice, among others, while making purchase decisions. A systematic examination and bibliometric evaluation were conducted to objectively and analytically reveal the relationship between cognitive biases and consumer purchasing decisions. By integrating both exclude and include criteria, 40 studies in all were identified and classified. The dataset from 2020 to 2024 was assessed in this study utilizing bibliometric analytic methods and publications from trustworthy journals such as Elsevier, emerald.com, mdpi.com, springer, and Wiley online library. To analyze citations, journals, and co-occurrence, the VOS viewer application was used. These results outline the issues, publications, and research tools that merit further investigation. Additionally, discuss how such biases affect marketing efforts and provide ways to lessen their detrimental consequences. In its conclusion, this study offers policymakers and marketers insightful information that can improve consumer decisionmaking and produce more successful marketing efforts.

title: Multi-Sensor Fusion Detection Method for SF6 Gas Concentration Using Transfer Learning
authors: You Li, Wen Kang, Ting Zhou, Xiao Wang, Zhou Xiong
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Due to the limitations of their operating principles and detection range, a single sensor can only capture partial characteristic information about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> gas concentration, resulting in low detection efficiency. Therefore, a multi-sensor fusion detection method for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> gas concentration using transfer learning is proposed. Sensors using different principles collect data at specific locations. After normalization and noise reduction, a weighted fusion strategy is used to integrate the multi-sensor data. Random perturbations are introduced to enhance model generalization. After data fusion, a dual transfer learning framework based on DenseNet169 and a one-dimensional convolutional model is constructed. Pretrained features are preserved by freezing some convolutional blocks, and multimodal features are fused using a concatenation strategy to generate a fused feature vector. After extracting multimodal features, a random forest algorithm is used to construct a prediction model. A decision tree is trained by minimizing the Gini coefficient. The prediction results of the decision tree are summed up using a majority voting mechanism to output the final <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> gas concentration value. Experiments show that the research method optimizes the mean absolute error (MAE) to 2.1ppm at low concentration gradients (a 70.5% reduction compared to the optimal value of the comparison method), reduces the root mean square error (RMSE) to 18.7 ppm at medium concentration gradients (a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 8. 7 {\%}}$</tex> reduction), and compresses the maximum absolute error (MaxAE) to 256.1 ppm at high concentration gradients (a 60.8% reduction). Furthermore, the coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}})$</tex> approaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 9}$</tex> across the full concentration gradient (an average improvement of 0.23 compared to the comparison method). The single-shot detection time (Td) is shortened by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 {\%}-45 {\%}$</tex>, and the dynamic response delay (TRd) is reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 {\%}} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{6 5 {\%}}$</tex>. Experiments demonstrate that the research method significantly improves the efficiency of SF 6 gas concentration detection by enabling cross-scenario knowledge transfer and deep mining of multi-source heterogeneous data through a transfer learning framework.

title: Explainable Hybrid Intrusion Detection: Combining Ensemble Learning and XAI for Transparent Cybersecurity
authors: Sultanul Arifeen Hamim, Md Tafhimul Haque Sadi, M. Firoz Mridha, Touhid Bhuiyan, M. Ameer Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Network intrusion detection faces significant challenges due to evolving cyber threats and the complexity of network traffic. This paper proposes a novel hybrid machine learning (ML) model integrated with Explainable AI (XAI) for enhanced detection accuracy and interpretability. Our approach combines Isolation Forest for efficient anomaly detection with a stacked ensemble classifier, utilizing XGBoost, Random Forest, Extra Trees, and a Multi-Layer Perceptron, with Logistic Regression as the meta-learner. Evaluated on the UNSW-NB15 dataset, the model achieved an impressive 97% accuracy, outperforming individual baseline models. Crucially, SHAP and LIME were incorporated to provide global feature importance and local instance-level explanations, respectively, addressing the “blackbox” challenge. This work demonstrates the development of a highly effective, transparent, and understandable network intrusion detection system, fostering greater trust and enabling more informed cybersecurity operations.

title: Comparative Analysis of Classification Algorithms for Predicting Healthcare Outcomes: A Study Using Patient Diabetes Data
authors: Trilok Nath Pandey, Ansh Pandey, Utkarsh Mishra, Sunil Kumar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In this research paper, we compare ten state-of-the-art classification algorithms to predict health risks in diabetes patients. With a real-world patient data set, we analyze the efficacy of SVM (Support Vector Machines), KNN (K-Nearest Neighbors), Decision Tree, ANN (Artificial Neural Networks), Logistic regression, Gaussian Naive Bayes, Bernoulli Naive Bayes, LDA (Linear Discriminant Analysis) and QDA (Quadratic Discriminant Analysis), CNN (Convolutional Neural Network), and RNN (Recurrent Neural Network). All models were quantified and compared against the important performance metrics normally seen in health risk prediction: accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score providing an extensive evaluation of the predicting power of each with respect to health care risk. We then assess and investigate the strengths/weaknesses of each algorithm and contextually their capacity to evaluate diabetes health risks. The findings of this research can assist health practitioners and researchers identify and enunciate any health predictions for health care that can be framed into any one of these models again improve the decisionmaking process and patient care. This study advances the ongoing discourse on machine learning for health to lend empirical data by comparing comparative effectiveness of alternative classification.

title: Improved YOLOv8s Cotton Apical Bud Detection in Complex Field Environments
authors: Haoran Wang, Musha. Yasenjiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Accurate detection of cotton apical buds is a prerequisite for automated mechanical or chemical topping. However, apical buds are small, partially occluded by leaves and branches, and captured under strong illumination changes and motion blur caused by vehicle movement, which makes robust detection in real fields highly challenging. Recent works have adopted improved YOLO variants for cotton apical bud detection and achieved promising performance, but most of them are still limited by insufficient exploitation of shallow details, sub-optimal localization of tiny buds and limited robustness under complex field conditions. In this paper, we present a compact yet effective YOLO-based framework for cotton apical bud detection in complex environments. Taking YOLOv8 as the baseline, we design a detail-aware backbone, a high-resolution detection head and a small-object-oriented loss, while keeping computation at a level suitable for on-board deployment. The model introduces a detailaware C2f (DA-C2f) module in the shallow backbone to strengthen edge and texture representation of buds, adds a P2 detection head dedicated to tiny buds, adopts a refined bi-directional feature pyramid with content-aware upsampling for cross-scale fusion, and uses a compound regression loss combining a shape-aware IoU term with a normalized distance metric. Experiments on a selfbuilt cotton apical bud dataset collected under diverse illumination, occlusion and motion conditions demonstrate that the proposed model achieves higher mAP and recall than several YOLO detectors, while maintaining efficiency suitable for integration into cotton topping equipment.

title: SmartCare: A Real-Time Fall Detection and Health Monitoring System
authors: Sambhav Tandon, Amit Kumar, Ritvik Singh, Nitika Dhingra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The exponential increase in the population of old age people has created an immediate demand for a dependable, realtime health monitoring devices that have the capability of monitoring vital health constraints and giving update on one of the most dangerous health issues, i.e. fall detection of old age people. This article will be presenting an smart wearable health detecting solution which is built using Arduino Nano 33 IoT development board and also uses various sensors like MPU6050 as motion detection module, an AD8232 sensor used for ECG monitoring of elder people and an analog based heart rate monitoring sensor. The system is designed to continuously collect the acceleration data and various other physiological data for identification of fall occurrence, and measures ECG signals and heart rate of the person. It also uses a 16*2 LCD display module so as to provide the real-time visualization of the data of person. Further, a buzzer is used to provide immediate alerts whenever any abnormal activity is detected. For improved accuracy of the system, moving average filters are also applied to the acceleration data of the person and heart rate values are also computed with a 10 seconds gap in between two values. It also uses a cooldown mechanism for reducing false positives during the repeated movements. Since it is a low cost and compact design, it will be a promising solution for the elder people. Future enhancements will include GPS location, cloud-based connectivity and machinelearning approaches for advanced anomaly detection.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Through Deep Neural Network
authors: Aryan Tyagi, Aman Chaudhary, Abdul Aleem
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Brain Tumor Detection has always been one of the most difficult task in the medical history due to its manual classification, the risk of inaccuracy in prediction is high. The amount of data stored is too voluminous, which further increases the chances of error. Brain tumor exhibit diverse pattern but due to similarity between tumor tissues & normal tissues, accurate detection becomes critical. A new technique of identifying and detecting type of brain tumor with 2D magnetic resonance brain image has been presented, which utilizes convolutional neural network (CNN). During the analysis of tumor in concurrent time, it is observed that tumors have difference in shape, size and are present in different locations. The proposed CNN method has been successful in detecting all types of tumors. When compared with different algorithms like support Vector Machine, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random forest, VGG16, Logistic Regression, and Light GBM, the proposed model gave best result with the accuracy of 97.87%.

title: Design and Implementation of a Software Metrics Dashboard for Quality Assurance Automation
authors: Shiva Tyagi, Tannu Kumari, Someshwar Prasad Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Ensuring software quality is often hindered by fragmented tools and scattered reports. This study proposes an integrated Software Metrics Dashboard for QA that combines key quality and operational metrics-including defect density, test coverage, resolution time, release stability, and system uptime-into a unified platform. Data is gathered from sources like Jira, Confluence, XRAY, and ServiceNow, while OpenCVbased visual analysis enhances detection of UI inconsistencies. The modular PRIME ETL architecture enables efficient metric extraction, transformation, and visualization. By integrating multiple metrics and visual inspections, the dashboard provides a comprehensive view of software health. A review of 60 studies on dynamic metrics (1992-2011) demonstrates their growing importance in software quality. The dashboard supports datadriven decisions, process optimization, and continuous improvement in software development.

title: Machine Learning-Based Identification of Leukemia Subtypes Through Gene Expression Profiling
authors: Iqra Nissar, Danish Raza Rizvi, Aqib Nazir Mir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Machine learning has become a powerful tool in improving cancer diagnostics, such as leukemia, a heterogeneous blood cancer that can be highly challenging to diagnose because of the complexity of its subtypes, along with clinical variation. Gene expression profiling of DNA microarray offers a high-throughput method to determine biomarkers and enable precise classification of leukemia subtypes. This study examines the performance of machine learning models, such as decision tree, random forest, support vector machine, and multi-layer perceptrons, for the problem of leukemia subtype classification based on high-dimensional gene expression data acquired from the CuMiDa repository. The minimum redundancy maximum relevance feature selection method was used to select the 30 most informative gene features out of the high-dimensional features, which is aimed at dealing with the issue of high dimensionality and feature reduction. The classification models were evaluated under two scenarios: when all gene features were used and when mRMR-selected features were used. The findings indicate that the feature selection method is a critical way of optimizing the model performance, with MLP achieving the optimal accuracies of 0.97 and 0.93 on two-class and five-class datasets, respectively. The performance of tree-based models remained subpar compared to other models. The results of these experiments demonstrate the significance of feature selection in analyzing high-dimensional expression of genes and highlight MLP and SVM as highly effective models to classify the leukemia subtypes.

title: ACSP: Activity-Based Cooperative Security Protocol for Internet of Things
authors: Abdul Ahad, Ash Mohammad Abbas
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: An increase in the devices connected to an Internet of Things (IoT) poses new security issues, especially in monitoring the activities of nodes and determining security violations. Conventional centralized security systems have limitations in terms of scalability, latency, and single points of failure. In this paper, we propose a protocol based on a paradigm in which nodes in an IoT record the activities of neighboring nodes to generate activity logs. The activity logs are analyzed to classify the behavior of nodes in the network. Analysis of activity logs enables nodes to identify possible threats, notify neighbors, and raise an alarm about the devices with malicious behavior in their neighborhood. The performance of the protocol is evaluated using simulations for IoTs in indoor and outdoor scenarios. The effect of the number of nodes, the fraction of suspicious nodes in the network, and the timeout for switching from a suspicious state to a malicious state of a node under observation on the number of malicious nodes in the network is investigated. The findings of the research may contribute in the design of a decentralized and adaptive system based on the cooperation of nodes to detect devices with malicious behavior in an IoT.

title: Errata
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)

title: Performance Evaluation of Heuristic and AI-Based Load Balancing Techniques for Edge Computing Systems
authors: Neetu Bansla, Oshin Sharma, Pankaj Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Cloud Edge computing has acquire application areas like autonomous vehicles, smart cities, IOT etc. where latency is a significant factor. Traditionally cloud use to be a single point for processing and hence there was no closeness to the data source resulting in poor response time or latency and poor bandwidth usage while working on real time data. Although, the heterogeneous devices and internet availability in edge computing brings a lot of computing challenges in resource allocation and load balancing, required for high system performance, reliability, and energy efficiency. In this paper a performance evaluation traditional and AI-based load balancing techniques designed for edge computing systems is presented. Due to their simple implementation, minimal overhead, traditional heuristic-based load balancing algorithms such as Round Robin (RR), Least Connections (LC), and Weighted Least Connections (WLC) are popularly used. Such algorithms are best suited for predictable contexts as they function as per the pre-set rules or static criteria. Although, their accuracy in practical implementation is not so popular due to their inability to adjust according to the resource heterogeneity, user mobility, and workload fluctuations. Such methods expertise in situations where high fluctuations and can be optimized during resource allocation dynamically on the basis of past results or patterns available and energy constraints. Metrics like adaptability, scalability, fault tolerance, computational complexity, energy efficiency, and Quality of Service (QoS) delivery are analysed for both traditional & AI based methods in this study.

title: Methodology of Complex Steganalysis Using Image Quality Characteristics Estimation
authors: Yaroslav L. Grachev, Valentina G. Sidorenko
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The paper presents a methodology for complex steganalysis that takes into account the quality characteristics of the analyzed image. The proposed method improves the accuracy of the joint application of statistical steganalysis methods, mainly by reducing false-positive results.

title: Pavitra Step: An IoT-Based Smart Health Monitoring Device with Android and Bluetooth Integration
authors: Rakhi Sharma, Utkarsh Raj, Santosh Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: It is time for affordable, durable, and culturally adaptable health monitoring solutions as the increasing incidence of lifestylerelated diseases, deteriorating air quality, and inadequate access to healthcare in marginalized regions all point to dire public need. While smart wearables already exist and are technologically capable, most are too expensive or fragile, or otherwise inappropriate for widespread application. Current literature reflects challenges in affordability and accessibility, while most IoT-based health devices face power consumption and scalability issues. Most previous footwear-based health systems have focused on gait analysis or a limited set of vital parameters, indicating the dire need for a more holistic and user-oriented solution. To address these limitations, we design Pavitra Step-an IoTenabled smart flip-flop that features sensors for heart rate and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SpO}_{2}$</tex> (MAX30102), air quality (MQ135), temperature and humidity (DHT22), and plantar pressure (FSR). The system uses an ESP32 microcontroller with BLE connectivity to relay data in real time to a multilanguage Flutter mobile application that supports more than 14 languages. The footwear has a 3D-printed EVA foam sole, ensuring comfort, water-proofing, and a culturally acceptable design that fits every user's needs and preferences. In an experimental evaluation with 30 participants over 14 days, the accuracy of the developed system was above 90 % compared to medical-grade instruments, with low latency and power usage, together with high user satisfaction. Pavitra Step is a major step forward in wearable health monitoring, as it integrates multiple sensors, environmental awareness, and inclusive design into a single affordable and robust platform.

title: Research on Multi-Level Safety Protection Technology for Robotic Operations in High-Voltage Environments
authors: Xiaolai Li, Chen Gao, Shiqiang Yang, Beichen Gao, Huang Wei, Xinru Cheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address critical challenges such as electromagnetic interference and insulation breakdown faced by specialized robots in complex environments involving strong electromagnetic fields and high voltages-such as high-voltage transmission line inspections-this paper proposes a layered collaborative protection system. Breaking through the limitations of traditional single-protection modes, this system employs a multi-level, integrated hardware-software protection strategy to significantly enhance operational safety and reliability under extreme working conditions. For electromagnetic shielding, a dual-layer composite structure of iron and Permalloy is employed to effectively suppress high-frequency electromagnetic interference and low-frequency magnetic fields. At the control system level, a dual-layer anti-interference architecture integrating hardware and software collaboration is established to further enhance electromagnetic compatibility. For high-potential protection, the support structure length is optimized based on equipotential operation principles. Combined with an active potential transfer strategy, this keeps the potential difference between the wheels below 0.01 V, effectively preventing transient discharge risks. Experimental results demonstrate the system's outstanding performance under extreme conditions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$110 \text{kV} / 500$</tex> A: sensor measurement error remains below 0.1%, communication bit error rate falls below 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup>, insulation performance meets air critical breakdown field strength requirements (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25-30 \text{kV} / \text{cm}$</tex>), and all functional modules operate stably. This research provides a systematic and implementable solution for the reliable operation of robots in strong electromagnetic-high potential composite environments.

title: Mathematical Model of Electronic Document System Dynamics for Identifying Business Process Bottlenecks
authors: Irina R. Shekhovtsova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The transition to electronic document management systems has become a critical strategic initiative for modern organizations. Measures taken to organize electronic document flows in a rational manner are particularly relevant, as their imperfections can lead to significant losses for organizations. A well-structured approach is proposed to analyze existing business processes, with attention to identifying potential bottlenecks through modeling. In particular, a dynamic system model for electronic document management based on a Markov scheme is introduced. The innovative approach is shown to conduct detailed cost assessments of both productive and non-productive document states, offering valuable insights into system efficiency.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)

title: Risk-Guided Car-Following and Lane-Changing Model for Safe Navigation of Autonomous Driving Vehicles
authors: Zhenqiang Ma, Hongjuan Zhang, Si Xu, Disheng Zhang, Bijun Li
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Car-following model (CFM) and lane-changing model (LCM) in intelligent transportation system (ITS) are the most prevalent continuous maneuvers behaviors in traffic flow. Most existing studies model these two behaviors independently, leading to the potential oversight of the consistency between them. Therefore, this paper proposes a risk-guided unified modeling approach for CFM and LCM to realize a reliable and efficient navigation system. First, a dynamically varying driving risk field model is proposed on the basis of vehicles' physical properties and motion characteristics, in order to capture the variations of risk induced by the vehicles during in the traffic environment. Meanwhile, a road risk field model incorporating curvature factors is developed to characterize the risk constraints that typical road markings impose on driving behaviors. Then, an improved nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC) method with multi-objective optimization is presented to integrate the LCM and CFM models. The risk borne by the ego vehicle is represented by superimposing scalar risk values and vector risks respectively, thereby guiding the ego-vehicle to move in the direction of decreasing overall risk. And a lateral phase function is designed to achieve a smooth transition between LCM and CFM behaviors. Finally, comparative analyses with existing models are conducted using driving data to verify the effectiveness and correctness of our model. It provides a unified CFM and LCM behaviors framework for safety navigation services across various scenarios.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)

title: Advanced Hierarchical Classification Approach for Document Categorization
authors: Mamadou Alpha Hawa Balde, Pirlouit Dumez, Meriam Belgaroui, Guillaume Prevost, Salah Zidi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel hierarchical classification framework that effectively addresses overlapping features while enhancing interpretability, enabling users to better understand the rationale behind document categorization. The model manages semantic overlaps by constructing a dynamic tree structure that clusters related categories through iterative refinement, thereby boosting accuracy. This transparent and scalable approach consistently outperforms traditional flat classifiers. Tested on a dataset of 80,000 documents, the tree-based model achieved an accuracy of 93%, surpassing both SGD and deep learning models. Although challenges such as error propagation and data sparsity remain, the framework proves robust and adaptable for large-scale document management. Its scalability allows it to accommodate evolving and complex datasets.

title: Radio Frequency Energy Harvesting for Smart Farming Applications: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Zaheer Abbas Balouch, Jahariah Sampe, Huda Abdullah, Zeti Akma Rhazali, Noor Hidayah Yunus
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: The surge of the Internet of Things (IoT) in smart farming has facilitated the mass adoption of sensor networks for monitoring soil, crops, livestock, and environmental parameters. A notable limitation of this paradigm is fundamentally constrained by its dependence on batteries, introducing significant challenges related to maintenance, cost, and environmental sustainability, specifically in the large or remote agricultural areas. Radio Frequency Energy Harvesting (RFEH) has emerged as a convincing strategy to achieve sustainable, battery-independent or battery-augmented operation of low-power agricultural sensors. This study presents a systematic investigation of a microstrip patch antenna (MPA) designed for integration into radio frequency energy harvesting (RFEH) systems targeting smart farming applications. The proposed antenna operates within the 2.4 GHz ISM band and achieves a notable gain of 2.78 dBi. Furthermore, it achieves efficient impedance matching, characterized by a low reflection coefficient below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$- 10\text{dB}$</tex>. This study highlights persistent operational difficulties, such as low energy densities achievable through RFEH, signal propagation matching in rural landscapes, and the design constraints imposed by the pursuit of energy independence in sensor networks. This study outlines the opportunities and challenges of RFEH in agriculture, with the objective of informing ongoing research and enhancing resilient and environmentally sustainable smart farming systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Ensemble Models for Enhanced Financial Volatility Forecasting
authors: Boutheina Zouaoui Drira, Haykel Hamdi, Ines Ben Jaafar
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: this paper presents a novel ensemble methodology that integrates deep learning models to enhance the accuracy and robustness of financial volatility forecasts. By combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and GRU networks, the proposed approach captures both spatial and temporal patterns in financial time series data. Empirical results demonstrate the superiority of this ensemble model over traditional forecasting methods in various financial markets.

title: Study on Dynamic Optimization and Multi-Objective Decision-Making of Supply Chain Carbon Footprint Based on GA-LSTM Hybrid Model
authors: Wenyu Wang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: In the context of dynamic optimization and multi-objective decision-making for the carbon footprint of global supply chains, this study aims to develop and validate a GA-LSTM hybrid model to enhance the accuracy, robustness, and practicality of carbon footprint prediction, addressing issues of insufficient prediction accuracy and low decision-making efficiency caused by endogenous and exogenous uncertainties. Methodologically, this paper optimizes LSTM hyperparameters (such as the number of hidden layer neurons, dropout rate, and learning rate) through genetic algorithms, constructs a two-layer LSTM structure to enhance the ability to capture long-term dependency features, and integrates a transfer learning mechanism to synergistically handle endogenous and exogenous uncertainties. Important results show that validation based on global datasets (EXIOBASE and World Bank) indicates a RMSE of 0.045, MAE of 0.032, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.963 for model performance testing; under noise interference, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\triangle$</tex> RMSE increases by only 8.2%, and △ RMSE is 12.5% when data is missing; in actual testing, the carbon footprint reduction rate reaches 18.7%, and the cost savings rate reaches 15.3%. The conclusion indicates that this model significantly optimizes the multi-objective decision-making efficiency of supply chain carbon footprint.

title: Ambient Frictions: Place-conscious Frictions to Foster Intentional Technology Use
authors: Solomon Musa Pitluck, Anup Sathya
publish: Proceedings of the Twentieth International Conference on Tangible, Embedded, and Embodied Interaction

title: DL-LoEM: Improving Data Locality and Load Balancing for MapReduce-Based Entity Matching
authors: Khadidja Midoun, Walid-khaled Hidouci, Malik Loudini, Nadjah Chergui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The entity resolution process consists to identify the duplicate entities, which is an important issue in data integration and data cleaning efforts. This process is very expensive in terms of the number of entity matching operations and the running time of each operation. To minimize this cost, MapReduce parallel programming model is widely used for blocking-based entity matching. However, the absence of skew handling mechanisms and the ignoring data locality factor during the distribution of intermediate keys through the Reducers can lead to a significant performance degradation in MapReduce job processing. To address these issues, we propose an enhancement to an existing approach for load balancing in MapReduce, referred to as the LoEM approach. In this paper, we introduce the DL-LoEM (Data Locality and Load Balancing for MapReduce-based Entity Matching) approach, which aims to improve both load balancing and data locality for blocking-based entity resolution in MapReduce. Our experimental results demonstrate that DL-LoEM improves the load balancing up to 52.3% and 14.7% compared to Hadoop and the LoEM approache, respectively. Additionally, DL-LoEM achieves improvements of up to 26.5% and 25.3% in data locality during the reduce phase compared to the Hadoop and LoEM approach, respectively.

title: Towards an Assistive Eye-Controlled Interface
authors: Mohamed Amine Yakoubi, Arslane Aouissi, Issameddine Amaidia, Walid Hariri, Sara Rekkal, Ahlem Melouah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Eye tracking is a technique used for capturing and interpreting eye movements to control systems without physical contact. This project leverages this technology to interact solely through gaze directions, enabling hands-free navigation in PDF documents. Using a convolutional neural network (CNN) based on MobileNetV2, a lightweight and efficient architecture was employed to develop a model capable of classifying eye movements. This model is integrated into a graphical user interface (GUI) built with Tkinter, providing an intuitive interaction experience with various types of documents, regardless of their size, while maintaining high precision and fluidity. Experimental results show an accuracy of 96%, demonstrating the effectiveness of this approach in terms of digital accessibility.

title: Smart Warehouse Management for SMEs: A Low-Code Digital Transformation Approach
authors: Mahaning Indrawaty Wijaya, Ali Gunawan, Richard Wiputra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Small and medium enterprises currently contribute to around 60 % of GDP in Indonesia. However, many of them still do not leverage the benefit of information technology to support their activity yet. Meanwhile, rapid technology development has been transforming the business in many industries, and also increasing the competition. Therefore, SMEs need to be aware and start to apply available technology to increase competitiveness. In this paper, a warehouse management system is developed using power apps to support warehouse management activity in one small medium enterprise that focuses on distributing fresh fruit. The application helps to eliminate manual and redundant activities such as manual entry, re-copying reports, and other activities that are very time-consuming. Besides digitizing the process, the system also can provide recommendations about which products have near expiry dates or have low stock levels. Thus, the company could use its time and resources to achieve more strategic goals rather than the operational routine that has low value-added.

title: AI-Driven Real-Time Synchronization (ASC-6): Optimizing Logistics Resilience and Financial Performance
authors: Lahcen Kandsi, Arif Jabir, Fouad Jawab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: In the era of Supply Chain 4.0--named after an adjectival portrayal of technologies like IoT, AI and DLT that adds up to a “4.0”-the days when logistics was about sequential batch processing are over, replaced by new real-time event driven networks moving in its place. In an age where the name of the game is real-time distribution, this paper will address the essential difficulties of systems engineering in complex cyber-physical environments, and specifically talk about a big hole: no full set frame system to make end-to-end real-time synchronization possible across vital shipping interfaces (Warehouse Management Systems (WMS), Transportation Management Systems TMS).) and hardware. To this end, we present the Architecture for Synchronization in Six Layers (ASC-6), which is a new and secure framework that forms a blueprint for high-fidelity logistics operations. ASC-6 coordinates and directs the data through a series of: •Acquisition (IoT/CDC): Persistent telemetry by IoT and CDC from core systems. •Communications (MQTT/Edge) Low latency data transfer with MQTT-based edge connection. •Harmonization (Kafka): Data harmonising across organisations, resulting in Unified Data Model for Semantic coherence among various partners. •Predictive Intelligence (LSTM): Using LSTM Neural Network for predicting supply chain deviations and scoring prescriptive actions. •(Event-Driven Orchestration (BRM): Automatically trigger remediation through a Business Rule Engine (BRM) for immediate operational response. •Governance (Blockchain) Write all sync events DLT for transparency, immutability and decentralized trust. The method involves a bibliometric analysis of 500 peer-reviewed papers (2000-2025) to validate the architectural design by identifying deficiencies in existing research regarding integrated frameworks.) Shortly, we will present a digital twin architecture able to calibrate and validate the ASC-6 based on an Agent-Based Model (ABM) in order to test it under different breaking disruption scenarios. The simulation results show that the prescriptive capability of ASC-6 dramatically decreased DL by 45 % on average (from minutes to milliseconds) compared with classical systems. The framework also results in a 69 % recovery improvement in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R T$</tex> after a major disruption, which causes significant measurable reduction of CNS. Such system design, integration and validation is a crucial cornerstone for realizing resilient, proactive and genuinely agile global networks the researchers and practitioners in Advanced Technologies for Supply Chain Management (ATSCM) are hoping to create.

title: Abnormal Action Detection for Surveillance Robot Based on Deep Ensemble Learning
authors: Souhila Kahlouche, El-Fani Roufaida, Tazekritt Malika
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Mobile robotic systems are increasingly deployed to ensure human safety, particularly in environments where early detection of abnormal or risky behaviors is essential. Identifying suspicious human actions from video streams, however, remains a complex task, especially when real-time inference is required. This study introduces a real-time framework that enables a surveillance robot to recognize abnormal human activities from visual data. Three distinct deep learning models are trained to capture both spatial and temporal cues of human motion. Their outputs are later fused using an ensemble decision mechanism, which significantly enhances recognition reliability compared to using any individual model alone. To train and evaluate the system, a dedicated dataset was built by merging samples from three public human-action datasets containing both normal and abnormal behaviors. Experimental tests conducted in real environments confirm that the proposed framework performs effectively and can be integrated into intelligent surveillance platforms.

title: Uncovering Hidden Connections: Iterative Search and Reasoning for Video-grounded Dialog
authors: Haoyu Zhang, Meng Liu, Yisen Feng, Yaowei Wang, Weili Guan, Liqiang Nie

title: Curriculum-Guided Ensemble Learning for Stage Classification of Liver Cirrhosis
authors: Rishabh Raj, Deepanshu Baghel, Partha Sarthi Bishnu, Vandana Bhattacharjee
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: For accurate classification of liver cirrhosis, which is vital for its early diagnosis and treatment, a novel approach for the classification of liver cirrhosis into different classes using structured clinical data, referred to as “Curriculum Learning,” has been developed. For this purpose, a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> patient records with 18 clinical features, which was used in this study, was curated, and there were no missing values in the dataset, which was balanced and consisted of three classes of liver health: healthy liver, fibrosis, and cirrhosis. In this study, two of the most popular “Curriculum Learning” approaches, referred to as “Easy-to-Hard” (ETH) and “Hard-to-Easy” (HTE), were used depending on the confidence level of the model, which was predicted by a base classifier and a logistic regression classifier. Several machine learning algorithms were evaluated, including Logistic Regression, SVM (linear, polynomial, RBF), KNN, Decision Tree, Random Forest, AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, and LightGBM. Our results indicate that the curriculum-guided training significantly improves model performance, with LightGBM achieving up to 95.90% accuracy under ETH regimes. The curriculum-based models showed improved generalization as compared to conventional training, especially in under-represented classes. The proposed approach demonstrates the efficacy of curriculum learning and confidence-aware data structuring in improving clinical decision support systems for liver diseases. This is a generalizable approach and can be applied to other progressive disease classification tasks.

title: Beyond Traditional Methods: Harnessing Deep Learning to Revolutionize Weather Forecasting
authors: Sakshi Priya, Aditi Shukla, Sakshi, Rashmi Sharma, Maria Jamal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: -weather has changed our lives in all the ways possible. From everyday decisions to long term planning it affects everything. As it is vary difficult to predict it becomes vary important to have prior knowledge about its pattern for safety and further scientific predictions. This research proposes Deep Learning(DL) methods for weather forecasting which uses models that can learn patterns and relationships from past long term data to make accurate predictions. This study focuses on techniques that can give optimal results even in the low resource environment. Various models like Convolutional Neural Networks(CNNs), Long Short-Term Memory(LSTM) and Transformer based architectures are studied, datasets like ERA5 reanalysis, Global Circulation Model(GCM) and observations from satellite like Integrated Multi-satellite Retrievals for Global Precipitation Measurement (IMERG). As traditional Numerical Weather Prediction(NWP) is computationally complex DL methods have shown much more optimal performance and high accuracy which makes the researchers lean towards data driven forecasting.

title: A Causal-XAI Framework for Retail Sales Forecasting: Addressing Transparency and Lack of Robustness in Deep Learning Models
authors: Somil Khade, Kavita R. Singh, K Karule
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Accurate sales forecasting in retail is very useful when managing inventory, planning replenishment strategy, and helping the business make good decisions in a retail environment. Various methods including Deep Learning (DL) and Ensemble techniques, have improved accuracy of sales forecasting in general [1] however, they do not distinguish between correlation and causal inference. Moreover, they cannot provide information on the underlying reasoning or rationale about the problem. Hence, these methods will produce a sales forecast and not provide actionable recommendations that would improve Return on Investment (ROI) related to pricing strategies or promotion strategies [2], [3]. Proposed new Causal-XAI framework guarantees reliable, trustworthy explanations for its predictions by using Double Machine Learning (DML) to correctly identify the true causes and Causal Transformer (CT) to use those causes for accurate and automatically clear predictions [4]. To apply our proposed framework, we are going to use Corporación Favorita Grocery Sales Forecasting dataset from Kaggle and evaluate the performance using the NWRMSLE [5]. The proposed method achieves not only superior forecasting accuracy but also presents causal information that directly leads to business actions aimed at improving marketing outcomes.

title: Low-Light Outdoor Single View Heritage Images Enhancement Using MIRNet V1
authors: Sandhya M. Chitnis, Prachi Mukherji
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Cultural heritage image documentation and preservation play a vital role for our future generations, tourism development, and storytelling. However, during image acquisition, inappropriate illumination conditions and practical limitations of photographic devices adversely affect image quality, often resulting in low-contrast heritage images. Natural light is especially important in outdoor spaces where many temple statues and monuments are located, and its absence or insufficiency further deteriorates the visual quality. Therefore, there is a need for curation of heritage images, where image-based curation algorithms are employed to recover low-light images without compromising their intricate details and cultural relevance. The curation algorithms are applied to synthetically simulated low-light heritage images of the benchmark Tanks and Temples dataset. Algorithms are also applied on database collected by the researcher through a mobile camera. In this work, the classical image-based algorithms applied - Histogram Equalization (HE) variants and Retinex-based approaches are compared with the deep learning-based model MIRNet v1. The deep learning MIRNet v1 is trained and tested on a combination of both datasets. The MIRNet v1 outperforms traditional algorithms in terms of SSIM by 0.9825 and PSNR by 32.44dB, resulting in improved quality of the processed images for human visual perception.

title: Intelligent Multi–Objective Energy Management of PV-Battery-Integrated Smart Homes via CFGWO
authors: Nilesh Suresh Mahajan, Prashant J. Gaidahane
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The research provides a rational cooperative foraging based grey wolf optimizer (CFGWO) which will optimize both user dissatisfaction and electricity cost of an intelligent home energy management system (HEMS), using cooperative foraging and benchmarked against other well established algorithms such as multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO) and artificial bee colony (ABC). A simulation study was conducted for a duration of 24 hours, that includes actual appliance models, rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) system, and a lithium-ion battery. The results demonstrated the ability of the CFGWO to optimize solutions much faster than both MOPSO and ABC methods. Additionally, it provided better solutions, and the best tradeoff between energy cost and comfort. The results demonstrate cost savings of up to 10.5 % when comparing the results of the CFGWO method to the MOPSO method, and a cost savings of up to 18.9 % when comparing the results of the CFGWO method to the ABC method. This framework provides a practical solution for the development of a real time, multi-objective energy management strategy for residential applications.

title: A Pulsed Strong Light Disinfection and Sterilization Equipment
authors: Jianjun Liu, Haibing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Pulse light technology, with its advantages of speed and efficiency, holds great potential for application in sterilization and disinfection in fields such as healthcare and food. This article introduces the development of a pulse light disinfection device, which relies on a pulsed light module as core component and achieves efficient killing of pathogenic microorganisms.

title: FPGA-Based FIR Filter for 2D Convolution in Image Processing Applications: A Review
authors: Nitin Kumar, Anuraj V, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan, Manish Verma, Baljit Kaur, Preeti Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This paper presents a hardware-efficient implementation of Image enhancement using a Kernel-based FIR filtering approach, targeting real-time applications through FPGA (Artix7 AC701 Evaluation Platform) deployment. FIR filters provide several advantages, such as linear-phase response, guaranteed stability, and non-recursive structure, making the hardware realization easier. The system was implemented and synthesized to evaluate parameters such as LUT usage, register count, DSP utilization, block RAM consumption, power usage, and propagation delay. The image processing module includes a dedicated 16-bit signed array multiplier, along with cascaded half and full adders, to efficiently perform kernel-based convolution. The designs achieved a delay of (10.27-11.08ns), moderate power consumption, and consistent resource usage across all techniques. The PSNR and MSSIM values are calculated by comparing the hardware-processed image with the software-processed image and show that the output is indistinguishable from the softwareprocessed image. So, no distortion or quality degradation happens for hardware implementation. The design supports grayscale images <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(256 \times 256)$</tex> with clear potential for expansion to color images. Future work will focus on extending the architecture to adaptive kernel computation using content-aware mechanisms. Overall, this research establishes the kernel-based FIR filter on an FPGA as a practical, efficient, and high-performance solution for real-time image enhancement.

title: Evaluating Analytical Workloads for Migrating from Relational to NoSQL Stores
authors: Himanshu Ranjan, Shelly Sachdeva
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The traditional relational database faces many difficulties, such as consistency and integrity issues, high read and write costs, performance degradation with large datasets, and complex queries in managing the data of today's world. The root cause of this scenario is the explosion of diverse data due to the advancements in the Internet of Things (IoT), Mobile devices, Social Networking, and Web Technology Applications. These data not only introduce costly joins, which lead to an increase in response time, but also add the risk of data redundancy. Unalignment of schema design with the query patterns is also a significant issue due to incorrect schema mapping. To overcome these challenges, many industries migrate their data from relational database systems to NoSQL document stores, which is a complex task. Such migration can be achieved through schema transformation using the three approaches: Complete Referencing, Total Embedding, and Hybrid. This paper showcases an overview of these three approaches by providing a comprehensive comparison and analytical experiments considering the workload queries to lay out a detailed description of each approach to focus on their unique properties and use cases for schema migration from relational to NoSQL stores. The results show that the hybrid approach performs better in average query execution time. In the case of overall space consumption, the total embedding approach consumes 21% less space than the hybrid approach and 31% less space than the complete referencing approach in the 10 Megabyte dataset, and for the 100 Megabyte dataset, the total embedding takes 11% and 29% less space than the hybrid and complete referencing approaches. In contrast, complete referencing shows the least data duplication in terms of redundancy. However, the hybrid approach balances the issues of data updation, high space consumption, and scalability by embedding frequently accessed data and referencing large data to limit space consumption. Thus, summarizes the potential of each approach to provide valuable insights by evaluating analytical workloads for migrating from relational to NoSQL stores.

title: Characterization of Flashover Along the Enclosure Screen Due to Moisture Dripping Along the Screen Surface
authors: Bo Lu, Wenbin Zhao, Shuang Zou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This study investigates the evolution of oil-paper insulation discharge triggered by severe moisture in transformers.
