Furthermore, the sensor exhibits a high amplitude sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77.26 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex>, a figure of merit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$39.285 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex>, and an RI resolution of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \times 10^{-5} \text{RIU}$</tex>, indicating precise detection capability. To confirm the practical robustness and reliability of the design, parametric studies were carried out to analyze the influence of key structural parameters, including metal layer thickness and the separation between the fiber core and surrounding air holes.

title: Optic Disc and Cup Segmentation with Bilinear-ResUNet for Automated Glaucoma Screening Using Cup-to-Disc Ratio
authors: Mohamed Mouhafid, Yatong Zhou, El Moundher Aouiche, Yassine Jaouad, Poornima Lankani Perera, Abdelmounim Boudi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Glaucoma remains a primary cause of irreversible visual impairment, often progressing without symptoms during its initial stages, which complicates early detection. Accurate segmentation of the optic disc (OD) and optic cup (OC) in retinal fundus images is essential for calculating the cup-to-disc ratio (CDR), a widely recognized biomarker in glaucoma diagnosis. Manual segmentation methods are time-intensive, subjective, and prone to inconsistency. Additionally, existing automated approaches frequently encounter difficulties related to limited feature extraction, image quality variability, and artifacts generated by transposed convolutions. In this study, we implement and evaluate a ResNet-34-based U-Net with bilinear upsampling for OD/OC segmentation, demonstrating its advantages for glaucoma screening. Experimental evaluation indicates that the model achieves improved performance in OD and OC segmentation compared to several conventional architectures, based on Intersection over Union (IoU) and Dice similarity metrics. Furthermore, the model demonstrates reliable glaucoma detection (GD) using area-based CDR (ACDR), vertical CDR (VCDR), and horizontal CDR (HCDR), with respective area under the curve (AUC) values of 0.9139, 0.9147, and 0.9591. These results suggest that Bilinear-ResUNet provides an effective solution for automated GD.

title: From Grid Following to Grid Forming in PV Grid Tied Single Phase Inverter
authors: Mourad Zebboudj, Syphax Ihammouchen, Toufik Rekioua, Djamila Rekioua, Ali Chebabhi, Mohammed Said Ouahabi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This research focuses on photovoltaic systems connected to single-phase inverters operating in two distinct modes: isolated operation using grid-forming inverters (GFMi) and grid-connected operation using grid-following inverters (GFLi). Given the increasing proportion of inverter-based energy sources in modern grids, GFLi systems can contribute to grid frequency stability under normal conditions. However, the primary aim of this study is to address challenges and failures that can occur in the electrical grid, such as disturbances, voltage sags, and distribution blackouts, by employing GFMi technology to supply loads independently during grid outages. The study was conducted using MATLAB/Simulink software, comparing the performance of damped and undamped LCL filters, with the damped configuration demonstrating superior performance. The results confirm the effectiveness of the GFMi system in supplying loads during disconnection from the electrical grid, achieving a THD of 2.63 % at the inverter output for islanded operation. For grid-connected GFLi operation, the damped LCL filter achieves a THD of 1.74 % for the current injected into the grid, meeting power quality standards.

title: The Design and Construction of a Drone-to-Drone Battery Swapping System
authors: Haoyuan Deng, Takyiu Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The paper explores a range extension concept for drones, in which one drone replaces the battery of another mid-mission. Previous research had explored portable battery-swapping stations and drone-to-drone battery charging, and this project extends the versatility further as the battery swap can be completed quickly and on any flat terrain, thereby combining the speed of battery swapping and the versatility of drone-to-drone charging into one system. Experimental results confirmed the viability of the concept for fast and versatile range extensions.

title: Survey on Embedding Methods Applied to Ontology Matching
authors: Guilherme Santos Sousa, Rinaldo Jose Lima, Cassia Trojahn
publish: ACM Computing Surveys
abstract: None

title: AI-Driven Discovery and Analysis of Natural Compounds in Oral Cancer: A Mini Review (2014–2024)
authors: Piyush Bhati, Neetu, Sresi Singh, Varun Goel, Abdul Arif Khan, Mohd. Tashfeen Ashraf
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Natural compounds (phytochemicals and marine derivatives) offer promising anticancer effects with low toxicity. In oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC), phytochemicals such as curcumin, quercetin, EGCG, and resveratrol modulate pathways (PI3K/Akt, NF-кB, apoptosis, etc.) to inhibit proliferation, metastasis, and induce apoptosis. Recent years have seen artificial intelligence (AI) accelerate discovery and analysis of such agents. The convergence of artificial intelligence and phytochemical-based therapy in oral squamous cell carcinoma remains insufficiently synthesized, despite substantial advances in both fields. We systematically searched PubMed, Web of Science, and IEEE Xplore (2014–2024) for studies on oral cancer, natural compounds, and AI methods (machine learning, deep learning, QSAR, molecular docking, network pharmacology, omics integration). Inclusion criteria covered peer-reviewed original research and reviews on AIbased virtual screening, QSAR modeling, network analysis, and integrative omics in this context. Major themes include AIdriven compound discovery, mechanistic elucidation, and predictive modeling for treatment. ML/QSAR models (e.g. Random Forest) trained on OSCC cytotoxicity data have been used to screen phytochemicals and analogs, identifying candidates with high tumor-specific toxicity. High-throughput virtual screening of natural libraries (including marine products) against OSCC targets (e.g. bacterial virulence proteins like P. gingivalis FimA) has uncovered lead molecules via docking and molecular dynamics. Network pharmacology and AI (including graph-based models) map multi-target interactions of phytochemicals. Studies show flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol, naringenin) target key OSCC proteins (COX-2, TNF, BCL2, caspase-3) and pathways (IL-6/STAT3, TNF/NF%B). For example, geniposide (a plant iridoid) was linked to >100 OSCC protein targets; docking and MD confirmed strong binding to AKT1 and EGFR, implicating PI3K/Akt-mediated apoptosis. Such AI-enabled in silico models complement experimental validation (e.g. gene/protein assays). AI methods combine transcriptomic, metabolomic and imaging data to identify OSCC biomarkers and predict therapy response. Deep learning on histopathology images and riskfactor data improves lesion classification; ML on plasma metabolomics has revealed diagnostic signatures for OSCC. These approaches suggest how phytochemicals might modulate cancer metabolism or microenvironment. In discussion, we address challenges (limited labeled data, compound bioavailability, AI interpretability) and future directions: employing advanced neural networks, larger natural-product databases, and multi-modal AI pipelines to optimize phytochemical therapies. In conclusion, AI has begun to revolutionize the discovery and understanding of natural anticancer agents in oral oncology, and further integration of AI tools promises to accelerate effective phytochemical interventions for oral cancer.

title: Career Prediction Model Using Big Data Analytics
authors: Ankit Kumar, Harsh Gupta, Ashutosh Yadav, Anju Joon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Selecting the right Career path is one of the biggest challenges students have to go through during their education. With new technologies, new jobs roles, new domains of skills, and more, students have to spend more time figuring out where their strengths and interests lie. This is why this research employs a more data-oriented approach to help students identify the potential career paths available through Big Data Analytics and Machine Learning. The system takes into account a student's school performance, tech skills, communication skills, and interests and proposes alternative career options that are valid and aligned to the student. We use Random Forest based predictive modeling which we have put in place to improve on career prediction results. We present results in Power BI dashboards which include interactive graphs and reports that display prediction trends. We have developed a very robust and adaptive model for career predication and guidance. We use Big Data to bring to light what is not seen in educational data sets. We integrated Machine Learning for more reliable and data supported predictions. We use Power BI for dashboards which in turn aid in effective decision making. We have put together a large picture which is to close the gap between what is taught in the academia and what is required in the career field. By combining analysis with real time visualization we aim to simplify the decision-making process and empower students to make sure footed, data based career choices.

title: Adversarially-Robust Federated Learning for Intrusion Detection in Decentralized Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Chirag Mavani, Hirenkumar Mistry, Amit Mahendragiri Goswami, Sundar Tiwari, Stuti Pandey, Keshav Kaushik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a FL-based adversarially-robust framework for intrusion detection in decentralized CPS. The approach adopts the robust aggregation that takes Krum, Multi-Krum and Bulyan in account to reduce the influence of adversarial attacks and maintain the model performance. Differential privacy is utilized to ensure sensitive data privacy during training, while a credibility-dependent filtering policy can dynamically react to malicious clients by updating the influence of clients according to their past performance. Comprehensive experiments show that the proposed approach outperforms the state-of-the-art FL aggregation methods remarkably in terms of detection accuracy, convergence speed, as well as robustness against different attacks (e.g., backdoor attacks, poisoning attacks). The proposed method is particularly appropriate for real-time, privacy-preserving, and scalable intrusion detection in CPS, guaranteeing the robustness against adversarial threats.

title: A Time-Frequence Cross-Modal Contrastive Learning Approach for Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Wei Dai, Boqun Lin, Qi Yi, Chen Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Rolling bearings are critical components in rotating machinery, and their condition monitoring is essential for equipment reliability. While deep learning-based fault diagnosis methods have achieved significant progress, they typically require large amounts of labeled data that are costly to obtain in industrial settings. This paper proposes a time-frequence cross-modal contrastive learning (TFCMCL) method to address this challenge. The method first applies variational mode decomposition (VMD) to suppress noise and enhance fault-sensitive features in raw vibration signals. The reconstructed time-domain waveforms and corresponding time-frequency spectrograms are then treated as complementary views for cross-modal contrastive learning. A joint loss function integrating cross-modal and intra-modal contrastive objectives is designed to ensure both consistency and discriminability of learned representations. Experiments on public datasets demonstrate that TFCMCL achieves 93.20% linear evaluation accuracy, maintains high accuracy with only 1 % labeled data, and yields 8.62 % average improvement in crossmachine transfer tasks, validating its effectiveness under labelscarce and domain-shift conditions.

title: A Machine Learning Based Classification of Sleep Disorder Using Lifestyle Parameters
authors: Manisha, Pragati Upadhyay, Pridhi Arora, Padmesh Tripathi, Preeti Katiyar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: There is increasing evidence that it may be difficulties to identify early detection and intervention of sleep disorders is significant challenges in healthcare. The World Health Organisation (WHO) and medical research groups identify a raising correlation between sleeping quality and mental health issues such as anxiety and depression. This study presents a predictive analysis of sleep disorders utilizing a multiclass classification approach using Lifestyle parameters this removes false claim that is produce by wearable device that is design for the detection of sleep problems, further investigating this significant integration. Through Emphasizes the journey of discovery and understanding validation we demonstrate that a Bidirectional I Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM architecture outperforms baseline machine learning models. The BiLSTM model perform high performance across key metrics, including accuracy, precision, and recall, by effectively identify with in feature set. These result integrated predictive models can enhance clinical decision making that personalized treatment programmes and remote digital health monitoring.

title: Empirical Comparison of Recurrent Neural Architectures for Multi-Label Toxic Comment Classification
authors: Sudhir Kumar Sharma, Sneha Vasishth, Jalaj Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The work deals with the problem of multi-label text classification, which aims to identify and classify user comments in terms of different forms of toxicity. Due to an increased amount of content created by users, the need for automated moderation has increased in order to keep online communities healthier. We compare three different models: LSTM, Bi-LSTM, and GRU. Then, we implemented the Word2Vec embedding layers, followed by layers composed of LSTM/Bi-LSTM/GRU and ending with a sigmoid output layer. Each model will be trained with an Adam optimizer with binary cross-entropy loss so as to prevent overfitting by incorporating early stopping criteria. Model performances are evaluated on accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, both micro and macro. Based on these results, it can be shown that the best overall performance has been exhibited when using the GRU model in capturing contextual meaning within the text. In addition, this also shows that the Bi-LSTM model was close to GRU but GRU was slightly better.

title: Hybrid Identity-Preserving Image Cartoonization Using Iterative Deep Learning Verification and Region-Aware Processing
authors: Ayush Garg, Tanay Chaplot, Supriya Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: All social media digital media have a cartoonizer filter of an image. Young or old, be it Instagram or Snapchat, everybody prefers to see themselves in cartoons. However, there is a mandatory issue: the filter changes the real human faces that you have, making you unrecognizable. The regular algorithms usually save 70-80% of your recognizable facial features, which leads to distortion of facial features. Thus, we constructed a system to combine the conventional picture processing with the current AI. There are three steps to our method. We take FaceNet-512 embeddings first, and run them in a continuous feedback loop-it keeps on checking itself to see whether you still have a recognizable face and increasing or decreasing the cartoon strength. Second, we do not treat your face in its entirety. With MediaPipe's state-of-the-art facial landmark detection (468 points) we are soft on the eyes (only 30% cartoon effect, the place of identity) and hard on backgrounds (100%). Third, we identify the emotion by recognizing the color pixel intensities and matching them with the values of the emotions defined. We ran 200 high-quality celebrity images on CelebA-HQ giving us nearly real results: 88 percent face recognition, which is double the norm of 80%. We preserved emotional expressions (100% preservation). We also dealt in 6 varieties of art. This study opens the way of individual avatars, instructions resources, career photographs, and accessibility where the humour of the photograph balances with the veracity of the appearance.

title: Design and Implementation of an Exciter-Based Automatic Voltage Regulator for Single-Phase Synchronous Generators
authors: Basit Ahmad, Maryam Muneer, Syeda Aqsa Jillani, Sheeraz Ahmed
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: In recent years, the expansion of power systems has been a critical focus of electrical engineering, as the growing demand for electricity necessitates increasingly reliable and stable power supplies. One of the primary challenges associated with this expansion is ensuring that the power system operates without interruption, even under fluctuating load conditions. This paper presents the design and implementation of an Automatic Voltage Regulator (AVR) specifically designed for a single-phase synchronous generator. The propose d AVR utilizes the exciter field control method, a widely recognized technique for voltage regulation, which adjusts the field current to control the generator's output voltage. The exciter control method is particularly effective for maintaining stable terminal voltage, as it directly influences the excitation of the synchronous generator. The AVR system operates on a close d-loop feedback control mechanism, continuously monitoring the generator's terminal voltage. Experimental results demonstrate the AVR's capability to maintain stable voltage across a range of load conditions, making it a robust and reliable solution for voltage regulation in single-phase synchronous generators. The proposed AVR design not only enhances the efficiency and reliability of power generation systems but also offers a costeffective and practical solution for distributed power generation applications, where maintaining a stable voltage is critical. This work provides valuable insights into the design and implementation of AVRs, contributing to the ongoing efforts to improve power system stability and performance.

title: Energy Management of a Community Microgrid Based on Solar PV Power Forecasting
authors: Shereen Siddhara Abdul Salam, Arif Bramantoro, Nor Asiah Muhamad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Solar power has recently been largely integrated into the energy mix due to its accessibility and sustainability. The implementation of rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) systems in the residential communities has also been on the rise, which in turn is a promising pathway to cleaner energy. However, the intermittent nature of the solar power and the mismatch between solar power generation and the residential load demand often lead to increased grid export. This raises concerns about the stability of the existing grid infrastructure. Community microgrids with a shared energy storage system offer a promising solution for effective utilization of the local PV power generation and minimization of the grid export. This study analyzes the energy management of a community microgrid modelled based on the real-time electricity consumption pattern of residential buildings and real-time PV generation data from a 10 kW rooftop solar installation in Brunei Darussalam. The energy management framework presented is based on solar PV power forecasting using an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) model followed by a Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) based optimal scheduling of a 200 kWh community shared energy storage system. The proposed framework was evaluated under two scenarios with the first case limited to residential load only and the second case considering the residential load combined with electric vehicle (EV) charging. The simulation results show that when compared to a PV-only scenario, the effective utilization and optimal scheduling of the energy storage system in the community microgrid reduces the energy export to the grid from 87.474 kWh to 22.091 kWh in the first case and from 52.37 kWh to 12.3 kWh in the second case thus enhancing the grid stability.

title: An Improved DWA Path Planning Algorithm Based on Net Goal Projection Efficiency
authors: Shifeng Chen, Zhanpeng Sun, Xiue Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: The Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) has advantages such as high real-time performance and strong robustness, but it is prone to falling into local minima and suffering from goal loss when applied in 3D scenarios. To address these issues, this paper proposes a DWA path planning method based on Net Goal Projection Efficiency (NGPE). First, the advantages and disadvantages of the existing DWA algorithm are analyzed; second, an NGPE-DWA evaluation function is designed by predicting the target projection distance of the net displacement of the trajectory; finally, algorithm simulation experiments are designed. The experimental results show that the proposed method significantly reduces the path length and planning time, and improves the efficiency and reliability of path planning.

title: FedEnviSense: A Federated Dual-Domain Acoustic Intelligence Framework for Urban and Environmental Event Detection
authors: Reema Sabreen A, Thiriveni R A, Abinaya K Samy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Environmental/urban sound monitoring harbours an important role towards safety, sustainability and ecological equilibrium, but unfortunately, many of the existing sensor systems are developed in isolation for different locations. While urban acoustic networks are designed for sounds such as gunshots and sirens, environmental and wildlife monitoring systems target calls of species, storms or sounds of illegal logging. This separation not only restricts potential knowledge from being transferred, adds deployment cost, but it also prevents smart cities and faraway conservation zones from collaborating in real time while preserving privacy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes FedEnviSense, a unified federated acoustic intelligence framework that integrates urban and environmental monitoring through distributed Internet of Things nodes. The system employs a lightweight convolutional recurrent neural network model optimized for on-device inference and trains collaboratively using a privacy-preserving federated averaging protocol with energy-aware client selection and optional differential privacy. Experimental evaluations using a simulated federation of ten clients across public datasets show that FedEnviSense achieves strong cross-domain generalization with centralized training reaching 92.5% accuracy and federated training maintaining 90.8% accuracy while preserving data privacy and reducing communication overhead. The framework demonstrates that a federated dual domain design can deliver accurate, efficient and ethical acoustic intelligence suitable for both smart city and ecological applications.

title: Real-Time Positioning System Based on Magnetic Field Matching in Underground Parking Lot
authors: Jing Sun, Hongjian Jiao, Tao Zhou, Liang Chen
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: In recent years, extensive research has been conducted in the field of indoor positioning. Many typical scenarios, such as shopping malls, hospitals, and residential buildings, feature underground parking facilities. In such environments, leveraging data collected from smartphone sensors for position estimation could significantly assist with parking. Within indoor settings, ubiquitous geomagnetic data with strong interference resistance serves as a vital signal source. Therefore, addressing the challenge of real-time positioning based on smartphone geomagnetic data in underground parking lot, this paper proposes a geomagnetic matching positioning method. During the offline phase, this method establishes a geomagnetic database for each parking lot section by matching trajectory positions via road network alignment and applying a geomagnetic clustering algorithm. In the online phase, real-time positioning is achieved using an improved sub-sequence query method and a dynamic time warping (DTW) algorithm constrained by feature points matching. Experiments conducted in a typical underground parking lot demonstrate that at a 95% confidence level, the cumulative distribution of positioning errors for the proposed method reaches 3.18 meters, whereas the error using only DTW reaches 7.25 meters. The proposed method has been validated as effective and has good positioning performance, providing an effective solution for real-time precise positioning in underground parking lot and other indoor scenarios.

title: Bone Fracture Detection and Type Classification
authors: Utkarsh Chaubey, Sankalp Mishra, Sarthak Dwivedi, Samyak Jain, Santosh Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis and classification of common bone injuries, particularly fractures, are paramount for ensuring appropriate medical care. While conventional diagnostic techniques rely on radiographic imaging, emerging developments in machine learning indicate a strong potential for automating this critical process. In this work, a convolutional neural network (CNN) model for X-ray image-based bone fracture detection and classification is presented. The model detects whether a bone is normal or fractured based on its X-ray image, and if it is, it classifies the type of fracture that occurred. The experimental results show that the suggested model is highly effective, achieving significant accuracy in fracture identification, classification and exceeding existing approaches. This study advances automated fracture diagnosis, which could improve patient care and treatment outcomes, and provides an efficient and user-friendly paradigm for healthcare professionals.

title: Hybrid Energy System Optimization Using Multiverse Optimizer and Metaheuristics for Improved Economic Load Dispatch and Cost Efficiency
authors: Manas Hembram, Swaraj Banerjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: In today's date power generation has achieves maximum energy output while distribution and affordable electricity have been very essential. But the world moving very fast towards the RES (Renewable energy sources) which reduces the fossil fuels consumptions, ozone layer depletion, human health, ecological balance, environmentally friendly and longterm sustainability. By using Economic load dispatch metaheuristics optimization techniques shows more reliable convergence then the conventional algorithms and faster. This paper investigates the effectiveness of the MVO (Multi-verse optimizer) applied for the Renewable energy sources and Nonrenewable energy sources. In both the 7 -unit and 15 -unit systems, GWO achieves lower costs than GA, BA, PSO, and DBA, with values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23,211.38 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> for NRES and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22,899.49 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> for RES. However, MVO consistently provides the best performance, achieves the lowest costs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22,910.94 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> (NRES) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22,414.60 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> (RES) for the 7 -unit system, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23,970.70 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> (NRES) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23,808.10 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> (RES) for the 15 -unit system. Here MVO achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 3 4 \% - 1. 5 4 \%}$</tex> lower cost and faster convergence then the other all algorithms.

title: Shoplifting Detection Using Convolutional Lstm Networks With Adam Optimiser
authors: Ruchika Mavis Daniel, Ayushi Chauhan, Avadhesh Kumar Prajapati, Sapna Juneja, Rekha Kashyap
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In today's era defined by modernization and unprecedented growth, the retail industry continues to rise. Theft remains a major problem, causing billions of dollars in losses annually. The research contained in this paper proposes a new framework for detecting theft in retail environments with intelligent video surveillance analysis. The formulation given here represents a hybrid Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory (ConvLSTM) approach that effectively merges both spatial and temporal data to document activities. The convolutional layers can extract spatial patterns and features in each frame sequence while the LSTM units model the temporal change of action over timestamps. This allows the system to develop an understanding of complex behavioral patterns that may be associated with retail shoplifting incidents. The Adam optimizer used here allows the learning process to converge faster and smoother as opposed to just using a standard stochastic gradient descent optimizer. Experiments on a varied dataset of retail theft scenarios indicate that the suggested model attains an 83 % rate of accuracy, exceeding the performance of traditional techniques for detection. Furthermore, the study points out challenges, including environmental variations and class imbalance that may reduce detection efficacy. The work proposes potential future developments, including alternative methods for data augmentation, as well as attention-based learning approaches, that would improve the robustness and adaptability of the model. Overall, this work provides an advancement in the use of intelligent, scalable, and automated security systems for the prevention of retail theft.

title: Machine Learning Driven Spectrum Sensing for Dynamic Access in Battlefield Radios
authors: Sachin Kumar, Megha Kataria
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Efficient spectrum sensing is critical for modern wireless communication systems, enabling adaptive use of the electromagnetic spectrum in dynamic and congested environments. This paper first examines time-series forecasting approaches for spectrum occupancy and highlights their limitations in rapidly changing or contested scenarios, such as battlefield environments. Conventional energy detection, which relies on fixed thresholding of received signal power, is also examined and found to be unreliable under noise uncertainty and low SNR conditions. To address these challenges, a Naive Bayes classifier is applied to statistical features extracted from spectrum measurements, achieving a detection accuracy of 95% for the frequency bands. The results demonstrate that even simple probabilistic classifiers can provide reliable and timely spectrum awareness, making them suitable for real-time applications in modern radio systems.

title: Design and Simulation of High-Performance GNRFET-Based Second-Generation Positive Current Conveyor for Analog Applications
authors: Tarannum Parvin, Neelofer Afzal, Sajad A. Loan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: We designed and simulated a second-generation positive Current Conveyor (CCII+) using graphene nanoribbon FET (GNRFET) at the 32 nm technology node. A desired performance is achieved by altering the number of ribbons and dimer lines in GNRFET. The performance parameters of GNRFET-based CCII+ are analysed and compared with CMOS-based CCII+ using HSPICE. It has been observed that the GNRFET-based CCII+ outperforms its CMOS counterparts. GNRFET CCII+ provides unity current and voltage gain. The current and voltage bandwidths of GNRFET CCII+ are 52 times and 116 times greater than those of CMOS CCII+, respectively. Additionally, GNRFET CCII+ demonstrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 9. 2 2 {\%}}$</tex> reduction in power consumption and a 49.44% increment in terminal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Y$</tex> resistance. Furthermore, a current-mode universal filter using GNRFET CCII+ is simulated and compared with the CMOS CCII+. GNRFET CCII+ universal filter reported better noise performance and is more energy efficient.

title: Contactless Breath Rate Classification Using mmWave Radar and Machine Learning
authors: Adeeba Khan, S M Saif Ali, Mohd Wajid, Abhishek Srivastava, Omar Farooq
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Accurate and timely monitoring of respiratory rate is critical for assessing patient health and detecting respiratory disorders. This research investigates the breath rate (BR) classification using mmWave FMCW radar and machine learning techniques, offering a contactless and non-invasive approach. A dataset, acquired using Texas Instruments AWR1642BOOST radar was utilized, at a fixed distance of 40 cm from 10 participants with various physiological conditions such as asthma and tachypnea, allowing the classification of BR into three distinct categories: Bradypnea (low), normal and tachypnea (elevated BR). Several machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Support Vector Classifier, Decision Tree, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest, and XGBoost, were evaluated across time windows of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 ~\mathrm{s}, 20 ~\mathrm{s}, 30 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>, and 40 s. Accuracy generally improved with longer windows, with Random Forest and XGBoost performing best: Random Forest achieved 98.41% for 40 s, and XGBoost reached 95.18% for 20 s. These results highlight the promise of combining radar sensing and AI for accurate, contactless respiratory monitoring and broader medical applications.

title: Deep Learning-Based Cross-Lineage Classification of Acute Leukemia Subtypes
authors: Saad Ahmed Syed, Humaira Nisar, Saeed Mian Qaisar, Lee Yu Jen, Rabeea Jaffari, Hum Yan Chai
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The accurate sub classification of acute leukemia is critical for patient prognosis and treatment planning. However, it remains a challenging task. Manual diagnosis is labor-intensive and prone to human errors. Existing Artificial Intelligence (AI) enhanced automated methods typically focus on either broad leukemia categories or fine-grained subtypes within a single cell lineage (myeloid or lymphoid). This study addresses a significant gap by formulating a novel cross-lineage classification task and investigating the feasibility of a single, unified deep learning (DL) model to solve it. Unlike prior work, focusing on subtypes within a single lineage, we combine key subtypes from both Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) and Acute Lymphocytic Leukemia (ALL) thereby creating a challenging seven-class problem. A comparative analysis is performed on four prominent architectures: ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, InceptionV3, and VGG16, all trained from scratch on the public Raabin dataset. The experimental results show that InceptionV3 attained a superior performance by achieving an average accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.51 \% \pm 0.04 \%$</tex>. Further class-wise analysis confirmed its robustness, with F1-scores exceeding 99% for all individual subtypes. These findings confirm that a single, unified model can effectively and accurately distinguish between fine-grained leukemia subtypes across different lineages. It leads towards a more comprehensive automated diagnostic tool.

title: Unveiling the Overlap: An XAI Approach for Distinguishing Depression and Bipolar Disorder from Reddit Posts
authors: Aniqa Khan, Zafi Sherhan Syed, Ali Bux Rajper, Shahnawaz Talpur, Jamil Junejo, Fahima Khan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Information shared on social media has been increasingly recognized as a valuable source for gaining large-scale insights into mental health. Depression and bipolar disorder share many symptoms, which makes them hard to tell apart early on. This paper looks at how Natural Language Processing (NLP) can help classify the two using Reddit posts. We study whether language on Reddit contains separable signals of depressive vs manic expression and how text classification models for this task explain their decisions faithfully. We built a balanced dataset from mental health subreddits and trained several machine learning and deep learning models. Among these, a BERT-based classifier achieved the highest testing accuracy of 88.80%, outperforming traditional baselines. To explain how the models work, we used SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations), a standard approach for eXplainable AI (XAI). We tested each model using standard metrics and reviewed the explanation outputs with mental health experts to incorporate their perspectives. The results show that these explainable methods improve transparency and reflect psychological patterns in automated mental health communication analysis. The potential benefits and limitations of XAI in mental health screening have been contextualized through feedback from experts from psychiatry and psychology. While this work is a preliminary study, it nevertheless has practical implications in mental health research and clinical settings.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Intelligent Optimization Analysis for Defect Suppression in Stamping Forming Processes
authors: Yichuan Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: To achieve precise suppression of multiple defects such as wrinkling, cracking, and springback in stamping forming, an attention-enhanced multi-scale feature fusion deep reinforcement learning (AM-MFF-DRL) algorithm is proposed to construct a defect-parameter coupling model. Based on Dynaform, 10,000 sets of DP590 steel stamping simulation data are generated, and Pearson analysis reveals the nonlinear coupling relationship between parameters and defects. The algorithm integrates multiscale feature extraction, attention enhancement, and a dualnetwork collaborative architecture, and designs a dynamic reward function to balance suppression effectiveness and optimization efficiency. Experiments show that the proposed algorithm achieves a wrinkling suppression rate of 92.3 %, a crack risk reduction rate of 87.6 %, and a springback control accuracy of 0.08 mm. Compared with GA, PSO, and basic DRL, it significantly improves in defect suppression effect, convergence speed (68.5 % reduction in iterations), optimization time (66.2 % reduction), and generalization ability (generalization error 0.032), providing an efficient solution for intelligent optimization of stamping processes under complex working conditions.

title: Policy, People, and Protection: A Decade of Behavioral Cybersecurity Research in the Public Sector
authors: Frank Sengati, Abdulkarim M. Jamal Kanaan, Ramesh Kumar Ayyasamy, Abdelhak Senadjki
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This systematic review examines empirical research on cybersecurity threats avoidance behavior among public sector employees. Using the PRISMA protocol, 23 studies out of 2627 published between 2015 and 2024 from Scopus, IEEE, and Emerald were reviewed subject to inclusion and exclusion criteria. Our results show a strong concentration of research in Southeast Asia, mostly in Indonesia and Malaysia, and a methodological dominance of cross-sectional quantitative literature. Theoretical diversity was observed with Protection Motivation Theory leading, followed by Theory of Planned Behavior and Technology Threat Avoidance Theory. Thematically, three major clusters emerged, namely cybersecurity awareness, behavioral responses, and policy compliance. Cognitive and attitudinal factors such as perceived severity, vulnerability, and self-efficacy were most common, while social, motivational, and emotional constructs were least featured. Awareness and knowledge-related factors played a dominant role, appearing in 69.5 % of the studies. Our work contributes by mapping an unexplored yet significant domain of behavioral cybersecurity in public institutions. The review exposes regional, methodological, and conceptual gaps. Our work advocates for future research to adopt comparative mixed-method approaches, expand theory utilization, and integrate organizational and emotional constructs to better understand the complexity of cybersecurity behavior in the public sector.

title: Electromagnetic Performance Comparison of E-Core and U-Core Five Phase Hybrid Excited Outer Rotor Flux Switching Machine
authors: Haris Shahbaz, Naseer Ahmad, Surat Khan, Yasir Shahbaz, Muhammad Zia Javed, Abdin Pasund
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: In this research, the electromagnetic performances of E-core and U-Core five phase outer rotor hybrid excited outer rotor flux switching machines (ORHEFSM) have been compared. The outer rotor flux switching machine (ORFSM) gives a structural advantage with high torque density, while the multiphase winding configuration increases fault tolerant capability and low torque ripples. Both designs are analyzed under the fixed constraint, keeping constant magnetic and electric loading, outer diameter, and axial length. This study explores operational principles and investigates various electromagnetic performances including magnetic flux linkage, cogging torque, back EMF, instantaneous torque, and average torque. The finding indicate that the E-core exhibits better flux linkage and higher electromagnetic torque compared to U-core design, make it more viable for direct drive application.

title: Optimizing Energy Efficiency in Cubesats: A Residual-Energy Minimization Approach
authors: Muhammad Hassan Yaqoob, Huang Hai
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: We present a residual-aware, mixed-integer methodology for sizing small-satellite electrical power systems (EPS) that replaces static worst-case rules with time-resolved, orbitwise design. The approach combines orbit-level energy modelling with integer sizing, so solar array area and battery capacity are treated as catalogue-constrained hardware rather than free continuous parameters. A backward-forward look-ahead evaluator computes, for each orbit, the minimum stored energy needed to satisfy eclipse demand and downstream orbits, then executes charge/discharge with efficiencies and state-of-charge (SOC) limits while classifying any remaining sunlit surplus as unstorable residual. A composite objective minimizes mission-total residual energy, normalized to a fixed baseline, while regularizing array area and battery capacity and penalizing unmet loads or SOC-floor violations. Latin Hypercube Sampling and a realcoded genetic algorithm explore the continuous design space, and an outer loop snaps solutions to manufacturable hardware (integer cell counts and catalogue battery SKUs). On a 101orbit MSAT case study, the method converges to a design with 9 cells and a 14.00 Wh battery, reducing battery capacity by 11.6% relative to a worst-case baseline while maintaining orbit-wise feasibility at the SOC floor. The resulting kJ-level residuals indicate high utilization rather than oversized margins. Stress tests with generation, efficiency, timing, load, and depth-of-discharge perturbations reuse the same evaluator to track SOC margins, residuals, and shortfall orbits and highlight load growth and DOD tightening as dominant sensitivities. Driven by actual access/coverage timelines and catalogue-constrained hardware, the method is directly deployable for CubeSats and can be reused in operations for timeline vetting and contingency analysis, making residual-aware sizing a practical pathway to lean, resilient small-satellite EPS.

title: Deep Learning Models for Real-Time Intrusion Detection and Anomaly Prevention
authors: Saikrishna Tipparapu, Yugandhar Suthari, Deepak Khamitkar, Syed Nazmul Hasan, Abdul Hadi, Sivakoteswararao Katta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) have become obsolete due to the growing sophistication of network attacks and threat evolution. This research paper emphasizes the urgent need for a signature-free approach based real-time intelligent automated adaptive intrusion detection system to fully address the constraints of signature-based mechanisms. This study investigates how deep learning models can prevent intrusions and anomalies in real time, specifically CNN, LSTM networks, and Autoencoders. The research shows that approaches based on deep learning achieve higher detection accuracy and reduce false positive more than traditional methods, as these models are evaluated on benchmark datasets NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017. In addition, it presents a hybrid model aligned with different spatial and temporal feature extraction techniques to aid the system in detecting more complex attacks. Proposed security systems that can cover attacks against botnets and phishing based on a hybrid model are shown to outperform new approaches, achieving an accuracy of 97.6%, suggesting the potential of deep learning techniques within scalable adaptive cybersecurity systems. This work presents critical insight and guidelines for the design of real-time, DL-based cybersecurity mechanisms.

title: Adaptive Multi-Modal Fusion for Accurate Cardiovascular Risk and Heart Failure Detection Using Transformer Architectures
authors: M. Sreenivasulu, S. Sabeel, Y. Naga Sai Ganesh, K. Ramanandin, E. Reddy Siva Lakshmi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: CVDs are one of the most common causes of death in the world, and there is a need to provide the most accurate and timely diagnosis tools. The current paper is an overview of a multi-modal deep learning architecture that combines ECG images, physiological measures and patient metadata to conduct a thorough cardiovascular analysis. The system uses DenseNet121 in extracting spatial ECG feature and transformer-based encoders in modeling temporal and clinical dependencies. A correlation-sensitive oversampling algorithm is proposed to solve the problem of uneven classes and a weighted fusion system is used to merge the modality-specific embeddings to make predictions in multi-task prediction. The experimental results show a very high performance with accuracy, precision, and AUC of the ECG classification being 99.29, 99.32, and 99.96, respectively, and cardiovascular risk and heart-failure prediction. The suggested model provides a clinically flexible, and scalable automated cardiovascular screening.

title: A Smart Wheelchair with Integrated Emergency Protocols and Multi-Mode Control System
authors: Shobham Guha, Arghya Ghosh, Kamakhya Narain Singh, Manas Mukul, Sadhana Sudershana, Chinmaya Misra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this paper we propose an intelligent wheel chair which consider patient safety and help mobility of patients in indoor environment specifically. Different sensors are used for mesasureing patient parameter and emergency situation like fire or obstacle or sudden fall. The “Nurse Assistant” module of the model help in absence of nurse and can function in manual or hand gesture mode. It provides real time alerts in any emergency situation. All modules are integrated and tested with real life data for smooth operations. Feedback taken from users and improved performance by fine tuning of software and sensor components. It enhances safety and work independently.

title: IoT in Electrical Protection: Remote-Controlled Circuit Breaker System
authors: Megha Chakole, Aryalaxmi Channe, Prachi Yerkar, Srushti Jibhakate, Sanket Deshpande, Sanjay Dorle
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The proposed IoT-Based Remote Control and Monitoring circuit breaker system is designed to improve electrical safety and control within the domestic and industrial environment. The system would be able to monitor and control the electrical circuits in real-time with the aid of the IoT technology in that way, responding in a timely manner to the threats. It works on microcontroller, Wi-fi and relay-based circuitry which contributes both to its efficiency in its operation and also security in its usage through the use of password. Solid state switching when compared to mechanical breakers is better in terms of response time as well as maintenance. The basic benefits of the system include enhanced safety, low maintenance price and it is automatable. Real-time monitoring gives a chance to make decisions with reference to data and reduce the downtime of the system to a minimum. It is the flexible system and can be applied both at manufacturing plants and residential buildings and at such crucial facilities like hospitals and data centers. This demonstrates the extent to which it can be applied in the actual world. The subsequent additions may be integration into other IoT device, predictive analytics to dictate false and better cyber security. The strategy provides a smarter safer and more automated alternative to the traditional method of controlling electric circuits by offering a flexible and efficient method.

title: Prediction of Electric Vehicle Charging Demand Using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
authors: Muhammad Nazeem Bin Dzahrin, Saeed Salem Saeed Ba Hashwan, Illani Bt Mohd Nawi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of electric vehicle (EV) charging demand is essential for efficient energy management in modern smart grids. This paper presents a deep learning approach using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to predict EV charging load based on historical charging patterns. The proposed model integrates temporal and behavioural features to effectively capture sequential dependencies in EV charging data. Additionally, model performance is compared with and without external inputs such as weather and traffic data to evaluate their impact on prediction accuracy. The dataset undergoes preprocessing and normalization before being used for training and evaluation. Performance metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> are used to assess model accuracy. Results indicate that the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model delivers high prediction performance and is capable of learning complex time-based patterns in energy consumption. This approach supports the development of smarter energy distribution strategies and contributes to improving the reliability of EV infrastructure planning. The proposed model achieved an MAE of 155.53 kWh, RMSE of 195.95 kWh, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score of 0.9590, indicating high predictive accuracy.

title: Influence Maximization of Social Networks Using DRL
authors: Deepak Negi, Gaurav Pandey, Isha Anand, Aditya Dayal Tyagi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Influence Maximization can be defined as identifying highly influential individuals in a social network in such a strategic way that it achieves the maximum coverage of information spreading. The problem with existing methods is that they consist of computationally intensive strategies involving greedy algorithms or are simplistic representations of reality. Recently, advancements in graph representation learning and decision-making have emerged as effective alternatives for existing methods. The current study proposes utilizing GNNs coupled with DRL for influence maximization. Rather it should learn from the structure of the network itself how it can select the seed set of users who have the largest effect. The authors applied their framework on real-world networks such as Facebook and Epinions, sourced from the SNAP dataset, employing the Independent Cascade information spread process on a diffusion model. The results are encouraging. GNN-DRL provides better performance compared to other simplistic approaches, including Degree Centrality. It does so in a more computationally efficient manner and also shows the ability to be scalable and flexible. The most noticeable aspect of this particular method would be its ability to learn the actual spread of influence in the network as it gains experience from the network itself. This work will not only make influence maximization more effective but also open up broad future directions of work on adaptive graphbased optimization techniques.

title: Pulse Amplitude Modulation (PAM) and Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) Using Real Voice Input
authors: Niveda L, Pooja S, Rupa Mishra, Pritam Bhowmik
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This project presents the design and implementation of Pulse Amplitude Modulation (PAM) and Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) techniques applied to a recorded voice signal, with the objective of evaluating and comparing their performance in qualitative and physically observable terms of signal modulation, demodulation, and fidelity of reconstruction. Using MATLAB SIMULINK as the simulation environment, both modulation schemes were developed and tested under similar conditions to analyze their respective impacts on communication quality. The study examines the efficiency of each technique in preserving the integrity of the original voice sample after demodulation, focusing on factors such as noise resilience, bandwidth requirements, and complexity of implementation. PAM, being amplitude-based, was observed to be more susceptible to noise, whereas PWM, relying on pulse duration, showed higher accuracy in waveform reconstruction. The results provide insights into the practical advantages and limitations of each modulation method, contributing to better decision-making for real-time voice communication systems and digital signal processing applications. Further, the model proposed gives a simple qualitative approach in comparing PAM and PWM against one another in terms of analysis rather than challenging existing techniques.

title: Optimised Deep Learning Framework for Forest Stand Segmentation in Image Gallery
authors: T.Hari Kala, Meghana. A, Jayasree Gundu, T.Hemanth Kumar, K.Karthik Kumar, Gadda Maddilety
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Sound forest stand and vegetation density determination plays a critical role in tracking of the ecological balance and determining the sustainability of the environment. Manual and threshold based solutions are usually problematic in the presence of overlapping textures and changes in illumination in satellite imaging. The given research is offering an effective automated method that can divide forests areas, determine the number of plants per square meter, and categorize the general well-being of a given zone. The model proved a validation and testing accuracy of 98.5% and 96.1% respectively which guarantees high precision in forest boundary availability and density estimation. The vegetation maps produced are able to clearly visualize the dense, moderate and sparse areas within the forest and assist in monitoring and conservation planning of forests over a long period of time. The suggested solution increases the accuracy, scalability, and interpretability of largescale analysis of forest resources.

title: Fabrication and Electrochemical Analysis of Fluorine-Doped Tin Oxide Thin Films for Photovoltaic Applications
authors: Md. Yasir Arafat, Yasmin Abdul Wahab, Sharifah Fatmadiana Bt Wan Muhammad Hatta, Mohammad Aminul Islam, Nurul Ezaila Alias, Mohd Rafie Johan, Maizan Muhamad, Hanim Hussin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Fluorine-doped tin oxide (FTO) thin films serve as essential transparent conductive electrodes in photovoltaic and optoelectronic applications due to their superior electrical conductivity, optical transparency, and stability. In this study, we report the successful synthesis and deposition of FTO thin films onto glass substrates using tin (IV) chloride hexahydrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{SnCl}_{4} \cdot 6 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$</tex> as a novel precursor, diverging from the commonly reported use of anhydrous <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SnCl}_{4}, \text{SnCl}_{4} \cdot \mathbf{5} \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$</tex>, or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S n C l}_{\mathbf{2}} \cdot \mathbf{2} \mathbf{H}_{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{O}$</tex>. The precursor solution was prepared and applied via spin-coating, followed by thermal annealing to enhance structural integrity. Electrochemical voltammetry characterization was performed to comprehensively assess charge transport properties, stability, and interfacial behavior, which are critical for photovoltaic applications. Cyclic voltammetry (CV) analysis revealed well-defined redox peaks, indicating efficient charge transfer mechanisms. Currentvoltage and current-time profiles demonstrated excellent electrical conductivity and stability under operational conditions. Nyquist plots from electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) confirmed a low charge transfer resistance, signifying enhanced carrier collection efficiency, while Bode plots provided insights into the capacitive behavior and charge storage dynamics of the films. These findings validate the potential of the synthesized FTO films as high-performance transparent electrodes for next-generation solar cells, ensuring improved efficiency and long-term operational reliability.

title: Single-Negative Metamaterial Using Adhesive Copper Laminate for Microwave Breast Imaging Applications
authors: Intan Shafinaz Abd. Razak, Zahriladha Zakaria, Ahmed Jamal Abdullah Al-Gburi, Maizatul Alice Meor Said, Syah Alam, Ariffuddin Joret
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This work presents a fabric-based coplanar waveguide (CPW) antenna integrated with an adhesive copper metamaterial (MTM) reflector positioned beneath the radiating element. The MTM, fabricated on a thin polyimide substrate, simplifies the fabrication process and improves structural thickness. A 5 × 5 single-negative MTM unit cell array functions as a reflector, enhancing the antenna's bandwidth and gain within 2.46–3.94 GHz. The MTM-integrated antenna exhibits wide operational bandwidths of 2.91–7.00 GHz (simulated) and 1.97-6.66 GHz (measured), achieving a peak gain of 6.76 dBi. The design is further validated using a delay-and-sum (DAS) algorithm for tumor image reconstruction, demonstrating its applicability in microwave imaging.

title: An Intelligent Vision-Based Architecture for Sign Language Recognition Using Pre-Trained Deep Networks
authors: Priyanka Kaushik, Sushil Bhargav, Nagendar Yamsani, Balakrishnan D, Dune Satyanarayana, Bhavik Kuchipudi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Sign language is an essential mode of communication among people who are deaf and mute and the traditional recognition systems are usually based on complicated hardware arrangements and large-scale computation recourses, which makes accessibility and real-time usability more difficult. The paper will introduce an extremely effective sign language recognition and detection system using OpenCV and a ready-made deep learning model, a combination of computer vision and artificial intelligence to provide highquality, real-time, and scalable gesture recognition. The device uses the OpenCV to enhance the preprocessing and extraction of functionalities in pictures, enhancing the segmentation of the hand gestures under different conditions, as well as other lighting sources, backgrounds, and hand poses. The capability to use a pre-trained deep learning model guarantees a high level of classification of both static and complex gestures and a drastic decrease in the large size of labeled datasets is a cost-effective and viable approach in real-life applications. The findings of the experiment show the high level of precision and reliability in a broad collection of sign gestures, which speaks of the adaptability of the system and its ability to withstand the differences in the environment. The framework, optimally utilizing deep learning inference through retrieval based processing and combining it with real-time processing of computer vision, has allowed minimization of latency, speedy response-time and improved usability that will lead to smart and user friendly sign language applications. Future directions will be on expanding the gesture data, adding dynamic and continuous gesture recognition, and optimizing the model to be used in mobile and embedded application. These innovations are meant to make things more accessible and create inclusive communication technologies. By and large, this study demonstrates the potential AI-based systems to revolutionize the technologies of sign language interpretation and make the hearing- and speech-impaired community more independent.

title: MSA-MRI: Multi-Scale Attention MRI Network for Brain Tumor Classification and Patch-Level Analysis
authors: Neha Kamat, Charvi Mahajan, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh, Twinkle Tiwari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Accurate identification of brain tumors from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) remains a challenging task due to the heterogeneous morphology, irregular boundaries, and varying intensity patterns present in tumor tissues. Automated systems need to provide reliable performance which helps radiologists achieve precise results in diagnosis and treatment planning. The study introduces MSA-MRI as a Multi-Scale Attentionbased framework which unites global contextual data with localized tumor-specific features into a single deep learning system. The proposed model uses a two-stream architecture which includes a global stream that uses EfficientNet to obtain complete brain images while the local stream processes several image patches through a transformer-based multi-head selfattention system which creates models of patch relationships. The system achieves effective multi-scale representation learning through global and local feature integration which results in better classification outcomes and improved interpretability. The training process uses MixUp data augmentation together with label smoothing to make the system more robust and better at handling different situations. The evaluation of the model uses the Kaggle Brain MRI dataset which includes four tumor categories together with a no tumor category. The system demonstrates high classification accuracy by successfully establishing connection with the system. The clinicians used patch-level attention visualization to create interpretable results which showed them the important areas needed for diagnosis while building trust through clinical transparent methods.

title: Operational Amplifier Circuit with Miller Compensation Using CMOS 180 nm Technology
authors: Mohamad Anas Luqman, Siti Fatimah Abd Rahman, Mohamad Faris Mohamad Fathil, Mohammad Nuzaihan Md Nor, Mohamed Fauzi Packeer Mohamed, Mohd Rosydi Zakaria
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: In modern electronics, efficient and highperformance operational amplifiers (op-amps) are essential, particularly for applications such as implantable medical devices. This work aims to advance the field by developing a two-stage operational amplifier with Miller capacitor compensation, implemented using Cadence 180 nm technology. The primary objectives were to design and analyze an amplifier that operates with an average power consumption of less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex> with a 1.8 V supply voltage and achieves a commonmode rejection ratio (CMRR) greater than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0 ~ d B}$</tex>. These goals were successfully achieved. The amplifier demonstrated power consumption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$250.595 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex> at a common-mode voltage (Vcm) of 1.6 V and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$220.01 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex> at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{cm}}$</tex> of 0.8 V. Additionally, it attained a CMRR of 92.2 dB, significantly exceeding the initial target. The use of CMOS technology in this design offers notable advantages, including low power consumption and high integration capability, making it ideal for applications ranging from portable health monitors to sophisticated implantable devices. Despite these advancements, maintaining balanced performance while optimizing power efficiency remains a challenge. This work addresses such challenges by incorporating advanced compensation techniques in the operational amplifier's design. The results of this work contribute to the development of highly energy-efficient CMOS op-amps, supporting a wide range of low-power applications.

title: A Robust CSP-EEGNet Framework for Motor Imagery EEG Classification in BCI Systems
authors: Naveen Pandey, Dilnawaz, Anand Mohan, R.S Anand
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The accurate interpretation of EEG signals is a major challenge for Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) based on Motor Imagery (MI). We proposed a CSP-Enhanced EEGNet model to classify Rest, Left-hand, and Right-hand motor imagery tasks. The raw signals are filtered using a band-pass filter from 8-30 Hz and a 50 Hz notch filter, followed by segmentation of the processed data into epochs of fixed length. The Common Spatial Patterns (CSP) technique is used to isolate the discriminatory activities of the motor cortex before the EEGNet architecture is trained. The network was trained for 55 epochs, and convergence was observed to be stable. The converged network achieved a training accuracy of 0.62 and a validation accuracy of 0.59, which is evidence of optimal learning with little to no overfitting. The confusion matrix showed that the model is accurate in identifying the Rest state and demonstrates a good separation of the Left and Right MI tasks. In spite of the variability of EEG data, the model achieved macro-averaged ROC-AUC scores of 0.653, 0.613, and 0.567, which show that the model adequately discriminates between classes. These findings confirm the proposed architecture as a robust, reproducible baseline for future real-time BCI development.

title: Performance Evaluation of Steel Fiber Reinforced Concrete: A Case Study Based Perspective
authors: Rutuja V. Bendre, Om S. Karange, Karuna G. Dandale, Mukesh S. Khedkar, Prasad D. Konde, Om A. Pise
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Concrete is the most widely used construction material in India; however, its brittle nature, low tensile strength, and limited ductility make it vulnerable to cracking and durability issues under dynamic loads and harsh service conditions. Steel fiber reinforced concrete, or SFRC, has become a popular way to improve overall structural performance, post-cracking behavior, and crack control. This study synthesizes results from recent experimental and field-oriented research to provide a case-studybased assessment of SFRC. Compressive strength, flexural strength, tensile behavior, durability, sustainability, and economic efficiency are used to evaluate the performance of SFRC. The findings show that flexural toughness, residual strength, and serviceability performance significantly improve while compressive strength gains are only modest. The study underscores the need for field-validated guidelines and shows how constructability, mix design, and fiber dosage affect real-world performance. The results back the use of SFRC as a performancebased material for long-lasting and environmentally friendly infrastructure development in India.

title: Space-Efficient Solar Energy Harvesting Through Smart Flexible PV Orientation Control
authors: Gurukarthik Babu Balachandran, Prince Winston David, Santhiya Jayaraj, MadhuMitha Mani Kannan, Manjuladevi Palani Kumar, Muthu Eshwaran Ramachandran, Dharun Raja Thirupathi, Rajesh Babu S
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: A flexible solar photovoltaic system that could assess solar module performance in a variety of space installations was described in this paper. This analysis represents the different configurations, which can be represented as (C0-C6). Solar modules are arranged in seven configuration levels (C0-C6) with reduced surface areas. It indicates the curvy, edge-mounted, and compact installations. The output voltage, current, and power were measured under the standard irradiance conditions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(1000 / \mathrm{m}^{2}\right)$</tex> for performance analysis. The analysis shows that total output power drops when the panel area is reduced, but at the same time, power density (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>) and efficiency are improved, with efficiency of 71.3 % in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C 6}$</tex> configuration. It concluded that flexible <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> modules can produce a good energy conversion even in complex areas. This is achieved because of better irradiation and surface availability. This study focuses the ability of flexible photovoltaic technology to be an effective and large-scale solution for energy production in urban and architectural environments that have space restrictions.

title: Performance Analysis of BPSK Modulation in AWGN Channels for Space Communication Systems
authors: Priyanka Ahlawat, Supriya Mishra, Pravin Patidar, Mayank Dave, Mohanchur Sarkar
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Satellite communication systems maintain reliable data transfer in noisy environments making them critical for low-power applications such as IOT and remote connectivity. This paper presents a simplified communication model for analyzing data transfer between satellites and terrestrial- based users. Binary phase shift keying modulation over an additive white gaussian noise channel is used to represent the system, with an emphasis on both uplink and downlink scenarios. The impact of different signal to noise ratio levels, from 0 to 20 dB, on bit error rate and propagation latency is examined using Matlab simulations. Results indicate that bit error rate falls around 0.1 at 5 dB to less than 0.001 at 20 dB, demonstrating the robustness of binary phase shift keying in maintaining low error rates even in noisy conditions. An initial step toward enhancing the reliability of satellite communication is provided by this study. It also creates opportunities for further research, in which enhanced channel models and adaptive modulation techniques may be essential to addressing the demands of space-terrestrial networks in the future.

title: Harnessing AI to Decode Adolescent Stress and Its Mental Health Consequences
authors: Sarita Peddi, Geetha Manoharan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The research paper emphasizes managing adolescent stress, to produce healthy Indian leaders. Engineering students examine youth mental health and stress. Teenage stress can impair cognitive, emotional, and social development. Youth face academic pressure, peer interactions, and career insecurity. Chronic stress causes anxiety, depression, and poor school performance, reducing students' quality of life. This quantitative study of 100 engineering students from multiple colleges from Telangana uses simple random sampling. For statistical analysis of key factors, nominal data were quantified by numbering each category. Student stress averaged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.16(\text{SD}=0.90)$</tex> on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-3$</tex> scale, indicating moderate to high levels. Academic expectations (55 percent), worries about being unemployed after graduation (40 percent), and life concerns account for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 2 \%}$</tex> of moderate to high stress. Stress was connected to anxiety and depression by over 60 % of respondents. Stress management and mental health awareness in schools and colleges may help, according to research. We recommend counseling, peer support, and stress-reduction for institutional students. Programs teach resilience and coping. Future leaders should prioritize youth mental health. Youth stress management could sustain India.

title: AI-Enabled Microgrid for Effective Energy Management with Battery Health Monitoring
authors: N. Sushma Devi, G. Dileep Kumar, S. Taraka Sanyasi Naidu, K. Padma, K. Manohar
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy resources into modern microgrids demands not only efficient energy management but also reliable energy storage solutions. This paper presents an AI-enabled microgrid framework that simultaneously addresses effective energy management and battery health monitoring. Solar energy is considered the primary source, where AI ensures intelligent prioritization between solar, battery, and grid for uninterrupted supply. At the same time, the system incorporates State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH) monitoring to minimize battery degradation during frequent source transitions. The proposed design ensures maximum utilization of renewable solar energy, reduced grid dependency, optimized load distribution, and extended battery life. Experimental results validate that the dual focus on energy management and storage health provides a sustainable, resilient, and intelligent microgrid solution.

title: Ai-Powered Multimodal Crop Disease Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Kamlesh Kumar Meghnath Yede, Praful V. Barekar, Charvi Suri
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Plant diseases drastically cut yield and profit. Although CNNs generalize well to image-labeled leaves in the lab, performance suffers in fields because of inconsistent lighting, occlusions, distracting scene background, and overlapping symptoms due to multiple coinciding stresses. Here we introduce an AI-enabled multimodal monitoring platform that integrates leaf imagery with sensor (temperature, humidty, soil moisture, and pump/motor ON/OFF) observations and image features optionally extracted with UAVs to enhance robustness. The pipeline encompasses four model families: (i) an image-focused ResNet-50 CNN baseline; (ii) image-only transformer (ViT-B/16); (iii) late multimodal fusion of the top image features with sensor-inspired MLP; and (iv) Attentionfusion model learning image-ViT embeddings and sensor context in unison.
