Tested on standardized PlantVillage/PlantDoc corpora within a balanced set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 5}$</tex>-class corpora and an expanded 65 -class corpora set, multimodal methods reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97-98 \%$</tex> test accuracy with better macro-F1, especially among rare and visually ambiguous classes, outperforming image-baseline methods. Interpretabilty comes in the form of Grad-CAM maps combined with attention-rollout providing lesion location with context attribution. All artifacts (PyTorch implementation, Colab notebooks, logs, confusion matrices, checkpoints) become available for reproducibility. Our findings demonstrate multimodality fortifying field-scale disease surveillance at scale, enhancing the potential for edge deployability with farmer-accepted transparent diagnostics with context awareness.

title: Fuzzy Logic Driven Kernel ELM Model with Gaussian Activation Function for Accurate Wind Forecasting
authors: Subhasmita Swain, P.K. Das, S. Nanda, S P Mishra
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid forecasting model that integrates fuzzy logic with a Gaussian kernel–based methodology, in which Gaussian Membership Functions (GMFs) are utilized as activation functions. The proposed model, termed the Fuzzy Logic Gaussian-based Kernel Extreme Learning Machine (FLGKELM), leverages the smooth and differentiable characteristics of GMFs, making them an effective alternative to conventional activation functions. These properties enhance the learning capability of the model and improve prediction accuracy, particularly for time-series forecasting applications. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated using a real-world dataset and compared with conventional forecasting methods. Experimental results demonstrate that the FLGKELM model achieves superior prediction accuracy, even in the presence of uncertainty and noise

title: “Power Transmission Line Fault Detection and Classification: Machine Learning Approach”
authors: Vaibhav Ghodeswar, Mirza Ansar Beg, Sonali R. Nandanwar, Mohammaed Zubair Sheikh, Aayushee. G. Kamble, Deepmala Tayade
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The reliable operation of transmission networks is crucial to providing an uninterrupted electricity supply. Faults in transmission lines, if not recognized and assessed quickly, can cause equipment damage, instability, and widespread blackouts. This study offers a successful method for classifying and identifying faults in power transmission lines by combining machine learning techniques with S-Transform. The S-Transform, a strong time frequency analysis technique, is used to extract differentiating characteristic s from voltage and current data during fault circumstances. These characteristics, which include both temporal and spectral data, are then utilized to train supervised classifiers such as support vector machines (SVM), k-nearest neighbors (KNN), Ensembles and decision trees (DT). The suggested technique is tested using simulation data for various fault kinds, locations, and noise conditions. The results show great accuracy in fault detection and exact categorization of fault types, such as LG, LL, LLG and LLL faults the combination of S-Transform with machine learning considerably improves the resilience and reliability of the fault diagnostic system, making it a potential option for current smart grid applications.

title: Advancements in Accident Detection for Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks: Techniques, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Niharika Singh, Jay Prakash, Ashish Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) have recently attracted a lot of interest in accident detection because of their essential service in road safety to ensure traffic flows smoothly. These networks used to support real time communications between vehicles (V2V) and infrastructure (V2I) serve as a base to intelligent transportation systems that can detect accident in a timely and precise manner. Recent improvements in the sphere of communication technologies, artificial intelligence as well as Internet of Things (IoT) have led to the creation of more efficient and stable mechanisms of accident detection. The given paper will provide a coherent overview of the latest methods being used in the detection of accidents, such as rule-based schemes, machine learning systems, IoT-driven frameworks, and identification systems that use a mix of different procedures to increase precision and stability. It also examines how edge computing can be incorporated into real-time computing of data, and how important communication protocols, including 5G and Dedicated Short-Range Communications (DSRC), will be to low-latency reporting in accidents. Moreover, the paper provides the information on the current difficulties, such as the problem of data privacy, scalability in an urban context, and absence of uniformity in VANET architectures. Suggestions are made on future research directions that should take advantage of improved technologies to solve these problems and make the VANETs capable of developing scalable, reliable, and real-time accident detection systems by exploiting 6G networks, hybrid AI models and blockchain to securely share its data. This research aligns with SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-being).

title: Fuzzy Logic Integrated Proportional Derivative Based Control of 2-DOF Robotic Manipulator
authors: Sonali Negi, Asha Rani, Vijander Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The control of robotic systems is a challenging task due to their non-linear dynamics, non-linear actuators, flexibility-induced deformations, environmental factors, singularities, sensor errors, etc. Problems like load variation, uncertainties, friction, coupling, nonlinearities, etc. further complicate the robotic control. Further, precise control of robotic arm manipulators is highly essential to ensure safety, accuracy and reliability in sensitive applications. Therefore, a Fuzzy logic integrated Proportional Derivative controller is designed for 2-link planar rigid robotic arm manipulator. The simulation of two degrees of freedom link planar robotic arm is carried out in MATLAB. The designed controller is compared with traditional PID and PD controller in terms of error-based parameters. The results reveal that the Fuzzy Logic integrated PD control scheme efficiently evaluates the required joint torques, based on fuzzy rules and outputs of PD controllers. It is also more challenging to control link 1 as compared to link 2 because link 2 acts as an additional weight on link 1. Hence the proposed controller outperforms PID and PD controllers with deviations from the desired trajectory.

title: A Lightweight Student-Teacher Network for Multimodal Industrial Anomaly Detection with RGB-D Data
authors: Zhifu Hou, Yongfa Lin, Xingjiang Han, Wenlong Wang, Kehua Zhang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In recent years, 2D anomaly detection methods have made notable progress, but they remain insufficient in capturing geometric defects. Although existing multimodal approaches introduce 3D information, they often rely on memory banks, resulting in high memory consumption and inference latency that hinder practical deployment. Therefore, we propose a Lightweight Asymmetric Student-Teacher Network (LASTNet), which adopts a frozen pretrained ConvNeXt as the feature extractor and builds a lightweight student network based on depthwise separable convolutions to reduce parameters while enhancing asymmetry with the teacher. Experiments on the MVTec 3D-AD dataset demonstrate that LASTNet achieves an I-AUROC of 95.2%, surpassing state-of-the-art 3D anomaly detection methods. Meanwhile, it attains an inference speed of 26.8 FPS and reduces the model size by 54% compared with baseline methods, striking a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency.

title: In-Plane and Out-of-Plane Measurement of the Spectralon Spectral BRDF for Sensor and Light Source Calibration
authors: Paul-Elie Pipelin
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Spectralon is a key component of measurement pipelines. Its uniform spectral response and diffuse appearance led it to be used for sensor and light source calibration. As a measured standard, it enables simplified absolute or relative calibrations, supporting indirect measurements such as irradiance estimation, area light characterization, and beam profiling. It is therefore essential to accurately represent its reflectance for all illumination and observation directions. This paper reports high-precision Bidirectional Reflectance Distribution Function (BRDF) measurements over thousands of configurations, including backscattering, and proposes analytical models for interpolation and extrapolation with a use case for estimating the intensity distribution of a light source.

title: Novel Control Technique Used in Power Transition Between Vehicle to Grid and Grid to Vehicle Systems With Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Venkata Koteswara Rao N, K. Bala Krishna
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In recent years, the adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has been increasing rapidly, which places an additional burden on local power grids. To minimize this stress, renewable energy-based EV charging infrastructures have been introduced, where renewable sources are integrated with the local grid. During periods when renewable energy is insufficient or unavailable, the EV battery can be charged directly from the grid. Conversely, when excess energy is available, power can be supplied back to the grid from the EV battery or renewable energy sources. This bidirectional energy flow concept aligns with Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) operations. The major challenge in this system is the precise control of the bidirectional converter that interfaces the power grid and the EV battery. To address this issue, a novel control technique is proposed for accurate signal management of the bidirectional converter, enabling smooth coordination among the EV battery, renewable energy systems, and the power grid. The proposed controller, named the Parameter-Based Digital Controller (PBDC), regulates the control signals based on renewable energy variations and battery parameters. The effectiveness of the proposed PBDC is validated through MATLAB simulations considering key inputs such as solar irradiance, wind speed, and the state of charge (SOC) of the battery.

title: Enhancing Detection of Malicious Traffic through Security Information and Event Management System
authors: D. Jeyamani Latha, M. Pandiyarajan, K. Murugeswari, Charan NK, Sushman S, Aravindh L
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Understanding how to put in place efficient security measures is crucial to avoiding the risks posed by cyberattacks. Malicious traffic detection is a sophisticated technique that uses multiple approaches to determine whether traffic is malicious or benign. Traditional malicious traffic detection methods typically rely on pre-defined signatures. We have suggested a SIEM technique to identify malicious traffic in real-time in order to solve this problem. SIEMs, or security information and event management systems, are an effective tool for avoiding, detecting, and responding to cyberattacks. In this scenario, SIEM systems with broad visibility were used to identify high-risk areas and proactively focus on mitigation strategies to reduce incident response costs and time.

title: Resume Analyzer: From Data to Direction
authors: Sruthi Nair, Supriya Gupta, Poorva Agrawal, Harshala Shingne
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Many applicants apply for a single position during a company's or organization's hiring procedure. The first step in the hiring process is reviewing each resume individually to choose the best applicant. However, For recruiters, this is a demanding and time-consuming process. This is because every applicant has a unique, distinct resume with various forms and components. Therefore, recruiters are unable to go with a one-by-one process. In order to save the time and effort required of recruiters in this process, we have suggested a method that makes it simple for recruiters to rank resumes in basic file formats. Natural Language Processing (NLP) is the foundation of this research. Candidates will upload their resumes in a variety of formats, most commonly in PDF. These resumes will be parsed for field extraction; the algorithm will then scale the resume and provide recommendations; such as necessary courses to take, abilities to add on a resume, or fields to include on a CV to boost its position. The rankings that will be generated will further be stored into the databases for the recruiters to see and understand which candidate is the best fit or worthy for that specific position. The Resume Analyzer uses NLP to accelerate review by fetching relevant information, discovering the area candidate is an expert in, and providing suggestions on career paths along with learning resources like YouTube videos for skill development. Further, using this tool will help job seekers in their career planning and assist recruiters in comparing candidates.

title: Comparative Analysis of Supervised Machine Learning Anomaly Classification on Active Power in Smart Grids.
authors: Mustasheen Mahi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The rapid adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and smart meters has increased the complexity of modern power grids, exposing them to diverse cyber-physical threats. With higher interconnectivity, smart grids are vulnerable to attacks that disrupt energy flow and compromise system reliability. This study introduces a framework for active power anomaly classification, identifying physical layer anomalies in smart grids. The continuous attribute, active power, was discretized into two categories-normal and anomalous-using quantile-based binning to ensure proper class balance. The framework evaluates supervised machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Linear Support Vector Classifier (LinearSVC), Random Forest, Gradient Boosting (GB), and AdaBoost. Experimental validation employed the Physical Attack subset of the IEEE Smart Grid Cyber-Physical Attack Dataset, with active power as the target feature. Results indicate that ensemble methods, Gradient Boosting and AdaBoost, achieved the highest classification accuracy of 97 %, demonstrating their effectiveness in classifying anomalies from active power variations. Feature permutation importance analysis via Random Forest further highlights active power as a dominant indicator for anomaly classification. While real-world deployment may encounter data variability and unseen attack scenarios, this study establishes a baseline for physical-layer anomaly classification and offers insights for extending the framework to broader cyber-physical domains.

title: Agribiotrace: An AI-Powered Monitoring and Risk Assessment System for Biodegradable Agricultural Films
authors: Akash Roshan S, Ebin A, Harish S
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Biodegradable mulch films (BDMs) are increasingly used in sustainable agriculture, but their adoption is limited by unpredictable degradation and associated environmental concerns. Existing monitoring methods, such as visual inspection and laboratory testing, are often slow and inadequate for realtime decision-making. This paper introduces AgriBioTrace, an AI-driven system designed for continuous monitoring and risk assessment of BDMs. The system generates physics-informed synthetic datasets to capture temperature, moisture, pH, UV exposure, and soil effects on degradation. Using these datasets, machine learning models are trained to predict degradation behavior and classify potential environmental risks. AgriBioTrace provides actionable insights through user-friendly web and mobile platforms, supporting farmers and stakeholders in proactive decision-making. By integrating domain knowledge with data-driven methods, this work advances practical tools for sustainable agricultural management.

title: The Algorithmic Compass: Navigating Human-Centric Adoption in Future Healthcare Systems
authors: Ashish Kumar Kalvakota, Dilip Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: While healthcare institutions are increasingly implementing Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems, adoption rates are far lower than desired. This paper proposes a unique framework, “The Algorithmic Compass,” which systematically addresses the human-centric factors influencing AI adoption. We explore, examine, and discuss the primary challenges to AI adoption, particularly the role of micro-cultural resistance. The framework consists of four core components: user experience, ethical alignment, clinical validation, and organizational governance. The Algorithmic Compass leverages AI-driven pattern analysis to detect resistance patterns, communication barriers, and workflow disruptions that impede the adoption of new technologies. Through feasibility studies conducted at selected medical facilities, we demonstrate how this framework can be used to recalibrate adoption strategies and address role-specific resistance with targeted micro-interventions. By incorporating human-in-the-loop validation, the framework ensures clinical safety and emphasises collaboration between humans and AI, rather than replacement. Our research concludes that successful AI adoption depends on a balanced focus on both cultural and technological readiness, with early interventions being crucial for long-term success.

title: An Agricultural Machine Learning Framework for Ensemble Regressor-Based Crop Price Prediction
authors: Rudrakshi Bhandekar, Yash Khadgi, Vedant Nawghare, Priya Dasarwar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The Indian economy is based on agriculture, which employs more than half of the working people and adds a lot to the country's GDP. However, the industry is very sensitive to changes like crop prices, which is a big risk for the producers but also makes markets very inefficient. Regular statistical analyses are also likely to miss the subtlety of nature and the way that price movement drivers depend on each other. This article avoids these problems by using cutting-edge machine learning models, like Decision Tree Regression and boosted Stacking Regressor architecture, to successfully predict crop prices. The algorithm takes into account past crop prices, weather conditions, soil quality, and records of past market demand from government and farm databases. The Stacking Regressor that was suggested uses XGBoost, Random Forest Regressor, and Ridge Regression as base learners and XGBoost as a meta-learner. The ensemble model can get a very high and stable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> metric value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 3 6 \%}$</tex> and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of just 0.18, which is very strong and can be used in real life. This research finding signifies a transformative shift in agricultural economics, enabling policymakers, farmers, and market experts to make informed decisions based on data. Future advancements in this project may incorporate real-time data streams, deep learning models, and time-series forecasting to enhance model accuracy and accessibility across diverse agricultural climates.

title: Synchronous Reluctance Motor Design and Optimization with Asymmetric Permanent Magnet Based on Magnetic Axis Offset Theory
authors: Longjun Zhai, Juntao Yu, Jiali Cui, Li Wang, Yuanziman Zhang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To improve the torque component utilization rate in traditional symmetric permanent magnet-assisted synchronous reluctance motors (PMASynRMs), we design an asymmetric rotor topology based on the magnetic axis offset theory, establish a mathematical model of torque, and close the current phase angles with the related torque components peak values by using asymmetric permanent magnets and flux barriers. Furthermore, we proposed an optimization method with nested multi-objectives. This can enhance the average torque and lower the torque ripple. It shows that the designed asymmetric PMASynRM, in comparison with the traditional structure, achieves an increase of 2.13% in average torque, a reduction of 32.7% in torque ripple, and an improvement in permanent magnet torque utilization rate from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 4 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 1 4 \%}$</tex> with a reduced amount of permanent magnets.

title: Daylight and Lighting Optimization in Passive House Design: Case Study of a 3D-Printed Building in Cold Climate
authors: Chaima Jetlaoui, Dahai Qi, Zouhour Araoud
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: This paper presents a practical workflow to integrate daylight and electric-lighting optimization into Passive House design. The approach links facade orientation, window-to-wall ratio, glazing selection, and exterior shading with quantitative lighting targets and whole-building primary energy accounting. Using a cold-climate multifamily case study in Amos (Canada), the method combines daylight autonomy and glare assessments with PHPP energy balance and specifies LED systems with daylight-linked dimming and occupancy control. Results indicate that a south-dominant glazing strategy—paired with moderated east and west exposures, selective glazing with balanced visible transmittance and solar factor, and exterior shading—reduces lighting electricity use and summer peaks while preserving winter solar gains. The proposed workflow is repeatable from early design through verification and shows that lighting, often treated as a secondary fit-out, is a primary lever for meeting Passive House primary-energy limits without compromising visual comfort.

title:
A First-Principles Study on the Synergistic Effect and Dielectric Strength Prediction in SF
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    -Based Binary Gas Mixtures
authors: Guobin Li, Zishu Hu, Ju Zhao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the dual challenges of preventing lowtemperature liquefaction and maintaining high dielectric strength in gas-insulated systems under extreme environmental conditions, this study develops a predictive model for the dielectric breakdown strength of SF6-based binary gas mixtures based on first-principles (DFT) calculations and microscopic molecular properties. First, key molecular parameters‐such as orbital energies, ionization energies, and polarizability—were extracted for SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> and common buffer gases(N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, CF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>, CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>). These parameters were used to construct a multivariate regression model for predicting the dielectric strength of pure gases. Subsequently, the intermolecular interaction energy between SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> and each buffer gas was quantified. A synergy coefficient N was introduced to characterize the discharge-enhancing interaction between components in gas mixtures. This coefficient was incorporated into a modified weighted model to predict the dielectric strength of SF6 binary mixtures. The results indicate that SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>/N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> achieves the best performance among the studied systems, retaining over 90% of pure SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>'s dielectric strength, while SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>/CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>/C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> also exhibit promising characteristics. The model highlights the role of microscopic molecular properties in governing macroscopic insulation behavior, offering guidance for designing eco-friendly alternatives to SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> in high-voltage applications.

title: Solar-Powered IoT-Based Smart Farming Model (SPISFM)
authors: Md. Mehedi Hasan Naeem, Kamrunnahar Ruma, Shakila Sultana, Nafiza Anjum, Moumita Barua, Md. Ashraful Islam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The Solar-Powered IoT-Based Smart Farming Model (SPISFM) represents an innovative solution to key agricultural inefficiencies, including excessive water consumption, high operational energy costs, and insufficient crop heath assessment by utilizing sustainable solar energy and advanced IoT enabled sensor system thus promoting green environment. The system incorporates distributed photovoltaic power generation, smart irrigation automation, and mobile-based real-time management for data-centric agricultural decision-making. The system employs soil moisture sensors and microcontroller-operated irrigation valves to deliver water precisely, when necessary, complemented by CCTV imaging with embedded machine learning. Through net metering, surplus photovoltaic output exported to the national grid, ensuring zero dependence on conventional fossil energy and enabling CO2-free irrigation. The SPISFM represents a sustainable, adaptable, and resource-efficient strategy aligned to the demands of modern farming.

title: Development of AI Powered Object Detection Voice Assistant
authors: Raji C, Harshith K, Veeresh, Sumukh Hegde, Vinod
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Object detection is computer vision technology that empowers the computer to identify and point out objects in images or videos, based on application, typically using deep learning for detecting and recognizing complex patterns. This project combines You Only Look Once Version 8 (YOLOv8), being the real-time object detection algorithm, and pairs the output with the Python Text to Speech (pyttsx3) for the audio feedback to create an AI-powered object-tracking voice assistant. Cameras strategically placed in locations like workspaces or closets continuously track items such as keys, books, or devices, and the data is sent to a central database. Users can interact with the system through voice commands to locate items, receiving precise audio responses. It integrates real-time object detection, centralised tracking and voice-based interaction in a manner that would give users an effective means of tracing the location of lost belongings at ease.

title: Data Resilience in Cyber-Physical Systems: Overcoming MQTT Data Loss for Reliable AI Subsystems
authors: Hebert de Oliveira Silva, Jessica Franzon Cruz do Espirito Santo, Rodrigo Vieira Dos Santos
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: This work introduces a resilient architecture for Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), designed to ensure reliable and uninterrupted data flows required by artificial intelligence subsystems for decision-making and anomaly detection. The architecture integrates physical edge components at SENAI São Caetano do Sul with virtualized services at SENAI Vila Mariana, combining redundant connectivity, secure VPN tunnels, local buffering, and synchronized storage replication. A federated learning layer and monitoring tools (Wazuh and Zabbix) reinforce both operational resilience and security. The proposed design specifically addresses the challenge of data loss in MQTT-based communications, a critical weakness in industrial IoT environments. By incorporating real-time fault detection, message loss mitigation, and edge-to-core backfill mechanisms, the architecture preserves the integrity and availability of telemetry streams even under connectivity failures or broker disruptions. Validation through an IoT case study under controlled transmission failure scenarios demonstrated that the framework sustained data completeness and continuity required for AI subsystems. These findings confirm that resilience can be achieved without compromising performance, bridging the gap between conceptual frameworks and real-world CPS deployments.

title: Preface
authors: N/A

title: Simulation of Heatwave Event using WRF Model across Uttar Pradesh, India
authors: Neha Kumari, Neena Singh, Bhanumati Panda, Vivek Singh, Neetak Kumar, Nitin Lohan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Numerical weather prediction (NWP) models are being used to predict and minimise the effects of the increasing number of extreme weather events occurring globally. Applying NWP models necessitates choosing the right physics parametrisation choices in order to closely describe atmospheric processes. The simulations of this work in to determine episodic Heat Wave (HW) events from 20<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-24<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> May 2023 over Uttar Pradesh (UP) using Weather Research and Forecasting (WRF) model, India with varying horizontal resolutions of 12 km for the whole of India (d01), and 4 km for the UP (d02) domain. In this study, the maximum temperature (Tmax) is analysed using WRF model during HW occurrences and this result is validated with fifth generation of the European reanalysis (ERA5). The experiment produces the temperature on this scale on the desired domain using Yonsei University Schemes (Planetary Boundary layer). The model shows uncertainty in the Tmax for the regions, indicating that complicated orography and location affect regional temperature. Furthermore, the characteristics associated to HWs like Tmax, relative humidity (RH) distribution is analysed to comprehend the dynamics of increasing HW intensity. Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and Correlation Coefficient (CC) for Tmax and relative humidity analysis are acquired in order to verify the findings with existing literature.

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)

title: Insights into Quinolone Resistance in Salmonella Typhi: Docking Analysis of Wild-Type and Mutant DNA Topoisomerase IV Subunit A
authors: Augustine S. Samorlu, Dharmsheel Shrivastav, Ved Vrat Verma, Shilpy Singh, Chetan Kumar Sharma, Varun Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The bacteria Salmonella enterica serovar Typhi (S. Typhi) cause a disease known as typhoid fever which is a major public health issue in various developing countries. The rise of antimicrobial-resistant strain, specifically mutation in DNA topoisomerase IV shows a major challenge to the health sector when treated with ciprofloxacin (quinolone antibiotics). This study aimed to investigate the interaction between topoisomerase IV A subunit and ciprofloxacin (wild-type and mutant; Ser80 → Ile) of S. Typhi. The binding affinity of topoisomerase (wild type) and mutant type are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 6. 5}$</tex> kilocalorie per mol and -6.0 kilocalorie per mol respectively. The difference is statistically significant but the changes did not affects ciprofloxacin binding capacity. For interaction analysis, we observed that Asp111, Gln90, and Arg87 residue actively contribute stabilization by Vanderwaal and hydrophobic forces and helps appropriate ligand orientation. In conclusion, mutation can contribute in partial resistance for ciprofloxacin treatment in Typhoid fever. Further research is needed for validation with the aid of MD simulation.

title: Cyber-Flexible Security Services for Resilient Operation of Renewable-Rich Power Systems
authors: Saman Nikkhah, Abbas Rabiee, Adib Allahham, Arman Alahyari, Shady Gadoue, Innocent Kamwa, Damian Giaouris
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: The digitalisation of power systems introduces substantial vulnerabilities, particularly concerning system stability and security, due to the growing risk of malicious cyber attacks. These threats, often enabled by the proliferation of Internet of Things assets, can target critical infrastructure by manipulating system load or altering renewable power generation, both of which directly affect the system security margin. This paper proposes a novel cyber-resilient optimisation strategy (CROS) framework to enhance cyber-resilience in power systems, particularly under conditions of high renewable power penetration. The framework explicitly accounts for the interplay between cyber and physical components and addresses how adversarial interventions can impact load and renewable generation inputs. Central to this approach is a cyber-conscious strategy (CCS) which intends to increase the cyber-resilience radius in the face of unknown cyber attacks without any information about the intention and characteristics of attacks. By embedding the cyber-flexible security service (CFSS), the framework supports real-time corrective actions, allowing operators to adapt flexibly to evolving cyber-threat scenarios. The CFSS defines a dynamic security margin, reflecting the system's ability to maintain system security under unknown cyber threats. The proposed multi-stage, bi-level co-optimisation model integrates a cost-risk analysis to guide techno-economic decision-making, empowering operators with a proactive and adaptive operational strategy to mitigate the impact of cyber incidents on modern, renewables-rich power systems.

title: Edge AI: Evaluating Dynamic-Pruned CNNs and Transfer-Learning for Transformers
authors: Anouar Chahbouni, Khaoula EL Manaa, Rachid EL Alami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) has shown strong potential across diverse applications, but bringing it to edge and other resource-constrained devices remains challenging. In this paper we compare two compact strategies: (i) Dynamic Attention-Guided Pruning (DAGP) to make CNNs small and fast, and (ii) Weighted Multi-Feature Extraction with Layer-Wise Training (WMFE-LWT), our best performing dynamic attention-based transfer-learning variant for Transformer backbones. We evaluate on PlantVillage (benchmark) and PlantDoc (real-world) datasets. For the CNN category, we test VGG16 and a custom CNN trained with DAGP, and include a non-optimized compact EfficientNet-B0 baseline. For the transformer track, we evaluate the Swin-Transformer Small (STS) with WMFELWT. Results show DAGP achieves a stronger accuracy-efficiency balance, yielding 99.3 % parameter reduction and models up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \times$</tex> smaller than the WMFE-LWT counterparts, while attaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+1.72 \%$</tex> (PlantVillage) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+0.26 \%$</tex> (PlantDoc) higher test accuracy. Although WMFE-LWT is less accurate in our setting, its 2.14 MB footprint remains attractive for training efficiency to leverage transformer generalization capability, representing a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95 \times$</tex> size reduction versus training Swin from scratch. On-device experiments on a Raspberry Pi 4 further highlight DAGP' s practicality, reaching 60 ms per inference <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3380 \times$</tex> faster than WMFE-LWT under identical conditions. Overall, the study clarifies the trade-offs between pruningbased CNNs (inference efficiency) and attention-guided Transformer transfer (training efficiency and generalization), offering practical guidance for compact, high-performance edge AI deployments.

title: Efficient 3D Modeling and Semantic Segmentation of Oil Palm Trees Based on PointNet
authors: Chuangang Zhao, Mohd Faisal Ibrahim, Rizauddin Ramli, Mohd Hairi Mohd Zaman, Seyed Abdollah Vaghefi
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: This paper presents an efficient 3D modeling and segmentation method for oil palm trees. A baseline model was constructed using RGB-D data to classify and segment the main structural components of oil palm trees, including trunks, crowns, and fruit bunches. Trained with the PointNet architecture, the model achieved a classification accuracy of 93.4% on the test set. The experimental results demonstrate that this PointNet-based deep learning approach can effectively represent the three-dimensional structure of oil palm trees and provide reliable technical support for subsequent research on intelligent harvesting systems.

title: Optimizing Support Vector Machines With Particle Swarm Optimization for Heart Disease Prediction
authors: Khalidou Abdoulaye Barry, Mohamed El Far
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Heart disease is the leading cause of mortality worldwide, and the number of deaths attributed to it continues to rise. This study aimed to optimize various machine learning techniques to classify patients based on their risk of developing heart disease. A machine learning model named PSO-SVM was developed, combining Support Vector Machines (SVM) with Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), where PSO served as an optimizer by tuning certain SVM hyperparameters. Experiments were conducted on the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset, and performance was evaluated using several metrics, including accuracy, F1-score, AUC-ROC, recall, and precision, all demonstrating satisfactory results. Integrating particle swarm optimization allows for a more efficient exploration of the SVM hyperparameter space compared to traditional grid search methods, thus reducing computational cost while maintaining strong performance. This contribution aims to provide a diagnostic support tool to assist cardiologists in the early identification of heart disease. Future work will explore various PSO algorithm variants combined with SVM for heart disease classification, a relatively underexplored area.

title: Heuristic-Inspired Percentage Attribution Index: A Novel Measure for Explainability in Skin Cancer Classification
authors: Inzamam Mashood Nasir, Sara Tehsin, Robertas Damaševičius, Nazar Hussain, Muhammad Abdullah Bilal, Rytis Maskeliūnas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Deep learning models are quite good at classifying skin cancer, but their decision-making processes are still not clear, which makes it hard for doctors to trust and use them. Explainable AI (XAI) methods, like attention maps, give visual hints, but they don't have standardized, measurable, and human-readable ways to measure how well they work. This work intro- duces a heuristic-inspired Percentage Attribution Index (PAI), an innovative measure of explainability that quantifes attention quality on a normalized scale from [0, 100]. PAI uses heuristics like compactness, center-bias, semantic-overlap, and contrast to make sure that model explanations ft with how people see things and with structures that are specifc to lesions. When tested on the HAM10000, ISIC 2019, and ISIC 2020 datasets, PAI improved classifcation accuracy by upto 3.8% and F1-score by upto <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 5 \%}$</tex>. It also gave <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 - 1 8 \%}$</tex> higher interpretability scores than Grad-CAM and SHAP. In addition to evaluation, PAI can be used as a regularizer in training to improve both performance and interpretability. The results help make dermatological AI systems more explainable in a way that is clinically useful, robust, and standardized.

title: Growing Resilient Agriculture through Sustainable Systems and Root Optimization for Transformative Solutions
authors: Elsa Andres, Giovanni Giannotta, Alina Ignatescu, Georg Niedrist, Abraham Mejia-Aguilar
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Climate change increasingly threatens Alpine grasslands and forests, demanding new approaches to understand and enhance plant resilience. Our study applies network science to identify the trait interactions that sustain ecosystem stability under variable temperature conditions. Using our own dataset from eight meadows across South Tyrol, combined with an altitudinal experiment simulating climate warming, we map Plant Trait Networks (PTNs) linking plant traits to environmental variables. Network metrics reveal the different characteristics of the structural organization of the grasslands. This data-driven framework bridges ecological observation with predictive modelling, offering a scalable tool to anticipate shifts in grassland productivity and guide species selection for climate-resilient agriculture.

title: Multi-Objective Evolutionary Algorithms for Human-Robot Collaborative Scheduling in Energy-Efficient Welding Shop
authors: Amel Haddaji, Houssem Eddine Nouri, Olfa Belkahla Driss
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: The Human-Robot Collaboration approach is indeed a pillar of modern manufacturing, integrating systems where humans and robots cooperate intelligently to optimize production processes. This paper presents a human-robot collaborative welding shop scheduling problem (HCWSSP), aiming to minimize makespan (Cmax) and total energy consumption (TEC). This study analyzes cooperation among operators (skilled welders, ordinary welders and robots) by comparing three well-known multi-objective evolutionary algorithms, such as NSGA-II, SPEA-2 and MOEA/D, on a randomly generated benchmark in order to identify which algorithm delivers the best performance for the HCWSSP problem. We evaluate performance using the hypervolume (HV) and generational distance (GD) metrics, then compare and analyze the results with respect to the Pareto front, HV, and GD. The benchmark data instances are organized and available at the following link: https://github.com/amelhaddajiha/HCWSSP.

title: Enhancing Cybersecurity with Federated Learning: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Marwa Agili, Bayrem Triki, Farah Jemili, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: With the exponential growth of cyber threats in modern networked environments, traditional centralized Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are increasingly inadequate, facing challenges in data privacy, scalability, and efficiency. Federated Learning (FL) offers a promising decentralized solution, enabling collaborative model training without the need for direct access to sensitive data. This work introduces a novel hybrid deep learning architecture that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), and Attention mechanisms, trained using federated learning techniques, including FedAvg, FedAdam, FedYogi, and FedProx. By leveraging these FL algorithms, the proposed model enhances the security and efficiency of intrusion detection systems across distributed environments. The approach is evaluated using the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset. The results show significant improvements in key performance metrics, such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score, while ensuring data privacy. This federated learning-based model offers a robust and scalable solution for improving cybersecurity, presenting a strong defense against evolving cyber threats in decentralized networked environments.

title: Study and Enhancement of MFA Security Through Predictive Phishing Detection in Cloud Environments
authors: Fatma Siala, Ghada Akacha
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Many companies rely on cloud services such as Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, AWS, or Azure because storing and analyzing all emails and requests on their own servers is challenging. In this context, implementing additional security layers, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA), is crucial to enhance data protection. However, this measure alone is not sufficient to ensure optimal security. We propose adding an extra security layer by integrating artificial intelligence.

title: Energy-Efficient and Sustainable Approaches to AS/RS Design and Control: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Omar Bahmid, Sihem Kouloughli, Amaria Bendiouis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In the pursuit of sustainability, controlling energy consumption to prevent wasteful usage has become a critical goal across industries. Within this context, improving energy efficiency represents a key focus in Automated Storage and Retrieval Systems (AS/RS). This comprehensive review examines recent advancements in AS/RS design, control, and operation, with particular emphasis on energy optimization and sustainable practices. The analysis encompasses system configurations, scheduling methodologies, control algorithms, and simulation-based evaluations to provide a holistic understanding of current approaches. This study synthesizes existing research to identify emerging trends, reveal methodological preferences, and establish directions for future investigations in energy-efficient AS/RS development.

title: Determination of the Minimum Capacitance for Self-Excitation of a Seig, Taking Into Account the Speed Ramp-Up
authors: Madjid Sibrahim, Rabah Rouas, Rahma Kachenoura, Salah Haddad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: The calculation of the minimum capacitance necessary to achieve self-excitation in a self-excited induction generator (SEIG) has been extensively investigated in prior research. Findings indicate that this minimum capacitance is primarily influenced by two parameters: the generator's rotational speed and its residual magnetism. The research presented in this paper demonstrates that, beyond the two previously identified factors, a third parameter the acceleration, or more precisely, the ramp-up of the rotational speed also plays a significant role in determining the minimum capacitance required for self-excitation. More generally, the self-excitation phenomenon itself is governed by four factors: the capacitor bank, the remanent magnetism, the rotational speed, and the rate of speed increase. In this study, multiple experimental self-excitation tests were performed for various ramp-up rates of the rotational speed, resulting in new insights into the behavior of the minimum excitation capacitance as a function of the SEIG's rotational speed. Using a simplified equivalent circuit model of the SEIG, a direct analytical relationship was established between the minimum excitation capacitance and the rotational speed. This relationship made it possible to determine the theoretical curve of the minimum excitation capacitance as a function of the generator's speed, which was then compared with experimental results. Furthermore, a MATLAB/Simulink model was developed to study the influence of rotational speed, excitation capacitance, and residual flux on the onset of self-excitation.

title: Review of Smart Systems in Aquaculture and Aquaponics
authors: Elarbi Kacemi, Khelifa Benahmed, Tariq Benahmed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this paper, we present a set of works between 2019-2025 in the field of aquaculture and smart aquaculture systems, which witnessed remarkable developments by integrating modern technologies such as artificial intelligence, Internet of Things and cloud computing. The works were based on a set of standards to monitor and automatically control environmental changes in the system and maintain its stability, and the works presented a set of technologies in the field of tracking the growth of crops including fish and plants and enhancing the efficiency of resource management to reduce waste. In addition to works that included proactive prediction of environmental variables, decision support systems for failure analysis, and automated feeder systems, with the aim of developing smart systems that address new challenges and solve previous shortcomings observed in the field.

title: Performance Assessment of ANN and MLR Models for Predicting NF/RO Desalination System
authors: Asma Adda, Salah Bezari, Salah Hanini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This study develops and compares artificial neural network (ANN) and multiple linear regression (MLR) models to predict the permeate flow rate of a small-scale NF/RO desalination pilot plant. The experimental dataset includes measurements of time, feed pressure, feed temperature, feed flow rate, feed conductivity and permeate conductivity; the target variable is permeate flow rate. Data were preprocessed (outlier screening and normalization) and split into training (80%), validation (10%) and test (10%) subsets. A feed-forward ANN with a (6–17–1) architecture was trained using the BFGS quasi-Newton algorithm (trainbfg); an MLR model was used as a baseline. Models were evaluated using the correlation coefficient (R), root mean square error (RMSE) and mean absolute error (MAE). The ANN model substantially outper-formed the MLR baseline showing higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> and markedly lower RMSE and MAE. These results demonstrate that the ANN provides reliable and accurate predictions of permeate flow rate and is suitable to support monitoring and predictive control of NF/RO systems.

title: Evaluation of the Effect of Synthetic Oversampling on Deep Neural Networks for Skin Lesion Analysis
authors: Yamina Bordjiba, Ali Seridi, Randa Salmi, Nabiha Azizi, Hayet Farida Merouani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Automated analysis of skin lesions using deep learning is a major challenge for the early diagnosis of melanoma and other skin cancers. However, the performance of deep neural networks remains severely limited by the class imbalance present in most medical databases, where benign cases greatly outnumber malignant lesions. This imbalance often leads to biased models, characterised by insufficient sensitivity for rare classes, particularly melanoma. The present study assesses the influence of applying the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) on enhancing the robustness and sensitivity of deep convolutional neural networks in automated skin lesion classification. The study is based on the HAM10000 dataset, which includes seven categories of dermoscopic lesions. After a pre-processing and data balancing phase using SMOTE, a pre-trained ResNet50 model was adjusted for the multi-class classification task. Performance before and after balancing was compared using several metrics, including accuracy, sensitivity and confusion matrix.

title: Design of an Intelligent Greenhouse Temperature Control System Leveraging IoT and MQTT Protocol
authors: Camara M'boirick, Nawfal Hidra, Said Doubabi, Naji Abdenouri
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: The climatic conditions in Marrakech greenhouse farms are very restrictive; in summer, the temperature exceeds 45° C, while in winter it approaches 3° C. These maximum and minimum temperatures affect the growth of vegetables and tomatoes within the acceptable temperature range; therefore, the need for intelligent regulation of the environment becomes inevitable. This paper reports a greenhouse temperature control system that uses Internet of Things (IoT) technology and the Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) protocol. The thermal behavior of the heating resistor and exhaust fan was identified using the MATLAB System Identification Toolbox, and Predictive Functional Control (PFC) was applied for regulation. Experimental results show that PFC stabilized the greenhouse temperature: the heating resistor allowed the temperature to reach a steady state after about 15 minutes, while the exhaust fan exhibited a 2 % overshoot with a response time of 20 minutes. The hybrid supervision architecture applied for regulation, based on local control in LabVIEW and remote monitoring in the cloud, guaranteed reliable system operation. Finally, the system was able to maintain the greenhouse microclimate within the optimal ranges required for crop growth under Marrakech's extreme climate.

title: Integrating Smart Contracts and Forecasting Models for Sustainable Energy Grids in India
authors: A.S. Kannan, E. Baraneetharan, R. Venkatasubramanian, S. Sasi, S. Jeyanthi, B. Elamurugan
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: India's ambitious renewable energy targets of 500 GW by 2030 and net-zero emissions by 2070 necessitate transformative energy trading solutions capable of harnessing distributed renewable sources. This paper introduces a blockchain-enabled peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading platform designed for India's diverse energy landscape, which includes rooftop solar, wind plants, and microgrids in both urban and rural areas. Built on the Ethereum foundation, the platform employs smart contracts to automate energy transactions between prosumers, reducing dependence on the conventional grid and advancing India's energy security goals. The system integrates machine learning algorithms trained on specific Indian usage patterns and weather conditions to forecast optimal trading times, accounting for seasonal changes, festivals, and industrial demand cycles. Key model assumptions include: (i) prosumers have bidirectional smart meters with IoT connectivity; (ii) weather data availability from Indian Meteorological Department stations; (iii) baseline electricity tariffs following state-level regulatory frameworks; and (iv) participants operate within Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission's P2P trading guidelines. Core parameters include LSTM networks with 50 hidden units, learning rate of 0.001, and 24-hour prediction horizons; Random Forest models with 100 estimators and maximum depth of 10; smart contract gas limits of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3,000,000$</tex> units; and dynamic pricing coefficients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.15$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.08$</tex> calibrated against Tamil Nadu industrial tariffs. Through automated transactions, the platform allows small-scale generators to sell surplus energy directly to local consumers, mitigating the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18-20 \%$</tex> distribution losses typical of the Indian grid. Pilot studies in Tamil Nadu and Maharashtra showcased significant results, including a 35-45% cost saving for participating industries and transparent carbon credit accounting, aligning with emerging ESG compliance needs. The platform contributes to the Digital India initiative by fostering a decentralized energy infrastructure that supports both economic development and environmental sustainability.

title: Analysis of CNN for Deepfake Detection on Images Generated by Latest AI Tools
authors: Madhuri A. Potey, Yasmin S. Khan, Atharv Gupta, Aryan Gupta, Atul Ranjan, Amruta Wani
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: In the past decade, deepfakes simply consisted of photoshopped images, which were quite easily distinguishable by the human eye. The current deepfake technology, fueled by AI is blurring the line between real and fake media. This raises serious concerns about misinformation, identity fraud, and political manipulation as many people, including technical experts are unable to identify real content from fake. As newer and better tools are being developed every day, there is a need to tackle the growing nuisance of deepfakes. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are a powerful solution, capable of identifying subtle inconsistencies in deepfake images and videos, that escape human eye. However, even these models are struggling to keep up with the rapid evolution of deepfake generation tools. This paper examines a CNN-based detection approach, highlighting its effectiveness on images generated in the previous year. It further evaluates the accuracy of deepfake detection on images generated by the state-of-the-art models like Grok3, ChatGPT, and Perchance AI to see which tool is the most powerful in evading detection.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)

title: Mathematical Modelling of Thunderbolt Lightning for Analysis of its Voltage Characteristics
authors: Bijendra Singh Verma, Nidhi Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Lightning is an extremely high voltage, high current natural phenomenon having complex behaviour. Understanding the rise up as well as discharge of voltage during a thunderbolt lightning is vital for meteorological studies, design of electric transmission systems and for looking into the possibilities of harvesting electrical energy from thunderbolt lightning. A novel equation for mathematical modelling of lightning voltage is proposed in this paper. Apart from time and maximum possible voltage due to charge in the clouds, this model is based on different parameters like the rate at which the voltage develops, the curvature of the voltage, the time over which voltage decays and the slope of the decay. Numerical calculations tabulated in tables 1 and 2 using different values of the parameters and their corresponding graphs illustrate the effects of variation in the voltage waveforms, revealing the flexibility of the model in reproducing different types of lightning strikes. The results obtained with the proposed mathematical model equation are compared with results available from other mathematical models. The proposed results provide a more descriptive mathematical modelling of lightning voltage.

title: Design and Analysis of Optimal Tilt Angles for Solar Panels to Maximise Output
authors: Nanda Kumar Enjeti, Harshitha Kummara, Sai Lakshmi Y, Poojitha Konde, Tejasree M, Venkatesh Reddy Birudala
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Angle at which solar panels are tilted has direct effect on incoming solar irradiance the panel surface gets. This tilt relationship has direct influence on how much electricity a solar photovoltaic (PV) system generates. To get the most energy out of solar panels, it it important to choose the right tilt angle. This is especially important in areas where the sun's position changes with seasons. This paper studies MATLABbased modeling to look into how to find the best tilt angles for solar panels and how those angles affect the amount of energy they produce. Solar geometry equations are used to figure out how the sun will be at different times of year, and plane-of-array irradiance is calculated for a number of different tilt settings. MATLAB looks at three different tilt strategies when figuring out how much photovoltaic energy is made: a constant annual tilt, a semi-annual tilt adjustment, and a monthly tilt adjustment. Every year, we figure out how much energy each method produces and how well it works. The results show that the different ways of changing the tilt produce different amounts of energy. They also shows that optimizing tilt consistently increases output. This paper compares the annual, semi-annual and monthly tilt angles. The purpose of this analysis is to choose the best tilt angles for small-scale and home solar panel installations that don't have solar tracking devices.

title: Brainnet - An Automated Brain Hemorrhage Classification and Segmentation Using Dualhead-Unet
authors: Bhushan Bawankar, Kavya Patel, Kedar Poul, Aryan Gaikwad, Prutha Raut, Gaurav Bagde, Aarsh Nimje
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: ICH is a neurological emergency with a high morbidity and mortality rate in that the diagnosis must be accurate and timely in order to decrease the death rate as well as the likelihood of the development of disability in the long term. Assessing the occurrence or absence of an ICH, and its subtypes, based on brain computed tomography (CT) scans (i.e., evaluations) manually is both time-consuming and interobserver variable to any time-sensitive emergency scenario. Our proposed architecture, dual- head U- Net, which incorporates ResNet34 encoder, detects the presence of ICH in a CT of a brain and potentially the subtypes of ICH; a multi-slice contextual input consisting of 81 channels is used, where the channels are 9 CT slices, and preprocessing includes, but is not limited to, gamma correction, Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and multi-window intensity normalization. These preprocessing techniques help to improve contrast in tissues, rectify differences in illumination and imitate radiological windowing to better present features. The implemented approach offers an effective, efficient, and clinically relevant way of integrating an AI-based interface to assist in accuracy and aid in a timely diagnosis and decision-making in neuro emergency.

title: Impact of Electrode Gap and Go Coating on Porous Paper-Based Impedance Humidity Sensor Performance
authors: Shin Pyng Gan, Mastura Shafinaz Zainal Abidin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This study investigates the effect of interdigitated electrode (IDE) gap sizes and graphene oxide (GO) coating on the performance of porous paper-based impedance humidity sensors. IDE configurations with gap sizes of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.4 \text{mm}, 1 \text{mm}$</tex>, and 2 mm were fabricated using silver (Ag) inkjet printing on hydrochloric acid (HCl) treated paper substrates. To observe the device's sensitivity comparison, an additional double-layer GO coating was applied to selected samples (1 mm gap's IDE). Sensor performance was evaluated under controlled humidity conditions ranging from 20 to 90% RH. Among those tested IDE configurations, the 2 mm gap design demonstrated the highest sensitivity at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.1 \mathrm{k} \Omega / {\%} \text{RH}$</tex> but showed moderate stability from Day 3 onward. The 0.4 mm gap achieved a sensitivity of 6.3 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{k} \Omega / {\%} \text{RH}$</tex> within 30 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 {\%} \text{RH}$</tex> but experienced a decline in stability after Day 2. In contrast, the 1 mm gap's IDE configuration showed the most balanced performance, with a sensitivity of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 \mathrm{k} \Omega / {\%} \text{RH}$</tex> and stable operation lasting up to 4 days. Next, higher sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.5 \mathrm{k} \Omega / {\%} \text{RH}$</tex> was successfully found in GO-coated sensors compared to 1.8 k <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Omega / {\%}$</tex> RH in non-GO sensors. However, the stability of the sensor at this point was slightly reduced, with an impedance discrepancy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta Z$</tex>, exceeding 10% by Day 3. These findings highlight the trade-offs between sensitivity and long-term stability, emphasizing the need to tailor electrode design and surface treatment based on specific application requirements.

title: Charge Prediction in Indian Judiciary Using Bhartiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) Sections and Machine Learning
authors: Shweta Maurya, Shiraz Khurana
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The significant growth of AI is increasing in multiple domains, and AI has also been used currently for legal intelligence. Legal judgment prediction (LJP) has numerous tasks in which judges and lawyers can be greatly helped through AI. This paper illustrates the several Indian & international datasets and models that are formed for charge prediction. In this research, we suggested a framework for creating a dataset and multi-charge prediction model using BNS sections for the Indian Judiciary. In previous research, the sections of IPC (Indian Penal Code) have been adopted for charge prediction in legal judgments. In 2023, changes were made to the IPC sections, and they were modified to Bharatiya Naya Sanhita (BNS). In this paper, we integrate the BNS sections for criminal cases in the Indian judiciary.

title: Reduced Graphene Oxide-Based Gas Sensor Using Surface Acoustic Wave Devices
authors: Mohd Hafizuddin bin Junaidi, Mazlee bin Mazalan, Anas Mohd Noor, Mohd Fairus Ahmad, Muhammad Salman Al Farisi, Tien-Dung Do, Wan Safwani Wan Kamarul Zaman, Yufridin Wahab, Asrulnizam Abd Manaf
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Surface Acoustic Wave (SAW) devices are increasingly utilized in gas detection due to their wireless, passive nature, and ability to generate unique signals. However, challenges related to sensitivity, selectivity, and response time limit their effectiveness in detecting gases. This study presents the development of a SAW device for the detection of nitrogen oxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{NO}_{2})$</tex> using Reduced Graphene Oxide (rGO). The SAW device is fabricated using conventional photolithography, with rGO prepared from commercial nanoparticles and deposited via drop casting. Prior to testing with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> gas, the rGO is characterized by UV-Vis spectroscopy and Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM), A gas box from PLA is used to confine the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> gas on the rGO-SAW sensor and the s21 parameter is measured by Vector Network analyzer (VNA) to evaluate the frequency shift. The result demonstrates that the rGO-SAW sensor exhibited remarkable performance, with a notable frequency shift of 40 kHz observed at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85 \text{ppm} \text{NO}_{2}$</tex> concentration. These results demonstrate the potential of rGO-SAW sensors for reliable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> monitoring in environmental applications.

title: GULL: Graph-guided Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Intelligent Aerial Ferry Routing in Digital Twins
authors: Jianrui Fan, Lei Lei, Shengsuo Cai, Gaoqing Shen, Zhilin Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Operating Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in volatile, infrastructure-denied environments poses a fundamentally multi-scale challenge: a Ferry UAV must make long-horizon data-ferrying decisions while reacting in real time to terrain constraints, topology uncertainty, and physical disturbances. This paper presents Graph-guided UAV Layered Learning (GULL), a single-agent hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for intelligent aerial data ferrying in dynamic multi-UAV networks. Specifically, GULL combines graph-guided reasoning over dynamic network nodes with a layered decision architecture that decouples long-horizon strategic routing from short-horizon tactical motion control. To support such multi-scale decision making, GULL integrates self-attention-based aggregation of dynamic node features with a dual-resolution spatial representation encoded by convolutional modules, where Demand, Entropy, and self-supervised Cost maps jointly capture service urgency, topological uncertainty, and terrain traversability. Built upon this multi-modal representation, a hierarchical policy is further equipped with an Adaptive Temporal Abstraction (ATA) mechanism that dynamically adjusts high-level decision horizons to balance delivery objectives against kinematic and energy constraints. To improve policy generalization, GULL is trained in a Digital Twin augmented by a Large Language Model (LLM) that automatically generates diverse, high-fidelity terrains. Extensive experiments show that GULL improves the priority-weighted delivery ratio by up to 47% and reduces average end-to-end latency by 50% compared with representative baselines, while exhibiting strong scalability and robustness under severe traffic congestion, packet loss, wind disturbance, and complex terrain conditions.

title: Deep Learning Integrated Blockchain in Drug Supply Chain Management
authors: Shivam Shivam, Shikha Gupta, Aleem Ali
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Supply chain pharmaceuticals plays an important role in delivering original and safe medicines to the patients. However, these system experiences serious issues such as coun- terfeit drugs, poor stakeholder transparency, poor demand fore- casting and lack of trust in information disclosure. The most recent technologies like blockchain and deep learning would assist in addressing these problems. A drug- journeys is a secured and transparent method to record and trace all the steps of the drug production, patient, and deep learning process, which is augmented with functionality provides an intelligence that detects anomalies, predicts demand, and fake drugs. Combined the two technologies will create a powerful system that can be used to make the drug supply chain more transparent and secure and efficient. In this review, the current research in the area of blockchain and deep learning in supply chain management will be summarized, outlines its potential application, and pinpoints the existing challenges and gaps in the research. It also suggests the future directions such as federated learning, hybrid blockchain models and pilot-projects in the real world which can facilitate the creation of smarter, more reliable pharmaceutical supply chains.

title: AquaGardian: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Smart Water Monitoring
authors: Shubham, Shreya Pal, Sakshi Patel, Vaibhav Saraff, Ruchi Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Water quality is one among the major issues envied by the environmental issue of India. The availability of safe water is still a struggle for many people since the groundwater is regularly getting pollution by the industrial effluents and untreated sewerage, fertilizers used in agriculture that get seeped into the water table. Getting this attention, many people face serious diseases, and this will further increase many ailments such as hair diseases, skin diseases, and bone tumors, which are not curable. To cope with this problem, IoT based Water Monitoring System named IoT-WMS will be used. The proposed system will use combination set up with hardware such as Raspberry pi and pH, Temp and TDS sensor for conducting instant analysis of water quality. The system will be continuously observing the nature of water and sending all the information on a personalised dashboard. As soon as some sort of unusual parameters is detected, a notification will be immediately sent by this system, which ensures the early action by authorities and prevents people using such impure water. The proposed IoT-WMS will prove very effective system towards ensuring safe and pure water at individual and individual household level. To ensure storage of clean water, is a vision to a smarter home, office and city.

title: AI-Based Adaptive Scheduling for PV-EV Charging Systems Under Real-Time Grid Interaction
authors: Tejas Sharma, Rashi Goel, Radha Gupta, Shalu, Updesh Kumar Jaiswal, Akhilesh Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The global evolution towards Electric Vehicles (EVs) is to achieve sustainable transportation. However, periodically charging of EVs are needed, and this charging could be optimized if charging stations are powered by solar energy. Current prediction models and scheduling techniques are unable to work on non-linear and time-dependent relationship of solar charging of EVs that reduces energy utilization and efficiency. This paper targets to overcome existing limitation by design an intelligent Artificial Intelligence (AI) based Machine Learning (ML) model using LSTM for the optimization of EV charging stations powered by solar energy. Five ML models are compared Long Short -Term Memory (LSTM), Support Vector Regression (SVR), XGBoost, Linear Regression, and Random Forest for examining their efficiency in prediction of solar power generation for EVs. The results of simulation show that the suggested LSTM model enhanced solar generation forecasting precision. This research also provides an extensive and scalable approach towards uplifting the efficiency, dependability and eco-friendliness of EV charging system.

title: Development of E-Portal for Case Management Hearing and Facilitation of Legal Proceedings
authors: Aaditiya Tyagi, Akshat Jain, Akash Tiwari, Ankit Chaudhary, Anmol Jain
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: When designing an e-Portal for judicial case management and virtual hearings the researchers had a clear vision. A system that could serve as a one-stop-shop for different stakeholders in the legal system, including, but not limited to, judges, advocates, litigants, court staff and administrators. This portal would enable the online filing of cases and hearing through video conferencing, automate the scheduling, take care of document uploads, and send out real-time reminders to all parties.
