Coming from a tech perspective, the portal was built with the MERN stack, and given secure and limitless scalability in accordance with the level of access given to each user.

title: Real-Time Stock Market Trend Estimation Using Online Machine Learning and Predictive Modeling Techniques
authors: Saurabh Pratap Singh Rathore, Afsar Ali, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Rathnamala S, Hari Jyothula, Yogesh Choudhary
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Inventory management is effective when firm and sound strategies are adopted in dynamic and changing business environments to make sure that operations of the units are running smoothly and to reduce the financial wastage. The article is a mixed response to early detection and prevention of stock management systems, which are achieved via the implementation of sophisticated methodologies of technology and analysis. The predictive analytics, machine learning algorithms, and real-time monitoring systems are discussed such that they allow identifying potential issues of the stock before it deteriorates. The combination of the informationbased analysis with automatic warning systems into one system will also enable the companies to maintain the level of stock actively, reduce the rate of stockout, and simplify the work of the supply chain. The proposed system will contribute to the development of the improved decision-making processes and facilitate the continuous improvements of the inventory management practices. There are several examples of case studies in different industries that are claimed to demonstrate how this method can be exercised successfully, and the actual benefits were achieved due to early detection and preventive actions. Ultimately, this paper can be traced back to the evolutionary process of stock management system and it renders business with the required tools to compete and survive in the constantly increasing competitive world.

title: Simulation and Embedded Implementation of GPS Spoofing Detection and Defense for UAV Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Richa Tengshe, Padmini Ashwini, Nidhi Bhadoria, Raja Thejaswini N
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The increasing autonomy of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in both civilian and military applications necessitates robust cyber-security measures to protect their navigation and control systems. This work addresses the critical vulnerability of UAVs to Global Positioning System (GPS) spoofing attacks, whereby malicious actors broadcast counterfeit GPS signals to deceive the vehicle's navigation system, leading to catastrophic loss of control and mission failure. The proposed detection framework is a tightly-coupled sensor-fusion system that cross-validates Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) and inertial measurements using a Kalman filter: the filter produces a predicted state and the algorithm continuously monitors the residual (difference) between that prediction and incoming GNSS updates; a significant, sustained residual indicates a likely GNSS anomaly (for example, spoofing) and should trigger an automated failsafe (land or hold). Experimental results show that Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU)-assisted prediction substantially reduces false navigation deviations during simulated GNSS disruptions, demonstrating that the Kalman residual is an effective indicator for spoof detection and that the approach can be ported to an embedded companion computer for real-time defense in a full UAV deployment. The efficacy and low computational overhead of this framework are validated through comprehensive Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) simulations using the ArduPilot/PX4 flight stack. The primary contribution of this research is a practical, open-source, and hardware-implementable solution that enhances the cyber-physical integrity of small UAVs, providing a robust and accessible foundation for further academic and educational projects.

title: Simultaneous System Reconfiguration and Electric Vehicle Charging Scheduling for Loss Minimization of a Practical Radial Distribution Network
authors: Soumi Nag, Sampriti Roy
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are an important sustainable and cost-effective alternative for the future. However, inclusion of EVs in the distribution network demands for EV charging facilities. Optimal allocation of EV charging station is required to reduce range anxiety of the EV owner and to prevent the network overload. However, without coordinated charging of EVs, it is difficult to control the stress on the power system. In this work, a 33-bus practical distribution network along with linear and EV loads is considered. Simultaneous load scheduling and system reconfiguration is carried out using various optimization techniques with the objective to reduce the energy loss and charging cost without violating the system constraints. The result thus obtained are evaluated in terms of minimizations of energy loss, convergence behavior, computational efficiency and voltage limits. The outcomes obtained verifies the effectiveness and capability of the proposed approach, for any practical network, keeping constraints within limit.

title: PromptHub: Enhancing Generative AI Performance with the Structured Prompt Reuse
authors: Supriya Gupta Bani, Riju Bhattacharya, Deepak Suresh Asudani, Brijmohan Lal Sahu, Anand Tamrakar, Prasanna Palsodkar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: In present day generation, together with the increasing adaptation of AI fashions collectively with ChatGPT, MidJourney, and stable diffusion, clients often face traumatic situations in coming across and managing excessive satisfactory prompts that yield powerful outcomes. To cope with this trouble, we propose an AI spark off Sharing Platform-a webbased solution devised to ease the creation, storage, and exploration of AI turns on. The web platform features complete CRUD operations, tag-based are searching for, and customer authentication via Google, enabling a collaborative environment in which customers can contribute, refine, and reuse activates. Built using next.js, MongoDB, NextAuth, and styled with Tailwind CSS, the software offers a scalable, responsive interface with an ultra-modern Glass Morphism-inspired UI. Users can maintain private profiles to showcase them prompt contributions, promoting statistics sharing among AI lovers, creators, and researchers. It supports the AI interaction through the usage of offering a centralized, consumerwelcoming area for spark off discovery and manage. The tool is a complete-means online utility developed the usage of next.js for each front-end and again cease, MongoDB for database manage, and NextAuth for simplified person authentication with Google sign-in integrated. It provides elementary CRUD operations, allowing the consumer so as to create, view, update, and delete activities, together with surfing content material through a tag-dependent search device. each user has a dedicated profile page, displaying their posted content, fostering community engagement and knowledge move. The design is applied the usage of Tailwind CSS, embedding a state-of-the-art GlassMorphism layout and adaptable format for ensuring usability on each tool.

title: Enhancing ASR Performance in Noisy Environments Via Bridging Approach
authors: Rahul Mishra, Mayurakshi Mukherji, Munender Varshney, Senthil Raja, Kingshuk Banerjee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In this research work, we propose a method for noise-robust automatic speech recognition systems in the presence of non-stationary noise, specifically background speaker noise. This proposed technique will be extremely useful for any ASR system/application that needs to be accessed in an outdoor environment. Technically, we focus on the integration of speech enhancement (SE) and automatic speech recognition (ASR) to improve the performance of a pre-trained ASR model in noisy conditions. We employ a technique called observation addition (OA) where the original noisy signal is added to the SE version of itself using a weighting approach and then subsequently passed through the ASR model. We tested this approach using the Google FLEURS dataset. In this dataset, each speech sample is evaluated at five different SNR levels. Using an attention-based deep learning network, we estimate the SNR from the noisy samples. The SNR is then mapped to the best OA coefficient, which is then used for assigning weights to noisy and enhanced signal. Our proposed approach improves ASR performance across all SNR levels. At lower SNRs, it gives a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 1 \%}$</tex> absolute gain at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 ~ d B}$</tex> when using a fixed OA coefficient for all utterances from a general domain.

title: Comparative Assessment of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques for PM2.5 Prediction
authors: Shivangi Shrivastava, Arunima Jaiswal, Sriya Singh, Srishti Agrawal, Manasi Bhagat, Shruty Ahuja, Nitin Sachdeva
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In this era of industrialization, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> has become a major public health worry as it can enter deep into the respiratory and cardiovascular systems of human beings. The National Ambient Air Quality Standard (NAAQS) defines 40 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> as the official PM2.5 limit, but many air quality monitoring sites across Delhi often report <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$</tex> levels exceeding this limit. According to the recently introduced National Clean Air Programme, which aims to reduce pollution levels by 40 % by the end of 2026, the development of an accurate framework for air quality prediction became necessary. This study uses four years (2021-2024) of data from Anand Vihar, Delhi, obtained from the CPCB, to compare various ML, DL, and Ensemble Models for hourly PM2.5 forecasting. The results reveal that hybrid ensemble models consistently outperform individual models. Feature selection based on Mutual Information produced higher accuracy than Pearson Correlation. Among all the approaches, the hybrid RF+GB ensemble trained on Mutual Information selected features achieved the highest accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} =0.99$</tex>; RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=11.83 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>). This study shows the importance of integrating optimized feature selection with ensemble learning to create <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$</tex> prediction systems that are reliable and scalable.

title: Towards Efficient Industrial Inspection: An Unsupervised Anomaly Detection Approach Based on ConvNeXt-V2
authors: Muhammad Zimmad, Androw Ling Kian Quan, Fityanul Akhyar, Hui Fuang Ng, Vikneswary Jayapal, Faseeh Ullah, Atif Khan, Chih-Yang Lin
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The detection of anomalies in industrial products using images is crucial for ensuring product quality and maintaining a company's reputation. Traditional manual inspection is often inefficient, costly inaccurate and struggle to handle the increasing complexity of products and diversity of manufacturing defects. These limitations stem from the inherent deficiency of human inspection. Manual inspection leads to errors, health risks, and inconsistent labor efficiency. To address these challenges, this paper presents a simple method for automated and unsupervised industrial image anomaly detection. Our method leverages a self-supervised, pre-trained deep learning model to detect anomalies in industiral products. Our proposed framework addresses these limitations by employing ConvNeXt-V2 as backbone. This model is inspired by the design of Vision Transformers and is a successor to ConvNeXt. This model is pre-trained on the ImageNet-1K and ImageNet-21K datasets. We use this model to extract features from defect-free images and draw the distribution of normal samples. We use the Mahalanobis distance for calculating the difference between the features of a test image and the learned distribution of normal samples during inference. We define a threshold that determines whether the image is normal or anomalous. Our experimental results on the MVTec-AD dataset demonstrate that ConvNeXt-V2 model achieves superior accuracy compared to its predecessor ConvNeXt across various industrial products; when combined with the Mahalanobis distance. This approach enhances effectiveness and adaptability for real-time quality control.

title: Laplacian Pyramid-Based Fusion with Contrast–Entropy Attention and Sign-Consistent Softmax for Enhanced Multimodal Medical Imaging
authors: Mohammed Rafiq, Ankur Maurya, Prabhishek Singh, Manoj Diwakar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Medical image fusion (MIF) helps in understanding and diagnosis of medical images by fusing the information from multimodal medical modalities such as Computed Tomography (CT) and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI). The traditional transform methods are not that great in fusing the low-frequency (LF) and high-frequency (HF) components, which results to loss of information. The proposed method (CGCEA-SCGS) based on Laplacian Pyramid-based fusion method delivers better detail preservation. The LF components are fused using Cross-Guided Contrast-Entropy Attention (CGCEA), while HF components are fused using Sign-Consistent Gated Softmax (SCGS). The reconstruction process is done by obtaining the coarse and details layers from the Laplacian Pyramid decomposition. The visual analysis is done using final fused image comparation and 3D surface plots. The parametric analysis is done using metrics like <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Q a b} / \mathrm{f}, \text{Q y}, \text{Q c b}$</tex>, and Qs. The diagnostic reliability of CGCEA-SCGS is validated by the achieving the highest overall performance.

title: Recent Advances of Battery Technology in India
authors: Debasish Patnaik, Lalbahadur Majhi, Rudranarayan Pradhan, Tapan Kumar Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: A battery is an electrical device consisting of two or more galvanic cells which are connected in series, parallel, or a combination of both, converting chemical energy into electrical energy through electrochemical process. To form a battery of high output voltage, cells are connected in series. With the increased use of portable and mobile devices, batteries have become a major source of power. This has led to rapid advances in battery technology, which has created a variety of options. Some non-rechargeable types of batteries include Zinc-Carbon and alkaline ones, while the rechargeable ones are the replacements of the older Nickel-Cadmium (Ni-Cd) and Nickel-Metal Hydride (NiMH) cells by the modern Lithium-ion batteries. The batteries serve as convenient portable power sources for a wide range of applications, including watches, hearing aids, radios, electric clocks, camera flashes, calculators, emergency lights, cordless devices, toys, telephone systems, and motor ignition systems. They are also important in specialized fields such as space exploration. Demand for batteries has grown rapidly in recent years. Between 2010 and 2018, global battery consumption grew by about 30% annually, reaching 180 GWh in 2018. This growth is forecast to continue at about 25% per year, with demand expected to reach 2600 GWh by 2030. Further cost reductions could push this figure to about 3562 GWh.

title: Experimental Study on an Unconventional Solar-Wind Integrated System
authors: Moumita Deb, Shantanu Acharya
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increasing demand for electricity, the reduction of fossil fuel reserves, and environmental concerns have prompted scientists to investigate renewable energy sources. Solar and wind are the ubiquitous resources. Thus, they are the most preferred resource for renewable power generation. Hybridization of these sources compensate their inherent limitation for generating steady power. This present paper presents an experimental investigation of an eccentric solar-wind integrated system. The system comprises a rotating PV panel with hanging blades for capturing wind. The PV subsystem in the developed prototype generates 15.81 W at an irradiance of 899.74 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, while the wind turbine produces 3.61 W at a velocity of 3.47 m/s. The power density, average power and efficiency of the developed scheme is found to be 49.92 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, 11.99 W and 16.81%, respectively.

title: An Efficient 1D CNN Framework for Accurate Cardiac Disease Detection from ECG Signal
authors: Mandakini Ingle, Ratnesh Litoriya
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Globally, Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) continue to be the primary cause of death, and successful treatment and patient outcomes depend on early and precise identification. The main diagnostic method for detecting cardiac anomalies such arrhythmias, myocardial infarction, and atrial fibrillation is electrocardiography (ECG). In stated research, we propose a deep learning-based technique that apply a 1D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to promptly analyze raw Electrocardiogram (ECG) data for the diagnosis and classification of cardiac issues. The model is trained on publicly available ECG datasets and use multiple convolutional and pooling layers to extract morphological and temporal patterns directly from the signal. There are two types of preprocessing methods to improve the signal quality optional wavelet transformation and normalization. The proposed method can accurately classify a number of cardiac diseases without manual feature extraction. Here it can evaluate F1-score, Specificity, accuracy and precision of the algorithm are confirmed. In this research this algorithm has significant potential to detect early cardiac issues and provide promise for real time solution, dynamic scalability in portable cardiac monitoring systems.

title: SpamSentry: Real Time Detection of Spam on Social Media Using Intelligent Monitoring Tools
authors: S.Ashok Kumar, Pandurangaraju. B, A. Praneeth Reddy, Y. Nandhitha, S.Venkata Praballika, U.Pavan Kalyan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The rapid rise is come with in a torrent of content. 1generated by users contributing to the fact that it becomes harder to distinguish between spam. and real posts. Since spam does not only reduce user interface but in addition to this is a danger to safety dependable current detection. systems are necessary. This project presents a new tool that can be used to detect spam messages quickly to solve this issue. To identify complex patterns suggesting of spam the system makes use of ensemble models like XGBoost as well as Gradient Boosting which are trained on a prepared dataset of social media posts. To provide high quality input for classification, the data is prepared for computation through text cleaning, encoding and vectorization. The analysis of the model shows exceptional outcomes with the general accuracy of 98.9 percent and good levels of precision and recall, which is a sign of successful spam detection. Real time social media content input and classification results are made possible by combining the system with simple interface. A user interface and strong ensemble models work together to offer a scalable and useful attack monitoring and prevention solution. While providing the potential for more enhancements like global spam detection, deep learning integration and broad implementation to address changing challenges in social media security this project shows the success of modern methods for machine learning for enhancing online safety and user experience.

title: Hybrid Multi-Feature Analysis for Image Forgery and Deepfake Detection
authors: Abhishek Tripathi, Vikas Gupta, Paturu Prathap Reddy, P Harshini, Pedenenikalva Charan Tej Reddy, Ramireddy Vamsidhar Reddy
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Reliable authentication of digital images has been now a days difficult due to the fast growth of image editing and generative technologies, which poses challenges to journalism, legal proceedings, and forensic. This paper presents a hybrid multi-feature image forgery and deepfake detection framework that integrates copy-move detection, noise variance analysis, JPEG artifact detection, lighting consistency evaluation, and edge artifact analysis. The framework performs feature-level fusion to compute a tampering confidence score and provides visual localization of suspicious regions, ensuring interpretable forensic analysis. Evaluation on the CASIA v2.0 dataset demonstrates that the proposed hybrid approach achieves higher accuracy and robustness than individual detection modules with feasible computational cost. By strengthening explainable image authentication, the framework supports digital forensic integrity and contributes to the objectives of Sustainable Development Goal 16 (Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions).

title: Machine Learning Based Emotion Detection using Multimodal Signal Fusion Approaches
authors: Arya Palackal Shijish, Kritika A., Meenakshy S., Riya Rajeev, Divya Sasidharan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Emotion recognition using physiological signals has shown a lot of attention because of its major applications in healthcare and human computer interaction. This study looks into a multimodal approach for emotion recognition using EEG, ECG, EMG, and fNIRS signals for efficient emotion classification. A preprocessing pipeline to remove noise, artifacts, and flat signals was implemented, followed by segmentation using sliding windows. Both linear (traditional statistical measures like mean, variance, standard deviation and root mean square) and nonlinear features (Sample Entropy, Permutation Entropy, and Hjorth parameters) were extracted to get the emotional cues within physiological responses. Experimental results demonstrate that EEG features, mainly linear features and combination of linear and non-linear features combined with machine learning classifiers like LabelPropagation and ExtraTrees, give high ac- curacies, with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0 0}$</tex>. In contrast, unimodal ECG, EMG, and fNIRS features showed limited discriminative power. Early fusion of EEG with ECG, EMG, and fNIRS improved classification performance, giving perfect accuracies with all the feature sets. These highlights the superiority of multimodal fusion over unimodal analysis, underscoring the potential of hybrid physiological signal integration for reliable emotion recognition systems.

title: Sampark: An India-Based, Secure, and Self-Reliant AI-Powered Chatting Application
authors: Dhruv Chaurasia, Dev Jain, Harsh Ambariya, Bhawna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Around the world, chat applications have emerged as essential tools for real-time digital communication. Modern users expect these platforms to be secure, privacy-centric, scalable, and accessible across multiple devices. In the Indian context, growing concerns regarding digital sovereignty, data localization, and national security have highlighted the limitations of foreign-hosted messaging services. To address these challenges, this paper presents Sampark, an India-based, secure, and self-reliant AI-powered chatting application designed to store and process user data exclusively on domestic servers. Sampark integrates end-to-end encryption using PointyCastle cryptographic primitives, decentralized backup through GunDB, and cloud-based services using Firebase, all deployed within Indian infrastructure. The application is developed using the Flutter framework to ensure cross-platform compatibility and efficient performance. Additionally, AI-powered conversational support using modern language models enhances user experience and interaction. The proposed system is fully aligned with the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Bill, ensuring legal compliance, privacy preservation, and secure data handling. Experimental evaluation demonstrates low message latency, reliable communication, and acceptable resource consumption. Sampark provides a scalable, privacy-first communication solution that supports India's vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat by promoting technological independence and secure national digital infrastructure.

title: Research on Low-Temperature Energy Management Strategies for Fuel Cell Hybrid Systems
authors: Sheng Zeng, Deshui Meng, Wei Cen, Hongwen He
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: Fuel cell vehicles (FCV) are widely recognized for their environmental benefits, high efficiency, and short refueling times, garnering significant attention both domestically and internationally. However, the challenges of low-temperature startup and the substantial decline in the performance of power batteries under cold conditions severely hinder the widespread adoption of FCVs. This study investigates low-temperature technologies for fuel cells and power batteries, designs a low-temperature thermal management system for a fuel cell hybrid powertrain, and develops low-temperature startup techniques along with a multimode coordinated control strategy for rapid cold-start. These solutions were validated through vehicle testing, demonstrating that the designed thermal management system and the multimode coordinated control strategy effectively enhance the overall efficiency and cold-start capability of fuel cell vehicles.

title: EEG-Informed Cognitive Analysis and Deep Learning Classification for Autonomous Driving on Grassland Highways
authors: Mingxing Gao, Yin Liang, Jingjing Yue
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: To enhance drivers' ability to respond to dynamic hazards on grassland highways, this study employed a modified Oddball paradigm with four visual scenarios: no herd and herds on the left, right, or center of the road. Participants responded via keypress while electroencephalogram (EEG) signals were recorded. This study introduces a novel EEG classification method that integrates continuous wavelet transform (CWT) with convolutional neural network (CNN) and further examines spatial differences in EEG responses through event-related potential (ERP) analysis. The proposed CWT-CNN model achieved 97% accuracy, 97.9 % precision, and an F1-score of 0.979. Moreover, significant differences in electrophysiological markers were observed. Notably, visual stimuli presented on the left side elicited the strongest EEG responses, suggesting a lateralized attentional preference. Based on these findings, the study recommends that perception systems in autonomous driving platforms prioritize the detection and cueing of left-field stimuli to facilitate efficient attentional allocation and cognitive processing, thereby improving safety in complex, dynamic driving environments.

title: Quantum-Inspired IoT Device Identification with Generative AI-Based Traffic Interpretation
authors: Sangeetha K, Niranjan S, Maharaja R, Pavithra J
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: The increasing number of devices connected to the Internet of Things (IoT) has produced complex network traffic that is difficult to classify and interpret. As such, we present a quantum-inspired hybrid AI framework capable of classifying IoT devices by leveraging several areas of exploration: Multi-Scale Random Fourier Feature (MS-RFF) encoding, a Residual MLP that we refer to as QPR-RMLP for device identification. Using the CIC IoT-DIAD 2024 dataset, we demonstrate classification accuracy while providing normative, human-understandable insight into how IoT devices behave on the network. This quantum-style feature learning model not only classifies devices with solid accuracy but also explains how those devices behave. By providing greater classification performance through generative interpretability in IoT device classification, it allows for greater transparency and security in IoT network intelligence.

title: Time-Optimal Trajectory Planning for a Six-Degree-of-Freedom Robotic Arm Based on an Improved Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Guan Zhang, Xuchao Pan, Wei Du
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issues of local optima and insufficient convergence accuracy in traditional particle swarm optimization (PSO) for six-degree-of-freedom robotic arm trajectory planning, this study proposes a modified PSO algorithm integrating dynamic inertial weighting, adaptive learning factors, and multi-niche mutation strategies. Joint trajectories are constructed using quintic polynomials, with the objective function being minimized execution time. The time series is optimized under constraints on joint angles, velocities, and accelerations. Simulation experiments demonstrate that compared to the standard PSO, this method reduces the robot's execution time by 17.0%, produces smooth trajectories without abrupt changes, and significantly enhances both operational efficiency and stability.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)

title: Electronic System Hierarchical Fault Diagnosis Method
authors: Haoyan Wu, Weiwei Hu, Xulan Zhu, Ran Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: As the scale of electronic systems continues to expand and their structures become increasingly complex, the probability of faults occurring and the difficulty of diagnosis have significantly increased. In response to the hierarchical, propagative, correlative, and uncertain characteristics of faults in electronic systems, this paper proposes a hierarchical fault diagnosis model that constructs a diagnosis system at three levels: module level, circuit level, and component level. The module-level diagnosis combines fuzzy fault trees with Bayesian networks, using key importance, fuzzy importance, and posterior probabilities to achieve fault localization; the circuit-level diagnosis is based on cross-correlation analysis, comparing the correlation between input and output signals with standard signals to make status judgments; while the component-level diagnosis employs a Particle Swarm Optimization Extreme Learning Machine (PSO-ELM) model to accurately identify multiple fault modes. Verification through examples of a radar transmitter and typical circuits indicates that this method can significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of fault diagnosis, demonstrating good engineering application value.

title: QoS-Aware Routing Approaches in Flying Ad-Hoc Networks (FANETs): An Analytical Survey
authors: Mansi Dubey, Kuldeep Narayan Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: A Flying Ad Hoc Network (FANET) relies on wireless communication to create an infrastructure-less and temporary network among Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). Due to the highly dynamic nature of FANETs, where network topology changes frequently, efficient routing strategies are essential to handle communication requirements and ensure reliable data delivery in real time. The data transmission between two UAVs may involve one or more intermediate relay devices to efficiently disseminate the data from the sender to the receiver vehicle. Therefore, Stable, optimized, and Secure routing is a challenging task in FANET. This work analyzes various routing mechanisms for FANET with limitations and challenges. We have also elaborated on the various classes of routing protocols. Moreover, it also comprises the comparative analysis of numerous existing secure, optimized, and stable routing methods for FANET. The performance evaluation of the various routing schemes is demonstrated by simulation experiments utilizing the network simulator (NS-2) under low-density and high-density traffic scenarios. The simulation results exhibit the performance of various routing protocols in different network scenarios.

title: Brain-Computer Interfaces for Inclusive Education: Transformer Models for Real-Time Cognitive State Detection
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Ankam Sreejyothsna, Manish Gupta, Aasheesh Shukla, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The disruptive potential of brain computer interfaces (BCI) represents a potential of inclusive education, as a way of providing an adaptable, nonverbal, and cognitivelyconscious path of communication between the students with special needs. The paper discusses the application of deep learning architecture implemented with transformers to identify real-time cognitive states using noninvasive electrophysiological data (primarily EEG), interprets whether it is possible to apply these systems into the classroom and remotelearning environment to provide personalization, accessibility, and agency. We survey more modern methodological advances of temporal-spatial attention, patching and hybrid convolutiontransformer models that increase the resistance to noise, intersubject variability and low signal-to-noise ratio of real-world learning situations. Empirical and simulation results show that transformer architectures, together with domain-specific preprocessing, adaptive calibration, and artifact mitigation pipelines, can provide low-latency inference with state of the art accuracy on mental-state categories that are useful in pedagogy (e.g., attention, workload, engagement, confusion). Such algorithmic benefits come with practical issues of latency and compute limitations to edge deployment, privacy and consent to neural data in schools, interpretability to educators and guardians and equal access between socioeconomic classes. Our system architecture will be conceptual, which, to the best of our knowledge, is optimized to the real-time inference of lightweight transformer encoders through the use of knowledge distillation and temporary patching, (ii) multimodal fusion with behavioral and contextual cues during disambiguation, and (iii) federated learning in a privacy-preserving manner to personalize models without centralising raw neural data. The evaluation roadmap is given with a focus on the issues of ecological validity (classroom trials), fairness (demographic and disability-status performance stratification), and human-centred acceptability study. We end with suggestions on interdisciplinary research, regulatory protection, and design principles that would more strongly emphasize the autonomy and inclusiveness of learners and acknowledge the existing technical and ethical boundaries. The viability and accelerated maturity of transformer methods of EEG-based decoding are substantiated by recent surveys and empirical research, but will need cautious sociotechnical design to be fielded.

title: Design of PID Controller for Reflector Antenna Servo System Based on RBF Neural Network
authors: Wei Wang, Binbin Xiang, Shike Mo, Zhen Shen
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: With the continuous increase in the aperture of reflector antennas, their structural natural frequency decreases and damping becomes smaller, leading to a narrower servo bandwidth. Meanwhile, under the influence of external environmental disturbances, the traditional PID control algorithm can no longer meet the high pointing and tracking accuracy requirements of antenna servo systems. Accordingly, this paper presents a parameter self-tuning PID control algorithm based on an RBF neural network. By leveraging the adaptability and self-learning ability of the RBF neural network, the control parameters of the model are adjusted automatically. The structure of the RBF neural network is constructed, and the iterative formulas for updating both the neural network parameters and the PID parameters are derived using the gradient descent method. Simulation results indicate that the proposed algorithm not only significantly improves the dynamic performance of the antenna servo system but also exhibits strong disturbance rejection capability and robustness.

title: Performance Enhancement of a Two-Stage PV Inverter with a Small DC-Link Capacitor
authors: V G Murali Krishna, Ravi J. Dattani, Sanjay Tolani, Sukanta Halder
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In two-stage single-phase grid-connected PV inverters, a high-capacitance aluminum electrolytic capacitor (AEC) is typically employed at the DC-link to maintain a stable DC voltage and to decouple the double-frequency ripple power from the grid. However, the limited reliability of AECs poses a major drawback. Film capacitors offer superior reliability but are commercially available only in low capacitance values. This work investigates the performance of a two-stage PV inverter with a small DC-link capacitor. The analysis reveals that reduced DClink capacitance leads to distortion in the grid-injected current. To address this, a modified control scheme is proposed, and its limitations are discussed. The analytical findings are validated through simulations conducted on a 230 V, 50 Hz, 1.5 kW twostage PV inverter.

title: LSTM-Based FOC Controller for IPMSM Motor Connected to the Inverse Dynamic Environment of the Electric Vehicle
authors: KM Shiwani, Arjun Deo, Swati Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In the present research, an LSTM-based FOC controller has been proposed for Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (IPMSM) connected to the Inverse Dynamic Environment of the Electric Vehicle. The proposed controller is adapted specifically for this application to enhance its ability to capture and process sequential information effectively. By incorporating LSTM-NN, the LSTM-FOC controller benefits from its specialized architecture designed to handle long-term dependencies and learn temporal patterns in the data and a field-oriented control approach, trained with the Adaptive Moment Estimation (Adam) algorithm. In addition, the field-weakening approach is used to improve motor performance. The effectiveness of the proposed controller is evaluated using MATLAB simulations performed using various drive cycles and compared to the PI Controller-based FieldOriented Controller. The results of the simulations clearly demonstrated the superiority of the LSTM-FOC controller over the PI Controller-based field-oriented control and have the potential to replace the Traction Control used in Electric Vehicles (EVs). The LSTM-FOC controller exhibited enhanced tracking accuracy, faster response times, and improved overall control performance compared to the conventional PI controller. This study provides valuable information on the development of effective control systems for IPMSMs in the dynamic environment of electric vehicles, which can improve the performance and overall efficiency of electric vehicles.

title: Multilocation Sequential Navigation for Autonomous Mobile Robots Based on IR-UWB Ranging
authors: Huan-Bang Li, Takeshi Matsumura, Yosuke Takiguchi, Chitake Karaki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: We propose and implement an autonomous mobile robot (AMR) navigation system based on impulse radio ultrawideband (IR-UWB). Traditional AMR navigation methods require prior mapping or the setup of a coordinate system for interested space, which can impose significant overhead. This preparatory work is acceptable for applications where AMRs perform repetitive tasks over extended periods in confined spaces. However, for scenarios involving one-off tasks executed briefly, such overhead becomes impractical. In contrast, our proposed method relies solely on IR-UWB ranging between IR-UWB terminals mounted on the AMR and those placed at designated locations. As a result, the overhead is minimal. Additionally, we incorporate transmit power control (TPC) to mitigate multipath effects and apply a moving average filter to smooth route fluctuation. We implement the navigation system using this approach and validate its effectiveness through experimental measurements.

title: Bone Fracture Detection and Localisation in X-Ray Using Real Time Object Detection Model
authors: Ali Alwadi, Girija Chetty, Mohammad Alwadi, Amjad Gawanmeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The rapid integration of Artificial Intelligence in medical imaging offers significant potential to enhance diagnostic support, reduce interpretation discrepancies and alleviate radiologists' workloads, particularly in emergency settings where speed and precision are critical. This study addresses the challenge of accurate and efficient bone fracture detection in X-ray images by developing an optimised deep learning-based system, trained and evaluated on the FracAtlas dataset. Our system, developed using the FracAtlas dataset comprising a curated collection of X-ray images, incorporates a tailored data augmentation strategy to mitigate dataset imbalance and a Cosine Annealing learning rate scheduler to stabilise convergence. The model was optimised through hyperparameter tuning, including an increased input resolution of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1280 \times 1280$</tex>. The proposed model achieved a mean Average Precision at IoU 0.50 (mAP50) of 0.93, which represents a significant improvement over established benchmarks a 9.4% relative increase over YOLOv5 (0.85 mAP50) and a 13.4% relative increase over Faster R-CNN (0.82 mAP50). It also demonstrated high precision (0.91) and recall (0.92), indicating robust performance in accurately identifying and localising fractures with a low rate of false positives and negatives. The optimised YOLO framework presents a highly accurate and reliable tool for automated bone fracture detection. The approach of combining robust data augmentation, advanced learning rate scheduling, and hyperparameter optimisation has proven to significantly enhance performance. This work contributes to improving AI diagnostic systems, with the potential to improve patient outcomes and facilitate wider adoption of intelligent systems in clinical radiology.

title:
High-Performance ZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Doped ZnO Varistors via Cold Sintering and Heat Treatment
authors: Qi Wang, Xuetong Zhao, Jianglin Wang, Yan Hu, Chengjun Ren
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: ZnO-based varistors are widely used as overvoltage protectors due to their strong nonlinear current-voltage characteristics. However, conventional sintering above 1200 °C causes grain coarsening, volatilization of Bi-rich phases, and high energy consumption. In this study, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{ZnO}_{2}-\text{In}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-\text{CaO}_{2}-\text{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-\text{NiO}- \text{Mn}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-\text{Co}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-\text{Bi}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-x \text{ZrO}_{2}(x=0,1,2,3 \text{wt} {\%})$</tex> ceramics were fabricated via the Cold Sintering Process (CSP) at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 0}^{\circ} \mathbf{C}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 0}$</tex> MPa, followed by annealing at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$850{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>. XRD confirmed the retention of the wurtzite ZnO structure, and SEM-EDS revealed dense microstructures with uniformly distributed Bi-rich intergranular phases. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{ZrO}_{2}$</tex> doping effectively suppressed grain growth and improved electrical performance. The 2 wt% ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> doped sample exhibited a grain size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.414 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, breakdown electric fields <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(E_{\mathrm{b}})$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$713 \mathrm{V} / \text{mm}$</tex>, and nonlinear coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\alpha)$</tex> of 45.75. These improvements are attributed to the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{ZrO}_{2}$</tex>-induced grain-boundary pinning effect and the formation of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z r}^{\mathbf{2 +}}$</tex> solid solution in ZnO. The results demonstrate that CSP provides a lowtemperature, energy-efficient route for developing highperformance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z n O}$</tex>-based varistors.

title: RESCUENET: Residual-LSTM Combined for Unified Epilepsy Detection
authors: Srivathsan B, Virubash Neppolian, R I Minu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: About 50 million people worldwide suffer from epilepsy, and effective management and treatment depend on continuous EEG monitoring. Traditional monitoring devices are heavy, restrict movement, and are not ideal for prolonged usage. Although behind-the-ear wearable EEG systems offer promising substitutes for ongoing ambulatory seizure monitoring, they require robust automated detection algorithms to overcome high false alarm rates and signal quality problems. Using the SeizeIT2 collection of behind-the-ear EEG recordings, the authors propose and study a novel hybrid architecture that uses bidirectional LSTM layers and residual neural networks for seizure identification. To detect intricate seizure activity patterns, the design blends the spatial feature extraction capabilities of ResNet with the temporal modeling prowess of recurrent neural networks. Comparing performance with the standard ResNet and ChronoNet models reveals that the ResNet-LSTM hybrid achieved the highest training accuracy (85.97%) among the models, providing a balanced sensitivity-specificity profile (27.67% sensitivity, 83.11% specificity). Implementation details include focal loss optimization, artifact rejection through RMS thresholding, and strategic class balancing to address dataset imbalance. The experimental results show that the hybrid model offers different performance trade-offs compared to the baseline models while keeping false alarm rate suitable for home settings. This balanced performance makes the system practical for daily use in home settings, helping epilepsy patients get reliable seizure monitoring without frequent hospital visits.

title: Accelerated Skin Prick Test (ASPT): A Low-Cost, AI-Guided Allergy Diagnostic System Using Microneedles and Image-Based Severity Scoring
authors: Kenneth Yuyo Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Allergies affect over 400 million individuals worldwide [1], straining healthcare systems through frequent visits and expensive diagnostic procedures. Conventional skin prick and serum immunoglobulin E (IgE) tests are invasive, costly, and require clinical staff. This study presents the Accelerated Skin Prick Test (aSPT), which integrates a dual-layer microneedle patch and a smartphone-based convolutional neural network (CNN) for automated allergic reaction scoring. Preliminary ex vivo validation was performed using agar hydrogel as a skin surrogate, where a commercially available titanium microneedle derma stamp (1 mm length, 36 -microneedle array) achieved 100 penetration success with visually confined, uniform allergen delivery and no intersite overlap. Reproducible artificial intelligence (AI) benchmarking on a curated image dataset showed a model area under the curve (AUC) of 0.91 and F1 score of 0.87 for severity grading. These results support the feasibility of aSPT as a rapid, low-cost, and accessible diagnostic platform suitable for broader clinical and field deployment.

title: SimCLR-Based Self-Supervised Learning for Effective Feature Representation
authors: Md Zaka Al Shahariar Tanvir, Inderdeep Kaur, A K M Shafkat Karim, Shahadat Arifin, Ushna Mondal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has become a potent approach in artificial intelligence, decreasing reliance on large annotated datasets by utilizing unlabeled data for learning representations. In contrast to conventional supervised methods, SSL allows models to identify hidden patterns and transferable features without the expense of manual labeling. Contrastive learning—particularly SimCLR—has outperformed other SSL techniques by clearly distinguishing between different images and making similar images appear more alike after being altered. In this research, we apply SimCLR utilizing PyTorch and Google Colab with the CIFAR-10 dataset. The preprocessing workflow involves random cropping, flipping, color variations, and conversion to grayscale. A ResNet-18 encoder featuring a projection head is trained with contrastive loss for 10 epochs, utilizing a batch size of 256. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is used to display learned embeddings, illustrating distinct clusters among different classes. Quantitative findings show that SimCLR reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy, surpassing a supervised ResNet-18 benchmark of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 8. 2 \%}$</tex>. These results validate the capability of SSL to learn distinguishing features without labeled data, highlighting its importance in fields like medical imaging, agriculture, and remote sensing. Subsequent studies must enhance label efficiency and broaden SSL applications to tackle domain-specific issues, such as detecting plant diseases and analyzing healthcare data.

title: Numerical Investigation of Ionic Wind Cooling for High-Power LEDs Thermal Management
authors: Ines Grabaa, Zouhour Araoud, Kamel Charrada, Georges Zissis, Laurent Canale
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Ionic wind, also referred to as electrohydrodynamic (EHD) airflow, represents a promising solution for compact and silent thermal management of electronic devices. This study presents a detailed numerical investigation of a needle-to-plate ionic wind generator applied to the cooling of a heated plate representative of high-power LED modules. Fully coupled electrostatics-transport of diluted species-laminar flow-heat transfer model is developed using COMSOL Multiphysics in a two-dimensional domain. Activating the ionic wind reduces the maximum plate temperature from 454.40 K to 444.25 K, corresponding to a temperature reduction of 10 K. The EHD-induced airflow reaches velocities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5-2 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> near the impingement region, leading to effective localized hotspot mitigation rather than global heat removal. Parametric analyses highlight the influence of applied voltage, electrode spacing, needle height, and dissipated power. The numerical results are consistent with previously reported experimental and numerical studies, confirming the relevance of ionic wind cooling for localized thermal management in electronic systems.

title: MCP-PUF: A Context-Hardened, ML-Assisted Authentication Protocol for Secure Healthcare IoT
authors: Pintu Chauhan, Udai Pratap Rao, Mishra Mridul Kailashchandra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Healthcare IoT (H-IoT) systems demand secure, low-latency authentication mechanisms resilient to modeling and replay attacks. This paper introduces MCP-PUF, a lightweight, context-aware protocol integrating Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs) with a Model Context Protocol (MCP) to enhance security in Electronic Healthcare Systems (EHS). By binding challenges to physiological context (e.g., heart rate, temperature) and SRAM entropy, MCP-PUF generates unpredictable session-specific challenges. A lightweight machine learning classifier at the EHS server validates device identity with resilience to environmental drift. Security analysis under Dolev–Yao and Canetti–Krawczyk models confirms forward secrecy and modeling attack resistance. On STM32 hardware, the protocol achieves 6.1 ms authentication latency, 48% memory savings, and over 98.5% classification accuracy. MCP-PUF is well-suited for critical-care devices, wearables, and low-power medical sensors in real-time healthcare deployments.

title: Reliability Assessment Method for Distribution Networks Incorporating Photovoltaic Power Generation Under Blizzard Conditions
authors: Jiajun Zhang, Haifeng Zhang, Jingying Yang, Runzi Lin, Tao Liu, Yutong Liu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Existing reliability assessment methods for distribution grids exhibit significant shortcomings when addressing extreme blizzard conditions. They either idealize photovoltaic output or simplify it to complete shutdown, leading to inaccurate evaluation results. This paper proposes a reliability assessment method for photovoltaic-integrated distribution grids that accounts for blizzard impacts. By establishing a three-state Markov model for photovoltaic units and combining it with sequential Monte Carlo simulation, the method simulates and analyzes changes in system reliability metrics under varying snow accumulation thicknesses. Case study results demonstrate that this method effectively quantifies the deterioration of system reliability caused by heavy snow disasters. When snow accumulation exceeds a critical threshold, the effective power supply range of PV systems shrinks from multiple load points to only the highest priority loads, directly causing a stepwise decline in overall system reliability. This methodology provides a theoretical basis for ensuring reliable operation and disaster prevention planning of distribution networks under extreme weather conditions.

title: Privacy-Preserving Electrical Load Forecasting Through Federated Learning
authors: Madderla Chiranjeevi, Lade Gunakar Rao, Naveen Venkat, Hariharan Ayappane
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Accurate electrical load forecasting is highly crucial to ensure the stability, efficiency, and sustainability of new power systems. Centralized data gathering from various utility producers and consumers by load forecasting models is conventional and raises serious concerns about data privacy, ownership, and security. This study points a federated learning-based privacy-preserving electrical load forecasting architecture to address these problems. Unlike traditional central-zed models, the new approach permits various stakeholders to cooperatively train a worldwide predictive model without disclosing their raw data. Instead, model updates are exchangeable, thus safeguarding confidential user data. In this paper, tested the framework's performance using state-of-the-art deep learning models for short-term and long-term load forecasting under different data distributions. Experimental results on benchmark smart grid datasets demonstrates that federated learning models achieves comparable or even better forecasting performance than centralized counterparts, while improving data privacy and scalability to a large degree. Moreover, the envisioned framework reduces communication overhead through adaptive aggregation techniques and assures the resilience of non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data. This paper illustrates how federated learning will revolutionize electrical load forecasting by reconciling forecasting accuracy, data privacy, and computational efficiency, making way for privacy-conscious applications in the smart grid.

title: Implementation of an ML-Based Credit Scoring System for Loan Approval
authors: Shalini Shukla, Sumit Dhariwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Credit scoring systems that are both accurate and efficient are more important than ever in today's financial world. Due to its simplicity and reliance on constrained data sources, classic credit scoring methodologies like the FICO score may not capture the complexities of modern financial behavior. The goal of this research is to improve the efficiency, fairness, and predicted accuracy of loan acceptance using a credit scoring system that is based on Machine Learning (ML). To sift through mountains of financial data—including applicant income, loan amount, credit score, and other demographic details—we use a mix of Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers. Tools like feature engineering and sophisticated data pretreatment methods are employed to generate valuable features, such the loan-to-income ratio. The study optimizes the model's efficacy by utilizing Grid SearchCV for hyper parameter tuning. Precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC are some of the evaluation metrics we use. Data insights and model performance are presented in a comprehensive manner using visualizations like 3D plots and heat maps. Improved accuracy and generalizability are two areas where the project's optimized Random Forest classifier outperforms Logistic Regression. Credit scoring systems can be improved by utilizing alternate data sources and dynamic models, according to the research. Furthermore, studies show that continuous evaluation is necessary to guarantee that models are fair and in accordance with regulatory requirements. We offer a workable solution that is prepared to be integrated into current loan processing workflows by preserving the most performing model for deployment. This work provides a robust, data-driven approach to credit rating, which paves the way for more accessible and efficient lending procedures.

title: Study on the Impact of Hyperparameters on the Performance of Inter-Turn Short Circuit Fault Diagnosis Models
authors: Shuya Li, Mingsheng Wang, Hong Zhang, Bin Liang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs), with their high efficiency and excellent dynamic performance, are widely used in various agricultural equipment. The complex working environment in agricultural production presents significant challenges to the safe operation of PMSMs. Inter-turn short circuit faults in the stator windings, one of the common electrical faults during motor operation, require early fault diagnosis to enhance motor safety. Deep learning technologies have rapidly developed in the field of fault diagnosis, but they also face computational challenges due to the numerous combinations of hyperparameters. This study uses an automatic feature extraction method based on dilated convolutional neural networks (DCNN) to investigate the recognition of PMSM inter-turn short circuit faults and the impact of hyperparameters. The hyperparameters are categorized into three types: primary hyperparameters, secondary hyperparameters, and stable hyperparameters, thus establishing a reasonable optimization sequence. This approach prepares hyperparameter priorities for subsequent optimization strategies, reducing computational costs and improving model training efficiency and diagnostic performance.

title: A Machine Learning Approach to Genomic Task Scheduling in Fog Computing
authors: Prashanth Choppara, Bommareddy Lokesh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This research focuses on the application of fog computing to improve the existing cloud computing augmentation of genomic data processing in the healthcare field. Genomic data has assumed tremendous importance in the practice of medicine whereby it is used in risk assessment, efficacy of drugs and developing unique preventive and therapeutic regimes for every patient. However, the amount of genomic data is immense, and it is often challenging to process it because it firstly needs proper computing resources, typically performed by cloud computing. Although cloud computing provides massive computation resources and storage, it lacks timeliness and security for real-time health care applications. Fog computing, which is a localization of the data processing models of cloud computing, offers a solution by minimizing delay and increasing data protection. This research seeks to establish a new approach in the schedule of the tasks in the fog computing setting based on Quantum AdaBoost for the best optimization of Genomic data. An integration of machine learning in the scheduling of tasks enables the flexibility of prioritization of the tasks in relation to the availability of computational resources. The need to constantly make real-time decisions, the problem of latency as well as the need to protect data make the proposed model suitable for personalized medicine. Performance measures like makespan and trust are computed and analyzed to demonstrate how fog computing can efficiently handle time-constrained and privacy-sensitive genomics data. This allow for faster and accurate healthcare decisions as more time is spent on decision-making and planning rather than on case preparation and investigation. Hibernate also harvest better patient results and increased treatment returns.

title: Decentralized Access Control Using Blockchain and Smart Contracts for Enhanced Cybersecurity
authors: Ahmed Afif Monrat, Karl Andersson
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: With the exponential growth of digital systems, traditional centralized access control mechanisms such as RoleBased Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) face significant challenges, including single points of failure, scalability limitations, and vulnerabilities to insider threats. These issues compromise the security, transparency, and auditability of access policies. This article explores the integration of blockchain technology with access control frameworks to mitigate these vulnerabilities. By leveraging decentralized ledgers and smart contracts, blockchain enhances transparency, tamperresistance, and traceability in policy enforcement and auditing processes. The study compares RBAC and ABAC implementations within blockchain environments, highlighting improvements in granularity, interoperability, and resilience. Through architecture models, smart contract code, and performance discussion, we demonstrate the potential of blockchain in enhancing access control security, transparency, and trustworthiness.

title: Substation Text Information Detection Based on Maximum Stable Extreme Value Area of Information Entropy Pre-Positioning
authors: Sha Luo, Zhiwei Zou, Yu Hao, Dengzhou Fang, Wei Sun, Qingwei Gao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In substation scenarios, accurate text detection is crucial for equipment management and maintenance. To address the limitations of the traditional MSER (Maximally Stable Extremal Regions) algorithm, which is susceptible to noise interference and insufficient detection accuracy in complex backgrounds, this paper proposes an improved MSER-based substation text detection algorithm incorporating information entropy pre-localization. The input image is first converted to grayscale, followed by the construction of an entropy distribution feature map through pooling and information entropy calculation. The text regions in the original image are then pre-localized based on their characteristic entropy ranges, enhancing the precision of MSER detection. Finally, the MSER algorithm is applied to the pre-localized regions for text extraction. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to conventional methods, the proposed approach significantly improves both accuracy and robustness in substation text detection.

title: Secure Sign-On for the Indian Smart Meter: A GMAC-Based DLMS/COSEM Authentication Framework
authors: Hari Prasad Musham, D. Shankar, S. Sudha, P. Kaliappan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The rapid deployment of smart meters in India, enabled by the Device Language Message Specification/Companion Specification for Energy Metering (DLMS/COSEM) protocol, has revolutionized energy management. However, the current implementation of HLS (High Level Security) sign-on authentication using Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) - 128 in ECB (Electronic Codebook) mode presents significant security vulnerabilities. The inherent weaknesses of ECB mode, including susceptibility to pattern leakage and replay attacks, expose critical smart meter infrastructures to potential breaches, undermining data confidentiality and system integrity. This paper identifies the key gaps in the existing authentication mechanism and proposes the adoption of the GMAC (Galois Message Authentication Code) mode for HLS sign-on authentication. GMAC provides strong data integrity verification and enhanced resistance to common cryptographic attacks, addressing the limitations of ECB mode. By leveraging the properties of authenticated encryption, GMAC offers a robust solution tailored to the specific needs of smart meters in India's energy grid. Through a detailed analysis of cryptographic methods and practical scenarios, this paper demonstrates how transitioning to GMAC can significantly bolster the security of smart meter communication. The proposed approach aligns with global cybersecurity standards, ensuring that India's smart meter infrastructure is safeguarded against evolving threats. The findings offer a roadmap for implementing secure, future-proof cryptographic solutions in smart grid systems.

title: Correlation-Driven Feature Prioritization for Stroke Prediction
authors: Ritulika Ghosh Dastidar, Jyotsna Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Stroke is a major cause of mortality and long-term disability, motivating the need for reliable and interpretable early prediction models. This paper presents a correlationdriven framework for feature prioritization in stroke prediction by combining variable-type-appropriate statistical association measures with supervised machine learning. Point-biserial correlation, Phi coefficient, and Cramér's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V$</tex> are used to quantify univariate associations with a binary stroke outcome, while logistic regression and random forest models assess multivariate predictive importance. All association analyses are performed on the original dataset, and class imbalance handling is restricted to the training phase within stratified cross-validation. Across five-fold stratified cross-validation, logistic regression achieved an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 7 9} \pm \mathbf{0. 0 2}$</tex>, while random forest achieved an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 3} \pm \mathbf{0. 0 1}$</tex>. Results consistently indicate that age, body mass index, and average glucose level are the most informative predictors, whereas several demographic and binary health indicators exhibit weaker standalone effects.

title: Roadside 3D Object Detection Enhanced by Spatial Location for Intelligent Traffic System
authors: Si Xu, Hongjuan Zhang, Zhenqiang Ma, Disheng Zhange, Zhibo Zhao, Bijun Li
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: With the development of BDS high-precision positioning technology, Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) have achieved centimeter-level positioning, but vehicles in traffic scenarios do not possess active positioning capabilities and require roadside systems for auxiliary positioning. Recently, roadside BEV-based 3D object detection technology has achieved significant progress and is widely applied in positioning and navigation, autonomous driving, and other fields, which provides the foundation for auxiliary positioning. However, large sensing ranges from the roadside result in sparse BEV features for foreground objects, which easily leads to missing detections of objects at the BEV level. Meanwhile, roadside perception typically relies on monocular cameras as perception devices, and monocular depth estimation often suffers from considerable errors. Such errors in depth estimation tend to cause large spatial position perception errors, which greatly restrict the development of roadside BEV-based 3D perception technology. To address the issue of position prediction errors in roadside 3D object detection, we propose an efficient 3D object detection framework dubbed RoadSideBEV. Specifically, we design a lightweight spatial region proposal network: by learning the positional distributions of different objects in the BEV space, this network alleviates the sparsity of BEV features and enhances the capability of perceiving objects' spatial positions. Furthermore, to alleviate the problem of insufficient monocular depth prediction, we put forward a foreground depth map and height map supervision method. Through pixel-level foreground depth and height supervision, this method effectively improves depth perception capability. Experimental results on DAIR-V2X and Rope3D demonstrate that RoadSideBEV achieves wonderful performance.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)

title: Genetic and Generic Algorithm for Text Mining for Metadata in Telecom Sector
authors: Bhola Gan Chaudhuri, Shalli Rani
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Text of the composition is devoted to giving an account of the development of the telecommunications sector in general as an economy area connected to high knowledge technologies. Customers' feedback, services requests, technical documentation, network activity logs and many other incoming calls produce a vast amount of unstructured data. Indeed, the issue of how to derive useful information automatically from such kind of data indicates the need for new efficient and effective approach to metadata generation which will help in extending information retrieval, decision making and automation. This paper investigates the effectiveness of Genetic Algorithms and their non-specific counterparts in the context of telecom sector text data mining and the extraction of metadata. Genetic Algorithms employs Evolutionary processes for enhanced feature selection and classification which increases accuracy and relevance of generated metadata. On the other hand, generic algorithms employ normal optimization techniques but are aimed at time and resource economization with little regard for complexity. The work has done a comparative analysis of these algorithms with regards to computational measurements of accuracy, time taken to execute tasks and scalability. The results of the experiments demonstrate that although Genetic Algorithms is more precise and more adaptable to complex datasets, they require more time to process compared to, generic algorithms. This research gives grounds on what factors to consider when choosing suitable algorithmic approaches to follow for the purposes of metadata extraction when dealing with selected areas of telecom data management.

title: Editorial Forensics with NLP: A Hybrid Model for Source Attribution and Narrative Drift Analysis
authors: Ananya Dixit, Sumit Goswami
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The paper presents a forensic NLP mechanism to unveil the institutional source of anonymized journalistic articles. The need for computational analysis to identify institutional fingerprints and editorial biases in defence journalism is addressed by integrating Sentence-BERT embeddings, stylometric features, and TF-IDF vectors into an ensemble model (LightGBM, XGBoost, SVM, RF, ET). Journalistic DNA Clustering, an unsupervised algorithm is introduced in this study for detecting style and semantic groupings and further extends to Intent Vector Mapping, where articles are clustered in a 3D semantic space along Technical Innovation, Strategic Geopolitics, and Economic Industrial axes, and lastly establishes the Narrative Leak Risk Score (NLRS) to quantify rhetoric, stylistic deviation, and semantic proximity. The framework achieves high source attribution accuracy of up to 94 % across nine sources, with Journalistic DNA Clustering yielding a Silhouette Score of 0.6741. The NLRS reaches a peak value of 0.7398, indicating strong institutional identifiability. These results validate the framework's effectiveness in media origin verification and propaganda forensics.

title: A Low-Frequency Vibration Energy Harvester Based on a Vibration-Rotation Mechanism
authors: Lin Guo, Jiajia Zhang, Haoyue Yang, Yongxi Liu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a low-frequency vibration energy harvester based on a vibration-rotation mechanism, which couples an electromagnetic generation unit with a triboelectric generation unit. A linkage drive structure and a one-way clutch are employed to convert low-frequency bidirectional vibration into unidirectional rotational motion of a ratchet wheel. The vibrational energy is then converted into electrical energy via the electromagnetic and triboelectric units for energy harvesting. Matched power management circuits are designed to convert the AC electricity into a 3.3 V DC regulated power supply for lowpower electronic devices. The harvester's structure was designed using SolidWorks. COMSOL simulations were utilized to optimize the structural parameters of the electromagnetic unit and to validate the feasibility of the triboelectric unit. A physical prototype was fabricated via 3D printing for experimental testing. Results demonstrate that the harvester can effectively collect lowfrequency vibration energy in the range of 0.5-5 Hz. At 5 Hz, the electromagnetic unit achieves an output of 5 V/ 17.5 mA, and by coupling a mechanical switch, the triboelectric unit reaches an output of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \mathrm{V}/15 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex>. This design features a simple structure and wide frequency band adaptability, effectively addressing existing challenges such as structural complexity, low efficiency, and narrow operational bandwidth. It provides an efficient and practical solution for low-frequency vibration energy harvesting.

title: Human Target Recognition in Dense Smoke Environments based on Multimodal Input and Image Smoke Removal Fusion Algorithm
authors: Yuhao Yang, Hengyu Shang, Fei Tang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Modern fire protection systems incorporate various sensors such as infrared and thermal imaging technologies.
