However, in actual fire rescue scenarios, accurate detection of human targets in smoke-filled environments remains significantly challenging due to the attenuation of visible and infrared light by dense smoke, complex ambient thermal fields, and interference from obstructing objects. To address this issue, this paper proposes a multimodal smoke-removal fusion algorithm for the precise identification of human targets in smoke-obscured environments. The process begins with spatiotemporal alignment of raw visible- light and infrared video streams to synchronize image frames. Subsequently, multiple image processing techniques–including Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and median filter denoising–are applied to mitigate smoke occlusion effects. Finally, human detection is performed using YOLOv8. The video annotation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm exhibits commendable smoke removal performance. Specifically, the YOLOv8m and YOLOv8n models achieved precision rates of 99% and 97.7%, respectively, in human target detection under dense smoke conditions. This indicates substantial potential for applications in fire rescue operations, security surveillance systems, and industrial safety inspections.

title: Anti-Interference Strategy for Partial Discharge Signals in AC UHV Transformers Based on Monomer Balance Method
authors: Xu Cao, Xi Chen, Ruofeng Qin, Cheng Cao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) detection is a critical method for assessing the insulation status of power equipment. However, in complex electromagnetic environments, external interference signals can easily mask genuine PD signals, compromising detection accuracy. This paper proposes an anti-interference strategy for transformer PD signal detection based on the Monomer Balance Method (MBM), aiming to enhance detection reliability and anti-interference capability. The MBM simplifies wiring and instrumentation by replacing the standard test object in traditional balancing methods with coupling capacitors, while simultaneously improving detection sensitivity. In experiments, an AC ultra-high-voltage (UHV) 1,000 kV transformer was studied. By introducing mixed signals of white noise and periodic narrowband interference into the windings, the anti-interference performance of the MBM was validated. Results demonstrate that the MBM effectively suppresses interference signals, reduces their amplitude, and significantly improves the detectability of PD signals. This method provides an effective hardware-based anti-interference solution for transformer PD detection.

title: A Robust ANOVA-Based Framework for Multi-Metric Comparison of Convolutional Neural Networks in Medical Imaging
authors: Mohamed Gasmi, Hakim Bendjenna, Abdallah Meraoumia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Rigorous comparison of convolutional neural network (CNN) performance in medical imaging requires inferential foundations to ensure clinical reliability and reproducibility. Existing approaches typically based on averages of aggregated metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC) do not recognize experimental variability and do not evaluate the significance of differences between models. This paper presents a multi-metric Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) framework that decomposes intermodel and intra-model variance to statistically test performance differences. Post hoc tests (Tukey's HSD) precisely identify which model pairs differ significantly while controlling for error due to multiple comparisons. The protocol includes systematic verification of assumptions (normality and homogeneity of variances) and proposes nonparametric alternatives in case of violations, ensuring robust inference. Experiments on several CNN architectures, including transfer learning, show that ANOVA provides a more rigorous evaluation than simple averages, supporting a reliable and reproducible model selection process, consistent with current methodological and clinical requirements. This framework establishes a standard for multi-metric inferential evaluation in medical imaging and recommends its adoption to enhance the scientific credibility and clinical impact of model comparisons.

title: Enhanced Finger-Knuckle-Print Recognition Using Deep Texture Feature Analysis
authors: Saoussen Djeddi, Yacine Belhocine, Meriem Mebarkia, Abdallah Meraoumia, Hakim Bendjenna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Feature extraction is a fundamental task in pattern recognition, since signals such as vectors or images, contain a large number of diverse features and are applied across multiple domains such as medical imaging, biometrics, remote sensing, and robotics. Owing to this importance, extensive research has been devoted to developing effective extraction techniques, often leading to highly competitive classification results. In fact, most challenges in designing Pattern Recognition (PR) systems are directly related to the feature extraction stage. This paper investigates the influence of deep learning methods on the performance of PR systems. For a fair evaluation, the efficiency of deep feature extraction is compared with both handcrafted and learned-handcrafted approaches. To perform this study, we designed a hand-based biometric recognition system, which serves as a representative PR application. The comparison focuses on three texture-based methods: Multiscale Local Binary Pattern (MLBP), Compact Binary Descriptor (CBD), and DCTNet deep learning. Experimental findings reveal that none of the examined approaches performs poorly compared with the others, but that the most suitable method should be selected according to the problem characteristics, the volume of data available, and the processing time requirements.

title: Feature Selection Using SHAP
authors: Naouel Ouafek, Tarek Berghout
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Accurate Remaining Useful Life (RUL) estimation of aircraft engines is essential for efficient maintenance planning and safety. The state-of-the-art deep learning models, while excellent in prediction accuracy, are typically non-explainable and encounter uncertainty about the sensor data. In this work, we propose an explainable RUL estimation framework using the C-MAPSS dataset. A BiLSTM network is used to capture temporal relationships among multivariate sensor readings, and SHAP is used for increased interpretability of the model and guiding sensor selection. Experimental results demonstrate that selecting the best informative sensors based on SHAP significantly improves the performance, reducing RMSE from 16.59 to 14.26 and increasing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>-score from 0.82 to 0.87. The penalty score also drops significantly, confirming the increased reliability and robustness of the model. This approach promotes the adoption of explainable AI in accountable prediction systems for aerospace use.

title: Optimal Energy Management in Interconnected Net-Zero Energy Multi-Greenhouse Systems
authors: Abdellatif Soussi, Ahmed Ouammi, Enrico Zero, Nicola Mignoni, Raffaele Carli, Mariagrazia Dotoli, Roberto Sacile
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: This paper proposes a cooperative control framework for a connected cluster of microgrids integrated with multiple smart greenhouses, forming a smart local energy network within the broader context of smart grids. Each microgrid includes renewable energy sources, water pumps, energy storage systems, communication and metering infrastructure, water reservoirs, and a group of greenhouses. Each greenhouse is equipped with heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C O_{2}$</tex> injectors, artificial lighting, sensors, local pumps, and fans. The primary goal is to develop a coordinated optimization strategy that enables efficient control of microgrid operations and manages inter-microgrid power exchanges, while ensuring high service quality. This coordination is facilitated through a bidirectional communication network, with a centralized controller overseeing and dispatching control signals. A comprehensive scheduling optimization algorithm is designed and implemented to manage the operation of the interconnected microgrids, considering system constraints. The aim is to improve energy efficiency and precisely regulate microclimate conditions to create an optimal environment for crop growth across all greenhouses.

title: Security of Grid-Connected Photovoltaic Plants: A Mini-Review and Future Directions
authors: Nassiha Boutana, Rokia Chekirou, Adel Mellit, Maamar Bettayeb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Photovoltaic systems are becoming increasingly vulnerable to cyber-attacks and threats as they are connected into modern energy grids, which can affect both their physical and digital components. Therefore, Cyber security is essential for maintaining the grid stability, energy reliability and safety. This paper provides a mini review of cyber-physical security in grid connected photovoltaic (GCPV) systems. Moreover, a new configuration based on the concept of secure artificial intelligence of things (SAIoT) is proposed and examined. Detection methods and mitigation strategies against possible threats and cyber-attacks are discussed and highlighted, with a particular focus on AI machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches. This work serves as a prelude to further research and practical applications in this topic.

authors: Rachid Chelghoum, Abdelhalim Brahimi, Nacerdine Bourouba, Juan Pablo Martínez Jiménez, Nacerdine Bouzit, Raouf Zerrougui, Abderrahim Yousfi, Okba Saidani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This work centers on the development of dielectric resonator antennas (DRAs) using binary composite materials made from epoxy resin (RE) and barium titanate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{BaTiO}_{3}$</tex> or BT). The composite samples are prepared under standard environmental conditions, specifically at room temperature and atmospheric pressure. Their dielectric properties are evaluated using the time domain spectroscopy (TDS) technique. The antenna structures are modeled, simulated, and fine-tuned utilizing the Ansys HFSS electromagnetic simulation software. The CDRA involves a design comprising two identical resonators, each combining a rectangular and a cylindrical shape of equal height. This design demonstrates ultra-wideband performance, achieving a 97% relative bandwidth spanning from 6 GHz to 17.32 GHz. Additionally, it supports dual-band circular polarization (axial ratio <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<3 ~\text{dB}$</tex>), with operational bands extending from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.62-10.14 \text{GHz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.74-16.94 \text{GHz}$</tex>. The main objective of this study is to propose innovative antenna configurations suited for modern wireless communication systems.

title: Comparative Optimal Dispatch Control Strategies for a Hybrid Renewable Energy System
authors: Mourad Zebboudj, Djamila Rekioua, Toufik Rekioua
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This research presents a study on a hybrid renewable energy system that integrates battery storage with photovoltaic panels, wind turbines, and a diesel generator. The study was conducted in Bejaia, Algeria, which possesses substantial potential for wind and solar energy resources. The integration of the diesel generator is essential as an auxiliary source to compensate for the intermittency of renewable resources, but it requires precise sizing according to daily peak load demands. The stand-alone system architecture is simulated under two different optimization algorithms using HOMER Pro (open-source version): combined dispatch (CD) and predictive dispatch (PS), to determine the optimal system cost. The impact of various component prices on the net present cost (NPC) and the cost of energy (COE), along with the emissions of polluting gases following diesel generator integration, were evaluated using sensitivity analysis. The results demonstrate that the predictive dispatch algorithm clearly outperforms the combined dispatch approach in optimizing investment costs, while the combined dispatch strategy achieves greater reductions in emissions and fuel consumption by the diesel generator.

title: Novel Mathematical Framework for Addressing Torsional Effect in Reinforced Concrete Structures
authors: Ashwini Kumar, Ganesh D, Sreyansu Satya Prakash, R. Padmapriya, Nishant Bhardwaj, Jagmeet Sohal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Torsional effects in reinforced concrete (RC) structures significantly influence structural performance, safety, and serviceability, especially under complex loading conditions. Accurately predicting these effects is essential for modern structural design and durability. Conventional design methods often rely on simplified assumptions and linear elastic models, which fail to capture nonlinear material behaviour, cracking patterns, and interaction between flexure, shear, and torsion. This leads to discrepancies between theoretical predictions and experimental or field results. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a Reinforced Concrete Torsional Framework (RCTF) that integrates Finite Element Modelling (FEM) with Nonlinear Regression (NLR) techniques. FEM enables detailed simulation of stress-strain responses and cracking mechanisms, while NLR enhances predictive accuracy by calibrating analytical outcomes with experimental data. The suggested framework was applied to the reinforced concrete beam and member subjected to torsional loads thus demonstrating the efficacy of the proposed framework in improving the predictive reliability of such problems, reducing the computational inaccuracies, and bridging the theoretical predictions and empirical practice for a particular problem. Results lead to understanding of torsional stiffness and deterioration of strength and failure mechanism in more comprehensive manner for engineer for the design of safer and efficient reinforced concrete structure. The framework advances torsional effect prediction beyond traditional methods. The RCTF method predicts <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40.7 \text{kN} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex> (S1), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$56.3 \text{kN} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex> (S2), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$72.1 \text{kN} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex> (S3), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$84.2 \text{kN} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex> (S4), demonstrating superior torsional accuracy.

title: Development of a Soil Nitrogen and Soil Moisture Sensor System for Precision Crop Management
authors: Marcellin Jay C. Panes, Cyrus Jay E. Galendez, Raymond F. Gabica
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and calibration of a soil nitrogen and soil moisture sensor system for smart greenhouse applications. Nitrogen nutrient sensors were tested under different fertilizer treatments to evaluate their variability and accuracy. Nine soil samples treated with varying concentrations of three fertilizer types were monitored for nitrogen and moisture content using a JXCT nitrogen sensor and a capacitive soil moisture probe. Regression analysis revealed strong correlations between projected and sensor-measured nitrogen levels, with the highest accuracy observed at soil moisture levels of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$82 \%\left(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}=0.7157\right)$</tex>. The Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) significantly contribute to reinforcing the findings, indicating the lowest error at the highest soil moisture. The results verify that integrating nitrogen and moisture sensors can provide realtime monitoring, reliability, and improve decisionmaking for fertilizer application and resourceefficient crop production. The study contributes toward scalable smart greenhouse systems for precision agriculture.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Driven Brain CT Scan Analysis Tool for Stroke Detection Using U-Net Architecture
authors: Francis John Saul M. Tin-ao, Rhea M. Bete, James Earl D. Cubillas, Jeffrey T. Dellosa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Stroke is a leading cause of death and long-term disability in the Philippines, where limited access to neurologists and diagnostic tools results in delayed treatment and poor patient outcomes. Manual segmentation of brain lesions in CT scans is time-consuming and prone to human error, especially in under-resourced healthcare settings. This study developed a desktop-based intelligent system that automates stroke lesion detection and classification using a U-Net Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and an intuitive graphical interface. The system was trained on 2,455 preprocessed brain CT scan images representing both ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes. Using the PyTorch framework, the U-Net model achieved high segmentation performance with mean Intersection over Union (IoU) scores of 0.87 for ischemic lesions and 0.82 for hemorrhagic lesions. The desktop interface was built with Electron JS and FastAPI, enabling real-time image visualization, segmentation overlay, and annotation tools. Usability testing with healthcare professionals using the System Usability Scale (SUS) yielded a mean score of 84.5, indicating excellent user satisfaction and interface effectiveness. STELLA.ai significantly reduced lesion detection time while maintaining diagnostic accuracy, addressing a critical need for rapid, accessible stroke evaluation in low-resource settings. The system offers scalable potential for integration into hospital workflows and expansion to other brain pathologies. Its successful implementation supports the role of artificial intelligence in enhancing diagnostic efficiency and equity in Philippine healthcare. Future work includes DICOM integration, pilot deployment, and model adaptation for broader neurological applications.

title: Supply Chain Resilience Optimization Method Based on Adaptive Multi-Objective Genetic Algorithm
authors: Xiaorong Jiang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: Supply chain resilience has become a critical issue in the context of global disruptions such as natural disasters, pandemics, and geopolitical conflicts. Traditional optimization methods often fail to balance multiple conflicting objectives, such as minimizing operational costs, improving recovery speed, and reducing environmental impact. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a supply chain resilience optimization method based on an Adaptive Multi-Objective Genetic Algorithm (AMOGA). The proposed method integrates adaptive crossover and mutation operators to dynamically adjust evolutionary parameters according to population diversity and convergence status. A multi-objective optimization model is constructed, incorporating resilience indicators such as redundancy, robustness, and recovery capability, while simultaneously considering economic and environmental performance. The algorithm generates a set of Pareto-optimal solutions, enabling decision-makers to analyze trade-offs among resilience, cost, and sustainability. Extensive simulation experiments under various disruption scenarios demonstrate that the proposed AMOGA achieves better convergence, diversity, and robustness compared to classical Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) and traditional single-objective approaches. The results indicate that AMOGA not only improves the adaptability of supply chain structures under uncertainty but also provides enterprises with more effective strategies for resilience enhancement and risk management. This study contributes to the development of intelligent decision-support tools for supply chain risk management and offers theoretical insights into the design of resilient and sustainable supply chains in a dynamic environment.

title: Optimization and Scheduling of Integrated Energy Systems Considering Source-Load Uncertainty
authors: Chen Pang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy and flexible loads introduces significant uncertainties to the Integrated Energy System (IES). How to overcome such uncertainties, make efficient and accurate scheduling decisions, and maintain the supplydemand balance of the entire energy system is a key challenge that the academic community is striving to address. Based on this source-load uncertainty, this paper proposes an improved Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) reinforcement learning method with dynamic noise balancing. Firstly, a mathematical model for IES scheduling is established by considering traditional power and heating supply equipment as well as flexible resources on both the source and load sides. Secondly, the IES scheduling problem is described as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), and the state space, action space, and reward function are specifically defined according to the system structure and optimization objectives. Finally, an improved TD3 algorithm based on dynamic balance of exploration noise is proposed to solve the optimized scheduling approach presented in this paper. Comparative experimental results show that the proposed algorithm reduces the economic cost by 2.36 % and 1.19 % compared with the DDPG and original TD3 algorithms, respectively. Additionally, it exhibits faster convergence speed, higher solution accuracy, and better adaptability to uncertain environments.

title: Design of Digital Control Equalization System for Ka-Band Traveling Wave Tube Amplifier
authors: YaLi Ma, YinXing Chen, Li Qiv, YiXue Wei
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: A digitally controlled equalization system solution for traveling wave tube amplifiers (TWTA) was designed, addressing the current problem of digital step attenuator (DSA) being unable to be used in TWTA. A circuit module with gain control, amplification, and shaping was designed, adapted for analog-to-digital conversion at the TWTA RF input signal. Multisim circuit simulation was used to test the circuit's performance, ensuring accurate and real-time control. A lowlevel system control program was written in C language using Keil5, performing frequency calculation, human-computer interaction, output control, and resource optimization. Finally, the system was built, and excitation signals of different frequencies and dynamic ranges were input and their data were recorded. Experimental results show that this scheme can be used to apply DSA to traveling wave tube amplifiers, with an overall delay of less than 2. 1 m s and a frequency error of less than 0. 0 2%. This scheme has strong practicality and development prospects in traveling wave tube amplifiers, and promotes the digitalization of traveling wave tube amplifiers.

title: Islanding Detection Using Optimized BI-LSTM Classifier with CWT for Microgrid Systems
authors: Bindu Vadlamudi, Ganta Srilakshmi, Yara Nagamalleswara Rao, Chirla satyanarayana Reddy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Today, identifying island conditions is a significant challenge for reliable and safe operation of microgrids which are integrated with Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) such as wind and photovoltaic systems. If island conditions are not identified accurately and quickly, it can cause severe damage to equipment and to utility workers who are working on the grid. This paper presents a novel intelligent classification method for detecting islanding in microgrids using a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BI-LSTM) network. In this method, Feature extraction is conducted through the continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT), and optimal classifier weights are tuned using the whale optimization Algorithm (WOA). To identify the reliability of the proposed classifier it was evaluated under various operating scenarios which includes asymmetrical faults like line to ground (L-G), double line to ground (LL-G)and line to line (LL). In addition system voltage and current signals are tested under varying levels of noise which are measured by using signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Although SNR is tradition- ally associated with communication systems, in this context, it serves as a metric to simulate noisy environments and assess classifier performance. These faults help the proposed model for reliable islanding detection. The proposed method was tested using the IEEE 33-bus distribution test system. It aimed to detect islanding and non-islanding events during different fault conditions. MATLAB/Simulink is implemented to confirm the proposed model performance over conventional techniques, with improved accuracy in detecting both islanding and non-islanding states.

title: IoT - Based Smart Meter Attack Detection Using N - BaIoT Dataset
authors: Khushi D. Gor, Mayur H. Doshi, Yash D. Mehta, Keval N. Mehta, Saurav Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Smart meters are key to smart grids and allow real-time monitoring and management of energy flow and use. They are however also subject to severe internet security threats such as Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) caused by botnets. In this paper, we propose an IoT-based attack detection framework to prevent such threats using smart meters. This study uses traffic data from the N-BaIoT dataset that consists of benign and malicious traffic produced by the Gafgyt and Mirai botnets to train our machine learning and deep learning models to identify normal and malicious smart meter activity. The outcomes indicate that our method yields a high rate of detection even with small false alarm rates, making it an efficient technique for enhancing reliability and strength in the security of smart meters in smart grid installations.

title: ML-Based Data-Driven Prediction and Sensor Placement Optimization for Thermal Safety in Electric Vehicle Batteries
authors: Aishvaria Gorityala, Sudha Radhika
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries are widely used in electric vehicles and stationary storage systems, but their safety is often compromised by localized overheating that can progress into thermal runaway. Conventional protection relies on densely placed temperature sensors, which increases wiring complexity, cost, and potential points of failure. This work proposes an integrated machine-learning approach that simultaneously identifies the most informative sensor locations and enhances fault detection accuracy using virtual temperature estimation. Various sensor-selection strategies-including mutual information analysis, permutation-based importance ranking, and active-learning-driven refinement-are examined to determine the minimum number of physical sensors required for reliable monitoring. To compensate for reduced hardware instrumentation, a hybrid convolutional and recurrent neural network is used to reconstruct temperatures at unsensed locations, enabling early detection of abnormal thermal behavior. Experiments conducted on laboratory thermalcycling data, supplemented with simulated abuse scenarios, demonstrate that the method can retain accuracy above 95% using only four to six thermistors instead of a full sensing array. This configuration lowers wiring requirements by over 40 % and identifies abnormal heating up to two minutes earlier than threshold-based methods. The approach is suitable for both newly manufactured packs and retrofit applications in EV batteries or charging-station storage units. The results confirm reliable detection of both single-cell and multi-cell thermal events, robustness under ambient variations of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and practical suitability for retrofitted EV battery packs and charging-station storage systems.

title: Research on PV-ESS Synergistic Strategy Considering Photovoltaic Uncertainty
authors: Jun Zhuang, Kuo Zhang, Jianliang Cai, Ruiyan Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Addressing the coupling issues of PV -storage systems in multi-scenario scheduling within electricity markets, this paper proposes a PV-ESS synergistic strategy considering photovoltaic uncertainty. Firstly, based on the uncertainty analysis of photovoltaic output, a model for energy storage to smooth PV fluctuations is constructed. Secondly, combined with electricity price uncertainty, a synergistic strategy for energy storage systems to participate in the electricity market is proposed. Finally, through a multi-timescale synergistic framework, the charging and discharging schedule of the energy storage system is optimized by comprehensively considering the objectives of smoothing PV fluctuations, maximizing revenue in the energy market, and maximizing revenue in the frequency regulation market. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy can effectively smooth PV fluctuations and achieve maximized economic benefits in both the energy and frequency regulation markets. Specifically, while smoothing PV fluctuations, the energy storage system generates profit in the energy market through a “store low and discharge high” strategy and provides flexible ancillary services in the frequency regulation market, significantly enhancing the system's economy and stability.

title: The Impact of Smart Lighting Systems on Residential Sustainability and Evaluation through the Smart Readiness Indicator
authors: Theodora Fassa, Christina Skandali, Zoe Gareiou, Lambros T. Doulos
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Smart technologies in residential environments can contribute to the optimization of both energy efficiency and quality of life. In this context, the present study investigates the role of smart lighting systems in the interaction between energy performance and user experience. The Smart Readiness Indicator (SRI) is applied to evaluate the smart lighting capabilities of a residential case study and to provide with insights into the role of lighting within the SRI framework.

title: A Design of Electric Control Brake Unit for Intelligent Delivery Vehicle on Campus
authors: Ziqi Wang, Wei Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper begins with a brief outline of the necessity and industry prospects associated with the application of electro-mechanical brake (EMB) systems in campus delivery vehicles. A detailed description is then provided of the design methodology, braking data calculations, and control procedures developed for the EMB system of an intelligent campus transport vehicle. Although limitations are imposed on the physical implementation by the constraints of the transport vehicle itself, this work is intended to serve as a useful reference for the design and research of EMB systems.

title: Computational Approaches for Emotion, Depression, and Suicidal Detection in Hinglish Text
authors: Jasreen, Anshika Yadav, Jiya Kumari, Nirma Peter, Isha Malhotra, Nidhi Goel
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: The growing prominence of code-mixed languages, particularly Hindi-English, on social media renders the detection of mental health concerns using Natural Language Processing (NLP) progressively more difficult. This proposed research explores recent computational methods developed to identify emotions, depression, and suicidal thoughts in code-mixed Hinglish social media posts. Existing literature indicates a modest but growing interest in this subject, along with a significant gap in publicly available datasets and multilingual model robustness. The examined approaches are divided into three categories: classical Machine Learning (ML), Deep learning (DL), and transformer-based models, and their performance, dataset characteristics, and limitations are discussed. Recent transformer models, including Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) and IndicBERT are currently the most accurate, achieving up to 97 % accuracy for depression detection and 98.54 % for suicidal intent detection, while hybrid DL models such as Convolutional Neural Network-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) is the best at detecting emotions with an accuracy of 83.21 % on Hinglish data. This study makes a contribution by combining disparate research, assessing comparative performance, and highlighting the significance of context-sensitive and culturally aware NLP systems for mental health detection in multilingual settings.

title: Multipath Angle-Based 3D Indoor Positioning System by Integrating 5G mmWave and Vision
authors: Dayu Yan, Yige Li, Haotian Dai, Xiaohan Yin, Ya Kong, Dongkai Yang, Guifei Jing
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) technologies based on 5G millimeter wave have witnessed extensive development. However, when focusing on indoor positioning scenarios, TOA-based 5 G positioning is constrained by time synchronization requirements, while AOA and AOD-based 5G angular positioning techniques are limited by factors such as base station quantity and multipath interference, failing to fully leverage the sensing capabilities of single base stations and multipath reflections. This paper proposes a multipath angle-based 3D positioning system that combines 5 G and visual measurements. By employing targeted beamforming to enhance multipath channel sensing performance and narrowing the search space for super-resolution methods, it effectively improves both positioning accuracy and efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves positioning accuracy by over 50 % compared to classical 5G angle-based positioning methods, which provides technical support for both research and application of 5 G mmWave-based positioning systems.

title: Mitigating Bias in Educational Assessments Using AI-Powered Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Kumar P, S. Thanga Ramya, B. Kalpana, Senthil Pandi S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is increasingly being integrated into educational assessments to support automated essay scoring, adaptive testing, performance prediction, and personalized feedback systems. While these technologies promise efficiency, scalability, and data-driven insights, they also risk reinforcing systemic inequities when underlying data or algorithms embed bias related to gender, ethnicity, socioeconomic status, or disability. Algorithmic bias in educational contexts can lead to unfair evaluations, reduced opportunities, and long-term adverse impacts on vulnerable learners. Recent studies emphasize that fairness in AI-powered assessment is not merely a technical challenge but also an ethical and societal imperative, demanding comprehensive strategies to ensure equity and transparency. This research addresses the critical challenge of bias in AI-powered educational assessments by proposing a holistic, multi-stage framework that integrates fairness-aware machine learning techniques with human-in-the-loop oversight. The proposed system is designed as a modular pipeline to rebalance datasets through re-sampling, re-weighting, and synthetic augmentation. Furthermore, the integration of causal visualization and auditing tools increased interpretability, empowering educators to identify biased features (e.g., attendance patterns correlated with socioeconomic disadvantage) and take corrective action. This study contributes threefold: first, by synthesizing state-of-the-art fairness techniques into a cohesive framework for educational assessments; second, by incorporating intersectional fairness and human oversight mechanisms often overlooked in prior works; and third, by empirically demonstrating that fairness and accuracy can co-exist within practical machine learning systems for education. The findings underline that equitable AI-driven assessment requires not only technical innovation but also institutional adoption of transparent, participatory practices. This research lays the groundwork for ethical, scalable, and bias-resilient AI tools that promote fairness and inclusivity in education.

title: SaveVision: AI-Based Tool for Detecting Cryptographic Vulnerabilities in Mobile Applications
authors: Sangeeta Mittal, Shreyash Gajbhiye, Sneha Arora
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Mobile applications are integral to managing sensitive user data; however, many fail to implement strong cryptographic practices, resulting in vulnerabilities classified under the OWASP Mobile Top 10, specifically Insufficient Cryptography (M10). This paper introduces “SaveVision”, an AI-based tool for the detection of cryptographic weaknesses in mobile apps. A total of 48 widely-used applications across five app categories namely, Social Media, Shopping, Gaming, Entertainment, and Productivity were analyzed. The study identified safety issues such as the reliance on outdated encryption algorithms and poor key management strategies. Every application exhibited at least one M10-related vulnerability, with weak encryption and insecure data storage being the most common. This paper presents a detailed app-specific risk assessment and demonstrates how SaveVision can be independently employed to evaluate the cryptographic integrity of any new mobile application prior to installation.

title: A Fault Detection Method for Low-Voltage Transformer Areas Based on Wide-Area Response Parameters of Active Distribution Network
authors: Wenxuan Chen, Jianyue Man, Guangjun Hao, Guorong Xu, Jiarong Zhu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of distributed generators (DGs), fault detection in transformer areas of active distribution networks is facing new challenges. Traditional local protection exhibits insufficient sensitivity and protection blind spots due to transformer impedance isolation and the impact of high-impedance faults. To address this issue, this paper proposes a fault detection and location method for transformer areas based on wide-area response parameters in active distribution networks. First, by analyzing the synchronicity and distribution proportion of voltage sags at multiple grid connection points, the existence of a fault and its corresponding line are preliminarily determined. Subsequently, at the line level, a fault location parameter integrating the power leap rate, power fluctuation amplitude, and voltage sag rate is constructed. Based on the spatiotemporal distribution characteristics of this parameter along the line, the theoretical fault distance is determined through polynomial fitting. Finally, by combining the theoretical fault distance with the RMS current values at the feeder outlets of each transformer area, the fault determination index is calculated to achieve precise location of the faulty transformer area. Theoretical analysis and case studies demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively detect and locate transformer area faults, particularly in complex scenarios such as highimpedance faults, thereby enhancing the sensitivity and reliability of protection in active distribution networks.

title: Unmasking Brigading: A Network-Based Framework for Detecting Coordinated Manipulation on Reddit
authors: Aswinth Narayan A, Heman Sakkthivel M. S, Nandakumar S. Murali, Lekshmi S. Nair
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Brigading, where large groups manipulate sentiment or voting behavior across subreddits, poses a significant threat to the integrity of online platforms such as Reddit. These actions extend beyond trolling and involve complex sociotechnical interactions among communities. Grounded in the theory of symbolic boundaries, this study examines how brigading emerges structurally through clustered subreddit interactions and synchronized behavioral patterns. A computational framework is proposed using social network analysis, in which subreddit interactions are modeled as hyperlink graphs augmented with temporal and sentiment metadata. Manipulation patterns are detected and visualized in near real-time by identifying downvote bursts, sentiment alignment, and subreddit centrality. Clustering algorithms, i.e., Louvain, Girvan-Newman, and DBSCAN, are employed to uncover latent community structures often exploited for coordinated attacks. Results indicate that brigading is not a statistical anomaly but a systematic effort to breach intercommunity boundaries. To mitigate these effects, a dynamic rate-limiting mechanism is introduced, adjusting user restrictions based on the severity of the attack, the subreddit's sensitivity, and the user's prior behavior. The proposed approach contributes to maintaining platform integrity by highlighting boundary violations and enabling adaptive, real-time interventions.

title: Robustness Comparison of Scalar and Vector Control Methods in Solar-Powered Sensorless Induction Motor Applications
authors: Anand Vardhan Pandey, Anjanee Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In this research, three various methods of control Volt/Hz control, rotor flux control, and direct torque control are evaluated for a system that delivers water which employs solar photovoltaic (SPV) arrays and induction motor drive (IMD). To ensure smooth operation, the system is designed in two stages. The first stage uses a boost converter that increases the solar photovoltaic array output voltage by dynamically modifying the duty ratio and enhance power extraction utilizing the incremental conductance method. Conversely, the second stage- DC to AC conversion for IMD-is attached to the water pumping system. Scalar control is quite easy to utilize; however, it possesses dependent torque and speed characteristics and poor dynamic flexibility. Vector control (FOC and DTC) has been utilized to enhance dynamic performance in a bid to offset scalar control's weak dynamic adaptability. Similar to a DC machine that is excited individually, the vector control provides independent torque-speed performance. The system's performance is carefully evaluated in a variety of dynamic situations, torque ripples, stator current ripples, flux response, etc. To perform the study, MATLAB/SIMULINK software models a 2.4 kW of SPV array is taken for, 2.2 kW IM fed VSI, and results shows that the corresponding usefulness of the different control strategies for solar PV water pumping systems is provided.

title: Research on Rare Image Sample Generation in Power Field Based on Flux. 1 Model
authors: Tianguang Yang, Gaoyang Zhang, Xiaona Jiang, Zixuan Wang, Hao Cui, Feng Zhao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the deep penetration and wide application of artificial intelligence technology in the power industry, security violation detection and equipment defect detection based on computer vision have become the key technical path to improve the intelligent level of power operation and maintenance. The performance of such detection tasks is highly dependent on highquality labeled samples. However, some scenes are sporadic, dangerous, or limited by shooting conditions, resulting in the scarcity of high-quality samples. In order to solve the problem of sample scarcity, this paper proposes a method of generating rare image samples in the power field based on flux. 1 model, which can automatically generate high-quality rare image samples when the real samples are scarce, provide a feasible scheme for the generation of rare samples in the power field, alleviate the sample dependence dilemma of artificial intelligence model, and further promote the application of intelligent detection technology in power safety production.

title: Qualitative Failure and Error Accumulation of Quasi-Dynamic Equilibrium Induced by the “Travel Time Paradox”*
authors: Qingliang Liu, Wenzhong Weng, Xiaomin Dai
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: The Quasi-Dynamic Traffic Assignment (SDTAQ) model is a crucial tool for balancing the stability of Static Traffic Assignment (STA) with the accuracy of Dynamic Traffic Assignment (DTA). However, the cost calculation formula relied upon by its mainstream framework has been identified as having a “non-separability” defect, known as the “travel time paradox,” which theoretically undermines the uniqueness of the equilibrium solution. Although this paradox has been identified theoretically, its practical impacts have not yet been sufficiently quantified. To address this, this study designs a rigorous comparative experiment in the Sioux Falls network, constructing a time-varying congestion scenario spanning three periods (peak onset, congestion accumulation, and congestion dissipation) to systematically evaluate the performance differences between the “non-separable” formula (Formula A) and a corrected “separable” formula (Formula B) under Stochastic User Equilibrium (SUE) conditions. The results demonstrate that the “travel time paradox” is not merely a theoretical flaw but also leads to severe qualitative errors in practical application. During the congestion accumulation period (T2), Formula A exhibits significant failure when processing multibottleneck paths, calculating an unreasonable travel time of 35.28 hours for a critical alternative route (Path 2162) (compared to 18.92 hours from Formula B). This error further causes Formula A to incorrectly determine the assigned flow on this feasible path as zero. Furthermore, this error possesses characteristics of interperiod propagation and accumulation: the total network delay (VHD) is underestimated by 3.0 % during T2, and due to state dependency, this underestimation widens to 7.0 % in T3. This study is the first to quantitatively reveal the severe impact of the “travel time paradox” on SUE solutions, recommending the adoption of the corrected “separable” cost formula in future SDTAQ model research and application to ensure theoretical consistency and simulation accuracy.

title: Glow Discharge of a Plasma Generator as a Tool for Ensuring the Quality of the Surface Layer of a Material
authors: Vladimir A. Logvin, Leonid M. Chervyakov, Natalia A. Bychkova, Svetlana V. Aleksandrova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The article considers devices for monitoring and controlling the technological parameters of glow discharge plasma generators, which ensure their sufficient operability for forming substantial bonds of a wide range of mechanical engineering products. An analysis of various methods for ensuring the quality of the surface layer of the material of mechanical engineering products is performed, and a ranking criterion is proposed according to the possibilities of their use in production. The process of forming substantial bonds of a product and methods for ensuring the required parameters of these bonds are described, depending on both the parameters of the glow discharge in a particular operation and on the organizational interaction of these operations during the process. Attention is paid to both the rational use of strengthening methods and to compliance with technological discipline when using the glow discharge of a plasma generator.

title: A Data Fusion Framework for Durability Evaluation of New Energy Vehicles Under Intelligent Driving Scenarios
authors: Xiaodong Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: This paper proposes a Data Fusion Framework for Durability Evaluation (DFDE) for new energy vehicles (NEVs) operating under intelligent driving scenarios. The DFDE integrates multi-modal signals from CAN, IMU, and LiDAR sensors and combines a CNN-LSTM temporal encoder with a Dynamic Bayesian Network (DBN) state estimator. Unlike conventional perception-oriented fusion systems, DFDE explicitly models the long-term dependency and stochastic degradation processes inherent in structural fatigue prediction. Experimental results show that DFDE achieves a 18.6% accuracy improvement in fatigue life prediction compared with baseline methods while maintaining real-time feasibility on embedded ECUs.

title: Dynamic Partition SMC and MRAS Parameter Identification for PMSM
authors: Jialei Ren, Huibo Liu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Addressing the issues of poor interference resistance in traditional PI speed controllers and high chattering in conventional sliding mode speed controllers for permanent magnet synchronous motor speed control, this paper proposes a strategy combining a dynamic partitioning adaptive sliding mode controller with MRAS parameter identification. First, a segmentally adjustable fractional-power sliding mode surface is constructed. By introducing a dynamic partitioning mechanism triggered by an error threshold, the objective optimization of achieving rapid convergence in large error regions and suppressing oscillation in small error regions is realized; a hybrid nonlinear gain function is designed to enhance robustness in large error regions. Simultaneously, a stepwise MRAS parameter identification strategy is adopted, replacing the traditional PI structure with fractional-order sliding mode control to enhance the identification accuracy and dynamic response capability of speed, resistance, and inductance parameters. Simulation results based on MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that compared to traditional control methods, the proposed control algorithm exhibits significant advantages in reducing chatter, improving convergence speed, and enhancing dynamic tracking accuracy, thereby offering high engineering application value.

title: Research on Key Technique Requirements of Standard Digitalization for New Power System
authors: Liyuan Wu, Xiaotong Zhang, Guang Zhang, Xiaodi Xie, Tao Deng, Sining Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the transition to low-carbon and digital transformation of power system, the digitalization of power standards can better fulfill their role in guiding development and facilitating integration, thereby supporting the construction of new power system. This paper focuses on the developmental trend of technical standards under new power system, analyzing key technologies for standard digitalization across four dimensions: standard development digitalization, standard content digitalization, standard implementation digitalization, and standard system planning digitalization. The analysis covers domestic and international standards, addressing core challenges, application scenarios, relevant organizations, and digitalization levels. Building on this foundation, the study identifies critical technological requirements for power sector standard digitalization, providing valuable references to accelerate the transformation and development of power system.

title: Investigation of the Effect of Different Activation Modes of KOH on the Electrochemical Properties of Water Hyacinth Biocarbon
authors: Hongli Ye, Ziyan Li, Yufei Zhou, Jianing Wang, Meiyan Li, Yan Bai
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Electrode materials with both excellent electrochemical performance and cost-effectiveness are urgently needed for the development of high-power energy storage systems. In this study, hyacinth-based biocarbon materials were prepared using a high-temperature calcination method with hyacinth as the research object. The effects of KOH activation on the electrochemical properties of hyacinth-based biochar materials prepared by immersion activation and milling activation were investigated. The results showed that the water hyacinth biochar materials prepared by milling activation assembled into electrochemical energy storage devices had the lowest equivalent series resistance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{Rs} \approx 0 \Omega)$</tex> and Rct, which ensured the minimization of energy loss and fast ion transport kinetics, exhibited excellent capacitive properties (hybrid bilayer/pseudocapacitive energy storage mechanism) corrosion resistance, and low charge-transfer resistance, which were attributed to their optimized pore structure and oxygen-functionalized surface.

title: The Impact of Digitalization on the Academic Environment
authors: Violetta V. Petrova, Ludmila I. Shramko
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: Academic successfulness of students is one of the indicators of efficiency and quality of the educational process. The article focuses on the academic environment as one of the factors affecting academic successfulness of students. It is acknowledged that due to digitalization all the elements of the academic environment such as infrastructural, societal and axiological have undergone significant changes which, in turn, have had an impact on the efficiency of the academic process and the quality of the educational result. The authors provide some research results on the attitudes of STEM students towards digitalization of various elements of the academic environment, give examples of positive and negative effects of digital innovations, analyze some consequences of introducing digitalization into educational practices and teaching and learning strategies and practices. The authors point out to the importance of the role of the teacher in estimating the effects of innovations introduced into students' learning and academic activities for the proper development of their cognitive and professional competences in the conditions of digitalization.

title: Between Innovation and Oversight: A Cross-Regional Study of AI Risk Management Frameworks in the EU, U.S., UK, and China
authors: Amir Al-Maamari, Abdulatif Alabdultif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) technologies increasingly enter critical sectors like healthcare, transportation, and finance, developing effective governance frameworks is crucial for managing ethical, security, and societal risks. This paper conducts a comparative analysis of AI risk management strategies across the European Union (EU), United States (U.S.), United Kingdom (UK), and China. Using a multi-method qualitative approach, we investigate how these regions classify AI risks, implement compliance, structure oversight, and respond to innovation. Findings from high-risk contexts demonstrate the advantages and limitations of different regulatory models. The EU implements a structured, risk-based framework prioritizing transparency, while the U.S. uses decentralized, sector-specific regulations that promote innovation but risk fragmented enforcement. The UK's flexible strategy facilitates agile responses but may lead to inconsistent coverage, whereas China's centralized directives allow rapid implementation while constraining public oversight. These insights highlight the need for AI regulation that is globally informed yet context-sensitive, balancing effective risk management with technological progress. We conclude with policy recommendations for enhancing effective, adaptive, and inclusive AI governance globally.

title: Evaluating Photovoltaic Systems in Gujarat: A Comparative Study of Annual Energy Output and LCoE Computation
authors: Dhyey Makwana, Vasu Vekariya, Siddharth Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Renewable energy sources are known to have Optimistic potential in the future. These resources provide overflowing amounts of energy in the form of solar rays. In Today's life, keeping in sight the increasing load demand and also the greenhouse effect, it has the proficiency to fulfill all the required demands. This article generally studies the PV system in the commercial sector of two different locations. By various configurations of PV systems, we want to propose the Levelized Cost of Energy (LCoE), Power Distribution, and Annual Power Generation. Further to clarify the results from the PV system, we will be comparing the data obtained from two different locations (i.e., Surat and Rajkot). Thus, from the obtained data, we would be able to see the noticeable changes in the Global Irradiance and the Annual Power Generation of two different cities in the same state. The analysis from this article will help the commercial sector maximize its power output and also help reduce power inefficiencies due to the weather conditions at two different locations. This research paper demonstrates the performance model of a 2 MW Commercial PV system.

title: Application of Covariance-Optimized Multi-Sensor Fusion Technology for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Jun Zheng, Bo Jiang, Shuangshaung Zhou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In terms of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) navigation systems, accurate state estimation is highly dependent on the error-state Kalman filter (ESKF) algorithm. With the increase in system state dimensionality, however, the computational complexity of covariance matrices, coupled with their storage requirements, rises sharply, thus posing challenges to real-time applications. To address this issue, an optimized covariance computation method based on sparse matrix techniques and incremental updates is proposed to significantly improve the efficiency of covariance updates. Experimental results indicate that this method is capable of maintaining the accuracy and achieving real-time performance of the ESKF algorithm on the low-cost embedded platform by greatly reducing the computational time and memory occupation. Simply put, the proposed method enhances the real-time capabilities of UAV navigation systems.

title: Development of Digital Models of Maintenance Processes at Wagon Repair Plants
authors: Andrey S. Shinkaruk, Anatoly A. Rogov, Ivan A. Vishnyakov, Elena V. Mitryukhina
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The article is devoted to the problem of ensuring the quality of maintenance and repair of the park of railway passenger wagons based on the management of production processes using information technology. To increase the efficiency of maintenance, the method of digital modeling of the processes of operation and repair of passenger railway wagons, based on the monitoring of their current state, collecting and accumulating information on basic technical parameters, is used. To improve the quality of the wagon repair, it is proposed to integrate the results of the check and diagnosis into a single digital product (digital wagon passport), which allows you to control the implementation of all technological repair operations.

title: Interleaved Carrier-Based PWM for Enhanced CMV Performance in Three-Phase Parallel-Connected IOBCs
authors: Mandvi Singh, Suvendu Samanta, Shyama Prasad Das
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: A common-mode voltage reduction technique for a three-phase parallel-connected non-isolated onboard integrated charger is proposed in this article. In a non-isolated charger, the common-mode voltage is detrimental to the circuit since it induces leakage current through parasitic capacitors. To reduce common-mode voltage, the interleaved modulation technique is employed in conjunction with sinusoidal pulse-width modulation and space vector pulse-width modulation techniques. In the proposed novel CMV reduction, the charging is utilized with large voltage vectors with minimum CMV. The CMV for both the techniques is limited to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm V_{d c} / 6$</tex> The modulation techniques are utilized here in a scalar manner. The modulation techniques are utilized here in a scalar manner. A detailed analysis of the common-mode voltage reduction technique is presented through a switching state diagram. All 64 voltage vectors are presented in the charging domain to make an informed selection of the common mode voltage. The harmonic distortion factor calculation based on the volt-second balance is also shown to show the effect of the interleaved modulation technique on phase current harmonics. The scaled-down lab prototype is developed for the proposed system, and the concept is validated with the exhaustive hardware results.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)

title: Exploring the Agriculture-ICT Intersection: A Perspective Study on Technological Adoption
authors: Jyoti, Sahil
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Agriculture, main source of food supply to the world, chief factor of economic growth, and the provider of the livelihood of the rural areas, is being increasingly improved by the deliberate incorporation of Information and Communication Technologies (ICT). The adoption of technologies has found to be quite imperative in the solving of problems that have been whether variability, resource scarcity, declining productivity, and the rising of demand for advanced technologies in agriculture. Recognizing the importance of ICT in agriculture, this study conducts a perspective analysis through a bibliometric review of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 5 8 9}$</tex> Scopus-indexed publications, published between 2000 and 2024 to ascertain the annual growth in research publications and citation patterns in the field of ICT-enabled agriculture. The study employs MS Excel and VOSviewer to analyze and identify the major ICT-enabled agriculture key areas including the technological enablers viz. Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Remote Sensing (RS), and Fog Computing etc, various agricultural areas and turns agriculture into extremely capable ones which include precision farming, automated monitoring, crop and soil management, livestock management, climate forecasting, pest and disease control, irrigation management, and disaster management. The findings depicts that ICT leads to a fundamental change in the manner that farmers, researchers, and policymakers can take timely, data-driven decisions which will result in increased productivity, lessened environmental impacts, and enhanced weather related risk resilience. By integrating literature, putting key areas and technological enablers into focus, this study offers a comprehensive understanding of ICT-enabled agriculture. It is a good platform for facilitating technology adoption, research growth that will lead to the creation of efficient, and ICT-enabled agriculture.

title: Attitude Tracking Control of VS System based on Adaptive FOPID Control Technique
authors: Akshaya Kumar Patra, Narayan Nahak, Girija Sankar Panigrahi, Manoj Kumar Debnath, Ramachandra Agrawal, Alok Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This study presents a FOPID controller for a Vehicle Suspension System (VSS), optimized using the Aquila Optimization (AO) algorithm. The primary objective is to enhance ride comfort by reducing vibrations and disturbances caused by uneven road surfaces. The VSS is modeled as a fourth-order system, which serves as the basis for designing the AO-FOPIDC. By integrating the AO algorithm, the conventional FOPID controller is refined to deliver improved control performance. The effectiveness of the proposed AOFOPIDC is validated through comparative analysis against several established control techniques. Results demonstrate that the approach successfully maintains the VSS dynamics within a stable operating range, offering improved precision, stability, and robustness.

title: Improved ACE Calculation Strategy for Highproportion Renewable Energy Power Systems
authors: Weihua Luo, Li He, Qiang Lan, Yongchao Sun, Chuanzhi Xie, Guangyuan Shi, Yongqiang Chen, Liang Ding
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: In recent years, the installed capacity of clean energy sources such as wind power and photovoltaic in China's power system has continued to rise, gradually becoming the primary energy form for domestic power supply and consumption. China's power system is progressively evolving toward a trend characterized by multiple DC transmission lines and a high proportion of clean energy. The unpredictable output characteristics of clean energy significantly exacerbate grid instability, with the frequency stability of the power system being particularly severely affected. Traditional ACE methods heavily rely on the precise estimation of frequency response characteristics, a prerequisite that is difficult to achieve accurately in power systems with high renewable energy penetration. To address the frequency regulation needs of such systems, this paper proposes a control strategy based on improved area control error. Unlike traditional ACE methods that depend on frequency offset factors, the IACE approach significantly enhances robustness against system parameter uncertainties by directly measuring generator power output and integrating a load damping compensation mechanism. To validate this strategy, comprehensive simulation analyses were conducted on a two-area system with renewable energy penetration rates of 57.1% and 53.3% in each area. Simulation results demonstrate that the IACE-based control scheme outperforms traditional ACE methods in frequency regulation performance compliance rates. This control approach provides a practical technical solution for automatic frequency regulation in future renewable-dominated power systems.

title: Enhanced Energy Efficiency in EV Batteries Through Fuzzy Logic and PI Controller Integration
authors: Bhabasis Mohapatra, Ritesh Dash, Renu Sharma, Binod Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This research compares the performance of two control methods i.e. classical PI controller and Fuzzy-PI controller for managing energy in electric scooters. The goal is to check how well these controllers can keep the DC voltage stable, manage battery current and control the battery state of charge during different driving conditions such as acceleration, crushing and braking. The analysis is done using simulations with standard driving cycles like UDDS and WLTP. The fuzzyPI controller uses adaptive logic that adjusts itself based on systems error and change in error, making it more responsive and accurate. In contrast the PI controller is intentionally made less effective by using Poor gain settings to highlights its weakness. Result shows that Fuzzy-PI controller maintains voltage very close to the desired level while the PI controller shows large deviations which may cause stress to the battery and reduce system safety. Although Fuzzy-PI has a higher peak current, it responds better to load changes and control the system more precisely. The SoC analysis over five cycles shows that the Fuzzy-PI uses more energy compared to PI controller which saves energy but performs poorly. The analysis has been carried out using Matlab Simulink model in order to validate the performance of the controller.

title: Dynamic ML-DL Switching for Smart City Traffic Forecasting Using Predictive–Adaptive Decision Algorithm
authors: Sanjay Suresh, Kichagari Sruthi, V. Uma Maheswari, Satheesh S, M. UmaDevi, P. Jayapriya
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: With the growing of smart cities, more and more attention has been paid to intelligent traffic forecasting systems that are responsive in real time and can make accurate prediction. Standard approaches depend either on light ML models for fast local inference or DL networks for robust multi-step forecasting. However, static dependence on a single paradigm sacrifices between latency, energy and quality of prediction. To tackle this problem, we present new PredictiveAdaptive Decision Algorithm (PADA) that changes dynamically between ML-based edge prediction and DL-based off-site forecasting for smart IOT-supported traffic systems. The proposed algorithm aims to optimally make decisions considering confidence score, network latency, energy level and task difficulty by using the reinforcement learning technique. Extensive experiments on large scale smart traffic datasets show the effectiveness and efficiency of PADA in improving overall forecast accuracy and saving energy cost, inference delay, which is promising for real-time management in smart cities.

title: Breast Cancer Prediction Model: Using AI to Understand and Explain Diagnosis
authors: Rojeena Khadka, Atef Mohamed Shalan, Mohamed Hefny
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The early detection of breast cancer can increase the chances of survival and have a significant impact on reducing treatment costs. While Machine learning can help predict whether a tumor is cancerous or not, these prediction models are often hard for a non-technical person to understand. This paper investigates the performance and interpretability of two Machine learning models-Random Forest Classifier and Logistic Regression -for identifying whether a tumor is cancerous (malignant) or not (benign) in breast cancer classification. These models trained on the Wisconsin breast cancer dataset of tumor characteristics are evaluated based on standard metrics like accuracy, precision, and recall. Subsequently, the implementation of SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) helps interpret feature contributions, enhancing explainability. Upon analyzing the models, Logistic Regression achieved a higher overall accuracy of 95.61 % while it offered greater transparency as well during SHAP analysis as compared to Random Forest Classifier. Overall, this paper emphasizes the potential of combining explainable AIdriven diagnostics in healthcare.

title: Coordinated Control of VSI Dead Angle ($\beta$) and Isolating Transformer Tap Changer for High Power Grid Connected Solar PV Based Power Generation
authors: Madan Gopal Sharma, Subir Karmakar, Bhim Singh
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents a grid-connected solar PV plant using a fundamental frequency switching (FFS) modulated threelevel neutral clamped point (NCP) VSI. Although this approach reduces switching losses and improves AC voltage levels, it introduces significant lower-order harmonics and high THD based on PV DC voltage levels. In order to optimize voltage THD, a coordinated control strategy of VSI dead angle (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>) and isolating transformer on-load tap changer is proposed. This method dynamically adjusts <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> and transformer tap positions in response to PV array voltage variations, ensuring both voltage matching at point of common coupling (PCC) and optimized voltage THD at NCP level. MATLAB simulations confirm stable steady-state and dynamic performance.

title: Program Committee: LESCDT 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)

title: Cloud Native Fraud Detection Frameworks Distributed AI Models for Financial and Insurance Application
authors: Narendra Narne, Aditya Koneru, Sreenivasarao Amirineni
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The rapid development of digital financial services has not only broadened access to online transactions but also made them larger and more complex. This has made the detection of fraud in financial and insurance services a more complex task. The conventional machine learning approach is not capable of identifying complex relationships in data, which can sometimes result in incorrect predictions and a flood of false alarms. To solve this problem, a cloud-based framework for fraud detection is proposed in the study by using a combination of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and LightGBM, which is a Light Gradient Boosting Machine. The study uses artificial data for financial transactions, which is created using a simulator known as PaySim. This simulator is used to mimic real-world transactions in mobile money services, which include transactions, payments, and withdrawals, along with fraudulent transactions. The outcomes of the experiments demonstrate that the presented framework is effective in fraud detection by outperforming conventional machine learning models such as K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Trees, Logistic Regression, and LightGBM. The proposed framework is able to achieve 99.97% accuracy, 99.99% precision, 99.98% recall, and a 99.98% F1 score, with PR AUC and ROC AUC at 0.99 and 0.972, respectively.

title: Deep Transfer Learning Approach for Monkeypox Skin Lesion Classification via VGG16 Architecture
authors: Ramya E, Rajkumar K, Pradhiksha K, Mounesh K, Prakash P, Karthikeyan A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Monkeypox (Mpox) is a recently identified viral infection that shares similarities with other skin ailments, complicating visual diagnosis. Rapid and precise identification of the disease is crucial to ensure prompt treatment and curb further transmission(2). This research employed a deep learning model, based on VGG16, and transfer learning to automatically categorise images of monkeypox skin lesions. A publicly available dataset consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 1 9 2}$</tex> images, including some depicting monkeypox and others that do not, was used for both training and evaluating the model. The findings indicated that the model reached an accuracy of 87.34 %, a recall rate of 96.79 %, a precision rate of 73.02 %, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 83.56 %.The results from training and validation demonstrated reliable convergence with minimal overfitting, indicating effective performance on samples not previously encountered. While the model detected most positive cases effectively, the presence of false positives reduced precision. In future work, we will aim to improve precision by reducing false positives and comparing VGG16 with other architectures like DenseNet121 and EfficientNet to boost diagnostic performance.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Models for Credit Scoring for Risk Forecasting in Big Financial Datasets
authors: Saurabh Kakkar, Bhulakshmi Makkena, Amit Chaudhary, Armaan Kakkar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Risk assessment of credit scoring should be done properly, and towards this end, sustainable lending procedures, particularly during the present digital banking age, wherein a significant portion of financial information is being calculated on a daily schedule. An explainable credit card scores machine learning model based on the XGBoost algorithm, a high-performance tool with excellent accuracy, affordability, and interpretability is presented in this study. The model uses the XGBoost algorithm, chosen for its high predictive performance and scalability. In order to facilitate fairness and interpretability and provide both global and local explanations of model behavior, explainable AI techniques such as SHAP and LIME are included.
