The proposed model achieves 99.71 % accuracy, 99.89 % precision, and 99.0 % F1-score, outperforming sophisticated models like CNN, AdaBoost, and Logistic Regression. The legitimacy of automated loan choices is boosted by a mix of performance and openness, and the model may be successfully applied to actual financial institutions. In general, this paper presents a strong, interpretable, and scalable credit-scoring model that meets the growing need for ethical, data-driven decision-making in the financial market.

title: Battery Energy Storage Supported Three-Phase Grid-Connected PV System
authors: Vineet Gautam, Rachna Garg, Kuldeep Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper evaluates the effectiveness of a dualstage power converter attached to a photovoltaic array with a battery energy storage system (BESS). This configuration includes the power and state controllability of the PV storage plant and increased ability to manage and strengthen the grid and the energy. The system construction consists of an inverter with 3-phases that is grid-synchronized and aC-DC Boost converter to increase the PV voltage. The battery's charging and discharging processes are handled by a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDC), which takes into account the current generation of energy and usage. The proposed control system is designed to ensure the maximum use of the PV output with the use of the maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm at the same time, while maintaining a steady DC-link voltage. The battery met the load's needs when solar generation was low and stored excess energy when production was at a peak, maintaining a stable power balance. Phase synchronization is done via a phase-locked loop (PLL). The outcome of the simulation proves the optimal functioning of the suggested system under different environmental and load dynamics. The combined operation of the solar system and the battery optimises energy usage, helps ensure the grid's stability, and extends the battery lifecycle.

title: A Context-Aware Deep Learning Framework for High-Precision Employee Turnover Forecasting
authors: G. Ramani, Lakshmi Praba V
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Employee turnover poses significant challenges to organizational stability, performance continuity, and talent management efficiency. Traditional predictive models often fail to capture the nuanced influence of behavioral, contextual, and organizational factors underpinning attrition decisions. This paper proposes DeepAttriNet (Deep Context-Aware Attrition Prediction Network), a context-aware deep learning framework designed to enhance employee turnover forecasting accuracy. The framework processes employee attributes, engagement indicators, performance records, and organizational structure variables through parallel encoding networks, allowing it to learn latent relationships and temporal behavior patterns. An attention-based fusion layer then prioritizes influential features, improving interpretability and prediction precision. Experiments conducted on real-world HR datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms conventional machine learning and standard deep neural network classifiers in terms of F1-score, precision, and recall. The model also provides interpretable insights into contextual drivers behind employee turnover, supporting data-informed workforce retention strategies.

title: VisuaQuery: An AI-Powered Natural Language to SQL and Visualization System
authors: Babul Mandal, Aditya Raut, Pranav Hopal, Pushkar Raut, Raunak Kalamkhede, Sanchit Yelne, Sandesh Shinde
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper introduces a Multimodal NLP-to-SQL and Visualization system. It is a Database Interaction System that facilitates database communication through text, image, and voice inputs without SQL knowledge. The system integrates OCR, speech-to-text, and a transformer-based Large Language Model (LLM) to transform user queries to accurate schemaaware SQL statement. A validation layer ensures query safety and correctness before execution. Experimental evaluation demonstrates high query accuracy, low response latency, and robust performance across modalities. The proposed approach improves accessibility, efficiency, and usability of database systems, making data retrieval more inclusive for non-technical users.

title: Agentic AI in Radiology: A Feedback-Driven Multi Agent Framework for Adaptive Report Generation
authors: R. Suganthi, P. Archana, T.P. Suvetha, Uma, R. Bharani
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: One of the tools that radiology offers is medical diagnosis. Medical doctors are able to spot the abnormal parts of the body through chest x-ray images. Moreover, the images are interpreted by radiologists who examine the x-rays visually. However, because the manual image inspection is timeconsuming, it is often accompanied by notification of delay and inconsistency of results. The paper presents a smart AI system that, via a conversational interface, makes the radiology reports automated from chest X-ray images. This system is equipped with a variety of intelligent agents that cooperatively work to improve the effectiveness and adaptability of the system. By using Streamlit interface, the system is able to reach an accuracy rate of 93% with its DenseNet-121 deep learning model. In this setting, the Perception Agent is the one in charge of the analysis of the visual information while the Report Agent employs Google Gemini 1.5 Pro, a large language model, for the generation of the fluently structured radiology reports, upon system input. At first, the system outputs the drafted report to the radiologist. After the doctor verifies the report's correctness, the system proceeds with saving the report. In a case where the doctor makes a request for an amendment, the system keeps the updated version of the report. The Feedback Agent keeps the record of this information and uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with ChromaDB to identify similar past cases. This, in turn, improves the quality of the next reports by giving additional references to the language model. The methodology is designed to lighten the diagnostic workload of radiologists, help medical students become better learners, and be a source of decision-making in regions where there is a shortage of specialists. Ultimately, this leads to better healthcare delivery in terms of efficiency and accessibility.

title: DyRAE: Dynamic Real Time Accountability Framework for Autonomous Ethical Agents
authors: M. Shailaja, Divya Maheshwari, Himanshi Sharma, Bandi Doss
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce a framework for autonomous agents to reason about ethical constraints on their actions and be accountable for them in real time. The system integrates ethical principles with social norms and legal rules within the decision-making process of the agent. It also keeps track of its actions and has self-correcting mechanisms when it suspects potential ethical issues. The framework employs a hybrid model which blends symbolic reasoning with neural components, allowing the agent to maintain adaptability while being explainable. Experiments in simulated high-stakes scenarios demonstrate that the approach preserves ethical behavior without impairing task execution. This indicates that considering dynamic ethics within the decision loop itself can enable trusted and transparent autonomous systems.

title: Design and Monitoring of a Tree-Inspired Vertical Axis Wind Turbine for Efficient Urban Wind Utilization
authors: Basit Ahmad, Syeda Aqsa Jillani, Mudassar Rafique, Aftab Ahmed Soomro
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Urban wind environments are characterized by low and turbulent flow, limiting the performance of conventional horizontal-axis wind turbines that require high and unidirectional wind speeds. This study presents the Wind Tree Mill, a biomimetic vertical-axis wind turbine (VAWT) system inspired by natural tree morphology, designed for efficient energy capture in low-speed urban conditions. Multiple blade profiles were modeled and optimized in SolidWorks to improve aerodynamic performance. Comparative simulation analysis revealed that the optimized curved-leaf blade design achieved a 32.8% increase in starting torque and a 27.4% reduction in drag coefficient relative to the baseline straight-blade Savonius model. Each turbine module is equipped with an independent DC generator, forming a modular micro-generation array capable of scalable deployment. Real-time performance monitoring using LabVIEW showed that, under average urban wind speeds of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, the Wind Tree Mill achieved an average electrical conversion efficiency of 18.6%, outperforming conventional VAWTs by 22% in equivalent wind conditions. Experimental validation demonstrated stable self-starting capability and sustained operation across multidirectional wind inflows. The compact, tree-like structure allows integration into confined commercial and residential spaces, promoting both aesthetic and functional synergy with urban architecture. The results confirm the feasibility of bio-inspired VAWT systems for decentralized renewable power generation and establish a framework for future development of smart, sensor-integrated wind harvesting systems within urban energy networks.

title: Noise-Robust Speech Recognition with Latency-Aware Performance Trade-Offs
authors: Ronit Dahiya, Suresh Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Real-world speech applications require Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models to achieve low error and remain reliable, responsive on clean as well as noisy audio. We create a latency aware evaluation report through a mixed benchmark made using LibriSpeech speaker utterance datasets with seven everyday noise families (babble, music, wind, city, rain, competing speaker, and sudden transient events) across multiple signal-to-noise ratios. We compare models from major ASR ecosystems- wav2vec 2.0 encoders, NVIDIA NeMo conformer CTC and transducer model, and OpenAI Whisper variants. We report both WER and speed metrics including latency and RTF, for each noise condition. We also highlight comparison among three diverse models (Whisper Small, Conformer Transducer Small, and wav2vec 2.0 Base) to show the real speed-accuracyrobustness trade-offs they offer. Manifests, metrics, and scripts are included to support reproducible, noise-aware ASR evaluation.

title: Digital Twin Technology for Smart Project and Risk Management
authors: Oluwafemi John Ajala, Ashwini L K, Maheedhar Vara Prasad Repalle, Meera K L, Revanayya, Krishnamoorthi M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: With this technology, a whole project can be viewed in real time using a digital twin. It is enabling project and risk management intelligence. This article gives an Executive Summary of Developing Digital Twin based IoT, AI, Cloudbased Solution for Risk Mitigation in Complex Projects in Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) sector Project. The system keeps synchronizing the physical information of the site with the virtual data at all times. With the available data, we estimate that some trains will arrive late. Therefore, we will also make deductions from simulation we run on the same. A mid-sized infrastructure project applied a practical approach for implementation. This research garnered better results than regular projects. Schedule compliance enhanced by 21 %. Cost deviation reduced by 17 % (lower). Unscheduled delay drop by 28 % (lesser). The module of risk prediction is AI driven. They can predict risky incidents with a 94.3 % success rate. We use a historical and real-time data to achieve it. Study findings show using a DT improves level of knowledge, effects of action and truthfulness of choice. In other words, it is a game changer for any future intelligent initiative and risk management.

title: Ai Model for Securing Healthcare IoT Systems Against Cyber Threats and Data Breaches
authors: Vikas Kamra, Prakhar Chandra, Aman Pal, Nitin Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This work suggest a framework that maintains privacy and is a system-level framework that secures healthcare IoMT by combining federated anomaly detection with remediation (automatically) and clinical prioritization. Local autoencoders identify device abnormalities and transmit encrypted updates to a central aggregator which ushers in a device-critical weighted FedAvg to focus learning on clinically important assets. An Alerts feed a transformer-based Dynamic Policy Generator (DSPG) which proposes remediation where a Risk Scoring Engine (RSE) prioritizes incidents on clinical impact to enforce the policy human-in-the-loop. The entire pipeline achieves detected 96% performance (weighted F1 = 0.960, AUC = 0.97, FPR = 3.30%) and decreases simulated median time-to-first-action by roughly 18% compared to a detection-only federated baseline (airdried, synthetic data, on a multi-client simulated IoMT prediction test). Average DSPG latency is approximately180 ms as well as 85 percent of sampled generated policies were recognized as correct or satisfactory by domain experts. Findings suggest that decentralized, privacypreserving designs can enhance detection on high-impact devices and fast and prioritized remediation can be provided through clinical settings.

title: Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems Process and Circuit Design Based on a Novel Piezoelectric Printhead Structure
authors: Yi Wang, Chaoran Liu, Qianlong Gao, Zhichao Lin, Zhiyuan Yin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper presents the co-design of a MEMS fabrication process and a compact driving circuit for a novel piezoelectric inkjet printhead. A dual-wafer MEMS process is developed, including nozzle formation, chamber etching, multilayer PZT thin-film deposition, and patterning of both the main actuator and a piezoelectric cutter. COMSOL simulations are used to evaluate the electromechanical behavior of the actuator, showing that multilayer PZT structures provide significantly larger deformation under low driving voltage. The integrated piezoelectric cutter effectively shortens the liquid filament during droplet breakup, thereby suppressing satellite droplet formation. On the electrical side, a programmable trapezoidal-wave generator is designed to provide microsecond-scale driving pulses with controllable slopes and plateau widths. Circuit simulations verify stable waveform generation suitable for high-speed inkjet operation. By jointly optimizing fabrication processes, actuator structure, and waveform characteristics, this work establishes a practical foundation for achieving low-voltage actuation, stable droplet formation, and improved performance in next-generation high-resolution piezoelectric inkjet printheads.

title: Generative Adversarial Networks for High Quality Restoration
authors: Nalladimmu Sasisri, RSumathi, Kunchapu Gowri Sai, Gundlamadugu Siva Kumar, Kapilavai Hanumaan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Enhancement of low resolution and damaged video content is an issue in fields like digital media restoration, surveillance analysis, online content creation, and archival preservation. We developed Real-Enhanced Super Resolution Generative Adversarial Networks (Real-ESRGAN) for real world video degradations. We present a pipeline which takes low quality input videos, puts each frame through the Real-ESRGAN_x4plus model for enhancement and then puts out a high-resolution version of the video and the model does an excellent job at reducing noise, improving compression artifacts, bringing out structural elements and bringing back lost texture. Also, unlike past approaches which were trained only on synthetic degradations incorporated realistic degradation modeling into Real-ESRGAN which in turn enables it to do robustly in the tough real world video capture conditions. Evaluations of different videos show increases in perceptual quality and in the quality metrics of PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS across the videos, ranging from mobile videos, surveillance videos, and overcompressed videos from the Internet. The proposed framework achieved visual improvement consistency, even though it was limited in severe motion blur and temporal consistency across frames. The system illustrates an effective and extensible approach to the enhancement of real-world video quality and provides the groundwork for future motion- and time- aware restoration techniques.

title: Artificial Lemming Algorithm Tuned FOPI-FOPD Controllers for Load Frequency Control in Hybrid Power Systems with Electric Vehicle Integration
authors: Jugajyoti Sahu, Bhabasis Mohapatra, Binod Kumar Sahu, Pradeep Kumar Mohanty, Amiya Kumar Naik
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In modern interconnected power systems, maintaining a stable frequency under varying load and generation conditions is a critical challenge, particularly with the growing integration of renewable energy sources. This study proposes an improved Load Frequency Control (LFC) strategy for a hybrid system incorporating thermal, geothermal, solar photovoltaic, wind, and electric vehicle units. A novel Artificial Lemming Algorithm (ALA) is developed for optimal controller tuning, featuring an energy-decreasing mechanism that dynamically balances exploration and exploitation. This enhances its capability to escape local optima and achieve superior global convergence compared to Sine Cosine Algorithm (SCA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The ALA-based Fractional Order Proportional-Integral and Fractional Order Proportional-Derivative (FOPI-FOPD) controllers are evaluated against SCA-based FOPI-FOPD, PSO-based FOPI-FOPD, and PSO-based PID controllers under load disturbances. Simulation results reveals that the ALA tuned FOPI-FOPD controller provides superior frequency and tie-line power regulation with minimal dynamic deviation. The system achieves the lowest undershoot values of −0.0365 Hz, 0.0136 Hz, and −0.0103 pu, and overshoot values of 0.0004 Hz, 0.0003 Hz, and 0.0011 pu for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{F}_{1}, \Delta \mathrm{F}_{2}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{P}_{\text{tie}}$</tex>, respectively. These findings validate that the proposed ALA based FOPIFOPD outperforms SCA and PSO based FOPI-FOPD, ensuring faster settling time, reduced oscillations, and improved frequency stability, making it a promising solution for modern hybrid power system applications.

title: Empirical Analysis of FedAvg, FedProx and SCAFFOLD for Heterogeneous Data Distributions*
authors: Farwa Ikram, Dipanwita Thakur, Antonella Guzzo, Giancarlo Fortino
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: With the rise of big data and AI advancements, Federated Learning (FL) has gained significant research interest. FL operates on decentralized, often heterogeneous (non-IID) data, posing challenges in distributed databases. While various FL algorithms address non-IID issues, few comprehensively evaluate their impact across different non-IID distributions or propose effective improvements. This paper empirically analyzes FedAvg, FedProx, and SCAFFOLD using two datasets and two non-IID data partitioning schemes-Dirichlet-based and classbased. Our study highlights practical bottlenecks and theoretical insights, concluding with future research directions to enhance FL algorithm performance.

title: Efficiency Improvement of Linear Permanent Magnet Motor for Electric Vehicle (EV) Applications
authors: Sharjeel Mushtaque, Wazeer Ali, Farida Memon, Aamir Memon, Syeda Maryam
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The transportation sector is the second largest energy consumer and is growing every year. Electric vehicles driven by electric motor reduce the usage of fossil fuels. Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) are particularly gaining attention in the Electric Vehicles (EV) world for their advantages over traditional motors, such as higher efficiency, lighter, and more compact. However, machines face difficulties in terms of efficiency, high magnetic attraction forces, cogging force, and mechanical losses, which in turn result inefficient motor performance and demand high entrance. In response to these challenges, we have proposed a translational motor design known as an air-cored linear permanent magnet synchronous motor. To model and analyze our proposed design we used Finite Element Analysis (FEA). The electromagnetic performance is evaluated and main results such as; flux-linkage, induced-emf, magnetic force, efficiency and total losses are determined. The obtained results are validated with conventional motor, which shows that proposed design has superior performance over existing motor.

title: Hybrid Semantic Inference via Ontology and Deep Embeddings
authors: Rahul Kumar Singh, Suresh Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The issues surrounding knowledge representation and reasoning may be extremely challenging with the areas of ambiguity, uncertainty and dynamic data. Deep contextual embeddings are grounded in natural language statistical trends and ontologies have interpretability and logical consistency. However, neither of the two approaches can do such strong and scalable reasoning on its own. A modular hybrid architecture proposed in this paper is a combination of deep contextualized embeddings and ontological reasoning. The symbolic constraints are matched with the neural similarity scores to make uncertainty-aware inference as indicated by the framework. The experimental evaluation of benchmark and domain specific data proves that the accuracy of the reasoning along with its robustness and interpretability of this system is superior to that of either ontology-only or embedding-only systems. The proposed methodology will give rise to the development of explicative and flexible knowledge systems and has a workable implementation on realistic domains of the field such as healthcare and decision support.

title: Detecting Cyberbullying in Social Platforms Through an Rf + Gradient Boosting Architecture
authors: D. Leela Dharani, D.S.V. Jyothi, L. Melinda, Kothapalli Phani Varma, Mylapalli Ramesh, Krishna Priya Burra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: With the emergence of the social media, cyberbullying has been becoming even more common and severely dangerous to the mental health of individuals. Online harassment is dynamic and often subtle, which makes accurate identification via traditional classification approaches to be both inaccurate and high in false positives. To overcome such challenges, the proposed study introduces a solid ensemble learning model, which integrates Random Forest (RF) and Gradient Boosting (GB) discriminators to achieve more accurate and precise cyberbullying identification. The data to which the model is trained and tested consists of user responses such as tweets on Twitter and other social media platforms. The preprocessing techniques used in NLP help in improving the representation of text; these include tokenization, stemming, and feature extraction. The performance of the ensemble model is compared to that of individual classifiers, namely Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Decision Tree (DT). RF+GB ensemble is proposed that had the accuracy of 96%, as compared with SVM (88%) and DT (92%). These findings indicate that the RF+GB ensemble method is more effective and reliable in identifying cases of cyberbullying content, thus promoting safer and more respectful interactions over the Internet.

title: Data-Driven Solar and Wind Resource Classification for Site-Specific Hydrogen Production in Arid Regions
authors: Khadersab Adamsab, Naser Hamood Al Haddabi, Adam Ali Mohammed Al Nasseri, Vinayak V Kulkarni, Rahila Begum Gadi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: This paper presents a data-driven synthetic model designed to classify renewable energy sources for site-specific hydrogen production. A synthetic yet realistic dataset of monthly solar radiation and wind energy data for 2023-2024 in selected governorates of Oman was designed. Energy was divided into low, medium, and high levels, and that was used to predict hydrogen production by two energy sources in a simple linear equation. Machine learning methods like XGBoost, random forest and linear regression were evaluated. As linear regression obtains the highest level of predictive accuracy is about 0.9976. The findings show the seasonal variation and reveal that Masirah, Dhofar, and Al Wusta could be the best locations for the sustained design for hydrogen generation. The methodology is a useful tool for decision-makers to facilitate strategic planning of green hydrogen infrastructure and promote sustainable energy transition in arid and semi-arid areas. The hybridization of solar-wind systems is demonstrated to mitigate variability and improve production reliability.

title: VisionGuard: A Hybrid Sparse-Predictive Lightweight Model for Real-Time Pedestrian Video Anomaly Detection
authors: Tanishka Sharma, Vidita Singh, Trapti Jindal, Kritika Yadav, Shivpratap Kushwaha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: With the increasing requirements for surveillance in smart cities pedestrian anomaly detection becomes more and more critical the current approaches lack of the ability to tradeoff between quality of the detection computational efficiency and real-time performance. Models relying on spatial features explicitly encode visual appearance, but do not encode the temporal behavior, LSTM- and 3D CNN-based methods encode motion well, but are computationally too expensive for edge deployment. In order to overcome the mentioned disadvantages, we introduce a computationally efficient Hybrid MultiTask Sparse-Predictive Network (HMSPN) that simultaneously reconstructs the spatial appearance and estimates the temporal motion in real-time. The structure combines a Sparse Denoising Encoder (SDE) for noise-robust spatial encoding and a Temporal Frame Predictor (TFP) constructed via highly optimized ConvLSTM units, jointly trained with a composite loss that governs reconstruction, prediction, and sparsity regularization. Results on standard datasets like UCSD Ped1, UCSD Ped2, Avenue and Shanghai Tech indicate that HMSPN is comparable or SOTA in accuracy and infers each frame at less than 25 ms. Experimental results demonstrate that the introduced model surpasses multiple SOTA methods in AUC and inference efficiency, rendering it apt for real-time anomaly detection on smart cameras, embedded systems, and other constrained IoT surveillance systems.

title: Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models on Sign Language Datasets
authors: Shweta Khalatkar, Purvaja Sawalakhe, Ashwini Gote, Rutuja Jaiswal
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Recognition of sign language has become an important area which is because of helping people who are deaf or hard of hearing communicate better. The rapid development of deep learning has resulted in a large number of various models being developed. These models include models such as convolutional networks such as ResNet, Inception, MobileNetV2 and EfficientNetB0, as well as recurrent networks such as LSTM and GRU. Each of these models has different amounts of computer power and accuracy levels of recognizing sign language. This paper offers a comprehensive evaluation of these deep learning models against one another on various sign language data sets, ASL, USL, GSL, and ISL are American Sign Language, Ukrainian Sign Language, and Indian Sign Language respectively, and Bengali Sign Language. We systematically benchmark these models on isolated sign recognition tasks and discuss the relative strengths, weaknesses and suitability of these models to be deployed in resource constrained environments. Our results provide us academics and practitioners in the field of sign language technology with practical insights by pointing out the trade-offs between model accuracy, efficiency and generalizability.

title: Review of Power System Load Models
authors: Saroj Kumar Mahapatra, Sunil Thomas
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The current power system is evolving through a transition governed by a change in generation type and characteristics. As a result of this transition, the system's basic identifier sensitivity has increased. These are voltage, frequency and power (active & reactive). Therefore, simulation model components that have a dependency on these parameters for their intermediate and final state will have to be reassessed, especially the load models wherein the concept of lumping is followed. Lumping of large load clusters or even smaller ones without due consideration of their internal voltage sensitivity would result in output mismatch. This review paper is intended to highlight observations on a few key papers, standards and journals, which are currently considered as benchmarks for load modelling. The aim is to propose modifications in the current load based on the network voltage sensitivity rather than a lumped model, currently used in utilities, wherein the voltage sensitivity is neglected.

title: Dynamic Sales Demand Prediction in Smart Retail Systems Using Retrieval-Augmented Generative Intelligence
authors: Rachna Rathore, Mahendra Singh Parmar, Bura Vijay Kumar, M. Mohamed Sirajudeen, Moghal Yaseen Pasha, Picheswar Gadde
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The paper presents a smart retail management system with the help of AI and the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework to facilitate highly flexible and accurate sales forecast. The integration of a Large Language Model (LLM) and vector database, which is supported by Snowflake, can make the system fast and efficient when it comes to searching data, but on the other hand, LlamaIndex is efficient in semantic searches and feature search of structured tables, which ensures deep conclusions on complex sets data. The research methodology is also based on a quite logical flow: the information is gathered using Kaggle, then it is shaped appropriately to allow missing values and the standardization of the inputs, and only the verified information is imported to Snowflake to be accessed to be able to make it quick. The system recognizes the appropriate sales trends, the customer behavioral trends, and the market indicators to produce data-driven and precise forecasting on the searches conducted with the cosine similarity. The performance measures were close in the evaluation of the model effectiveness where Mean Absolute error (MAE) was 95.7, Root Mean square error (RMSE) was 94.3, an r-square was 87 and F1-score was 94. These findings show that it is efficient in minimizing errors in prediction and trade-off possible accuracy and recall. Coupled with the Snowflake and its ability to search data successfully and LlamaIndex and its semantic extraction technique, it is possible to ensure the accuracy of the predictions is significantly higher and ensure the improvements in the management of inventory and resources. Furthermore, the hybrid strategy can be implemented to enhance the operational effectiveness, cost and agility reduction, as well as the sales strategy enhancement. Developments will be undertaken on future integration of outer market and social media information, means incorporation and exploration of higher hybrid architecture to enhance predictive accuracy, flexibility, and strength in retail landscapes of rapidly changing retail environments.

title: Tabular Data Transformation into 2D Image for CNN-Based Migraine Classification
authors: Hassiba Laifa, Assia Brek, Hakim Bendjenna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Migraine is a widespread neurological disorder that severely affects the quality of life for millions of people around the world. Correct migraine-type classification is an important issue for diagnosis and treatment. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel approach for migraine-type classification by transforming tabular data into a 2D image for a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model. Traditional machine learning (ML) approaches have the drawback of failing to capture the relationships between features in tabular datasets, consequently yielding a suboptimal classification performance. The proposed method tries to overcome this limitation by converting the tabular dataset into images. In that way, the CNN is able to extract the spatial patterns between features. Experimental results show that this approach significantly enhanced the migraine-type classification when compared to the classical ML model, such as XGBoost, that we have taken for benchmarking. By using the capability of CNNs for learning feature relationships, the presented approach outperformed all baseline methods.

title: Optimizing Batteries Charging Time Inside an Autonomous Photovoltaic System Installed on a House's Roof
authors: Salah Tamalouzt, Kamel Djermouni, Ali Berboucha, Kaci Ghedamsi, Djamal Aouzellag
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Autonomous renewable energy systems are limited by their intermittent nature, which requires efficient storage strategies to ensure reliable operation. This paper investigates a new battery charging method, referred to as staircase voltage charging, applied to photovoltaic (PV) generators installed on a residential rooftop. The method is further enhanced with fuzzy logic control and compared with the conventional Constant Current—Constant Voltage (CC/CV) charging technique. Two scenarios are analyzed: a constant PV source and a typical solar profile from southern Algeria. The maximum power point tracking is achieved using the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed fuzzy-assisted staircase charging method offers faster and more efficient charging compared to CC/CV, particularly under variable PV conditions.

title: Comparative Analysis of Pure ZnO, Ag-Mn Codoped ZnO, and Mn:ZnO/Ag/Mn:ZnO Multilayer Transparent Conductive Oxides for Photovoltaic Applications
authors: Fouaz Lekoui, Khaoula Settara, Rachid Amrani, Elyes Garoudja, Walid Filali, Slimane Oussalah, Driss Dergham, Salim Hassani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of multilayer structuring and co-doping on the optoelectronic properties of zinc oxide (ZnO) thin films. Pure ZnO, Ag-Mn co-doped ZnO, and Mn:ZnO/Ag/Mn:ZnO multilayered thin films were synthesized using the Rapid-Thermal-Evaporation (RTE) technique and deposited onto standard glass substrates. The structural characteristics were analyzed via X Ray Diffraction (XRD), while the optical and electrical properties were assessed using Ultraviolet-Visible spectrophotometry and the four-point probe method, respectively. XRD analysis confirmed a wurtzite crystalline phase in all annealed films, with an additional secondary silver phase. The films exhibited both high optical transparency and electrical conductivity, with transmittance in the visible range (600 nm) varying between 60 % and 80 %. Notably, the multilayered configuration demonstrated an optimal figure of merit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.68 \times 10^{-3} ~\text{nm} \cdot \Omega^{-1}$</tex>, underscoring its potential for optoelectronic applications.

title: Iot-Based Climatic Environment Monitoring System Design for a Cattle Breeding Buildings
authors: Sihem Souiki, Mourad Hadjila, Reda Yagoub, Abdelillah Boudjella, Oussama Ahed Messaoud
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: The study aim is to improve modern technology in cattle breeding through the implementation of automatic control and monitoring systems in cattle farms. The system will provide breeders with an effective means to monitor and manage their livestock buildings through IoT technology to achieve optimal production while minimizing costs. Following this idea, we divided the work into three parts: The first part consisted of constructing a farm-wide climate parameter automatic detection system with wireless connections to a treatment centre for real-time remote Monitoring of Climatic Parameter over Time. For the second part, we are creating an Internet-based platform for real-time display and storage of climate parameters including visualization, as well as the visual analysis of these data on the application. For the last part, a prototype of a stable was built so that the system's performance and functionality could be tested.

title: An IPCore Protection Techniques Survey
authors: Moisés Rubén Velázquez-Vera, Ramón Parra-Michel, Vidkar Anibal Delgado-Gallardo, Fernando Peña-Campos
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Electron Devices Conference (LAEDC)
abstract: One of the mainly reasons why there is a high speed at which new innovative creations are ready for their implementation is due to the intellectual property (IP) reutilization and collaboration between different entities. Due to the high value and effort invested in creating IPs, to stole or to extract information of the IP modules is becoming a more common and damaging practice. Even worst, the rogue attacker that exploits the security vulnerabilities can be a supposed trusted partner entity. Therefore, practically all external entities should be treated as potential attacker, which resulted in a continuous increasing number of IP security threats and their corresponding countermeasures techniques. In this paper, a review of the most important vulnerability threats are presented in order to provide a comprehensive classification of the countermeasure techniques. This information led to identify a list of suggested best-practices techniques to be incorporated at the design stage for diminishing future IP vulnerabilities.

title: Forecast-Driven Portfolio Optimization: A Machine Learning GJR-GARCH-Copula Framework for High-Frequency Mean-CVaR Portfolios
authors: Zied Jelassi, Haykel Hamdi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This study proposes a comprehensive framework for high-frequency asset price prediction and portfolio optimization, integrating machine learning techniques with econometric models to enhance risk-adjusted returns. The study develops and compares forecasting models, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Random Forests, for five assets: the S&P 500 index, Gold, Brent, the Bloomberg Commodity Index, and Bitcoin. The forecasts are coupled with volatility modeling via GJR-GARCH to account for asymmetric shocks and dependence structures estimated through Student-t copulas. These components are then integrated into a Mean-Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) optimization framework, which generates portfolios tailored to varying risk appetites. The findings of the study demonstrate that forecasts enhanced by machine learning techniques significantly surpass those derived from historical data-based optimization, particularly in volatile regimes. This is evidenced by the superior risk-adjusted returns achieved by portfolios guided by LSTM and RF models. The findings of this study call into question traditional asset allocation paradigms, highlighting the critical role of dynamic dependence modeling and forward-looking volatility adjustments in modern portfolio management.

title: Predicting Infrastructure Vulnerability to Climate Change from Street Level Imagery
authors: Grace Iordanov
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Understanding and decreasing the vulnerability of infrastructure to climate change is paramount for climate-resilient urban planning, but descriptive assessments of vulnerability remain with limits in terms of scale and accessibility. In this paper I propose a novel-vision-based framework to predict infrastructure vulnerability from street-level images, with weakly supervised labels that are coming from datasets for geospatial climate risk. I also created a large dataset through combining Mapillary street-level images with U.S. Census tract boundaries and by overlaying other climate risk indicators such as FEMA's National Risk Index, NOAAs sea-level rise projections, and Landsat 8-derived urban heat exposure maps for making the multimodal labels for vulnerability. I will use CLIP-based visionlanguage encoders for extracting high-level semantic representations from images and training heads for lightweight regression and classification for predicting the scores for climate vulnerability. This method does not require manual annotations and allows for risk estimation at city-scale within regions that are data-sparse. The experimental results that span across five geographically diverse cities show a strong correlation amongst the scores that were predicted and ground-truth risk metrics, along with qualitative analyses indicating consistency in the alignment of important visual cues that include tree cover, building materials, and elevation-related exposure.

title: Enhanced Battery Health Forecasting with Dynamic Thresholds Using Multi Input LSTM Networks
authors: Rafik Saddaoui, Karim Oudiai, Mhenna Nachef, Hamid Hamiche, Rachid Zirmi, Mourad Laghrouche
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Reliable forecasting of battery state-of-health <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SoH})$</tex> is vital for extending the lifetime and safety of energy storage systems. In this work, we combine a multi-input long short-term memory (LSTM) network with an adaptive thresholding strategy to make SoH prediction both accurate and practical. Unlike conventional static thresholds, the adaptive mechanism adjusts decision boundaries as the battery condition evolves, leading to earlier and more dependable anomaly detection. Our ablation study shows that this approach reduces prediction errors by up to 18% in RMSE and 15% in MAE compared to a static-threshold baseline. The model itself is compact, with about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0. 7 k}$</tex> parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{0.20 MB})$</tex>, and runs efficiently on a Raspberry Pi 4B, where it achieved an average inference latency of 6.2 ms and required less than 10 MB of memory well within the limits of embedded platforms. When compared with more complex Transformer and attentionLSTM architectures reported in the literature, our method reaches similar levels of accuracy while remaining far lighter and easier to deploy. These results show that the proposed framework not only improves prediction quality but also meets the practical requirements of real-time battery management systems.

title: Robustness of Fuzzy Logic Based DTC for DFIM in Electric Vehicle Traction under Road Slope and Wind Speed Disturbances
authors: Ahmed Chantoufi, Aziz Derouich, Najib El Ouanjli, Said Mahfoud, Abderrahman El Idrissi, Houssam Ech-Chaouy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: The transportation sector is one of the main sources of greenhouse gas emissions. This has prompted countries around the world to promote the decarbonization of this sector. This goal will be achieved by replacing internal combustion vehicles with electric vehicles (EV). To contribute to this transition, improvements to the powertrain of EV are necessary. In this context, this article presents a comparison between two control strategies used for Doubly-Fed Induction Machine (DFIM) control: conventional DTC and fuzzy logicbased DTC (FL-DTC). DFIM is used in the powertrain of EVs. This study examines DFIM operation in the face of challenges such as slope and wind variations, as well as sudden changes in EV speed (acceleration and deceleration to simulate realistic DFIM operation in EVs. The results indicate that FL-DTC offers high tracking accuracy and faster dynamic response, as well as a significant reduction in torque and power ripple compared to conventional DTC. These improvements ensure significant optimization of operational stability and efficient energy management within the traction system of EVs, confirming the relevance of FL-DTC as a robust control strategy for the propulsion of DFIM-based EVs.

title: A Comprehensive Regression Framework for Photovoltaic Systems: From Data Analysis to Real-Time Notifications
authors: Mohamed Karom, Zakia Hasnaoui, Meryem Yahyaoui, Abdellah Kadaoui, Chaieb El Bekkaye, Abdelouahad Tahani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: This paper suggests a comprehensive and costeffective photovoltaic maintenance system intended to meet the growing demand for smart monitoring solutions in the market. The approach integrates various processes for data collection and analysis, regression modeling, real-time anomaly detection, and automated notification capabilities. The key objective is to enhance remote monitoring, data management, and fault detection by leveraging both historical meteorological and power databases. The workflow begins with the use of two wellestablished representatives of Machine Learning paradigms to produce regression models based on eight variables. Correlation analysis was then applied for feature selection to reduce computational load without compromising overall quality. This action leads to a refined and meaningful subset that was assessed against the first full model using well-known metrics such as the root mean squared error and the coefficient of determination. The results show that the optimized model maintains high accuracy while streamlining the protocol for rapid execution. Finally, to operationalize the method, a unified architecture for frequent data collection was implemented. The pipeline feeds a fault detection mechanism that tracks disparities between projected and real power and flags potential under performance in realtime, prompting appropriate physical inspections. This end-toend setup bridges renewable energy practical applications with the field of Machine Learning. It was specifically built for operators with limited technical expertise, introducing a versatile and affordable strategy to improve the reliability of PV systems.

title: The Role of Compensation and Benefits in Employee Retention: A Study in the Technology Sector
authors: Prerna, Sukhman Ghumman, Joyce R, Varsha Agarwal, Shaik Fakruddin Ali Ahmed, Manoranjan Dash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Compensation and benefits are important factors that decide the fate of an employee, especially in the hotly competitive tech industry where talent mobility is high. Ensuring fairness and effectiveness of reward mechanisms with the workforce is imperative for sustainability of workforce stability and catalyzing organizational growth. Nevertheless, current approaches to assessing compensation and benefits are dominated by conventional survey or linear models, which fail to adequately address the uncertainty, subjectivity and interdependence that are inherent in the multiple factors affecting employee retention. These deficiencies reduce precision as well as strategic utility of human resource decision making. To address these gaps, this study employs Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (F-AHP) framework that utilizes fuzzy logic and hierarchical analysis to enable a systematic evaluation of compensation and benefit components under uncertainty and sparseness of judgement. The proposed approach is applied to rank key determinants (such as salary, health benefits, stock options and flexible working policies) within a leading technology firm. This methodology will help human-resources managers determine which benefits have the greatest relative priority among employees for retaining them. Findings show that F-AHP provides better prioritization with flexible working arrangements and performance-linked incentives being key retention drivers and providing actionable insights for evidence-based HR policy. The proposed method improves the prioritization of the factors of compensation by 93 per cent, and of health benefits by by 94.5%, flexible work policies by 95%, and performance-related incentives by 95.4%.

title: Compressed Sensing for High-Speed Maneuvering Radar Targets: A Fundamental Analysis
authors: Amgad A. Salama, Syed T. Shah, Insaf Ullah, Ahmed Gamal Abdellatif, Mahmoud A. Shawky
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: This paper investigates the basic theoretical limits that are inherent in the application of compressed sensing (CS) in the detection and parameter estimation of high speed maneuvering radar targets. Through rigorous mathematical analysis, the study examines the signal properties and limitations of dictionary design and bounds in the amount of performance for reconstruction of targets with complex motion including acceleration and jerk. The analysis shows that there are serious issues to achieve the restricted isometry property (RIP) on realistic parameter spaces, because dictionary coherence exceeds practically acceptable levels. We obtain lower bounds, Cramers-Rao for parameter estimation, and show that despite theoretical computational advantages offered by the separable dictionary structures of CS, the signal features of maneuvering targets violate fundamental assumptions of CS. These results indicate that compressed sensing may not be the best framework in which to approach this class of radar problems, and hence provide a theoretical rationale for exploring other methodologies.

title: Examining Big Data's Role in Shaping Monitoring Practices in the Age of Data Policing
authors: Antony Allwyn Sundarraj, Lakshya Swarup, V.P. Arul Kumar, Sanjay Bhatnagar, S. Hemelatha, Saritha Srinivasmurthy Raghotham
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Contemporary environments are characterized by the flood of surveillance infrastructures, making the exploitation of Big Data for the aggregation, scrutiny and manipulation of digitized information salient features of institutional and governmental monitoring mechanisms. This data centric paradigm generates an array of concerns including breaching of privacy, bias in algorithms and the limitations inherent on conventional forms of monitoring that are often reliant on incomplete data sets and have a mostly retrospective viewpoint. To overcome these hindrances, this study intends to present Data Policing Framework (DPF) where Big Data computational platforms such as Hadoop and Spark are focused together with probabilistic modelling specifically Bayesian inference to complement predictive monitoring and risk assessment mechanism. The framework helps in the systematized acquisition, processing, and management of large datasets that improve the acuity and timeliness of surveillance operations. Empirical evaluation of the DPF in various monitoring contexts has shown high increases in performance, which can be seen in high detection efficacies, low incidence of false positives and evidence-based decision making, which transcends the limitations of conventional methodologies. These results highlight the strong synergy that is possible in combining big-data analysis and probabilistic modelling in modern data policing activities.

title: Navigating Financial Instability: An Interpretable Paradigm for Multi-Label Crisis Prediction
authors: Ravjot Kaur, Gurasis Singh, Debashis Guha
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: We present a rigorous methodology for applying Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) to the continual prediction of banking, systemic, currency and inflation crises. Traditional models, trained on earlier regimes, are prone to catastrophic forgetting when updated on new regimes for financial crisis prediction that is a sequential, non-stationary learning problem with data arriving across time and heterogeneous jurisdictions. EWC mitigates this by constraining important parameters, quantified via a diagonal-Fisher-information approximation, to remain near previously learnt optima while permitting flexibility in less critical directions. We formalise the Bayesian derivation underpinning EWC, present the practical diagonalFisher estimator, and integrate the method into a reproducible training pipeline for multi-label crisis prediction. Our protocol uses representative task partitions, rigorous Fisher estimation, lambda scheduling, and a suite of evaluation metrics to compare performance with baseline traditional models. We provide ablation studies for Fisher estimation, penalty aggregation, and sequential walk-forward evaluation, and discuss limitations and interpretability for policy use. Our experimental model is tested for 2 years prior financial crisis prediction on Harvard Business School's global crisis database for 42 years and 70 countries, and it demonstrates ROC-AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 3. 1 2 \%}$</tex> in inflation crises, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 3. 5 3 \%}$</tex> in systemic crises, 77.63% in currency crises and 67.51% in banking crises predictions, outperforming the traditional models' results. We also assess the sensitivity of EWC to its core hyperparameters for real-world application. By integrating SHAP, this work aims to bridge probabilistic continual-learning theory and operational early-warning systems, making models both interpretable and robust in dynamic financial environments.

title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection with Transformers and Context-Aware GANs
authors: Hadil Ben Romdhane, Farah Jemili, Khaled Jouini, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: The growing sophistication of cyber threats highlights critical limitations in traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), particularly regarding class imbalance, limited contextual understanding, and poor generalization to novel attacks. This paper introduces the RAG-GAN Transformer, a hybrid IDS architecture that integrates three key components: a conditional Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for minorityclass data augmentation, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) module for external context integration, and a TabTransformer encoder for structured network traffic representation. Experimental results on the LUFlow dataset demonstrate notable improvements in detection accuracy, macro F1-score, and class balance. The proposed model offers a scalable, context-aware solution for intelligent intrusion detection in diverse and imbalanced network environments.

title: Prediction Augmented Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Autonomous Vehicle Lane-Change Decision Making
authors: Jianquan Chen, Cheng Peng, Zhen Wang, Tao Gao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Reliable lane-change decision making remains a key challenge for autonomous driving, particularly under partial observability where the intentions of surrounding vehicles are uncertain. Most deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approaches rely solely on instantaneous observations, which limits foresight and may lead to unsafe or inefficient maneuvers. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a Perception-Prediction-Decision DRL framework that integrates perception, prediction, and decision making into a unified architecture. A lightweight prediction module is employed to provide short-term trajectory prediction of surrounding vehicles, thereby enriching the agent's observation space with future context. The framework is implemented with PPO and SAC algorithms, and the performance is evaluated through extensive simulations in CARLA. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed framework markedly improves lane-change performance, with PPO achieving higher lane-change success rates and significantly lower collision rates compared to baselines, thereby suggesting the benefit of embedding prediction into the DRL decision-making.

title: Dynamic LSTM-Based Energy Consumption Prediction for Smart Home Applications
authors: Kiran Saripudi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The increasing integration of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and advanced measurement systems in smart homes has amplified the need for accurate short-term energy consumption forecasting. Reliable load prediction enables intelligent energy management, cost optimization, and improved demand-response strategies for residential environments. This study investigates the performance of three deep learning architectures—Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Dynamic LSTM (D-LSTM)—for predicting household energy demand using historical consumption data. The proposed D-LSTM model is designed to adapt to fluctuating consumption patterns in smart homes, capturing both short- and long-term temporal dependencies with high precision. Experimental results, derived from real-world residential power consumption datasets, demonstrate that D-LSTM outperforms traditional LSTM and GRU models in forecasting accuracy and peak demand detection. The findings highlight the potential of D-LSTM-based models to enable proactive energy control, support renewable integration, and enhance the operational efficiency of smart home energy management systems.

title: A Hybrid Transformer-Based Approach for Optimal Multimodal Phishing Detection
authors: Vidhula Thomas, Anubhav Sharma, Manmohan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Phishing websites continue to be a threat because of the misleading impression they give and increasing sophistication. A phishing website is a site that imitates a legitimate website, convincing users to provide their sensitive information. Traditional approaches to detect phishing websites based on URLs and/or HTML no longer work because phishing approaches continue to evolve. This evolution led to the emergence of visual similarity detection approaches that attempt to detect phishing websites based on visual similarities to legitimate websites. These visual similarity-based methods can often result in high false positive detection rates, but are also still impacted by everchanging URLs and impersonated entities. Consequently, this publication leverages a state-of-the-art multimodal method using a hybrid transformer and deep learning techniques for detecting phishing websites. The model is further developed using pre-trained architectures, where the model provides a local and semantic representation of the URL and HTML DOM graphs. Features are selected using a modified horned lizard optimization (MHLO) method to deal with the high-dimensional data and increase model efficiency. The classification model is a wavelet deep belief neural network (W-DBNN) to reliably separate phishing websites from legitimate websites. The simulation results on the Common Crawl, Phishtank, and Mendeley datasets illustrate how the proposed MHLO+W-DBNN model is effective. It performs well across several public datasets. The proposed MHLO + W-DBNN multimodal phishing detection model integrates transformer-based feature extraction with enhanced deep learning classification. It attained an accuracy of 98.856 %, precision of 97.105 %, recall of 98.328 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 97.713 %, surpassing current CNN, Transformer, and GCN models by as much as 11.63 %. The Modified Horned Lizard Optimization (MHLO) effectively identified high-impact features, enhancing performance and minimizing computational burden. By amalgamating URL and HTML modalities, the model apprehends both semantic and structural phishing indicators, thereby augmenting its resilience against advancing attack patterns. This research suggests a highly accurate, adaptable, and generalizable detection approach, providing a robust basis for AI-based phishing defense systems in real-world cybersecurity applications.

title: IndoorNavX: ARCore-Powered Markerless Indoor Navigation
authors: Meer Mehta, Kushal Mehta, Kushagra Chauhan, Monika Mangla, Monali Sankhe, Pravin Hole
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Route planning between spatial locations constitutes a fundamental requirement for autonomous systems and robotic platforms. Satellite-based positioning systems effectively serve outdoor environments; however, enclosed architectural spaces including commercial complexes, medical facilities, and transportation hubs present significant localization challenges due to satellite signal attenuation within building structures. Consequently, conventional interior positioning approaches depend on infrastructure-based technologies such as Quick Response markers, Bluetooth Low Energy proximity sensors, and wireless signal mapping. These methods demonstrate scalability constraints stemming from extensive hardware deployment requirements and maintenance overhead. Addressing these constraints, this research presents a feature-based Augmented Reality framework for interior wayfinding, implemented within the Android development environment utilizing Google's ARCore spatial computing library and Sceneform rendering toolkit. The developed solution integrates surface recognition algorithms, continuous motion estimation, and Visual-Inertial Odometry for environmental spatial interpretation, delivering instantaneous three-dimensional directional guidance to users. Environmental understanding capabilities enable predefined route storage through virtual anchor positioning combined with multi-sensor data fusion, facilitating offline operational capacity. A streamlined interaction interface presents vectorized directional indicators, waypoint markers, and terminal location arrows, enhancing overall user engagement quality. The suggested system has the capability to use its numerous applications from smart campuses to AR-based building management.

title: Dynamic Bus Scheduling Using Transformer-Based Demand Forecasting
authors: Shreesh Gawande, Rishi Mishra, Jay Kumar Vagairya, Rahul S, Prasoon Kumar Bharati, Nidhi Meshram, Veena Anand
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Inefficiencies in urban bus scheduling, mainly driven by changing passenger demand, cause a high level of waste in operations and a poor perceived customer experience. Static schedules often lead to a mismatch between the passenger demand and scheduled supply. This looks like either full buses during peak periods or under-utilized “ghost buses” during off-peak periods. This paper describes the design and implementation of an intelligent forecasting system for the adaptive transit landscape of New York City. We introduce a new multivariate dataset built from four years of historical MTA bus ridership data combined with GTFS route specifications, citywide traffic behavior, and weather data. At the heart of the proposed system is a Transformer-based neural network used to model long-range dependencies in time-series data, which will forecast hourly passenger volume. Additionally, the system has a robust backend API and a dynamic dispatch algorithm that creates a methodology for converting raw predictions into scheduling recommendations. The model shows performance on the test set with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.91 and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 23.05. This work serves as a proof-of-concept for a from reactive to a predictive, data-driven public transit operations.

title: Integrative Network Pharmacology and Molecular Dynamics Uncover Monoterpenoid-Based Inhibitors Targeting Aurora Kinase B in Prostate, Testicular, and Bladder Cancers
authors: Satya Vivek Jaiswal, Nidhi Mishra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Aurora kinase B (AURKB) plays a critical role in maintaining chromosomal integrity and regulating mitosis, which has emerged as a potential target in prostate, testicular, and bladder cancers. In this study, the transcriptomics dataset from these malignancies was analyzed to identify the commonly upregulated genes, followed by protein-protein interaction (PPI) network analysis, which revealed that AURKB was the top-ranked hub gene based on betweenness centrality. Monoterpenoid compounds from PubChem were subjected to structure-based virtual screening against AURKB using docking. The lead compound (PubChem ID 16732680) exhibits a higher docking score (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.178 \text{kcal} / \text{mol}$</tex>) than the reference compound, enzalutamide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.495 \text{kcal} / \text{mol}$</tex>), and demonstrates favourable drug-likeness properties. The stability of the AURKB-ligand complex was further evaluated through a 100-nanosecond molecular dynamics simulation. These findings suggest that monoterpenoids may serve as a potential candidate for targeting AURKB, highlighting their therapeutic relevance for male-specific cancers.

title: Comparative Study on Optimal Directional Overcurrent Relay Coordination Using Linear and Nonlinear Approaches
authors: Raghvendra Tiwari, Devesh Jaiswal, Raj Kapur Kumar, Biranchi Narayan Kar, Roshan Chitranshi, Amiya Kumar Naik
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The primary goal of relay coordination is to determine the optimal relay configurations that minimize total operating time while ensuring proper coordination between each relay pair. This paper presents a comparative analysis of linear and nonlinear programming techniques for achieving optimal directional overcurrent relay (DOCR) coordination. The coordination problem is formulated as a constrained optimization task, incorporating both linear and nonlinear models. The effectiveness of the proposed coordination schemes is evaluated using different relay characteristics, including normal inverse (NI), very inverse (VI), extremely inverse (EI), and custom-defined curves. The Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) is applied to obtain the optimal settings, and its performance is compared against that of the Genetic Algorithm (GA). To demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach, the distribution part of the IEEE-14 bus test system (7-bus microgrid test system) is considered.

title: A Perceptual Evaluation Model for Tunable White Luminaires Establishing Better Simulation of Human Visual Perception
authors: Anastasios Dimitrakis, Evangelos-Nikolaos D. Madias, Athanasion Kotsenos, Frangiskos V. Topalis
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Tunable White (TW) LED luminaires enable dynamic spectral variation across a wide CCT range and are increasingly used in human-centric lighting applications. Conventional color-rendition frameworks, such as CIE CRI and IESTM-30, evaluate spectral changes by comparing each SPD to a reference illuminant matched to its CCT. While effective for static scenes, this approach does not fully reflect situations where the SPD varies but the surrounding visual environment, and thus the observer's adaptation state remains comparatively stable. To address this, the present study introduces a perceptually anchored evaluation method in which all SPDs are assessed against a single fixed reference illuminant, CIE D65. Spectral measurements were obtained from a TW luminaire across eight CCT points, and both standard TM-30-24 and constant-reference analyses were performed using the LuxPy library. Room renderings were generated to visualize perceptual outcomes under a fixed adaptation degree. Results show that although standard TM-30 metrics indicate consistently high fidelity, the constant-reference scheme reveals meaningful hue and chroma deviations at warm CCTs. This dual-framework approach enhances cross-CCT assessment of dynamic lighting.

title: Camera Spectral Reconstruction from 2 RGB Triplets for Lighting Analysis
authors: Nicolas Da Costa, Raphaël Labayrade
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: We propose a spectral reconstruction method associated with an imaging device (based on a commercial camera) to obtain hyperspectral images of scenes illuminated by light sources emitting different spectra. Rather than using a single RGB triplet for each image pixel, the main idea of this paper is to collect additional information, such as a second RGB triplet, and derive the underlying spectrum using the augmented data (RGB1, RGB2). We focus on building an efficient spectral reconstruction pipeline in terms of accuracy and computing speed. The resulting hyperspectral image can be used to assess a scene lit by various sources against any spectral sensitivity functions, allowing to estimate XYZ and LMSR colors, blue hazard, circadian efficacy, etc.

title: Simulation Study of an $\text{In}_{\mathrm{x}} \text{Ga}_{1-\mathrm{x}}$ as Vertical TFET Biosensor for Ultra-Low Concentration Lung Cancer Biomarker Detection
authors: Swati Dixit, Varun Mishra, Manisha Pattanaik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The research suggests making a biosensor that uses a Vertical Tunnel Field Effect Transistor (VTFET) with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I n 0. 8 G a 0. 2 A s}$</tex> as the source material. This choice of InxGa1-xAs, specifically with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x=0.8$</tex>, is intended to boost the device's ability to detect lung cancer. In0.8Ga0.2As has taken as source material because it provides better electrostatic control, a steeper subthreshold slope, and enhanced tunneling characteristics. This biosensor is built to detect lung cancer by recognizing certain biomarkers, including CEA (Carcinoembryonic Antigen), NSE (Neuron-Specific Enolase), and CYFRA-21 (Cytokeratin 19 Fragment). The simulation assumes interface charge values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> for CEA, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.1 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> for NSE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24.3 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> for CYFRA-21. The device structure is created using the Silvaco Atlas TCAD tool to examine its electrical behavior under different interface charge densities caused by these biomarkers. The proposed device results shows NSE Biomarker has excellent Subthreshold slope Sensitivity of value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35.61 \text{mV} /$</tex> decade. These qualities make it a good choice for point-of-care testing and early detection of lung cancer.

title: Magnetic-Solid-Acoustic Coupling Simulation and Experimental Analysis of GIS Basin Insulator Under Disruptive Discharge Fault
authors: Xiao Tan, Yingkai Long, Zhuofan Kang, Xiong Liu, Siquan Li, Xiaoxiao Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: GIS equipment with internal defects is prone to breakdown faults during operation, and the study of vibration characteristics and acoustic characteristics of the breakdown can provide theoretical basis for the judgment of GIS breakdown faults. Firstly, the physical model of GIS breakdown defects is established, the GIS breakdown defects are simplified and equated, the defect test is carried out, and the magnetic-solid-acoustic threefield coupling is analyzed; finally, the same type of GIS test platform is constructed, and the defects are set to simulate the breakdown of the GIS when it is in operation, and the acoustic and vibration data are collected and analyzed. The results show that, when the breakdown occurs, the electromagnetic force on the basin insulator increases dramatically, which in turn leads to obvious displacement deformation of the GIS equipment, triggering the noise of violent vibration, with a maximum sound pressure of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 ~ P a}$</tex> and a maximum sound pressure level of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5 ~ d B}$</tex>; the vibration acceleration of the measurement points arranged at the faulty insulator produces a pulsed abrupt change when the breakdown occurs, followed by a rapid attenuation. The consistency between the simulation and test results is good, which verifies the accuracy of the simulation model and provides a reliable basis for the subsequent fault diagnosis study of GIS breakdown.

title: Analysing and Preventing Threat Using Forward Hunting
authors: Alok Kumar Tiwari, Tridev Sharma, Saptadeepa Kalita, Avinash Kumar, Vinam Tomar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Cybersecurity is facing vital challenges from sophisticated and advanced threats which cannot be easily countered using the traditional methods of reactive defence mechanism. In this regard, threat hunting and forward hunting have emerged as active techniques that emphasize extensive use of hypothesis-based detection and prediction of adversary behaviour. The review presented in this paper explains the existing literature regarding honeypots, machine learning (ML), graph-based analytics and other automated hunting framework and discloses their strengths and limitations and real-world usability. Quality logs of enemies are as is shown in the case of honeypots, where ML increases scalability and detection anomalies and forward hunting frameworks and automated defence prediction possible largescale areas include deployment, false positive minimization and Al-driven hunting reliability that need more attention. The article concludes the view that honeypots intelligence should be blended with. Forward hunting and Machine Learning based analytic is a fruitful line of research to anticipatory defence. Future studies are instead to construct adaptive honeypots, graph-scales explainable AI systems, based models, and integrate into SIEM/SOAR smoothly platforms for real time response. Though the advancement of cybersecurity practices and operation must be in position to reactive monitoring to intelligent driven predictive and proactive defence ecosystem which can properly respond to the evolving threats.

title: SS-IQ: Smart Summarization and Information Quantification
authors: Aman Kumar, Danish Raza Rizvi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The development of NLP technology has been taking the capabilities of information generation, extraction, and summarization to unprecedented levels. The quality and suitability of the extracted information in terms of the extracted summaries and the set objectives are being traditionally measured on the basis of surface-level criteria; however, the information content coverage in relation to the purpose of the document or test is critical. Although significant advancements have been achieved in the development of automated summarization technologies during the last few years, these technologies have reached the next level of sophistication and can be used for precise quantification. This work presents conventional and advanced remarkable approaches to Smart Summarization (SS), which has resulted in the powerful incorporation of Large Language Models.
