This work has thoroughly explored the evolution of these approaches, ranging from simple syntactic approaches to the development of deep semantic understanding, including rule-based, statistical, traditional machine learning, and state-of-the-art neural network approaches. Moreover, we discuss in detail the evaluation metrics and major challenges related to semantic drift, scalability, and domain-related issues. Further, this work also deals with issues related to information coverage and completeness. This survey is finally intended to be a treasured collection of work for researchers and practitioners, which includes an in-depth survey of available work and promising future directions to realize effective Information Quantification (IQ).

title: Area and Power Efficient MixColumns Architecture for Lightweight AES Implementations
authors: Salman Ahmed, Nabihah Ahmad, Chessda Uttraphan, Naveed Ahmed, Ardhi Wijayanto, Fareesa Khan, Abdinasir Hirsi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is the most widely adopted block cipher for securing data in applications ranging from wireless communication to IoT devices. Although AES provides robust security, its hardware implementation can be costly in terms of area and power, posing a critical challenge for resource-constrained platforms such as edge devices and battery-powered systems. Among the four AES round transformations, mixcolumns is one of the most computationally expensive due to the multiple <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{GF}\left(2^{8}\right)$</tex> multiplications and XOR operations it requires, making it a major contributor to logic utilization, dynamic power, and critical path delay. This work presents a resource-optimized, FSM-controlled architecture that replaces conventional multipliers with an x-time-based shift-andXOR approach, serially processes bytes to minimize combinational logic, and reuses a single multiply-by-two block across computation cycles. A multiplier-based design was also implemented as a baseline to quantify area and power trade-offs. Post-synthesis results on an Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA demonstrate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 5 \%}$</tex> reduction in LUT utilization, minimized register count, and more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 \%}$</tex> reduction in dynamic power compared to the multiplierbased implementation, while maintaining functional correctness and one-byte per cycle throughput. The proposed design offers a scalable building block that can be integrated into lightweight AES cores and cryptographic accelerators in IoT and edge computing platforms. Full integration into a lightweight AES encryption engine for secure and energy-efficient deployments is a future work goal for this implementation.

title: Interpretable Machine Learning Models for Predicting Loan Defaults
authors: N Srujana Roy, Kaushik Sekaran, Suvarna Hugar, Maragoni Mahendar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The Prediction accuracy of loan default are important for the financial sector to reduce credit risks and support better decision making. In this work, we proposed a machine-learning based framework to predict loan defaults using demographic, financial, and behavioural information. We used ensemble models (Random Forest and XGBoost) to provide strong classification performance. In addition to predictive accuracy, we applied explainable AI models discussed here to support model and feature interpretability (SHAP: SHapley Additive exPlanations, and LIME: Local Interpretable ModelAgnostic Explanations). Explanations are provided at both the global and local level so that stakeholders understand how loan features, such as credit score, income, and loan amount contribute to the prediction outcomes. Our results exceed expectations in terms of accuracy and ROC-AUC score, and the demonstration of our proposed approach, from start to finish will have transparency that demonstrates applicability to banking in the real-world so trusted and accountable loan predictions can occur.

title: Cloud-Enabled Hybrid Random Forest-Support Vector Machine Model for Early Detection and Prevention of Natural Disasters
authors: M. Giri, K. Ramesh Babu, V. Narsing Rao, Ch. V. Narayana, M. Srinivasa Rao, N. Babu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Natural disasters may lead to many kinds of losses worldwide like many people were loss their life, loss of good looking nature, and also loss economically. Identification and predicting situation is more important to manage disasters, many conventional way of disaster predicting models shows greater overhead and analysis of data is done with delay that in turn causes big damage. In this research paper we proposed hybrid approach by combining SVM (support Vector Machine) with RF (Random Forest) to handle data over cloud platform and the method will identify and prevent from disasters to sustain green environment. Disaster data samples were collected with help of satellites, data collected through IoT devices, metrological data, from datasets like MODIS, NOAA, and data samples has details about environment (moisture of soil, velocity of winds, humidity, and temperature). Most the data samples are collected from flood prone areas. RF technique measured ranks of feature vectors and these features are classified by SVM technique, experiments are conducted by using collected disaster datasets, and we observed outcomes. Our disaster prediction model exhibits 96 percent of accuracy with latency time 1.9 seconds, shows 94.6 percent F1 score, and achieved good outcome when compared with similar kinds of disaster prediction approaches. The proposed model is really useful to many research labs, disaster management teams, and as well as government agencies.

title: Decomposition-Based Convolutional Neural Network for Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Sebamai Parija, Subhashree Subudhi, Mrutyunjaya Sahani, Susanta Kumar Rout
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The neurological disorder known as epilepsy affects over 50 million individuals globally. While electroencephalography (EEG) is vital for monitoring brain activity and diagnosing epilepsy, processing each EEG signal requires time. A study identified to detect ictal, preictal, and interictal EEG segments using convolutional neural networks (CNNs). The Bonn University and Bern Barcelona EEG database were used in our research work to evaluate the performance of deep learning algorithm. It has been noted that the deep learning methodology alone is insufficient to yield improved results; rather, the proposed algorithm is the combination of advance signal processing algorithm namely empirical mode decomposition (EMD) to extract features and the CNN is used to obtained distinct signatures, fed to random vector functional link network (RVFLN) classifier to detect the epileptic seizure events efficaciously with an accuracies of 99 % and 92 % for both the EEG databases respectively.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Algorithms for Automated Depression and Anxiety Detection From Textual Data: An Ensemble Learning Approach
authors: Sunil Kumar, Anuwanshika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Mental health disorders such as anxiety and depression are becoming a greater problem across the globe, affecting millions. Identifying individuals with mental health disorders early is important to provide interventions as quickly as possible, but screening method traditionally take a lot of time and are subjective measures to detect the presence of mental health disorders. This work provides a systematic comparative study of several machine learning algorithms to assist in automated detection of anxiety and depression in individuals from text-based data. The research proposes an ensemble learning approach that will train multiple classifiers to achieve an increase in the detection accuracy of disorders over individual classifiers used to detect depression and anxiety. The dataset is comprised of textual entries that have been labeled, and then preprocessed utilizing high-level feature extraction and text cleaning methods. Results show that ensemble methods provide better detection and classification performance over individual classifier methods to achieve the greatest precision and accuracy rates, ultimately achieving 99% accuracy across the models. This study, therefore, highlights the potential of NLP and ensemble learning for assessment in mental health, and paves the way to further research into an automated method of detecting psychological disorders.

title: Forecast-Driven Microgrid Cost Optimization Using the MAS-Hybrid Model
authors: Bhabasis Mohapatra, Adithya Ballaji, Ritesh Dash, Binod Kumar Sahu, Swarnika Gupta
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Economic operation of microgrids requires not only technical balancing of supply and demand but also cost-aware decision-making under variable tariff conditions. This paper proposes a forecast-driven operational strategy that minimizes the average price of electricity by integrating predictions of load, solar generation, and tariff into the scheduling process. Forecasts are generated using Holt-Winters, ARIMA, SARIMA, and the newly developed MAS-Hybrid model (Median of ARIMA-SARIMA-Holt-Winters). The framework is evaluated on real 15-minute interval data from the Duruguda microgrid for April 2025, resampled at hourly resolution. Results show that forecast-based operation reduces reliance on high-tariff imports compared to a baseline scenario with no forecasting. Among all methods, MAS-Hybrid consistently achieves the greatest reduction in cost per unit, demonstrating that improved prediction accuracy translates directly into tangible economic benefits. The findings establish forecasting as not only a statistical exercise but also a practical tool for cost optimization in renewable-rich microgrid environments.

title: Comparative Analysis of Different Transformerbased Models for Cardiovascular Disease Risk Stratification Using Biomarker Data
authors: Ekta Tiwari, Dipti Shrimankar, Yuvraj Sharma, Luca Saba, Jasjit S. Suri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is a major cause of mortality and morbidity in the world. Risk assessment has been enhanced greatly by the incorporation of multivariate strategies and sophisticated computational models. Recent advancements in deep learning, especially Transformer-based models on tabular data, offer an effective model to capture the complex interactions of biomarkers and improve CVD risk stratification. This paper will suggest a predictive model based on statistical and clinical data of various biomarkers. The conventional machine learning (ML) models compared to deep learning models and three Transformer-based architectures carotid Self-Attention and Intersample Attention Transformer (cSAINT), carotid Feature Tokenizer (cFT), and carotid Tab Transformer. Other experiments evaluate how data augmentation, learning behaviour, and recurrent model fusion impact predictive performance. The results of the area-under-the-curve (AUC) indicate that the Transformer-based models perform better than the traditional ML and DL models in the risk prediction of CVD. In particular, cSAINT had AUC of 0.8875, cFT of 0.9489 and carotid Tab Transformer had the highest performance of 0.9728. This evidence proves that carotid Tab Transformer is a successful model that can be used to model both categorical and continuous biomarkers, with a higher predictive performance and good generalization. Altogether, the suggested Transformer-based model offers a clinical-scale, scalable approach to early risk of CVD and individualized patient care.

title: Techno-Economic Evaluation and Optimization of Medium-Deep Geothermal Heat Pump Systems Integrated with Phase Change Heat Storage
authors: Jun Liu, Yuping Zhang, Zeyuan Wang, Fenghao Wang, Zhe Ma, Haozheng Qin, Hangyu Li
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: As an effective approach for deep geothermal energy utilization, medium-deep ground source heat pump (MDGSHP) systems have attracted increasing attention in northern China as a low-carbon solution for district heating. However, traditional MD-GSHP systems exhibit insufficient demand-side flexibility, limiting their capacity for real-time coordination with power grid operations. The incorporation of thermal energy storage (TES) into MD-GSHP systems presents a promising pathway to enhance energy flexibility and support demand response. In this study, an MD-GSHP system integrated with latent heat TES was proposed. Firstly, a dynamic simulation model of the integrated system was established based on TRNSYS platform and validated against field data. Sensitivity analysis was then conducted to analyze the influence of PCM storage tank parameters on system performance. Finally, techno-economic optimization was performed to identify the optimal design parameters and the system's potential for performance improvement. The results show that the optimal PCM charging temperature of the heat pump is strongly dependent on the storage tank volume and PCM melting temperature. As the melting temperature and charging temperature increase, both lifecycle economic performance and energy efficiency exhibit a nonmonotonic trend-rising initially and then declining. Under the joint optimization of economic performance and energy efficiency, the system achieves a levelized cost of energy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.481 \text{CNY} / \text{kWh}$</tex> and an SPF of 6.02 after 20-year operation. The research results provide a theoretical basis for guiding the design and operation of deep borehole geothermal systems with integrated TES.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Method for Comprehensive Battery Performance Analysis with SVR and KNN Models
authors: Ramya P S, Darshan Purushottam Naik, Manikantha D S, Ritesh Dash, Bhabasis Mohapatra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this research work is based on battery performance analysis by using Support Vector Regression(SVR) and K nearest Neighbor (KNN) model to predict the battery state of charge, state of health and remaining useful life. Real time data acquisition by using sensor and noise reduction algorithm enhances the prediction accuracy. The advantage of SVR and KNN in capturing both globalized trend and localized variations have made it possible to integrate the system.The findings has presented in this research ensures and increasing efficiency from 81.03 percent to 88.74 percent. The research work has been carried out using machine learning model of MATLAB 2024A.

title: Sustainable Energy Multilevel Inverter Fed Motor Drives for Rural Industrial and Electric Vehicle Charging Applications
authors: Venkata V Anjaneyulu B, K. Narasimha Rao, M. Ramalinga Raju
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: True green sustainable energy involves generating power primarily from zero-emission renewable energy resources (RES). Among all RES, photovoltaic (PV) and windbased systems are the most feasible and abundantly available. Integrated renewable-based microgrids represent an optimal solution for electrification and small-scale energy needs in rural and hilly regions. For rural industrialization and electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure, alternating current (AC) microgrids are essential. Multilevel inverters (MLIs) play a crucial role in generating high-quality AC power to operate industrial drives and EV chargers. This paper evaluates 5-, 7-, and 9-level MLIs controlled by adaptive schemes in a MATLAB/Simulink environment to achieve low total harmonic distortion (THD) outputs suitable for AC drives and EV charging stations. Among the tested topologies, the cascaded Hbridge 9-level MLI delivered optimal performance under both loaded and unloaded conditions when supplied from renewable sources. The outcomes substantate the selection of multilevel inverters instead of traditional two-level versions for rural, small-scale industrial operations and electric vehicle charging setups. This directly resolves a shortcoming recognized in earlier literature that failed to designate appropriateness for electric vehicle systems.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)

title: Development of a CloudPSS-Based Dynamic Phasor Measurement Module Using a Recursive Prony Algorithm
authors: Hao Sun, Xinyao Yu, Jian Wang, Honglong Chen, Yi Jiang, Hongjian Sun, Caihai Zou, Zongshuai Jin
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: With the increasing integration of renewable energy sources and the growing power-electronization of power systems, dynamic behaviors have become more complex, posing higher demands on real-time dynamic monitoring technologies. Traditional phasor measurement units (PMUs) based on Fourier transform algorithms exhibit significant measurement inaccuracies under non-steady-state conditions, limiting their ability to accurately track rapidly varying dynamic signals. To address this challenge, this paper presents a dynamic phasor measurement method using a recursive Prony algorithm and develops a custom measurement module on the CloudPSS electromagnetic transient simulation platform. The module integrates a recursive least squares (RLS) approach with a forgetting factor and the Prony algorithm to track signal parameters such as frequency, amplitude, and phase in real time, effectively capturing oscillations and sudden frequency changes under dynamic conditions. By constructing and testing a representative test system on the CloudPSS platform, this study validates the proposed method as an effective solution for dynamic monitoring in power systems, while also demonstrating the flexibility and practicality of CloudPSS in supporting custom algorithm development.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction Using Multi-Model Comparative Analysis and LIME Interpretability
authors: Zulfikar Ali Ansari, Nafees Akhter Farooqui, Noorishta Hashmi, Nitin Chopde, Pradumn Kumar Gupta, Gyan Chand Yadev
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a painful and usually symptomless disorder, which requires early diagnosis to avoid renal failure. This study describes a predictive machine learning model of CKD, which combines several classification algorithms and model interpretability by Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME). A CKD dataset with 400 patient records and 26 clinical attributes, which is publicly available, was trained and tested using five supervised learning algorithms, including Logistic Regression (LR), Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN), the Random Forest, and XGBoost. Preprocessing of the data involved normalisation, categorisation, and the filling of missing values to ensure consistency of the models. It was experimentally proven that ensemble models were higher in performance as XGBoost and the Random Forest had 98.8% and 98.5% accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{F} 1=0.99$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AUC}=1.00)$</tex> respectively, which significantly surpassed all other classifiers. The interpretability offered by LIMF revealed the effects of the main biomarkers (serum creatinine, blood urea, haemoglobin, and specific gravity) on the prediction made by the model. The suggested framework produces accurate diagnostic results and, at the same time, improves clinical confidence with its interpretable predictions being transparent. This will be further expanded in future work, based on federated and cross-institutional datasets, to perform scalable CKD diagnosis without compromising privacy.

title: Poseperfect: Real-Time Yoga Pose Recognition and Posture Quality Assessment
authors: Harsh, Harsh Sagar, Naveen Agrahari, Bharat, Mr. Bhuvnesh Malik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Yoga improves physical and mental well-being, but its benefits depend on correct posture execution. Incorrect alignment can reduce effectiveness and increase the risk of injury. While existing AI-based systems largely focus on pose identification, posture quality assessment remains limited.This work presents a yoga pose recognition approach using MediaPipe Pose for extracting 33 body landmarks, from which nine key joint angles are computed to represent pose geometry. These anglebased features are used for classification using a ResNet50-based deep learning model, achieving an accuracy of approximately 85 % on the evaluated dataset.A posture correction mechanism based on comparing user joint angles with reference poses is also conceptually discussed as a future extension. The proposed approach demonstrates the effectiveness of landmark-driven pose representation for yoga pose recognition and provides a foundation for developing real-time posture correction systems.

title: Disaster Prediction and Environmental Risk Analysis Using Machine Learning
authors: Prateek Agarwal, Anshika Agarwal, Abhay Gangawr, Anshul Singh, Akash Singh, Ankit Thapa, Aniket Mohan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: One of the best strategies to curtail the destruction and protect human life is prediction of natural disasters but this is becoming complicated owing to the changing weather patterns. To find the solutions to this issue, it is required to implement the system of artificial intelligence that is being trained on a large body of environmental data to predict the probability of events like floods or hurricanes. Through the resulting predictions, anticipatory risk warnings are created thus allowing the implementation of advance measures that minimize the possible damages in the event such occurrences take place. The instruments of forecasting that we use are being built and justified against a subset of past data, location-specific data, and weather-related data. These contributions operate branches into a basic risk-assessment system that provides the individuals with a simplified model of assessing the threat levels and finding the appropriate reaction.

title: Detection of Rice Leaf Diseases Using Deep Learning and FSDR-SMOTE for Class Imbalance Mitigation
authors: Mukesh Kumar Singh, Prachi Gupta, Omprakash Kushwaha, Jitendra Kumar, Amit Garg, Nidhi Sharma, Pavan Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Rice is one of the most important cereals, especially in Asian countries, where it not only provides subsistence but also creates livelihoods for millions of farmers. Rice diseases such as bacterial leaf blight, brown spot, and rice blast are detrimental for crops and reduce yield as well as quality of crops. Therefore, early identification of these diseases is crucial for sustainable agriculture. Nonetheless, the available dataset on rice diseases suffers from class imbalance, where some classes of diseases is underrepresented, leading to biased model prediction. In this study we integrate a transfer learningdriven convolutional neural network with Feature Space Distribution Refinement Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (FSDR-SMOTE) to address class imbalance. Unlike SMOTE, which generate samples uniformly, FSDR-SMOTE generates synthetic samples in intra-class covariance structures. In this convolutional neural network with transfer learning was employed, and fine-tuning was used for accurate identification of rice diseases. The results show that the proposed model outperforms baseline models without FSDR-SMOTE and achieves an accuracy of 92%.

title: Unified AI-Driven Interoperability in IoT: A Security-Centric Perspective for Next-Generation Networks
authors: Chintham Sandeep, Sheshikala Martha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: A unified security approach to mitigate the threats exposed by sprawling heterogeneous internet of things (IoT) ecosystems is vital but challenging. This study proposes the first unified AI-enabled framework to assess operational autonomy and end-to-end encryption of next-gen IoT networks. Compliance with smart standards to unlock intelligent decision-making is also included. The framework applies deep learning to dynamic threat modeling and graph-based policy synthesis for instantaneous adaptation to architectural, device-level, and protocol shifts. Our design enables compliance with changing SLA provisions in a zero-trust framework while securing data in transit within piecemeal IoT layer siloed architectures. Evaluations across a mixed test smart grid, industrial, and home automation IoT scenarios demonstrated a 28.4 % reduction in inter-platform latency, 37.2% improved attack-initiated flow autoscaling, and mitigation of attack vectors on flow with zero loss of operational throughput. Merely 28.4% cross-platform latency reduction demonstates the pragmatic value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> and capturing cryptographic coordination for achieving cross-cutting interoperability at scale. The approach also closes a policy-driven optimization loop for learning and feedback, instrumental in achieving a design-to-secure interoperation of IoT deployments. This experiment creates a repeatable technique and benchmarking platform that will pioneer policy-aware and AIsecured interoperability in critical future IoT systems.

title: Integration of a Natural Dye (Hibiscus) as Emissive Layer in an OLED
authors: Doh Konate, Ghada M'Barki, Urbain Niangoran, David Buso, N'Guessan Raymond Kre, Georges Zissis
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: We present an exploratory study on the integration of a natural hibiscus extract as the emissive layer (EML) in a multilayer OLED structure composed of ITO (anode), NPD as the hole transport layer, hibiscus (EML), BPhen as the electron transport layer, and Ca as the cathode. Spectroscopic analysis in ethanol reveals that the photoluminescence of the extract exhibits an emission peak around 743 nm. The fabricated OLED device shows a dual emission: one in the blue region, attributed to NPD, and another in the red region, corresponding to the hibiscus emission. Although the luminance remains low (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.6 \text{cd} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> at 14.25 V), the emission spectrum confirms effective electroluminescent activity. This work provides a first proof of concept for the use of natural hibiscus dye in OLED devices. Further optimizations will be undertaken to improve the efficiency and spectral purity of the emission.

title: Correlative Analysis on the Effect of Time and Concentration Variation of Various Enzyme Wash (Acidic, Neutral and Granular) on Denim Garments
authors: Waziha Farha, Umme Habiba Rahman, Rejaul Karim, Md. Alauddin Sany, Md. Limon Hosen Miazi, Sanid Al Rafsan, Tanvir Hossain Ashiq
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Garment wash plays a vital role in denim or casual apparels. A garment was can change outlook, softness, and overall appearance of a garment by changing shade, comfort, dark & light and patchy look. And Enzyme wash plays an important role in garments washing sector in terms of environment and cost. Currently garments industry mainly uses calluses enzyme to speed up the abrasion process. When we think about enzyme, a few milligrams of enzyme can achieve better result, shade, precision and speed which can need tons of stone needs through brute force. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup> In this study we try to understand differentiate between Acidic, Neutral, and Granular enzyme by comparing GSM measurement, Shrinkage Test, color resistance to perspiration, color durability to rubbing, shed retention capability to wash etc.

title: Dense Harmonic Detection Method for Distributed Photovoltaic Grid Connection
authors: Zheng Li, Xifeng Liu, Fang Chen, Le Zhang, Xiaolong Guo, Xiuju Yang, Xiahui Li, Chunkai Xing, Zijing Liu, Wanyue Zhang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In recent years, the grid-connected capacity of distributed photovoltaic power has grown rapidly. The nonlinear characteristics and output power fluctuations of its power electronic inverters have caused dense harmonics, leading to the overlap of grid signal spectra and increasing the difficulty of harmonic parameter estimation. The terminal load of the distribution network is complex, further intensifying the complexity of harmonics. Fundamental spectrum leakage can easily overwhelm weak amplitude harmonics, threatening the safe and stable operation of photovoltaic grid-connected systems. The traditional harmonic detection method based on Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) has the problems of main lobe overlap and side lobe interference in dense harmonic scenarios, and the detection accuracy drops. This paper considers the spectral characteristics and the distribution features of leakage energy, and discriminates the dense components based on neural networks to achieve the robust classification of dense signal components. Based on the discrimination results and frequency domain features, the frequency parameter estimation of multi-component signals is achieved. Simulation tests show that this method has a strong component recognition ability for dense harmonic signals, scalability and noise resistance, providing a supporting technology for harmonic control in distributed photovoltaic grid-connected systems.

title: AI-Driven Solar Tracking and Cleaning for Energy Optimization
authors: G.V. Meenakshi, S. Shalini Priyanka, R. Raghavan, Kavitha Balamurugan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The project substantially enhances the efficiency and lifespan of solar panels through AI-assisted sun tracking, along with a smart cleaning system. AI-Driven Smart Sun Tracker and Automated Cleaning System is a low-cost, scalable, and environmentally friendly way of achieving the maximum solar energy output with a clean and stable energy source for the future. They require routine maintenance and cleaning to continue at maximum efficiency. This project aims to design a Smart Sun Tracker system, which adjusts the solar panel position smartly to keep it constantly in line with the sun's light. Sensors, motors, and an algorithmic controller are used to monitor the sun's path across the sky and optimize the angle of the solar panels for peak energy production. In addition to this, a dust sensor is incorporated into the system that triggers a cleaning mechanism upon achieving a predetermined threshold of dust buildup. This keeps the panel surface free from dust, ensuring maximum functionality. The integrated system maximizes overall solar panel efficiency, increases their lifespan, and makes solar energy more dependable. By resolving key areas of solar panel maintenance, the project enhances day-long energy output through dynamic tracking and minimizes human intervention requirements. Integration of smart technology makes maintenance easier and keeps panels clean by minimizing downtime. The system is versatile in terms of handling different environmental conditions, minimizing cost by improving efficiency and longevity. This project encourages the scalability of solar energy technology, assists in the development of sustainable energy, and may lead to additional innovation in renewable energy technology.

title: An Improved Phase-Locked Loop Method for Three-Phase Unbalanced Voltage
authors: Gong Zhang, Bo Zhou, Dongmei Zhao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Under the trend of weak power grid in the power system, the three-phase electricity in the grid often experiences three-phase imbalance and harmonic conditions, which pose higher requirements for the rapidity, accuracy, and stability of the phase-locked loop. Considering that the traditional phase-locked loop method is difficult to meet these requirements, this paper puts forward an improved phase-locked loop method for three-phase unbalanced voltage. Use the improved second order generalized integral phase-locked loop(SOGI-PLL) to perform line voltage phase-locked and phase angle detection; Heron formula and Sine theorem is used for the three-phase phase angle calculation and phase voltage amplitude calculation. The correctness of the proposed design method is verified through Simulink simulation experiments. Research results show that when grid voltage has a DC voltage offset, harmonic interference and voltage sag, this phase-locked method can still detect phase changes accurately and quickly.

title: Edge—Cloud Collaborative Predictive Analytics for Smart Grid Fault Detection and Maintenance
authors: Anup Gade, Anil Kumar, Durga Prasad Yadav, Akhil Gupta, Monali Gulhane, Pawan Kumar, Nitin Rakesh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The process of assembling smart grids has been accelerated through modernization of power systems. In such systems, fault discovery and pre-empting maintenance are major concerns towards ensuring that the systems run very well. Conventional deterministic approaches tend to show latency problems, bandwidth problems and the inability to respond to grid conditions that vary rapidly. Through this study, it is demonstrated how edge and cloud predictive analytics can be applied together to detect and correct faults in smart grids. This is supposed to assist in these problems. This structure functions by having the edge devices and the cloud systems collaborate to learn with time and react in real time. It obtains sensor and Internet of Things (IoT) information at the edge continuously on various aspects of the grid, such as power lines, substations, and spread green sources. The existence of edge computer units allows identifying problems in real time and categorizing them due to their preparation, noise reduction, and feature extraction. The cloud infrastructure generates enormous real and artificial data databases to update advanced machine learning and deep learning models. This is to make sure that models are able to handle new mistakes and work better. This two-way communication minimizes downtime, stabilization of the grid and predictive repair costs. The proposed method can offer scalable, reliable and accuracy in smart grid management. This is accomplished using live edge detection, and retraining on the cloud. Validation through both real and artificial data sets increases accuracy, memory and mean time between failures.

title: Incremental Graph Neural Networks for Dynamic Channel Assignment in HAN and NAN Deployments
authors: Pavan Parola
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In Home and Neighborhood Area Networks (HANs) and (NANs), dynamic channel reassignment requires conflict-free frequency allocation with minimal network disruption during topological changes. Our paper proposes an incremental Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework that combines both Graph-SAGE and Graph Attention Network (GAT) architectures for minimal channel reassignments in smart grid communication networks. Our method utilizes a Dynamic Graph Processor for structural feature extraction. We combine this with conflict-aware training which uses adaptive penalty functions. Our rigorous experiments on synthetic graphs (ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 - 1 0 0 0}$</tex> nodes) demonstrate our approach achieves zero interference conflicts while reducing channel changes by 99.4 % compared to that of DSATUR (1.1 vs 185.1 average changes per update) while maintaining solid computation times across different network types.

title: Hybrid CNN-LSTM Framework for Real-Time Deepfake Detection with Spatio-Temporal Analysis
authors: Sorabh Gupta, Amit Kumar Bindal, Gulshan Dasmana, Aayush Shrivastva, Akshay Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Deepfake media are of concern with regard to digital disinformation, identity theft and integrity of multimedia content. To overcome these challenges, this paper offers a resource-efficient, portable deepfake detection system to apply in the real world. The system architecture comprises of the inputs, face recognition and alignment, hybrid CNN/LSTM features recognition, classification, and output. The system is flexible to various deepfake methods and deployment environments and conditions due to a modular architecture. The dataset of the proposed solution is the Deepfake Detection Challenge. Preprocessing consists of frame extraction, Haar Cascades, facial landmarks to face alignment, flipping, rotating and contrast modulation. MesoNet CNN structures with hybrid CNNLSTM have anomalies of deepfakes in terms of space and time. Accuracy, precision and F1-score experimental results are 91.5, 91.2 and 90.7. The findings with low-light, distorted faces, and different facial features prove the resilience of the model. The system can be used on small computer infrastructure with real-time applications because the latency to inference is low. This paper demonstrates that deepfake detectors based on realistic methods can be precise at small hardware. This trade-off between computational complexity and detection rates is very large, allowing real-time media verification systems to be scaled.

title: Research on the Insulation Performance of $\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \mathrm{O}_{2}$ and $\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \text{CO}_{2}$ Mixed Gases
authors: Liang Zhang, Dandan Han, Meng Zou, Shihao Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The mixed gas of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> can reduce the amount of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> used and has research significance. This paper conducts experimental research on the discharge characteristics of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \text{CO}_{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> mixed gases under different conditions. Firstly, for the insulation strength test of SF6 and its mixed gases, the necessary equipment for the test was set up, including a test chamber, transformer, pressure gauge, voltage regulator. The test was conducted under a slightly uneven electric field (electrode distance of 2 mm). Subsequently, experimental analysis was conducted on the discharge process of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \text{CO}_{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> gases with different mixing ratios under normal pressure and mixed gases at 0.2-0.5MPa. The breakdown voltage of the mixed gas also increases approximately linearly with increasing pressure. The breakdown voltage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \text{CO}_{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6} / \mathrm{N}_{2} / \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> mixed gases increases with the increase of the mixing ratio <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K$</tex>, while the electrode distance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex> and gas pressure <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{P}$</tex> remain constant. Different mixed gases have different changes in breakdown voltage when the mixing ratio changes. This experiment tested two systems and obtained the discharge characteristics of mixed gases in slightly uneven electric fields. By comparing the breakdown voltage with pure <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> gas, it provides experimental basis for reducing the use of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> and optimizing environmentally friendly insulation gas, and provides reference value for the research of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> mixed gases.

title: FASHION 4.0: Managing the Shift Towards Sustainable and Digital Fashion Ecosystems
authors: Santha Sorubini P, Jayasree Krishnan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Modern digital revolutions in the fashion industry result from the industry-wide use of technology systems, including the Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI), blockchain protocols, and Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR). Advanced technologies have transformed product development and business strategies, standard operating methods, and sustainability measures throughout the fashion industry. The fashion industry currently ranks among the highest global polluters, increasing pressures for environmental reforms. Digital tools increase supply chain visibility while improving manufacturing efficiency through platforms such as Farfetch, Stitch Fix, and Roblox's Nikeland. Virtual Fitting Rooms (VFRs) integrated with mobile applications enhance satisfaction and reduce product returns, while blockchain supports transparency and trust. Immersive technologies reduce waste and enable consumer education. This study analyses modern fashion patterns, identifies gaps in implementation, and proposes strategic directions for sustainable industrial improvement.

title: NeuroBridge – A Federated AI System for Real-Time Mental Health Crisis Detection Using Multi-Device Behavioral Drift
authors: Skandesh Goutham Ranganathan J, Parvathy K S, Umamageswaran J
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The increasing incidence of mental health crises demands the development of proactive, privacy-conscious, and real-time intervention approaches. Although several AI-driven tools have been envisioned over the past few years, most are restricted by centralized data structures, one-dimensional analy sis, and non-real-time responsiveness. In this paper, we propose NeuroBridge, a new, software-only federated AI design with the goal of identifying early warning signs of mental health decline via passive behavioral drift analysis across a variety of personal devices such as smartphones, web browsers, and wearable devices. In contrast to prior approaches that depend on centralized data aggregation or users' explicit contribution, NeuroBridge harnesses the power of strong on-device models coupled with cross-device behavioral patterns, including reduced typing speed, unstable app switching, delayed response to messages, and changing vocal tone. Behavioral drift is represented as a dynamic temporal signal, not static snapshots, which allows for more contextualized and incremental interpretation of the user's men tal states. The platform leverages federated learning to ensure data privacy with constant model updates from anonymized, localized training in devices. NeuroBridge utilizes differential privacy mechanisms and secure aggregation protocols to maintain privacy. The system further includes an advanced escalation process that selects appropriate interventions—ranging from journal prompts to alerting guardians—based on evaluated risk levels. By mapping out complete representations of system architecture, data flow visualizations, and real-world demonstrations of use cases, this paper demonstrates how NeuroBridge brings together mental health research, artificial intelligence, and user-centered software development. By setting aside the ethical use of AI and real-time flexibility without compromising the confidentiality of users, NeuroBridge sets a standard for future digital mental health systems.

title: Multi-Sequence Control Method for Output Power Balance in Distribution Systems Under Environmental Uncertainty
authors: Guilian Wu, Jinlin Liao, Shiyuan Ni, Sudan Lai
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In power distribution systems, only the relationship between wind turbine output power and wind speed is deterministic, resulting in poor system stability. Therefore, a multi-sequence control method for balancing distribution system output power under environmental uncertainty is proposed. Drawing on the Gaussian mixture model, a multi-sequence control framework is established, covering four levels: ultra-short-term, short-term, medium-term, and long-term. Energy storage device charging and discharging are flexibly controlled based on realtime operating conditions. A power balancing circuit is designed within the multi-sequence framework. Through coordinated control at different levels, a balanced and stable distribution system output power is achieved. Experimental results demonstrate that the design method can rapidly suppress power fluctuations, achieving a multi-sequence power balance deviation rate as low as 6.8% for 2000 samples. Furthermore, the method effectively avoids malfunctions at illumination levels of 800-1200 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{W} \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> and temperatures of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-35^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, improving the stability of the distribution system under complex environments.

title: Omni-Bot: An Autonomous Inspection Robot for Underground Parking Garages Using Multi-Modal SLAM and Reinforcement Learning-Based Navigation
authors: Chenyang Zhang, Yinong Cao, Xin He, Pingfeng He, Bingtao Wang, Hang Yang, Tingwei Wang, Changhui Jiang, Fei Han, Fashuai Li, Yuwei Chen
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Underground parking garages present a challenging sensing and navigation setting due to the absence of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) signals, limited illumination, and repetitive structural motifs. We develop Omni-Bot, an autonomous inspection platform that integrates a four-view panoramic industrial camera array, a 360° Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) sensor (Livox Mid-360), and a highfrequency inertial measurement unit (IMU) to realize multimodal odometry and mapping through the proposed Omni-LIVO pipeline. On top of this state estimator, a hybrid local planning framework merges a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based reinforcement learning (RL) policy with a Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) feasibility filter, ensuring that learned maneuvers remain dynamically safe. A lightweight image enhancement module stabilizes low-light observations before YOLO-based semantic detections are projected into the LiDAR grid map via calibrated camera-LiDAR extrinsics, which enables target tagging without perturbing the navigation controller. Field trials conducted in operational garages report sub- 0.20 m mapping error over kilometer-scale traversals, a 97 % navigation success rate with minimum 0.42 m obstacle clearance, and robust licenseplate recognition under severe illumination degradation. These results demonstrate that the proposed perception and planning stack delivers a practical inspection solution for GNSS-denied, visually and geometrically degraded indoor environments.

title: A Novel Spectral Sequence Fingerprint Localization Method for Unmodulated VLP
authors: Hao Zhang, Yuan Zhuang, Tianming Huang, Jiasheng Zhou, Xiansheng Yang
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Visible light positioning technology has garnered significant attention due to its high precision and the ubiquity of infrastructure, and is considered one of the most promising technologies for achieving low-cost indoor positioning and navigation services. However, this technology typically requires external hardware for modulation, a limitation that severely hinders its large-scale application. To address this, this paper proposes a non-modulated visible light fingerprint positioning method based on RGB three-channel spectra. This method captures inherent differences in the spectral composition of lighting LEDs to construct distinctive spectral sequence fingerprints, and utilizes an LSTM-based network to model temporal dependencies, enabling regression from spectral features to position sequences. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve meter-level positioning under ordinary indoor lighting conditions without relying on external hardware modulation.

title: Cyclone-Induced Rainfall Extremes Using GEV Analysis Along Cyclone Fani's Track
authors: Viaan Sharma, Prashant Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Tropical cyclones are responsible for widespread flooding and extreme precipitation in coastal regions. This study investigates the spatial and statistical characteristics of rainfall extremes induced by Cyclone Fani, a high-impact storm that affected Eastern India in May 2019. We employ satellite-based IMERG precipitation data and cyclone trajectory data from IBTrACS. Using the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution, we model the rainfall maxima and estimate return levels at impacted grid locations. The spatial rainfall climatology, fitted GEV parameters, return level predictions, and comparisons with long-term means offer a comprehensive view of flood potential during the cyclone. This integrated data-driven approach supports future flood preparedness and climate resilience planning.

title: Evolutionary Game Model for Lane-Changing Decisions of Autonomous Trucks
authors: Zhankai Yang, Xiangdong Hu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: In view of the dynamic interaction characteristics of expressways, an evolutionary game model for the lane-changing behavior of autonomous trucks is proposed. By quantifying the coupling relationship among safety, efficiency, and environment using the composite loss coefficient, the cargo value weight and the environmental sensitivity, the eigenvalue analysis of the Jacob matrix is employed to analyze the asymptotic stability of the system equilibrium points. Simulation results validate a fundamental strategic dichotomy: conservative behaviors dominate under elevated safety parameters, whereas aggressive lane-changing prevails when timeliness parameters dominate. This elucidates the evolutionary mechanism of multi-agent collaboration, providing an actionable framework for dynamic traffic control that balances safety and efficiency in mixedautonomy scenarios.

title: Research on Motor Fault Diagnosis Method Based on MFCC-GFCC Fusion and LDA Dimension Reduction
authors: Jinlong Li, Zhongbin Chen, Wei Li, Zhiqiang Shuai
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of incomplete representation of single features and low computational efficiency of high-dimensional features in audio diagnosis of motor faults, a feature optimization method that integrates MEL frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCC) and gamma-pass frequency cepstral coefficients (GFCC) and combines linear discriminant analysis (LDA) for dimensionality reduction is proposed. This method first extracts the MFCC and GFCC features of the audio signal, and then concatenates them into fused features after normalization and sequence alignment. Subsequently, LDA is utilized to project the high-dimensional features onto the lowdimensional discriminative subspace, significantly reducing the data dimension while retaining the maximum inter-class discrimination degree. Finally, the optimized features are input into the Gaussian Mixture Model - General Background Model (GMM-UBM) for training and recognition. The experimental results on the IDMT-ISA-ELECTRIC-ENGINE dataset show that the equal error rate (EER) of this method (MFCC20+GFCC5+LDA2) is as low as 0.0125, and both the accuracy rate and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 {\%}}$</tex>. Moreover, feature dimensionality reduction has increased the efficiency of model training and testing by more than 50%. This study confirms that the proposed feature processing scheme can effectively enhance the performance and efficiency of motor fault audio recognition.

title: SEMD: An Attention-Enhanced Multi-Decoder Seq2Seq for Direct Multi-Step Wind Power Forecasting
authors: Yao Zhou, Xuduo Cai, Wangxin Fang, Yixiong Jia, Xingyu Lei
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Accurate multi-horizon wind power forecasting is essential for secure and economical power-system operation. To achieve this goal, we propose a Single-Encoder Multi-Decoder (SEMD) sequence-to-sequence architecture that produces direct multi-step predictions in one forward pass while explicitly modeling cross-horizon dependencies. Concretely, a shared encoder extracts historical representations, and a decoder-side temporal multi-head self-attention layer couples the sequence of future steps before horizon-specific decoders generate per-horizon outputs. We evaluate the approach on two real-world wind-farm datasets at 15-, 30-, and 45-minute horizons using MSE, MAE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and compare against Independent Single-Horizon (ISH) and Joint Multi-Horizon (JMH) baselines. Experiments show that SEMD generally achieves lower error than both baselines; the gain over JMH is most pronounced at 15 minutes (MSE improvement more than 20%), and the gain over ISH increases with horizon length (more than 10% MSE at 45 minutes). In addition, SEMD requires substantially less training time than ISH while remaining close to JMH, striking a favorable accuracy-efficiency balance. These results indicate that combining shared encoding with decoder-side temporal attention is an effective recipe for multi-horizon wind power forecasting.

title: Coordinated Control for Connected and Automated Buses at Stops
authors: Xinyue Wei, Liying Wei
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: Considering the phenomenon of queue spillback at bus stops that seriously impairs overall traffic efficiency, this study proposes a coordinated control strategy for connected and automated buses (CABs) within the intersection to facilitate more sustainable development of urban traffic and bus priority system. The strategy integrates an enhanced vehicle kinematic model with an adaptive signal control scheme to jointly achieve real-time speed optimization for CABs and dynamic right-of-way distribution at the intersection. Based on the SUMO simulation platform, we conducted comparative analyses of four control strategies under different traffic flow rates. The results demonstrate that the proposed integrated approach not only significantly reduces bus delays and energy consumption while effectively constraining the queue length at the stop, but also exerts no adverse impact on the travel efficiency of private cars.

title: Procceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)

title: Digital Modeling of the Frequency Characteristics of the “Grain-Matrix” System of Diamond-Abrasive Tools
authors: Martin M. Yakhutlov, Sergey A. Sheptunov, Vladimir N. Azarov, Maryana R. Kardanova, Oxana V. Islamova, Zalim N. Deunezhev
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: A digital model of the stress-strain state in the grain-matrix system of a diamond abrasive tool under an external force load changing according to a harmonic law has been developed. The model is based on the solution of a dynamic problem of elasticity theory using the finite element method. It allows one to obtain the amplitude-frequency characteristics and oscillation shapes of the system under study. An analysis of the influence of system parameters on natural oscillation frequencies was performed and an assessment of the possibility of occurrence of resonance phenomena during operation of the tool was made.

title: Automatic Camera Calibration Method for Solving the Problem of Moving Object Detection
authors: Diana I. Kalita, Pavel A. Lyakhov, Ulyana A. Lyakhova, Anzor R. Orazaev, Viktoria D. Kovaleva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: Camera calibration is a key task in computer vision and plays an important role in many applications related to visual surveillance. A calibrated camera allows correcting perspective distortions of an object's image. In addition, it provides the ability to extract metric information from images that is robust to changes in viewing angle. Using calibrated cameras, it is possible to use pre-known 3D models to estimate the spatial position of objects, which increases the resilience of detection and tracking systems to noise and partial occlusions. This paper presents an automated method for restoring camera parameters from traffic video recordings. With only camera installation height data, the proposed algorithm allows fully determining both internal and external camera parameters based on the appearance and dynamics of objects in the video. The method has been tested under various conditions, and the results obtained confirm its effectiveness and practical applicability in moving object detection tasks.

title: Quantum Errors Analysis of a Superconducting Quantum Processor
authors: Jawwad Farooq, Ekram Khan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Quantum devices in the noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) era are highly susceptible to different types of quantum noise, which directly impact the reliability of experimental results. This work provides a characterization of error behavior arising from quantum noises on IBM's superconducting quantum processor, offering practical insights for algorithm design, benchmarking, and error-mitigation strategies on a real superconducting quantum processor in the NISQ era. The study begins with the measurement of the basis state, without applying any gate, to quantify state-preparation and measurement (SPAM) errors. Additionally, the errors are further quantified due to the application of single-qubit Pauli-X, two-qubit CNOT gate and other multi-qubit Controlled-X gates. Each circuit was executed with 1024 shots, and the percentage error between the expected and observed outputs was computed. The results show that the single-qubit Pauli-X gate exhibits relatively low errors in comparison with the two-qubit CNOT gate, whereas other multi-qubit Controlled-X gates lead to significantly higher deviations. This work also analyzes the noise effect of a fourqubit Controlled-X gate being implemented using a three-qubit Controlled-X gate with an extra auxiliary qubit, and the result shows a significant reduction in the error rate.

title: LSTM-Enhanced Wireless Clock Synchronization for UWB Systems
authors: Wenhua Tong, Bofeng Li, Jing Qiao
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Ultra-wideband (UWB) is renowned in Internet of Things (IoT) scenarios for its high positioning update rates and centimeter-level accuracy. Time Difference of Arrival (TDOA)-based positioning combines time measurements from multiple devices to estimate the location of a target device. However, these measurements are expressed in each device's own time scale, which is inherently offset and exhibits distinct time-varying drifts that must be compensated for during the estimation process. Inspired by this challenge, this paper introduces a novel sequential data-driven approach to wireless clock synchronization (WCS) based on the long short-term memory (LSTM). Experimental results show that, compared with the error accuracies of 0.0319 m and 0.0252 m relative to the two master anchors (MAs) achieved by traditional approaches relying on historical parameters to estimate future clock offsets, the proposed approach improves the accuracy to 0.0308 m and 0.0242 m. Furthermore, compared with deferred approaches that wait for the next Clock Correction Packet (CCP), the proposed approach achieves comparable estimation accuracy while reducing deferring from 0.1 s to 0.01 s. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach attains accurate tracking and prediction of clock deviations while maintaining real-time performance, achieving higher precision time mapping, and reducing TDOA measurement uncertainty.

title: CNNs and Vision Transformers for Human Action Recognition on the Kaggle HAR Image Dataset: A Clean-Split, Calibrated Benchmark with Statistical Testing
authors: Seema Malik, Himanshu Mittal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: We present a comparative study of five ImageNet-pretrained architectures for image-based Human Action Recognition (HAR) on the Kaggle “Human Action Recognition (HAR)” dataset: ResNet-50, EfficientNet-B0, ConvNeXt-Tiny, ViT-Tiny/16, and Swin-Tiny. All models are trained with a single PyTorch pipeline that standardizes preprocessing, augmentations, optimization (AdamW), and selection of the best-epoch checkpoint by validation macro-F1. On the held-out test split, Swin-T and ResNet-50 provide the strongest results (top-1 accuracy 83.7% and 83.2%; macro-F1 83.6% and 83.0%), closely followed by ViT-T/16 (81.6%) and EfficientNet-B0 (81.1%); ConvNeXt-Tiny trails at 76.7%. Beyond accuracy, we report macro/micro/weighted-F1, macro-precision/recall, top-5, and one-vs-rest ROC-AUC with per-class breakdowns and normalized confusion matrices. We examine learning dynamics, robustness notes, and calibration considerations. Results suggest that hierarchical transformers (Swin) and classic CNNs (ResNet) achieve comparable performance on this medium-scale snapshot HAR, with consistent confusions among visually similar categories (sitting vs. using_laptop; calling vs. listening_to_music). We release a plotting script that produces all figures used herein.

title: Knowledge-Driven Fault Diagnosis Method for Deep-Sea Equipment
authors: Jingyuan Yang, Qi Jiang, Wenfei Li, Zhenjie Wang, Yong Han
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: In contemporary knowledge-driven fault diagnosis research for deep-sea equipment, the relationships between entities and long-tail entities (e.g., rare components or infrequent faults) are often overlooked, resulting in limited diagnostic performance. To address this, this paper proposes a knowledge graph(KG) embedding model based on self-supervised contrastive learning, named MECL, with ocean-going diesel engines as the research object. The model effectively captures edge information and long-tail entity features in the graph, enabling rapid inference of potential fault causes and associated operations. Experimental results demonstrate that MECL significantly outperforms baseline models in link prediction on both public datasets and a self-constructed marine diesel engine maintenance knowledge graph (MEKG). This study provides efficient and intelligent fault diagnosis and maintenance decision support for deep-sea equipment, substantially enhancing equipment support capabilities during ocean voyages.

title: Research on Mixed Task Allocation for AGV in Automated Warehousing
authors: Hailong Song, Jiandong Qiu, Guangcai Lei, Shutong Liu
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: For automated guided vehicles (AGVs) operating in automated warehouses, hybrid task allocation presents a complex and challenging decision-making problem. Based on this, this paper investigates an AGV task allocation method utilizing adaptive large neighborhood search and harmonic search algorithms. Building upon the hybrid task execution sequence established by the adaptive large neighborhood search and harmonic search algorithms, an unbalanced task decomposition strategy for AGVs is proposed. A mathematical model for AGV task allocation is formulated, aiming to maximize time savings and minimize travel distance. Finally, the improved ALNS-HS algorithm accomplishes unbalanced task allocation in automated warehousing. Results demonstrate that the improved algorithm reduces task allocation time, generates balanced task sequences for AGVs across all zones, and ensures equitable task distribution per vehicle.

title: Optimizing Quality Assurance with Software Metrics: A Hybrid QA Framework
authors: Shiva Tyagi, Rajat Singh, Priyanshu Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Software Metrics and Quality Assurance (Hybrid QA) discusses how modern development environments can improve software quality, performance, and reliability by combining traditional and Agile software metrics. The study emphasizes how quantitative metrics like defect density, maintainability, automation coverage, velocity, and defect removal efficiency can be used to evaluate and enhance software development processes. While lines of code and cyclomatic complexity are examples of conventional software metrics that offer structural insights, they are not flexible enough to accommodate the rapid, iterative nature of Agile and DevOps processes. This paper overcomes this lacuna by proposing a Hybrid QA Framework that offers the best of both worlds: conventional precision mixed with Agile flexibility, underpinned by automation and CI/CD pipelines. By comparative analysis and graphical modeling, the study also shows that hybrid metric adoption and automation significantly reduce defect density while improving delivery efficiency and customer satisfaction. The findings suggest a better approach that offers a scalable and efficient solution for maintaining software quality in evolving software development ecosystems.

title: Polymer-Based Wearable Antenna with Frequency Selective Surface for Enhanced WBAN Performance
authors: Shivani Sharma, Dipali Bansal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) has been effectively incorporated along with the wearable patch antenna to reduce on-body losses and improve the proposed antenna's radiating gain for Wireless Body Area Networks (WBAN) in the presented work. Both the antenna and miniaturized FSS sheet is structured on flexible polymer-based substrates with lower heat dissipation rate and bending endurance. The designed antenna operates with resonant frequency at 5.8 GHz of Industrial Scientific Medical (ISM) band for wireless applications. To reduce the effect of Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) and to enhance the gain of the designed antenna, a thin sheet of FSS is arranged below the proposed antenna as an Artificial Magnetic Conductor (AMC) reflector. The value of gain achieved with FSS is 10 dB with the reflection coefficient of –39.11 and the Voltage Standing Wave Ratio (VSWR) of 1.02. The proposed antenna can be used in WBAN for the medical applications with a bandwidth of 200 MHz.

title: A New Dynamic Reconfiguration Technique for Solar Powered EV to Improve the Power Extraction
authors: Prince Winston David, Prem Ganesh Kumar, John Raj Sethuraman, G. Sakthivel, Partha Sarathy Suresh, Palpandian Murugesan, Kesavan Murugesan
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The partial shading on the Photovoltaic (PV) array used for an electric vehicle to charge the battery bank substantially reduces the charging efficiency and output power. The partial shading on a series-connected module reduces the array current, leads to a reduction of output power. To eradicate this occurrence, this research introduces the adaptive reconfiguration algorithm to avoid the series connection of partially shaded modules. The proposed adaptive reconfiguration algorithm has five sets of modules, with a fixed part located on top, with 4 modules series-connected, which are parallel connected to the adaptive part with 2 series-connected modules each on front & back, and 2 seriesconnected modules each on right & left sides of the electric vehicle. During the partial shading, the algorithm reconfigures the adaptive part, and it connects the modules with higher and lower irradiation as a separate series-connected string. This approach improves the string current and array current to boost the output power. For validation, the proposed algorithm is assessed on six partial shading scenarios by evaluation parameters. Notably, the proposed algorithm has delivered superior performance with a performance enhancement of 52 % for case 3.

title: PVDF-Based PD Detection for Electrical Equipment Research on Acoustic-Magnetic Integrated High-Sensitivity Perception Technology
authors: Ding Cong Lin, Xiao Er Liu, Kun Zhang, Hao Chen, Zu Fei Yan, Da Peng Han, Guo Zhi Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) constitutes an early indicator of insulation degradation in electrical equipment. Accurate detection of PD signals is crucial for ensuring the safe operation of power systems. Conventional PD detection technologies, such as ultrasonic detection and ultra-high frequency (UHF) detection, suffer from limitations including single-function sensors, weak anti-interference capabilities, and difficulties in internal installation. To address these issues, a polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF)-based acoustic-magnetic integrated sensor is proposed: utilising PVDF as the substrate to achieve simultaneous sensing of ultrasonic signals (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \text{kHz}-200 \text{kHz}$</tex>) and UHF signals (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.3 \text{GHz}-3 \text{GHz}$</tex>); The sensor structure is optimised using a trapezoidal ground plane, combined with a multivariate joint optimisation method to refine critical parameters including medium plate thickness, floor height, and feeder spacing. Simulation verification using HFSS and COMSOL software demonstrates: the sensor exhibits a voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) below 5 across the 0.45 GHz - 3 GHz band, and below 3 within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.51 \text{GHz}-3 \text{GHz}$</tex> band; with an ultrasonic resonance frequency of 100 kHz and maximum sensitivity reaching 63dB. While meeting PD detection requirements, the sensor achieves miniaturisation and internal installation, offering a novel solution for integrated acoustic-magnetic PD detection in electrical equipment.

title: A Novel CQO-CFOA Empowered 3DoF Cascaded Control Strategy for Enhanced Frequency Regulation in Multi-Area Restructured Power System Considering Practical Challenges
authors: Ranjan Kumar, Pranav Prakash Singh, Ashiwani Kumar, Abhishek
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This study describes a load frequency control (LFC) mechanism for a two-area deregulated power system integrating thermal and hydro plants. A novel Chaotic Quasi-Oppositional Catch Fish Optimization Algorithm (CQO-CFOA) tunes controller parameters to boost frequency regulation under varying conditions. The suggested hybrid controller improves overall stability and dynamic frequency response. It is a three-degree-of-freedom fractional-order PID with derivative filter (3DoF-PFIDN) cascaded with a proportional-integral-proportional derivative (PI+DI). Compared to conventional optimisation methods, integrating CQO-CFOA with the hybrid controller results in improved damping, better exploration-exploitation balance, and faster convergence. The deregulated two-area system's performance under various load disruptions and contractual scenarios is evaluated using simulation studies. The approach's superiority for contemporary unregulated networks is confirmed by the results, which exhibit decreased overshoot, undershoot, and settling time along with increased stability against nonlinearities.

title: 2024 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)

title: Application Exploration of Embedded Control Platform in Industrial Collaborative Robots
authors: Cimin Su, Chunxing Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: As the “brain” and “central nervous system” of industrial collaborative robots, the performance and design philosophy of the embedded control platform directly determine the intelligence level, safety, and ease of use of the robot. Collaborative robots impose stringent requirements on the realtime performance, precision, and safety of control systems. The embedded control platform, with its high integration and low power consumption, provides an ideal control solution for collaborative robots. Unlike traditional industrial robots that often adopt the “PC + motion control card” architecture, collaborative robots generally utilize highly integrated embedded control platforms due to comprehensive considerations of size, cost, power consumption, and real-time performance. This platform is not merely a computing unit but a comprehensive system integrating hardware, low-level software, middleware, and core algorithms. By constructing a heterogeneous control architecture with an ARM Cortex-A72 processor and an FPGA coprocessor, it achieves a control cycle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0}$</tex> microseconds and a system response latency of 3.2 milliseconds. Typical industrial scenario validation tests demonstrate that its assembly accuracy can reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.05$</tex> millimeters. Compared to traditional PC-based control systems, the embedded control platform achieves performance improvements of 60 % and 63.2 % in control cycle and response latency, respectively.

title: AcciGuard: Smart Accident Detection and Emergency Response for Intelligent Transportation System
authors: Raghuvansh Mani Singh, Saurabh Singh, Rhythm Garg, Pawan Kumar Singh, Anju Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Road accidents are increasingly becoming a worldwide issue, and their impact poses a threat to human life. The ever-increasing population density and growth of economies are contributing factors towards road accidents in developing countries like India. The problem further worsens because of increased traffic on roads and less safety infrastructure available to handle individual cases, especially in developing and underdeveloped countries. This work proposes a smart accident detection and emergency response system (AcciGuard) to handle these issues and provide a cost-effective solution to road safety issues due to accidents happening, especially on highways, where timely medical assistance requires precise location of the accident spot as well as early detection of critical and life-threatening collision impacts.
