Acciguard is an IoT-based real-time crash-detection and fast-emergency-alarm system that can be incorporated in vehicles to support smart transportation systems, further improving the smart cities' infrastructure. Acciguard employs onboard sensors, microcontrollers, and cloud-based analytics to automatically identify vehicular collisions, the level of collision impact, and inform the emergency services and registered contacts and the nearest police station with real-time location information. The architecture consists of an accelerator and GPS devices combined with a communication layer based on GSM API and cloud API, which provides robust data transmission with low latency. An anomaly detection module built upon machine learning increases the level of accuracy by distinguishing between real accidents and false positives. Under different driving and impact conditions, experimental validation indicates great accuracy in the recognition of events and lower response time.

title: Numerical Study on Dielectric Structural Design for Flexible MEMS Capacitive Pressure Sensors in Electrical Vehicle (EV) Safety Applications
authors: H. M. M. Hajizi, H. Aris, M. H. Ismail, N. I. M. Nor, A.N. Nordin, S. Johari, A. A. Aziz
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of dielectric geometry on the sensitivity and mechanical response of a flexible MEMS capacitive pressure sensor designed for thermal runaway detection in electric vehicle (EV) batteries. A sandwiched parallel-plate architecture was adopted with consistent material and structural parameters, while only the dielectric geometry was varied. Five sensor designs were evaluated: one with a flat dielectric layer and four with dielectric grids of increasing complexity (2 × 2 to 5 × 5 arrays). The designs were simulated using IntelliSuite® under a pressure sweep of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-0.5 \text{MPa}$</tex>. Results revealed that increasing the number of grid cells in the dielectric structure enhances sensor sensitivity. Design 5, with a 5×5 grid, demonstrated the highest capacitance change <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\Delta \mathrm{C}=4.49 \text{nF})$</tex> and mechanical displacement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\Delta Z=0.427 \mu \mathrm{m})$</tex>, compared to the flat dielectric Design <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1(\Delta C=1.17 \mathbf{n F}, \Delta Z=0.061 \mu \mathrm{m})$</tex>. Additionally, von Mises stress distribution analysis revealed that higher grid numbers resulted in reduced stress concentration across the diaphragm, indicating improved mechanical reliability. These findings confirm that dielectric geometric optimisation significantly improves both sensitivity and stress distribution, offering a promising strategy for high-performance, flexible pressure sensors in safety-critical EV applications.

title: IoT-Enabled Solar-Powered Precise Home Agriculture for Plant-Specific Water Management
authors: Dikshant Lanjewar, Purva Raut, Koyal Gote, Yash Waware, Subodh Thote, Shital. S. Kewte
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Water scarcity is a major issue Faced by the Home gardener in the home agriculture. The traditional irrigation systems often wasteful and inefficient due to lack of awareness. The irrigation Scheduling reduce the water wastage. Precise home agriculture can take over the traditional farming by applying technological advancement such the sensors and IOT which improve efficiency and reducing the amount of the stress on the farmers. This paper presents solar powered IoT based home irrigation system for plant specific volumetric requirement water management dispenses water to different plants such as fruit, vegetable, and ornamental species based on their real needs. The systems include solar panel, Battery storage, ESP-32 microcontroller, sensors for automatic irrigation scheduling. The system utilizes both rainwater and well water which is managed by float sensors and selection of dual DC pumps via solenoidoperated valves. Individual plant requirements are set with potentiometers connected to an ESP-32 microcontroller, allowing precise and environmentally conscious water delivery. The tomato and chill's plant were selected to demonstrate differential water requirement according their growth. The solar energy powers the whole setup with battery Backup which ensures sustainability as well as off grid functionality. The data is displayed on Blynk IoT app for remote monitoring and analysis.

title: Enhanced Cluster Head Management for Energy-Efficient Wireless Sensor Networks with Mobile Sink Support
authors: Musa Ahmed Zayyad, Shamsu Abdullahi, Anas Yahaya, Illani Bt Mohd Nawi, Kamaluddeen Usman Danyaro, Mujeeb Ur Rehman Shaikh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) comprise battery-powered sensor nodes that monitor and transmit data in remote or harsh environments. Energy efficiency remains a key challenge, as replacing or recharging batteries is often impractical. While protocols like the Enhanced Energy Efficient Routing Algorithm with Mobile Sink Support (EERAMSS) aim to minimize energy usage, they still suffer from re-clustering overhead and premature cluster head (CH) depletion. This paper proposes an improved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C H}$</tex> management algorithm using static clustering and residual energy-based selection to reduce unnecessary CH rotations. Simulations in NS2 show that the algorithm improves stability, node longevity, and energy dynamics—maintaining full node participation for 1450 rounds (20% improvement), sustaining energy for 1900 rounds (24% improvement), and reducing control message overhead by 32%. The algorithm outperformed both EERAMSS and Stable Election Protocol (SEP) in terms of stability, energy efficiency, and control message overhead. These results demonstrate the method's potential to extend WSN lifetime. Future work will explore its adaptability to heterogeneous and dynamic environments to ensure broader real-world applicability.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)

title: BCOP-PINN: BCOPs Based Physics Informed Neural Network for Solution of Singularly Perturbed Problems
authors: Brahmvidya, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Boundary Characteristics Orthogonal Polynomials are used for prediction of solution to perturbation problems in various studies. In this paper we propose a hybrid PINN that is BCOPs based PINN to improve the accuracy of prediction and the root mean square error is compared with that of classical PINN. The paper includes discussion of Finite Difference Method with Shishkin mesh using upwind discretization to solve convection diffusion problem.

title: CKD-Net: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: Srihitha Chinthaginjala, M. Mallikarjuna Rao, D. Venkatesh, M. Bhagya Lakshmi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a severe global health issue, which has crippled millions of people across the world where the timely detection is the most important element in preventing a transition to the End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD). Although the conventional machine learning methods have been promising, they are not usually able to deal with the high-dimensional non-linear characteristics of clinical information. This study provides an in-depth study of the state-of-the-art Deep Learning (DL) methods to predict CKD, in particular, it analyses Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and Deep Belief Networks (DBN). We also suggest a new hybrid CNN-LSTM network that combines both time dependency modeling and space feature extraction. We use a dataset of 400 patient records consisting of 24 attributes, apply a strict preprocessing procedure consisting of SMOTE to balance classes and MICE to handle missing data. Experimental data shows that the hybrid model proposed is able to perform best with 99.2 % accuracy, which is better than standalone architectures. The present study confirms the effectiveness of deep learning in nephrology and adds to the increasing list of studies that prove AI-based personalized medicine.

title: A Secure Blockchain-Based Architecture for Digital Certificate Generation and Validation
authors: Abhishek Tripathi, Vikas Gupta, Gullapudi Lalith Kumar, Pinnamaneni Praneeth, Chittipolu Kiran Kumar, Kancheti Chaitanya
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Digital certificates are very important for proving identity at work, school, and in government. But existing systems are still vulnerable to forgery, duplication, and tampering. This paper presents a blockchain-based system for issuing and verifying digital certificates, leveraging QR codes, cryptographic hashes, and Ethereum smart contracts to ensure secure and tamper-proof validation. The system achieves 85 TPS with 10 nodes, low latency of 1.2-2.0 seconds, and a 92% validation success rate. Compared with traditional approaches, this method is safer, more reliable, and user-friendly. It aligns with SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure) and SDG 16 (Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions) by promoting trust, transparency, and sustainable digital growth.

title: Explainable Multi-Modal Hybrid Deep and Machine Learning Framework for Alzheimer's Detection Using MRI and Clinical Data
authors: Apoorva Bhardwaj, Amita Sharma, Manisha Rajoriya
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disorder that progresses over time, and its early identification continues to pose a significant challenge in clinical settings. Conventional diagnostic approaches depending on neuroimaging or clinical assessment frequently do not fully reflect the disease's complex, multifaceted characteristics. This research showcases an interpretable multi-modal hybrid learning framework that combines deep learning models trained on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) data with machine learning models applied to numerical clinical features. Convolutional and transformer networks capture distinguishing imaging characteristics, whereas gradient-boosting methods evaluate organized clinical factors. The integrated hybrid system shows enhanced classification capabilities at various disease stages, producing interpretable and balanced results that surpass single-modality approaches. Techniques for explainability like Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM++) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) clarify model reasoning and highlight important biomarkers, creating a clear and trustworthy computational method for precision-focused Alzheimer's identification and clinical decision support.

title: Halide-Induced Colorimetric Sensing of $\text{Pb}^{2+}$ in Water via NaI-Functionalized AgNPs on Paper
authors: Marco Laurence M. Budlayan, Jeanne Phyre Lagare-Oracion, Jonathan N. Patricio, Lyka B. De La Rosa, Rey Y. Capangpangan, Susan D. Arco
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This study presents the development of a paper sensor for the colorimetric detection of lead ions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{P b}^{\mathrm{2}+}\right)$</tex> in aqueous systems using silver nanoparticle with immobilized sodium iodide (NaI-AgNPs). Silver nanoparticles were synthesized via chemical reduction and successfully deposited on cellulose filter paper, forming a yellow-tinted sensing substrate. Functionalization with sodium iodide enabled specific halide-metal coordination, allowing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{P b}^{{2 +}}$</tex> ions to induce a distinct color change from pale to intense yellow due to the formation of lead(II) iodide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{PbI}_{2})$</tex> on the surface. The sensor demonstrated high selectivity for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{P b}^{{2 +}}$</tex> over other common metal ions, with a dynamic detection range of 0.1−100 ppm and a visual detection limit of approximately 1 ppm. UV-Vis spectroscopy confirmed a characteristic absorbance peak at 494 nm associated with lead interaction. This halide-assisted, AgNP-amplified mechanism offers a low-cost, rapid, and instrument-free platform for on-site lead detection, providing a promising solution for environmental monitoring in resource-limited settings.

title: Development of Internet of Things (IoT) Based Fleet Monitoring System
authors: Huzein Fahmi Hawari, Loy Ken-Ji, Francis Emmanuel Chinda
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Fleet management systems are essential for improving operational efficiency in the logistics and transportation sectors. Conventional solutions often combine positioning, communication, and diagnostic tools, but many still depend on manual reporting and provide limited real time analytics. This work introduces an Internet of Things (IoT) based fleet management platform that automates the collection, transmission, and visualization of key vehicle parameters, including distance traveled, location, speed, and fuel consumption. The system integrates an On Board Diagnostics II (OBD II) scanner with a microcontroller and an IoT platform, enabling continuous monitoring without human intervention. The mobile app enables real-time tracking of vehicles and deliveries, helping courier companies reduce delays with better route planning and visibility. Field tests were conducted with different drivers and vehicle types to validate data accuracy and assess driving behavior. Performance was measured using Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) for speed and average fuel economy. Results show that smoother and more consistent driving leads to better fuel efficiency, with the best driver achieving 38.55 km per liter and the lowest RMSE of 2.59. Compared to existing fleet monitoring systems, this solution offers continuous automated data collection, integrated driver behavior scoring, and the ability to scale for large fleets through lightweight data transmission and cloud processing. The study demonstrates the feasibility of a cost effective and scalable IoT fleet management solution that can improve predictive maintenance, optimize delivery schedules, and support real time operational decisions for both small and large transportation enterprises.

title: Integration Strategies for Electrochemical Sensors in Digital Microfluidic Devices: Recent Advances and Applications
authors: Beh Khi Khim, Md Farhan Aizuddin Md Azlin, Beh Khi Poay, Tan Yar Loon, Aziah Ismail, Asrulnizam Abd Manaf
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Digital microfluidic (DMF) devices integrated with electrochemical sensors represent transformative technology for point-of-care diagnostics and environmental monitoring. This paper examines cutting-edge integration strategies developed between 2020-2025, emphasizing AIenhanced automation and advanced nanomaterials. State-of-the-art systems demonstrate detection limits reaching 74 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p g} \cdot \mathbf{m L}^{-\mathbf{1}}$</tex> for protein biomarkers in cancer diagnostics with volume precision achieving 0.17% accuracy. Key developments include smartphone-based platforms, MXene-based sensors, and plug-and-play architectures addressing traditional challenges in electrode fouling and manufacturing scalability.

title: Corporate Sustainability Monitoring Using Hybrid Neuro-Fuzzy Systems with Unsupervised Clustering for Decision Support
authors: Keerthi H K, Lis Melissa Yapanto, Guru Basava Aradhya, Nikitha Sreekanthaswamy, Sajiv G, P. Nagaraj
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Monitoring corporate sustainability is increasingly important for stakeholders wishing to prioritize corporate ethical and environmental responsibilities. This study presents a hybrid approach to a Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System named ANFIS that incorporates unsupervised clustering to increase transparency and interpretability for decision makers when evaluating sustainability. The three parts to the methodology were fuzzifying the ESG indicators, to establish an adaptive neuro-fuzzy model allowing for learning complicated relationships between variables, and utilizing clustering methods to organize companies based on their sustainability performance. Ultimately, synthesizing the ESG scores and behaviors of the corporations through Giandini, the ANFIS is designed to produce a transparent decision-support framework (or support system) for effective data-driven decision-making. The designed rule-based decision support allows for enhanced strategic insights for the decision-maker by aligning sustainability patterns with specific governance actions. The suggested approach recognizes the limitations of current methods as it offers a more complete evaluation of corporate governance as it relates to ESG indicators.

title: Organizing Committee Chairs
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)

title: Emerging AI Approaches for Malicious URL Detection: A Comprehensive Survey of ML, DL, and Embedding-Based Methods
authors: Vartica Kushwaha, Abhishek Kumar, Radhika Soni, Jagmeet Kaur, Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Cyberattacks are becoming more common on the rapidly growing internet. Attackers often use fake or malicious website URLs to trick people to share their confidential information or money. Since attackers now use advanced tricks - such as hiding the real link, redirecting users to another page, or faking a trusted website (domain spoofing), traditional methods like relying on a basic blacklist are no longer enough to stop them. This review paper looks at how we've improved the technology for finding these dangerous URLs. We started with basic machine-learning programs and have moved on to advanced systems using deep learning, combinations of different models (hybrid), methods based on breaking down the URL into code (embedding-based), and models like the ones used in modern language AI (transformer-driven). We looked closely at eleven important studies and compared what kind of information they used to spot a fake URL (like the words in the link, the content of the page, or the server information), the types of models they used, the data they tested with, and how accurate they were. The results show that the most accurate methods are those that use deep learning, hybrid models (like combining CNN and LSTM), and embedding-based methods, especially those that use large language models. These methods are much better at finding complex attack patterns than older techniques. This review points out our current successes, the main obstacles we still face, and where we need to go next to build malicious URL detection systems that are faster, more accurate, and more trustworthy.

title: Bio-Inspired Calibration of the Self-Correcting Battery Model as the Core of Digital Twin Framework
authors: Walid Merrouche, Boughiout Islam Abd Elsammed, Hegazy Rezk, Badis Lekouaghet, Mahmoud S. Abouomar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Accurate modeling of batteries is paramount in formulating the reliable digital twin (DT) in the areas of energy storage and smart mobility. The Enhanced Self-Correcting (ESC) model, an interesting improvement of standard equivalent circuit models (ECMs), is the main component of the physical twin as it encapsulates dynamic voltage behavior along with the hysteresis phenomena. This manuscript suggests the use of two new state-of-the-art metaheuristic algorithms in the calibration scheme of ESC model: the African Vultures Optimization Algorithm (AVOA) and the Walrus Optimization Algorithm (WaOA). Parameter identification is performed under a wide range of temperatures of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 2 5 d e g C}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 4 5 d e g C}$</tex> to achieve robustness under different operating conditions. Comparative analysis demonstrates that the bio-inspired methods increase the accuracy and convergence and reduce the sensitivity to the thermal fluctuations. Although this investigation would be restricted to offline calibration, the validated ESC (electromagnetic steering control) model provides a foundation for DT deployment and in turn allows for continuous and reliable estimation of state-of-charge (SoC) and state-of-health (SoH).

title: Automatic Annotation of Python Programming Exercises Using Hierarchical Multi-Label Classification
authors: Imene Labiod, Nabiha Azizi, Yasmine Chachoui, Tahar Bensebaa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical multi-label classification approach for automatically annotating Python programming exercises with pedagogical concepts. The model combines a transformer-based encoder (BERT) to capture semantic information from exercise descriptions with a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) that encodes prerequisite relations between concepts in a pedagogical graph. The resulting representations are fused to predict the set of relevant concepts required to solve each exercise. Experimental results show that the hierarchical model (BERT + GCN) achieves a macro F1-score of 0.94 and improves subset accuracy from 0.52 (BERT) to 0.56, demonstrating its effectiveness in generating coherent and pedagogically meaningful annotations for Python programming exercises.

title: Design and Implementation of a Low-Cost Automatic Weather Station Based on the Internet of Things
authors: Sefia Attia, Abdelkader Mechernene, Mourad Loucif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This project involves creating a system for collecting and displaying measurements from an automated and cost-effective weather station designed for renewable energy installations. It is part of a monitoring strategy based on the Internet of Things (IoT). The prototype is energyautonomous and powered by small solar panels and lithiumpolymer batteries, via an MCP1700-LDO (low-dropout voltage) regulator. Environmental data, such as ambient temperature, solar radiation intensity, wind speed and direction, UV index, etc., are collected without human intervention using various sensors. The station is located on the roof of the research laboratory building of the Faculty of Technology at the University of Tlemcen. The Stevenson shield was entirely designed and manufactured using 3D printing to protect the station. The data is collected and transmitted by an ESP32 board equipped with a Tensilica Xtensa LX6 processor and a Wi-Fi module. This board offers excellent value for money for IoT projects. It is programmable with several programming languages, such as C/C++ or Micro-Python, thanks to its various libraries. The ThingSpeak platform was chosen to store the collected information online (cloud), display it on smartphones, tablets and computers, and predict the temperature using the LSTM deep learning model to evaluate the station's performance and reliability.

title: Wearable RFID-Integrated Vibration Tag for Silent, Long-Term Sleep Apnea Monitoring and Posture Sensing
authors: Saddaoui Rafik, Aoughlis Samir, Oudiai Karim, Nachef Mhenna, Hamiche Hamid, Laghrouche Mourad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a functional and portable tag sensor aimed at offline detection of apnea incidents with real-time alerts for the patient. The system continually monitors chest and upper abdomen movements via an embedded acceleration sensor. Upon identifying an apnea episode, the event is logged into both the internal EEPROM of the EM4523 transceiver and an SD card, capturing the date, time, and the patient's posture at the time of occurrence. When the patient aligns with the UHF Reader, the tag transmits the stored apnea data, respiration rate, and current body position. The reader, utilizing the modern AS3993 chip, is interfaced with a Raspberry Pi acting as a local server connected to the Cloud, facilitating internet-based updates for the attending physician. This infrastructure enables data collection from several RFID tags across various locations, allowing comprehensive analysis of apnea events. Once clinicians determine a patient likely suffers from sleep apnea, they contact them by email to retrieve extended data from the SD card, which includes long-term breathing records spanning several months.

title: Integrating SysML and UML in the Design and Lifecycle Management of Machine Learning-Enabled Digital Twins: A Review and Case Study on Smart Environmental Monitoring Systems
authors: Alba Merdani, Enida Sheme, Elinda Meçe, Eduart Torba
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: The increasing complexity of smart environmental monitoring systems, including air, water, and noise pollution, demands robust methodologies for their design, deployment, and lifecycle management. Digital Twins (DTs), as real-time virtual representations of physical systems, offer a transformative approach by enabling simulation, prediction, and adaptive control. This case study explores the integration of Systems Modeling Languages (SysML) and Unified Modeling Language (UML) with Machine Learning (ML) within the context of Digital Twin architectures for environmental applications. By analyzing state-of-the-art research and engineering frameworks, we identify how SysML and UML facilitate the structural and behavioral modeling of environmental DTs, and how ML components can be embedded into system models to enable predictive and adaptive capabilities. The paper provides a review and a comparative analysis of modeling techniques, tool chains and design patterns of other research papers. It also emphasizes their role in environmental systems monitoring air quality (temperature, PM2.5, PM10, UV), water quality (pH, turbidity, conductivity), and noise levels. Finally, the study highlights existing challenges and proposes research directions for modelbased design, semantic interoperability, and ML integration in future smart city Digital Twins.

title: Monitoring Olive Tree Water Status in Semi-Arid Regions Using Dendrometry and Sap Flow Measurements
authors: Abdelhafid Elallaoui, Saïd Khabba, Salah Er-Raki, Adnane Chakir, Valérie Le Dantec, Pierre-Louis Frison, Lionel Jarlan
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: In semi-arid regions, irrigation accounts for more than 85% of total water use, often resulting in inefficient water management and overexploitation of groundwater resources. Therefore, the rational use of irrigation water requires accurate monitoring of plant water status to optimize water application and maintain crop productivity. Among the available approaches, dendrometric and sap flow measurements provide powerful, complementary tools for assessing the dynamic water balance of trees. Dendrometric measurements, based on high-resolution monitoring of trunk radius variations, offer valuable insights into short-term fluctuations in plant water status and long-term growth dynamics. These variations reflect reversible daily cycles of trunk shrinkage and swelling associated with water storage and depletion in living tissues. In this study, we investigated the potential of the nighttime trunk radius increment slope derived from dendrometric data as an indicator of olive tree water status under semi-arid conditions. To this end, dendrometers recording trunk radius variations every 5 minutes and seven heat dissipation probes (TDP) measuring sap flow every 30 minutes were installed on olive trees located in the Chichaoua region of the Haouz plain, Morocco. In parallel, meteorological parameters and soil moisture were continuously monitored to provide complementary environmental data. The results showed that the nighttime stem radius increment slope was significantly correlated with both vapor pressure deficit (VPD) and sap flow, demonstrating its sensitivity to variations in plant water status. The results suggest that the nighttime trunk radius increment slope is a promising physiological indicator of tree water status under semi-arid conditions.

title: AI-Powered Demand Forecasting to Reduce Overproduction and Food Waste in the Agri-Food Supply Chain
authors: Saiful Izwan Suliman, Roslina Mohamad, Shahrani Shahbudin, Murizah Kassim
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: Overproduction and food waste undermine food security and sustainable resource use, especially for perishables with event- and weather-sensitive demand. We develop an explainable AI forecasting framework that fuses historical sales with exogenous drivers-weather, calendar events, socioeconomic indicators, promotions, and sentiment-and operationalizes three complementary model families: LSTM (sequence learning), XGBoost (nonlinear tabular), and Prophet (decomposed trend/seasonality). Using a realistically calibrated synthetic dataset reflecting Southeast Asian operating conditions (2019-2023), we evaluate models under regular and peak-demand windows (e.g., Ramadan) with RMSE, MAPE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>, and bias, and test paired daily errors using the Wilcoxon signedrank test. LSTM yields the best accuracy, significantly outperforming Prophet and XGBoost. SHAP analyses highlight temperature, rainfall, holiday proximity, and promotions as dominant drivers. The framework's accuracy and transparency enable tighter inventory policies to reduce spoilage and cost. We discuss limitations of synthetic data and outline pathways for real-time deployment with IoT freshness sensors and multimodal data fusion

title: An Embedded AI Model for Defect Detection in Photovoltaic Modules using a Jetson Nano-Powered Unmanned Aerial Vehicle
authors: Nouamane Kellil, Adel Mellit, Toufik Benkherouf, Maamar Bettayeb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this work, we propose an embedded computer vision system based on a You Only Look Once (YOLO) model, suitable for real-time defects detection in photovoltaic (PV) plants. The dataset is a set of images taken using an RGB camera, capturing various types of PV module defects. A YOLOv9-based detection pipeline was developed and optimized for this dataset to serve as a benchmark. The YOLOv9 model was then fine-tuned and integrated into a lightweight architecture suitable for deployment on resource-constrained hardware. The final implementation was carried out on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano platform, enabling real-time inference capabilities in embedded environments. Results validate the effectiveness of the designed prototype, demonstrating high detection accuracy and efficient performance, confirming its suitability for practical field deployment.

title: Hybrid WCA-HS Based Control for Autonomous Quadrotor Navigation in Precision Agriculture
authors: Nesrine Tenniche, Boubekeur Mendil, Farid Ghilas, Lamine Brikh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Autonomous Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly used in precision agriculture to support sustainable land and water management through efficient monitoring. This paper presents a control method based on the recently developed hybrid metaheuristic algorithm, Water Cycle Algorithm-Harmony Search (WCA-HS), to optimize control laws for improved trajectory tracking of a quadrotor UAV. The approach is evaluated using the Integral of Time-weighted Absolute Error (ITAE) cost function and benchmarked against five established optimization algorithms: WCA, HS, Differential Evolution (DE), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Artificial Bee Colony (ABC). Results demonstrate superior performance in tracking accuracy, convergence speed, and control stability. This work supports advanced UAV-based solutions for water-efficient, renewable-powered agriculture, aligning with the goals of integrated food, water, and energy systems.

title: Drone Autonomous Navigation for Response to Disaster Using the Techniques of Swarm Intelligence
authors: Danish Kundra, Jagtej Singh, Syed Rashid Anwar, Raghavendra R, Subhaluxmi Sahoo, Joany R. M.
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Drone autonomous navigation is a central factor in orchestrating disaster response operations to provide efficient search, surveillance and rescue operations within environments that are often inaccessible and unsafe to human agents. Swarm intelligence paradigms provide an underlying scaffold for coordination of multiplicitous unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) to tenuously in such intricate contexts action concerted. Nevertheless, methodologies currently in use often face limitations, such as poor planning of routes, communication delays, and limited adaptability of circumscribed movements in high dynamic disaster estates. These deficiencies limit the ability to refine the routing strategies and the ability to maintain the coordinated coverage among affected domains. In order to address the limitations, in this work, a Drone Swarm Navigation Framework (DSNF) that combines Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and advanced path planning strategies has been introduced. PSO is used to optimise inter drones coordination and while the path planning module ensures efficient obstacle circumvention and adaptive navigation through the unknow disaster terrains. The DSNF augments the disaster response by facilitating autonomous mission of optimal trajectories, judicious allocation of tasks and coverage of large areas whilst minimising the overlap. Consequently, response efficiency is improved, mission duration is reduced and the ability to react in real time to dynamic conditions is guaranteed. Empirical outcome has shown that the DSNF outperforms their traditional counterparts initiates by having better navigational accuracy, lower energy consumption and increased resiliency within a disaster state. Specifically, the method increases navigational accuracy by 97.2 Mis, reduces energy consumption by 158 mAh, shortens the mission completion time by 25 min, and increases coverage efficiency by 96.9 %.

title: Detecting Mental Health Conditions from Reddit Posts using Machine Learning
authors: Mujeeb Ur Rehman, Sohail Khalid, M. Adel R. Alkhulaiteet, Menahil Khawar, Farhan Ullah, Imran Baig
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Social media platforms such as Reddit host a large volume of user-generated content that can reveal indicators of mental health conditions. This study presents a machine learning framework for the multi-class detection of mental health states, including Normal, Depression, Suicidal, Anxiety, Bipolar, Stress, and Personality Disorder, from Reddit posts. A dataset of relevant subreddits was collected and labeled in these seven categories. The text was cleaned, and features were extracted using TFIDF, LDA topics, sentiment scores, and basic linguistic cues. Multiple classifiers were trained, including logistic regression, linear SVM, random forest, Light Gradient Boosting Machine, and XGBoost, with SMOTE oversampling applied to address class imbalance. A stacked ensemble model combining the base classifier outputs achieved the best performance with an accuracy of 91%, macro-F1 of 0.92, and AUC of 0.93. These results show that interpretable, relatively lightweight machine learning models can accurately classify a range of mental health states from social media texts, supporting the development of scalable tools for the early detection and intervention of mental health issues.

title: Hybrid AI Framework for Energy-Efficient Last-Mile Delivery in Urban Areas: A PRISMA-Based Systematic Review
authors: Jihad Mkhalfi, Fouad Jawab, Imane Moufad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: In this article, we conduct a systematic literature review of 28 academic papers published between 2016 and 2024 about hybrid artificial intelligence systems directed towards energy efficient last-mile urban delivery activities. We examine key AI techniques - including the use of machine learning in demand forecasting and adaptive routing processes, reinforcement learning for optimizing energy consumption for last-mile delivery using electric vehicles, multi-agent systems for optimizing highly complex urban logistics, and utilizing various metaheuristic algorithms for optimizing routing and scheduling of delivery vehicles. We identified autonomous delivery that leverages drones and robots guided through Artificial Intelligence mission planning. Despite this research on hybrid artificial intelligence for energy efficient last-mile delivery activities, we found little work focused on a human-centered approach without the utilization of Artificial Intelligence. Thus, we see it as an opportunity for future investigations to improve energy efficient urban delivery simply through the improvement of non-AI based methods. We examined a contribution for hybrid AI-assisted methods to provide evidence towards an existing gap in human-centered inquiry concerning energy-efficient urban delivery activities.

title: Enhancing Wind Energy Forecast Accuracy Through Weibull Parameter Optimization: A Real Case from Tangier, Morocco
authors: Mohamed Bousla, Ali Haddi, Youness El Mourabit
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: This study aims to enhance the accuracy of wind energy forecasting by comparing four Weibull parameter estimation methods Method of Moments (MM), Maximum Likelihood Method (MLM), Power Density Method (PDM), and Standard Deviation Method (STDM) using real SCADA data from the Tangier wind farm (2024). The results demonstrate that all methods provide consistent and reliable estimations, with STDM achieving the highest accuracy (0.46% deviation) from actual production. These findings confirm the robustness of the Weibull model and highlight the importance of selecting the appropriate estimation technique for precise wind resource assessment.

title: Comparative Analysis of YOLOv8, YOLOv11, and Faster R-CNN for Multi-Crop Plant Disease Detection
authors: Rim Gasmi, Marwa Chander, Mohamed Amine Badache
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Plant diseases constitute a major challenge to global agriculture, causing substantial economic losses and threatening food security. Conventional detection methods are often labor-intensive, time-consuming, and unsuitable for large-scale implementation. This study presents a comparative analysis of three deep learning models—YOLOv8, YOLOv11, and Faster R-CNN—for automated detection of plant diseases in corn, wheat, and palm leaves. Unlike previous research that has primarily focused on individual crops or on classification tasks, this work addresses multi-crop disease detection through object detection techniques, evaluating their generalizability and suitability for real-time applications. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv11 achieves the most favorable trade-off between accuracy, precision, and robustness (mAP@0.5:0.95 = 0.69 for corn, 0.682 for wheat, and 0.66 for palm), making it particularly suitable for deployment in real-time smart agriculture systems. YOLOv8 shows advantages in inference speed and compatibility with edge devices, whereas Faster R-CNN provides high recall but is constrained by its computational demands. Overall, this study contributes to the advancement of scalable, AI-driven agricultural monitoring solutions.

title: Modeling of Degradation Phenomena in Photovoltaic Modules
authors: Hajji Rania, Aziz Abdelhak, Messaoudi Abdelhafid, Yahyaoui Saloua
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: In this work, we first carried out an initial investigation into the degradation of a solar cell, focusing specifically on how the band gap energy changes and on the main factors that contribute to the cell's deterioration. We also examined the impact of external conditions-such as heat, humidity, and dust-on the performance of photovoltaic panels. In addition to this analysis, a detailed study of a single photovoltaic cell was performed by modeling its electrical characteristics. The developed model was implemented and simulated in MATLAB, allowing us to observe how variations in the material properties-particularly the evolution of the band gap energy-play a role in the degradation mechanisms that affect photovoltaic cells over time.

title: Analysis of Long-Term Performance Degradation in Photovoltaic Modules
authors: Amar Hadj Arab, Bilal Taghezouit, Fatah Mehareb, Smail Semaoui, Kamel Abdeladim, Ismail Bendaas, Abdelhak Razagui, Kada Bouchouicha, Saliha Boulahchiche, Fella Tobbal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This study investigates the long-term performance degradation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> crystalline silicon photovoltaic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{PV})$</tex> modules from the first grid-connected PV system installed at the Centre de Développement des Energies Renouvelables (CDER) in Algiers, Algeria, located along the Mediterranean coast. In continuous operation since 2004, the system provides a valuable case for assessing real-world aging effects. Despite sharing identical nominal specifications, manufacturing origin, and installation conditions, the modules exhibited varying degradation rates. After 20 years of outdoor exposure (2004–2024), seven of the fifteen modules showed power losses exceeding the standard warranty limit of 20% nominal power degradation.

title: Sustainable Power Generation via Olive Solid Waste Gasification in the Tizi Ouzou Region
authors: Abdenour Elias, Abderrezak Kennas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Each year, mills in the Kabylia region generate thousands of tons of olive-wastes, which is typically discarded or burnt in fields. This study addresses the valorisation of olive solid waste through gasification. A theoretical study and simulation was conducted on a small-scale plant designed for heat and power generation. The plant consists of a downdraft gasifier, an externally-fired gas turbine, and an organic Rankine cycle. The syngas produced in the gasification process showed the following molar composition: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2} 16.86 \%$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO} 20.66 \%$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4} 1.62 \%, \text{CO}_{2} 9.4 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}_{2} 50.7 \%$</tex>. The syngas had an LHV of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.71 \text{MJ} / \text{kg}$</tex> and a gasification efficiency of 72.15%. When supplied with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$112 ~\text{kg} / \mathrm{h}$</tex> of olive pomace, the EFGT generated an electrical output of 75.8 kWe. The flue gases exiting the turbine powered the organic Rankine cycle, facilitating the generation of both electrical and thermal energy. This study evaluates the performance of six working fluids: cyclohexane, isopentane, R113, benzene, R245fa, and methanol. Among these fluids, Isopentane demonstrated the highest electrical power output, producing 35.4 kWe and achieving a combined total output of 111.2 kWe. This resulted in an improvement in electrical efficiency from 18.3% to 24.07%. Based on the thermodynamic performances, a thermo-economic evaluation was conducted, incorporating the sale of both electricity and thermal energy in the form of hot water. To evaluate the economic viability of the system, four key indicators were calculated: net present value, profitability index, internal rate of return, and payback period. The cost-free availability of biomass significantly contributed to favourable economic outcomes

title: Anomaly Detection in Datacenter Energy Consumption Using an Autoencoder
authors: Karima Oukfif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Optimizing energy consumption is one of the most challenging topics in datacenter infrastructures. Identifying unexpected high resource utilization led to detecting energy consumption anomalies. The purpose of this work is to detect deviant energy consumption behaviors characterized by unusual usage of resources. We propose an artificial intelligence model for unsupervised anomaly detection. The model is constructed as a Robust Autoencoder with an optimized architecture of layers. We performed the assessments using the most recent real production dataset (Alibaba Cluster Trace). The experiment's results demonstrated the efficiency of the model that identifies anomalies compared to a standard model.

title: Gigarad Ionizing Radiation-Induced Carrier Mobility Degradation in 28 Nm Commercial Cmos
authors: Farzan Jazaeri, Ashkhen Yesayan, Amin Rassekh, Jean-Michel Sallese
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Electron Devices Conference (LAEDC)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental study on the impact of total ionizing dose (TID) damage on free carrier mobility in a commercial 28 nm bulk CMOS technology, with exposure levels reaching up to 1 Grad. The analysis is based on a proposed, accurate methodology for extracting free carrier mobility, which assumes drift-diffusion transport in advanced field-effect transistors [1], [2]. Using this approach, we investigate the degradation of carrier mobility caused by Coulomb scattering from radiation-induced interface trapped charges. The study provides new insights into the physical mechanisms behind mobility reduction in deeply scaled nodes under high-dose radiation. The extracted mobility trends offer valuable benchmarks for reliability assessment and radiation-hardened design in nanoscale CMOS technologies.

title: A Universal AI-Based Extractive Text Summarizer with Multilingual Translation and Speech Synthesis
authors: V. Usha, Sandeep Karagatla, Mahesh Thummala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In the current digital world, users are overwhelmed by huge volumes of textual information, and it is difficult to find the necessary information and determine it effectively. The paper describes a Universal Text Summarizer, a smart program, which creates concise summaries of text documents automatically via the use of Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods. The proposed model AIMuTS is extractive in nature and summarizes sentences by identifying and assigning scores on these sentences depending on frequency of words and linguistic meaning in order to produce a reduced version of the original text. The system is compatible with various file formats such as TXT, PDF, DOCX and CSV and hence allows the extraction of data in different sources. Other features like multilingual translation with the use of Google Translate API and text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis make the platform very accessible and easy to use. The web interface is built using Streamlit and provides an easy usability experience with users having the capability to summarize, translate, listen to, and download their summaries in PDFs. The effectiveness of the system has been proven through experimental results showing that the system is effective in reducing large documents into brief summaries without much loss in meaning which is useful to students, researchers and professionals. The suggested model is viable and scalable in automated content summarization and multilingual accessibility.

title: AI-Empowered Resource Allocation for Wirelessly Powered Pinching-Antenna Systems
authors: Saeid Pakravan, Mohsen Ahmadzadeh, Ming Zeng, Xingwang Li, Fang Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper considers a multi-user system, where the users first harvest energy from the base station and then use the harvested energy to transmit information via non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA). A pinching-antenna array is adopted to assist the energy transfer and information transmission, owing to its ability to adapt to dynamic propagation conditions. To enhance the system's energy efficiency (EE), we formulate a joint optimization problem involving antenna positioning, transmit power control, and time-switching ratio selection. The problem is non-convex due to the coupled variables, nonlinear energy-harvesting characteristics, and uncertainties in user locations and battery states. To effectively solve this problem, a deep reinforcement learning based algorithm is proposed to autonomously learn near-optimal resource allocation policies in dynamic environments. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed PA-assisted scheme achieves significant gains in EE compared with conventional fixed-antenna schemes.

title: Quantifying and Improving the Accuracy of Electromagnetic Transient-Transient Stability Hybrid Simulation
authors: Bin Wang, Qiang Zhang, Xiaochuan Luo, Slava Maslennikov, Mingguo Hong, Xinghao Fang, Tongxin Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources introduces new dynamic challenges to modern power grids, such as sub- and super-synchronous oscillations and other faster dynamics. These dynamics are typically fast in nature and are difficult to accurately model and analyze using standard transient stability (TS) methods, necessitating the need for electromagnetic transient (EMT) analysis. However, EMT simulations are notoriously slow for large-scale grids due to both equation formulations and computational limitations. To overcome this challenge, EMT-TS hybrid simulation is often used, since it offers a balanced trade-off between accuracy and speed, making it feasible to perform EMT analysis on large systems. One open question about EMT-TS hybrid simulation is the accuracy of the EMT-TS boundary or interface. This paper introduces an error index to quantify EMT-TS hybrid interface errors, identifies conditions where the hybrid simulation approach may become inaccurate, and suggests EMT region expansions to improve the simulation accuracy. Additionally, a three-sequence hybrid interface model is proposed to mitigate inaccuracies caused by unbalanced conditions.

title: Using K-Means Clustering for Unsupervised Profiling of Heart Disease Patients
authors: Khaoula Benabderrahim, Mounira Tarhouni, Salah Zidi, Najoua Benneji
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Heart disease remains one of the leading causes of mortality worldwide. Understanding the underlying clinical profiles of patients is essential to improving diagnosis and enabling personalized treatment. In this study, we adopt an unsupervised machine learning approach based on the K-Means clustering algorithm to identify and analyze latent subgroups within the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset. Our main contribution is the development of an unsupervised profiling method that automatically identifies homogeneous subgroups of heart disease patients according to their clinical characteristics. After normalizing the data and removing outliers using the distance from cluster centroids, each patient is assigned to one of three clusters. We then characterize each cluster by its average clinical profile and the proportion of patients diagnosed with heart disease. The resulting clusters reveal distinct patterns associated with varying levels of cardiovascular risk. This exploratory analysis highlights patient heterogeneity and provides a valuable foundation for targeted medical interventions and the development of future explainable models. The entire analysis was conducted in Python using the Google Colab platform, ensuring reproducibility and computational efficiency.

title: A Survey of Home Healthcare Optimization Problem
authors: Emna Ben Amri, Bilel Marzouki, Olfa Belkahla Driss
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: In recent years, the demand for Home HealthCare (HHC) services has significantly increased, driven by an aging population and the growing prevalence of chronic diseases. Home care systems support patients who require ongoing and regular medical attention outside of hospital settings. These services encompass a broad spectrum of care, including medical treatments, rehabilitation, and psychosocial support, typically delivered by healthcare professionals, social workers, and sometimes volunteers. Due to the complex constraints and multiple objectives involved, the Home HealthCare Optimization Problem (HHCOP) is classified as NP-hard. This classification implies that while exact solution methods can be effective for small instances, they become computationally infeasible for realistically sized problems. As a result, heuristic and metaheuristic approaches are often more suitable for practical applications, as they provide satisfactory solutions within reasonable computational times, even if they do not guarantee optimality. This paper presents a state-of-the-art review of the Home HealthCare Optimization Problem, highlighting its significance, the various existing variants, and the range of methods employed to address it.

title: SOC Estimation Method for Aqueous Sodium-Ion Batteries Based on Electro-Thermal Coupling Model
authors: Zhong Dai, Dongpo Ban
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the deviation of State of Charge (SOC) estimation due to the influence of temperature change on internal resistance and other parameters of aqueous sodium-ion battery during operation, a method for SOC estimation of aqueous sodium-ion battery based on electro-thermal coupling model was proposed in this paper. Firstly, the electrical and thermal characteristics and coupling relationship of the aqueous sodiumion battery were analyzed, and the electro-thermal coupling model of the aqueous sodium-ion battery was established by combining the equivalent circuit model and the two-state lumped parameter thermal model. Secondly, the parameters of the equivalent circuit model and the thermal model are identified by the forgetting factor recursive least squares algorithm. On this basis, combined with the temperature estimation module, the SOC estimation algorithm of the sodium-ion battery based on adaptive unscented Kalman filter is proposed. Finally, an experimental platform for water-based sodium-ion batteries was built to verify the algorithm at different temperatures. A large number of experimental results verified the effectiveness and applicability of the algorithm in SOC estimation of aqueous sodium-ion batteries.

title: Resilient Sensorless Current Regulation Technique for SVPWM-Based Inverters Operating Under Voltage Perturbations in DC Microgrid Wind Systems
authors: Pravat Kumar Rout, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Sarthak Mohanty, Manoj Kumar Debnath
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This work presents current shaping sensorless simplified approaches-sensorless current shaping, currently designed for application on space vector PWM (SVPWM) inverters of DC-linked wind energy systems. Owing to the absence of voltage sensors, the design can reduce system complexity, hardware costs, and sensitivity to noise. The control structure consists of two coordinated loops-a power control method (PCM), which commands the power exchange within the DC grid, and non-linearity control method (NCM), which cleans current distortions and optimizes the inverter performance under variable loading conditions. An innovative method for current decomposition and resistance imitation has been developed, for the enhancement of harmonic rejection, improvement of the current regulation, and stability of the whole system. The system model is developed and verified through simulations. Indeed, the results demonstrated that the introduction of this algorithm drastically improves power quality, transient response, and voltage resilience compared to conventional sensor-based SVPWM control methods, thus being indicated for reliable DC-grid based wind system.

title: A Setting Optimization Scheme for Hierarchical Protection of Distribution Networks with Inverterinterfaced Distributed Generators
authors: Baiyuan Liu, Benzhuang Fan, Qiyue Yang, Xinya Lu, Junyi Liu, Yisong Qi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Addressing the reduced adaptability of traditional current protection in distribution networks due to high penetration of inverter-interfaced distributed generation (IIDG), this paper proposes a hierarchical protection setting optimization method. By analyzing IIDG fault characteristics under low-voltage ride-through control, an optimization model is constructed with objectives of minimizing total operating time, maximizing protection sensitivity, and minimizing load loss, while considering the coordination constraints of upper and lower-level protection. To solve this model, a multi-objective particle swarm optimization algorithm is enhanced by introducing a chaotic mapping to increase population diversity. An adaptive splitting strategy is employed to improve solution distribution, and a final setting scheme is selected from the Pareto optimal solutions via cluster analysis. Simulation results based on the IEEE 33-node system demonstrate that the proposed method effectively balances protection speed, sensitivity, and selectivity, outperforming standard MOPSO. This provides a viable solution for protection setting in distribution networks incorporating IIDGs.

title: Day-Night Operation of a PV-Fed Simplified Multilevel Inverter for Harmonic Mitigation and Reactive Power Compensation
authors: Nikhil Agrawal, Tirupathiraju Kanumuri, Anshul Agarwal, Devasuth Govind, Nitish Kumar, Anurag Das
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents a simplified reduced count photo-voltaic (PV) fed multilevel inverter (SRCMLI) based shunt active power filter to alleviate power quality issues, such as current harmonics, poor power factor and provide support to reactive power imbalance in the system. The SRCMLI have a superior performance compared to conventional MLI-based SAPE, in terms of low harmonic distortion in output voltage and low-cost index. Furthermore, an advanced PWM technique, i.e. Trianguzoidal PWM, is utilized to generate switching pulses for SRCMLI, and exhibits better performance than the conventional PWM technique regarding % THD in grid current, reactive power support, and switching losses. An Adaptive notch filter extracts the fundamental component from the load current. The proposed system operates in two scenarios: in one scenario, when sunlight is available, i.e., daytime and in the second scenario, when sunlight is not available, i.e. nighttime. During the daytime, the SRCMLI provides active power and compensation; at night, it acts solely as a compensator. The system's performance is analyzed through MATLAB/ Simulink 2023a, which provides satisfactory performance in both scenarios.

title: EDTRCAFV: Exploring Disease Patterns and Troponin Risk Levels through Clustering Analysis and Feature Visualization
authors: Kamepalli S L Prasanna, D Anupama, Jajam Nagaraju, N Vimala, Suluru Lokesh, Gunnam Chandramouli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In this study, we challenge disease pairs and risk strata of troponin using the state-of-the-art. Apply k-means clustering method in Machine learning (ML) to multivariate dataset that depicts Lab features and disease classes. A Monte Carlo simulation was performed to select the best number of clusters from convergence for maximizing silhouette coefficient. This stratification discloses new states and finer grained descriptions of trajectories. Troponin were also stratified by risk to evaluate cardiovascular risk more comprehensively. Some have been decoded by feature visualization, and differentiable features amount to pretty much disease classification. Their results were exemplary: they all had a zero-standard deviation with equal mean hamming distance strings, and an ANN model with a 95% accuracy rate on the testing dataset. In particular, this study exemplifies an approach by which novel analytic methods for high-dimensional representations of complex disease patterns may be leveraged to guide clinical decisionmaking.

title: Numerical Simulation of Long-Term Heat Extraction Performance of Deep Borehole Heat Exchanger Array with Variable Depth Arrangement
authors: Shaofeng Wang, Tongchang Jiang, Anyao Pan, Jiawen Guo, Hangyu Li, Shengchao Ma, Fenghao Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: In this paper, a variable depth layout scheme is proposed for the heat extraction performance of deep borehole heat exchanger array in long-term operation. A three-dimensional unsteady heat transfer model is established by the OpenGeoSys platform, and the heat exchange process of five boreholes under different depth combinations is numerically simulated. The simulation period is 10 years, and four variable depth arrangements (groups B, C, D, and E) and an equal depth control group (group A) are set up to analyze the changes of inlet and outlet water temperature and subsurface temperature field. The results show that the variable depth layout can effectively improve the temperature field distribution and reduce the thermal interference between borehole array. After 10 years of operation, the maximum temperature difference between the subsurface temperature field reaches 1.35 °C, and the outlet water temperature increases by 8.25 °C at the highest, showing better thermal recovery ability and long-term stability. Symmetrical variable depth arrangement (such as group B or E) has good applicability in severe cold areas. The research provides a new idea for the vertical optimization design of underground borehole array, and has reference value for promoting the efficient and stable development of geothermal heating systems.

title: Reconfigurable Streaming Architecture for AI-Based Fault Prediction of Power Equipment: Optimization and Result
authors: Tian Lan, Yuanliang Lan, Peng Zhang, Xiaoyu Fan, Guoliang Zhang, Jia Wu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithm has been increasingly utilized in fault prediction for high voltage power equipment, such as transformers. Such power equipment presents highly complex structures, operational conditions, and mechanisms. Vast multimodal datasets, including electrical, mechanical, thermal, and environmental signals, are demanded as inputs for the AI based fault prediction algorithm, leading to high computational cost. In this paper, the computing pattern of a given AI-based fault predication algorithm, as an example, is analyzed and then optimized based on reconfigurable streaming architecture. The operation fusion is implemented in this paper. The longest executable path within a subgraph is identified. In this manner, more compute modules can be chained together, allowing data to stream directly through multiple operations without interruption. By completing more computation before data has to be written back to external memory, the architecture minimizes redundant memory traffic and avoids unnecessary memory access. With compute path fusion optimization in vector core, the overall time consumption for computing the AI based fault predication algorithm can be reduced by 30 %. The results show the effectiveness of the reconfigurable streaming architecture for AIbased fault prediction of power equipment.

title: Ai Intergarted Smart Battery Management Systems for Sodium Ion Batteries Using Arduino and Machine Learning
authors: K. Priyaswi, K. Rama Sudha
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The economic viability and environmental Sustainability of Sodium -ion batteries (SIBs) have attracted increasing attention as potential energy storage solutions for electric vehicles and grid - scale applications. However, the reliable operation and safety of SIBs strongly depend on accurate state estimation, Particularly the state of charge (SOC),under dynamic operating conditions. This paper presents an Artificial intelligence enabled Smart battery management system(SBMS) framework designed for Sodium ion batteries, combining embedded system architecture with machine learning based predictive modelling. The Arduino UNO platform is considered as target embedded controller for sensor interfacing, control logic and system integration. Machine learning techniques, including Logistic regression (LR),Genetic programming (GP), and Kolmogorov -Arnold Networks (KAN) are employed to estimate SOC and analyse battery operating behaviour using voltage, current and temperature features. The proposed framework and comparative performance of the implemented learning models. The obtained findings indicate that AI -based estimation methods significantly enhance SOC prediction performance, supporting intelligent decision -making in Battery management Systems. The Presented SBMS architecture offers a scalable and cost-efficient platform for Sodium -ion battery management.

title: Automated Bill-Of-Materials and Cost Forecasting Using AI and Real-Time Market Data
authors: Abhishek Tripathi, Gauri Morankar, Chirukuri Manohar, Suryavamshi Dasharath, Manchala Abhishek, Robba Hemanth
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Accurate prediction of construction costs is major challenge due to dynamic material prices, region wise disparities, and project-specific complexities. This work presents an AIdriven cost estimation framework that integrates cost database archives, current price feeds, and advanced machine learning (ML) models to deliver precise and reliable predictions. Natural language processing permits to give information in different ways. They can make bills for materials and run simulations with options for sustainability on their own customization. The method helps with cost transparency, reduces overruns, and makes planning easier by cutting down on the amount of manual work required and making it simpler to make financial decisions. The proposed architecture supports sustainable infrastructure planning and innovation, aligning with SDG 9 and SDG 11 objectives.

title: AIOT Chip-Set for Health Communication and Rehabilitation Techniques
authors: Pawan Singh, Nishant Prakash Mishra, Rohit Singh, Mihir Prakash, Aastha Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This AIOT Chipset for Communication and Rehabilitation provides an overview of the state of patient-doctor communication and data analysis by integrating real-time intelligence directly into wearable medical devices. In order to develop a system where the device itself processes and comprehends patient condition instantly without depending on cloud servers, standard data collection is required. We discovered a serious weakness in contemporary treatment: the “visibility gap,” which occurs when physicians stop monitoring a patient's development as soon as they leave the clinic. Episodic check-ups frequently miss the minute biological changes that show whether a treatment plan is truly effective or needs to be modified because recovery varies every day. Concentrate This disconnect is dangerous for patients recovering from stroke or managing Alzheimer's disease because early indicators of regression, such as minor alterations in walking patterns or agitated behavior, are frequently overlooked until they worsen and become emergencies. The structural and analytical method for creating a custom chipset that processes biological signals on the hardware instead of sending noisy, raw data to a distant server is explained in this paper. We can quickly filter out background noise and spot important health trends, like irregular movement or elevated stress levels, by executing analysis algorithms locally on the device. Doctors will receive this result, which they can view on their dashboard to see how patients are doing on a daily basis and ultimately aid in better treatment.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis of Underground Endless Rope Traction Vehicle Track
authors: Jun Qian, Hui Luo, Chi Zhao
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: The underground endless rope traction transport system in coal mines is a crucial link in mine production. Faults such as track wear, derailment, and rope breakage directly threaten transportation safety and production efficiency. However, the widespread lack of fault samples in actual mine operation and maintenance further renders the accuracy and realtime performance of existing diagnostic methods insufficient to meet the requirements of intelligent operation and maintenance. This paper proposes a novel diagnostic algorithm that utilizes an improved VMD-WT method to denoise the collected vibration signals, effectively separating noise from useful components. The denoised time-domain signals are then converted into timefrequency maps using STFT, fully mining fault feature information. A CNN model is constructed to classify these timefrequency maps, achieving intelligent identification of the track status. Research results show that the proposed algorithm, trained on samples from harsh environments, can still maintain high precision under small-sample conditions. It provides a practical solution for the intelligent operation and maintenance of underground track transport systems and holds significant practical importance for promoting the intelligent upgrading of operation and maintenance in mine transport systems.

title: Forecasting of Renewable Energy Using Deep Learning Models
authors: M. Thirunavukkarasu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: To meet the diverse energy demands worldwide, renewable energy based electrical generation is becoming more and more environmentally benign. For the purpose of planning, developing, and expanding power systems, accurate forecasting and reliable details about diverse renewable resources are promising. Therefore, multiple kinds of deep learning models have been proposed in the present study, and their hybridization models are assessed for multi-objective forecasting of temperature, wind speed, and solar irradiation. The proposed model considered a time series of meteorological data for a location in Kanyakumari, Tamil Nadu, India, and the obtained data was preprocessed, trained, tested, and evaluated using different statistical parameters. A higher value of the coefficient of determination provides information about the proportion of variance in datasets, and a lower value of RMSE directly focuses on the error magnitude of the various models. It has been observed that both GRU and hybridization of GRU perform an efficient computation with higher values of the coefficient of determination and Pearson correlation coefficient, which enhance predictive accuracy and robustness with minimum values of mean absolute error and root mean squared error. According to the results, combining multiple deep learning models can enhance each model's potential by utilizing its unique strengths and compensating for its shortcomings.

title: Reinforcement-Fine-Tuned LLM Navigation Decision-Making System: A Dynamic Obstacle Avoidance Method Based on the Time-Decay Reward Mechanism
authors: Yukang Lou, Zhen Cao, Haoyu Wang, Zilong Xiao, Hongjuan Zhang, Zhen Dong, Bijun Li
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: With the development of large models, there is an urgent demand for the advancement of technologies integrating large models with navigation. Addressing the decision bias issue of current LLM-based navigation decision models in indoor dynamic scenarios where long-term path optimization is prioritized over short-term risk avoidance, this paper proposes a LLM Reinforcement Fine-Tuning Model based on time-decay reward Mechanism (TDR-LLM-RFT). TDR-LLM-RFT constructs a “perception-decision-feedback” closed-loop system, takes multimodal navigation data as input and designs a time-decay reward function. It dynamically allocates the reward weights for “immediate obstacle avoidance” and “long-term path planning” through an exponential decay coefficient, thereby prioritizing the triggering of short-term risk responses. Meanwhile, based on the VLN-R1 reinforcement fine-tuning paradigm, the PPO algorithm is used to fine-tune the base LLM (Llama3 7B), enabling it to adapt to dynamic navigation decision logic. Experimental results show that in the dataset, compared with traditional discrete RL models, TDR-LLM-RFT achieves a 5.7 % increase in the success rate of dynamic obstacle avoidance and a 6.2 % reduction in decision delay. Moreover, it still maintains an 84.3 % semantic satisfaction rate in high dynamic density scenarios.

title: A Progressive Noise-Robust Laser Stripe Center Extraction Method
authors: Yajing Zhou, Zhan Li, Yichao Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: In intelligent robotic welding, the accuracy of laser stripe centerline extraction has a decisive impact on welding quality. To address the issues of traditional methods, such as weak anti-interference capability and poor real-time performance and accuracy, this paper proposes a progressive noise-robust laser stripe center extraction method that can adapt to spatter, reflections, arc light, smoke and other noise sources in welding environments. Firstly, based on the geometric characteristics of the single-line laser stripe, an adaptive seed selection and dualconstraint region growing algorithm is proposed for preliminary extraction. Secondly, connected components are extracted with noise elimination, and then further segmented into primitives via an improved Douglas-Peucker algorithm incorporating slope constraints. Finally, an improved linear interpolation algorithm based on neighborhood consistency verification and edge envelope detection is designed to interpolate the broken areas of the laser stripes, connecting the primitives into complete laser stripes. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method has strong anti-interference capability and can effectively extract the complete and accurate laser stripe centerline.

title: Vehicle Path Planning Algorithm Based on D3QN
authors: YiTong Han, YaSenJiang MuSha, JiaHui Hu, YueQi Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: To address the challenges of low sample efficiency, slow convergence, and limited stability in traditional Deep QNetwork (DQN)-based intelligent vehicle path planning, this study proposes a D3QN algorithm that integrates the Dueling DQN and the Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN). First, the Dueling network architecture separates the state-value function from the action-advantage function, thereby enhancing the network's perception and decision-making capabilities in complex environments. Second, it integrates a prioritized experience replay mechanism to improve sample efficiency. Finally, it incorporates the DDQN mechanism, which employs distinct value functions for action selection and value estimation, thereby mitigating Q -value overestimation and enhancing convergence stability. Extensive experiments validate the algorithm's accuracy and feasibility, demonstrating performance that is comparable to, or even surpasses, that of several classical algorithms.

title: Edge-Enabled Deep Learning Frameworks for Heart Disease Prediction: A Comprehensive Review
