authors: A. Ashwini, B. Bharathi Kannan, A. Barveen, N. Sheela, N. Vyshnavi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Heart disease constitutes one of the causes of death in the world. It has a major impact on raising the price of healthcare and initiating premature deaths. The detection and the subsequent diagnosis of cardiovascular disease in its initial stages is the key to preventing the mortality and offering the prevention. Conventional cloud-based diagnostic of heart diseases systems are currently experiencing the shortcomings on the basis of high latency, low bandwidth, and privacy threats that renders them inefficient in making clinical decisions in real time. New perspectives of cardiac monitoring efficiency, in a distributed manner, emerge due to emerging developments in edge computing and deep learning. This paper gives a summary of the most recent advancements in the area of early cardiovascular disease anticipation with a definite emphasis on the integration of deep learning algorithms into the edge-based frameworks. This paper will discuss datasets, preprocessing methods, feature selection algorithms and classification models that have been used over recent years. It also points out relevance of edge-based design paradigms in offering privacy preserving, scalable and rapid cardiac analytical services. Also, the paper recognizes aspects of current research that remain gap-filled, like the unsatisfactory evidence in support of federated learning, the challenges associated with model compression, and the need to combine heterogeneous data modalities. And finally, it provides directions, such as lightweight deep learning systems, edge and cloud system interactions, and energy consumption optimization, which leads to the creation of smart, patient-centered, and real-time cardiac care systems infrastructure.

title: Caspian Pipeline Consortium Equipment Monitoring System
authors: Aleksandr V. Belov, Irina V. Arutyunova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The equipment condition assessment system of the Caspian Pipeline Consortium is purposed to enhance the efficiency of monitoring and to manage the operation of pipeline systems. The primary objective of this initiative is to develop an information system that enables continuous monitoring of pipeline conditions, thereby minimizing maintenance costs and reducing risks associated with potential failures. To achieve this goal, several tasks have been defined, including the analysis of existing pipeline condition assessment methods, the collection and structuring of data, the application of statistical analysis to identify patterns, and the development of a predictive model for equipment condition. The most important aspect is the evaluation of the effectiveness of the predictive model, which will justify its feasibility and advantages over traditional approaches.

title: Fusion of Inertial Navigation and Visible Light Positioning for High-Accuracy Indoor Pedestrian Localization on Smartphones
authors: xx x, xxx xxx, xxx xxx
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: With the increasing demand for Location-Based Services (LBS), high-precision indoor positioning technology has become a research hotspot. Pedestrian Dead Reckoning (PDR) is a technique for pedestrian localization that leverages inertial sensor, allowing for positioning without reliance on external signals. This makes it particularly well-suited for indoor environments. However, the low-quality measurements generated by consumer-grade Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) in smartphones limits the accuracy of PDR-based positioning. Visible Light Positioning (VLP) can provide high-precision position but it is difficult to applied into mobile due to limited sampling rate. In this paper, we propose a seamless integration of Pedestrian Dead Reckoning (PDR) and Visible Light Positioning (VLP) to enable continuous, reliable, and high-precision indoor localization on mobile devices. First, we investigate smartphone-based positioning techniques, then develop a robust PDR algorithm to enhance accuracy and stability. Finally, we implement an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)-based fusion framework to optimally combine PDR and VLP, improving localization performance in dynamic indoor environments. To assess the algorithm's performance, we set up an experimental scenario. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves positioning accuracy, with an enhancement of 26% to 28% compared to PDR positioning.

title: Multivariate Time Series Forecasting of Air Quality Index with Transformer Models
authors: Mukesh Kumar Bharti, Vaibhav Sharma, Ananya Raj, Vaishnavi Bisen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of Air Quality Index (AQI) is crucial for public health and environmental planning. Traditional time series models struggle to capture complex, non-linear relations in multivariate air quality data. This paper compares advanced deep learning methods for AQI forecasting using the Air Quality Index dataset. This dataset contains features like PM2.5, PM10, NO, Temperature, and Apparent Temperature. We evaluate a Transformer model, designed to learn long-range dependencies, against various models, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN). The standard pre-processing steps were applied, followed by training and evaluation on time series sequences. We obtain promising results based on Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) show the Transformer consistently outperforms other models. This work highlights the potential of Transformer networks for accurate, reliable multivariate air quality forecasting to support better environmental strategies.

title: Fault Diagnosis Method for the Tuning Area of Jointless Track Circuit Based on LSTM Neural Network
authors: Lunhui Xu, Runnan Xu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The safety of train operation can be seriously affected by faults in the tuning area of jointless track circuits, and the traditional periodic maintenance may not be sufficient to meet the increasing demand. To address this problem, a fault diagnosis model for the tuning area of jointless track circuits based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network is proposed in this study. Firstly, the tap state model of ZPW-2000A jointless track circuit is established, and the short-circuit current amplitude curves under various operating conditions are obtained from the model. Next, the Complementary Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition (CEEMD) algorithm is used to extract the feature components to generate the sample data required for building the fault diagnosis model. Finally, a fault diagnosis model based on LSTM neural network is established and compared with Back Propagation (BP) neural network model and Support Vector Machine (SVM) model in the same environment. In terms of diagnostic accuracy and training time, the LSTM neural network fault diagnosis model outperforms the other models and has a better application value.

title: SSFE-Net: Spectral-Spatial Fusion and Enhancement Network for Hyperspectral and Multispectral
authors: Junyue Huang, Long Ma
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Hyperspectral and multispectral image fusion plays a pivotal role in elevating the spatial resolution of hyperspectral data and, consequently, in broadening their applicability across downstream tasks. While recent deep-learning solutions have attained remarkable success in hyperspectral and multispectral fusion super-resolution. However, in this field, there is still room for further improvement in feature fusion of hyperspectral multispectral images and extraction of small-scale spatial features. To further enrich the research in this direction, we design a new network SSFE-Net, a fusion framework that jointly pursues fusion of hyperspectral multispectral image features and extraction quality of small size features. SSFE-Net integrates a Hyperspectral Multispectral Feature Fusion Module (HMFFM) that realize efficient integration of hyperspectral image channel information and multispectral image spatial information; and an Edge FineGrained Feature Enhancement Module (EFGFEM) that is capable of adaptive adjustment according to the ground objects, thus ensuring robust representation of intricate land-cover entities. Extensive experiments on three datasets demonstrate that SSFENet surpasses state-of-the-art competitors on various evaluation indicators and the calculation complexity and parameter quantity are in a reasonable range.

title: Explainable Flight State Recognition: Toward Intelligent Pilot Training Systems
authors: Anam Iqbal, Graham Wild, Kanwal Iqbal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The rapidly growing aviation industry's success heavily depends on pilots receiving intensive training, which ensures safety of air travel. Pilots confront diverse situations during different flying states of an aircraft; therefore, they need to have a thorough understanding of these flight regimes and retain proper control in order to foresee and manage any hazards. This study aims to improve the identification of flying states by integrating Explainable AI (XAI) with classification models. Different classification models are employed to flight data for classifying straight and level and turning state of cruise phase of an aircraft. XGBoost (XGB) model is found to be very confident in its predictions for both flight states and showed superior performance than its counterparts with the highest values of precision, recall, F1 score and accuracy. Further, Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) technique is employed to improve explainability by locating the key features that impact the XGB model's outcomes and lead model's conclusion to be certain or uncertain about the predicted flight state. The findings indicate that XAI has the power to increase the transparency and interpretability of AI models in aviation industry and to enhance pilot performance diagnostics during specific flight states, thus enhancing training and flying safety.

title: A Multi-Objective Integrated Optimisation Framework for Grid Outage Scheduling Based on Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yue Zhu, Yang Zhang, Rui Liu, Jiutai Jin, Yu Xing
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the accelerated development of new power systems, optimising grid outage planning faces challenges such as high dimensionality, non-linearity, and multi-objective conflicts. Addressing the requirements of “ensuring safety, guaranteeing supply, and facilitating renewable integration,” this paper proposes a distributed collaborative optimisation framework based on multi-agent reinforcement learning. By designing multi-agent reward functions, value decomposition algorithms, and agent communication coordination mechanisms, it achieves multi-objective collaborative decision-making. The integration of knowledge transfer and online learning enhances the algorithm's adaptability and efficiency. Experiments demonstrate that this framework outperforms conventional methods in optimisation efficiency, multi-objective balance, and system robustness, providing an effective technical pathway for intelligent distributed orchestration of large-scale grid outage planning.

title: “Digital Audit” System Usage in Assessment the Company's Security System Performance
authors: Ilya I. Livshitz, Pawel A. Lontsikh, Natalia P. Lontsikh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: This publication addresses the issue of optimizing the current practice of management systems auditing security. The importance of this challenge is determined by the known contradictions between the requirements of existing standards (e.g. ISO/IEC 27001 series) and different stakeholders' expectations. Interesting discussion among expert auditors motivated by dynamic rather than fixed “paper” audits and top management committed to achieving the effectiveness of their own safety management systems is currently underway. The publication presents the results of practical testing of a system of “digital audits” for a large company, which is supported by unbiased data. The system of “digital audits” is characterized by a faster feedback cycle, application of numerical metrics and flexible (dynamic) process of considering the criteria, audit evidence and evaluation of corrective actions. The scientific novelty lies in proposing a closed-loop system of internal audits providing fast feedback and a uniform comparable assessment for different stakeholders. The obtained outcomes may be of interest to experts of certifying authorities performing safety management system audits in accordance with the requirements of both national and global standards.

title: Coal Mine Safety Monitoring System Based on Multi-Source Communication
authors: Xiaoxiao Qi, Mingming Yang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the challenges in coal mine underground operations-such as limited monitoring coverage of hazard sources, low accuracy and reliability in data acquisition and transmission, and complex wiring requirements-this paper proposes a safety monitoring system incorporating a multi-source communication strategy. The system integrates ZigBee wireless technology with CAN bus technology to enable flexible and reliable data communication. To address key performance concerns, this study supplements tests on ZigBee's actual underground transmission distance, multi-source communication reliability enhancement mechanisms, data acquisition terminal power consumption optimization, multi-hop communication latency impact, multi-sensor data fusion evaluation, and dynamic environment adaptability. Through the design and implementation of hardware and software for data acquisition terminals, along with functional validation in a laboratory environment, the proposed system significantly extends the monitoring range and improves the accuracy and reliability of data transmission.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)

title: Modeling of RC Snubber, Ferrite Bead and Gate Drive Impedance for Optimal EMI Suppression and Switching Loss Trade-Off in SiC MOSFET Power Converters
authors: Jamlick M. Kinyua, Mutsumi Aoki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The relentless drive towards extremely fast switching frequency, elevated operating voltage, increased thermal capabilities, and reduced switching losses have positioned Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs based converters at the forefront in high-performance power electronics applications. This brings in the benefits of enhanced switching frequencies, improved power density, and enhanced dynamic response. Unfortunately, this is critically constrained by severe Electromagnetic Interference (EMI), high-frequency ringing and undesirable switching oscillations. These challenges are addressed through a systemic modelling and analysis mitigation measures, the holistic cooptimizing ferrite bead on the gate loop, RC snubber, and gate drive impedance simultaneously. An LTspice simulation framework was developed, incorporating the manufacturer's spice models to accurately model parasitics and quantify losses. The proposed methodology shows that the addition of the mitigation technique offers a practical trade-off between EMI suppression and switching performance, without increasing the switching losses.

title: Impedance Modeling Method and Stability Analysis in Oscillation Scenarios
authors: Honglu Xu, Zixuan Guo, Ni Zhen, Liping Gao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The existing methods are mainly based on impedance modeling of the steady-state operating point before oscillation. Due to the frequency domain characteristics of the operating point changing during the oscillation process, the existing modeling methods will no longer be applicable. Therefore, an impedance modeling method suitable for oscillation scenarios is proposed. Firstly, taking into account the influence of oscillation components on frequency domain characteristics, a frequency domain small signal model for the main circuit and control link is established. Based on this, a frequency domain impedance model of the AC port of voltage source converter (VSC) is constructed, taking into account the oscillation component. Furthermore, based on the maximum peak Nyquist criterion, the stability during the oscillation process is analyzed. Finally, based on the parameters of a direct-drive wind power grid connected system, a system model is built through an experimental simulation platform. The stability analysis results of the system during the oscillation process were compared with and without considering the oscillation component, indicating that the established model can accurately characterize the impedance characteristics of the interaction between the new energy converter and the grid.

title: Optimization Application of FPGA-Based GA-BP Neural Network in Fire Gas Detection
authors: Zhiliang Cai, Jieming Huang, Dawei Lu, Xiquan Li, Xiaorong Huang, Yinhua Li, Weibiao Xiong
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issues of traditional backpropagation (BP) neural networks, such as susceptibility to local optima and slow convergence in early fire gas detection, this paper proposes a BP neural network model optimized by a genetic algorithm (GA) and implemented on an FPGA hardware platform. First, the initial weights and biases of the BP neural network are optimized using the global search capability of the GA to construct a three-layer GA-BP network. Subsequently, the forward propagation of the GA-BP model is implemented on FPGA hardware through modular design and a pipelined parallel architecture. Fixed-point quantization and segmented approximation of activation functions are incorporated to optimize resource utilization. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the traditional BP network, the GA-BP model improves convergence speed by 28.57%. The FPGA system reduces the response time from 1.8ms in software implementations to 60ns, with an average relative error of only 0.86% compared to software-based results. This study provides a high-precision, low-latency hardware solution for fire detection in energy storage stations.

title: Development of an AI-Consultant for a Design Engineer Using a Neural Network and a Dynamo Script
authors: Olga V. Smirnova, Win Ko Myint Thu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: This article describes to the creation of an advisory tool for design engineers based on modern artificial intelligence technologies. The basis of the tool is a neural network trained on a vast array of data, including modern rules and regulations and data from technical documentation. The system interacts with users through a special interface on the Dynamo platform, which is integrated directly into the workspace of BIM solutions such as Autodesk Revit.

title: Development of the Methodology for Automation of Access Control to the Resources of a Transport Enterprise
authors: Nataliya M. Kuznetsova, Tatiana V. Karlova, Anna N. Zapolskaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The main feature of the information environment of the transport enterprise is the heterogeneity of the subjects of requests to its resources. While the objects of information relations are the resources of the transport enterprise, the employees of the transport enterprise, its customers, and the equipment and materials suppliers are the subjects of information relations. In this regards, ensuring the access control to the resources of a transport enterprise is actual problem. The article is devoted to development of the methodology for automation of access control to the resources of a transport enterprise.

title: Dual-CVAE: Unified Data Augmentation and Feature Extraction for Resnext-GRU Phishing Detection
authors: Sunil Kumar S, Nikhil Prasanna A, Parthiban R, P. Shanthi, E. Shanmuga Priya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Phishing attacks represent a persistent and evolving threat to cybersecurity, leveraging sophisticated social engineering tactics that consistently circumvent traditional detection mechanisms. Existing solutions often struggle with the dynamic nature of these threats and the inherent class imbalance found in phishing datasets. Furthermore, state-of-the-art ensemble methods often involve substantial computational overhead, which makes them difficult to deploy in environments with limited computational resources. To overcome this limitation, this study proposes a lightweight hybrid deep learning framework aimed at improving both efficiency and detection performance. The proposed architecture synergistically combines a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) with a ResNeXt-Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) classifier. The CVAE serves a dual purpose: first, as a generative model to augment the training data with realistic, class-conditioned synthetic samples, thereby mitigating class imbalance without external algorithms like SMOTE; and second, as a streamlined feature extractor that transforms a highdimensional, sparse feature space into a dense, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6}$</tex>-dimensional latent representation. This compressed feature set is then processed by a custom ResNeXt-GRU model engineered to capture complex, non-linear patterns and sequential dependencies within the data. Experimental evaluation on a public phishing dataset demonstrates the efficacy of this approach, achieving a validation accuracy of 91.44 % and a training accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97-98 \%$</tex>. This work contributes a significant advancement in phishing detection by creating an integrated, lightweight framework that addresses data quality, feature extraction, and classification in a single end-to-end pipeline.

title: Enhanced Hardware Trojan Detection Using Hybrid Machine Learning Models
authors: Shreya Kamlesh Pathak, Rajvi Bharodiya, Rani Kumari
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Global chip manufacturing and the expanding use of third-party intellectual property (IP) have raised the possibility of hidden Hardware Trojans (HTs) within integrated circuits (ICs). These malicious changes can leak data, reduce performance, or cause system failure. Traditional detection methods depend on golden reference chips or detailed side channel tests, which are costly and hard to scale. This work presents a hybrid Machine Learning (ML) framework that combines supervised, unsupervised, and semi supervised approaches for effective HT detection. To make the model more robust, a synthetic data augmentation technique is used to mimic Process-voltage-temperature (PVT) variations. The enhanced dataset includes 5,000 circuit samples with four key features: power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{mW})$</tex>, leakage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mu \mathrm{A})$</tex>, toggle rate, and delay (ns). Several ML models (Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, XGBoost, K-Means and Isolation Forest) were tested among which Random Forest (RF) achieved the highest accuracy of 95%, better than the 91.38% benchmark. The proposed hybrid framework works even with partially labeled data and removes the need for golden chips, enabling scalable and automated Trojan detection.

title: Critical Analysis of Traditional and Adaptive Cloud Task Scheduling Algorithms: Dynamic and Heterogeneous Workload Issues
authors: Saquib Ali, Raza Abbas Haidri, Nafees Akhter Farooqui
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: An essential platform for offering scalable and adaptable computer resources is cloud computing. However, because workloads and infrastructures are constantly changing, task scheduling continues to be one of the biggest problems. From traditional heuristics and meta-heuristics to machine learningbased and hybrid approaches, this study offers an organised survey and classification of cloud work scheduling algorithms. Additionally, key scheduling goals including makespan reduction, cost effectiveness, energy usage, resource utilisation, and QoS adherence are examined. Furthermore, the limitations of established static techniques such as HEFT in diverse contexts and real-time fluctuations are examined. Different trade-offs between computing complexity, scalability, and adaptability are revealed via an intensive comparative examination. The study concludes by highlighting unresolved issues and suggesting future lines of investigation for AI-driven and energy-conscious scheduling techniques. The goal of this study is to assist practitioners and researchers in the rapidly changing field of cloud task scheduling.

title: An Adaptive Elitist-Driven Arithmetic Optimizer for Efficient DG Planning in Radial Power Distribution Grids
authors: Manasi Patro, Usharani Raut
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In the present restructured power system infrastructure, Distributed Generation (DG) allocation is considered to be one of the most efficient strategies adopted by the distribution companies to manage a multitude of techno-economic and environmental concerns. This work attempts to find the optimal size of DG units using a multi-objective Adaptive Elitist-Driven Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (AEAOA) with weighted coefficients and a loss sensitivity factor (LSF) based on reactive power loss to find the potential sites. The proposed AEAOA in contrast to traditional techniques, integrates the novel adaptive elitist mechanism into the Arithmetic Optimization framework, enhancing convergence stability and global search precision of DG planning. The efficacy of the algorithm is tested on IEEE 33-bus distribution test system under practical voltage dependent load models. The accuracy and good applicability of the proposed model are confirmed by comparing with other multiobjective models in terms of the convergence characteristics, voltage profile improvement and power loss minimization.

title: A Web-Based License Plate Recognition System with YOLO and EasyOCR in Urban Traffic Law Enforcement
authors: Muhammad Awais, Taimoor Riaz, Muhammad Majid Zaman, Sabeen Masood, Rizwan Ahmed Khan, Abdul Sami
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This paper proposed a web-based License Plate Recognition (LPR) system, which is supposed to automate the process of vehicle registration number recognition and create echallan of the traffic violation. The system of the proposed architecture is quite efficient as it unites image processing and deeper learning-based recognition pipeline; as a result, the manual work of data entry can be significantly reduced, and the performance of the enforcement can be automated. OpenCV is used to complete preprocessing (e.g., edge detection and noise reduction); EasyOCR is used to read the characters on the plate segments. The web interface is a light weight Flask framework through which a web interface exposes the services to the officers. The model learns a local Pakistani license plate data and measures performance with standard performance indicators. The system can be applied to real-world traffic scene because the results of the experiments reveal the system can have stable detection and recognition rates under diverse circumstances in the real world.

title: Transformer-Based Abstractive Summarization of Text from Legal Domain
authors: Shubham Harbola, Aman Dogra, Seema Shukla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital textual data has created a strong demand for effective automatic text summarisation systems that can generate succinct and insightful summaries due to the quick expansion of digital textual data. Automated methods are crucial because manual summarisation is time-consuming and impractical when working with largescale documents. This project introduces an abstractive text summarisation system that, instead of depending on sentence extraction, learns the semantic structure of the text to produce summaries that are human-like. The Hugging Face Transformers framework is used to implement the suggested system using contemporary transformer-based encoder-decoder architectures, namely BART and T5. The BillSum dataset, which includes legislative documents and their summaries, as well as a domainspecific dataset selected to assess performance in a targeted application domain, were used to train and assess the model. We used standard metrics like ROUGE to measure performance, and the results show that transformer-based models can make coherent, fluent, and contextually accurate summaries of domain-specific data.

title: Generative AI-Based Vision-Language Framework for Tomato Leaf Disease Identification and Context-Aware Treatment Recommendations
authors: Gunjan Thakur, Vandana Sood, Amitava Choudhury
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This work proposes a novel Vision-Language Generative AI framework for automated plant disease detection and treatment recommendation. The proposed framework integrates a fine-tuned Swin Transformer-based vision encoder with the OpenAI GPT-OSS-120B generative language model to analyze tomato leaf images and generate transparent diagnostic outputs along with context-aware treatment suggestions. The multimodal pipeline first classifies the disease type and subsequently produces relevant textual recommendations to support informed agricultural decision-making. The model was evaluated on a five-class tomato leaf dataset comprising early blight, late blight, bacterial spot, septoria leaf spot, and healthy samples. Experimental results indicate a classification accuracy of 95.81% and semantic alignment between visual features and the generated text, highlighting the framework's potential for intelligent and interpretable plant disease management.

title: Design and Simulation of Apple Picking Flexible Hands
authors: Shuo Ma, Nannan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: To address low-efficiency manual apple picking and high damage of automatic devices, this study develops a bionic flexible gripper by integrating mechanical design and electronic control, after analyzing apple morphological and physical properties. With three “A”-arranged flexible fingers, the gripper achieves precise electronic opening and closing control via a micro servo motor and system, adapting to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14.92-196.58 \text{mm}$</tex> apple diameters. Finite element analysis shows motor-driven fingers bend synchronously, forming an apple-conforming envelope with excellent wrapping and clamping stability. This work validates the bionic fin-ray finger's advantages, and highlights electronic engineering's pivotal role in agricultural robot end effector performance-specifically in intelligent perception and precise actuation.

title: Real-Time Decision Engines in Financial Trading Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Gagandeep Singh, Sonam Sharma
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper examines how real-time financial trading decision-making systems can be developed and evaluated with the help of a deep learning reinforcement system (RL). An open-source OpenAI Gym compatible trading environment is developed which simulates a realistic market environment, using aspects like order execution and slippage, transaction costs and market impact. Models are learned using historical stock data that is converted into time-series and technical indicators. Two reinforcement learning paradigms (policy-gradient (PPO) and value-based (DQN) with the recent stability methods) are executed and evaluated within a single pipeline, which implements the support of walk-forward back-testing and simulated real-time streaming based on a lightweight API framework. The performance of the model is benchmarked in several areas: financial (accumulation and annualized returns, Sharpe and Sortino ratios, largest drawdown), trading (turnover, frequency and slippage), and machine learning (training variance, convergence behaviour). The discussion also looks into how the structure of rewards, transaction costs, and the level of latency is affecting the behaviour of the agents, with support of explainability studies to explain trading policies. In general, this paper provides a reproducible, deployment-ready framework that strictly compares RL strategies in terms of policy and value with reproducible approaches in near-realistic trading environments and determines the design and risk management approaches required to enable responsible simulated deployment.

title: Sensitivity Analysis and Optimization of Hybrid Energy Systems Using Metaheuristic Approaches with Techno-Socio-Economic Enhancement
authors: Ganesh Navnath Surwase, Ingudam Chitrasen Meitei, Rajkumari Malemnganbi Devi
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization framework for power generation in an off-grid Hybrid Renewable Energy System (HRES) integrating solar photovoltaic and wind energy. To mitigate the stochastic variability of renewable sources, the system incorporates battery energy storage supported by a secondary diesel generator as a backup. The study emphasizes the economic, technical, and social feasibility of the system through an effective energy management strategy that ensures reliability while minimizing costs and emissions. Mathematical modeling and optimization techniques are applied, with particular focus on the recently developed Fire Hawk Optimization (FHO) algorithm. The performance of FHO is benchmarked against the Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) and the. Results demonstrate that FHO achieves superior economic performance, yielding a minimum annual system cost of $ 31,038 and a cost of energy of $ 0.1238/kWh for an annual load demand of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$250,760 \text{kWh}$</tex>. The optimized configuration effectively balances renewable generation with battery operation, significantly reducing reliance on fossil fuels while offering surplus energy potential during certain months. This study highlights the promise of advanced metaheuristic algorithms in enhancing the design and operation of sustainable off-grid energy systems along with sensitivity analysis.

title: Optimizing Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Algorithms in the Prediction of EEG Based Epileptic Seizure State Using Federated Dataset
authors: Ayyappan G, Yogananda P, Thankaraj A, Madhanmohan S, Annamalai Giri A, Uma Devi M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The issue of seizure forecasting pertains to patient safety and clinical management because it gives patient caregivers early alerts in the run-up to the occurrence of seizures. The current paper introduces a comparative machine learning model on the problem of classifying the multi-class state of seizure based on a large-scale dataset of EEG signals of 289014 samples and 52 time-, frequency-, and nonlineardomain features. The data is characterized as interictal, preictal, ictal, and postictal and is preprocessed and stratified split so that equal consideration is given to all. Six classifiers have been applied, and they are; Random Forest, Bagging, IBK (k-NN), Multilayer Perceptron, BayesNet, and JRip using the WEKA environment. The analysis of performance was based on Accuracy, Precision, Recall, ROC, PRC and computational efficiency. It was found that the best accuracy was obtained on Random Forest (92.11 %), followed by JRip with great recall and PRC (0.97 each) and the shortest execution time (0.03 s), so it is the best choice in real-time applications. The results show that the ensemble and rulebased approaches are more effective than probabilistic and shallow learners, which confirm their application in the EEGbased seizure detection and prediction. Comparative understanding gained through this research work can be used to come up with hybrid and explainable models to be used on an actual clinical implementation of a neurological monitoring system.

title: Spiking Neural Network Models for Real-Time Sensory-Motor Integration in Bio-Inspisred Robotic Systems
authors: Senthil Kumar S, Barath G, Maduvanthi S, Thulasi P, Ramesh R, Kavitha K
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The design of bio-inspired robots has more and more relied on neuromorphic computation to imitate the efficiency and plasticity of biological creatures. Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) being the third generation of artificial neural networks that are characterized by their event-driven nature and temporal processing efficiency have attracted potential for online sensorymotor integration. This paper provides a generic framework using SNN models to achieve dynamic response of low-latency interactions between sensory inputs and motor outputs for robotic systems. The architecture includes biologically motivated spikebased neural coding strategies and spike-timing dependent plasticity (STDP) so as to enable it to learn from environmental stimuli and perform complex motor behaviours with low energy use. Experiments on a bio-inspired robot arm show improved reactivity and reaction latency and motion adaptability with respect to classical ANN controllers. The results highlight the great promise of SNNs to shape a future where real-time control systems for autonomous robotics can integrate energy-efficient and bio-inspired approaches for individuals and large population of robotic systems performing tasks with lifelike sensory-motor coordination.

title: Prediction of Genetic Trait Expression Using Data Analytics
authors: Vikram Yadav, Laxman Kumar Busetty, Venkateswara Gogineni, Vinod Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Modern genomic studies increasingly use computational methods to uncover patterns in large DNA datasets. In this study, we develop an unsupervised clustering pipeline for analyzing DNA sequences and discovering genetic patterns. Our approach combines k-mer feature extraction, TF-IDF vectorization, SVD-based dimensionally reduction, and KMeans clustering to group similar sequences. The method was evaluated on a dataset of 1,683 DNA sequences and produced clearly separated clusters with distinct compositional characteristics, particularly between GC-rich and A-rich sequence motifs. The clustering approach also helped identify a small number of unusual sequences that may represent rare genetic variants. Because labeled trait data were not available, the study focuses on unsupervised evaluation rather than supervised trait prediction accuracy. The pipeline demonstrated effective clustering performance with a mean silhouette score of 0.47, while motif-level analysis provided biologically interpretable insights into sequence patterns. This paper also discusses the limitations of the current approach, ethical considerations related to genomic data analysis, and possible future directions for extending the framework toward clinical trait prediction. The results provide a useful starting point for future work in computational genomic pattern discovery.

title:
Influence of Ionophore Layer on TiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Based EGFET Nitrate Sensor
authors: Nur Syahirah Kamarozaman, Cherlestra Hendera Lomius, Sukreen Hana Herman, Zurita Zulkifli
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This paper presents the characterization and performance evaluation of an Extended-Gate Field-Effect Transistor (EGFET) nitrate sensor utilizing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> and ionophore incorporated TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/N16-DBP composite-based sensing electrodes, both prepared via spin-coating. The main objective of this work is to examine how material composition and surface morphology affect the performance of TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based EGFET nitrate sensors, and to assess the potential of ionophore incorporation in enhancing their sensitivity and selectivity. Surface characterization was performed using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM), digital microscopy, and contact angle measurements. Results showed that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T i O}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> films exhibited smoother and more uniform surfaces, while the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TiO}_{2} / \mathrm{N} 16$</tex>-DBP composite film displayed rougher textures and increased hydrophobicity. Electrical characterization was conducted using a Keysight B1500 Semiconductor Parameter Analyzer in sodium nitrate solutions ranging from 10 to 30 ppm. The TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/N16-DBP composite electrode demonstrated improved sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 9 ~ m V} /$</tex> decade and higher linearity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 9 5 0 7}$</tex>) compared to the pure TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> electrode, which achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$66.2 \text{mV} /$</tex> decade and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}=0.9259$</tex>. These findings confirmed that incorporating N16-DBP ionophore enhanced the electrochemical performance of EGFET nitrate sensors.

title: Optimized Slip Mode Frequency Shift (SMS) Based Anti-Islanding Technique for Single-Phase Grid-Connected Converters
authors: Kajal Y. Gedam, S.G. Kadwane
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper presents an Enhanced Slip Mode Frequency Shift (E-SMS) anti-islanding technique of single phase grid-connected converters. The proposed solution improves the traditional SMS system by using adaptive positive feedback to increase the speed of the frequency deviation following the loss of grid connection and increasing the maximum phase shift. Although these enhancements have no impact on the quality of power when it is operating normally, they significantly reduce the Non-Detection Zone (NDZ) and enable quicker islanding detection with large load quality factor settings. A comprehensive comparison of the ESMS technique with Active Frequency Drift (AFD) and Sandia Frequency Shift (SFS) shows the superiority of E-SMS technique, as it detects islanding in less than 0.3 s. The findings prove that the IEEE 1547 frequency deviation requirements are met entirely. The E-SMS technique is more reliable, cost effective and easy to integrate, hence its application proves full compliance with the IEEE 1547 frequency deviation criteria. E-SMS method would be an ideal choice to the distributed generation systems that employ photovoltaics due to its enhanced reliability, low cost, and ease of integration.

title: AI-Driven Diagnostic Analysis: Innovations, Challenges, and Future Directions in Medical Imaging and Disease Detection
authors: Surbhi Jha, Neeta Singh, M.A. Ansari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: AI is advancing, including medical imaging and the diagnosis of disease. AI-powered diagnostic applications help clinical practitioners anywhere assess health problems faster and easier and more accurately. These tools can assess any kind of clinical data, such as images, patient retention records, and genetic data. They involve strategies like machine learning, deep learning, and natural language processing to analyze doctors' prescriptions. These AI technologies can make clinicians' treatment choices easier by detecting patterns, recognizing anomalies, and forecasting probable diagnoses. This paper provides a survey of current evidence, describing examples of using AI medical imaging technologies, including AI-empowered imaging applications to assist in diagnosing disease promptly and the development of individualized treatments. The provided evidence recognizes challenges that AI applications face including data and predictability bias, transparency issues, and legal issues of AI in healthcare. In conclusion, this paper discusses the latest implementations, smart systems improvement and future research required for AI in healthcare.

title: Reduction of Arc-Induced Mechanical Stress on a 1000 kV Power Transformer Tank through Embedded High-Voltage Lead Exit Design
authors: Ke Wang, Jinzhong Li, Yikun Zhao, Peng Li, Shuqi Zhang, Geqi Li, Xiang Wang, Liang Luo, Peng Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: The high-voltage lead exit is installed in a compact turret external to a 1000 kV power transformer tank. Strong shock waves induced by high-energy arc faults in the lead exit pose a serious risk to turret and tank safety. To enhance the arc withstand capability of 1000 kV power transformer tanks, this study proposes a novel embedded high-voltage lead exit design. Compared to traditional configurations, the enlarged embedded lead exit facilitates the dispersion and attenuation of arc-induced pressure waves, thereby reducing the risk of explosion and combustion. A comprehensive evaluation—including electric field distribution, mechanical strength, and explosion resistance —is conducted to validate the structural reliability and arc fault tolerance of the proposed design. Notably, an arc pressure simulation method based on the bubble pulsation approach is introduced, in which actual arc power is injected into a gas volume equivalent to the arc length to model the pressure source. The Johnson–Cook (JC) failure criterion is employed to accurately evaluate material failure. Simulation results confirm that the embedded configuration significantly enhances arc fault resistance under various internal fault scenarios. This novel design offers a practical solution for improving the safety and reliability of ultra-high-voltage (UHV) transformers and provides valuable insights for optimizing high-voltage lead exit structures in critical applications.

title: Predicting Failure Rate Trends for Led Drivers in Large Installations
authors: Barry Preston, Stuart Mucklejohn
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: For managing large scale LED lighting installations it is important to recognize if there is an increasing or decreasing failure rate. This will guide the replacement strategy and help with warranty disputes. LED chips rarely fail completely; failures are most likely to be due to drivers. The failure rates of LED drivers can be predicted using the exponential distribution while the associated confidence bounds can be calculated using the binomial distribution. Failure rate trends can be determined using the Laplace Test.

title: Short-Term EV Charging Demand Forecasting Using XGBoost: An Ensemble Learning Approach
authors: Utkarsh Ade, Akanksha Shukla, Suresh Tejavath
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: With growing popularity of electric cars (EVs) creates difficulty in power grid management. For the same, the prediction of EV charging demand is required. In this study, an ensemble learning-based machine learning algorithm called XGBoost is employed for hourly EV charging load forecasting. Real-world session data was used to convert the dataset into hourly average power usage, which was then statistically examined. A filtered MAPE metric for times of high demand, together with MAE, MAPE, and RMSE, was used to assess the model. The performance of XGBoost is compared with SARIMAX, a time-series forecasting method. Results indicate that XGBoost performs significantly better than SARIMAX, particularly when it comes to capturing nonlinear demand patterns during peak hours. Further, the efficacy of the XGBoost method is demonstrated through 24-hour forecasts, which show how well XGBoost matches real load trends.
