title: Defect Detection Algorithm for Silicon Rod End Faces Based on DLL-YOLO
authors: Yupeng Liu, Xiaofu Ji
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issues of large-scale variation in silicon rod end-face defects and the false positives and missed detections caused by external interferences, we propose an improved object detection algorithm based on YOLOv8, named DLL-YOLO. First, we design the C2f-DCN (C2f Deformable Convolutional Network) module, which incorporates deformable convolution and introduces a dual-branch feature extraction structure to process multi-level semantic information in parallel. This allows the model to capture richer feature representations and effectively handle the problem of significant variations in defect scales. In addition, the Large Separable Kernel Attention (LSKA) mechanism is integrated into the SPPF module to better capture spatial and channel information during feature extraction, thereby reducing interference from complex backgrounds. Finally, a lightweight network design is adopted to significantly improve detection speed while maintaining high accuracy. A self-constructed dataset is used to validate the effectiveness of the DLL-YOLO algorithm. Experimental results show that, compared with YOLOv8, DLL-YOLO improves detection accuracy by 8.4%, with parameters and computational cost reduced to only 2.6M and 7.4 GFLOPs, respectively. This achieves a balance between model performance and lightweight design, making it more suitable for industrial applications in silicon rod end-face defect detection.

title: Fuzzy Logic Controller for Fuel Cell Voltage Regulation in Renewable Energy Applications
authors: K. Manickavasagam, Zubair Ahmad Mir, Kartik Singh, Sanchit Sharma, Sanjeet Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper presents a fuzzy logic controller for fuel cell voltage regulation in renewable energy systems. The controller uses two inputs: output voltage measurement from the fuel cell and voltage derivative. Fuel cells are important for clean energy, but have varying output voltage that needs control. The fuzzy logic approach handles this voltage variation better than traditional controllers. Simulation results show the controller maintains a stable voltage output under different load conditions. The system is tested using MATLAB software with various operating conditions.

title: Efficient Deepfake Video Detection Using ResNext CNN and Temporal LSTM Networks
authors: Md Shohel Arman, Md Asaduzzaman, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Since deepfake films allow for the production of extremely convincing manipulated media, they represent serious threats to the integrity of information. These videos are produced utilising sophisticated machine learning models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). This study introduces a hybrid deep learning framework that efficiently detects deepfakes by combining Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal analysis with ResNext Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for spatial feature extraction. By applying transfer learning, the model reduces computing overhead while achieving great accuracy and efficiency. For training and assessment, a meticulously selected dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 0 0 0}$</tex> videos that was evenly distributed between authentic and fraudulent content was utilised. During preprocessing, video frames' facial features were separated and cropped to provide a high-quality face-only dataset. The suggested model proved its resilience in detecting modified information with an astounding 95% detection accuracy on the test set. The model's superiority over baseline techniques was demonstrated through performance validation using metrics like precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. In order to combat the swift advancement of deepfake technology, this study highlights the significance of creating flexible detection methods. Subsequent efforts will concentrate on extending detection capabilities to encompass full-body movements and incorporating the framework into easily available tools such as browser-based plugins for continuous use.

title: Exploring Light-Induced Circadian Stimuli: Influence of Occupant's Spatial Positioning and Gaze Orientation in Office Settings
authors: Gabriele Zocchi, Morteza Hosseini, Georgios Triantafyllidis
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: This pilot study investigates how indoor spatial factors influence melanopic light exposure, which is critical for regulating circadian rhythms and supporting well-being. Specifically, it examines the effect of position relative to a window and viewing direction on melanopic equivalent daylight illuminance (mEDI) in a typical office setting. Over 10 days, 1600 mEDI measurements were collected using a spectrometer at eye level. The analysis compares two distances from the window (1.8 m and 4 m) and two viewing directions (towards the window and 90° away). Results show that viewing direction has a stronger impact on mEDI than distance, with higher and more variable values recorded when facing the window. These findings suggest simple strategies - like orienting workstationsto enhance circadian-effective lighting in workplace design.

title: Enhancing Educational Lighting Quality and Efficiency through Occupancy Aware Reinforcement Learning in Libyan Classrooms
authors: Mamoud Abdullah, Amrou Dhunnis, Adam Bouras, Zouhour Araoud
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: This paper investigates the integration of Reinforcement Learning (RL) into the Libyan educational sector to address the critical challenges of energy instability and substandard lighting quality. By implementing an occupancy-aware control framework, classrooms can autonomously adapt to real-time human presence and ambient daylight. This approach aims to maximize visual comfort for students while significantly reducing the energy waste prevalent in traditional static lighting systems. The study demonstrates that intelligent, adaptive systems are essential for ensuring educational continuity in regions facing infrastructure fragility.

title: Initial Results from the Swedish Cohort in a Wearable Light Logger Field Study
authors: Maria Nilsson Tengelin, Stefan Källberg
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Wearable light loggers enable the assessment of personal light exposure in real-world settings, providing insight into both visual and non-visual effects of light on human physiology. This paper presents initial results from the Swedish cohort of the MeLiDos multi-center field study. Seventeen participants wore spectrally resolved light loggers at multiple body locations for one week. We examine inter-individual variability in light exposure, compare measurements across sensor positions, and analyze photopic illuminance and melanopic equivalent daylight illuminance (m-EDI). Results show strong seasonal and inter-individual variability, systematic differences between logger positions, and pronounced diurnal variation in the melanopic/photopic (m-EDI/Ev) ratio. These findings suggest that wearable light logging is a viable approach in field studies, provided that methodological challenges are carefully addressed.

title: Detection of Silicone Oil Concentration Using a Two-Dimensional Photonic Crystal Sensor
authors: Sarra Bendib, Saidani Okba, Yousfi Abderrahim, Nadhir Djeffal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Silicone oil is widely used in biomedical applications, such as intraocular tamponade agents in retinal surgery, lubricants in implantable devices, and insulating layers in microfluidic systems. Monitoring its concentration is critical for ensuring patient safety, maintaining device performance, and enabling precise control in lab-on-chip platforms. In this work, we propose and analyze a two-dimensional (2D) photonic crystal (PhC) sensor to detect silicone oil concentration in aqueous media. The proposed structure leverages the sensitivity of the defect-mode resonance to change in refractive index to achieve high sensitivity and specificity in the detection of varying silicone oil concentrations. Simulations demonstrate that the resonant wavelength shifts proportionally with the silicone oil volume fraction, providing a reliable optical signature for real-time monitoring.

title: Comparative Analysis of MPPT Algorithms for Photovoltaic Systems under Partial Shading: A MATLAB/Simscape-Based Study
authors: Ahmed Chebri, Fatima Zohra Boukahil, Assala Mouffouk, Boubekeur Azoui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: The performance of photovoltaic (PV) systems is highly sensitive to partial shading, which can introduce multiple power peaks and significantly reduce energy yield. This paper investigates and compares three Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms Perturb and Observe (P&O), Fuzzy Logic Control (FLC), and an improved Sliding Mode Control (SMC) under partial shading conditions. The system and algorithmic approaches are implemented and simulated using MATLAB/Simscape. Simulation results show that the conventional P&O algorithm fails to track the Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP), converging instead to a local maximum. In contrast, both FLC and improved SMC successfully reach the GMPP. Among them, the FLC algorithm achieves the fastest response time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.006 \mathrm{s})$</tex>, the lowest power loss, and the highest partial shading efficiency (99.7%), outperforming the others in accuracy and stability. These findings highlight the effectiveness of intelligent and nonlinear control strategies in optimizing PV system performance under non-uniform irradiance.

title: Combinatorics of Inductance States in Reconfigurable Air Coils
authors: Michael Helbig, Norbert Seliger
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: We propose a group theory based mathematical model for unambiguous inductance states of a reconfigurable air coil inductor utilizing elastic snapping of wire segments. Vertical (axial) and horizontal winding symmetries under group actions reveal distinguishable tuning regions and individual variation states therein. The model is applied to coils of prismatic (cylinder like) and truncated pyramidal (conical like) shape with edges forming a regular polygon as basis, and verified by numerical magnet field simulation.

title: Beyond Labels: Contrastive Representation Learning for Scalable Image-Based Plant Disease Diagnosis
authors: Syed Shayan Ali Shah, Hafeez Anwar, Mansour Ali Saif Alnuaimi, Majed Mansoor Alabd Alshehhi, Mohamed Isa, Obaid Juma Selayem Al Harthi, Farman Ullah
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Plant diseases are a threat worldwide, mainly in areas with limited access to expert diagnosis; however, deep learning is able to automate disease detection by methods that depend on huge datasets, which are both time consuming and costly to make. CNN's often struggle with real world image challenges such as cluttered backgrounds and noise. We propose a self supervised learning (SSL) framework for detecting plant diseases using the PlantVillage dataset. Our methods will use SimCLR-style contrastive pretraining on unlabeled images which is then followed by a fine tuning lightweight CNN (ConvNext-Tiny) with regularization techniques such as CutMix,Mixup, label smoothing which will enhance performance in low supervision scenarios. Consequently, our approach achieve an F1-score of 98.12% and 97.67% accuracy both outperforming recent CNN and vision transformers methods. By less reliance on labeled data and improving robustness to real-world conditions, this strategy provides both a scalable and practical solution for plant disease detection.

title: Hybrid Shaping with LIME and Counterfactual Explanations (DiCE) for Explainable AI in Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Mohamed Aziz Ben Kacem, Farah Jemili, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: The increasing sophistication and prevalence of cyber threats necessitate advanced cybersecurity systems capable of not only detecting intrusions but also providing clear and actionable explanations. Traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) often fall short in this regard, presenting users with alerts accompanied by technical details that lack context and hinder effective response strategies. To address this critical gap, we propose a novel explainable AI (XAI) framework designed to personalize cybersecurity explanations based on individual user expertise and the dynamic threat landscape. Our approach leverages a Random Forest classifier for robust intrusion detection and integrates counterfactual explanations (CFEs) to offer specific mitigation recommendations. By incorporating the MITRE ATT&CK framework, we contextualize threats within a well-established knowledge base, and through dynamic user adaptation, we tailor explanations to the user's level of understanding. The framework employs SHAP and LIME to provide both global and local interpretability of the Random Forest model's decisions. Crucially, we utilize the dice_ml library to generate diverse counterfactual explanations, empowering users with concrete steps they can take to neutralize identified threats. This personalized and explainable approach aims to transform opaque security alerts into actionable insights, thereby enhancing the overall effectiveness of cybersecurity defenses.

title: Intelligent Perception and Early Warning Framework for Agricultural Product Supply Chain Risks Based on Large Language Model and Knowledge Graph
authors: Jingyu Li, Ziguang Lu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: In the context of globalization and climate uncertainty, agricultural supply chains face increasingly complex, dynamic, and multi-source risks that threaten food security and system resilience. Traditional early-warning systems, primarily based on structured data and predefined rules, fail to capture latent dependencies and high-frequency unstructured information, resulting in poor timeliness, accuracy, and interpretability. To address these challenges, this study proposes an intelligent risk perception and early-warning framework that deeply integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with Knowledge Graphs (KGs) for agricultural supply chain management. The framework is designed to (1) automatically extract risk entities and relations from heterogeneous textual streams, (2) construct a dynamically evolving knowledge graph that encodes causal dependencies, and (3) reason about risk propagation paths using a hybrid mechanism that couples graph-based structural inference with LLM-driven semantic reasoning. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves entity and relation extraction F1-scores of 0.87 and 0.83, respectively. In forecasting risk levels over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-7$</tex> day horizons, the system outperforms traditional rule-based, KG-only, and LLM-only baselines, achieving MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.042$</tex>, AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.91$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E C E}=$</tex> 0.028, indicating both superior predictive accuracy and probabilistic calibration. The proposed framework contributes a novel paradigm that systematically fuses the semantic reasoning capacity of LLMs with the structural interpretability of KGs, advancing from “risk perception” to “risk cognition”. Beyond methodological innovation, it provides a deployable technical pathway for enhancing early warning, resilience, and sustainability in agricultural supply chains.

title: Enhancing Beekeeping with UAV Technology: Applications, Planning, Challenges, and Regulatory Insights
authors: Soumya Soundous Toumi, Seloua Haddaoui, Badreddine Miles, Salim Chikhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), commonly known as drones, have significantly transformed the agriculture industry by offering applications that enhance efficiency and productivity. This paper introduces the evolving role of UAVs in agriculture, emphasizing their potential to revolutionize traditional practices. It explores the diverse uses of drones in agricultural settings, with a special focus on their importance in beekeeping practices. The discussion highlights the critical role of planning in the seamless integration of these devices for efficient beehive monitoring. The paper then explains the methodology proposed and classifies UAVs based on their configurations and capabilities, including fixed-wing, multi-rotor, and rotary drones, providing valuable insights for stakeholders to select the most suitable UAV for their specific agricultural needs. While UAVs offer substantial benefits, such as crop monitoring, disease detection, and yield prediction, the paper also addresses their limitations, including restricted flight time, payload capacity, and data processing challenges. It underscores the need for technological advancements and operational refinements to overcome these constraints. Additionally, the paper emphasizes the importance of adhering to regulatory frameworks governing UAV operations in agriculture, outlining key rules and guidelines to ensure safety and compliance. Following these regulations is crucial for minimizing risks and promoting responsible UAV use. The insights provided in this paper serve as a valuable resource for stakeholders looking to leverage UAV technology to improve productivity and promote sustainability in agriculture.

title: Early-Season Water Use Patterns in Wheat and Barley Under Semi-Arid Conditions: A Lysimetric Approach
authors: Omar Bouziane, Mohamed Meddi, Amina Mazighi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Efficient early-season water use is critical for optimizing cereal crop performance in semi-arid environments. This study investigates the early water dynamics of wheat and barley during the first 40 days of growth using high-resolution lysimeter data. The dataset, collected at the Higher National School of Hydraulics in Blida, Algeria, includes daily measurements of precipitation, evapotranspiration (ETP), soil moisture, and drainage. The analysis reveals that barley initiates evapotranspiration activity earlier than wheat, correlating with a more rapid depletion of surface soil moisture. In contrast, wheat maintains slightly higher moisture in the root zone, potentially due to slower canopy development and root activity. Most importantly, temporary saturation events in barley plots led to minor but detectable drainage losses, highlighting a crop-specific vulnerability to water percolation. These findings underscore the distinct water-use strategies of each crop and demonstrate the value of lysimeter data as a foundational dataset. This high-fidelity, real-world data can be used to train and validate artificial intelligence (AI) models for predicting crop-specific water fluxes. Such models, when integrated with low-cost, renewable-energy-powered soil moisture sensors, can enable the development of intelligent irrigation systems that dynamically optimize water applications, thereby enhancing agricultural resilience under water-limited conditions.

title: Generative AI-Enabled Master Data Management for Finance and Enterprise Governance Systems
authors: Arunkumar Thirunagalingam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: High quality master data is essential in ensuring reliability of the counts and reduction ratios to monitor blocking efficiency systems. Current Master Data Management (MDM) strategies are still repeated with data store evaluation counts and derived metrics, and weak traceability cross-platform visibility. Activities that require pipeline process metrics are rapidly adapting to the demands of continuous learning. This article proposes a Generative AI-based MDM framework that combines innovation area index for numeric encoding. The innovation is to use KPI Comparison to generate and create comprehensive and official master records through the synthesis of inconsistent financial data sources including accounts receivable, accounts payable and general ledger hierarchies. When applied in a banking and enterprise governance environment, the framework improves the quality of data, increases the speed of financial reconciliation, and minimizes the risks of a compliance crisis by introducing traceable, explainable and adaptive master data. Experimental findings can show quantifiable increases in record accuracy and efficiency of governance overrule based systems. In this study, generative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> is proposed as a disruptive enabler of resilient and trusted financial master data environments that link operational data management needs with companywide governance needs.

title: An Automatic Face Mask Detection System Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Ashwika Rathore, Raja Lakshmi, Aakash Sharma, Dornadhula Dhanya, Kunal Meher, Febin Prakash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has emerged as a powerful tool in public health monitoring, particularly in automatic face mask detection systems that enhance community safety. These systems are crucial for implementing preventive measures and minimizing the risk of airborne disease transmission. However, existing approaches face several challenges, including low detection accuracy in dynamic environments, slow processing speed, and difficulty handling variations such as partial occlusion, diverse lighting conditions, and different mask types. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a Face Mask Detection Framework (FMDF) that integrates the YOLOv5 object detection algorithm with a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). YOLOv5 ensures real-time object localization, while the Deep CNN enhances feature extraction and classification, resulting in higher precision and faster performance when distinguishing between masked and unmasked individuals. The proposed system demonstrates strong applicability across various settings, including airports, offices, hospitals, schools, and other public spaces, to ensure compliance with mask regulations and support safety monitoring. Experimental results demonstrate that FMDF achieves superior accuracy, robustness, and efficiency compared to traditional methods, rendering it a reliable solution for large-scale, real-time tracking. The proposed FMDF achieves 95.4-93.8% accuracy, 26.1-58.6 FPS speed, 86.7-94.6% precision robustness, and 92.5-96.4% F1-Score.

title: Design Science Research Methodology (DSRM) for IoT-Enabled Predictive Maintenance Towards Sustainability
authors: Faisal Mohd Amin, Norena Abdul Karim Zamri
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: The importance for sustainable and resilient food production systems has positioned smart agriculture as a key enabler of food security in both rural and urban contexts. Within the Industry 5.0 framework that emphasizes sustainability, resilience, and human-technology collaboration, predictive maintenance has emerged as a crucial component for ensuring reliable and efficient operation of IoT-enabled agricultural systems. However, conventional cloud-based machine learning (ML) approaches face limitations due to their dependence on high-bandwidth connectivity, centralized processing, and continuous data transmission-leading to latency, energy inefficiency, and data privacy concerns.

title: Emergence of Altruistic Behavior in a Population of Cognitive Ants: A Prospective Solution to Data Center Location Problem
authors: Malak Chniter, Ilhem Kallel, Slim Kanoun
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Recently, bio-inspired systems have become increasingly intriguing to solve complex optimization problems without centralized control, making them an effective solution for developing efficient applications. Addressing the behavioral analysis of cognitive populations in these systems represents the cornerstone of the robustness of real-time applications. In this paper, we simulate an Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) system guided by simple local rules enabling ants to build cognitive maps, learn multiple objectives, and plan their paths. The accumulation of local learning leads to the emergence of collective behaviors such as specialization, selfishness, or altruism. The latter results in actions where some ants favor collective success. Thus, simple behaviors at the individual level produce self-organized and efficient dynamics at the global level. The simulation results in this paper describe how the local learning with the accumulation of individual decisions advocates the creation of new structures that allow altruistic Ants to improve their adaptive behavior in unknown environments. The data center location problem constitutes a canonical testbed for the deployment of this system, enabling the strategic placement of data centers to meet highdemand regional requirements. In-depth characterization of its time complexity is pivotal for the development of scalable, realtime optimization algorithms.

title: Identifying Precursors to Cardiac Arrest: CNN Superiority in Detecting Arrhythmias and Myocardial Infarction from Vital Signs
authors: Chams Eddine Fathoun, Mohamed Ridda Laouar, Safa Abid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The early detection of patient deterioration in critical care settings remains a paramount challenge. This study presents a comparative analysis of deep learning architectures for classifying three acute conditions-Acute Myocardial Infarction, Arrhythmias, and Respiratory Failure/Hypoxia-from multivariate vital sign time series. We trained and evaluated Long Short-Term Memory networks, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and Gated Recurrent Units on a large-scale dataset of 4.6 million measurements from over 20,000 patients. The CNN architecture significantly outperformed its counterparts, achieving a superior overall accuracy of 92.72%. It demonstrated robust performance across all classes, with particularly perfect discrimination for Respiratory Failure. These results indicate that CNNs are exceptionally adept at extracting salient features from physiological time-series data for clinical prediction. The high accuracy underscores the potential for integrating such models into real-time clinical decision support systems to provide early warnings of deterioration, thereby enabling proactive intervention and improving patient outcomes.

title: Analytical Study of Arabic Speech Emotion Recognition Using Spectral Features and Machine Learning on the KEDAS Dataset
authors: Dalal Djeridi, Bachir Said, Mourad Belhadj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Recently, emotion recognition (ER) has emerged as a compelling field of study. Speech emotion recognition (SER) is a crucial aspect of ER and plays a vital role in human-computer interaction. This study explores various aspects of Arabic SER, focusing on feature relationships and how spectral components and speaker characteristics influence emotion recognition. We review current Arabic SER methods and introduce additional machine learning techniques to enhance performance across a wider range of emotional categories. This research contributes significantly to the growing demand for emotion identification technology in natural language processing, human-computer interaction, and mental health diagnostics. It addresses a crucial gap in the literature on low-resource languages such as Arabic, laying the foundation for future advancements in SER technologies. For experimentation, we employed the KEDAS dataset, which comprises five distinct emotions: fear, anger, sadness, happiness, and neutrality. We extracted spectral characteristics and speaker traits, then analyzed their associations to better understand the relationship between features and emotions. Several machine learning classifiers were applied to the emotion categorization task, including Support Vector Machines (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (K-NN), Logistic Regression (LR), and Random Forest (RF). The highest accuracy among the individual classifiers was achieved by SVM at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0 \%}$</tex>. Furthermore, a stacking ensemble learning classifier, based on the estimators of the four classifiers, achieved an accuracy of 71 % and delivered the best overall performance across all emotions.

title: Graph-Based Deep Learning Model for Identifying Cyber Threats in Cloud Platforms
authors: Mahatma Reddy Marri, Sai Bhuvana Kurada, Shweta Gupta, Saswata Dey, Sachin Kumar, Rahul Chauhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The prevailing trend of cloud computing services has presented a broad spectrum of security threats which is attributed to the dynamic and distributed nature of cloud environment. Though standard machine learning methods have developed a high level of intrusion detection, these are often limited in their ability to perform by the nonlinear relationships between network traffic data that are complex and nonlinear in nature. In this regard, the paper attempt to present a deep learning-driven architecture that can successfully identify and categorize cyber threats in cloud repositories by using both semantic and structural insights in network traffic. The pre-trained word embedding models as Word2Vec and GloVe are used in the proposed methodology to obtain features that can add semantics to the input data. These characteristics are then converted into graphical representations and handled with a Recurrent Graph Neural Network (RGNN), which has the ability to capture spatial and temporal relationships in the data, therefore, enabling the accurate detection of malicious behaviour. The system is tested and trained on a standardized benchmark CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset, a large-scale set of attack vectors in a realistic cloud setup. As experimental findings indicate, the RGNN model can reach maximum detection accuracy of 98.958 % thus, outperforming traditional deep learning baselines.

title: Advanced Multi-Objective Control Strategy for Precision Torque Management in Brushless DC Motors
authors: Jaspreet Sidhu, V. Sivachidambaranathan, Kunal Meher, Vesapogu Joshi Manohar, Amiya Kumar Naik, Vinoth R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Brushless DC (BLDC) motors are widely employed in industrial and automotive systems due to their high efficiency, reliability, and superior torque characteristics. Achieving precise torque management is critical for ensuring smooth operation and extended motor life. However, conventional control strategies often suffer from nonlinearities, parameter variations, and inadequate adaptability, leading to torque ripple, reduced efficiency, and unstable dynamic responses. To overcome these limitations, this work introduces a Fuzzy-Neuro Multi-Objective Control Framework (FNMOCF), which synergistically integrates fuzzy logic with a multi-objective genetic algorithm for torque optimization. The fuzzy logic component enables adaptive decision-making under nonlinear and uncertain conditions. At the same time, the genetic algorithm provides global optimization for balancing competing objectives such as torque ripple minimization, response time, and energy efficiency. The proposed FN-MOCF is applied to BLDC motor drive systems for high-precision torque regulation, ensuring robustness against disturbances and parameter variations. Simulation and experimental evaluations reveal that FN-MOCF significantly reduces torque ripple, enhances dynamic stability, and improves efficiency compared to conventional methods. This study demonstrates the potential of FN-MOCF as a reliable advanced control solution for precision torque management in modern BLDC motor applications. FN-MOCF improves performance by reducing torque ripple to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 1}-\mathbf{3. 0 \%}$</tex>, achieving dynamic stability of 1.3 Nm, maintaining efficiency at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87.1-91.5 \%$</tex>, and ensuring robustness with minimal errors of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 1 0 - 0. 2 2 N m}$</tex>.

title: A Novel Self-Supervised Learning Approach for Anomaly Detection in Adversarial Network Traffic
authors: Anjali Awasthi, Sumita Mishra, Shriram K Vasudevan, Jyoti Grover
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The increasing complexity and frequency of cyber attacks have created a dire need for a more resilient and adaptive network Intrusion Detection System(NIDS). Traditional anomaly detection systems face a serious challenge from the rise of sophisticated cyberattacks, especially hostile manipulations of network traffic. To avoid signature-based and even some anomaly-based detectors, these attacks are made to look like legitimate traffic. The lack of labeled attack data is a significant obstacle to creating strong defenses. In order to overcome this data scarcity of labeled attack data, this paper suggests a novel approach using selfsupervised learning (SSL) framework for anomaly detection in networks which makes use of enormous volumes of unlabeled normal traffic. Our method pre-trains a multi-modal encoder by combining contrastive learning with a set of carefully crafted pretext tasks, including temporal context prediction, conceptual graph structure reconstruction, and a special pseudo-adversarial perturbation prediction/reconstruction task. By explicitly teaching the model to recognize minute, self-generated departures from typical patterns, this pseudo-adversarial task increases the model's sensitivity to actual adversarial manipulations. Robust latent representations are subsequently extracted by the pretrained encoder and fed into a one-class classifier for anomaly detection. Using the difficult CIC-IDS2018 dataset, we compare our SSL approach to a conventional unsupervised Isolation Forest (as a baseline) and a supervised Random Forest classifier (as an upper bound). Our self-supervised approach shows promising results for a reliable as well as a scalable solution for real-world cyber security, as preliminary results show that it significantly outperforms traditional unsupervised methods in detecting both generic anomalies and stealthy adversarial traffic.

title: Real-Time FPGA-Accelerated Image Processing Using Vivado HLS
authors: Asfandyar Jamali, Farida Memon, Shoaib Khaskheli, Shayan Ahmed Khan, Maheen Nawaz Gill
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Real-time image processing is a fundamental requirement in modern applications such as autonomous navigation, real-time surveillance, medical imaging, and industrial inspection. These applications demand rapid and efficient processing of high-resolution visual data, which traditional CPU and GPU-based platforms often fail to deliver in embedded environments due to limitations in latency, power efficiency, and scalability. Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) offer a reconfigurable hardware platform with inherent parallelism and low power consumption, making them ideal for such computationally intensive tasks. This paper presents the design and implementation of a complete FPGA-accelerated image processing pipeline developed using Vivado High-Level Synthesis (HLS) on the Xilinx Zybo Z7-20 board. The pipeline consists of three main stages: Bayer to RGB conversion, gamma correction, and Canny edge detection - each implemented as an optimized HLS module using techniques such as pipelining and dataflow. These modules are integrated via AXI4-Stream and AXI-Lite interfaces for efficient data movement and synchronization. The system processes live video streams from a camera at resolutions of 1080 p at 30 frames per second and 720 p at 60 frames per second, delivering results to real-time HDMI output. Performance evaluation demonstrates that the system achieves high processing speed, efficient resource utilization, and low power consumption of approximately two watts with an operating temperature of around 48° C, making it well-suited for embedded real-time vision applications. The use of Vivado HLS significantly accelerated the development cycle and highlights the potential of high-level design methodologies for complex FPGA-based systems.

title: Optimal Tuning of PSO Parameters for Active Power Loss Minimization of an Interconnected Power Network
authors: Deepanwita Dhur, Biplab Bhattacharyya
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This research focuses on the optimal tuning of PSO parameters in reducing active power loss of a connected network. For this purpose, a unified framework is done, that integrates weak node detection with optimal reactive power compensation through advanced metaheuristic algorithm i.e. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). This approach identifies the optimal PSO parameter setting which provides the most improved outcome in reactive power planning (RPP) and the efficient placement of shunt capacitors. The technique is verified on standard IEEE 14-bus test system in MATLAB Simulation environment, thereby demonstrating the scalability, robustness and adaptability of the framework. The optimized PSO setting precisely minimize active power loss, thereby proving to be a reliable and intelligent solution for modern reactive power management.

title: Design and Analysis of P51 Master Protection Relay Scheme for Negative Sequence in DFIG
authors: Bhabasis Mohapatra, Renu Sharma, Adithya Ballaji, Ritesh Dash, Binod Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced LSTM-PI controller for negative sequence voltage suppression in a DFIG grid interconnected system. The traditional PI and PR controller exhibit limitations in adjusting the controller gain under balanced and unbalanced fault conditions leading to slower transient response and thereby decreasing stability. In order to address this issue, research has been done with LSTM model adaptive PI controller which continuously tunes the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K_{p}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K_{i}$</tex> in real time based on the historical dataset. The negative sequence relay upon its detection of fault the trained LSTM model predicts optimal PI gain to mitigate the negative sequence components. The real time control implementation has been tested with IEEE standard such as IEEE1547 and C50.13 grid codes. This methodology strictly compiling the detection of fault points in the proposed DFIG grid interconnection system. A comparative analysis between PI, PR and LSTM-PI controller has been presented.

title: Blockchain Enabled Self-Sovereign Identity and Consent Framework for Secure Healthcare Data Access
authors: Shanmugham M, Senthilkumar C
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The medical systems are becoming challenged by the issues associated with the secure verification of patient identities, privacy-protecting access control, and dynamic consent management of Electronic Health Records (EHRs). Conventional centralized designs infringe on patient-centered autonomy, have poor identity management, and put sensitive information at the risk of inaccessible information. The paper introduces a proposal to develop a decentralized and privacy preserving mechanism of access control using blockchain-enabled Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI), as proposing a combination of verifiable credentials (VCs), smart contracts, and zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs). The proposed Consent-Driven Decentralized Identity Verification Algorithm (CD-DIVA) enables the patient to create decentralised identities, issue tokens of consent, and dynamically assign or remove access rights without having to involve third-party authorities. Smart contracts enable the conjurant enforcement of various consent terms and offer an inauditable audit trail and ZKPs grant healthcare providers with the ability to demonstrate their authority without revealing sensitive information. Experimental assessments show the framework to be efficient in minimizing the access latency, eliminating unauthorized disclosures and providing effective interoperability of heterogeneous healthcare systems. This contribution will be a complement to the current blockchain-based healthcare solutions since it helps adopt a patient-centric, tamper-proof, and privacy-preserving healthcare ecosystem by bridging the access control and trust gap in such systems.

title: BrainWave Hub: AI-Powered Platform for Collaborative Brainstorming and Creative Ideation
authors: Minal Azhar, Alishba Aleem, Sania Bhatti
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Brainstorming stands as a basic method for creative problem-solving, yet conventional methods fail to provide structured frameworks alongside scalable solutions and smart feedback systems. The AI-based collaborative platform BrainWave Hub solves these problems by uniting real-time brainstorming with intelligent idea linking and structured refinement capabilities. This paper demonstrates the development and testing of BrainWave Hub through a comparison of its features against current solutions and explains its core advancements. The Phi-2 model from Meta receives domain-specific training to help generate ideas and summarize sessions. The model demonstrates its ability to assist creative work in flexible, distributed teams according to experimental findings. The platform advances the development of smarter creative tools that enable innovative teamwork between technical and interdisciplinary professionals.

title: Opening the Web to all: A Systematic Literature Review of Web Accessibility Research and Future Outlook
authors: Ibrar Ahmed, Richa Gupta, Ihtiram Raza Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: To achieve real digital inclusion, online accessibility is a critical and recognized issue that needs strong engineering techniques to remove barriers faced by people with disabilities. Despite substantial study yielding a variety of ideas, approaches, and tools, it is still difficult to evaluate and integrate these solutions into modern development workflows. There is a requirement for the new, engineering-oriented perspective because earlier literature reviews often have been too narrow in focus or insufficiently rigorous in their technical synthesis. In this Paper, 95 initial studies on the design and evaluation process were reviewed as part of a Systematic Review of Web Accessibility Research Papers between 2010 and 2025. The review addresses important areas: framework engineering, automated testing methods, assistive technology, and the increased use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to enhance inclusivity to the best of our understanding, it is the primary systematic literature review that unifies accessibility research with the emphasis on the engineering approaches that promote development of easily reachable web-based platforms. In addition to existing contributions, we indicate methodological trends, shared limitations, and current gaps in the research. It offers new opportunities to developers, designers, and researchers in the form of best practices, new approaches, and open challenges to research. Finally, the results leave the door open to the evolution of improved accessibility testing and a more inclusive internet for all.

title: Design and Implementation of Network Camera Control by Human Face Tracking Based on Deep Neural Network Module
authors: Amrarul Islam, Ifiok Otung, Muhammad Majid Hussain, Taiwo Okikiolu, Rehana Sarker, Amit Sarker, Hammad Nazir
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents a real-time control system for network cameras based on face tracking. The system relies on IP communication to guide the camera, allowing it to follow human faces or other selected targets automatically. To maintain stable motion, camera settings are adjusted continuously so that both facial and non-facial features are recognized over a sequence of frames. The method achieves reliable detection under different appearances, including variations in skin tone and clothing, which strengthens its use in practical conditions. A deep neural network (DNN) is applied to improve detection accuracy, supported by an optical feedback loop that increases responsiveness. In addition, a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controller is used to correct frame displacement caused by object movement, resulting in more precise control. The work aims to develop practical design guidelines for embedding DNN modules in IP cameras, focusing on feature selection, tracking speed, and two-dimensional movement as an alternative to conventional approaches.

title: Exploring Carbon Trading Mechanisms in Road Transportation: An Insurance Industry Perspective
authors: Ma Qingjia, Li Puchao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: To support the achievement of carbon neutrality targets in the transportation sector and promote the sustainable development of the new energy vehicle (NEV) insurance industry, this study proposes introducing a carbon trading mechanism into road transportation with insurance companies as the compliance entities. By leveraging premium-based price transmission, the mechanism can guide end-users toward low-carbon transitions while optimizing insurers' revenue structures. This research examines the coverage of the transportation carbon market and suggests adopting relative intensity control as the method for setting the cap, while employing the baseline approach for allocation. Based on vehicle ownership data in Tianjin, the market is estimated to cover over 5.84 million tons of carbon emissions. Furthermore, an analysis of insurance premiums and loss ratios indicates that the marginal abatement cost for insurers is approximately 1,222 RMB per ton, which is significantly higher than the current carbon market price. Finally, through scenario analysis, this study explores the impacts of key factors-including the rate of cap reduction, NEV incentive coefficients, and vehicle mileage-on market performance and discusses corresponding policy responses, providing valuable references for the design of transportation carbon markets

title: LESA: A Bert-Based Legal Document Summarization and Clause Extraction Framework
authors: Himanshu Kumar Bhagat, Rudransh Goel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Legal documents pose a major difficulty in analysis because of complexity in terms of language and use of specific terminology as well as length. The time-consuming aspect of manual processing of these documents not only consumes time but also is vulnerable to human error and inconsistency. In this paper, the author presents LESA (Legal Summarization and Analysis), a new hybrid system that uses the state-of-the-art natural language processing methods to automatically analyze legal texts using abstractive legal document summarization and accurate clause identification. We use a fine-tuned BART model to produce coherent summaries and a clause-identification and categorization rule-based models together with a Legal-BERTbased classifier. We tested our system on a filtered set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 2 5 0}$</tex> non-disclosure agreements and service contracts, and the summarization score was ROUGE-1 of 0.68, and the overall clause extraction score was 0.82. As can be seen through the comparative analysis, LESA is much more effective than the traditional extractive methods such as the TextRank and the TF-IDF. The system is a tremendous development in technology of legal systems which has some possible applications in both legal practice and management as well as in legal access to justice by the population. We make contributions in the form of the new hybrid architecture, a detailed evaluation metrics and the publication of benchmark datasets to encourage continued research in the area of legal document processing.

title: Design and Implementation of IoT Based Energy Monitoring System for Smart Energy Meters
authors: Mayur Vaidya, Yash Sonje, Ritika Bhandari, Varad Joshi, Krutuja Gadgil, Sachin Shelar
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In the modern era, for optimal power utilization and to lower costs, effective energy monitoring is required. This paper presents the design and implementation of an IoT-based monitoring and visualization system for multi-function meters. A multi-function meter (Secure Make - Elite 440 model) is integrated with an IoT gateway (Teltonika Make - TRB 145 model) using RS-485 Modbus communication to measure real-time electrical parameters. The data collected and then data is sent to the cloud via Azure IoT Hub were it is stored and processed for further analysis. A web-based dashboard is developed for the visualization of real-time and historical data by which users are able to monitor energy usage efficiently. The developed system offers a real-time, user-friendly, scalable energy monitoring system, used for both residential and commercial industries. In addition to cost savings and operational efficiency, the developed system supports the Sustainable Development Framework (SDF) and is aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy) as it promotes clean energy transitions and smarter energy management on the demand side. Experimental evaluation shows that the proposed system achieves near real-time performance with an average end-to-end latency of approximately 0.8 s, a maximum observed latency of about 1.3 s, zero packet loss and stable three-phase voltage measurements under continuous operation.

title: Hybrid XGBoost-ANN Model for Solar Radiation Prediction with High Spatial Resolution
authors: Bongoni Naresh, Amit Kumar Yadav, M Meghana
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Solar energy is a clean and natural option as it is environmental friendly. To find solar radiation in good places is quite a job as it helps retain the flora and fauna in the ecosystem. Through this research, we are studying about bringing in hybrid machine learning mechanism to get a good prediction quotient for solar radiation. With this hybrid structure with aim at XGBoost extreme gradient increase with ANN (artificial neural network). These XGBoost structures deal with difficult complex structures in different seasonal data structures. ANN does well with nonlinear structures. Combined structure here displays good results against individual ANN and XGBoost model predictability. MAE, RMSE, MSE and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> metrics are used for good outcome calculation analysis. These results aid in energy forecasting applications for detailed studies about solar radiation predictions

title: A Review on Flexible and Wearable Antennas for Next-Generation Wireless Applications
authors: Preeti Sharma, Shradha Singh Parihar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The need for high-performance antennas that can handle high data speeds, compactness, and user comfort has increased due to the quick development of 5 G and the soon-tobe 6 G communication technologies. Increased bandwidth, isolation, and conformability for both on-body and off-body communication are provided by flexible and wearable antennas, which have become a crucial answer to these problems. The latest advancements in flexible and wearable antennas are thoroughly reviewed in this study, with an emphasis on substrate materials, design configurations, decoupling methods, and performance optimization tactics. Trade-offs between flexibility, dielectric stability, and fabrication feasibility are demonstrated by comparing various flexible substrates, including polyimide, PDMS, and Rogers ULTRALAM. Lastly, this review paper outlines the difficulties and potential avenues for future research in incorporating flexible antennas into wearable technology and smart textiles that are connected to the Internet of Things.

title: HCSEM: A Hybrid Contextual and Syntactic Evaluation Metric for Question Answering Systems Beyond BLEU
authors: Monika Singh, C. Patvardhan, C. Vasantha Lakshmi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: As more and more large language models (LLMs) are developed, it is more important to be able to accurately judge the answers given by them. Standard evaluation metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, and METEOR depend mostly on lexical overlap, so they are not good for judging responses that preserve the meaning by using different reasoning structures or paraphrasing. While embedding-based approaches improve semantic awareness, they often overlook syntactic coherence and hierarchical meaning composition. To overcome these limitations, we propose a training-free hybrid evaluation framework, HCSEM that include both syntactic structure as well as semantic understanding to measure the quality of the text. HCSEM combines contextual embeddings from GPTbased encoders to get the conceptual meaning and uses normalized parse-tree and dependency alignment measures to model structural similarity. Adaptive n-gram semantic matching is also used to assess the meaning of phrases, making sure that both token-level and compositional semantics are well represented. The experiment is held on two datasets on standard evaluation techniques which indicates that HCSEM surpasses traditional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N}$</tex>-gram and BERT-based evaluation metrics. We also evaluate HCSEM on Question Answering tasks across six widely used LLMs. The experimental results indicate that HCSEM aligns more closely with human judgment than traditional lexical and embedding-only metrics. The GPT-4o mini has the highest semantic similarity score, followed by the GLM-4-9B and the LLaMA-3-8B. These results show that HCSEM is a reliable and meaning-oriented to evaluate modern QA systems. It is a useful alternative to metrics that only focus on lexical or neural networks-based evaluation.

title: Multi-Channel Drive Circuit for Electro-Optic Modulator with High Half-Wave Voltage
authors: Hao Lou, Yanlin Chen, Xiaodong Xu, Xiaowei Qin
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Electro-optical modulators (EOMs), which alter optical signal properties like phase via external electrical signals, are vital for optical communication networks. However, EOMs with short-electrode designs or operating in high-frequency broadband regimes require significantly increased half-wave voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\pi}$</tex>) for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi$</tex>-phase modulation. To address this challenge, this study proposes a multi-channel drive circuit. Adopting a modular architecture, the circuit consists of four core modules: an FPGAbased digital control module, a high-precision DAC conversion array, a two-stage amplification drive circuit with programmable high-voltage output, and a multi-channel clock synchronization system. Implemented as a 32-channel phase drive system, it achieves 10 Mbps modulation rate, 22.49 V average output voltage range, over 7-bit (128-level) dynamic resolution, 0.42 % voltage accuracy, and 16.4 ns rise time. A test platform integrating the hardware with a simulated optical phased array (OPA) system validates the circuit's output effectiveness. This research provides a critical hardware solution for high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\pi}$</tex> and high-precision EOMs, with significant application potential in quantum optics, coherent optical communications, and related fields.

title: A Novel Lightweight Stream Cipher Based on NLFSRs and Chaos Function
authors: Mohammad Ubaidullah Bokhari, Shaharyar Alam Ansari, Shahnwaz Afzal, Shahnawaz Ahmad
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is already an established phenomenon where numerous devices are interconnected and used daily. Such devices collect and send out enormous amounts of information, and experts believe people will depend more on IoT over the years. As the volume of shared information continues to grow, there is an increasing need to strengthen security measures. The wide range of data IoT must protect is difficult due to IoT's lack of capacity regarding hardware, power, and storage. Conventional encryption techniques cannot be utilized effectively in these types of surroundings. To solve these obstacles, we suggest a lightweight cipher capable of strong encryption without abundant resources. In this study, Two Non-Linear Feedback Shift Registers (NLFSRs) are utilized in conjunction with a logistic chaotic function where the NLFSRs are initialized using 40-bit seed keys. Our method, in comparison to the best algorithms such as Trivium, Grain, and Grain128 performs the best when it comes to the speed and number of encryption and decryption cycles that can be completed within a certain duration. In addition to proving compliance with NIST, the algorithm achieves an impressive strong avalanche effect where even minimal alterations to the input result in substantial and unpredictable results. This feature, together with the high level of increase in the keystream and the low level of security the keystream offers, increases the algorithm's resistance to cryptographic attacks.

title: SmartFlow: An AI-Powered Traffic Control System
authors: Kiran Kumar, Akshay Tiwari, Javed Miya, Sandeep Raj, Neha Tyagi, Mitu Matta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In cities, traffic congestion at intersections is a frequent issue, resulting in lost time, higher fuel consumption, and environmental pollution. Traditional traffic signal control systems often rely on basic timers or fixed schedules, which can lead to inefficiencies during busy periods or unexpected situations. This paper seeks to develop an smart traffic light control system that optimizes signal timing at intersections by employing machine learning algorithms and vehicle recognition methods. By accurately tracking the presence and movement of vehicles in real-time, the system can adjust the phases and durations of signals dynamically to respond to changing traffic conditions and reduce congestion. The approach utilizes a dynamic traffic algorithm that calculates the necessary green light duration for each road based on input factors such as inflow rate, outflow rate, and queue length. Unlike traditional methods that set signal timings irrespective of traffic conditions, the core concept is to allocate green time effectively according to the level of congestion. [11]

title: Model Comparison for Intelligent Drowsiness Detection Using Machine Learning
authors: Neeru Chauhan, Sarika Choudhary
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The primary reason behind major road accidents is drowsiness and drowsiness detection has become increasingly critical in safety-sensitive environments such as transportation and industrial operations. In this research, machine learning models are compared, with a focus on Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The dataset used is NTHUDDD2 which consists of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 6, 5 0 0}$</tex> images for drowsiness detections. Model performance is assessed using accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score. Accuracy for Random Forest is more than SVM and CNN which is 0.998 while that for SVM is 0.997 and for CNN is 0.870. Precision for Random Forest is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 9 9}$</tex>, for SVM is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 9 6}$</tex> and for CNN is 0.889. Recall and F1 score for Random Forest is 0.997 and 0.998 respectively for SVM is 0.997 and 0.997 respectively while for CNN is 0.847 and 0.867 respectively. The difference between the results of Random Forest and SVM is minor but Random Forest is superior model overall.

title: TORONS: Mapping the Unseen Web
authors: Suryansh Deshwal, Kanishka, Ambar Chakarvartty, Bhuvnesh Malik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The Dark Web, which consists of most of the internet, is composed of all the anonymous networks that hide the identity of the user and server, which allows threat actors to perform illegal and wrong activities. These anonymous networks comprise TOR, I2P and freenet, etc. TORONS is a dark web crawler, i.e. designed to efficiently extract and archive the content from both Tor and I2P, and more of these networks simultaneously, on the same machine with minimal resource utilization, providing some real-world features like webarchives (Dark web), Classified database for model training, custom Crawler engine, etc. Unlike other crawlers, TORONS uses a NLP-based hate speech detection algorithm that searches for specific hate keywords on different dark Web forums and creates a web archive of all crawled websites. Crawled data is stored in a MongoDB-based database, creating a web archive for the dark web. This data can later be used to train various AI models for a more perfect segregation of such dark web forums. This data can then be used for making practical tools for OSINT, cyber forensics and security research in anonymized ecosystems. The crawler uses a specialized technique to spawn and system level and physical threads and cores for parallel processing and asynchronous crawling on each particular process. It also supports CLI interface reducing the usage for resources from GUI and window managers, etc. It also has a modular structure which helps in using different devices to setup the different features for separate and secure processing. The crawler is entirely based on a CLI interface, offering efficient resource monitoring and suitable for different hardware conditions for various systems

title: A Comparative Analysis of Trust Management Approaches for Attack Mitigation in IoT
authors: Bhaskar Maithani, Ajay Vikram Singh, Said Broumi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) and Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) underpin critical applications in healthcare, smart cities, and industrial automation, yet their resource-constrained nature exposes them to sophisticated attacks that evade conventional security measures. Trust management that assesses the behavior of nodes from either direct or indirect observations becomes a critical countermeasure. This survey reviews the various trust management methodologies — covering group-based, fuzzy logic, machine learning, blockchain, statistical, game-theoretic, time-series, hybrid, and social-based — and each is exemplified by a mainstay model. From reported results in the literature, fuzzy and hybrid trust models typically achieve detection accuracies above 85-92% against blackhole and selectiveforwarding attacks, while machine-learning-based trust models reach up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{9 6 \%}$</tex> accuracy against Sybil and on-off attacks. We compare the strengths and drawbacks of such mechanisms through their performance in defending against a wide range of trust-based attacks on a gradient. Blockchain-enabled trust models provide strong tamper resistance but incur <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0}-\mathbf{4 0} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> higher energy overhead compared to lightweight statistical and group-based schemes, which consume up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25-50 \%$</tex> less energy. It provides attack types, model mappings, and mitigation capabilities, serving as a guideline for academics and industrial researchers to develop trustworthy IoT/WSN-based systems.

title: Federated Learning: Architecture, Challenges, and Accuracy Requital with Client Scaling in Healthcare IoT Systems
authors: Prachi Gawande, Yogita Dubey, Punit Fulzele
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Sensitive data handling is very difficult these days. To overcome this, the Federate Learning (FL) approach is best which allows collective model training. In this training, the sensitive data is not centralized. FL helps to keep data private especially in healthcare care where patient monitoring is done continuously through wearable devices. But when the clients increases in FL, the accuracy of model decreases. This happens due to the variety of data distributions, delay in communications and limited synchronization. This paper investigates the theoretical underpinnings of FL, its main merits, and its limitations. It's also shows findings along with simulations that shows a logarithmic drop in accuracy when the count of client increases. A weighted aggregation algorithm is implied to help in the improvement of performance. In this paper the reviews of recent developments from 2022 to 2025 in federated learning and its relevance to healthcare has been studied.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Real-Time Tennis Match Analysis
authors: Santosh Kumar Srivastava, Swati Vashisht, Suman Avdhesh Yadav, Sunil Kumar Maakar, Hare Ram Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Deep learning technology has revolutionized real-time performance analysis and intelligent decision-making in the field of tennis. The conventional analysis methods heavily relied on manual annotation and post-event processing which is time-consuming and not applicable to large-scale or real-time applications. This papers reviews recent deep learning methods for on-the-fly tennis analytics focusing on player localization, ball trajectory estimation, stroke type classification and point winner prediction. We use state-of-the-art deep learning models such as CNNs, RNNs and Transformer based architectures to capture space and time dynamics of the tennis point. The fastagile and small-volume characteristics of the tennis ball are difficult to detect, for this reason we introduce customized neural networks for the detection of small objects. And multimodal data fusion - where disparate modes of data (e.g., visual with sensor readings and contextual metadata) are combined - has been demonstrated to improve the accuracy and robustness of analysis greatly. The presented framework emphasizes applications in various areas, such as telematic sport competition platforms, automatic referee systems, immersive sports TV experience, performance-oriented coaching devices and engagement with fans. Potential future work include the creation of foundational deep learning models in specifically for sports analytics, the integration with physics-aware neural networks for accurate motion and trajectory modelling and explainability artificial intelligence methods to ensure interpretability and fairness in decision making. In conclusion, real-time tennis analysis using deep learning will revolutionise contemporary training approaches, media coverage and referee judging of matches.
