title: Multi-Modal Biometric Recognition Smart Access Control System for Doors: An All-Inclusive Security Framework
authors: Anitha. S, Kapilkrishnan A B, Pushpalatha Naveenkumar, S.V. Nagalakshmi, Selvaraj. G, S. Gowdhamkumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: In this study, a novel smart access control system, which incorporates multiple biometric identification technologies, has been introduced to make commercial establishments safer in the modern world. The proposed approach incorporates the concepts of facial recognition, fingerprint scanning, as well as RFID identification, which combined, result in a highly secure multiple factor identification system. The system possesses a distributed database, which facilitates easier handling of multiple users in varying operational environments, secure communication channels that safeguard data, as well as edge computing, which enhances speed. This approach also rectifies significant loopholes in the safety features offered by existing access control systems, which make it impregnable using hardware security modules whose updates cannot be modified in any way, anti-spoofing algorithms, or detection of liveliness. The accuracy rate in the experiments conducted using 500 registered members in varying scenarios was rather satisfactory at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 9. 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy in verification, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 1 \%}$</tex> false acceptance rate, and 0.79 % false rejection rate. Users also reported feelings of safety and security since it only took an average of 0.8 seconds for the sensor to turn on and unlock the door. In the 30 -day experiment conducted on continuous operation, the system performed 99.7 % accurately. Some novel safety features include the utilization of AES-256 encryption, secure TLS 1.3 connectivity, cancellable biometric templates, and multiple audit tracking features. It's easy to interface the app with existing approaches in management, handling, and processing IDs, surveillance, or buildings due to its modularity, which possesses standard API interfaces. It can be operated independently, in multiple configurations, or in networks.

title: An Intelligent Geo-Tagging and Tree Plantation Tracking System Using Flutter and FastAPI for Scalable Environment Monitoring
authors: Mayur Bhoye, Atharwa Waghmare, Vaishnavi Jokhe, Tanishk Chandgude, Priyanka More
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Manual monitoring of reforestation efforts suffers from inefficient paper-based tracking and unverifiable records, hindering transparency and scalability. This paper proposes a Geo-Tagging Tree Planting Tracking System that integrates mobile-first data capture with geospatial analytics to enable real-time, verifiable forest restoration monitoring. Built on Flutter (offline-first mobile capture with SQLite) and FastAPI (cloud synchronization with PostgreSQL), our system records GPS-tagged, timestamped tree data, including species, age, health status, and geospatial coordinates, for comprehensive audit trails. Demo deployment across 50 field surveys by 10 surveyors across 5 locations captured 2,500 trees with 98% data completeness and reduced field-to-dashboard verification time from 7 days to 2 hours (97% reduction vs. manual methods). The web-based analytics dashboard delivers geospatial insights, including 85% plantation survival rate, at-risk tree density heatmaps, and species distribution analysis, enabling data-driven reforestation management at scale.

title: AI-Based Intelligent Hackathon Team Formation Through TF- IDF Factorization, Collaborative Filtering, and Graph Neural Network Modeling
authors: Prachi Rani, Pari, Laxman Singh, Sujal Singh, Kartik Singh, Rekha Kashyap
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The TeamBuilders platform presents a very smart method for producing a well-designed team for the hackathons. This platform has AI-derived matchmaking ability. This app helps to connect people through their skill set in which they are expertise. The inbuilt AI model suggests teams that fit well, based on suitability, skill variety, and priority-based interactions. This AI model helps to enhance the efficiency of team formation. This work provided a scalable solution, elevating hackathon collaboration by using this AI model.

title: Security Enhancement in Agricultural Application Using RSA Algorithm and Deep Learning Technology
authors: Asha Mary Chacko, Anubhav Sharma, Manmohan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The agricultural industry is getting more involved in digital technologies, making it vulnerable to numerous cyber threats that can jeopardize data integrity, interfere with automated systems, and slow down productivity. In this research, we put forward a hybrid security model for smart agriculture that combines deep learning-based intrusion detection with adaptive RSA encryption. From the CICIDS2017 subset (Friday Working Hours Afternoon DDoS) dataset, a neural network model was trained to identify anomalous traffic with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{j} 99.9 \%$</tex> precision, high precision, recall, F1 score, and AUC; 1.0. Dynamic RSA key size selection (1024, 2048, or 3072 bits) is based on threat prediction and computational load proxies, trading off encryption speed against security. Key generation, encryption, and decryption time measurements illustrate the practicality of the method. The intended system improves secure communication in agriculture IoT scenarios by facilitating timely threat identification and context-aware encryption.

title: A Simulation-Based Performance Analysis of an Integrated Solar PV and Fuel Cell System for Sustainable EV Charging
authors: Prabhu sankaranarayanan G, Siva sankar G
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The increasing recognition of electric vehicles (EVs) demands that it be capable of autonomous, renewable charging infrastructure. To transcend this, the intermittent characteristic of solar Energy and ensure reliability in continuous charging abilities, this Research proposes a solar-powered EV charging station in tandem with fuel cell energy storage. Using photovoltaic (PV) panels, the System converts solar energy into electrical energy that can be stored or directly used for charging. Electrolysis converts surplus energy to form hydrogen, which is stored and used in fuel cells, producing electricity when the irradiance of the sun is low. This hybrid approach provides a constant power supply for charging EVs by combining the instantaneous benefits of solar energy and the reliability of fuel cells. Based on simulation calculations, such a system can effectively reduce reliance on grid energy, reduce carbon emissions, and provide a greener way to meet the demands for EV charging. Integration of Fuel cells provides a strong and green charging system, which helps counter the problems with the uncertainty of solar power. This Model serves as a reference for future developments in energy storage and green transport

title: An Improved Piecewise Direct Standardization (PDS) Calibration Transfer Method
authors: Jing Zhu, Baohua Huang, Ai Jiang, Ruqiang Tian, Hao Guo, Yongzhou Chen
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the challenges in NIRS calibration transfer, this study proposes an OPLS-PDS model that integrates Orthogonal Projections to Latent Structures with Piecewise Direct Standardization. The method extracts latent structures from source domain spectra using OPLS to separate predictive information from orthogonal noise. By reusing the feature extraction parameters, it establishes a transfer matrix between domains through PDS. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms conventional methods in prediction accuracy and stability.

title: LiteFakeNet: Efficient Deepfake Image Detection with Depthwise Separable Convolutions
authors: Suprove Chandra Sarkar, Ashrafia Esha, Touhid Bhuiyan, Md. Maruf Hassan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The rapid rise of generative AI has encouraged the spread of deepfake photographs, jeopardizing digital media credibility and security. Efficient detection algorithms are crucial to counter misinformation and sustain trust in visual content. This research proposes to construct a novel convolutional neural network (CNN) that achieves significant accuracy in deepfake photo recognition while minimizing computational and energy costs. We introduce a unique lightweight CNN architecture, LiteFakeNet that leverages depthwise separable convolutions, trained and tested on the CIFAKE dataset of 120,000 real and AI-generated images. Our model achieves an accuracy of 90.5%, precision of 91.2%, and recall of 89.8%, with a compact design of around less than a million parameters along with only 0.16 million FLOPs. This research extends deepfake detection by proposing a scalable, energy-efficient method that maintains robust performance. This efficiency of LiteFakeNet aligns with Industry 5.0 paradigm by enabling human-AI collaboration for media verification and supports sustainable green technology that reduce the energy consumption of the deep learning model.

title: Modeling, Analysis, and Design of a Solar-Assisted Light Electric Vehicle Drive System Using a Triple Active Bridge Converter
authors: Alauddin Ahmed, Fariha Anjum Oshin, Rehnuma Siddique, Wahi Farhan Hoque
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The growing demand for sustainable and clean transportation has driven interest in integrating renewable energy sources into electric vehicle (EV) systems. This paper presents a solar-assisted light electric vehicle (LEV) powered by a Triple Active Bridge (TAB) DC-DC converter, which enables efficient bidirectional power transfer among a photovoltaic (PV) source, traction battery, and auxiliary loads. A detailed mathematical model of the TAB converter is developed, highlighting its capability for dynamic power routing and galvanic isolation. System performance is analyzed under varying load conditions, including transient response and torque variation, using simulation results. The findings demonstrate the converter's ability to manage power flow efficiently, ensure system stability during sudden load changes, and utilize excess solar energy to charge onboard batteries. The proposed architecture reduces dependency on grid electricity and enhances the practicality of solar-powered LEVs in urban environments, particularly in regions like Bangladesh where light EVs are widely adopted.

title: Enhanced Core Temperature Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries Using EKF and PSO-Based Thermal Modeling
authors: Keyur K. Patel, Mahmadasraf A. Mulla, Sanjay Tolani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In battery management systems (BMS), accurate estimation of the core temperature of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) is crucial for ensuring operational safety, enhancing performance, and preventing thermal runaway. Direct measurement of core temperature is impractical due to sensor placement limitations and reliability concerns. Therefore, model-based estimation techniques are employed. This study proposes a method that integrates an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) with a two-state lumped thermal model to estimate core temperature. The Bernardi model is used to characterize heat generation, accounting for electrochemical overpotential and entropy changes. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is applied to fine-tune model parameters derived from pulse charge/discharge and constant current experiments. A novel core temperature estimation equation is introduced and incorporated into a spatially driven thermal model to enhance accuracy. The mean absolute errors between estimated and measured core and surface temperatures are approximately 0.024° C and 0.3° C, respectively, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Tractor with Green Charger Using Buck Zero Current Switching Converter for Agriculture
authors: Diksha Khare, Nitin Dhote, Swapna Choudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: For the new generation with increasing demand for power supply, Hybrid renewable energy systems (HRES) are an essential and immediate solution. During the recent years, Renewable Energy technologies are going through constant advancement and hence offering a fair share in Power supply requirements of new generation. In this paper we have presented Hybrid powered model comprising a photovoltaic, a wind generator plus battery bank storage of power considering the uncertainty of weather conditions. DC-DC converter namely ZCS Buck Converter is supplied from this storage battery. This HRES topology supplies low voltage battery charger. Batteries play a significant role in day to day life, as a reliable energy source for storage systems, However, conventional battery chargers are typically bulky and experience significant power dissipation during the charging process, leading to poor efficiency. Therefore, selecting an appropriate method of charging is crucial to improve the efficiency of charging and prolong the overall lifespan of the battery. The duty ratio of the converter can control the operation of the Hybrid renewable energy systems HRES operation be and therefore can supply the variable DC load.

title: Model-Based Sensor Fault Diagnosis for Hydraulic-Electro Servo Systems
authors: Xinyu He
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The safety, reliability and accuracy of the sensors are particularly crucial for the operation control of the hydraulic system. In this paper, we propose a sensor fault diagnosis scheme for hydraulic system from a model-based perspective. Studying the fault mechanism of hydraulic systems is of great significance for achieving fault diagnosis. Therefore, we first establish the dynamic mathematical model of the hydraulic system, and then linearize it near the equilibrium point to establish the state space model of the system. Furthermore, by constructing an augmented state vector that is composed of the original state of the system and sensor fault, a descriptor observer is used to estimate the new augmented state vector. To analyze the stability of the observer and design the gain of the observer, the Lyapunov method is employed to achieve the convergence of the sensor fault error. Finally, numerical simulation of hydraulic system with sensor bias sensor fault is implemented to verify the effectiveness of our proposed scheme.

title: Machine Learning Based Fraud Detection in UPI Transactions
authors: Ankur Das, Niladri Das, Priyanka Biswas, Nirmalya Kar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The evolution of India's digital payments landscape received an enormous boost with the development of Unified Payments Interface (UPI). However, with the convenience brought about by the rapid adoption of UPI comes the increased number of fraudulent activities, which is beginning to undermine the trust users have placed in the system. Protection of system users and trust requires focus on the real-time detection of fraudulent UPI transactions. With UPI's increasing utilization, the main goal of the project is to address this prominent issue using machine learning techniques. This project seeks to construct a robust and intelligently designed scheme that intelligently manages large scales of UPI transaction data to accurately identify fraudulent patterns. The system, through advanced algorithms and real-time execution, aims to flag suspicious transactions instantaneously. Focus is placed on advancing security for digital payments, lowering risk, while reinforcing the structure within which online financial transactions are conducted. Trust and security of UPI transactions will be fortified together with the protection this project offers in relation to the overarching digital economy.

title: A Novel Shade-Tolerant PV Array Reconfiguration for Enhanced Energy Yield
authors: Kashika Baranwal, Prem Prakash, Vinod Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Uneven irradiance conditions over a PV array have a profound influence on its overall energy yield and operational reliability. To address this, a novel shade resilient PV array architecture is proposed in this paper that strategically repositions PV modules with minimal shifting. The proposed method is simulated on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9 \times 9 \text{PV}$</tex> array, and its performance is extensively compared with other popular state-of-art-methods (i.e., Optimal SudoKu, Parquet SudoKu, Chaos Map, Index Based Matrix, and Odd-Even-Prime), based on five critical indicators, namely power improvement, mismatch loss, fill factor, execution ratio, and row current variance. Further, this study pioneers the incorporation of a prominent statistical metric, standard deviation, to assess the wiring requirements of various reconfiguration approaches. The results reveal a substantial reduction of up to 35.27% in mismatch loss, and up to 100% reduction in standard deviation and row current variance by proposed method with respect to conventional TCT configuration.

title: Safety Belt Detection and Recognition for Wind Turbine Climbing Operations Based on YOLOv8
authors: Haiping Jiang, Qi Cao, Peng Hu, Chuanchao Fan, Can Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: There is a risk of falling from high altitude during the production and construction process of wind turbines. Therefore, artificial intelligence technology is needed to achieve intelligent detection of standard seat belt wearing. To this end, an improved model based on YOLOv8 was proposed, which integrates the CSPDarknet53 backbone network, PAFPN feature fusion module, and anchor free detection head to construct a closed-loop management system of “detection warning voice intervention recording”. In terms of data, we collected on-site video surveillance images from Longyuan Power, covering conditions such as strong light, weak light, and shadows. Six types of targets were marked, and 16000 training sets and 1600 validation sets were created, covering various scenes at the bottom of the unit tower to improve the accuracy of model detection. There are three technological innovations in this article. Firstly, using a ladder as an anchor point to dynamically plan the work area, filtering out irrelevant interference and reducing false alarm rates; Secondly, by adopting continuous frame detection and integrating multiple frame results in non crawling scenes, the false alarm rate has been further reduced by 5%; Thirdly, by adding IOU post-processing filtering to the trapezoidal model, the accuracy of trapezoidal scene detection can reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{8 5 \%}$</tex>, forming a safety detection system with strong engineering applicability and promoting the safety and intelligent upgrading of the new energy wind power industry.

title: A Hierarchical Planning Framework Enhanced by Feedback Mechanisms and Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Long Yang, Feng Shuang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In robotic task planning, researchers have made significant progress with symbolic planners that use representations like planning domain definition language (PDDL) and LLM-based task planners. Previous research has typically focused on planning for the entire task goal. However, in longhorizon tasks, these approaches often suffer from low accuracy due to the expanding search space. To address the challenge, we propose a novel framework that combines multimodal large language models with PDDL. Our approach leverages LLMs to break down complex tasks into subgoals, creates subplans for each subgoal, and then integrates them into an initial action plan. This plan is evaluated and iteratively refined using a custom validator. By reducing the search space and making sub-goals more manageable, our framework enhances overall task success. The real-time feedback provided by the validator allows for dynamic adjustments, further improving the success rate of planning. We evaluate our framework in three planning domains, and the results demonstrate a significantly higher success rate in long-horizon tasks compared to existing LLM-based planners. Furthermore, it shows impressive performance in scenarios with misleading or distracting information, highlighting its robustness in handling complex and noisy environments.

title: Design and Implementation of a PI-Based Closed-Loop Buck Converter for Low-Power Charging Applications
authors: Singaravelan Arumugam, Anusree Shibindas, Sai Goutham S, Sathish M S, Rajendra S Keshannavar, Praful K Vyapari, C Likith, Pranav G Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The paper presents a design and implementation of a closed-loop buck converter using a proportional integral (PI) control strategy for low-power battery charging applications. Initially the designed buck converter was tested with an open loop by varying the duty cycle with a switching frequency of 25 kHz using a microcontroller. An IR2110-based gate driver circuit was used to drive the PWM from the microcontroller to feed it to the MOSFET of the buck converter. The open-loop voltage control demonstrates a linear response to changes in the duty cycle, ranging from 10 % to 50 %, with increments of 10 % for the resistive load (R load). A voltage divider circuit was designed and implemented to measure the output voltage and feed it back to the controller to perform the closed-loop control. The proposed voltage sensor was tested and calibrated and found to have 0.08 % error, which is acceptable to use for the proposed buck converter. The closed loop was implemented later using the voltage sensing circuit. The PI controller continuously adjusts the PWM duty cycle to maintain output voltage stability under dynamic load and input conditions. Experiment validation was conducted across a range of output reference voltages (2 V to 15 V) using a 30 V programmable DC source as a fixed input to the converter, demonstrating fast transient response, minimal steady-state error, and stable regulation. These results confirm the effective functioning of the converter in a closed-loop configuration, with potential applicability in controlled DC charging systems.
